// DBML Schema for Business: Clinical Trials
// Version: 1
// Generated on: 2026-05-07 02:33:14

Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Clinical Trials - Version 1'
}

Table study.protocol {
  protocol_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the clinical study protocol record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the protocol data product.']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Every protocol is conducted under an IND. This link is required for annual IND report generation, clinical hold management, and safety reporting obligation tracking. ind_cta_number is a denormalized t']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which participants are assigned to study arms. \'Randomized\' indicates use of RTSM/IRT for random assignment; \'non-randomized\' indicates assignment by investigator or other non-random method. Drives RTSM configuration in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol amendment was formally issued or approved. Null for the original protocol version. Used to establish the amendment timeline and regulatory notification deadlines.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for the protocol amendment (e.g., Amendment 1, Amendment 2). Null for the original protocol version. Used to track the amendment history and link to regulatory submission records in Veeva Vault.']
  amendment_rationale varchar [note: 'Formal justification for the protocol amendment describing the scientific, safety, or operational reasons for the change. Required for regulatory submissions and IRB/IEC notifications. Stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the protocol amendment per regulatory definitions. \'Substantial\' amendments require prior regulatory authority and IRB/IEC approval before implementation; \'non-substantial\' require notification only; \'administrative\' are clerical corrections with no impact on study conduct or subject safety.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol version received final sponsor and/or regulatory/IRB/IEC approval, making it the operative protocol for study conduct. Tracked in Veeva Vault quality management workflow. Establishes the effective date for GCP compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this protocol record was first created in the source system or ingested into the lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  dsmb_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol mandates the establishment of a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent safety oversight and interim analysis review. Typically required for Phase III trials and studies with significant safety risks.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) number or Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS) number assigned under EU CTR 536/2014. Required for all clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA. Used for EMA regulatory tracking and public registry disclosure.']
  gcp_compliance_declaration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol includes a formal declaration of compliance with ICH E6(R2) Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines and applicable regulatory requirements. Required for all interventional clinical trials. Tracked in Veeva Vault.']
  ich_compliance_declaration boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol formally declares compliance with applicable ICH guidelines (E6, E8, E9, E10, etc.). Distinct from GCP declaration; covers the broader ICH guideline framework including statistical and design standards.']
  intervention_model varchar [note: 'Describes the strategy for assigning interventions to participants. Determines the statistical analysis plan (SAP) structure, number of arms, and eCRF design in Medidata Rave. Aligns with CDISC SDTM ARM domain.']
  irb_iec_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) notification or approval for this protocol version or amendment. Tracks compliance with ethics review requirements across jurisdictions. Sourced from Veeva Vault regulatory module.']
  is_adaptive_design boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study employs an adaptive design allowing pre-specified modifications to the trial or statistical procedures based on interim data analysis. Triggers additional regulatory review requirements and DSMB/DMC oversight obligations.']
  is_pediatric boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study includes a pediatric population (subjects under 18 years). Triggers Pediatric Investigation Plan (PIP) requirements under EMA and Pediatric Study Plan (PSP) under FDA PREA/BPCA, and specific ICF/assent requirements.']
  masking_type varchar [note: 'Level of blinding applied to the study to minimize bias. Specifies which parties (participant, investigator, outcomes assessor, data analyst) are blinded to treatment assignment. Critical for RTSM/IRT configuration and unblinding procedures.']
  number_of_arms varchar [note: 'Total count of treatment arms (groups) defined in the protocol. Drives RTSM randomization configuration, eCRF arm assignment, and statistical analysis plan (SAP) structure. Must be consistent with the intervention model.']
  planned_duration_months varchar [note: 'Total planned duration of the study in months from first subject first visit (FSFV) to last subject last visit (LSLV) or database lock (DBL), as specified in the protocol. Used for timeline planning, resource forecasting, and milestone tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Anticipated date of last subject last visit (LSLV) or database lock (DBL) as defined in the protocol and project plan. Used for resource planning, regulatory submission timelines, and study close-out activities.']
  planned_sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects planned for enrollment across all study sites and arms as specified in the protocol. Derived from the statistical power calculation in the SAP. Used for site feasibility, patient recruitment planning, and IP supply forecasting.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Anticipated date of first subject first visit (FSFV) or study initiation as defined in the protocol and project plan. Used for milestone tracking, site activation planning, and regulatory submission timelines in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  primary_endpoint varchar [note: 'The principal outcome measure used to evaluate the primary objective of the study. Defines the variable, measurement method, timepoint, and population. Drives eCRF design in Medidata Rave and the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  primary_objective varchar [note: 'Formal statement of the primary scientific objective of the study as defined in the protocol. Describes the main question the study is designed to answer. Directly linked to the primary endpoint and powers the sample size calculation.']
  primary_purpose varchar [note: 'The primary reason for conducting the clinical study. Determines regulatory pathway, endpoint requirements, and IRB/IEC review criteria. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: treatment|prevention|diagnostic|supportive_care|screening|health_services_research|basic_science|device_feasibility — promote to reference product]']
  protocol_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, sponsor-assigned unique alphanumeric identifier for the clinical study protocol (e.g., ABC-2024-001). Used across all regulatory submissions, site documents, and operational systems as the primary business key. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.']
  protocol_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the protocol version. \'draft\' indicates under development; \'approved\' indicates IRB/IEC and sponsor approval obtained; \'amended\' indicates a formal amendment is in effect; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'final\' indicates study completed and protocol finalized; \'retired\' indicates withdrawn. Sourced from Veeva Vault quality management workflow.']
  registry_identifier varchar [note: 'Public clinical trial registry identifier such as ClinicalTrials.gov NCT number (e.g., NCT12345678) or WHO ICTRP identifier. Required by ICMJE for publication eligibility and by FDA/EMA for transparency. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  regulatory_submission_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory authority submission for this protocol version or amendment (e.g., IND amendment to FDA, CTA variation to EMA/MHRA). Tracks compliance with regulatory notification and approval requirements. Sourced from Veeva Vault regulatory submissions module.']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or lay-language title of the clinical study used in operational communications, CTMS displays, patient-facing materials, and internal reporting. Distinct from the full regulatory title.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this protocol record was sourced. Primary sources are Oracle Clinical One (study design and protocol configuration) and Veeva Vault (eTMF and regulatory document management). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_identifier varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating operational system (Oracle Clinical One study ID or Veeva Vault document ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'ICH-defined phase of clinical development for the investigational product. Determines regulatory requirements, sample size expectations, and study design complexity. \'Not Applicable\' used for non-IND studies such as device or observational studies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Phase I|Phase I/II|Phase II|Phase II/III|Phase III|Phase IV|Not Applicable — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  study_population_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the target patient population for the study, including disease characteristics, demographic parameters, and key eligibility criteria summary. Used in IRB/IEC submissions, regulatory filings, and patient recruitment materials.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the study design indicating whether participants receive an intervention or are observed. Determines applicable regulatory framework, IRB review requirements, and reporting obligations.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full official title of the clinical study protocol as it appears on the protocol document and regulatory submissions. Used in IRB/IEC submissions, clinical trial registries, and the Clinical Study Report (CSR).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this protocol record in the source system or lakehouse. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the protocol document (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 3.1). Combined with protocol_number, uniquely identifies a specific iteration of the protocol. Incremented with each formal amendment. Sourced from Veeva Vault.']

  Note: 'Master record for the clinical study protocol including all formal amendments (version history). Captures protocol number, version, title, phase, therapeutic area, indication, investigational product, study design parameters (allocation method, intervention model, masking details, primary purpose, number of arms, adaptive design flags), primary/secondary objectives summary, study population description, planned sample size, study duration, sponsor reference, IND/CTA number, protocol status (draft, approved, amended, final), amendment history (amendment number, date, type, rationale, regulatory submission status, IRB/IEC notification, impact assessment), and GCP/ICH compliance declarations. SSOT for protocol identity, design parameters, and amendment lifecycle. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Veeva Vault.'
}

Table study.study_protocol_amendment {
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each protocol amendment record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  amendment_regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Protocol amendments are submitted as IND amendments or CTA variations filed in regulatory binders. study_protocol_amendment tracks fda_submission_date, ema_submission_date, pmda_submission_date. Linki']
  amendment_tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Each protocol amendment generates a TMF essential document (DIA TMF Zone 4). Amendment tracking and TMF completeness assessments require linking the amendment record to its filed TMF document. Inspect']
  amendment_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.version. Business justification: Each protocol amendment produces a new document version in the eTMF. Regulatory binders and IRB/IEC submissions require amendment-to-document-version traceability. Role prefix \'amendment_\' reflects th']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: A study protocol amendment is by definition an amendment TO a specific protocol. While study_protocol_amendment already has study_id → study_study, it lacks a direct FK to the protocol it amends. This']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Regulatory affairs teams must link each internal study amendment record to its corresponding regulatory submission record in the regulatory domain. This is required for amendment implementation tracki']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this protocol amendment belongs. Links the amendment to the study master record.']
  affected_sections varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or free-text enumeration of protocol section numbers and titles modified by this amendment (e.g., \'5.1 Inclusion Criteria, 8.3 Visit Schedule, 10.2 Safety Monitoring\'). Enables targeted review by site staff.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'The date on which the amendment was formally authored or finalized by the sponsor. This is the document date, distinct from submission or approval dates.']
  amendment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing all changes introduced by this amendment, including modified sections, added or removed procedures, and updated criteria. Serves as the primary change log for regulatory and audit purposes.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for the amendment within a given protocol (e.g., AM-001, AM-002). Used by sponsors, health authorities, and IRB/IEC to uniquely reference a specific amendment version. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the protocol amendment. Tracks progression from internal drafting through regulatory approval and implementation. \'Superseded\' indicates a later amendment has replaced this one.']
  amendment_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the primary purpose or scope of the amendment (e.g., \'Addition of New Safety Monitoring Criteria\'). Used in regulatory submissions and internal tracking.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment per regulatory definitions. \'Substantial\' amendments require prior health authority and ethics committee approval before implementation. \'Non-substantial\' amendments may be implemented with notification only. \'Administrative\' covers clerical corrections with no scientific impact. Aligns with EU CTR 536/2014 and FDA guidance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sponsor representative (e.g., Chief Medical Officer, Head of Regulatory Affairs) who provided final internal approval for the amendment.']
  change_category varchar [note: 'Primary category of change introduced by the amendment. Used for analytics, regulatory reporting, and impact triage. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eligibility_criteria|dosing_regimen|visit_schedule|safety_monitoring|endpoint|statistical|icf|administrative|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this amendment record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  dsmb_dmc_recommendation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment was triggered by a formal recommendation from the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC). Requires documentation of the DSMB/DMC meeting minutes.']
  edc_configuration_updated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave) has been updated to reflect the protocol changes introduced by this amendment. Critical for data integrity and eCRF alignment.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the amended protocol becomes operative at clinical sites. Sites must implement protocol changes on or after this date. May differ from approval date if a transition period is granted.']
  ema_submission_date date [note: 'Date the amendment was submitted to the European Medicines Agency (EMA) or relevant national competent authority under EU CTR 536/2014. Null if no EU sites are involved.']
  ema_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory submission status with the European Medicines Agency (EMA) or relevant EU national competent authority for this amendment.']
  fda_submission_date date [note: 'Date the amendment was submitted to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) as an IND amendment. Null if the study is not conducted under a U.S. IND.']
  fda_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory submission status with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). \'On_clinical_hold\' indicates FDA has placed a hold pending resolution of safety concerns.']
  icf_version_updated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment necessitates a new version of the Informed Consent Form (ICF). When true, a new ICF document version must be created and approved before re-consent activities begin.']
  impact_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Structured narrative summarizing the assessed impact of the amendment on study operations, subject safety, data integrity, site procedures, and timelines. Produced during the amendment review process.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'The date by which all active clinical sites must have fully implemented the amendment. Used for monitoring follow-up and escalation planning in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the lead or central IRB/IEC granted approval for the amendment. For substantial amendments, implementation cannot proceed until this approval is received.']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of this amendment. Required for GCP compliance at all participating sites.']
  irb_iec_status varchar [note: 'Current review and approval status from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). \'Approved_with_conditions\' requires sponsor response before full approval.']
  pmda_submission_date date [note: 'Date the amendment was submitted to Japan\'s Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA). Null if no Japan sites are involved.']
  pmda_submission_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory submission status with Japan\'s Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency (PMDA) for this amendment.']
  previous_protocol_version varchar [note: 'The protocol version number that was in effect immediately before this amendment was applied. Enables version chain traceability for audit and regulatory inspection purposes.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Scientific, safety, or operational justification for the amendment. Required by health authorities to assess the necessity and appropriateness of protocol changes. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Veeva Vault.']
  reconsent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enrolled subjects must provide updated Informed Consent Form (ICF) signatures due to material changes introduced by this amendment. Drives re-consent workflow in Clinpal eConsent.']
  safety_driven_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment was initiated primarily due to a safety signal, Serious Adverse Event (SAE), SUSAR, or DSMB/DMC recommendation. Safety-driven amendments may require expedited regulatory review.']
  sap_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires an update to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Changes to primary/secondary endpoints, analysis populations (ITT, PP), or statistical methods trigger SAP revision.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this amendment record was sourced (Oracle Clinical One for study design, Veeva Vault for document management, or manual entry). Supports data lineage in the lakehouse.']
  sponsor_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor\'s internal governance (e.g., medical, regulatory, and legal sign-off) formally approved the amendment prior to external submission.']
  subject_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment has a direct impact on currently enrolled subjects (e.g., changes to dosing, visit schedule, eligibility criteria, or safety monitoring). True triggers mandatory re-consent assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this amendment record in the lakehouse silver layer. Required for change data capture, audit trail, and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  veeva_vault_document_code varchar [note: 'The document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF to the amendment document. Enables direct cross-reference to the source document management system for audit trail and TMF completeness tracking.']

  Note: 'Tracks all formal amendments to an approved clinical study protocol. Records amendment number, amendment date, amendment type (substantial/non-substantial), description of changes, rationale, regulatory submission status per health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA), IRB/IEC notification status, effective date, and impact assessment on ongoing subjects. Supports the protocol lifecycle from initial approval through database lock. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Veeva Vault.'
}

Table study.study {
  study_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the clinical study record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the study_study entity. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: study_study is the top-level master entity for a clinical trial; protocol is the master record for the study protocol. Adding protocol_id FK to study_study formalizes the authoritative link between th']
  acronym varchar [note: 'The short alphanumeric acronym or abbreviated name assigned to the clinical study for operational use across CTMS, EDC, and internal communications (e.g., APEX-HF, BRIGHT-2). Facilitates quick identification in dashboards, reports, and cross-system references.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the last patient completed the last study visit (Last Patient Last Visit - LPLV). Recorded upon confirmation in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Triggers database lock preparation, CSR authoring, and TMF archival workflows.']
  actual_enrollment varchar [note: 'The actual cumulative number of subjects enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) across all study sites at the current point in time. Updated from Medidata Rave EDC enrollment data. Used for enrollment tracking KPIs and regulatory reporting.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the first patient was enrolled or the first study procedure was performed (First Patient First Visit - FPFV). Recorded upon confirmation in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for regulatory reporting, timeline variance analysis, and KPI measurement.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'The sequential count of protocol amendments issued since the original protocol approval. A value of 0 indicates the original protocol with no amendments. Increments with each approved protocol amendment. Used to track protocol evolution and regulatory submission history.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'The blinding (masking) classification applied to the clinical study indicating which parties are unaware of treatment assignment. Drives operational procedures for unblinding, DSMB/DMC oversight, and safety reporting. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Clinical One.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the study record was first created in the source system (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements, data lineage tracking, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  csr_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the Clinical Study Report (CSR) was submitted to the regulatory authority or sponsor. The CSR is the comprehensive document summarizing study methods, results, and conclusions per ICH E3 guidelines. Tracked in Veeva Vault regulatory submissions.']
  ctms_study_code varchar [note: 'The native study identifier assigned by Oracle Siebel CTMS, the system of record for this entity. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit, reconciliation, and data lineage purposes per ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_cutoff_date date [note: 'The date used as the data cutoff for interim or final analyses, defining the observation window for subject data included in the analysis dataset. Specified in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and used to generate SDTM/ADaM datasets in SAS Drug Development.']
  dbl_date date [note: 'The date on which the clinical database was formally locked following completion of all data cleaning, query resolution, and medical coding activities. Database Lock (DBL) is a critical regulatory milestone that freezes the dataset for statistical analysis. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  design_type varchar [note: 'The methodological design classification of the clinical study (e.g., Parallel Group, Crossover, Single Arm, Factorial, Adaptive). Informs statistical analysis plan design, randomization schema, and protocol complexity. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One study design configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Parallel Group|Crossover|Single Arm|Factorial|Adaptive|Basket|Umbrella|Platform — promote to reference product]']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'The regulatory development phase of the clinical study per ICH and FDA/EMA classification (Phase I through Phase IV). Drives regulatory requirements, statistical analysis plan complexity, safety reporting obligations, and resource planning. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS Protocol Phase field.']
  edc_study_code varchar [note: 'The study identifier assigned within the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave) used for clinical data collection. Enables cross-system traceability between the CTMS master record and the EDC operational system. Required for data lineage and audit trail purposes per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'The European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) registration number assigned by the EMA for clinical trials conducted in the EU/EEA (e.g., 2023-001234-56). Required for all interventional trials in EU member states under EU CTR 536/2014.']
  first_site_initiation_date date [note: 'The date on which the first investigational site was formally initiated (Site Initiation Visit completed) and authorized to enroll subjects. A key operational milestone tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS for timeline KPI reporting.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'The date of initial approval granted by the lead Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) for the study protocol. This is a prerequisite for study initiation and subject enrollment. Tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Veeva Vault.']
  is_randomized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the clinical study employs randomization to assign participants to treatment arms (True) or uses a non-randomized assignment method (False). Determines RTSM/IRT configuration requirements in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One.']
  nct_number varchar [note: 'The ClinicalTrials.gov registry identifier assigned by the U.S. National Library of Medicine upon study registration (e.g., NCT12345678). Mandatory for studies subject to FDAAA 801 and ICMJE registration requirements. Used for public disclosure and regulatory cross-referencing.']
  number_of_arms varchar [note: 'The total number of treatment arms or groups defined in the study protocol (e.g., 2 for a two-arm parallel study, 1 for a single-arm study). Drives RTSM/IRT configuration in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One randomization setup.']
  planned_country_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of countries in which the clinical study will be conducted. Drives regulatory strategy (multi-national submissions), country-level feasibility, and operational complexity assessment.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The originally planned date for last patient last visit (LPLV) or study completion as defined in the protocol and project plan. Used for resource planning, site contract management, and regulatory timeline commitments.']
  planned_site_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of investigational sites to be activated for the clinical study as defined in the feasibility assessment and study protocol. Used for site selection planning, budget forecasting, and resource allocation in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The originally planned date for first patient first visit (FPFV) or study initiation as defined in the study protocol and project plan. Used for timeline tracking, milestone management, and KPI reporting in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  primary_regulatory_region varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory jurisdiction governing the clinical study, determining the lead regulatory authority (e.g., US for FDA, EU for EMA, JP for PMDA). Drives applicable GCP guidelines, submission requirements, and safety reporting timelines.']
  sponsor_study_number varchar [note: 'The sponsor-assigned unique alphanumeric identifier for the clinical study (e.g., ABC-1234-001). This is the primary business identifier used across all sponsor systems, regulatory submissions, and inter-organizational communications. Corresponds to the Protocol Number in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  study_status varchar [note: 'The current operational lifecycle status of the clinical study. Drives workflow gating, resource allocation, regulatory reporting obligations, and system access controls across CTMS, EDC, and eTMF. This is the authoritative status field sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS. Transitions are tracked in the study status history product.']
  study_type varchar [note: 'The fundamental classification of the clinical study design indicating whether participants receive an investigational intervention (Interventional), are observed without intervention (Observational), or receive investigational product outside a trial (Expanded Access). Determines applicable regulatory frameworks and GCP requirements.']
  target_enrollment varchar [note: 'The planned total number of subjects to be enrolled across all study sites as specified in the approved protocol and statistical analysis plan (SAP). Drives site allocation, recruitment planning, and power calculations. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for early termination of the clinical study when study_status is Terminated. Captures the primary rationale (e.g., Safety Concerns, Lack of Efficacy, Business Decision, Regulatory Hold, Enrollment Failure). Required for regulatory notifications and final CSR. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Safety Concerns|Lack of Efficacy|Business Decision|Regulatory Hold|Enrollment Failure|Sponsor Decision|Other — promote to reference product]']
  title varchar [note: 'The complete official title of the clinical study as stated in the approved protocol document. Used in regulatory submissions, IRB/IEC applications, informed consent forms, and the Trial Master File. Corresponds to the Protocol Title field in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  tmf_archival_date date [note: 'The date on which the Trial Master File (TMF) was formally archived following study close-out. The TMF must be retained for a minimum of 15 years (or longer per regional requirements) after study completion. Tracked in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the study record was most recently modified in the source system (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Top-level master entity representing a clinical trial as a business and operational unit. Captures the unique study identifier (sponsor study number, ClinicalTrials.gov NCT number, EudraCT number), study title, acronym, therapeutic area, indication, development phase, study type (interventional/observational), blinding type, randomization flag, study status with full lifecycle history (planned, startup, active, on-hold, completed, terminated), planned and actual start/end dates, target enrollment, closure details (DBL date, CSR submission, TMF archival), sponsor reference, and CRO assignment. SSOT for study identity and lifecycle state across all domains. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

Table study.arm {
  arm_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each study arm record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the study_arm data product.']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'FK to study.investigational_product']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this arm belongs. Links the arm to its governing study protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Treatment arms can be added, modified, or removed by protocol amendments (e.g., adding a new dose arm in a dose-escalation study). Linking arm to study_protocol_amendment enables full amendment tracea']
  adaptive_design_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this arm is part of an adaptive trial design where arm allocation, sample size, or continuation may be modified based on interim analysis results per a pre-specified adaptation rule.']
  arm_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the arm within the study. Maps directly to the ARMCD variable in the CDISC SDTM ARM domain and is used in randomization and data tabulation.']
  arm_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the study arm as defined in the protocol (e.g., \'Drug A 10mg QD\', \'Placebo\'). Maps to the ARM variable in the CDISC SDTM ARM domain.']
  arm_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the study arm, reflecting whether the arm is planned, actively enrolling, suspended, closed to enrollment, or discontinued per protocol amendment.']
  arm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the arm per ClinicalTrials.gov and CDISC controlled terminology, indicating the nature of the intervention assigned to subjects in this arm. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Experimental|Active Comparator|Placebo Comparator|Sham Comparator|No Intervention|Other — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Specifies the level of blinding applied to this arm: Open Label (no blinding), Single Blind (subject blinded), Double Blind (subject and investigator blinded), or Triple Blind (subject, investigator, and sponsor blinded).']
  clinical_one_arm_code varchar [note: 'The native arm identifier assigned by Oracle Clinical One (the source system of record for study design and patient randomization). Used for system-of-record traceability and data lineage from source to lakehouse.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this study arm record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctms_arm_reference varchar [note: 'The arm reference identifier as recorded in Oracle Siebel CTMS for site monitoring, visit tracking, and enrollment management. Supports cross-system reconciliation.']
  discontinuation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early discontinuation of the study arm, if applicable (e.g., safety signal, futility, sponsor decision, regulatory hold). Required for regulatory reporting when an arm is terminated prior to planned completion.']
  dose_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the dose level and regimen for the investigational product in this arm (e.g., \'10 mg once daily\', \'200 mg BID\'). Supports dose-response analysis and protocol compliance monitoring.']
  dosing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of investigational product administration for subjects in this arm (e.g., QD - once daily, BID - twice daily, QW - once weekly). Aligns with CDISC SDTM EX domain EXDOSFRQ variable.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the study arm was closed to enrollment or treatment, either per protocol schedule or due to early termination. Null if the arm is still active.']
  interim_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this arm is subject to one or more pre-specified interim analyses by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) during the study.']
  intervention_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the intervention assigned to subjects in this arm, including drug name, dose, route of administration, and dosing schedule as specified in the study protocol.']
  intervention_type varchar [note: 'Category of the therapeutic intervention assigned to subjects in this arm. Aligns with ClinicalTrials.gov intervention type classifications and supports regulatory submission categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Drug|Biologic|Device|Procedure|Behavioral|Dietary Supplement|Radiation|Genetic|Combination|Other — promote to reference product]']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment assignment for this arm is blinded to subjects, investigators, or both (double-blind). Supports blinding integrity tracking and unblinding event management.']
  is_control_arm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this arm serves as the control arm (comparator) in the study design. True for placebo, active comparator, or sham comparator arms used as the reference group in statistical analyses.']
  itt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subjects enrolled in this arm are included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  max_subjects_count varchar [note: 'The maximum allowable number of subjects that may be enrolled in this arm, including any over-enrollment buffer defined in the protocol. Distinct from planned_subjects_count which reflects the statistical target.']
  min_subjects_count varchar [note: 'The minimum number of subjects required in this arm to maintain statistical validity of the study, as defined in the SAP. Used for futility analysis and early stopping rule evaluation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, clarifications, or protocol-specific instructions related to this arm that are not captured in structured fields. For internal use by study team.']
  planned_subjects_count varchar [note: 'The planned (target) number of subjects to be enrolled and randomized into this arm as specified in the study protocol and statistical analysis plan (SAP).']
  pp_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subjects enrolled in this arm are included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Subjects with major protocol deviations may be excluded.']
  randomization_ratio varchar [note: 'The allocation ratio for randomization of subjects into this arm relative to other arms (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1:1\'). Used by the RTSM/IRT system (Medidata Rave RTSM) to control subject assignment.']
  randomization_ratio_numeric decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric representation of the allocation weight for this arm relative to total allocation (e.g., 0.50 for a 1:1 ratio arm). Supports quantitative analysis and reporting of enrollment balance.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the investigational product is administered to subjects in this arm (e.g., Oral, Intravenous, Subcutaneous, Intramuscular). Aligns with CDISC SDTM EX domain EXROUTE variable and WHO-DD terminology.']
  safety_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subjects enrolled in this arm are included in the safety analysis population (all subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment), as defined in the SAP.']
  sdtm_arm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain identifier confirming this arm maps to the ARM domain in the SDTM submission dataset. Always \'ARM\' per CDISC standard; included for regulatory submission traceability.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number defining the display and processing order of arms within the study. Used for consistent ordering in reports, eCRFs, and SDTM datasets.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the study arm became active and eligible for subject enrollment, as defined in the approved protocol or protocol amendment. Supports enrollment timeline tracking.']
  treatment_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of treatment for subjects in this arm, expressed in days. Used for supply planning, visit schedule design, and protocol compliance monitoring.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this study arm record. Required for audit trail compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable) principle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this study arm record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or system identifier of the user or process that created this study arm record. Required for audit trail compliance under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable) principle.']

  Note: 'Defines each treatment arm or cohort within a clinical study. Records arm name, arm code, arm type (experimental, active comparator, placebo comparator, sham comparator, no intervention, other), description of intervention, planned number of subjects per arm, randomization ratio, arm status, and CDISC SDTM ARM domain mapping. Supports multi-arm and adaptive trial designs. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.'
}

Table study.endpoint {
  endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a study endpoint record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the study_endpoint data product.']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: Safety endpoints reference specific CTCAE grade thresholds (study_endpoint has ctcae_version and ctcae_grade as plain attributes). Linking to ctcae_grade supports SAP authoring, SDTM CTCAE domain mapp']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: study_endpoint has meddra_soc_code as a plain attribute — safety endpoints are defined using MedDRA terminology. Linking to meddra_term supports SDTM FATEST/CETERM mapping, signal detection alignment,']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this endpoint belongs. Links the endpoint to its governing protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Endpoints are formally defined in the protocol and can be modified by amendments (e.g., changing a secondary endpoint to primary, adding exploratory endpoints). Linking endpoint to study_protocol_amen']
  adam_parameter_code varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM PARAMCD value (≤8 characters, uppercase) that uniquely identifies the analysis parameter corresponding to this endpoint within the ADaM dataset. Required for CDISC-compliant regulatory submissions to FDA and EMA.']
  adam_parameter_name varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM PARAM value — the full descriptive label for the analysis parameter corresponding to this endpoint (e.g., \'Overall Survival Time (Days)\'). Displayed in analysis output tables and listings.']
  alpha_level decimal(10,6) [note: 'The pre-specified Type I error rate (significance level) for this endpoint (e.g., 0.05 for two-sided, 0.025 for one-sided). Defined in the SAP and referenced in the protocol.']
  analysis_population varchar [note: 'The analysis population to which this endpoint applies: Intent-to-Treat (ITT), modified ITT (mITT), Per Protocol (PP), safety, Pharmacokinetic (PK), or biomarker population. Defined in the SAP and referenced in the Clinical Study Report (CSR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ITT|mITT|PP|safety|PK|biomarker — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_timepoint varchar [note: 'The protocol-defined timepoint or window at which the endpoint is assessed (e.g., \'Week 24\', \'End of Treatment\', \'Month 6 ± 7 days\'). Directly maps to the visit schedule and SDTM VISITNUM.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this endpoint record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  ctcae_version varchar [note: 'The version of the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) used to grade safety endpoints (e.g., \'5.0\', \'4.03\'). Applicable for oncology and safety-focused endpoints.']
  directionality varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the statistical test for this endpoint is one-sided or two-sided. Determines the alpha allocation and critical value used in hypothesis testing.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this endpoint definition became effective, typically aligned with the protocol version approval date. Supports temporal versioning across protocol amendments.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'The date on which this endpoint definition was superseded or retired, typically aligned with a subsequent protocol amendment. Null for currently active endpoints. Supports temporal versioning.']
  endpoint_category varchar [note: 'Scientific domain category of the endpoint: efficacy (treatment effect), safety (harm characterisation), tolerability, Pharmacokinetics/Pharmacodynamics (PK/PD), quality of life (patient-reported outcomes), or biomarker. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|safety|tolerability|PK_PD|quality_of_life|biomarker — promote to reference product]']
  endpoint_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this endpoint within the study (e.g., EP-PRI01, EP-SEC03). Used as the externally-known business identifier in SAP cross-references and regulatory submissions.']
  endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the endpoint as defined in the study protocol, including the precise measurement, population, and timeframe. Directly referenced in regulatory submissions.']
  endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable name of the endpoint as it appears in the protocol and Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) (e.g., \'Overall Survival\', \'Change from Baseline in HbA1c at Week 24\').']
  endpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the endpoint definition: draft (under authoring), active (protocol-approved), amended (modified via protocol amendment), retired (removed from protocol), locked (post-Database Lock). Drives SAP version control.']
  endpoint_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the endpoint role within the study hierarchy: primary (confirmatory, drives sample size), secondary (supportive), exploratory, safety, Pharmacokinetic (PK), Pharmacodynamic (PD), or biomarker. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|secondary|exploratory|safety|PK|PD|biomarker — promote to reference product]']
  estimand_intercurrent_event_strategy varchar [note: 'The strategy for handling intercurrent events per ICH E9(R1): treatment_policy (ignore occurrence), hypothetical (counterfactual), composite (incorporate into outcome), while_on_treatment (restrict to on-treatment period), or principal_stratum (condition on intercurrent event status).']
  estimand_population varchar [note: 'The target population component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand, specifying the patient population to which the treatment effect applies (e.g., \'all randomised patients with baseline HbA1c ≥ 7.5%\').']
  estimand_population_level_summary varchar [note: 'The population-level summary measure component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand (e.g., \'difference in means\', \'hazard ratio\', \'odds ratio\'). Specifies how individual outcomes are aggregated to a treatment effect estimate.']
  estimand_treatment_condition varchar [note: 'The treatment condition component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework, describing the treatment regimen of interest (e.g., \'randomised treatment regardless of adherence\', \'treatment as prescribed\').']
  estimand_variable varchar [note: 'The variable component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand, defining the outcome measure for each patient (e.g., \'change from baseline in HbA1c at Week 24\'). Directly corresponds to the ADaM analysis variable.']
  measurement_instrument varchar [note: 'The validated instrument, scale, or tool used to measure the endpoint (e.g., RECIST 1.1, HAMD-17, SF-36, CTCAE v5.0, EORTC QLQ-C30). Required for regulatory submission and biostatistical analysis planning.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code associated with this endpoint when the endpoint relates to an adverse event or safety outcome. Enables standardised safety reporting per ICH M1.']
  missing_data_strategy varchar [note: 'The pre-specified strategy for handling missing data for this endpoint per the SAP: Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF), Baseline Observation Carried Forward (BOCF), Multiple Imputation (MI), Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM), observed cases only, worst-case, or best-case imputation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LOCF|BOCF|MI|MMRM|observed_cases|worst_case|best_case — promote to reference product]']
  multiplicity_adjustment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a multiplicity adjustment (e.g., Bonferroni, Holm, Hochberg, gatekeeping procedure) is applied to this endpoint to control the family-wise error rate. Critical for confirmatory trial design and regulatory acceptance.']
  multiplicity_procedure varchar [note: 'The specific statistical procedure used for multiplicity adjustment for this endpoint (e.g., \'Hochberg step-up\', \'Bonferroni\', \'Fixed-sequence testing\', \'Graphical gatekeeping\'). Populated only when multiplicity_adjustment_flag is True.']
  non_inferiority_margin decimal(18,6) [note: 'The pre-specified non-inferiority or equivalence margin (delta) for the endpoint, expressed in the unit of the primary outcome measure. Applicable only when statistical_hypothesis is non_inferiority or equivalence. Regulatory-critical value.']
  objective_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study objective that this endpoint operationalises, enabling objective-to-endpoint traceability required by the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'The section number or heading in the study protocol document where this endpoint is formally defined (e.g., \'Section 3.2.1 Primary Endpoint\'). Enables direct cross-reference to the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'The section number or heading in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) where the analysis method for this endpoint is specified. Provides the scientific rationale chain from objective through endpoint to SAP.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain from which the source data for this endpoint is collected (e.g., AE, LB, EX, RS, TU, TR). Establishes the SDTM-to-ADaM data lineage chain required for regulatory submission.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this endpoint within its objective type group (e.g., 1st primary, 2nd secondary). Used for hierarchical ordering in the SAP and regulatory submission tables.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this endpoint definition originates: Medidata Rave (EDC), Oracle Clinical One (study design), SAS Drug Development (biostatistical analysis), or manual entry. Supports data lineage and audit.']
  source_system_endpoint_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this endpoint record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One endpoint object ID, SAS Drug Development analysis parameter ID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and ETL lineage.']
  statistical_hypothesis varchar [note: 'The formal statistical hypothesis framework applied to this endpoint: superiority (treatment better than control), non-inferiority (treatment not worse by a defined margin), equivalence (treatment within a symmetric margin), or descriptive (no formal hypothesis). Drives SAP design.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the endpoint outcome is expressed (e.g., \'months\', \'mg/dL\', \'mmHg\', \'score\', \'%\', \'events/patient-year\'). Required for ADaM AVALU mapping and regulatory submission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this endpoint record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and change control requirements.']

  Note: 'Defines the formal objectives and their associated endpoints (primary, secondary, exploratory) for a clinical study. Captures objective text, objective type, endpoint name, endpoint type (primary, secondary, exploratory, safety, PK, PD, biomarker), endpoint category (efficacy, safety, tolerability, PK/PD, quality of life), measurement instrument or scale, timepoint of assessment, statistical hypothesis (superiority, non-inferiority, equivalence), estimand definition per ICH E9(R1), objective-to-endpoint traceability, and CDISC ADaM parameter mapping. Provides the scientific rationale chain from objective through endpoint to SAP. Directly referenced by the biostatistics domain for analysis planning.'
}

Table study.eligibility_criterion {
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each eligibility criterion record in the clinical study. Primary key for the eligibility_criterion data product.']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: eligibility_criterion has meddra_pt_code and meddra_pt_name as plain attributes — inclusion/exclusion criteria referencing medical conditions are MedDRA-coded for SDTM IE domain mapping and regulatory']
  previous_criterion_eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior version of this criterion that was superseded by the current record. Enables full version history chain for audit trail and regulatory traceability of criterion evolution across protocol amendments.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version under which this criterion was introduced or last modified. Supports amendment-driven criterion tracking.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this eligibility criterion is defined. Links the criterion to its parent study protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: eligibility_criterion has amendment_number as a STRING and is_amendment_driven BOOLEAN flag but no FK to study_protocol_amendment. Eligibility criteria are frequently introduced or modified by protoco']
  applicable_population varchar [note: 'Specifies the sub-population to which this criterion applies when it is not universally applicable to all subjects (e.g., reproductive status criteria apply to FEMALE_ONLY, pediatric dosing criteria apply to PEDIATRIC). Supports conditional screening logic.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'The documented scientific or operational rationale for introducing or modifying this criterion, particularly when driven by a protocol amendment. Required for regulatory submissions and IRB/IEC amendment packages.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this eligibility criterion record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  crf_page_reference varchar [note: 'The page or section reference in the electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) where this eligibility criterion is assessed and documented. Used for site training, monitoring, and Source Data Verification (SDV) activities.']
  criterion_category varchar [note: 'Clinical or operational category classifying the nature of the criterion. Supports structured analysis and site training. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEMOGRAPHIC|DIAGNOSIS|LAB_VALUE|PRIOR_THERAPY|CONCOMITANT_MEDICATION|REPRODUCTIVE_STATUS|WASHOUT_PERIOD|CONSENT|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  criterion_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the criterion within its type (inclusion or exclusion) as it appears in the study protocol. Used for ordering and referencing in site screening checklists and EDC eligibility modules.']
  criterion_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or condensed label for the criterion used in EDC display, site training materials, and operational screening tools where full text is impractical. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE domain variable IETESTCD.']
  criterion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eligibility criterion. DRAFT indicates under review; ACTIVE indicates approved and in use; SUPERSEDED indicates replaced by an amended version; RETIRED indicates removed from the protocol.']
  criterion_text varchar [note: 'Full verbatim text of the eligibility criterion as written in the approved study protocol. This is the authoritative, protocol-exact wording used in site screening checklists, EDC eligibility modules, and regulatory submissions. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE domain variable IETEST.']
  criterion_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the criterion is an inclusion criterion (subject must meet) or an exclusion criterion (subject must not meet) for enrollment eligibility. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE domain variable IECAT.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'The display sequence order for this criterion within its type group (inclusion or exclusion) as it should appear in site screening checklists, EDC eligibility modules, and protocol documents. May differ from criterion_number when criteria are reordered by amendment.']
  edc_field_oid varchar [note: 'The Object Identifier (OID) of the corresponding field in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave) eligibility module. Enables direct mapping between the protocol criterion and the EDC data collection form for data lineage and validation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this criterion version became operationally effective for subject screening. Aligns with the IRB/IEC approval date of the applicable protocol version.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a change to this criterion requires formal IRB/IEC approval before implementation at sites. Typically True for all criteria changes driven by protocol amendments.']
  is_amendment_driven boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this criterion was introduced or modified as a result of a protocol amendment. True when the criterion originated from or was changed by a formal protocol amendment. Critical for regulatory audit trails and amendment impact analysis.']
  is_critical_criterion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this criterion is designated as critical, meaning a violation would constitute a major protocol deviation requiring immediate reporting to the sponsor and potentially the IRB/IEC. Critical criteria are typically safety-related or have significant impact on data integrity.']
  lab_test_code varchar [note: 'The standardized laboratory test code (e.g., LOINC code or sponsor-defined code) for the lab parameter referenced in this criterion. Applicable for lab value criteria such as eGFR, hemoglobin, or platelet count thresholds.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code associated with the diagnosis, condition, or medical history referenced in this criterion. Enables standardized medical coding for regulatory submissions and cross-study analysis.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this eligibility criterion record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  numeric_threshold_comparator varchar [note: 'The logical comparator defining the relationship between the subject\'s measured value and the numeric threshold (EQ=equal, NE=not equal, LT=less than, LE=less than or equal, GT=greater than, GE=greater than or equal). Used in conjunction with numeric_threshold_value.']
  numeric_threshold_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the numeric threshold value (e.g., years, mL/min/1.73m², kg/m², mg/dL). Follows CDISC controlled terminology for units.']
  numeric_threshold_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantitative threshold value associated with the criterion where applicable (e.g., age ≥ 18, eGFR ≥ 60, BMI ≤ 35). Used for lab value, vital sign, and demographic criteria that have a numeric boundary.']
  protocol_version_first_applicable varchar [note: 'The protocol version number (e.g., v1.0, v2.1) in which this criterion first became applicable. Used to determine which subjects enrolled under which protocol version were subject to this criterion.']
  protocol_version_last_applicable varchar [note: 'The protocol version number in which this criterion was last applicable before being superseded or retired. Null if the criterion is still active in the current protocol version.']
  requires_source_doc_verification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) is required for this criterion during monitoring visits. When True, the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) must verify the criterion assessment against source documents at the site.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which this criterion was retired or superseded. Null if the criterion remains active. Used to determine the applicable criterion set for subjects screened on a given date.']
  screening_guidance_notes varchar [note: 'Operational guidance notes for site staff on how to assess and document this criterion during subject screening. May include clarifications, assessment methods, source document requirements, and common pitfalls. Sourced from the study\'s screening manual or site training materials.']
  sdtm_ie_testcd varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM-compliant short code for the criterion, conforming to the IE domain IETESTCD variable. Maximum 8 uppercase alphanumeric characters. Used for regulatory submission datasets and CDISC compliance validation.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of source document required to verify this criterion (e.g., medical records, laboratory report, imaging report, physician attestation, birth certificate). Used to guide site staff and CRAs during screening and monitoring.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this eligibility criterion record originated (e.g., Oracle Clinical One for study design, Medidata Rave for EDC eligibility module). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_criterion_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this criterion record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One criterion object ID, Medidata Rave field OID). Enables reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the source system.']
  time_window_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of time for the time window associated with the criterion (e.g., DAYS, WEEKS, MONTHS, YEARS). Used for washout periods, prior therapy windows, and diagnosis recency requirements.']
  time_window_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The numeric duration of the time window associated with the criterion (e.g., prior therapy washout period of 4 weeks, prior diagnosis within 6 months). Used in conjunction with time_window_unit.']
  waiver_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation waiver or exception can be granted for this criterion on a per-subject basis. When True, a formal waiver request process is required and must be documented in the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  waiver_authority varchar [note: 'The body or role authorized to grant a waiver for this criterion (e.g., Medical Monitor, Sponsor Medical Director, IRB/IEC). Applicable only when waiver_allowed is True.']
  who_dd_drug_code varchar [note: 'The WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) code for the medication referenced in this criterion, applicable for prior therapy or concomitant medication criteria. Enables standardized drug coding for regulatory submissions.']

  Note: 'Defines inclusion and exclusion criteria for subject enrollment in a clinical study. Records criterion number, criterion type (inclusion/exclusion), criterion text, criterion category (demographic, diagnosis, lab value, prior therapy, concomitant medication, reproductive status, other), protocol version applicability, amendment-driven change flag, CDISC SDTM IE domain mapping, and operational screening guidance notes. Authoritative source for site screening checklists and EDC eligibility modules.'
}

Table study.study_visit_schedule {
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each study visit schedule record in the lakehouse. Primary key for the study_visit_schedule data product.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: In many clinical trials, particularly adaptive designs and dose-escalation studies, visit schedules are arm-specific — different treatment arms may have different visit frequencies, PK sampling visits']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: study_visit_schedule has epoch_name as a STRING but no FK to study_epoch. Visits in the Schedule of Events belong to specific study epochs (Screening, Treatment, Follow-up) — this FK formalizes that s']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version or amendment under which this visit schedule was defined. Enables tracking of schedule changes across protocol amendments.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study for which this visit schedule is defined. Links the Schedule of Events (SoE) to its governing study protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: study_visit_schedule has amendment_number as a STRING but no FK to study_protocol_amendment. Visit schedules (Schedule of Events) are versioned by protocol amendments — this FK formalizes that traceab']
  cdisc_visit_reference varchar [note: 'Standardized CDISC controlled terminology code or reference for the visit type, used to align the visit schedule with CDISC SDTM submission datasets and regulatory data packages (e.g., CDISC CT VISIT term).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this visit schedule record was first created in the lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the visit schedule was superseded or retired, typically when a protocol amendment introduced a new schedule version. Null for the currently active schedule version.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the visit schedule became effective for the study, typically aligned with protocol approval or amendment effective date. Supports temporal versioning of the Schedule of Events.']
  fasting_duration_hours varchar [note: 'Minimum number of hours the subject must fast prior to the visit, when fasting_required is True. Specified in the protocol and communicated to sites and subjects.']
  fasting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject must be fasting prior to this visit (e.g., for fasting lipid panels, glucose measurements, or PK sampling). Communicated to subjects via informed consent and visit reminder materials.']
  is_dosing_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether investigational product (IP) administration or dosing occurs at this visit. Used by RTSM/IRT systems for drug supply planning, IP accountability tracking, and site operational scheduling.']
  is_in_itt_population boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data collected at this visit contributes to the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Used by biostatisticians to configure ADaM datasets and analysis flags.']
  is_in_pp_population boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data collected at this visit contributes to the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Supports biostatistical analysis dataset configuration.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the procedure is mandatory (True) or optional (False) at this visit per the protocol Schedule of Events. Mandatory procedures are required for protocol compliance; optional procedures may be performed at investigator discretion. Used in data completeness checks and query generation in EDC.']
  is_pk_sampling_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Pharmacokinetics (PK) blood or urine sampling is required at this visit. Used by DMPK teams, central labs, and RTSM/IRT systems to plan sample collection kits and logistics.']
  is_primary_endpoint_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this visit is a primary endpoint assessment visit as defined in the study protocol. Flags visits where the primary efficacy or safety endpoint data is collected, critical for data completeness monitoring and regulatory submission.']
  is_randomization_visit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subject randomization occurs at this visit via the RTSM/IRT system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One, Medidata Rave RTSM). Critical for enrollment tracking, DSMB reporting, and regulatory milestone monitoring.']
  is_unscheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this visit is an unscheduled visit (True) that occurs outside the planned Schedule of Events, typically triggered by a safety event, adverse event follow-up, or investigator decision. Unscheduled visits are tracked separately in CTMS and EDC.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or footnotes associated with this visit or procedure as specified in the protocol Schedule of Events (e.g., \'Perform only if subject is female of childbearing potential\', \'Collect within 30 minutes of dosing\'). Captures protocol-specific instructions for site staff.']
  planned_study_day varchar [note: 'The planned study day on which this visit is scheduled to occur, relative to the study reference start date (Day 1 = first dose or randomization). Negative values indicate pre-treatment visits (e.g., Day -14 for screening). Aligns with CDISC SDTM VISITDY variable.']
  planned_study_week varchar [note: 'The planned study week on which this visit is scheduled, derived from planned_study_day for reporting convenience. Used in study timelines, subject visit planning, and site management dashboards.']
  procedure_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the clinical procedure or assessment. Drives CDISC SDTM domain mapping, EDC form routing, and operational planning for site staff. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vital_signs|laboratory|ecg|imaging|dosing|pk_sampling|physical_exam|questionnaire|biomarker|concomitant_medication|adverse_event|other — promote to reference product]']
  procedure_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific clinical procedure or assessment required at this visit (e.g., Vital Signs, 12-Lead ECG, Hematology Lab Panel, PK Blood Draw, Physical Examination, Patient-Reported Outcome Questionnaire, Study Drug Dosing). Each visit-procedure combination is a distinct record.']
  procedure_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordering sequence of the procedure within a visit, defining the recommended order of assessments (e.g., vital signs before blood draw, ECG before dosing). Used for site operational workflow and EDC form ordering.']
  requires_central_lab boolean [note: 'Indicates whether samples collected at this visit-procedure must be processed by the central laboratory (True) rather than a local laboratory. Drives sample kit logistics, central lab requisition, and supply chain planning.']
  requires_local_lab boolean [note: 'Indicates whether samples collected at this visit-procedure are to be processed by the site local laboratory. Used for safety lab monitoring where rapid turnaround is required (e.g., pre-dose safety labs).']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this Schedule of Events (SoE), aligned with the protocol amendment version (e.g., v1.0, v2.0, Amendment 1). Tracks iterative changes to the visit schedule over the study lifecycle.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The two-letter CDISC Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) domain code to which data collected at this visit-procedure maps (e.g., VS for Vital Signs, LB for Laboratory, EG for ECG, EX for Exposure/Dosing, PC for Pharmacokinetics, QS for Questionnaires, PE for Physical Examination). Critical for regulatory submission data package preparation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this visit schedule record was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Clinical One). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across systems in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_visit_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this visit record in the originating operational system (Medidata Rave or Oracle Clinical One). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability per ALCOA+ principles.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this visit schedule record was last modified in the lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  visit_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated duration of the visit in minutes as defined in the protocol or site operational plan. Used for site scheduling, resource planning, and subject burden assessment.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the clinical visit as defined in the protocol Schedule of Events (e.g., Screening, Baseline, Week 4, End of Treatment, Follow-Up 1). Aligns with CDISC SDTM VISIT variable.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the visit within the study schedule (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Used for ordering visits and aligns with CDISC SDTM VISITNUM variable. Unscheduled visits may use decimal values represented as separate records.']
  visit_short_label varchar [note: 'Abbreviated label for the visit used in EDC (Electronic Data Capture) systems, reports, and data listings (e.g., SCR, BL, W04, EOT, FU1). Configured in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One for eCRF navigation.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this visit schedule record. Draft indicates under development; active indicates approved and in use; amended indicates modified by a protocol amendment; superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; retired indicates no longer applicable.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the visit by its functional role in the study timeline. Drives site operational planning, subject visit tracking in CTMS, and CDISC SDTM epoch mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screening|baseline|treatment|follow_up|end_of_study|unscheduled|early_termination|safety — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  window_after_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the planned study day that the visit may occur and still be considered protocol-compliant (late visit tolerance). Used by sites and CTMS to flag out-of-window visits. Aligns with CDISC SDTM TVENRL variable.']
  window_before_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the planned study day that the visit may occur and still be considered protocol-compliant (early visit tolerance). Used by sites and CTMS to flag out-of-window visits. Aligns with CDISC SDTM TVSTRL variable.']

  Note: 'Defines the planned Schedule of Events (SoE) for a clinical study including all visits and their required procedures. Captures visit name, visit number, visit type (screening, baseline, treatment, follow-up, unscheduled, end-of-study), planned study day/week, visit window tolerances, epoch association, and for each visit the specific procedures/assessments required (vital signs, lab draws, ECG, imaging, questionnaires, dosing, PK sampling, physical exam) with mandatory/optional flags, CDISC SDTM domain mappings, and eCRF form references. This is the master template for site operations, subject visit planning, and EDC configuration. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave.'
}

Table study.visit_procedure {
  visit_procedure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each visit procedure record in the Schedule of Events matrix. Primary key for the visit_procedure data product.']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: visit_procedure.ctcae_grading_applicable indicates safety assessments requiring CTCAE grading. Linking to the specific ctcae_grade definition supports EDC configuration, SDV requirement setting, and S']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: EDC build validation and SDV planning: each protocol-defined procedure specifies the eCRF form used to collect its data. This link enables automated verification that all protocol-required forms are b']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: Visit procedures that require laboratory work reference a specific lab panel. Linking visit_procedure to lab_panel enables automated lab kit assignment, SDTM LB domain mapping from the protocol schedu']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version or amendment under which this visit procedure was defined. Enables tracking of procedure changes across protocol amendments.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) to which this visit procedure belongs. Links the procedure to its parent study context.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: visit_procedure has amendment_number as a STRING but no FK to study_protocol_amendment.
