Clinical procedures at visits can be added or modified by protocol amendments — this FK formalizes that traceabi']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled visit (study visit) at which this procedure is to be performed. Establishes the visit-procedure association in the Schedule of Events.']
  adam_dataset varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM dataset name to which this procedure\'s data contributes for statistical analysis (e.g., ADLB, ADVS, ADEG, ADPC). Used by SAS Drug Development for biostatistical analysis and CSR generation.']
  cdash_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC CDASH domain code that defines the data collection standard for this procedure\'s eCRF (e.g., VS, LB, EG, PE). Ensures the eCRF design in Medidata Rave aligns with CDASH standards for downstream SDTM mapping.']
  central_lab_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specimens collected by this procedure must be processed by the sponsor-designated central laboratory (as opposed to a local site laboratory). Drives sample kit supply chain and central lab requisition workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this visit procedure record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctcae_grading_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CTCAE grading criteria apply to results collected by this procedure (e.g., laboratory abnormalities graded per CTCAE v5.0). Drives safety data review workflows and adverse event reporting linkage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this procedure definition was retired or superseded by a protocol amendment. Null for currently active procedures. Used to determine which procedure version applies to a given subject visit date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this procedure definition became effective in the study protocol. For amended procedures, reflects the date the amendment was approved and implemented at sites.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of clinical equipment or device required to perform this procedure at the site (e.g., \'Holter Monitor\', \'Spirometer\', \'Dual-Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DXA) Scanner\', \'Electrocardiograph\'). Used for site feasibility assessment and equipment qualification tracking.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes required to complete this procedure at the site. Used for visit duration planning, site burden assessment, and subject scheduling optimization.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procedure is required (mandatory) or optional at the associated visit per the study protocol. Mandatory procedures must be completed for the visit to be considered evaluable; optional procedures may be performed at investigator discretion.']
  is_pk_assessment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procedure involves pharmacokinetic (PK) sample collection or assessment. PK procedures require precise timing relative to dosing and specialized sample handling per the bioanalytical plan.']
  is_primary_endpoint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procedure collects data that directly contributes to the study\'s primary efficacy or safety endpoint. Used to prioritize monitoring, SDV, and data cleaning activities.']
  is_safety_assessment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procedure is designated as a safety assessment per the protocol (e.g., safety labs, ECG for QTc monitoring, vital signs for blood pressure monitoring). Drives safety data review workflows and DSMB/DMC reporting.']
  local_lab_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether local site laboratory processing is permitted for this procedure (e.g., for safety monitoring labs that require rapid turnaround). May coexist with central_lab_required for procedures requiring both central and local processing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for protocol-specific instructions, special handling requirements, or clarifications related to this procedure that are not captured in structured fields (e.g., \'Collect sample within 2 hours of dosing\', \'Perform in triplicate\').']
  oracle_clinical_one_task_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding task or activity in Oracle Clinical One study design module. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse visit procedure record and the Oracle Clinical One study build configuration.']
  procedure_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the clinical procedure type used to group procedures in the Schedule of Events matrix and CDISC SDTM domain mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vital_signs|lab_draw|ecg|imaging|questionnaire|dosing|pk_sampling|physical_exam|biopsy|ecog_assessment|other — promote to reference product]']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the procedure within the study protocol (e.g., VITALS-01, LBDRAW-02, ECG-01). Used as the business identifier for the procedure in the Schedule of Events matrix and eCRF mapping.']
  procedure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the clinical procedure, including instructions, conditions, and any protocol-specific requirements (e.g., fasting requirements, timing relative to dosing, specimen handling instructions).']
  procedure_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the clinical procedure or assessment (e.g., \'Vital Signs\', \'12-Lead ECG\', \'Fasting Blood Draw\', \'ECOG Performance Status Assessment\'). Used in Schedule of Events displays and site training materials.']
  procedure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the visit procedure definition within the study protocol. Draft procedures are under development; active procedures are in use; amended procedures have been modified by protocol amendment; retired procedures are no longer required.']
  qualified_personnel_required varchar [note: 'Specifies the qualification or role required to perform this procedure (e.g., \'Phlebotomist\', \'Radiologist\', \'Cardiologist\', \'Trained Study Nurse\'). Used for site staff delegation log and training requirement planning.']
  rave_form_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Medidata Rave eCRF form associated with this procedure (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Tracks form versioning to ensure data collected aligns with the correct protocol version and supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  requires_fasting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject must be in a fasting state prior to this procedure (e.g., fasting lipid panel, fasting glucose). Drives subject instruction materials and visit scheduling constraints.']
  requires_unscheduled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this procedure definition applies to unscheduled visits (e.g., safety follow-up, early termination). Distinguishes procedures that are only applicable at unscheduled visits from those in the planned Schedule of Events.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code to which this procedure\'s collected data maps (e.g., VS=Vital Signs, LB=Laboratory Test Results, EG=ECG Test Results, EX=Exposure, PE=Physical Examination). Drives SDTM dataset construction for regulatory submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VS|LB|EG|IE|DS|AE|CM|EX|MH|PE|PR|SC|TU|RS|FA|other — promote to reference product]']
  sdv_requirement varchar [note: 'Specifies the Source Data Verification (SDV) requirement level for data collected by this procedure. Drives risk-based monitoring plans and CRA visit scope. 100_percent requires full SDV; risk_based applies statistical sampling; critical_only limits SDV to primary endpoint data.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of the procedure within the visit, defining the recommended execution sequence (e.g., 1=vital signs, 2=blood draw, 3=ECG). Used to generate the ordered Schedule of Events and site procedure checklists.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen collected during this procedure, if applicable (e.g., blood, urine, tissue biopsy, CSF). Drives laboratory supply chain planning, sample handling instructions, and CDISC SDTM LBSPEC mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|urine|csf|tissue|saliva|stool|plasma|serum|whole_blood|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  specimen_volume_ml decimal(10,3) [note: 'Volume of biological specimen required for this procedure in millilitres (mL). Used for blood volume tracking per subject visit, laboratory supply planning, and protocol feasibility assessment (total blood volume per subject).']
  timing_reference varchar [note: 'Specifies the timing reference point for procedure execution relative to a clinical anchor event (e.g., pre_dose, post_dose, fasting). Critical for PK sampling, vital signs, and lab draws where timing relative to dosing affects data interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_dose|post_dose|any_time|fasting|pre_meal|post_meal|bedtime|morning|evening — promote to reference product]']
  timing_window_post_minutes varchar [note: 'Allowable time window in minutes after the nominal procedure time point (positive offset). Defines the acceptable late execution window for protocol compliance assessment (e.g., +30 minutes for a post-dose PK sample).']
  timing_window_pre_minutes varchar [note: 'Allowable time window in minutes before the nominal procedure time point (negative offset). Defines the acceptable early execution window for protocol compliance assessment (e.g., -30 minutes for a pre-dose PK sample).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this visit procedure record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Defines the specific clinical procedures, assessments, and data collection activities required at each scheduled visit. Records procedure name, procedure category (vital signs, lab draw, ECG, imaging, questionnaire, dosing, PK sampling, physical exam, biopsy, ECOG assessment), visit association, whether the procedure is mandatory or optional, CDISC SDTM domain mapping for the procedure, and eCRF form reference in Medidata Rave. Enables the Schedule of Events matrix construction.'
}

Table study.epoch {
  epoch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a study epoch record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the study_epoch data product.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version that defines this epoch. Epochs may be added, removed, or modified across protocol amendments.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this epoch belongs. Links the epoch to its governing study protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: study_epoch has amendment_number as a STRING but no FK to study_protocol_amendment. Study epochs (Screening, Treatment, Follow-up periods) can be restructured by protocol amendments — this FK formaliz']
  ae_reporting_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adverse event (AE) and serious adverse event (SAE) collection and reporting obligations are active during this epoch. Drives safety data collection rules in the EDC and Oracle Argus Safety.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding methodology applied during this epoch as specified in the protocol. Determines data access controls and unblinding procedures applicable to the epoch period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this epoch record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail requirements under FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this epoch definition version ceased to be effective, typically due to a protocol amendment superseding it. Null for the currently active epoch definition version.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this epoch definition version became effective in the study protocol. Used for amendment tracking and determining which epoch definition applies to a given subject enrollment date.']
  epoch_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the epoch\'s purpose, activities, and clinical objectives as specified in the study protocol. Provides context for data reviewers and regulatory submissions.']
  epoch_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the study epoch as defined in the protocol (e.g., \'Screening\', \'Run-in\', \'Treatment Period 1\', \'Washout\', \'Follow-up\', \'Extension\'). Maps to CDISC SDTM TE.EPOCH variable.']
  epoch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the epoch definition within the study protocol. DRAFT indicates under development; ACTIVE indicates approved and in use; AMENDED indicates modified by protocol amendment; SUPERSEDED indicates replaced by a newer version; RETIRED indicates no longer applicable.']
  epoch_type varchar [note: 'Controlled vocabulary classification of the epoch\'s functional purpose within the study design. Drives SDTM dataset construction and subject disposition analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SCREENING|RUN-IN|TREATMENT|WASHOUT|FOLLOW-UP|EXTENSION|BASELINE|OPEN-LABEL|DOUBLE-BLIND|SINGLE-BLIND|PLACEBO|OBSERVATION — promote to reference product]']
  extension_epoch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this epoch is an optional or mandatory study extension period beyond the core treatment phase. Extension epochs may have separate regulatory approval requirements.']
  follow_up_epoch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this epoch is a post-treatment follow-up period for safety monitoring and long-term outcome assessment. Drives safety reporting timelines and subject disposition coding.']
  ip_administered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether investigational product (IP) is administered to subjects during this epoch. Drives IP accountability tracking, supply chain planning, and SDTM exposure domain (EX) linkage.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this epoch record has been logically deleted (soft delete). True = record is logically deleted and should be excluded from active queries; False = record is active. Supports data retention compliance without physical deletion.']
  itt_population_epoch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subject participation in this epoch contributes to Intent-to-Treat (ITT) population eligibility determination. Used in biostatistical analysis and SAP population definitions.']
  maximum_duration_days varchar [note: 'Protocol-specified maximum number of days a subject may remain in this epoch before mandatory transition or discontinuation. Enforced by CTMS and EDC visit scheduling logic.']
  minimum_duration_days varchar [note: 'Protocol-specified minimum number of days a subject must remain in this epoch to be considered evaluable. Used for per-protocol (PP) population eligibility determination.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Protocol-specified planned duration of the epoch expressed in days. Used for visit scheduling, subject timeline calculations, and study feasibility planning. Maps to CDISC SDTM TE.TEDUR (ISO 8601 duration converted to days for analytics).']
  planned_duration_iso varchar [note: 'Protocol-specified planned duration of the epoch expressed in ISO 8601 duration format (e.g., \'P28D\', \'P4W\', \'P6M\'). This is the authoritative CDISC SDTM TE.TEDUR value used for regulatory submissions.']
  planned_end_day varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined study day on which this epoch is planned to end, relative to the study reference start date. Used for visit schedule construction and subject timeline alignment.']
  planned_start_day varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined study day on which this epoch is planned to begin, relative to the study reference start date (Day 1). Used for visit schedule construction and subject timeline alignment.']
  primary_endpoint_epoch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the primary efficacy endpoint assessments are collected during this epoch. Used in statistical analysis plan (SAP) to define the primary analysis window.']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section number or identifier within the study protocol document that defines this epoch (e.g., \'Section 6.2.1\'). Enables traceability between the data model and the source protocol document.']
  randomization_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subject randomization occurs during this epoch. True = randomization event occurs within this epoch (e.g., Treatment Period 1 start); False = no randomization in this epoch.']
  sdtm_epoch_code varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM controlled terminology code for the epoch as submitted in the Trial Elements (TE) domain ETCD variable. Must conform to CDISC CT epoch codelist (C99079). Used for regulatory submission dataset construction.']
  sdtm_te_domain_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this epoch record is mapped and included in the CDISC SDTM Trial Elements (TE) domain for regulatory submission. True = included in TE domain; False = excluded (e.g., internal planning epoch not submitted).']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering of the epoch within the study timeline. Determines the chronological position of the epoch relative to other epochs. Maps to CDISC SDTM TE.TEPRNM variable.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or short label for the epoch used in data displays, visit schedules, and SDTM dataset headers (e.g., \'SCR\', \'RUN\', \'TRT1\', \'WASH\', \'FU\'). Maps to CDISC SDTM TE.ETCD variable.']
  source_system_epoch_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this epoch as recorded in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One study design module or Medidata Rave EDC). Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.']
  source_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this epoch definition was sourced (e.g., Oracle Clinical One, Medidata Rave). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  treatment_arm_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this epoch applies to a specific treatment arm only (True) or is common across all arms of the study (False). Relevant for crossover and multi-arm study designs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this epoch record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  washout_epoch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this epoch is a washout period during which prior treatment effects are expected to dissipate before the next treatment period. Used in crossover study design analysis.']
  window_after_days varchar [note: 'Allowable number of days after the planned epoch start date within which the epoch transition is considered protocol-compliant. Used for protocol deviation assessment.']
  window_before_days varchar [note: 'Allowable number of days before the planned epoch start date within which the epoch transition is considered protocol-compliant. Used for protocol deviation assessment.']

  Note: 'Defines the temporal epochs (periods) of a clinical study such as Screening, Run-in, Treatment Period 1, Washout, Treatment Period 2, Follow-up, and Extension. Captures epoch name, epoch short name, epoch sequence number, epoch type, planned duration, and CDISC SDTM TE domain mapping. Epochs provide the temporal framework for visit scheduling, subject disposition, and SDTM dataset construction.'
}

Table study.study_milestone {
  study_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each study milestone record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Primary key for the study_milestone data product.']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Many study milestones are epoch-specific events — for example, \'Treatment Period Start\', \'Screening Completion\', \'Follow-up Period End\' are milestones that mark the beginning or end of specific epochs']
  predecessor_milestone_study_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier of the milestone that must be achieved before this milestone can begin. Enables dependency chain modeling for critical path analysis and schedule impact assessment.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: study_milestone has protocol_version as a STRING but no FK to protocol. Study milestones (e.g., Protocol Final, First Patient In, Database Lock) are associated with specific protocol versions — this F']
  regulatory_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_milestone. Business justification: Integrated timeline management requires linking operational study milestones (e.g., first patient enrolled, database lock) to corresponding regulatory milestones (e.g., IND annual report due, NDA subm']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this milestone belongs. Links the milestone to the study protocol and overall study lifecycle.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protocol amendment that introduced or modified this milestone. Null if the milestone was defined in the original protocol. Supports change impact traceability per GCP requirements.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: CTMS milestone tracking at the site level (FPFV, LPLV, SIV, site activation) is a named operational process. Site-level milestones drive enrollment forecasting, critical path analysis, and contractual']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Key milestones (First Patient In, Database Lock, Study Completion) require TMF evidence documents (enrollment confirmation, DBL certificate). CTMS/eTMF integration depends on milestone-to-document tra']
  achievement_confirmed_by varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual who formally confirmed and recorded the milestone as achieved in the CTMS. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements and audit trail documentation.']
  achievement_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the milestone achievement was formally confirmed and recorded in the CTMS. Distinct from actual_date (the business event date) — this is the system confirmation timestamp per ALCOA+ requirements.']
  actual_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the milestone was actually achieved. Null if the milestone has not yet been reached. Compared against planned_date to compute schedule variance for sponsor reporting and KPI tracking.']
  actual_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'For enrollment-type milestones, the actual number of subjects enrolled at the time of milestone achievement. Compared against target_enrollment_count to assess enrollment performance at key study milestones.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The approved baseline date for the milestone, set at study kick-off or after a formal re-baseline event. Preserved as an immutable reference point even when planned_date is revised, enabling true baseline vs. current schedule variance analysis.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, sponsor communications, or contextual information related to the milestone. Supports collaborative tracking and audit documentation in CTMS.']
  completion_criteria varchar [note: 'Formal, measurable criteria that must be satisfied for the milestone to be considered achieved (e.g., \'100% of target sites activated\', \'All CRFs locked and queries resolved\'). Supports objective milestone verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this milestone record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  ctms_milestone_ref varchar [note: 'Native milestone identifier from the Oracle Siebel CTMS source system. Preserved for cross-system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CTMS.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this milestone record originated (e.g., Oracle Siebel CTMS, Oracle Clinical One, manual entry). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the silver layer.']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'Type of scheduling dependency between this milestone and its predecessor (e.g., Finish-to-Start means the predecessor must finish before this milestone can start). Supports critical path method (CPM) scheduling in CTMS.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'The current best-estimate date for milestone achievement, updated dynamically as study conditions evolve. Distinct from planned_date (baseline) and actual_date (confirmed). Supports rolling forecast reporting to sponsors.']
  is_contractual boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is tied to a contractual obligation with the sponsor (e.g., payment trigger, deliverable deadline). Contractual milestones are tracked for financial and legal compliance purposes.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone lies on the critical path of the study schedule. Critical path milestones directly impact the overall study completion date and receive heightened monitoring attention from the sponsor and CRO.']
  is_regulatory_milestone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone has a direct regulatory reporting obligation (e.g., IND submission, first patient enrolled notification, DBL, NDA/BLA submission). Regulatory milestones trigger compliance tracking and health authority notifications.']
  lead_lag_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of lead (negative) or lag (positive) applied to the dependency relationship with the predecessor milestone. Allows scheduling flexibility where a milestone can start before or must wait after its predecessor.']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the milestone for portfolio-level reporting and sponsor dashboards. Distinguishes operational milestones (site activation, monitoring) from regulatory (IND, NDA/BLA submissions) and data management (DBL, CSR) milestones.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the milestone within the study (e.g., FPFV, LPLV, DBL, CSR_SUBMIT). Used as the business identifier in CTMS and sponsor reporting.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the milestone, including entry/exit criteria, acceptance conditions, and any special instructions. Provides context beyond the milestone_name for operational teams and auditors.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the milestone (e.g., \'First Patient First Visit\', \'Last Patient Last Visit\', \'Database Lock\', \'Clinical Study Report Submission\'). Used in sponsor dashboards and CTMS timeline views.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the milestone indicating whether it has been achieved, is pending, is delayed relative to plan, has been waived, or cancelled. Core field for CTMS timeline tracking and sponsor reporting.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the milestone by its operational or regulatory function. Drives reporting groupings and critical path analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protocol_approval|site_activation|enrollment|data_collection|safety|regulatory_submission|database_lock|study_closeout|csr_submission|other — promote to reference product]']
  notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether achievement of this milestone triggers a formal notification to the sponsor, health authority, IRB/IEC, or other stakeholder. Drives automated alert workflows in CTMS.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the milestone achievement notification was dispatched to the relevant stakeholders. Null if notification has not yet been sent. Supports compliance audit trails for regulatory reporting obligations.']
  planned_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled calendar date by which this milestone was expected to be achieved, as defined in the study protocol or project plan. Used as the baseline for variance calculations and critical path analysis.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The organizational unit or role accountable for achieving this milestone (e.g., Sponsor, CRO, Site, Data Management, Regulatory Affairs). Used for accountability tracking and escalation routing in CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sponsor|cro|site|data_management|biostatistics|regulatory_affairs|safety|finance — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this milestone within the study\'s overall milestone schedule. Defines the logical ordering of milestones for timeline visualization and Gantt chart rendering in CTMS.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'Clinical development phase of the study at the time this milestone is defined (Phase 1, 2, 3, 4). Contextualizes the milestone within the drug development lifecycle for portfolio analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phase_1|phase_1_2|phase_2|phase_2_3|phase_3|phase_4|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'For enrollment-type milestones (e.g., FPFV, 50% enrollment, LPLV), the target number of subjects to be enrolled at the time of milestone achievement. Provides quantitative context for enrollment milestone tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this milestone record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between the planned_date and the actual_date (or forecast_date if not yet achieved). Positive values indicate delay; negative values indicate early completion. Used for sponsor KPI reporting and study performance analytics.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded explanation for any deviation between the planned and actual/forecast milestone date. Required when variance_days is non-zero. Supports root cause analysis and CAPA tracking per GCP requirements.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized coded classification of the variance reason to enable aggregate analysis across studies and portfolios. Complements the free-text variance_reason field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: site_delay|enrollment_shortfall|regulatory_hold|protocol_amendment|sponsor_decision|resource_constraint|covid_impact|other — promote to reference product]']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or role of the authorized individual who approved the milestone waiver. Required when milestone_status is \'waived\'. Supports GCP compliance and sponsor governance documentation.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Documented rationale for waiving a milestone when milestone_status is \'waived\'. Required for regulatory audit trail and sponsor approval. Captures the business or scientific justification for the waiver decision.']

  Note: 'Tracks key operational and regulatory milestones in the study lifecycle. Records milestone name, milestone type (protocol approval, first site activation, FPFV, LPLV, DBL, CSR submission, study close-out, regulatory submission, enrollment milestones), planned date, actual date, milestone status (pending, achieved, delayed, waived), responsible party, variance reason, and critical path flag. Supports CTMS timeline tracking, sponsor reporting, and study performance analytics in Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

Table study.country {
  country_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the study-country record. Primary key for the study_country data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Each country\'s study activation, enrollment start, and regulatory hold status are governed by a specific CTA. Linking study_country to the CTA record enables site activation workflows, enrollment gati']
  cta_regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Each country\'s CTA submission (tracked via cta_submission_date, cta_approval_date on study_country) is filed in a country-specific regulatory binder. Country regulatory leads need direct traceability ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing regulatory authority responsible for clinical trial oversight in this country (e.g., FDA for US, EMA/national competent authority for EU member states, PMDA for Japan). Links to the health_authority reference product.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study for which this country-level record is maintained. Links the country configuration to the study protocol.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Country-level regulatory submissions generate country-specific TMF documents (translated ICF, country protocol synopsis, local ethics approval). eTMF country-level document management requires direct ']
  activated_site_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of investigative sites that have been activated and authorized to enroll subjects in this country. Compared against planned_site_count for country-level site activation KPI reporting.']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Overall operational activation status of the country within the study lifecycle, distinct from enrollment_status. Reflects the country\'s readiness and operational state as tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Clinical One.']
  actual_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of subjects enrolled (consented and randomized or screened) in this country to date. Compared against planned_enrollment_target for enrollment performance reporting in Medidata Rave and Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  actual_screened_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of subjects screened at sites in this country to date. Used to calculate the realized screen failure rate and to assess enrollment efficiency.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total approved budget allocated for all study activities in this country, expressed in the country\'s local currency (see currency_code). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA project accounting and used for country-level financial performance tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code identifying the country where the clinical study is conducted (e.g., US, DE, JP). Used as the primary geographic identifier for regulatory and enrollment reporting.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Full English name of the country as defined by ISO 3166-1 (e.g., United States, Germany, Japan). Used for display in regulatory submissions, reports, and dashboards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this study-country record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and audit trail requirements.']
  cta_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority granted approval for the clinical trial to proceed in this country. Milestone used for site activation planning and regulatory timeline KPI reporting.']
  cta_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the CTA or IND application with the national regulatory authority. Drives regulatory hold flags and country activation readiness assessments.']
  cta_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Trial Authorization (CTA) or Investigational New Drug (IND) application was submitted to the national regulatory authority for this country. Tracked in Veeva Vault for regulatory timeline management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the country (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Used for country-level budget planning, site payment processing, and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA.']
  data_privacy_framework varchar [note: 'Primary data privacy regulatory framework applicable to subject data collected in this country (e.g., GDPR for EU, HIPAA for US, PIPEDA for Canada, APPI for Japan). Drives data handling, consent, and transfer requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: GDPR|HIPAA|PIPEDA|PDPA|APPI|LGPD|other|not_applicable — promote to reference product]']
  dropout_rate_assumption decimal(5,4) [note: 'Assumed proportion of enrolled subjects expected to withdraw or be lost to follow-up in this country, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1000 = 10%). Used in sample size and enrollment buffer calculations.']
  enrollment_closure_date date [note: 'Date on which enrollment was formally closed in this country, either due to target achievement, regulatory hold, or study termination. Distinct from last_subject_enrolled_date as it reflects the administrative closure decision.']
  enrollment_rate_assumption decimal(7,4) [note: 'Assumed average number of subjects enrolled per active site per month in this country, used in enrollment forecasting and timeline modeling. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 1.5000 subjects/site/month).']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment lifecycle status for this country within the study. Drives enrollment forecasting, site activation planning, and country-level feasibility reporting in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'EudraCT number assigned by the European Medicines Agency (EMA) for clinical trials conducted in EU/EEA member states. Required for EU CTR compliance and EudraCT/CTIS registration. Applicable only for EU countries.']
  feasibility_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the country-level feasibility assessment was completed and the feasibility_score was assigned. Used to track the currency of feasibility data for enrollment planning.']
  feasibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score (typically 0–100) representing the overall feasibility assessment rating for conducting the study in this country, based on site availability, patient population, regulatory environment, and infrastructure. Derived during the Site Selection and Feasibility Assessment process.']
  first_site_activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the first investigative site in this country was activated and authorized to enroll subjects. Key milestone for country-level startup timeline KPI reporting in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  first_subject_enrolled_date date [note: 'Date on which the first subject was enrolled (consented and randomized or screened) at any site within this country. Marks the start of active enrollment for country-level timeline tracking.']
  icf_version_required varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) approved by the national IRB/IEC and regulatory authority for use in this country. Ensures sites use the correct country-specific ICF version as tracked in Clinpal and Veeva Vault.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the national or central IRB/IEC granted ethical approval for the study to proceed in this country. Required before any subject enrollment can commence.']
  irb_iec_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the study protocol and informed consent materials were submitted to the national or central Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) for ethical review in this country.']
  last_subject_enrolled_date date [note: 'Date on which the last subject was enrolled in this country. Used to calculate country-level enrollment duration and to trigger enrollment completion milestones.']
  local_language_code varchar [note: 'Primary local language code for the country using IETF BCP 47 format (e.g., de-DE, ja-JP, fr-FR). Drives Informed Consent Form (ICF) translation requirements and eCRF localization in Clinpal and Medidata Rave.']
  local_regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Country-specific regulatory reference or dossier number assigned by the national competent authority upon CTA/IND submission or approval (e.g., EudraCT number for EU, IND number for US, CTN for Australia). Stored in Veeva Vault.']
  national_registry_number varchar [note: 'Country-specific clinical trial registry number assigned by the national registry (e.g., ISRCTN, UMIN for Japan, CTRI for India). Distinct from EudraCT and ClinicalTrials.gov identifiers. Required for WHO ICTRP compliance.']
  planned_enrollment_target varchar [note: 'Target number of subjects planned to be enrolled in this country as defined in the study protocol and country feasibility assessment. Drives enrollment forecasting and country-level resource allocation.']
  planned_screen_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of subjects to be screened in this country, derived from the planned enrollment target and the assumed screen failure rate. Used for site capacity planning and patient recruitment forecasting.']
  planned_site_count varchar [note: 'Number of investigative sites planned to be activated in this country as defined in the study feasibility assessment and country-level enrollment plan. Used for resource planning and site activation tracking.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory hold (e.g., FDA Clinical Hold under 21 CFR 312.42, or equivalent national authority hold) is currently in effect for this country, preventing or suspending subject enrollment.']
  regulatory_hold_lift_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory hold was lifted by the national authority, allowing enrollment to resume. Null if the hold is still in effect.']
  regulatory_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for the regulatory hold in this country, as communicated by the national regulatory authority. Populated only when regulatory_hold_flag is True.']
  regulatory_hold_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory hold was imposed by the national authority in this country. Used for regulatory timeline impact analysis and sponsor reporting.']
  screen_failure_rate_assumption decimal(5,4) [note: 'Assumed proportion of screened subjects expected to fail screening criteria in this country, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.3000 = 30%). Used in enrollment forecasting models to derive planned_screen_count from planned_enrollment_target.']
  subject_data_transfer_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subject-level clinical data collected in this country may be transferred cross-border to the sponsor or CRO data systems under applicable data privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR Chapter V adequacy decisions, SCCs). Critical for EDC data flow configuration in Medidata Rave.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this study-country record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and change audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Manages the countries in which a clinical study is conducted including country-level enrollment planning. Records country code (ISO 3166), country name, regulatory authority, CTA/IND submission and approval dates, approval status, first site activation date, planned and actual number of sites, planned and actual enrollment targets per country, enrollment rate assumptions, screen failure rate assumptions, country-level enrollment status, and regulatory hold flags. Supports global regulatory planning, enrollment forecasting, and country-level feasibility assessment.'
}

Table study.investigational_product {
  investigational_product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or investigational device record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this master resource entity.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Post-approval change management and label reconciliation require tracing which investigational_product record corresponds to the regulatory approval. Regulatory affairs teams routinely link approved I']
  country_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.country_registration. Business justification: Country marketing authorizations are granted for specific IP formulations/strengths. Regulatory teams need direct IP-to-registration traceability for variation submissions, renewal management, and pos']
  ib_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.version. Business justification: The Investigator\'s Brochure (IB) is a GCP-required essential document with mandatory versioned updates distributed to sites. IP records must reference the current IB document version for site distribu']
  imp_tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier (IMPD) / Investigator\'s Brochure is a DIA TMF Zone 4 essential document. IP management teams and regulatory affairs must locate the current IMPD/IB TMF do']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: An investigational product is authorized for clinical use under a specific IND/IMPD. This link is required for IP-level regulatory compliance tracking, clinical hold impact assessment, and safety repo']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: The IND/IMPD submission for an investigational product is filed in a regulatory binder. Regulatory affairs teams track which binder contains the IND/IMPD for each IP to manage submission status, agenc']
  special_designation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.special_designation. Business justification: Special designations (orphan drug, breakthrough therapy, fast track) are granted for specific investigational products. Regulatory strategy and IP lifecycle management require direct traceability from']
  active_substance varchar [note: 'Name of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) or active substance in the investigational product. May differ from the INN for novel compounds. Used in IMPD/IND CMC sections, pharmacovigilance, and SDTM datasets.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Chemical Abstracts Service (CAS) Registry Number uniquely identifying the active substance chemical structure. Used in regulatory submissions, safety data sheets, and cross-referencing with pharmacological databases.']
  controlled_substance_schedule varchar [note: 'DEA controlled substance schedule classification for the investigational product (e.g., Schedule II, Schedule IV) or \'not_controlled\' if not a controlled substance. Drives regulatory compliance requirements for storage, dispensing, and accountability at investigational sites.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigational product master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, ALCOA+ principles, and GCP compliance.']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'Current clinical development phase of the investigational product at the time of record creation or last update (e.g., Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3). Informs regulatory strategy, risk classification, and portfolio management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: preclinical|phase_1|phase_1_2|phase_2|phase_2_3|phase_3|phase_4|post_marketing — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the investigational product as administered to subjects (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution for injection, lyophilized powder). Captured per CDISC SDTM EX domain and referenced in supply chain and pharmacy management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|solution_for_injection|lyophilized_powder|patch|inhaler|suppository|cream — promote to reference product]']
  ema_product_number varchar [note: 'EMA-assigned product number for the investigational product as registered in the EU Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS) or EudraCT. Required for EU regulatory submissions and EU CTR 536/2014 compliance.']
  first_in_human_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigational product was first administered to a human subject in any clinical study. Key milestone for regulatory timelines, development history, and portfolio analytics.']
  formulation varchar [note: 'Qualitative description of the investigational product formulation including excipients, vehicle, and any special formulation characteristics (e.g., \'immediate-release tablet with microcrystalline cellulose\', \'sterile aqueous solution pH 7.4\'). Captured in the IMPD/IND CMC section.']
  imp_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned internal drug or device code uniquely identifying the investigational product across all studies (e.g., ABC-1234). Serves as the externally-known business identifier referenced in protocols, supply chain, and regulatory submissions. Maps to the drug code field in Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.']
  impd_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the current Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier (IMPD) or IND on file with the regulatory authority for this product. Tracked in Veeva Vault for regulatory submission management and amendment history.']
  ind_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the IND application (FDA) or equivalent regulatory authorization application was first submitted for this investigational product. Tracked in Veeva Vault for regulatory milestone reporting.']
  investigational_product_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the investigational product including its therapeutic purpose, background, and any additional context not captured in structured fields. Used in protocol background sections and regulatory submissions.']
  investigational_product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigational product master record (e.g., active for ongoing studies, discontinued if development halted, superseded if replaced by a new formulation). Drives supply chain and regulatory workflow decisions.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigational product is used in a blinded (masked) study design (True) or open-label (False). Drives blinding and unblinding procedures in Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One randomization configuration.']
  is_comparator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product record represents a comparator (active control or placebo) rather than the primary investigational product (True = comparator). Enables differentiation of IMP vs. comparator in supply chain, SDTM, and safety reporting.']
  is_placebo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this product record represents a placebo (True) rather than an active investigational product or comparator. Used in RTSM randomization configuration, supply chain, and SDTM EX domain coding.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the pharmaceutical manufacturer or contract manufacturing organization (CMO) responsible for producing the investigational product. Referenced in the IMPD/IND CMC section and supply chain records in SAP S/4HANA.']
  manufacturer_site varchar [note: 'Physical site or facility name and location where the investigational product is manufactured. Required for regulatory submissions (IND/IMPD) and GMP compliance tracking. Distinct from the manufacturer legal entity name.']
  mechanism_of_action varchar [note: 'Pharmacological mechanism by which the investigational product exerts its therapeutic effect (e.g., selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor, PD-1 checkpoint inhibitor). Captured in the IND/IMPD and referenced in the protocol background section.']
  ndc_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned National Drug Code (NDC) number for the investigational product, if applicable. Used for US regulatory submissions, pharmacy dispensing records, and drug accountability tracking. May be absent for novel compounds not yet assigned an NDC.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments regarding the investigational product record, such as special handling instructions, known stability issues, or interim regulatory communications. Captured by data management or regulatory affairs teams.']
  packaging_description varchar [note: 'Description of the primary and secondary packaging configuration for the investigational product (e.g., \'blister pack of 10 tablets in a carton\', \'single-use vial 10 mL in a kit\'). Referenced in supply chain, pharmacy, and RTSM kit configuration.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigational product by modality type (e.g., small molecule, biologic, gene therapy, medical device). Drives regulatory pathway selection, storage requirements, and supply chain handling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: small_molecule|biologic|gene_therapy|cell_therapy|medical_device|combination_product|vaccine|radiopharmaceutical — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory approval status of the investigational product (e.g., approved for a different indication, purely investigational, withdrawn from market). Informs regulatory strategy, labeling requirements, and comparator sourcing decisions.']
  retest_period_months varchar [note: 'Period in months after which the investigational product must be retested before continued use, applicable to active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs) and certain formulations. Distinct from shelf life; used in supply chain and QC planning.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the investigational product is administered to subjects (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical). Captured per CDISC SDTM EX domain EXROUTE variable and referenced in protocol and pharmacy instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|intrathecal — promote to reference product]']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Approved shelf life of the investigational product in months from the date of manufacture, as established by stability studies and documented in the IMPD/IND. Drives expiry date calculation, supply planning, and resupply triggers in Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  sponsor_product_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor\'s internal product or compound code used in portfolio management systems, distinct from the IMP code used in clinical operations. Enables cross-referencing between clinical development and commercial product pipelines.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage conditions for the investigational product as specified in the IMPD/IND and product label (e.g., \'Store at 2–8°C, protected from light\', \'Store below 25°C\'). Critical for supply chain cold chain management and site pharmacy compliance.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the investigational product. Used by supply chain and site pharmacy to configure cold chain monitoring alerts and validate storage compliance.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the investigational product. Used by supply chain and site pharmacy to configure cold chain monitoring alerts and validate storage compliance.']
  strength varchar [note: 'Nominal strength or concentration of the active substance in the investigational product as labeled (e.g., \'10 mg\', \'100 mg/mL\', \'5 mg/kg\'). Captured in the IMPD/IND and referenced in supply chain, pharmacy, and SDTM EX domain.']
  strength_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the investigational product strength (e.g., mg, mg/mL, IU). Standardized per CDISC controlled terminology to enable cross-study comparisons and SDTM dataset generation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|mg/mL|mg/kg|mcg|mcg/mL|IU|IU/mL|g|% — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  therapeutic_class varchar [note: 'Therapeutic or pharmacological class of the investigational product (e.g., oncology, cardiovascular, CNS, anti-infective). Used for portfolio analytics, regulatory strategy, and study feasibility assessments.']
  unit_of_dispensing varchar [note: 'The unit in which the investigational product is dispensed to subjects at the investigational site (e.g., tablet, vial, kit, syringe). Used in RTSM dispensing records, drug accountability logs, and SDTM EX domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|kit|syringe|patch|sachet|bottle|ampoule — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigational product master record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, ALCOA+ principles, and change management tracking.']
  who_dd_code varchar [note: 'WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) drug code assigned to the investigational product for standardized drug coding in adverse event reporting, SDTM datasets, and regulatory submissions. Enables cross-study signal detection in Oracle Argus Safety.']

  Note: 'Master record for the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or device used in a clinical study. Captures IMP name, INN (International Nonproprietary Name), drug code, formulation, dosage form, route of administration, strength, manufacturer, IND/IMPD reference number, ATC code, WHO-DD drug code, mechanism of action, therapeutic class, storage conditions, and shelf life. SSOT for IMP identity referenced by supply chain, safety, and data management domains.'
}

Table study.dosing_regimen {
  dosing_regimen_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the dosing regimen record within the Clinical Trials data platform. Primary key for the dosing_regimen product in the study domain.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Regulatory approvals specify the approved dosing regimen on the product label. Post-approval change management and label reconciliation require tracing which dosing_regimen record was approved, enabli']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study arm (treatment group) to which this dosing regimen applies. A study may have multiple arms each with distinct regimens (e.g., active drug arm vs. placebo arm).']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: A dosing regimen is active during a specific study epoch (e.g., a loading dose regimen during the Run-in epoch, a maintenance dose regimen during the Treatment epoch). dosing_regimen already has effec']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational product (IP) being administered under this dosing regimen. Supports traceability from regimen to supply chain and accountability logs.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study for which this dosing regimen is defined. Links the regimen to the study protocol and all associated study-level metadata.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: dosing_regimen has amendment_number as a STRING but no FK to study_protocol_amendment. Dosing regimens are introduced or modified by protocol amendments — this FK formalizes that traceability. No bidi']
  adam_adex_mapping_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing how this dosing regimen maps to the CDISC ADaM ADEX (Analysis Dataset for Exposure) domain, including derivation rules for cumulative dose, dose intensity, and relative dose intensity calculations used in biostatistical analysis.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding level applied to this dosing regimen as defined in the study protocol (e.g., \'double-blind\' where neither subject nor investigator knows the treatment assignment). Impacts RTSM/IRT dispensing logic and unblinding procedures.']
  comparator_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this regimen represents an active investigational product, placebo, vehicle control, sham procedure, or no-treatment arm. Critical for ITT and PP population definitions and regulatory benefit-risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dosing regimen record was first created in the data platform, conforming to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  dose_escalation_step varchar [note: 'Sequential step number within the dose escalation scheme (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Enables ordering of dose cohorts in escalation studies and supports dose-response curve construction in PK/PD modeling.']
  dose_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical form of the investigational product as administered (e.g., \'TABLET\', \'CAPSULE\', \'SOLUTION FOR INJECTION\', \'PATCH\'). Conforms to CDISC SDTM EX.EXDOSFRM controlled terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TABLET|CAPSULE|SOLUTION FOR INJECTION|PATCH|POWDER|CREAM|SUPPOSITORY|INHALER — promote to reference product]']
  dose_level decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric dose amount to be administered per dosing event as specified in the protocol (e.g., 10.0, 50.5, 100.0). Represents the planned dose per administration. Used in PK/PD modeling and dose-response analysis.']
  dose_modification_rules varchar [note: 'Narrative description of protocol-defined rules for dose modifications, reductions, interruptions, or discontinuations based on toxicity or adverse events (e.g., \'Reduce by 25% for Grade 2 AE; discontinue for Grade 3 SAE\'). Supports safety monitoring and CTCAE-based dose management.']
  dose_reduction_levels varchar [note: 'Number of permitted dose reduction levels defined in the protocol (e.g., 2 levels: Level -1 and Level -2). Used in oncology studies to manage toxicity while maintaining therapeutic benefit. Supports safety dose-response analysis.']
  dose_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the dose level (e.g., \'mg\', \'mg/kg\', \'mg/m2\', \'mcg/kg\'). Conforms to CDISC SDTM EX.EXDOSU controlled terminology. Essential for PK/PD modeling and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|mg/kg|mg/m2|mcg|mcg/kg|IU|mL|g — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dosing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of dose administration using standard clinical abbreviations (e.g., \'QD\' = once daily, \'BID\' = twice daily, \'QW\' = once weekly, \'Q2W\' = every two weeks). Conforms to CDISC SDTM EX.EXDOSFRQ controlled terminology. Drives subject visit scheduling and PK sampling windows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: QD|BID|TID|QID|QW|BIW|TIW|Q2W|Q4W|ONCE|PRN — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dosing_interval_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric interval between doses expressed in days (e.g., 1.0 for QD, 0.5 for BID, 7.0 for QW). Provides machine-readable precision for automated scheduling in RTSM/IRT systems and PK modeling when frequency alone is insufficient.']
  dosing_route varchar [note: 'Route by which the investigational product is administered (e.g., \'ORAL\', \'INTRAVENOUS\', \'SUBCUTANEOUS\', \'INTRAMUSCULAR\', \'TOPICAL\', \'INHALATION\'). Conforms to CDISC SDTM EX.EXROUTE controlled terminology. Critical for PK absorption modeling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ORAL|INTRAVENOUS|SUBCUTANEOUS|INTRAMUSCULAR|TOPICAL|INHALATION|INTRANASAL|TRANSDERMAL — promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this dosing regimen version ceased to be effective (e.g., superseded by an amendment). Null for currently active regimens. Supports temporal versioning and historical audit of regimen changes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this dosing regimen version became effective in the study, typically aligned with protocol approval or amendment approval date. Supports temporal versioning of regimen changes across the study lifecycle.']
  escalation_scheme_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the dose escalation scheme applied in this regimen (e.g., \'3+3 design\', \'Accelerated Titration\', \'BOIN design\', \'mTPI-2\'). Populated only when is_dose_escalation = True. Supports Phase I safety and PK/PD analysis.']
  food_effect_instruction varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined instruction regarding food intake relative to dosing (e.g., \'fasted\' = at least 8 hours fasting before dose, \'fed\' = standard high-fat meal before dose). Impacts PK absorption and bioavailability. Conforms to FDA food effect guidance.']
  infusion_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of intravenous or subcutaneous infusion in minutes, applicable when dosing_route is \'INTRAVENOUS\' or similar. Null for non-infusion routes. Critical for PK modeling of IV drug exposure and site nursing procedure planning.']
  is_dose_escalation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regimen is part of a dose-escalation scheme (True) or a fixed-dose regimen (False). When True, the escalation_scheme_description and mtd_flag fields become operationally relevant. Common in Phase I oncology studies.']
  is_mtd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dose level has been designated as the Maximum Tolerated Dose (MTD) by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or study team (True = MTD confirmed). Critical for Phase I dose-finding studies and regulatory submissions.']
  loading_dose_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the loading dose is administered before transitioning to the maintenance dose. Null if no loading dose is defined. Supports PK/PD modeling of drug accumulation and steady-state calculations.']
  loading_dose_level decimal(18,6) [note: 'Initial higher dose administered to rapidly achieve therapeutic drug concentrations before transitioning to the maintenance dose level. Expressed in the same unit as dose_unit. Null if no loading dose is specified in the protocol.']
  maximum_dose_level decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum allowable dose level for this regimen as defined in the protocol (e.g., dose cap for safety). Expressed in the same unit as dose_unit. Used in dose modification rules and safety monitoring.']
  pd_assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pharmacodynamic (PD) biomarker assessments are required for subjects on this dosing regimen (True = PD assessments mandated per protocol). Supports PK/PD modeling and dose-response analysis.']
  pk_sampling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pharmacokinetic (PK) blood sampling is required for subjects on this dosing regimen (True = PK sampling mandated per protocol). Drives PK visit scheduling, sample collection logistics, and DMPK analysis planning.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Total planned duration of the dosing regimen expressed in days as specified in the study protocol (e.g., 84 days for a 12-week treatment period). Used for treatment period planning, supply forecasting, and CDISC SDTM EX.EXENDY derivation.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the study protocol under which this dosing regimen was defined or last amended (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Enables traceability of regimen changes across protocol amendments. Supports TMF/eTMF document management and regulatory audit trails.']
  randomization_ratio varchar [note: 'Allocation ratio for randomization to this arm/regimen expressed as a colon-separated ratio (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1\', \'1:1:1\'). Used in RTSM/IRT configuration and statistical analysis plan (SAP) for sample size and power calculations.']
  regimen_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this dosing regimen within the study protocol (e.g., \'REG-001\', \'ARM-A-DOSE-1\'). Used in protocol documents, eCRFs, and CDISC SDTM EX submissions.']
  regimen_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the dosing regimen as defined in the study protocol (e.g., \'Low Dose QD\', \'High Dose BID\', \'Placebo TIW\'). Used in protocol documents, SAP, and CSR narratives.']
  regimen_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dosing regimen within the study. \'draft\' indicates protocol development; \'active\' indicates currently administered; \'suspended\' indicates temporarily halted (e.g., pending safety review); \'discontinued\' indicates permanently stopped; \'completed\' indicates all subjects have completed this regimen.']
  sdtm_ex_mapping_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing how this dosing regimen maps to the CDISC SDTM EX (Exposure) domain variables, including any derivation logic, controlled terminology mappings, or special handling required for regulatory submission datasets. Supports CDISC compliance and eCTD submission.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this dosing regimen record was sourced or is managed (e.g., \'Oracle Clinical One\' for study design, \'Medidata Rave\' for EDC-captured exposure data). Supports data lineage and ETL traceability in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  starting_dose_level decimal(18,6) [note: 'Initial dose amount at the start of the regimen, particularly relevant for titration or escalation regimens where the dose changes over time. Expressed in the same unit as dose_unit. Supports PK/PD baseline modeling.']
  treatment_period_label varchar [note: 'Label identifying the treatment period within the study to which this regimen applies (e.g., \'Period 1\', \'Run-In\', \'Maintenance\', \'Extension\'). Supports crossover and multi-period study designs. Maps to CDISC SDTM EX.EPOCH.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this dosing regimen record was last modified in the data platform, conforming to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  washout_period_days varchar [note: 'Duration of the washout period in days required between treatment periods in crossover studies, or between prior therapy and study treatment. Null for parallel-group designs. Supports PK carryover analysis and crossover study scheduling.']

  Note: 'Defines the dosing regimen for each investigational product within a study arm. Records regimen name, dose level, dose unit, dosing frequency, dosing route, dosing duration, dose escalation scheme (for dose-escalation studies), dose modification rules, maximum tolerated dose (MTD) flag, and CDISC SDTM EX (Exposure) domain mapping. Supports PK/PD modeling and safety dose-response analysis.'
}

Table study.objective {
  objective_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a study objective record in the clinical trial data platform. Primary key for the study_objective entity.']
  primary_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary endpoint directly associated with this objective. Establishes the objective-to-endpoint traceability chain required by the SAP and regulatory submissions. Nullable for objectives that map to multiple endpoints.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this objective belongs. Links the objective to its governing protocol and study master record.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Study objectives are formally stated in the protocol and can be modified by amendments (e.g., refining the primary objective, adding secondary objectives). Linking objective to study_protocol_amendmen']
  adam_dataset_reference varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM dataset name(s) used for the primary analysis supporting this objective (e.g., ADEFF, ADTTE, ADAE). Provides direct linkage from objective to analysis-ready datasets in SAS Drug Development.']
  alpha_level decimal(6,4) [note: 'Pre-specified Type I error rate (significance level) allocated to this objective in the SAP. Typically 0.05 for primary objectives or a fraction thereof when multiplicity adjustments are applied. Drives sample size calculations and hypothesis testing.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this objective (as part of the protocol or amendment) received formal approval from the sponsor and/or IRB/IEC. Marks the transition from draft to active status and is required for regulatory audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this study objective record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  csr_section_reference varchar [note: 'Section number within the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where results for this objective are reported. Provides traceability from protocol objective through analysis to regulatory submission document per ICH E3 CSR structure.']
  ctd_section_reference varchar [note: 'Module and section reference within the Common Technical Document (CTD) / electronic CTD (eCTD) where this objective\'s results contribute to the regulatory dossier (e.g., Module 5.3.5.1). Supports regulatory submission traceability.']
  dsmb_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether interim results for this objective are to be reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) during planned interim analyses. Drives interim analysis planning and unblinding procedures.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the objective ceased to be effective, typically due to a protocol amendment superseding it. Null for currently active objectives. Supports temporal versioning of protocol objectives.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the objective became effective in the approved protocol. For original protocol objectives, this is the initial protocol approval date. For amended objectives, this is the amendment approval date.']
  hypothesis_type varchar [note: 'Statistical hypothesis framework associated with this objective. Superiority objectives aim to demonstrate a treatment effect greater than control; non-inferiority objectives aim to show the treatment is not worse by a defined margin; equivalence objectives test for no meaningful difference; descriptive objectives have no formal hypothesis.']
  intercurrent_event_strategy varchar [note: 'ICH E9(R1) estimand attribute specifying the strategy for handling intercurrent events (post-randomization events that affect interpretation of the outcome) for this objective. Strategies include while-on-treatment, hypothetical, composite, principal stratum, and treatment policy.']
  irb_iec_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a change to this objective requires IRB/IEC review and approval (e.g., substantial protocol amendment). Drives the amendment workflow and site notification process.']
  multiplicity_adjustment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether statistical multiplicity adjustment (e.g., Bonferroni, Hochberg, hierarchical testing) is required for this objective due to multiple comparisons. Informs the SAP multiplicity control strategy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, clarifications, or internal commentary related to this objective that does not fit structured fields. Used by data managers and biostatisticians during protocol development and SAP authoring.']
  objective_number varchar [note: 'Structured alphanumeric identifier for the objective within the protocol (e.g., P-1 for Primary Objective 1, S-2 for Secondary Objective 2). Used for cross-referencing in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and Clinical Study Report (CSR).']
  objective_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the study objective. Draft objectives are under authoring review; active objectives are in the approved protocol; amended objectives have been modified by a protocol amendment; superseded objectives have been replaced; retired objectives have been removed from the protocol.']
  objective_text varchar [note: 'Full verbatim text of the study objective as stated in the approved protocol. This is the authoritative scientific statement of what the study aims to demonstrate or evaluate, used verbatim in regulatory submissions and the CSR.']
  objective_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the study objective by its scientific hierarchy and regulatory weight. Primary objectives drive the primary endpoint and sample size calculation; secondary objectives provide supportive evidence; exploratory objectives generate hypotheses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|secondary|exploratory|safety|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic — promote to reference product]']
  population_description varchar [note: 'Description of the analysis population to which this objective applies (e.g., Intent-to-Treat (ITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety Population). Aligns with the estimand population attribute per ICH E9(R1).']
  population_type varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the analysis population associated with this objective. Intent-to-Treat (ITT), modified ITT (mITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety, Pharmacokinetic (PK), or Pharmacodynamic (PD) population. Drives dataset selection in SAS Drug Development CDISC ADaM programming.']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'Section number within the study protocol document where this objective is formally stated (e.g., Section 2.1, Section 3.2). Enables direct navigation to the source text in the protocol and eTMF.']
  regulatory_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this objective is a formal regulatory commitment (e.g., a post-marketing commitment to FDA or EMA, or a condition of approval). Regulatory commitment objectives require heightened change control and reporting.']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'Section number or identifier within the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that addresses the analysis corresponding to this objective. Provides direct traceability from protocol objective to statistical methodology, as required for regulatory submissions.']
  scientific_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for why this objective is scientifically important and how it contributes to the overall study goals. Supports regulatory review, protocol synopsis, and investigator brochure alignment.']
  sdtm_domain_reference varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain(s) that contain the data supporting this objective (e.g., ADAE, ADEFF, ADTTE). Enables automated linkage between protocol objectives and SDTM/ADaM datasets in SAS Drug Development.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this objective within its objective type group (e.g., first primary objective = 1, second primary objective = 2). Governs display order in the protocol, SAP, and CSR sections.']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Concise human-readable label for the objective used in dashboards, reports, and CTMS displays where the full objective text is too lengthy. Typically 10–15 words summarizing the scientific intent.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this objective record was sourced or authored (e.g., Oracle Clinical One study design module, Veeva Vault protocol document). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this objective record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One objective GUID, Veeva Vault document section ID). Enables back-referencing and reconciliation with the system of record.']
  summary_measure varchar [note: 'Statistical summary measure used to quantify the treatment effect for this objective (e.g., difference in means, odds ratio, hazard ratio, response rate difference). Corresponds to the population-level summary attribute of the ICH E9(R1) estimand.']
