  testing_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the hierarchical testing procedure for this objective. Level 1 is tested first; subsequent levels are tested only if the preceding level achieves statistical significance. Implements the gatekeeping strategy defined in the SAP.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area or disease indication to which this objective is aligned (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Supports cross-study analytics and portfolio-level objective benchmarking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this study objective record in the data platform. Required for change tracking, data lineage, and compliance with 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  variable_description varchar [note: 'Description of the variable (outcome measure) used to address this objective, as defined in the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework. Specifies what is measured on each subject to evaluate the treatment effect.']

  Note: 'Formally documents the primary, secondary, and exploratory objectives of a clinical study as stated in the protocol. Captures objective number, objective type (primary, secondary, exploratory), objective text, associated endpoint references, ICH E9(R1) estimand linkage, and protocol version in which the objective was introduced or modified. Provides the scientific rationale traceability from objective to endpoint to SAP.'
}

Table site.investigational_site {
  investigational_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each investigational site record in the clinical research enterprise. Primary key for the master site record referenced by all study-site associations, feasibility assessments, and capacity records.']
  local_lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Sites with local labs must reference the qualified lab_facility for specimen processing, local safety lab results, and site activation checklists. investigational_site.has_local_laboratory is a boolea']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'FK to site.principal_investigator']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigational site was formally activated and authorized to begin enrolling subjects into clinical trials. Marks the completion of all site initiation activities including IRB/IEC approval, regulatory submissions, and staff training.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the investigational site facility. Required for regulatory submissions, site visit logistics, and TMF documentation.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the investigational site (suite, floor, building, department). Supplements the primary address for precise facility location identification.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the investigational site is located. Used for geographic distribution analysis, patient recruitment planning, and regulatory submissions.']
  closeout_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigational site was formally closed out, completing all study-related activities, resolving outstanding data queries, and returning or destroying unused investigational product. Triggers archival obligations per GCP.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the investigational site is located. Determines applicable regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, Health Canada), GCP guidelines, and data privacy regulations (GDPR, HIPAA).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigational site master record was first created in the system of record (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) nine-digit identifier for the investigational site organization. Used for sponsor contracting, grant management, and financial due diligence.']
  edc_system varchar [note: 'Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system deployed at the investigational site for eCRF data entry and clinical data management. Determines site training requirements, data integration pathways, and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance approach.']
  eudract_site_number varchar [note: 'European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) site-level identifier assigned for EU-regulated clinical trials. Required for EMA regulatory submissions and EU CTR 536/2014 compliance reporting.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigational site by facility category, indicating the type of healthcare or research institution. Drives feasibility scoring, site selection criteria, and therapeutic area alignment assessments.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the investigational site, used for transmission of regulatory documents, IRB/IEC correspondence, and sponsor communications where electronic submission is not available.']
  fda_fei_number varchar [note: 'U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) Facility Establishment Identifier (FEI) number assigned to the investigational site. Required for FDA-regulated clinical trials and referenced in IND applications and NDA/BLA submissions.']
  gcp_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigational site\'s current GCP certification expires. Triggers renewal workflow and site eligibility review. Sites with expired GCP certification are ineligible for new study activations.']
  gcp_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current Good Clinical Practice (GCP) certification status of the investigational site, reflecting compliance with ICH E6(R2) training and audit requirements. Sites must maintain active GCP certification to participate in sponsor-funded clinical trials.']
  gcp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current GCP compliance status of the investigational site based on the most recent monitoring visit, audit, or inspection findings. Drives risk-based monitoring intensity, CAPA requirements, and site eligibility for new study assignments.']
  has_dedicated_research_staff boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigational site employs dedicated clinical research staff (e.g., Clinical Research Coordinators (CRCs), research nurses) exclusively for clinical trial conduct, as opposed to relying solely on general clinical staff.']
  has_local_laboratory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigational site has an on-site clinical laboratory certified to process and analyze clinical trial specimens. Relevant for protocol feasibility, sample logistics planning, and central vs. local lab decisions.']
  has_pharmacy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the investigational site has an on-site pharmacy capable of receiving, storing, dispensing, and accounting for investigational medicinal products (IMPs) per GCP and regulatory requirements.']
  iras_number varchar [note: 'UK Integrated Research Application System (IRAS) project identifier assigned to the site for studies conducted under MHRA oversight. Required for UK clinical trial regulatory submissions.']
  irb_iec_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) responsible for ethical oversight of clinical research conducted at this investigational site. Required for regulatory submissions and TMF documentation.']
  irb_iec_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or accreditation number of the IRB/IEC overseeing the investigational site. Required for FDA OHRP registration verification and EU CTR ethics committee identification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality assurance (QA) audit conducted at the investigational site by the sponsor, CRO, or regulatory authority. Used to track audit frequency and compliance with quality management plans.']
  last_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent on-site or remote monitoring visit conducted by a Clinical Research Associate (CRA) at the investigational site. Used to track monitoring frequency compliance and schedule next visit per the monitoring plan.']
  max_enrollment_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of subjects the investigational site can enroll and manage concurrently across all active clinical trials. Used for site capacity planning, feasibility assessments, and enrollment forecasting in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  next_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next monitoring visit at the investigational site, as scheduled in the CTMS monitoring plan. Supports CRA workload planning and ensures compliance with risk-based monitoring visit frequency requirements.']
  number_of_exam_rooms varchar [note: 'Total number of dedicated examination or procedure rooms available at the investigational site for clinical trial subject visits. Informs site capacity assessments and feasibility scoring.']
  open_capa_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently open Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPAs) assigned to the investigational site resulting from monitoring visits, audits, or regulatory inspections. A key GCP compliance KPI tracked in MasterControl.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the investigational site within the clinical research enterprise. Drives site eligibility for new study assignments, monitoring visit scheduling, and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|pending_activation|closed|suspended|in_feasibility — promote to reference product]']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the investigational site facility. Used for CRA monitoring visit coordination, sponsor communications, and emergency contact during study conduct.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the investigational site. Used for geographic catchment area analysis, patient recruitment radius calculations, and regulatory address verification.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor- or CRO-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the investigational site within the clinical trial management system (CTMS). Used as the operational business key across Medidata Rave, Oracle Siebel CTMS, and Oracle Clinical One for site cross-referencing.']
  site_email varchar [note: 'Primary institutional email address for the investigational site. Used for sponsor and CRO communications, regulatory correspondence, and eTMF document distribution.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Official legal or operating name of the investigational site facility (e.g., hospital, clinic, research center). Used for regulatory submissions, TMF documentation, and sponsor communications.']
  site_tier varchar [note: 'Sponsor or CRO-assigned tier classification reflecting the site\'s strategic importance, historical performance, enrollment capacity, and GCP compliance track record. Tier 1 represents highest-performing strategic sites; Tier 4 represents new or lower-performing sites.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier for this investigational site record in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Oracle Siebel CTMS site ID). Enables traceability from the Silver Layer lakehouse record back to the source system for lineage and reconciliation.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the investigational site is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, IRB/IEC assignment, and geographic enrollment analytics.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the investigational site location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling monitoring visits, adverse event reporting timestamps, and cross-regional data reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the investigational site master record. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and ALCOA+ audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']

  Note: 'Master record for each investigational site participating in clinical trials. Captures site identity, physical address, facility type, therapeutic area specializations, GCP certification status, regulatory registration numbers (e.g., FDA FEI, DUNS), site tier classification, and operational status. This is the SSOT for site identity and profile across the clinical research enterprise, referenced by all study-site associations, feasibility assessments, and capacity records.'
}

Table site.site_contact {
  site_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each site contact record within the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Primary key for the site_contact entity.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site with which this contact is associated. Links the contact to the parent site record in the CTMS.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: site_contact tracks all personnel at an investigational site, including Principal Investigators (is_pi: BOOLEAN flag). When a contact record represents a PI, linking it to the principal_investigator m']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Site contact CVs, GCP certificates, and delegation log entries are TMF essential documents per ICH E6 R2. site_contact has denormalized cv_document_ref and delegation_log_ref plain attributes; replaci']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site contact record within the CTMS staff roster. Drives access provisioning, delegation log validity, and monitoring visit eligibility.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the site contact is based. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, GDPR/HIPAA applicability, and multi-regional trial management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the site contact record was first created in the source CTMS system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  ctms_contact_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this contact as assigned by the source Clinical Trial Management System (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  ctms_user_code varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier assigned to the site contact within the CTMS (Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports access control, audit trail, and activity tracking for site staff within the trial management platform.']
  delegation_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the site contact\'s delegation of authority for trial tasks was revoked or expired. Null if delegation is currently active. Used for audit trail and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  delegation_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the site contact is authorized to perform delegated trial tasks as documented in the site delegation of authority log. Defines the effective start of the contact\'s trial responsibilities.']
  department varchar [note: 'Institutional department or clinical unit within the investigational site where the contact is based (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Clinical Research Unit). Supports site staff organization and monitoring visit planning.']
  edc_system_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site contact has been provisioned with access to the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave) for electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) data entry or review.']
  edc_user_code varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier assigned to the site contact within the EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave). Required for audit trail attribution of eCRF data entries per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary professional email address of the site contact. Used for study communications, monitoring visit notifications, and eTMF correspondence.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the site contact\'s association with the investigational site was terminated or concluded. Null if the contact is currently active. Used for staff roster management and audit trail.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number associated with the site contact, used for regulatory document transmission and IRB/IEC correspondence where electronic submission is not available.']
  financial_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the site contact submitted their financial disclosure form to the sponsor. Required for regulatory compliance tracking and NDA/BLA submission readiness.']
  financial_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Status of the site contact\'s financial disclosure form submission, required by FDA regulations for investigators to disclose financial interests that may affect study integrity. Mandatory for NDA/BLA submissions.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given (first) name of the site contact individual. Used for staff roster management, correspondence, and delegation log identification.']
  gcp_training_date date [note: 'Date on which the site contact most recently completed GCP training. Used to determine training currency and trigger renewal reminders in compliance with ICH E6(R2) requirements.']
  gcp_training_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the site contact\'s current GCP training certification expires. Drives compliance alerts and site staff qualification monitoring within the CTMS and document management systems.']
  gcp_training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or platform that delivered the GCP training (e.g., CITI Program, TransCelerate, sponsor-provided training). Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  gcp_training_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the site contact\'s Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training certification. GCP training is a mandatory regulatory requirement for all site staff involved in clinical trial conduct per ICH E6(R2).']
  human_subjects_training_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the site contact\'s human subjects research protection training certification. Drives compliance monitoring and renewal alerts.']
  human_subjects_training_status varchar [note: 'Status of the site contact\'s human subjects research protection training (e.g., CITI Human Subjects training). Required by OHRP and IRB regulations for all personnel involved in human subjects research.']
  irb_iec_approval_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the IRB/IEC approval document that covers this site contact\'s participation in the trial, particularly relevant for PI and Sub-Investigator roles. Links to the eTMF approval record.']
  is_pi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this site contact holds the role of Principal Investigator (PI) for the study at this site. The PI bears overall responsibility for the conduct of the clinical trial at the site per GCP guidelines.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this individual is the designated primary contact for the investigational site. The primary contact receives all official study communications, monitoring visit notifications, and regulatory correspondence.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language of the site contact for study communications, expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, de-DE). Supports localized communication in multinational trials.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family (last) name of the site contact individual. Used in combination with first_name for full identification on delegation logs, monitoring reports, and regulatory submissions.']
  license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the site contact\'s professional medical or pharmacy license. Triggers renewal alerts to ensure continued regulatory compliance and site qualification validity.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Professional medical or pharmacy license number of the site contact. Required for PI and Sub-Investigator qualification verification and regulatory submission documentation.']
  license_state varchar [note: 'State or jurisdiction in which the site contact holds their professional medical or pharmacy license. Used for regulatory compliance verification during site qualification.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number of the site contact for after-hours urgent communications, including SAE reporting and emergency site escalations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional contextual information about the site contact, such as special qualifications, communication preferences, or operational notes captured by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) during site monitoring.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary professional phone number of the site contact. Used for urgent safety communications, monitoring visit coordination, and SAE follow-up.']
  professional_title varchar [note: 'Professional credential or academic title of the site contact (e.g., MD, PhD, PharmD, RN). Required for delegation log entries and regulatory qualification documentation per GCP guidelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|PhD|PharmD|RN|NP|PA|CRC|MSc|DO|RPh — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Functional role of the contact at the investigational site. Determines delegation authority, GCP training requirements, and access rights within the CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PI|Sub-Investigator|CRC|Pharmacist|Lab Contact|Study Coordinator|Regulatory Contact|Data Entry Operator|Site Manager|Nurse — promote to reference product]']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Medical or scientific specialty of the site contact (e.g., Oncology, Neurology, Cardiology). Relevant for Principal Investigator (PI) and Sub-Investigator qualification assessments during site feasibility and selection.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the site contact was first associated with the investigational site for clinical trial activities. Marks the beginning of the contact\'s active involvement in the study at this site.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the termination or deactivation of the site contact\'s association with the site. Required for audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness, particularly for for-cause terminations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when the site contact record was most recently modified in the source CTMS system. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Tracks all personnel contacts associated with an investigational site, including Principal Investigators (PI), Sub-Investigators, Clinical Research Coordinators (CRC), pharmacists, and lab contacts. Captures role, contact details, GCP training status, delegation log reference, and active/inactive status. Supports site staff roster management within CTMS.'
}

Table site.principal_investigator {
  principal_investigator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Principal Investigator (PI) master record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins and regulatory traceability.']
  board_certification varchar [note: 'Name of the specialty board certification held by the PI (e.g., American Board of Internal Medicine - Oncology). Validates clinical expertise required by the study protocol.']
  board_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI\'s board certification expires. Monitored to ensure ongoing qualification compliance during study conduct.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the PI master record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  ctms_investigator_code varchar [note: 'Source system investigator identifier from Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CTMS.']
  cv_version_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent version of the PI\'s curriculum vitae (CV) on file. The CV is a required TMF document demonstrating the PI\'s qualifications per ICH E6(R2).']
  debarment_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent FDA debarment check was performed for this PI. Periodic re-checks are required per sponsor SOPs.']
  debarment_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the FDA debarment check confirming the PI is not listed on the FDA Debarment List under 21 CFR Part 312.70. Mandatory pre-study qualification step.']
  delegation_log_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PI\'s delegation of authority log is current and up-to-date in the eTMF. A delegation log documents tasks delegated to sub-investigators and CRCs per ICH E6(R2).']
  disqualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI was disqualified from conducting clinical trials, either by a regulatory authority or by the sponsor. Nullable for active investigators.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for PI disqualification, if applicable. Populated only when lifecycle_status is \'disqualified\'. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary professional email address for the PI used for regulatory correspondence, system access provisioning, and CTMS notifications.']
  eudract_investigator_number varchar [note: 'European Clinical Trials Database (EudraCT / CTIS) identifier assigned to the PI for EU-based clinical trial registrations under EU CTR 536/2014.']
  fda_investigator_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned investigator identifier, if applicable, used in IND applications and regulatory submissions to uniquely identify the PI in FDA systems.']
  financial_disclosure_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI\'s financial disclosure form (FDA Form 3454 or 3455) was most recently submitted. Required for NDA/BLA/MAA regulatory submissions.']
  financial_disclosure_status varchar [note: 'Status of the PI\'s financial disclosure filing (FDA Form 3454/3455) required under 21 CFR Part 54. Discloses financial interests that could affect study objectivity.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal given name of the Principal Investigator as it appears on medical licensure and FDA Form 1572.']
  form_1572_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent FDA Form 1572 was signed and submitted for this PI. Used for regulatory compliance tracking and TMF completeness verification.']
  form_1572_status varchar [note: 'Current submission status of the FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator) for this PI. The Form 1572 is a regulatory commitment by the PI to conduct the study per GCP and the approved protocol.']
  gcp_training_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI most recently completed ICH Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training. Required for regulatory compliance and site initiation visit (SIV) clearance.']
  gcp_training_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI\'s current GCP training certification expires. Triggers re-training workflow in MasterControl to maintain continuous compliance.']
  gcp_training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or platform that delivered the GCP training (e.g., CITI Program, TransCelerate, NIH). Used for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  inspection_history_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PI has been subject to a prior FDA, EMA, MHRA, or other regulatory authority inspection. Used in risk-based site selection and monitoring planning.']
  institution_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the PI\'s primary institution is located. Determines applicable regulatory framework (FDA, EMA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada, MHRA).']
  institution_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary academic or medical institution with which the PI is affiliated (e.g., Johns Hopkins Hospital, Mayo Clinic). Used for site identification and regulatory submissions.']
  irb_iec_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current IRB/IEC approval status for the PI at their primary investigational site. Tracks whether the PI is authorized to conduct human subjects research under the approved protocol.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, or Health Canada) at a site where this PI was the Principal Investigator.']
  last_inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome classification of the most recent regulatory inspection involving this PI. FDA classifications: No Action Indicated (NAI), Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI), Official Action Indicated (OAI).']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal family name of the Principal Investigator as it appears on medical licensure and FDA Form 1572.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the PI within the organization\'s investigator registry. Drives eligibility for new study assignments and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|inactive|suspended|pending_qualification|disqualified|retired — promote to reference product]']
  medical_license_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI\'s medical license expires. Triggers renewal alerts in CTMS to ensure continuous licensure compliance throughout study conduct.']
  medical_license_number varchar [note: 'State or national medical license number issued to the PI by the relevant medical licensing authority. Required for FDA Form 1572 and site qualification documentation.']
  medical_license_state varchar [note: 'Two-letter U.S. state code (or equivalent jurisdiction code) where the PI\'s medical license is issued and active. Used to validate practice authority at the investigational site.']
  npi_number varchar [note: '10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) assigned by CMS to the PI. Used for U.S.-based regulatory submissions, billing reconciliation, and provider identity verification.']
  orcid_number varchar [note: 'Globally unique ORCID identifier for the PI, enabling unambiguous researcher identification across publications, regulatory submissions, and clinical trial registries.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary professional contact phone number for the PI, used for urgent safety communications and site monitoring coordination.']
  pi_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigator role within the study hierarchy. Distinguishes the PI with full regulatory accountability from sub-investigators and coordinating investigators. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: principal_investigator|sub_investigator|coordinating_investigator|national_coordinating_investigator — promote to reference product]']
  prior_study_count varchar [note: 'Total number of clinical studies the PI has previously conducted as Principal Investigator. Used as a key metric in site feasibility assessments and sponsor qualification reviews.']
  professional_title varchar [note: 'Academic or professional degree designation of the PI (e.g., MD, PhD, PharmD). Used to verify minimum qualification requirements per protocol and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MD|DO|PhD|PharmD|MD PhD|MBBS|DVM|NP|PA — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  protocol_training_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the PI has completed all required protocol-specific training for their currently assigned studies. Managed via MasterControl training management module.']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the PI was formally qualified and approved by the sponsor or CRO to conduct clinical trials. Marks the start of the PI\'s active lifecycle in the investigator registry.']
  specialty varchar [note: 'Primary medical or scientific specialty of the PI (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for protocol feasibility matching and site selection scoring.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the PI master record. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total number of years the PI has been conducting clinical trials. Used in site feasibility scoring and sponsor qualification assessments.']

  Note: 'Master record for Principal Investigators (PIs) conducting clinical trials. Captures PI credentials, medical license numbers, specialty, board certifications, GCP training history, FDA Form 1572 submission status, financial disclosure status, debarment check results, and professional affiliations. Distinct from site_contact as PIs have regulatory accountability and their own lifecycle across multiple studies and sites.'
}

Table site.feasibility_assessment {
  feasibility_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the site feasibility assessment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: In multi-arm and dose-escalation studies, feasibility assessments evaluate a site\'s capacity for specific arms (e.g., does the site have PK sampling capability for the high-dose arm?). Arm-specific fe']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site being evaluated for feasibility.']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Feasibility assessments formally evaluate a specific lab facility\'s capability (lab_capabilities_available flag). Sponsors require the assessed lab_facility identity for lab qualification decisions, c']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) proposed for this site and study during the feasibility evaluation.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Feasibility assessments evaluate a site against a specific protocol version — protocol complexity, visit burden, and eligibility criteria drive feasibility scoring. Regulatory and operational reports ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this feasibility assessment is being conducted.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Feasibility questionnaires and assessment reports are TMF artifacts (DIA Zone 2, pre-study documentation). Linking feasibility_assessment to its TMF document enables TMF completeness tracking and supp']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the feasibility assessment questionnaire was initiated or formally submitted by the site. Principal business event date for this transaction.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the feasibility assessment, used in CTMS and sponsor communications (e.g., FA-2024-0042). Unique within a study.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the feasibility assessment workflow within the CTMS. Tracks progression from initial draft through sponsor review to final decision.']
  competing_trial_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently active or planned clinical trials at the site that compete for the same patient population or therapeutic area. High competing trial load may reduce enrollment capacity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this feasibility assessment record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail requirements under GCP and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor or CRO made the final site selection decision (selected, not selected, or on hold) based on this feasibility assessment.']
  dedicated_research_staff_count varchar [note: 'Number of dedicated clinical research staff (Clinical Research Coordinators (CRCs), research nurses, data entry staff) available at the site to support this study.']
  edc_access_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has the necessary IT infrastructure, internet connectivity, and hardware to access the study\'s Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave).']
  enrollment_experience_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score component reflecting the site\'s historical enrollment performance, patient population access, and therapeutic area experience. Contributes to the composite feasibility score.']
  estimated_enrollment_capacity varchar [note: 'The site\'s self-reported estimate of the number of eligible subjects it can enroll for this specific study over the planned enrollment period. Core metric for site selection decisions.']
  estimated_screen_failure_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Site\'s estimated percentage of screened subjects expected to fail eligibility criteria for this study. Used to adjust enrollment projections and calculate required screening numbers.']
  feasibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score (typically 0–100) assigned to the site based on weighted evaluation of all feasibility criteria including enrollment potential, infrastructure, and experience. Used for comparative site ranking.']
  feasibility_score_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical tier classification derived from the feasibility score (e.g., High ≥ 75, Medium 50–74, Low < 50). Used for rapid site prioritization and sponsor reporting.']
  gcp_training_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site\'s Principal Investigator and key study staff hold current GCP training certifications as required by ICH E6(R2) and applicable regulatory authorities.']
  imaging_capabilities_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has the required imaging infrastructure (e.g., MRI, CT, PET) specified in the study protocol for efficacy or safety assessments.']
  irb_iec_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the site\'s Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) relationship at the time of feasibility assessment. Active IRB/IEC is a prerequisite for site activation.']
  lab_capabilities_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has on-site or contracted laboratory capabilities required by the study protocol (e.g., local lab processing, sample storage, cold chain management).']
  patient_population_size varchar [note: 'Estimated number of patients in the site\'s catchment area who match the study\'s target indication and could potentially be eligible for enrollment.']
  pharmacy_capabilities_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has an investigational drug pharmacy with appropriate storage, dispensing, and accountability capabilities as required by the study protocol.']
  phase varchar [note: 'The regulatory phase of the clinical trial (Phase I through Phase IV) for which the site is being assessed. Influences the complexity and infrastructure requirements evaluated.']
  prior_study_experience_count varchar [note: 'Number of prior clinical studies the site has conducted in the same therapeutic area or indication. Reflects site expertise and GCP compliance track record.']
  questionnaire_response_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional site comments, clarifications, or qualitative responses provided by the site during the feasibility questionnaire completion.']
  questionnaire_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the feasibility questionnaire template used for this assessment (e.g., v2.1). Ensures traceability of assessment criteria across protocol amendments.']
  regulatory_compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score component reflecting the site\'s GCP compliance history, inspection findings, audit outcomes, and regulatory standing. Derived from CTMS compliance tracking data.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sponsor or CRO representative who reviewed and approved the feasibility assessment and made the site selection decision.']
  selection_decision varchar [note: 'Final site selection outcome derived from this feasibility assessment. Indicates whether the site was selected, not selected, placed on hold, or awaiting decision.']
  site_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the investigational site is located. Used for regional enrollment planning and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  site_infrastructure_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score component of the overall feasibility score reflecting the site\'s physical and technical infrastructure readiness (EDC, lab, pharmacy, imaging). Scored on a standardized scale.']
  site_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification of the investigational site (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific). Used for regional enrollment tracking and sponsor reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this feasibility assessment record was sourced (Oracle Clinical One or Oracle Siebel CTMS).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this feasibility assessment in the originating source system (Oracle Clinical One or Oracle Siebel CTMS). Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation.']
  sponsor_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments entered by the sponsor or CRO reviewer following evaluation of the feasibility questionnaire. Documents rationale for selection decisions or follow-up requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the completed feasibility questionnaire was formally submitted by the site to the sponsor or CRO for review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this feasibility assessment record. Supports audit trail and data integrity requirements under GCP and 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Records the site feasibility evaluation conducted prior to site selection for a specific study. Captures feasibility questionnaire responses, estimated enrollment capacity, patient population availability, competing trial load, infrastructure readiness (EDC access, lab capabilities, pharmacy), therapeutic area experience, and feasibility score. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and CTMS feasibility workflows.'
}

Table site.selection {
  selection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the site selection decision record. Primary key for the site_selection data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the site activation record that was initiated as a result of this selection decision. Null until the site transitions from selected to activation workflow. Establishes the selection-to-activation traceability chain.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: In adaptive and multi-arm trials, site selection decisions are arm-specific: a site may be selected for the control arm but not the experimental arm based on infrastructure. Operational site selection']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Site selection decisions are made within the regulatory framework of the CTA for each country. Linking selection to the CTA enables country-level enrollment planning against CTA-approved subject count']
  feasibility_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior feasibility assessment record that informed this site selection decision, establishing traceability from feasibility to selection.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site being evaluated and selected or rejected for participation in the study.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) proposed or confirmed for this study-site pairing at the time of selection. The PI\'s qualifications are a key selection criterion per GCP.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Site selection decisions are made against a specific protocol version; protocol_version is a plain-text denormalization. Regulatory audit trails and TMF require traceability of which protocol version ']
  sample_size_calc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sample_size_calc. Business justification: Site selection assigns target_enrollment_count and enrollment_rate_per_month to each site based on the study\'s sample size calculation. A direct FK enables audit trail showing that per-site enrollment']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) for which this site selection decision was made. Links the selection event to the governing protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: site_selection records the formal site selection decision for a study-site pairing. study_site is the canonical association entity representing the full lifecycle of that pairing. The selection decisi']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Site selection documentation (selection rationale, committee decisions) is a TMF artifact (DIA Zone 2). The existing tmf_document_reference is a plain string. A proper FK enables TMF completeness trac']
  activation_target_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the selected site should complete all activation requirements and be ready to enroll the first subject. Used for study timeline management and milestone tracking.']
  competing_trial_count varchar [note: 'Number of other active clinical trials running concurrently at this site at the time of selection. Used to assess site capacity and potential enrollment competition.']
  conditional_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of conditions that must be met before a conditionally selected site can proceed to activation (e.g., additional staff training, equipment procurement, IRB approval). Populated when selection_status is \'conditionally_selected\'.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the investigational site is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination and multi-regional trial management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site selection record was first created in the source system. Used for audit trail and data lineage per ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctms_site_record_code varchar [note: 'The source system record identifier from Oracle Siebel CTMS for this site selection record, enabling traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational CTMS.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the site selection decision was formally recorded in the CTMS system, supporting 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  enrollment_rate_per_month decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected number of subjects the site is expected to enroll per calendar month, based on feasibility assessment data. Used for study timeline planning and site performance forecasting.']
  feasibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score derived from the feasibility assessment, summarizing the site\'s overall suitability for the study. Higher scores indicate greater suitability. Used as a key input to the selection decision.']
  first_patient_in_target_date date [note: 'Target date for enrolling the first subject (First Patient In / FPI) at this site. A key study milestone used for enrollment planning and sponsor KPI reporting.']
  gcp_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has been confirmed as GCP-compliant at the time of selection, based on prior audit history, inspection records, and training verification.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) granted approval for the study at this site. Null if approval has not yet been obtained at time of selection.']
  irb_iec_status varchar [note: 'Status of the IRB (Institutional Review Board) or IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approval at the time of site selection. Tracks whether ethics oversight is in place or pending.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this site selection record. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or contextual information recorded by the selection committee or CRA that do not fit into structured fields. Supports comprehensive documentation per ALCOA+ principles.']
  patient_population_size varchar [note: 'Estimated number of eligible patients in the site\'s catchment area who meet the study\'s inclusion/exclusion criteria, as assessed during feasibility. A primary driver of the selection decision.']
  pi_qualification_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Principal Investigator\'s (PI) qualifications, CV, and GCP training have been verified and confirmed as adequate for this study at the time of selection, per ICH E6(R2) requirements.']
  prior_audit_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site has any prior significant audit findings or regulatory inspection observations that were considered as part of the selection decision. Supports risk-based site selection.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for the site selection decision, documenting the business and scientific reasons the site was chosen or rejected. Required for TMF documentation and sponsor oversight.']
  region varchar [note: 'Sponsor-defined geographic region grouping for the site (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific). Used for regional enrollment tracking, CRA assignment, and operational reporting.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory submission (e.g., IND amendment, CTA, or national competent authority notification) is required before this site can be activated. Drives regulatory workflow initiation.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason a site was not selected. Populated only when selection_status is \'rejected\' or \'withdrawn\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_patient_population|inadequate_facilities|pi_not_qualified|competing_trials|irb_issues|capacity_constraints|sponsor_decision|other — promote to reference product]']
  rejection_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text elaboration of the rejection reason, providing additional context beyond the standardized rejection_reason_code. Populated only when the site is rejected or withdrawn.']
  selection_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the formal site selection decision was made and approved by the selection committee. This is the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  selection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this site selection decision record, used in CTMS, eTMF, and sponsor communications (e.g., SS-PROT001-00042). Serves as the human-readable reference number.']
  selection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site selection decision. Drives workflow transitions from feasibility through activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: selected|rejected|pending_review|conditionally_selected|withdrawn|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  selection_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the site selection: whether the site is a newly added site, a replacement for a terminated site, a backup site held in reserve, or an expansion site added to increase enrollment capacity.']
  sponsor_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor formally approved the site selection decision. Null if sponsor approval is not required or has not yet been received.']
  sponsor_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor must provide explicit written approval before this site selection decision is finalized. Relevant for CRO-managed studies where sponsor sign-off is a contractual requirement.']
  sponsor_site_number varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the site within the context of this specific study, used in EDC, RTSM, and regulatory submissions. Distinct from the internal site_id.']
  target_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'The planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site as agreed at the time of selection. Used for enrollment planning, site performance benchmarking, and resource allocation.']
  visit_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal site selection visit (also known as a pre-study site visit or PSSV) was conducted at this site prior to the selection decision, as recommended by ICH E6(R2).']
  visit_date date [note: 'Date on which the site selection visit (pre-study site visit / PSSV) was conducted. Null if no visit was conducted or if the visit is pending.']

  Note: 'Records the formal site selection decision for a study-site pairing, including selection rationale, selection committee approval, selection date, rejection reasons (if applicable), and the transition from feasibility to activation. Captures the business decision event that moves a site from candidate to selected status for a given protocol.'
}

Table site.study_site {
  study_site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the study-site association record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: study_site tracks regulatory_approval_date as a denormalized field but lacks a FK to the formal approval record. Linking to approval enables regulatory status rollup across sites, supports inspection ']
  central_lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Each study_site is assigned a central laboratory for specimen analysis. This drives specimen shipment routing, lab kit ordering, result reporting, and eTMF documentation. Role-prefixed \'central_\' beca']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Each study_site in EU member states is governed by a specific CTA for that country. Regulatory affairs and site management teams track the CTA covering each study_site for compliance monitoring, enrol']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site master record. Links to the site entity representing the physical or organizational research location.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) assigned to lead clinical research activities at this site for this study. The PI is responsible for the conduct of the trial per ICH E6(R2) GCP.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Study sites are activated under a specific protocol version; regulatory submissions, IRB approvals, and site agreements all reference the governing protocol. protocol_number on study_site is a denorma']
  study_id varchar [note: 'FK to study.study.study_id — Critical for study-site relationship — study_site is the association entity linking studies to sites, must have direct FK to study for enrollment tracking and site activation workflows.']
  budget_agreed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total agreed budget amount for this site\'s participation in the study, as specified in the Clinical Trial Agreement. Covers per-patient fees, overhead, and procedure costs. Managed in SAP S/4HANA project accounting.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the agreed site budget amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and reconciliation in SAP S/4HANA.']
  capacity_subjects varchar [note: 'Maximum number of subjects the site has declared capacity to enroll and manage concurrently for this study, based on staff resources, facilities, and patient population. Used in feasibility assessment and enrollment planning.']
  cda_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the Confidential Disclosure Agreement (CDA) between the sponsor/CRO and the site was fully executed. A prerequisite milestone in the site activation workflow before protocol sharing.']
  contract_execution_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) or Clinical Site Agreement between the sponsor/CRO and the site institution was fully executed. A mandatory activation milestone before site initiation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this study-site association record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  drug_shipment_auth_date date [note: 'Date on which authorization was granted to ship investigational medicinal product (IMP) to this site. A critical activation milestone managed via RTSM/IRT (e.g., Medidata RTSM). Shipment cannot proceed without IRB approval and regulatory clearance.']
  edc_provisioning_date date [note: 'Date on which the site was provisioned and granted access to the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave) for this study. Required before site staff can enter subject data into eCRFs.']
  enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of subjects who have been enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) at this site to date. Updated from EDC/RTSM data feeds. Used for real-time enrollment performance monitoring.']
  enrollment_close_date date [note: 'Date on which enrollment of new subjects at this site was formally closed, either due to target achievement, sponsor decision, or protocol amendment. Marks transition to \'enrollment_complete\' status.']
  enrollment_open_date date [note: 'Date on which the site was authorized to begin enrolling subjects into the study. Marks the transition to \'enrolling\' status. Used for site performance analytics and FPFV (First Patient First Visit) tracking.']
  enrollment_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of subjects to be enrolled at this site for this study, as agreed between the sponsor/CRO and the site during feasibility and site selection. Used for site performance tracking and enrollment forecasting.']
  fpfv_date date [note: 'Date of the first subject\'s first protocol-specified visit at this site. A critical KPI milestone in clinical trial management used to measure site activation speed and study startup performance.']
  gcp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current GCP compliance status of the site for this study based on monitoring visit findings, audit results, and CAPA resolution. Drives risk-based monitoring intensity and escalation decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: compliant|minor_findings|major_findings|critical_findings|suspended|not_assessed — promote to reference product]']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the IRB or IEC granted initial approval for the study protocol at this site. Milestone tracked in CTMS and eTMF for regulatory compliance.']
  irb_iec_approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval reference number issued by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) authorizing the conduct of this study at this site. Required before any subject enrollment can begin.']
  irb_iec_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current IRB/IEC approval expires. Sites must obtain renewal before this date to continue enrollment. Triggers automated alerts in CTMS for renewal tracking.']
  last_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent monitoring visit (on-site or remote) conducted at this site for this study. Used to calculate time since last visit and trigger overdue monitoring alerts in CTMS.']
  lplv_date date [note: 'Date of the last subject\'s last protocol-specified visit at this site. Marks the end of active subject participation and triggers site close-out activities. Key milestone for study timeline management.']
  monitoring_visit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of monitoring visits (on-site and remote) conducted at this site for this study to date. Used for site oversight reporting and resource planning.']
  next_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next monitoring visit at this site for this study. Scheduled in CTMS based on risk tier, enrollment activity, and monitoring plan requirements.']
  open_query_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently open data queries (discrepancies or clarification requests) raised against eCRF data submitted by this site in the EDC system. A key data quality KPI for site performance management.']
  protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of protocol deviations recorded at this site for this study. Includes both minor and major deviations. Used for site quality oversight, regulatory reporting, and CAPA management.']
  regulatory_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the required regulatory submission (e.g., IND amendment, national competent authority notification) was made for this site to participate in the study. Applicable for sites in jurisdictions requiring site-level regulatory approval.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason why a candidate site was not selected or was subsequently withdrawn from the study. Populated only when site_status transitions to a rejected or withdrawn state. Supports feasibility learning and sponsor reporting.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk-based monitoring tier assigned to this site for this study, reflecting overall site risk profile based on enrollment performance, GCP compliance history, data quality, and PI experience. Determines monitoring visit frequency and SDV scope.']
  sae_count varchar [note: 'Total number of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) reported from subjects enrolled at this site for this study. Used for safety signal monitoring and site-level safety oversight. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.']
  screen_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who were screened but did not meet eligibility criteria and were not enrolled. Used to calculate screen failure rate, a key site performance metric for feasibility planning.']
  screened_count varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects who have undergone screening procedures at this site, regardless of eligibility outcome. Used to calculate screen failure rate and assess site recruitment efficiency.']
  selection_date date [note: 'Date on which the site was formally selected by the sponsor or CRO for participation in the study, following feasibility assessment and committee review.']
  selection_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for selecting this site for the study, including patient population access, PI expertise, infrastructure capability, and prior performance. Captured during the site selection committee review.']
  site_closeout_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal site close-out visit was completed and the site was officially closed for this study. Triggers archival of site documents in eTMF and final reconciliation activities.']
  site_number varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric site number unique within the study protocol. Used in EDC, RTSM, and regulatory submissions to identify the site within the trial context. Typically formatted as a 3–10 character code (e.g., \'US001\', \'DE002\').']
  site_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site within this study. Drives operational workflows in CTMS: candidate (under feasibility review), selected (approved for activation), initiated (Site Initiation Visit completed), enrolling (actively recruiting subjects), enrollment_complete (target met or enrollment closed), closed (site formally closed out).']
  siv_date date [note: 'Date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was conducted by the CRA. The SIV confirms site readiness, trains site staff on the protocol and EDC system, and is a key activation milestone before first subject enrollment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this study-site association record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  withdrawn_count varchar [note: 'Number of enrolled subjects who withdrew consent or were discontinued from the study at this site. Tracked for safety reporting, ITT/PP population analysis, and site performance assessment.']

  Note: 'Association entity representing the full lifecycle relationship between a specific study (protocol) and an investigational site, from selection through activation to closeout. Captures site selection decision (rationale, committee approval, rejection reasons), activation workflow milestones (CDA execution, IRB submission, SIV scheduling, drug shipment authorization, EDC provisioning), site number assignment, enrollment targets, enrollment open/close dates, site status (candidate, selected, initiated, enrolling, enrollment complete, closed), and FPFV date. This is the operational hub for all study-site activity and the primary entity for site lifecycle management.'
}

  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the site activation workflow. Tracks overall progress from initiation through full activation or termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|activated|on_hold|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  activation_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the activation workflow — whether this is a new site being onboarded, a previously closed site being re-activated, a replacement for a terminated site, or an expansion site added mid-study.']
  actual_activation_date date [note: 'The date on which the site was formally activated and cleared to enroll subjects. Corresponds to the completion of all mandatory activation milestones.']

  cda_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Confidentiality Disclosure Agreement (CDA) milestone within the site activation workflow.']

  days_elapsed varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days elapsed from the initiation of the activation workflow (CDA execution or site selection confirmation) to the current date or actual activation date. Used as a key performance indicator (KPI) for activation cycle time.']
  drug_shipment_auth_date date [note: 'The date on which authorization was granted to ship investigational medicinal product (IMP) to the site. Requires completion of all prerequisite activation milestones including IRB/IEC approval and investigator agreement execution.']
  drug_shipment_auth_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) shipment authorization milestone for this site activation.']
  edc_access_provisioned_date date [note: 'The date on which site staff were granted access to the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (e.g., Medidata Rave) for this study, enabling electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) data entry.']
  edc_access_status varchar [note: 'Current status of EDC system access provisioning for site staff as part of the site activation workflow.']
