  financial_agreement_executed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site budget and payment agreement (Clinical Trial Agreement financial schedule) has been fully executed between the sponsor/CRO and the site institution, a prerequisite for site activation.']
  fpfv_date date [note: 'The date on which the first subject at this site completed their first protocol-mandated visit. A key clinical operations milestone marking the start of active enrollment at the site.']
  gcp_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site staff (PI and CRC) have completed mandatory Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training as required prior to site activation and subject enrollment.']
  investigator_agreement_date date [note: 'The date on which the Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) or Investigator Agreement was fully executed between the sponsor/CRO and the Principal Investigator (PI) or site institution.']
  investigator_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Investigator Agreement / Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) milestone in the site activation workflow.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the IRB or IEC granted formal approval for the study to proceed at this site. A mandatory regulatory milestone before subject enrollment can begin.']
  irb_iec_approval_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current IRB/IEC approval expires and renewal is required to continue subject enrollment at the site.']
  irb_iec_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the IRB/IEC review and approval milestone for this site activation. Tracks the ethical review lifecycle from submission through approval or rejection.']
  irb_iec_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the study protocol and informed consent documents were submitted to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) for ethical review and approval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing qualitative notes, issues, or comments related to the site activation workflow, including reasons for delays, hold conditions, or special circumstances noted by the CRA or project manager.']
  on_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Describes the reason the site activation workflow has been placed on hold, if applicable. Populated when activation_status is \'on_hold\'. Examples include sponsor decision, regulatory query, or site resource unavailability.']
  planned_enrollment_target varchar [note: 'The number of subjects the site is expected to enroll in the study as agreed during site selection and activation planning. Used for capacity planning and performance tracking.']
  protocol_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the investigational site confirmed receipt of the study protocol and related documents from the sponsor or CRO.']
  protocol_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the protocol receipt milestone in the site activation workflow, indicating whether the site has confirmed receipt of the study protocol.']
  regulatory_pkg_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority granted approval or clearance for the regulatory submission package, permitting the study to proceed at this site.']
  regulatory_pkg_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory package submission milestone in the site activation workflow.']
  regulatory_pkg_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory submission package (including essential documents such as investigator brochure, protocol, and site-specific regulatory forms) was submitted to the applicable health authority.']
  siv_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) was successfully completed. Completion of the SIV is a prerequisite for site activation and subject enrollment.']
  siv_scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned date for the Site Initiation Visit (SIV), during which the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) trains site staff on the protocol, EDC system, and GCP requirements.']
  siv_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) milestone within the site activation workflow.']
  target_activation_date date [note: 'The planned date by which the site is expected to be fully activated and ready to enroll the first patient. Used for milestone tracking and timeline management in CTMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this site activation record was last modified. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under ICH E6(R2) GCP, ALCOA+ principles, and FDA 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Tracks the end-to-end site activation workflow milestones from site selection through first patient first visit (FPFV). Captures completion dates and status for each activation task: confidentiality agreement execution, protocol receipt, IRB/IEC submission, regulatory package submission, site initiation visit (SIV) scheduling, investigator agreement execution, drug shipment authorization, and EDC access provisioning. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS activation tracker.'
}

Table site.irb_iec_approval {
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each IRB/IEC approval record within the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: EU/EEA trials require a valid CTA before IRB/IEC approval is actionable for site activation. Regulatory affairs teams track which CTA governs each site\'s ethics approval for compliance reporting and i']
  ethics_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_approval. Business justification: The site.irb_iec_approval (CTMS-recorded local approval) and compliance.ethics_approval (QMS formal approval record) represent the same regulatory event from different system perspectives. Linking the']
  ethics_committee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approving ethics committee (IRB or IEC) master record. The committee master data is owned by the ethics domain.']
  ethics_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_submission. Business justification: An IRB/IEC approval is the outcome of an ethics submission. Linking the approval record to the originating submission enables end-to-end ethics workflow traceability (submission → approval), supports ']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site for which this IRB/IEC approval applies. Each approval is specific to a study-site combination.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: IRB/IEC approvals in clinical trials are PI-specific — the approval names the PI who is authorized to conduct the study at the site. The principal_investigator record tracks irb_iec_approval_status an']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: IRB/IEC approvals are granted for a specific protocol version — a core regulatory requirement (ICH E6 GCP). approved_protocol_version and approved_protocol_date are denormalized; a proper FK to protoc']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: IRB/IEC approval documents are mandatory site file contents per ICH E6 R2. Linking irb_iec_approval to site_file enables site file completeness checks to verify the approval is filed, supporting GCP c']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this IRB/IEC approval was obtained. Links the approval record to the study master data.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: IRB/IEC approvals are frequently amendment-specific — each protocol amendment requires separate ethics committee review and approval. amendment_number is a plain-text denormalization. Linking to study']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: irb_iec_approval records IRB/IEC approvals for a specific study-site combination. study_site is the canonical association entity for the study-site pairing. Adding study_site_id directly links each ap']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: IRB/IEC approval documentation is submitted as part of the regulatory submission package. Linking irb_iec_approval to the submission enables regulatory affairs to confirm ethics documentation is inclu']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: IRB/IEC approval letters are TMF essential documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 4). Regulatory inspectors verify the approval letter is filed in the TMF. A clinical-research domain expert expects i']
  approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions, stipulations, or required modifications attached to a conditional approval. Must be resolved and documented before full approval is granted. Null if no conditions were imposed.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the IRB or IEC formally granted approval for the study to proceed at this site. This is the principal real-world event date for the approval lifecycle. Site activation cannot proceed before this date.']
  approval_letter_received_date date [note: 'The date on which the site or sponsor received the official IRB/IEC approval letter. May differ from the approval_date if there is a postal or administrative delay.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IRB/IEC approval record: pending (submission received, awaiting decision), approved (full approval granted), conditionally_approved (approved with conditions to be resolved), suspended (approval temporarily halted), withdrawn (approval revoked), expired (approval period lapsed without renewal).']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the approval: initial (first approval for the study-site), amendment (approval of a protocol or ICF amendment), continuing_review (periodic re-approval), renewal (expiration-driven renewal), or waiver (waiver of specific requirements). Drives the approval lifecycle workflow.']
  approved_icf_date date [note: 'The date of the approved ICF version. Used together with approved_icf_version to confirm the exact ICF document authorized for subject consent at this site.']
  approved_icf_version varchar [note: 'The specific version number of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) approved by the IRB/IEC for use at this site. Subjects must be consented using only the IRB/IEC-approved ICF version.']
  committee_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of ethics committee that issued the approval: IRB (Institutional Review Board, U.S.), IEC (Independent Ethics Committee, international), REC (Research Ethics Committee, UK), HREC (Human Research Ethics Committee, Australia), or EC (Ethics Committee, generic). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IRB|IEC|REC|HREC|EC|CCRB — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  conditions_resolved_date date [note: 'The date on which all conditions attached to a conditional IRB/IEC approval were formally resolved and confirmed by the ethics committee. Null if no conditions were imposed or if conditions remain outstanding.']
  continuing_review_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the continuing review submission must be received by the IRB/IEC to ensure uninterrupted approval. Typically set 30-60 days before the expiration date to allow processing time.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this IRB/IEC approval record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctms_approval_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier for this IRB/IEC approval in the Oracle Siebel CTMS source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the current IRB/IEC approval expires. Enrollment and study activities must cease at this site if a renewal is not obtained before this date. Critical for continuing review scheduling.']
  gcp_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRB/IEC has confirmed that the study will be conducted in accordance with GCP guidelines as part of the approval. Relevant for regulatory inspections and audit readiness.']
  local_language_icf_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRB/IEC has approved a local-language translation of the ICF for use at this site. Required for sites where the subject population is not primarily English-speaking.']
  local_language_icf_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the local-language ICF approved by the IRB/IEC. Null if no local-language ICF was required or approved.']
  max_enrollment_approved varchar [note: 'The maximum number of subjects that the IRB/IEC has approved for enrollment at this site under this approval. Enrollment must not exceed this number without a new amendment approval.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or contextual information related to this IRB/IEC approval record that are not captured in structured fields. Used by CRAs and CRCs for operational notes.']
  pediatric_enrollment_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRB/IEC has specifically approved enrollment of pediatric subjects at this site. Requires additional parental consent and assent documentation per applicable regulations.']
  regulatory_authority_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA) has been notified of the IRB/IEC approval as required by applicable regulations. Supports regulatory submission tracking.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority was notified of the IRB/IEC approval decision. Null if notification is not required or has not yet been sent.']
  reinstatement_date date [note: 'The date on which a previously suspended IRB/IEC approval was reinstated, allowing study activities to resume at the site. Null if the approval was never suspended or has not yet been reinstated.']
  review_meeting_date date [note: 'The date on which the IRB or IEC convened to review the submission for this study-site. Relevant for full-board reviews; may be null for expedited reviews.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the type of review conducted by the ethics committee: full_board (convened meeting review), expedited (review by one or more members per 21 CFR 56.110), exempt (qualifies for exemption per 45 CFR 46.104), or administrative (non-substantive administrative review).']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the study documentation package (protocol, ICF, investigator brochure, etc.) was formally submitted to the IRB or IEC for review. Marks the start of the review clock.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'The date on which the IRB or IEC suspended the approval for this study-site. Enrollment and study procedures must halt upon suspension. Null if the approval has never been suspended.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for suspension of the IRB/IEC approval. Supports safety signal investigation, CAPA tracking, and regulatory reporting. Null if the approval has not been suspended.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this IRB/IEC approval record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  vulnerable_population_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRB/IEC approval explicitly covers enrollment of vulnerable populations (e.g., minors, pregnant women, prisoners, cognitively impaired individuals). Requires additional safeguards per applicable regulations.']
  waiver_of_consent_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRB/IEC has granted a waiver or alteration of the informed consent requirement for this study-site. Applicable in specific circumstances such as emergency research.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the IRB/IEC approval was formally withdrawn or revoked. Null if the approval has not been withdrawn.']

  Note: 'Records IRB (Institutional Review Board) and IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approvals for a study-site combination. Captures submission date, approval date, approval number, expiration date, continuing review due date, approved protocol version, approved ICF version, approval conditions, and the approving ethics committee identity. Tracks the full approval lifecycle including amendments and renewals. Distinct from the ethics domain which owns the committee master data.'
}

  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, internal comments, or operational notes related to this regulatory submission that are not captured in structured fields. Used by CRAs and regulatory affairs staff for tracking follow-up actions.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory body or ethics committee to which this site-level submission was directed (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada, IRB, IEC, or local health authority).   regulatory_timeline_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days from submission date to approval date, representing the actual regulatory review cycle time for this submission. Used for site activation performance analytics and benchmarking against regulatory authority standard timelines.']
  response_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the site or sponsor submitted a formal response to regulatory authority or ethics committee queries or conditions. Used to track responsiveness and compliance with regulatory timelines.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the site or sponsor must respond to regulatory authority or ethics committee queries, conditions, or requests for additional information arising from the review of this submission.']
  review_comments varchar [note: 'Narrative comments or conditions provided by the regulatory authority or ethics committee as part of their review decision. Captures required modifications, conditions of approval, or reasons for deferral or rejection.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal outcome of the regulatory authority\'s or ethics committee\'s review of this submission. Distinct from submission_status in that it captures the specific decision rendered (e.g., approved with conditions, deferred pending additional information).']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the regulatory submission outcome. Supports sponsor oversight tracking and compliance with ICH E6(R2) notification requirements.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of the regulatory submission outcome (approval, rejection, or conditions). Required under ICH E6(R2) for sponsor oversight of site regulatory compliance.']
  submission_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the submission indicating whether it is an initial submission, a periodic renewal, a protocol amendment, a site closure notification, or a safety report submission to the ethics committee or regulatory authority.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the site-level regulatory submission was formally submitted to the regulatory authority or ethics committee. Serves as the principal business event date for this transaction.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the regulatory submission was transmitted to the regulatory authority or ethics committee (e.g., electronic portal, paper, email). Relevant for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and audit trail.']
  submission_portal_reference varchar [note: 'The tracking or confirmation number issued by the regulatory authority\'s electronic submission portal (e.g., FDA ESG, EMA CTIS, MHRA IRAS) upon receipt of the submission. Provides external confirmation of receipt.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the site-level regulatory submission. Tracks progression from draft preparation through regulatory authority review to final disposition (approved, rejected, withdrawn, or superseded by an amendment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Submitted|Under_Review|Approved|Rejected|Withdrawn|Superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site-level regulatory submission. Includes FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator), FDA Form 3454/3455 (Financial Disclosure), IRB/IEC applications, local health authority notifications, and country-specific site authorization documents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA_Form_1572|FDA_Form_3454|FDA_Form_3455|IRB_IEC_Application|Local_Health_Authority_Notification|Site_Authorization|Amendment|Other — promote to reference product]']
  submission_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this submission document, used to track amendments and resubmissions (e.g., v1.0, v2.0, Amendment 1). Supports version control of regulatory documents in the eTMF.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory submission record was last modified in the CTMS or data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']

  Note: 'Tracks site-level regulatory submissions required for trial conduct, including FDA Form 1572 (Statement of Investigator), financial disclosure forms (FDA Form 3454/3455), local health authority notifications, and country-specific site authorization documents. }

  action_item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of action items identified and documented during the Site Initiation Visit. Used to track outstanding items that must be resolved before or shortly after first patient enrollment.']
  actual_visit_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the Site Initiation Visit was conducted. May differ from the scheduled date due to rescheduling. This is the principal real-world event date for the SIV.']

  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for cancellation or rescheduling of the Site Initiation Visit, when applicable. Populated only when visit_status is \'cancelled\' or \'rescheduled\'. Used for root cause analysis and site management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this Site Initiation Visit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  edc_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system training (e.g., Medidata Rave) was completed during the Site Initiation Visit. Site staff must be trained on eCRF completion prior to first patient enrollment.']
  first_patient_enrollment_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Site Initiation Visit has resulted in formal authorization for the site to enroll the first patient (First Patient First Visit - FPFV). This flag drives site activation status in the CTMS.']
  first_patient_enrollment_authorized_date date [note: 'The date on which the site was formally authorized to enroll the first patient following successful completion of the Site Initiation Visit. Key milestone for site activation and study timeline tracking.']
  gcp_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GCP compliance requirements were reviewed and confirmed as met at the site during the Site Initiation Visit, including staff training records, facility adequacy, and regulatory documentation.']
  icf_process_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether training on the Informed Consent Form (ICF) process and procedures was completed during the SIV. Ensures site staff understand the requirements for obtaining valid informed consent from study subjects.']
  ip_handling_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether training on Investigational Product (IP) receipt, storage, dispensing, accountability, and return/destruction procedures was completed during the SIV.']
  open_action_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of action items from the Site Initiation Visit that remain unresolved/open at the time of record update. A non-zero value may indicate a site activation risk.']
  protocol_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study protocol training topic was covered and completed during the Site Initiation Visit. Protocol training is a mandatory SIV agenda item per GCP requirements.']
  regulatory_authority_approval_obtained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required national or regional regulatory authority approval (e.g., IND from FDA, CTA from EMA/MHRA) was confirmed as obtained prior to the Site Initiation Visit. A prerequisite for site activation.']
  rtsm_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) / Interactive Response Technology (IRT) system training was completed during the SIV. Required for sites to perform patient randomization and manage investigational product supply.']
  safety_reporting_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether training on safety and Adverse Event (AE) / Serious Adverse Event (SAE) reporting procedures was completed during the SIV. Mandatory per GCP to ensure timely and accurate safety reporting.']
  scheduled_visit_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the Site Initiation Visit is scheduled to occur, as recorded in the CTMS. Used for resource planning and site activation timeline tracking.']
  siv_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the SIV, typically generated by the CTMS (Oracle Siebel CTMS) and referenced in the Trial Master File (TMF). Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory audit purposes.']
  siv_signoff_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sponsor or CRO representative who provided formal sign-off on the Site Initiation Visit, confirming all objectives were met and the site is activated.']
  siv_signoff_date date [note: 'The date on which the Site Initiation Visit received formal sign-off from the sponsor or CRO representative, confirming site readiness for first patient enrollment.']
  siv_signoff_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the formal sign-off status of the Site Initiation Visit by the sponsor or CRO. Sign-off confirms that all SIV objectives were met and the site is authorized to enroll the first patient. Distinct from visit_status.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this Site Initiation Visit record was sourced or integrated into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this SIV record in the originating operational system (e.g., Oracle Siebel CTMS visit ID). Enables reverse traceability from the lakehouse back to the system of record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this Site Initiation Visit record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  visit_end_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the Site Initiation Visit concluded. Used together with visit_start_time to calculate visit duration and assess adequacy of training time.']
  visit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional observations, comments, or contextual information recorded by the CRA during or after the Site Initiation Visit. Supplements the structured visit report.']
  visit_report_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA completed and finalized the Site Initiation Visit report. Regulatory guidelines typically require visit reports to be completed within a defined number of days post-visit (e.g., 10 business days). Filed in the eTMF.']
  visit_report_due_date date [note: 'The contractually or procedurally defined deadline by which the SIV report must be completed and filed in the eTMF. Used to monitor CRA compliance with SOP timelines.']
  visit_start_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the Site Initiation Visit commenced on the day of the visit. Used for duration calculation and audit trail purposes per ALCOA+ principles.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the Site Initiation Visit. Tracks progression from scheduling through completion or cancellation. Used for CTMS workflow management and site activation tracking.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the Site Initiation Visit was conducted on-site at the investigational site, remotely (e.g., via video conference), or as a hybrid combination. Relevant for regulatory acceptability and risk-based monitoring frameworks.']

  Note: 'Records the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) event conducted by the CRA prior to first patient enrollment. Captures visit date, CRA assigned, attendees, training topics covered (protocol, EDC, RTSM, safety reporting, ICF process), action items identified, visit report completion date, and SIV sign-off status. Distinct from ongoing monitoring visits tracked in the monitoring domain.'
}

Table site.enrollment_performance {
  enrollment_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each enrollment performance record at the study-site-reporting period level. Primary key for this data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.activation. Business justification: Enrollment performance begins after site activation. Linking enrollment_performance to the activation record provides the activation context (activation date, enrollment target, FPFV date) that frames']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: In multi-arm and adaptive trials, CTMS enrollment dashboards track enrollment counts, dropout rates, and target achievement per arm at each site. Arm-specific enrollment performance is required for ra']
  dmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: Enrollment performance reporting periods, query counts, and SDV metrics are governed by the DMP\'s data collection strategy and query management conventions. Clinical data managers and monitors use thi']
  edc_study_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: Enrollment performance metrics (open_query_count, protocol_deviation_count) are derived from EDC data under a specific build. Data integrity audits and build-change impact assessments require knowing ']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Enrollment performance metrics are epoch-specific: screening epoch enrollment rates differ from treatment epoch completion rates. CTMS enrollment dashboards and operational reports segment site perfor']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which enrollment performance is being measured. Links to the site master record in the CTMS.']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: enrollment_performance tracks irb_iec_approval_status and irb_iec_approval_expiry_date as part of the performance reporting context. Adding irb_iec_approval_id links each performance record to the aut']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Enrollment performance is tracked at the study-site level and the Principal Investigator is directly accountable for enrollment outcomes. Linking enrollment_performance to principal_investigator enabl']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Enrollment performance reports are protocol-version-specific — screen failure rates and enrollment targets can change with amendments. protocol_number is a plain-text denormalization. Operational dash']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: Enrollment performance monitoring requires reconciling randomized_count and screen_failure_rate against the active rand_scheme to detect stratification imbalances and allocation drift. Biostatistician']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) for which enrollment performance is being tracked. Links to the study master record in the CTMS.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Enrollment performance snapshots must be tied to the active protocol amendment at the reporting period: amendments change eligibility criteria and enrollment targets, so performance metrics are only i']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: enrollment_performance tracks actual vs. planned enrollment at the study-site level over time. study_site is the canonical association entity representing the full lifecycle of a study-site pairing. A']
  active_subject_count varchar [note: 'The number of subjects currently active (enrolled and not yet completed or discontinued) at this site as of the end of the reporting period. Reflects current site workload and capacity utilization.']
  actual_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of subjects enrolled (randomized or consented per protocol definition) at this site during the reporting period. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC and Oracle Clinical One randomization data.']
  actual_screened_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of subjects who underwent screening procedures at this site during the reporting period, as captured in the EDC (Medidata Rave) and reconciled with CTMS.']
  completed_count varchar [note: 'The number of subjects who completed all protocol-required study procedures and visits at this site during the reporting period. Used for PP population analysis and study close-out planning.']
  cumulative_dropout_count varchar [note: 'The total number of enrolled subjects who have discontinued participation at this site from study activation through the end of the current reporting period. Used for retention analysis and ITT/PP population tracking.']
  cumulative_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'The total number of subjects enrolled at this site from study activation through the end of the current reporting period. Primary metric for tracking progress toward the site\'s enrollment target.']
  cumulative_screened_count varchar [note: 'The total number of subjects screened at this site from study activation through the end of the current reporting period. Enables longitudinal enrollment trend analysis and forecasting.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Indicates the originating system from which this enrollment performance record was sourced or integrated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation between CTMS and EDC systems.']
  dropout_count varchar [note: 'The number of enrolled subjects who discontinued participation (withdrew consent, were lost to follow-up, or were discontinued by investigator) during the reporting period. Impacts ITT and PP population sizes.']
  dropout_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The proportion of enrolled subjects who dropped out during the reporting period, expressed as a percentage (dropout_count / actual_enrolled_count × 100). Stored as a raw business metric from CTMS; used for retention risk assessment and site performance management.']
  enrollment_rate_per_month decimal(8,2) [note: 'The average number of subjects enrolled per calendar month at this site during the reporting period. Sourced from CTMS enrollment tracking; used for enrollment forecasting and site performance benchmarking.']
  enrollment_target_achievement_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the site\'s total enrollment target achieved cumulatively as of the end of the reporting period (cumulative_enrolled_count / site_enrollment_target × 100). Stored as a raw business metric from CTMS for site performance dashboards.']
  enrollment_variance varchar [note: 'The difference between actual enrolled subjects and planned enrolled subjects for the reporting period (actual_enrolled_count - planned_enrolled_count). Negative values indicate under-enrollment; positive values indicate over-enrollment. Stored as a raw business metric from CTMS.']
  gcp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'The current GCP compliance status of the site as assessed by the most recent monitoring visit or audit. Drives risk-based monitoring decisions and regulatory escalation. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS monitoring records.']
  icf_signed_count varchar [note: 'The number of subjects who signed the Informed Consent Form (ICF) at this site during the reporting period. ICF signature is the first step in the enrollment process and is tracked separately from screening and randomization per GCP requirements.']
  irb_iec_approval_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the current IRB/IEC approval for this study at this site expires. Sites must obtain renewal before this date to continue enrollment. Tracked in Veeva Vault regulatory module.']
  irb_iec_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the study at this site from the governing Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). Sites may not enroll subjects without valid IRB/IEC approval.']
  next_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'The date of the next scheduled monitoring visit at this site, as planned in the CTMS. Used for CRA workload planning and ensuring monitoring frequency meets the risk-based monitoring plan.']
  open_query_count varchar [note: 'The number of unresolved data queries (discrepancies or missing data flags) raised against this site\'s eCRF data in the EDC system as of the end of the reporting period. A key data quality indicator for site performance.']
  performance_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment performance status of the site for the reporting period, indicating whether the site is meeting, exceeding, or falling short of its planned enrollment targets. Drives CRA escalation and site management actions in the CTMS.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'The originally planned date by which the site was expected to complete enrollment of its assigned target, as defined in the site enrollment plan or study protocol timeline.']
  planned_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'The target number of subjects planned to be enrolled (randomized or consented per protocol definition) at this site during the reporting period. Derived from the site-level enrollment plan agreed with the sponsor.']
  planned_screened_count varchar [note: 'The number of subjects planned to be screened at this site during the reporting period, as defined in the site enrollment plan or feasibility agreement. Baseline for screen failure rate analysis.']
  projected_completion_date date [note: 'The forecasted date by which the site is expected to complete enrollment of its assigned target, based on current enrollment rate. Updated each reporting period in the CTMS for timeline management.']
  protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'The number of protocol deviations recorded at this site during the reporting period. Includes both major and minor deviations as classified per the study deviation management plan. Sourced from CTMS deviation tracking.']
  randomized_count varchar [note: 'The number of subjects who were randomized (assigned to a treatment arm via RTSM/IVRS) at this site during the reporting period. Distinct from enrolled count in studies where enrollment precedes randomization.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this enrollment performance record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this enrollment performance record was most recently updated in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the enrollment performance reporting period. Together with reporting_period_start_date, defines the window for all enrollment metrics in this record.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the enrollment performance reporting period (e.g., start of a monthly or weekly reporting window). Used to define the temporal boundary for all enrollment metrics in this record.']
  reporting_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the reporting period for this enrollment performance record. Determines the cadence at which site-level enrollment metrics are aggregated and reviewed.']
  sae_count varchar [note: 'The number of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) reported at this site during the reporting period. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety. Used to assess site safety profile and trigger risk-based monitoring escalation.']
  screen_failure_count varchar [note: 'The number of subjects who were screened but did not meet eligibility criteria and were therefore not enrolled during the reporting period. A key metric for site feasibility and protocol amendment decisions.']
  screen_failure_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The proportion of screened subjects who failed screening, expressed as a percentage (screen_failure_count / actual_screened_count × 100). Stored as a raw business metric captured from CTMS; used to assess site eligibility criteria adherence and protocol feasibility.']
  site_capacity varchar [note: 'The maximum number of subjects the site can concurrently manage based on staff, facilities, and resources, as assessed during site feasibility. Used to evaluate whether the site is operating within capacity.']
  site_enrollment_target varchar [note: 'The total number of subjects the site is contracted or planned to enroll over the full duration of the study, as agreed in the site initiation agreement or enrollment plan. Denominator for cumulative enrollment progress percentage.']
  site_number varchar [note: 'The sponsor- or CRO-assigned unique site identifier code for the investigational site. Used as the externally-known business identifier for the site in regulatory submissions and CTMS reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks actual vs. planned enrollment performance at the study-site level over time. Captures reporting period, planned enrollment target, actual subjects screened, subjects enrolled, screen failure count, screen failure rate, dropout count, and cumulative enrollment to date. Enables site-level enrollment forecasting and performance management. Sourced from CTMS enrollment tracking integrated with EDC subject data.'
}

Table site.agreement {
  agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Reference to the original site agreement record from which this amendment was derived. Null for original agreements (amendment_number = 0). Enables amendment lineage tracking and supersession chain reconstruction.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.activation. Business justification: Site agreements (CTA, CDA) are executed as part of the site activation workflow. The activation record tracks financial_agreement_executed and investigator_agreement_date, which directly correspond to']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Site agreements are executed contingent on regulatory approval. Linking site_agreement to the regulatory approval record enables contract validity tracking against approval status and supports audit t']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Site agreements are scoped to the CTA under which the site is authorized to conduct the trial. The agreement\'s financial terms and subject enrollment limits are bounded by the CTA. This link is requir']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site that is party to this agreement. Links to the site master record in the CTMS, enabling site-level performance and compliance reporting.']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: site_agreement tracks irb_iec_approval_required (flag) and irb_iec_approval_date as part of the agreement terms. Adding irb_iec_approval_id links the agreement to the authoritative IRB/IEC approval re']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Site agreements (CTAs) are signed by the Principal Investigator. The existing pi_name text field is a denormalized representation. Adding a proper FK to principal_investigator allows joining to get PI']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Clinical trial agreements (CTAs/CTUs) define scope and budget tied to a specific protocol version. Protocol amendments can trigger contract renegotiation. Linking site_agreement to protocol enables co']
  selection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.selection. Business justification: Site agreements are initiated following the formal site selection decision. The selection record contains the rationale, sponsor approval, and enrollment targets that inform the agreement terms. Linki']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Executed site agreements (CTAs, CSAs) are mandatory site file documents per ICH E6 R2. Linking site_agreement to site_file enables site file completeness verification to confirm the executed agreement']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) for which this site agreement is executed. A single site may have separate agreements per study. Links to the study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: site_agreement tracks site-level contractual agreements (CTA, CDA, budget) for a specific study-site combination. study_site is the canonical association entity for the study-site pairing. Adding stud']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the executed agreement document as stored in the Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) in Veeva Vault. Enables direct navigation from the CTMS record to the source document for inspection readiness.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to the site agreement (e.g., CTA-2024-0042). Used for cross-system tracking, regulatory correspondence, and audit trails. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS or Veeva Vault contract management module.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the site agreement. Tracks progression from initial drafting through negotiation, execution, and eventual expiry or termination. Drives workflow routing in Veeva Vault and Oracle Siebel CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Under Negotiation|Pending Execution|Executed|Expired|Terminated|Superseded — promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site-level agreement. CTA (Clinical Trial Agreement) is the primary investigational site contract; CDA (Confidential Disclosure Agreement) governs pre-trial information sharing; Budget Amendment captures renegotiated financial terms; Site Budget Agreement formalizes per-patient and overhead fee structures; Letter of Intent signals site commitment prior to full execution.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential amendment version number for this agreement record. A value of 0 indicates the original agreement; values ≥1 indicate successive amendments. Enables version tracking of negotiated changes over the study lifecycle.']
  closeout_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed fee payable to the investigational site upon study close-out to cover archival, final monitoring visit support, and regulatory document finalization activities. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  confidentiality_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration in years for which confidentiality obligations under the agreement remain in force after the agreement\'s expiration or termination. Particularly relevant for CDA (Confidential Disclosure Agreement) components.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this site agreement record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  cro_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the Contract Research Organization (CRO) acting as the contracting party on behalf of the sponsor, if applicable. Captures the legal entity name when the CRO executes the agreement under delegated authority.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all financial terms within this site agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-country trials.']
  data_privacy_addendum boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a data privacy addendum (DPA) or data processing agreement is included as part of or annexed to this site agreement, addressing GDPR, HIPAA, or other applicable data protection obligations for patient data.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the site agreement becomes legally binding and operative. Marks the start of the contractual relationship between the sponsor/CRO and the investigational site. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date on which all required parties signed and fully executed the site agreement. May differ from the effective date when agreements are backdated or have a future effective date. Critical for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the site agreement is scheduled to expire or cease to be operative. Nullable for open-ended agreements. Triggers renewal workflow alerts in Veeva Vault. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  governing_law_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this site agreement. Critical for multi-national trial legal compliance and dispute resolution.']
  indemnification_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site agreement includes an indemnification clause protecting the investigational site and PI from third-party claims arising from the sponsor\'s negligence. Standard in CTA templates but may be absent in certain jurisdictions.']
  insurance_coverage_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement mandates that the sponsor or CRO maintain clinical trial insurance coverage for the duration of the study at this site. Regulatory requirement in many jurisdictions.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the clinical trial insurance policy covering this site agreement. Populated when insurance_coverage_required is True. Used for regulatory inspection documentation.']
  ip_ownership_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site agreement contains an intellectual property ownership clause specifying that all study data, inventions, and results generated at the site are owned by the sponsor. Standard in CTA templates.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRB (Institutional Review Board) or IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approval is a condition precedent to the agreement becoming operative. Relevant for agreements at sites in jurisdictions requiring ethics committee review prior to contract execution.']
  legal_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor\'s or CRO\'s legal team has completed their review and approved the final agreement text prior to execution. Part of the quality control workflow tracked in Veeva Vault.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal review of the site agreement was completed and sign-off was granted. Populated when legal_review_completed is True. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  negotiation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which formal contract negotiation commenced between the sponsor/CRO and the investigational site. Used to calculate cycle time KPIs for site activation performance reporting in CTMS.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing supplementary notes, special conditions, deviations from standard templates, or negotiation context relevant to this site agreement. Supports audit trail and institutional knowledge retention.']
  overhead_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Institutional overhead or indirect cost rate (expressed as a percentage) applied on top of direct site costs as negotiated in the site budget agreement. Typically charged by academic medical centers or hospital institutions.']
  payment_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Defines the payment trigger mechanism agreed upon in the site budget agreement. Determines how and when invoices are generated and processed in SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice receipt within which payment must be remitted to the investigational site, as stipulated in the site agreement (e.g., Net 30, Net 45, Net 60). Drives accounts payable processing in SAP S/4HANA.']
  per_patient_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Agreed fee payable to the investigational site for each evaluable patient who completes the study per protocol. Core financial term in the site budget agreement. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  publication_rights_clause boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site agreement includes a publication rights clause defining the investigator\'s right to publish study results and the sponsor\'s right to review manuscripts prior to submission. Relevant for academic site agreements.']
  site_signature_date date [note: 'Date on which the authorized representative of the investigational site (e.g., PI or institutional official) signed the agreement. May differ from the sponsor/CRO signature date in multi-party execution workflows.']
  sponsor_signature_date date [note: 'Date on which the authorized representative of the sponsor or CRO signed the agreement. The later of site_signature_date and sponsor_signature_date typically defines the execution_date.']
  startup_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time fixed fee payable to the investigational site upon activation to cover site initiation activities such as staff training, IRB/IEC submission preparation, and regulatory document collection. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the site agreement was early-terminated prior to its scheduled expiration. Populated only when agreement_status is \'Terminated\'. Supports regulatory close-out documentation.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for early termination of the site agreement. Required for regulatory reporting and site performance analytics. Populated only when agreement_status is \'Terminated\'.']
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted budget value for this site agreement, inclusive of all per-patient fees, procedures, overhead, and any fixed fees. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Used for financial forecasting and accrual management in SAP S/4HANA.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this site agreement record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Tracks site-level contractual agreements including Clinical Trial Agreements (CTA), Confidential Disclosure Agreements (CDA), and site-specific budget agreements. Captures agreement type, execution date, effective date, expiration date, contracting parties, negotiation status, and key financial terms (overhead rate, per-patient fee structure). Complements the agreement domain which owns master service agreements at the sponsor level.'
}

Table site.site_cra_assignment {
  site_cra_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CRA-to-study-site assignment record in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.activation. Business justification: CRA assignments are established during or after site activation. The activation record defines the SIV schedule and monitoring approach that the CRA assignment operationalizes. Linking site_cra_assign']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: CRA monitoring assignments are executed under a governing monitoring SOP (ICH E6 R2 §5.18). The SOP version in effect at assignment start determines monitoring approach, SDV rates, and visit frequency']
  dmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: CRA assignments specify sdv_rate_pct and monitoring_approach, both defined in the DMP. Regulatory inspections require evidence that CRA SDV activities align with the governing DMP version. This link e']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site to which the CRA is assigned for monitoring coverage. Identifies the physical or virtual site conducting the clinical trial.']
  monitoring_cra_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_cra_assignment. Business justification: CTMS site CRA assignment records are the operational precursor that spawns a monitoring execution assignment. Linking these enables CTMS-to-monitoring traceability required for CRA workload audits, ha']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study-level monitoring plan document that governs the monitoring activities for this assignment. Links to the monitoring plan record in the CTMS or eTMF.']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: CRA assignments are made to study-site combinations where a specific PI is responsible. The CRA works directly with the PI for monitoring visits, delegation log verification, and GCP compliance. Linki']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: CRA assignments are protocol-version-specific: protocol training completion, SDV requirements, and monitoring frequency are defined per protocol version. Regulatory inspections require traceability fr']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Risk-based monitoring (ICH E6 R2) mandates that CRA assignment intensity and visit frequency are driven by site risk assessments. Linking site_cra_assignment to risk_assessment enables audit trail of ']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: CRA delegation logs and qualification documents are mandatory site file contents per ICH E6 R2 5.18. Linking site_cra_assignment to site_file enables verification that CRA credentials and delegation e']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this CRA assignment is made. Links the assignment to a specific investigational study protocol managed in the CTMS.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: site_cra_assignment records the assignment of CRAs to study-site combinations for monitoring. study_site is the canonical association entity for the study-site pairing. Adding study_site_id directly l']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this CRA assignment was formally approved by the responsible manager or clinical operations lead. Required for GCP compliance and audit trail documentation.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information relevant to this CRA-site assignment (e.g., site-specific access requirements, language considerations, sponsor preferences).']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the CRA assignment record as generated by the CTMS (e.g., CRA-ASGN-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference, audit trails, and operational communication.']
  assignment_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for creating or modifying this CRA assignment. Supports operational reporting and root cause analysis for assignment changes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_assignment|replacement|coverage_gap|workload_rebalance|site_activation|escalation|other — promote to reference product]']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CRA assignment. \'pending\' = approved but not yet started; \'active\' = currently monitoring; \'on_hold\' = temporarily suspended; \'completed\' = assignment concluded per plan; \'cancelled\' = terminated before completion; \'transferred\' = reassigned to another CRA.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the role of the CRA within the site monitoring coverage model. \'primary\' indicates the main responsible monitor; \'backup\' covers absence; \'co_monitor\' indicates joint monitoring; \'lead\' designates a senior oversight role; \'remote\' indicates centralized/remote monitoring only.']
  confidentiality_agreement_signed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned CRA has signed the required confidentiality or non-disclosure agreement (NDA) for this study prior to accessing study data. True = signed; False = pending. Regulatory and sponsor compliance requirement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CRA assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  ctms_assignment_ref varchar [note: 'The native assignment identifier or record number from the source Oracle Siebel CTMS system. Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  delegation_log_entry_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CRA\'s monitoring responsibilities have been formally documented on the site\'s delegation of authority log, as required by GCP. True = confirmed on delegation log; False = pending confirmation.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA\'s monitoring responsibility for the assigned study-site combination is scheduled to conclude or was actually concluded. Null for open-ended active assignments.']
  estimated_travel_days_per_visit decimal(4,1) [note: 'The estimated number of travel days required per monitoring visit for this CRA-site assignment. Used for resource capacity planning, travel cost estimation, and CRA workload calculation.']
  gcp_training_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned CRA holds a current, valid GCP training certification at the time of assignment. True = GCP training is current and valid; False = training expired or not on file. Regulatory compliance requirement per ICH E6(R2).']
  gcp_training_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the assigned CRA\'s GCP training certification expires. Used to proactively manage compliance and ensure no lapse in qualified monitoring coverage.']
  geographic_territory varchar [note: 'The geographic territory or region to which this CRA assignment belongs (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'Western Europe\', \'APAC\'). Used for territory-based workload planning, travel budgeting, and CRA deployment optimization.']
  handover_date date [note: 'The date on which monitoring responsibility was formally transferred from the predecessor CRA to the current CRA. Relevant for assignment transfers and ensures no monitoring coverage gap.']
  handover_visit_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal handover monitoring visit was conducted between the outgoing and incoming CRA at the site. True = handover visit completed; False = not yet completed or not applicable.']
  last_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent monitoring visit (on-site or remote) conducted by this CRA at the assigned site under this assignment. Used for visit scheduling, overdue visit tracking, and site oversight reporting.']
  monitoring_approach varchar [note: 'Defines the monitoring methodology applied by the CRA for this assignment. \'on_site\' = traditional in-person SDV; \'remote\' = off-site data review; \'hybrid\' = combination; \'centralized\' = statistical/data-driven central monitoring per risk-based monitoring (RBM) frameworks.']
  next_monitoring_visit_date date [note: 'The date of the next planned monitoring visit (on-site or remote) for this CRA-site assignment as scheduled in the CTMS. Drives visit scheduling workflows and resource planning.']
  open_action_items_count varchar [note: 'The current number of unresolved monitoring action items or follow-up items outstanding for this CRA-site assignment. Supports site oversight, escalation management, and GCP compliance tracking.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The originally planned end date for the CRA assignment at the time of creation. Enables comparison against actual end date to track assignment duration variances and resource planning accuracy.']
  protocol_training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned CRA has completed the required study protocol-specific training prior to initiating monitoring activities at the site. True = training completed; False = pending. Regulatory requirement per GCP.']
  protocol_training_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA completed the study protocol-specific training required for this assignment. Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'The risk classification of the assigned site as assessed under the risk-based monitoring (RBM) framework. Determines monitoring intensity, visit frequency, and SDV scope for this assignment. \'high\' = intensive monitoring required.']
  sdv_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The agreed or actual percentage of critical data fields subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) for this CRA-site assignment, as defined in the monitoring plan. Reflects risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy. Valid range: 0.00 to 100.00.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA\'s monitoring responsibility for the assigned study-site combination becomes effective. Used for coverage planning and workload scheduling.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for ending or cancelling the CRA assignment before or at its planned conclusion. Populated only when assignment_status is \'completed\', \'cancelled\', or \'transferred\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: study_completion|site_closure|cra_departure|reassignment|performance|sponsor_request|other — promote to reference product]']
  territory_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary country of the CRA\'s assigned territory (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Supports geographic reporting and regulatory jurisdiction tracking.']
  total_visits_completed varchar [note: 'The cumulative count of monitoring visits (on-site and remote) completed by this CRA at the assigned site under this assignment. Used for performance tracking and monitoring coverage reporting.']
  travel_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether overnight or significant travel is required for the CRA to conduct on-site monitoring visits at this assigned site. True = travel required; False = local or remote only. Used for travel budget planning and CRA logistics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this CRA assignment record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  visit_frequency_planned varchar [note: 'The planned frequency of on-site or remote monitoring visits for this CRA-site assignment as defined in the monitoring plan. Drives visit scheduling and resource forecasting in the CTMS.']
  workload_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the CRA\'s total available working capacity allocated to this specific study-site assignment. Used for workload balancing across multiple concurrent assignments. Valid range: 0.00 to 100.00.']

  Note: 'Records the assignment of Clinical Research Associates (CRAs) to study-site combinations for monitoring coverage. Captures CRA identity reference (FK to workforce domain), assignment start/end dates, assignment type (primary, backup, co-monitor), geographic territory, workload allocation percentage, and assignment status. Enables monitoring coverage planning, workload balancing, and visit scheduling within CTMS.'
}

Linking closeout to tmf_completeness enables closeout wor']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The closeout visit report is a mandatory TMF artifact (DIA Reference Model Zone 5). Linking site_closeout to its TMF document supports essential_doc_archival_status tracking, TMF completeness assessme']
  visit_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.visit_report.
