Direct FK to visit_report enables TMF completeness checks and final payment release workflows; closeou']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the CAPA record in MasterControl, if a CAPA was initiated as a result of close-out findings. Null if no CAPA is required.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan is required at this site based on findings identified during the close-out visit or cumulative monitoring. Triggers CAPA workflow in MasterControl.']
  closeout_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this close-out event, typically generated by the CTMS close-out workflow (e.g., CO-2024-0042). Used for cross-system referencing and regulatory correspondence.']
  closeout_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the site close-out process as tracked in the CTMS close-out workflow. Drives downstream regulatory notification and payment release actions.']
  closeout_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason driving the site close-out. Distinguishes between normal study completion, early termination for safety or operational reasons, and sponsor-initiated decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned_completion|early_termination|protocol_amendment|sponsor_decision|regulatory_action|site_request — promote to reference product]']
  completed_date date [note: 'Date on which all close-out activities were formally completed and the site was officially closed. Marks the end of the site lifecycle for this study.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site close-out record was first created in the CTMS close-out workflow. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  drug_accountability_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which investigational medicinal product (IMP) accountability reconciliation was formally completed and signed off at the site.']
  drug_accountability_status varchar [note: 'Current status of investigational medicinal product (IMP) accountability reconciliation at the site. Confirms that all dispensed, returned, and destroyed drug quantities are fully accounted for per GCP requirements.']
  early_termination_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for early termination of the site, if applicable. Populated only when closeout_type is \'early_termination\'. Supports regulatory reporting and sponsor decision documentation.']
  essential_doc_archival_date date [note: 'Date on which all essential documents were confirmed as archived at the investigational site, satisfying ICH E6(R2) Section 8 requirements.']
  essential_doc_archival_status varchar [note: 'Status of archival of all essential documents required by ICH E6(R2) Section 8 at the investigational site. Confirms that the site\'s Trial Master File (TMF) is complete and archived per regulatory requirements.']
  final_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the final payment disbursed to the investigational site at close-out, denominated in the contract currency. Represents the net amount after reconciliation of all advances and prior payments.']
  final_payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the final payment to the investigational site was processed and confirmed, closing all financial obligations under the site budget agreement.']
  final_payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the final financial payment to the investigational site upon close-out, covering all outstanding per-subject fees, procedure fees, and close-out visit fees per the site budget agreement.']
  gcp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall GCP compliance assessment for the site at close-out, based on findings from the close-out visit and cumulative monitoring history. Informs the site\'s eligibility for future studies.']
  imp_return_date date [note: 'Date on which unused investigational medicinal product (IMP) was returned to the sponsor/depot or destroyed on-site per the drug destruction protocol.']
  imp_return_status varchar [note: 'Status of the return or on-site destruction of unused investigational medicinal product (IMP) at the site. Distinct from drug accountability reconciliation — this tracks the physical disposition of remaining IMP stock.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal site close-out process was officially initiated in the CTMS, typically following LSLV confirmation and sponsor authorization.']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of study completion or early termination at this site.']
  irb_iec_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formal notification to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) regarding study completion or early termination at this site, as required by GCP and applicable regulations.']
  lslv_date date [note: 'Date of the last protocol-required visit for the last subject enrolled at this site. LSLV is a critical clinical milestone that triggers the initiation of the close-out process and database lock activities.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for CRA or study manager notes regarding the close-out process, including any outstanding issues, special circumstances, or follow-up actions required after the close-out visit.']
  outstanding_queries_count varchar [note: 'Number of unresolved data queries (discrepancies) remaining in the EDC system at the time of close-out visit. A non-zero value indicates data cleaning is incomplete and may delay database lock contribution.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the final site payment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency global trials.']
  pi_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) formally acknowledged the close-out letter and confirmed all site obligations were fulfilled.']
  pi_acknowledgement_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) has formally acknowledged receipt of the close-out letter and confirmed completion of all close-out obligations, as required by ICH E6(R2).']
  protocol_deviations_count varchar [note: 'Total number of protocol deviations recorded at this site during the study. Summarized at close-out for site performance assessment and inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).']
  regulatory_authority_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the applicable regulatory authority was formally notified of the site close-out.']
  regulatory_authority_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of notification to applicable regulatory authorities (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA) regarding site close-out, as required under applicable IND/CTA regulations.']
  sdv_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of critical and non-critical data fields that have been source data verified (SDV) at the site as of the close-out visit. Reflects data quality and monitoring completeness per the monitoring plan.']
  site_file_transfer_date date [note: 'Date on which the site\'s investigator file and essential documents were successfully transferred to the sponsor or CRO central archive.']
  site_file_transfer_status varchar [note: 'Status of the transfer of the site\'s investigator file and essential documents to the sponsor or CRO archive. Applicable when physical or electronic records must be transferred from the site to a central repository.']
  subjects_completed_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who completed all protocol-required visits and procedures at this site. Used to calculate site completion rate and assess protocol adherence.']
  subjects_discontinued_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who discontinued from the study prior to completing all protocol-required activities at this site. Includes withdrawals, screen failures post-enrollment, and protocol deviations leading to discontinuation.']
  subjects_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects who were enrolled (consented and randomized or assigned to treatment) at this site during the study. Used for final enrollment reconciliation at close-out.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this site close-out record in the CTMS. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  visit_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) conducted the formal close-out monitoring visit at the investigational site. This is the principal real-world event date for the close-out transaction.']

  Note: 'Records the formal site close-out process for a study-site upon completion or early termination. Captures close-out visit date, last subject last visit (LSLV) date, database lock contribution date, drug accountability reconciliation status, essential document archival status, site file transfer status, final payment status, and regulatory notification completion. Sourced from CTMS close-out workflow.'
}

Table subject.trial_subject {
  trial_subject_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a trial subject record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the trial_subject entity. All subject-level transactional records in the subject domain reference this identifier.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: SDTM ACTARM/ARM derivation and enrollment-by-arm reporting require linking each subject to their assigned study_arm. Clinical operations teams track arm-level enrollment against randomization ratios d']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Regulatory compliance requires every enrolled subject to be traceable to the active CTA for their country. Regulatory inspectors verify subject enrollment counts against CTA-approved planned_subject_c']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: FDA 21 CFR Part 312 and ICH E6(R2) require that each enrolled subject is traceable to the specific IRB/IEC approval governing their enrollment, including approved ICF version and maximum enrollment co']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study in which this subject is enrolled or screened. Links the subject to the parent study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which this subject was screened or enrolled. Supports site-level enrollment analytics and monitoring.']
  age_at_enrollment varchar [note: 'Subject\'s age in years at the time of enrollment or first dose, calculated from date of birth and enrollment date. Used for eligibility verification, demographic reporting, and subgroup analyses. Stored as a derived-at-source value from Oracle Clinical One.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject completed all required study procedures per protocol, including end-of-study visit. Used for subject disposition reporting, CSR preparation, and database lock readiness assessment.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the investigational site where the subject is enrolled. Used for geographic stratification, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and multi-regional trial reporting. Corresponds to CDISC SDTM DM COUNTRY variable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trial subject record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  data_lock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject\'s data has been locked as part of the Database Lock (DBL) process, preventing further modifications to the subject\'s eCRF data. True = subject data locked; False = data still open for entry or query resolution. Critical for CSR preparation and regulatory submission readiness.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Subject\'s date of birth used to calculate age at screening, enrollment, and key study milestones. Classified as restricted PHI/PII. Stored in yyyy-MM-dd format. May be partially masked (year only) in certain jurisdictions per GDPR/HIPAA de-identification requirements.']
  eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject met all inclusion and exclusion criteria as assessed during screening. True = all eligibility criteria confirmed met; False = one or more criteria not met. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One eligibility assessment.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was formally enrolled in the study following confirmation of eligibility criteria. Distinct from screening date and randomization date. Used for enrollment tracking, site performance KPIs, and study timeline reporting.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subject within the study, reflecting their progression from screening through completion or withdrawal. Drives subject disposition reporting and ITT/PP population flags. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screened|enrolled|randomized|completed|withdrawn|screen_failed|discontinued|on_treatment|follow_up — promote to reference product]']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Subject\'s self-reported ethnic origin as collected at screening, coded per CDISC SDTM controlled terminology. Distinct from race per FDA demographic reporting requirements. Used in regulatory demographic summary tables.']
  first_dose_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject received the first dose of investigational product. Used as the reference anchor for treatment-emergent adverse event (TEAE) analysis, PK sampling schedules, and per-protocol population definitions.']
  itt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). True = subject is in ITT population; False = excluded. Used in primary efficacy analyses per ICH E9 principles.']
  last_dose_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject received the last dose of investigational product. Used to define the end of the treatment-emergent window for adverse event analysis and to calculate treatment duration. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC exposure domain.']
  pp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Subjects may be excluded from PP due to major protocol deviations, insufficient drug exposure, or missing primary endpoint data.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has at least one recorded protocol deviation during the study. True = one or more deviations recorded; False = no deviations. Summary flag for quick filtering; detailed deviations are tracked in the compliance.protocol_deviation product.']
  race varchar [note: 'Subject\'s self-reported racial category as collected at screening, coded per CDISC SDTM controlled terminology and FDA demographic reporting requirements. Used for regulatory demographic tables and diversity reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE|ASIAN|BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN|NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER|WHITE|MULTIPLE|UNKNOWN|NOT REPORTED — promote to reference product]']
  randomization_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was randomized to a treatment arm via the IRT/RTSM system. Defines the start of the treatment period and is used as the reference date for TEAE analysis windows and ITT population inclusion. Null for screen-failed subjects.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'Unique number assigned to a subject at the point of randomization via the IRT/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One). Determines treatment arm assignment and links to investigational product supply allocation. Null for subjects who were screened but not randomized.']
  safety_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the safety analysis population (typically all randomized subjects who received at least one dose of investigational product). Used for adverse event incidence tables and safety reporting.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a screened subject did not proceed to enrollment, coded per protocol-defined eligibility criteria categories (e.g., INCLUSION CRITERIA NOT MET, EXCLUSION CRITERIA MET, SUBJECT WITHDREW CONSENT, INVESTIGATOR DECISION). Populated only for subjects with enrollment_status = screen_failed.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject first underwent screening procedures for the study, including signing of the Informed Consent Form (ICF). Marks the start of the subject\'s participation timeline. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave EDC.']
  screening_number varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to a potential subject at the point of screening, prior to randomization or enrollment confirmation. Used to track pre-enrollment eligibility assessments. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave EDC.']
  serious_adverse_event_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has experienced at least one Serious Adverse Event (SAE) during the study. True = one or more SAEs reported; False = no SAEs. Summary flag for subject-level safety profiling; detailed SAE records are managed in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex of the subject as recorded at screening. Coded per CDISC SDTM controlled terminology (M=Male, F=Female, U=Unknown, UNDIFFERENTIATED). Used for demographic reporting, eligibility criteria verification, and PK/PD subgroup analyses.']
  source_subject_key varchar [note: 'The native primary key or identifier for this subject record in the originating operational system (e.g., Medidata Rave subject OID, Oracle Clinical One patient ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the source system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned unique subject identifier within the study, typically formatted as site-subject (e.g., 001-0042). This is the primary operational identifier used across EDC, CTMS, and regulatory submissions. Corresponds to SUBJID in CDISC SDTM DM domain.']
  subject_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this subject record was sourced or is mastered (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Clinical One, Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and ALCOA+ compliance tracing.']
  unique_subject_identifier varchar [note: 'Globally unique subject identifier combining sponsor, study, and subject number (e.g., SPONSOR-STUDY-001-0042). Mandatory CDISC SDTM variable used across all regulatory submission datasets to unambiguously identify a subject across studies and submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trial subject record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject withdrew from the study or was discontinued, if applicable. Null for subjects who completed the study. Used in subject disposition analysis and survival/time-to-event analyses.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason for subject withdrawal or discontinuation from the study, per CDISC SDTM DS domain controlled terminology (e.g., ADVERSE EVENT, WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT, PROTOCOL DEVIATION, LOST TO FOLLOW-UP, PHYSICIAN DECISION, DEATH). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ADVERSE EVENT|WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT|PROTOCOL DEVIATION|LOST TO FOLLOW-UP|PHYSICIAN DECISION|DEATH|LACK OF EFFICACY|OTHER — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a unique trial participant (subject/patient) screened or enrolled in a clinical study. Serves as the SSOT for subject identity including screening number, subject number, randomization number, demographic profile (age, sex, race, ethnicity, date of birth, country), key eligibility flags, and current enrollment status. All subject-level transactional records in this domain reference this entity. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave EDC.'
}

  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. This is a ']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) responsible for the subject\'s care and study conduct at the investigational site. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.']

Business justification: Enrollment coordinators must record which protocol amendment version was in effect at enrollment, especially when amendments change eligibility criteria or require reconsent. This supports regulatory ']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: ICH E6(R2) requires that the site file be verified as complete before subject enrollment. Site monitors use this link during enrollment eligibility checks and TMF reconciliation to confirm ICF, IRB ap']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this subject is being enrolled. Links to the study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the subject is being enrolled. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS site master.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Enrollment-to-randomization reconciliation is a standard CTMS data integrity check. randomization_number, treatment_arm_code, treatment_arm_description on enrollment are denormalized from subject_rand']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial participant (subject/patient) master record. Links to the subject demographic and identity record.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the blinding status of the subject\'s treatment assignment at the time of this record. \'blinded\' = treatment assignment is masked; \'unblinded\' = treatment assignment has been revealed (e.g., emergency unblinding or end-of-study unblinding); \'open_label\' = study design is open-label.']
  cohort_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the study cohort or stratum to which the subject belongs, used in stratified randomization designs. Distinct from treatment arm — a cohort may span multiple arms.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject completed all required study procedures and visits per the protocol. Populated only when enrollment_status = \'completed\'. Used for study close-out and CSR reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was first created in the data platform. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Supports ALCOA+ auditability and data lineage requirements.']
  eligibility_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has been confirmed as meeting all inclusion/exclusion criteria defined in the study protocol prior to enrollment. True = all criteria verified; False = criteria not yet confirmed or subject failed screening.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was formally enrolled into the clinical study following confirmation of eligibility and completion of informed consent. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for this enrollment transaction. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subject within the enrollment process. Represents the subject\'s progression from initial screening through study completion or withdrawal. LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Clinical One.']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the enrollment event type. \'new\' = first-time enrollment in this study; \'re_enrolled\' = subject re-enrolled after a protocol-allowed break; \'rollover\' = subject rolled over from a preceding study; \'replacement\' = subject enrolled as a replacement per protocol.']
  first_dose_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject received the first dose of investigational product (IP). Defines the start of the treatment-emergent period for TEAE analysis. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  icf_signed_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject (or legally authorized representative) signed the Informed Consent Form (ICF), confirming voluntary agreement to participate. Must precede any study-specific procedures. Sourced from Clinpal eConsent.']
  icf_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) signed by the subject at enrollment. Critical for regulatory compliance to confirm the subject consented under the correct IRB/IEC-approved version. Sourced from Clinpal eConsent and Veeva Vault.']
  itt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). True = subject is in the ITT population. Used by biostatistics for primary efficacy analysis.']
  last_dose_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject received the last dose of investigational product (IP). Defines the end of the treatment-emergent period. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  last_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the subject\'s most recent completed study visit. Used to assess subject engagement, identify lost-to-follow-up risk, and support study close-out activities. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  pp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). True = subject meets all protocol compliance criteria for PP inclusion.']
  randomization_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject was randomized to a treatment arm via the IRT/RTSM system. Null if the subject has not yet been randomized or was screen-failed prior to randomization. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.']
  randomization_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has been confirmed as eligible for randomization following completion of the run-in or screening period. True = subject meets all randomization criteria per protocol. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.']
  safety_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the safety analysis population (typically all subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment). Used for adverse event and safety reporting.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Coded or free-text reason why a subject failed screening and was not enrolled into the study. Populated only when enrollment_status = \'screen_failed\'. Used for site feasibility analysis and protocol amendment decisions.']
  screening_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject first presented at the site and underwent initial screening procedures to assess eligibility for the study. Represents the first contact milestone in the subject lifecycle.']
  screening_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the subject at the time of screening, prior to formal enrollment or randomization. Used to track subjects who may not proceed to enrollment. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this enrollment record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer. Expected values: Oracle_Clinical_One, Oracle_Siebel_CTMS, Medidata_Rave.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native primary key or record identifier from the originating operational system (Oracle Siebel CTMS or Oracle Clinical One) for this enrollment record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit traceability per ALCOA+ principles.']
  stratification_factor_1 varchar [note: 'Value of the first stratification factor used in the randomization scheme (e.g., disease severity, geographic region, biomarker status). Captured at randomization from the IRT/RTSM system. Specific factor definition is protocol-dependent.']
  stratification_factor_2 varchar [note: 'Value of the second stratification factor used in the randomization scheme (e.g., prior treatment history, age group, sex). Captured at randomization from the IRT/RTSM system. Null if the protocol uses fewer than two stratification factors.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the subject at the site level, typically formatted as site-number + sequential subject number (e.g., 001-001). This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER used in eCRFs, EDC, and regulatory submissions. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject\'s treatment assignment was unblinded, if applicable. Populated only for emergency or end-of-study unblinding events. Null for blinded or open-label subjects.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for unblinding the subject\'s treatment assignment. \'emergency\' = medical emergency requiring knowledge of treatment; \'end_of_study\' = routine unblinding at study completion; \'dsmb_request\' = DSMB/DMC-directed unblinding; \'regulatory_request\' = unblinding requested by a regulatory authority.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this enrollment record was last modified in the data platform. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Supports ALCOA+ auditability and change tracking requirements.']
  visit_compliance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of required protocol visits completed by the subject relative to the total number of scheduled visits. Calculated as (completed visits / scheduled visits) × 100. Used for per-protocol population determination and site performance monitoring.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject withdrew from the study or was withdrawn by the investigator. Populated only when enrollment_status = \'withdrawn\'.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason for subject withdrawal from the study (e.g., adverse event, subject decision, lost to follow-up, protocol deviation, sponsor decision). Populated only when enrollment_status = \'withdrawn\'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adverse_event|subject_decision|lost_to_follow_up|protocol_deviation|sponsor_decision|investigator_decision|death|other — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal enrollment event of a subject into a clinical study at a specific investigational site. Tracks screening date, enrollment date, randomization eligibility confirmation, enrollment status (screened, enrolled, randomized, withdrawn, completed), and the site and study context. Represents the lifecycle milestone from first contact through study entry. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Clinical One.'
}

  randomization_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each randomization assignment record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: Randomization assignment is the definitive arm allocation record. FK to study_arm enables IRT-to-CTMS arm reconciliation, SDTM ACTARM derivation, and blinded arm unmasking workflows. treatment_arm_cod']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: The blinding configuration active at randomization must be traceable per subject for unblinding procedure documentation, SAE unblinding policy application, and GCP audit trail. Required for regulatory']
  dosing_regimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen. Business justification: Randomization determines which dosing regimen a subject follows. FK to dosing_regimen enables IP dispensing validation against the assigned regimen, dose escalation cohort tracking, and SDTM EX domain']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: A randomization_assignment is the formal event of randomizing a subject within a specific enrollment context. In clinical trials, a subject may be screened and enrolled multiple times (re-screening, r']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Randomization is epoch-specific (subjects are randomized into the treatment epoch). randomization_assignment currently stores epoch_name as a denormalized plain string. A proper FK to study.epoch norm']
  irt_transaction_id varchar [note: 'The unique transaction reference number generated by the IRT/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One IRT) at the moment of randomization. Serves as the audit trail linkage back to the source system transaction log. Critical for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  prior_assignment_randomization_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the previous randomization assignment record that this record supersedes or replaces. Populated only when re_randomization_flag = True or assignment_status = \'replaced\'. Enables lineage tracking across re-randomization events.']
  rand_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_code. Business justification: IRT/RTSM reconciliation requires proof that the correct randomization code slot was consumed per subject assignment. Auditors and regulators require this traceability link between the assignment recor']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: ICH E9 requires that the exact randomization list version used for each assignment be documented. Linking randomization_assignment to rand_list enables IRT validation audit trail, list version traceab']
  randomization_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: treatment_arm_code and treatment_arm_description on randomization_assignment are denormalized from the canonical treatment_arm definition. FK enables direct join to arm-level protocol parameters for S']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: The SAP defines the randomization scheme (sap.randomization_scheme). When SAP amendments change the randomization method, regulatory submissions require traceability from each randomization event to t']
  stratification_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.stratification_cell. Business justification: Each randomization assignment occupies a specific stratification cell; balance monitoring per cell and SDTM STRAT variable derivation require this link. stratification_factor codes/names on randomizat']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this randomization assignment was generated. Links the assignment to the governing study protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the subject was randomized. Used for site-level randomization balance reporting and monitoring.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Reconciliation between CTMS randomization_assignment records and IRT subject_randomization records is a mandatory data integrity check for SDTM submission and IRT audit. Links the subject-domain and r']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial participant (subject/patient) who received this randomization assignment. Core party reference for this transaction.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this randomization assignment record. \'active\' indicates the assignment is in force; \'cancelled\' indicates it was voided (e.g., randomization error); \'replaced\' indicates superseded by a re-randomization; \'unblinded\' indicates the blind has been broken for this assignment; \'pending\' indicates awaiting confirmation from RTSM.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the blinding state of this randomization assignment. \'blinded\' means the treatment identity is masked; \'open_label\' means the study is non-blinded by design; \'unblinded\' means the blind has been intentionally broken (e.g., for a SAE or emergency unblinding). Critical for data integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for cancellation of this randomization assignment, populated only when assignment_status = \'cancelled\'. Examples include \'Randomization error\', \'Subject ineligible post-randomization\', \'System error\'. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this randomization assignment was cancelled in the RTSM system. Null when assignment_status is not \'cancelled\'. Part of the lifecycle audit trail.']
  cohort_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the cohort or sub-study within the trial to which this randomization assignment belongs (e.g., \'COHORT-A\', \'PART-2\', \'EXPANSION\'). Relevant for multi-cohort, basket, or umbrella trial designs. Null for single-cohort studies.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the investigational site where randomization occurred (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Used for regional randomization balance reporting and regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, PMDA, and other health authorities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization assignment record was first created in the Silver layer lakehouse. Represents the ingestion/creation audit timestamp, distinct from the business event randomization_timestamp. Required for ALCOA+ data integrity compliance.']
  dispensing_visit_number varchar [note: 'The protocol-defined visit number or name at which the investigational product was dispensed following randomization (e.g., \'Visit 2 - Day 1\'). Links the randomization event to the subject visit schedule for compliance tracking.']
  irt_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the IRT/RTSM system that generated this randomization assignment. Supports multi-system environments and audit traceability. Values include \'Medidata Rave RTSM\', \'Oracle Clinical One IRT\', or \'Other\' for legacy/custom systems.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'The unique kit or pack number of the investigational product (IP) dispensed to the subject at randomization, as assigned by the RTSM system. Links the randomization event to the specific IP supply unit for accountability and supply chain traceability.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'The version of the study protocol that was in effect at the time of randomization. Ensures the assignment is traceable to the correct protocol amendment, which is critical for regulatory submissions and deviation assessments.']
  randomization_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the randomization assignment has been confirmed and accepted in the RTSM system (True) or is in a provisional/pending state (False). A confirmed assignment is the authoritative allocation record.']
  randomization_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the subject was randomized, derived from randomization_timestamp for day-level reporting, SDTM mapping, and enrollment tracking. Stored as yyyy-MM-dd. Used in CDISC SDTM DM.RANDDT (date portion).']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'The statistical randomization method used to generate this assignment (e.g., \'block\' for permuted block randomization, \'minimization\' for minimization/adaptive biased-coin, \'adaptive\' for response-adaptive, \'simple\' for simple randomization, \'stratified_block\' for stratified block). Captured from the RTSM configuration at time of assignment.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique randomization code assigned to the subject by the IRT/RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One IRT) at the time of randomization. This is the business identifier referenced in eCRFs, regulatory submissions, and audit trails. Corresponds to RNDMNO or equivalent field in RTSM.']
  randomization_ratio varchar [note: 'The allocation ratio applied at randomization (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1\', \'1:1:1\'). Represents the relative probability of assignment to each treatment arm as defined in the protocol and RTSM configuration.']
  randomization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the subject was randomized as recorded by the IRT/RTSM system. This is the authoritative business event timestamp for the randomization transaction. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  re_randomization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assignment is a re-randomization event (True) rather than the initial randomization (False). Re-randomization may occur in crossover designs, adaptive trials, or following a protocol deviation that necessitates re-assignment.']
  sdtm_randfl varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM-compliant randomization flag (Y/N) indicating whether the subject was randomized, as mapped to the DM.RANDFL variable. Populated during SDTM transformation to support regulatory submission datasets.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating IRT/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One IRT). Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver layer lakehouse and the operational source system for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  stratification_factor_3_code varchar [note: 'The code or value of the third stratification factor applied at randomization. Null if the study protocol defines fewer than three stratification factors.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for breaking the blind on this assignment, populated only when blinding_status = \'unblinded\'. Examples include \'SAE requiring treatment knowledge\', \'DSMB interim analysis\', \'Subject withdrawal\'. Null when blinding is intact.']
  unblinding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the blind was broken for this randomization assignment. Null when blinding_status is \'blinded\' or \'open_label\'. Used for safety reporting timelines and DSMB audit trails.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization assignment record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Tracks data currency and supports incremental processing. Required for ALCOA+ data integrity compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a subject\'s randomization event, capturing the randomization code, treatment arm assignment, stratification factor values applied at randomization, randomization date and time, IRT/RTSM transaction reference, blinding status, and kit number assigned. Represents the authoritative allocation record produced by the RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM / Oracle Clinical One IRT). Distinct from the randomization scheme design owned by the randomization domain.'
}

Table subject.subject_visit {
  subject_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subject visit record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the subject_visit data product.']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) visit form in the EDC system (Medidata Rave) associated with this visit. Links visit scheduling metadata to the data collection form for completeness tracking.']
  edc_study_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: Each subject visit is conducted and data captured under a specific EDC build version. Audit trail requirements (21 CFR Part 11, ICH E6) demand knowing which build was active at visit time. This suppor']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: A subject visit is performed within the context of a specific enrollment. One enrollment has many visits. Adding enrollment_id to subject_visit properly scopes each visit to its enrollment event, enab']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: Each subject visit occurs within a study epoch. FK to study_epoch enables epoch-level visit compliance analysis, SDTM SV epoch derivation, and epoch-based data review in EDC. visit_epoch is a denormal']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Principal Investigator (PI) or sub-investigator who conducted or supervised this visit at the investigational site. Required for regulatory accountability per GCP.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where this visit was conducted or was scheduled to occur. Supports site-level visit compliance monitoring.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Each subject visit is an instance of a planned visit in study_visit_schedule. This FK enables visit window compliance reporting (planned vs actual day), protocol deviation detection for missed/out-of-']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Visit window compliance and SDTM VISITDY derivation require computing study day relative to randomization date. Linking subject_visit directly to subject_randomization enables this without routing thr']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial participant (subject/patient) who performed or missed this visit. Core party reference for the transaction.']
  actual_visit_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the subject attended the visit and assessments were performed. Maps to SVSTDTC in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Null if the visit was missed or not yet conducted. Principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  actual_visit_end_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the visit concluded, applicable for multi-day visits or inpatient stays. Maps to SVENDTC in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Null for single-day visits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subject visit record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_entry_status varchar [note: 'Status of eCRF data entry for this visit in the EDC system (Medidata Rave), indicating whether data collection has been initiated, is in progress, completed, locked, or frozen. Used for data management tracking and database lock readiness assessment.']
  days_from_planned varchar [note: 'Signed integer representing the deviation in days between the actual visit date and the planned visit date (actual minus planned). Negative values indicate early visits; positive values indicate late visits. Zero indicates on-schedule. Used for visit compliance analytics and protocol deviation assessment.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the visit in minutes from check-in to check-out as recorded in the CTMS or EDC. Used for site capacity planning, resource utilization analysis, and subject burden assessment.']
  early_termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for early termination of the visit or the subject\'s participation, applicable when visit_type is \'early_termination\'. Captures clinical, safety, or administrative reasons per protocol requirements.']
  informed_consent_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a valid, signed Informed Consent Form (ICF) was confirmed on file prior to this visit (True) or not (False). Critical GCP compliance requirement; absence may constitute a major protocol deviation.']
  is_scheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this visit was a protocol-scheduled visit (True) or an unscheduled/ad-hoc visit not defined in the protocol schedule of assessments (False). Unscheduled visits may indicate safety events or protocol deviations.']
  is_unblinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment assignment was unblinded at or prior to this visit (True) or the subject remained blinded (False). Relevant for double-blind studies and impacts data integrity assessments.']
  missed_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why a scheduled visit was missed by the subject (e.g., subject unavailable, adverse event, travel, withdrew consent, site closure). Populated only when visit_status is \'missed\'. Required for protocol deviation assessment and regulatory reporting.']
  missed_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized coded value for the reason a scheduled visit was missed, aligned with sponsor or protocol-defined coding scheme. Complements missed_reason free text for structured analytics and regulatory submissions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical or operational notes recorded by the site staff or CRA regarding this visit, including observations about subject condition, procedural issues, or site-specific circumstances. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS or Medidata Rave.']
  planned_visit_date date [note: 'The target date on which the visit was scheduled to occur per the protocol-defined visit schedule, calculated from the subject\'s reference date (e.g., randomization date or first dose date). Used to compute visit window compliance.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this visit resulted in a protocol deviation (True), such as a visit outside the allowed window, missed required assessments, or procedural non-compliance. Triggers downstream protocol deviation record creation in the compliance domain.']
  query_count varchar [note: 'Number of open data discrepancy queries (DCFs) associated with this visit\'s eCRF data in the EDC system. Used for data quality monitoring and site performance tracking. Non-zero values may block database lock.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this visit record was sourced (Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Siebel CTMS, Oracle Clinical One, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.']
  source_system_visit_key varchar [note: 'The native primary key or unique identifier for this visit record in the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave visit OID, Siebel CTMS visit row ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the system of record.']
  study_day varchar [note: 'The relative study day on which the visit occurred, calculated from the subject\'s reference start date per CDISC convention (Day 1 = reference date; Day -1 = day before; no Day 0). Maps to SVSTDY in CDISC SDTM SV domain.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this subject visit record in the source system or Silver layer. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  visit_code varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the visit within the study schedule (e.g., SCR, BL, V01, V02, ET, EOS). Maps to VISITNUM/VISIT in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Used as the externally-known business identifier for the visit.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier for the visit as defined in the study protocol schedule of assessments. Maps directly to VISITNUM in CDISC SDTM SV domain. Used for ordering visits chronologically within a subject\'s participation.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current completion status of the visit indicating whether the subject attended and completed all required assessments, missed the visit entirely, partially completed assessments, had the visit rescheduled, or the visit was cancelled. Core lifecycle status field for monitoring trigger logic.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the visit by its role in the study protocol lifecycle. Drives SDTM SV domain population and visit compliance analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screening|baseline|treatment|follow_up|unscheduled|early_termination|end_of_study — promote to reference product]']
  week varchar [note: 'The nominal study week associated with this visit as defined in the protocol schedule of assessments (e.g., Week 1, Week 4, Week 12). Used for time-point labeling in biostatistical analyses and ADaM dataset derivations.']
  window_close_date date [note: 'The latest acceptable date for the visit to occur per the protocol-defined visit window (planned date plus the allowed late deviation in days). Used to determine visit window compliance status.']
  window_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the actual visit date fell within the protocol-defined visit window, occurred early (before window open), occurred late (after window close), was missed entirely, or is not applicable (e.g., unscheduled visits). Critical for protocol deviation identification and monitoring trigger logic.']
  window_open_date date [note: 'The earliest acceptable date for the visit to occur per the protocol-defined visit window (planned date minus the allowed early deviation in days). Used to determine visit window compliance status.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each scheduled or unscheduled study visit performed by (or missed by) a subject, capturing visit name, visit code, planned visit date per schedule, actual visit date, visit window compliance status (early, within window, late, missed), visit type (screening, baseline, treatment, follow-up, unscheduled, early termination, end-of-study), investigational site where visit occurred, visit duration, and visit completion status. Serves as the backbone for visit compliance tracking, monitoring trigger logic, and SDTM SV domain generation. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC and Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

  compliance_deviation_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.deviation_classification. Business justification: GCP compliance requires every subject deviation to be classified against a standard taxonomy to determine regulatory reporting obligations, CAPA triggers, and CSR inclusion. The deviation_classificati']
  correspondence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.correspondence. Business justification: subject_deviation has regulatory_reportable_flag and irb_iec_reportable_flag. Regulatory-reportable deviations generate formal correspondence with health authorities. Linking subject_deviation to regu']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Protocol deviations are assessed against the specific SOP that was violated or not followed. Regulatory inspectors and QA teams require traceability from each deviation record to the governing SOP ver']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Protocol deviations are identified during EDC data review against specific eCRF forms. Clinical data managers and monitors must trace which form triggered the deviation for CAPA tracking, regulatory r']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: Eligibility deviations (enrolling a subject who fails an inclusion/exclusion criterion) must be traceable to the specific criterion violated for FDA/EMA inspection readiness and IRB reporting. The rev']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Protocol deviations are enrollment-specific events — they occur within the context of a subject\'s participation in a specific study enrollment. Adding enrollment_id to subject_deviation properly scope']
  ethics_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_submission. Business justification: Subject deviations with irb_iec_reportable_flag=true must be reported to the IRB/IEC via a formal ethics submission. Linking the deviation to the submission that reported it supports regulatory inspec']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: IND annual reports (21 CFR 312.33) require sponsors to summarize protocol deviations by IND. Directly linking subject_deviation to ind_application enables automated IND annual report compilation and F']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the deviation occurred. Used for site-level deviation trending, monitoring, and risk assessment.']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: IRB/IEC-reportable deviations (subject_deviation.irb_iec_reportable_flag) must reference the specific approval record to track submission status, approved ICF version at time of deviation, and continu']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Protocol deviations are frequently identified through abnormal lab results (e.g., subject continued dosing despite creatinine exceeding stopping rule). Linking subject_deviation to the triggering lab_']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: GCP requires PI awareness and sign-off on protocol deviations. Regulatory submissions (IND annual reports, inspection responses) must identify the responsible PI per deviation. Delegation log verifica']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Subject-level deviation records in the EDC are formally escalated and registered as protocol deviations in the compliance system. This FK links the subject-facing deviation record to the formal compli']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: GCP regulatory inspections specifically audit and cite protocol deviations. Inspectors review deviation logs and may formally cite individual deviations as findings. Linking subject_deviation to regul']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: Protocol deviations are assessed against the SAP version in effect to determine their impact on analysis population inclusion and data integrity. Regulatory submissions require deviation impact assess']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this deviation was identified. Links the deviation to the governing protocol and sponsor.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Deviations are assessed against the protocol amendment in effect at occurrence. Linking to study_protocol_amendment enables amendment-driven deviation trend analysis and regulatory submission of devia']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: Risk-based monitoring reports and protocol deviation trending are performed at the study_site level (not just investigational_site). study_site carries study-specific risk tier, protocol number, and m']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Protocol deviations are frequently identified at or associated with a specific study visit. subject_deviation carries a denormalized visit_name (STRING) that should be normalized via FK to subject_vis']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Protocol deviations must be formally documented and filed in the TMF per ICH E6(R2) and sponsor SOPs. Regulatory inspectors verify that each deviation has a corresponding TMF-filed deviation report. T']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) for whom this protocol deviation was recorded. Core party reference linking the deviation to the enrolled participant.']
  validation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.validation_rule. Business justification: Deviations are frequently triggered by EDC validation rule failures (e.g., out-of-window visit, prohibited medication). Linking deviation to the triggering validation rule supports root-cause analysis']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: Protocol deviation management requires traceability to the specific planned procedure that was violated or omitted. CAPA tracking, site quality reports, and FDA/EMA inspection readiness all require pr']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Deviation resolution follows a formal document workflow: review, sponsor notification, CAPA assignment, and closure approval. Clinical trial management systems track this workflow lifecycle. This FK l']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the deviation record was formally closed in the quality management system. Provides higher precision than resolution_date for audit and compliance reporting.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or contextual notes related to the deviation that do not fit into structured fields. May include reviewer notes, sponsor feedback, or monitoring observations.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the immediate corrective action taken to address the deviation and prevent recurrence at the subject or site level. Distinct from the formal CAPA process which is tracked separately in MasterControl.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deviation record was first created in the source system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  data_integrity_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the deviation compromised the integrity of study data collected from the subject. Informs decisions on data exclusion, sensitivity analyses, and per-protocol (PP) population definitions.']
  deviation_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the deviation by severity and impact. \'Major\' deviations significantly affect subject safety or data integrity; \'important\' deviations may affect subject rights or study data; \'minor\' deviations have minimal impact. Drives regulatory reportability and CAPA requirements per ICH E6(R2).']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the specific deviation that occurred, including what protocol requirement was not met, the circumstances, and any relevant context. Captured by the Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC) or Clinical Research Associate (CRA) in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  deviation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the deviation record as assigned by the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) or MasterControl. Used for cross-system referencing, regulatory submissions, and audit trail purposes.']
  deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the deviation record. Tracks progression from initial identification through review, resolution, and closure. Drives operational dashboards and compliance monitoring in Oracle Siebel CTMS and MasterControl.']
  identified_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the deviation was first identified or discovered, which may differ from the occurrence date. Used to calculate detection lag and assess monitoring effectiveness.']
  irb_iec_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) overseeing the study. Separate from regulatory authority reportability. True = must report to IRB/IEC.']
  occurrence_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the protocol deviation actually occurred. This is the principal real-world event date, distinct from the date the deviation was identified or reported. Critical for ALCOA+ compliance and regulatory timelines.']
  pi_aware_date date [note: 'The date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) was informed of the deviation. Used to track compliance with site notification requirements.']
  pi_aware_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) at the site has been made aware of this deviation. True = PI has been informed. Required for GCP compliance as the PI bears ultimate responsibility for conduct of the trial at the site.']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section, clause, or requirement number within the approved study protocol that was deviated from (e.g., \'Section 6.3.2 - Inclusion Criteria\'). Enables precise traceability to the protocol document.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.), IRB/IEC, or sponsor per applicable regulations and the study protocol. True = reportable; False = not reportable. Drives regulatory submission workflows.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was formally reported to the sponsor, IRB/IEC, or regulatory authority as applicable. Used to track compliance with reporting timelines mandated by GCP and applicable regulations.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was formally resolved and closed in the quality management system. Used to calculate time-to-resolution metrics and assess site responsiveness.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying root cause of the deviation as determined through investigation. Used to inform corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) and systemic quality improvement. Captured in MasterControl.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause to enable trending and systemic analysis across deviations. Categories align with standard quality management frameworks used in MasterControl.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain to which this deviation maps for regulatory submission. Protocol deviations are mapped to the DV (Protocol Deviations) domain per CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this deviation record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. Primary sources are Oracle Siebel CTMS and MasterControl.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (Oracle Siebel CTMS or MasterControl). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  sponsor_notified_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the deviation. Used to verify compliance with contractual and regulatory notification timelines.']
  sponsor_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this deviation. True = sponsor has been notified. Required per GCP obligations for sponsor oversight of investigational sites.']
  subject_continuation_decision varchar [note: 'Decision regarding the subject\'s continued participation in the study following the deviation. Informs subject disposition tracking and intent-to-treat (ITT) and per-protocol (PP) population assignments.']
  subject_rights_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the deviation affected the rights or well-being of the trial subject, including informed consent procedures, privacy protections, or voluntary participation rights.']
  subject_safety_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the deviation had an impact on subject safety. \'None\' indicates no safety impact; \'potential\' indicates possible but unconfirmed impact; \'confirmed\' indicates a verified safety impact. Critical for regulatory reporting decisions and DSMB/DMC review.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific type of protocol deviation (e.g., eligibility criteria violation, missed visit, incorrect dosing, prohibited medication use, consent procedure deviation). Sourced from the MasterControl deviation classification taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ELIG_CRIT|MISSED_VISIT|INCORRECT_DOSE|PROHIBITED_MED|CONSENT_PROC|RANDOMIZATION|SAMPLE_TIMING|WASHOUT|CONCOMITANT_MED|ENDPOINT_ASSESS — promote to reference product]']
  type_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable label corresponding to the deviation_type_code, providing a plain-language description of the deviation type for reporting and review purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this deviation record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a protocol deviation identified for a subject, capturing deviation category (major, minor, important), deviation type code, description, date of occurrence, date identified, root cause, impact on subject safety and data integrity, corrective action taken, CAPA reference, and regulatory reportability flag. Tracks deviations from the approved protocol per ICH E6(R2) GCP requirements. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS and MasterControl.'
}

Table subject.disposition {
  disposition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subject disposition milestone record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the disposition data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete formal disposition event in the subject study lifecycle.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Disposition records AE-driven discontinuations (ae_related_discontinuation flag). SDTM DS domain mapping (DSDECOD=\'ADVERSE EVENT\') and regulatory narratives require identifying the specific causative ']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: Disposition records capture the arm a subject was in at discontinuation or completion. FK to study_arm enables arm-level completion/discontinuation analysis for CSR disposition tables and SDTM DS doma']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Disposition data is captured on the DS eCRF form; CRAs must reference the specific form version during SDV and SDTM DS domain generation. The existing ecrf_form_reference is a denormalized text field;']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: A disposition milestone (completion, withdrawal, screen failure) is a formal event within a specific enrollment. One enrollment can have multiple disposition milestones (e.g., screen failure then re-s']
  estimand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.estimand. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) estimand framework requires disposition events (treatment discontinuation, death) to be classified as intercurrent events per the estimand\'s ICE strategy. Regulatory submissions and CSR Sec']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Disposition events (completion, withdrawal, death) require PI sign-off per GCP. Regulatory submissions (CSR, IND safety reports) must identify the responsible PI for each subject disposition. Inspecti']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protocol deviation record that directly caused or is associated with the subject\'s discontinuation. Populated when the coded reason is PROTOCOL DEVIATION. Links disposition to the compliance domain.']
  randomization_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.randomization_assignment. Business justification: Disposition records for randomized subjects should reference the specific randomization assignment that was active at the time of disposition. This enables direct linkage between a subject\'s final stu']
  randomization_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: CONSORT flow and ITT/PP disposition reporting require disposition records linked directly to the randomized treatment_arm entity. disposition.arm_id points to study.arm (design entity), but regulatory']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: The SAP defines how disposition data is analyzed — CONSORT flow, withdrawal analysis, and DS domain TLFs are all SAP-specified outputs. Linking disposition to the governing SAP version supports regula']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this disposition event occurred. Links the disposition record to the parent study master.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the subject\'s disposition event was recorded. Used for site-level CONSORT flow and enrollment reporting.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: SDTM DS domain and CONSORT flow reporting require disposition records to be traceable to the specific IRT randomization event. Treatment-related discontinuation analysis depends on this link; randomiz']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial participant (subject/patient) whose formal disposition milestone is being recorded. Core PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC subject master.']
  ae_related_discontinuation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject\'s discontinuation was due to an adverse event (AE) or serious adverse event (SAE). When True, links to the safety domain for AE-driven disposition analysis and regulatory safety reporting.']
  analysis_population_itt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population based on disposition and protocol criteria. Drives primary efficacy analysis dataset inclusion. Determined by biostatistics per the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  analysis_population_pp boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population. Subjects with major protocol deviations affecting efficacy assessment are typically excluded. Determined by biostatistics per the SAP.']
  analysis_population_safety boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is included in the Safety analysis population (subjects who received at least one dose of investigational product). Drives safety tabulations in the CSR and regulatory submissions.']
  coded_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized coded reason for discontinuation or withdrawal after medical coding against MedDRA or sponsor-specific dictionary. Corresponds to SDTM DSDECOD. Used in CSR disposition tables and regulatory submissions.']
  data_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED — timestamp when the disposition record was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous data entry requirements and audit trail compliance.']
  death_cause varchar [note: 'Verbatim or coded cause of death for subjects with DEATH disposition. Used in safety narratives, CSR, and regulatory submissions. Coded against MedDRA when applicable.']
  death_date date [note: 'Date of subject death when the disposition term is DEATH. Required for survival analysis, time-to-event endpoints, and expedited safety reporting. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC death records and Oracle Argus Safety.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) — the calendar date on which the formal disposition milestone occurred for the subject (e.g., date of randomization, date of last study visit, date of withdrawal). Corresponds to SDTM DSSTDTC (start date component). Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'LIFECYCLE_STATUS of this disposition record within the EDC/data management workflow. Indicates whether the record is the current active disposition, has been superseded by a correction, is pending data review, or has been cancelled. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC record status.']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Full ISO 8601 timestamp of the disposition event when time-of-day precision is available. Supplements disposition_date for events requiring precise timing (e.g., time of death, time of last dose). Corresponds to SDTM DSSTDTC full datetime.']
  ds_seq varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the order of disposition records for a given subject within the study, corresponding to the SDTM DSSEQ variable. Supports multiple disposition milestones per subject (e.g., enrolled then discontinued).']
  early_termination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject terminated study participation before completing all protocol-required activities. Distinct from study_completion_flag in that it specifically flags premature exit for operational tracking and site performance metrics.']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the subject\'s last known contact with the study team, including phone calls, remote visits, or any study-related communication. Used for lost-to-follow-up determination and survival analysis censoring.']
  last_dose_date date [note: 'Date of the subject\'s last administration of investigational product (IP) prior to or at the disposition milestone. Critical for calculating treatment duration, exposure windows, and safety follow-up periods. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC dosing records.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED — timestamp of the most recent modification to this disposition record in the EDC system. Supports audit trail requirements and data change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  last_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the subject\'s last scheduled or unscheduled study visit prior to or at the disposition milestone. Used to determine last study contact and assess visit compliance. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC visit records.']
  meddra_pt varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) assigned to the coded discontinuation reason. Enables standardized cross-study analysis and regulatory reporting of disposition reasons. Populated by the medical coding process.']
  meddra_soc varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) associated with the coded discontinuation reason Preferred Term. Supports regulatory tabulation and signal detection for disposition-related safety analysis.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding the disposition reason (e.g., 26.0, 26.1). Required for regulatory submission traceability and reproducibility of coded terms.']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the type of disposition milestone. Used to group disposition events for CONSORT flow diagram construction and CSR disposition tables. Aligns with SDTM DSCAT variable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SCREENING|ENROLLMENT|RANDOMIZATION|TREATMENT|FOLLOW_UP|COMPLETION|DISCONTINUATION|WITHDRAWAL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  milestone_subcategory varchar [note: 'Sponsor-defined subcategory further classifying the disposition milestone within its category (e.g., PROTOCOL DEVIATION, ADVERSE EVENT, SPONSOR DECISION under DISCONTINUATION). Corresponds to SDTM DSSCAT variable.']
