  query_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is at least one open data discrepancy query against this disposition record in the EDC system. Supports data management oversight and database lock (DBL) readiness tracking.']
  safety_followup_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual end date of the post-discontinuation safety follow-up period. Populated when safety_followup_required is True. Used to track completion of safety obligations for discontinued subjects.']
  safety_followup_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject requires a post-discontinuation safety follow-up period per protocol requirements. True when the subject discontinued early and protocol mandates continued safety monitoring. Drives safety follow-up visit scheduling.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a screened subject failed to meet eligibility criteria and was not enrolled. Populated only for SCREEN FAILURE disposition. Used for site feasibility analysis and protocol amendment decisions.']
  sdtm_ds_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique record identifier from the CDISC SDTM DS (Disposition) domain dataset, enabling traceability between the Silver Layer lakehouse record and the regulatory submission dataset. Supports eCTD submission package linkage.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Status of source data verification (SDV) for this disposition record, indicating whether the CRA has verified the data against source documents. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS monitoring records. Supports GCP compliance and monitoring oversight.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this disposition record was sourced. Primarily Medidata Rave EDC for disposition data. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements in the Silver Layer.']
  study_completion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject completed the study per protocol (True) or did not complete (False). Drives CONSORT flow diagram completion counts and CSR disposition summary tables.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the subject at the site (e.g., 001-001). Corresponds to SDTM USUBJID or site-assigned subject number. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  term varchar [note: 'Standardized disposition term describing the subject\'s formal study milestone (e.g., COMPLETED, SCREEN FAILURE, DISCONTINUED, WITHDRAWN BY SUBJECT, LOST TO FOLLOW-UP, DEATH). Corresponds to SDTM DSTERM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: COMPLETED|SCREEN FAILURE|DISCONTINUED|WITHDRAWN BY SUBJECT|LOST TO FOLLOW-UP|DEATH|ENROLLED|RANDOMIZED — promote to reference product]']
  verbatim_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text verbatim reason for discontinuation or withdrawal as entered by the investigator or site staff in the eCRF. Captured prior to coding. Supports ALCOA+ attributability and audit trail requirements. Populated only for discontinuation/withdrawal milestones.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each formal disposition milestone of a subject through the study lifecycle (screened, enrolled, randomized, completed, discontinued, withdrawn, lost to follow-up, death). Records disposition term per MedDRA or sponsor-specific coding, disposition date, reason for discontinuation or withdrawal (verbatim and coded), initiator (subject, investigator, sponsor), last dose date, last visit date, safety follow-up requirement, and last study contact date. Represents the SDTM DS domain SSOT and drives CONSORT flow diagrams and CSR disposition tables. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.'
}

Table subject.eligibility_assessment {
  eligibility_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each eligibility assessment record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protocol deviation record created when a subject was enrolled despite failing this eligibility criterion. Populated only when protocol_deviation_flag is True.']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Inclusion/exclusion criteria are captured on the IE eCRF form. Linking eligibility_assessment to ecrf_form normalizes the denormalized edc_form_name, supports SDV of eligibility data, and enables SDTM']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: Each eligibility assessment row evaluates one protocol-defined criterion. FK to eligibility_criterion enables SDTM IE domain derivation, criterion-level screen failure rate analysis, and waiver tracki']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Eligibility assessments (inclusion/exclusion criteria checks) are performed as part of the screening phase of a specific enrollment. Linking eligibility_assessment to enrollment enables direct queryin']
  ethics_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_approval. Business justification: Eligibility criteria assessments must be conducted under the protocol version authorized by a valid ethics approval. This link enables verification that eligibility assessments used criteria from an I']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: Eligibility assessments must be conducted under a valid IRB/IEC approval with a specific approved ICF version and max enrollment cap. eligibility_assessment.irb_iec_approval_required flag exists but n']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Eligibility criteria are frequently lab-based (eGFR, HbA1c, platelet count). The eligibility_assessment must reference the specific lab_result that drove the pass/fail decision to support SDV, regulat']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: The PI or delegated sub-investigator conducting eligibility assessments must be identified for GCP compliance, delegation log verification, and regulatory inspection readiness. assessor_role exists as']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Eligibility criterion failures or waivers (e.g., enrolling a subject who does not fully meet inclusion/exclusion criteria) are formally documented as protocol deviations. This FK links the eligibility']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this eligibility assessment was performed. Links the assessment to the governing protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Eligibility criteria change with protocol amendments; the amendment version governing an assessment is critical for regulatory submissions and screen failure analysis. FK to study_protocol_amendment e']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the eligibility assessment was conducted.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Eligibility assessments are performed during screening visits. eligibility_assessment carries a denormalized screening_visit_number (STRING) that should be normalized via FK to subject_visit. Adding s']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial participant (subject/patient) whose eligibility is being assessed. Core party reference for this transactional record.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the eligibility criterion was assessed for the subject. Principal real-world event date for this transactional record. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE.IEDTC (date portion).']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this eligibility assessment, typically generated by the EDC system (Medidata Rave or Oracle Clinical One). Used for cross-system traceability and audit trail references.']
  assessment_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the eligibility criterion assessment for this subject. MET indicates the criterion was satisfied; NOT MET indicates failure; NOT ASSESSED indicates the criterion was not evaluated. Maps to CDISC SDTM IE.IEORRES.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the eligibility criterion assessment was recorded in the EDC system. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  assessor_role varchar [note: 'Role of the site staff member who performed the eligibility assessment (e.g., Principal Investigator, Sub-Investigator, Clinical Research Coordinator). Required for GCP delegation log compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or clinical notes entered by the assessor regarding this eligibility criterion assessment. May include clarifications, borderline findings, or contextual information relevant to the eligibility decision.']
  data_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this eligibility assessment record was first entered into the EDC system. Distinct from assessment_timestamp (when assessment occurred). Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous data entry monitoring.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this eligibility assessment record was sourced (Medidata Rave EDC or Oracle Clinical One). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  edc_record_reference varchar [note: 'Source system record identifier from the EDC platform (Medidata Rave or Oracle Clinical One) for this eligibility assessment entry. Enables traceability back to the source system for SDV and audit.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRB/IEC approval is required for the waiver or exception associated with this eligibility criterion assessment. Drives regulatory workflow routing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this eligibility assessment record was last updated in the source EDC system. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and ALCOA+ data integrity compliance.']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Data lock status of this eligibility assessment record in the EDC system. LOCKED records cannot be modified without an audit-trailed unlock. Supports Database Lock (DBL) readiness and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  not_met_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason explaining why the subject did not meet this eligibility criterion. Populated when assessment_result is NOT MET. Supports clinical review and protocol deviation analysis.']
  overall_eligibility_status varchar [note: 'Overall eligibility determination for the subject based on the complete set of inclusion/exclusion criteria assessments. Drives subject enrollment decision. Lifecycle status for this assessment record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ELIGIBLE|INELIGIBLE|PENDING|WAIVER_REQUESTED|WAIVER_APPROVED|WAIVER_DENIED — promote to reference product]']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eligibility criterion assessment resulted in a protocol deviation (i.e., subject enrolled despite not meeting a criterion without an approved waiver). Triggers deviation reporting workflow.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the study protocol under which this eligibility assessment was performed. Critical for audit trail when protocol amendments change inclusion/exclusion criteria.']
  query_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an open data discrepancy query exists against this eligibility assessment record in the EDC system. Supports data cleaning status monitoring and DBL readiness tracking.']
  re_screening_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eligibility assessment is part of a re-screening attempt following an initial screen failure. Supports enrollment funnel analytics and re-screening rate KPI reporting.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Coded or free-text reason for screen failure when the overall eligibility determination is INELIGIBLE. Used for enrollment analytics, site performance reporting, and protocol feasibility review.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code for this eligibility assessment record. Always \'IE\' (Inclusion/Exclusion Criteria Not Met) for regulatory submission datasets. Supports SDTM mapping and NDA/BLA/MAA submission.']
  sdtm_sequence_number varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM sequence number (IESEQ) uniquely identifying this record within the IE domain for a given subject. Required for regulatory submission dataset construction.']
  sdv_date date [note: 'Date on which Source Data Verification (SDV) was completed for this eligibility assessment record by the CRA. Null when SDV has not yet been performed.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Status of Source Data Verification (SDV) performed by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) for this eligibility assessment record. Tracks monitoring compliance per the monitoring plan.']
  source_data_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document or data entry (e.g., medical record number, lab report ID, physical examination note) that supports the eligibility criterion assessment. Required for Source Data Verification (SDV) by CRA.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor or IRB/IEC approved the protocol exception or waiver for this eligibility criterion. Null when no waiver was requested or waiver was denied.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical or scientific justification provided by the investigator for requesting a protocol exception or waiver for this eligibility criterion. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  waiver_requested boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation waiver or exception was requested for this criterion when the subject did not meet it. True when a waiver request has been submitted to the sponsor or IRB/IEC.']
  waiver_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the protocol-specified exception or waiver request for this eligibility criterion. Applicable only when waiver_requested is True. Tracks sponsor or IRB/IEC decision.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of the inclusion/exclusion criteria assessment performed for a subject during screening. Captures each criterion code, criterion text, assessment result (met/not met/not assessed), assessment date, assessor identity, and overall eligibility determination. Tracks waiver requests and protocol-specified exceptions. Provides the audit trail for subject eligibility decisions required by GCP. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC and Oracle Clinical One.'
}

Table subject.status_history {
  status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subject status transition record in the lifecycle history table. Primary key for the subject_status_history data product.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Subject status transitions (withdrawal, early termination, study suspension) triggered by an AE require referencing the causative event for regulatory audit trail and GCP compliance. Medical monitors ']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Subject status at database lock is critical for analysis dataset construction; the final status (completed, withdrawn, etc.) captured at DBL determines CONSORT flow categories and analysis population ']
  disposition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.disposition. Business justification: Disposition milestones (completion, withdrawal, screen failure, death) are the formal clinical events that drive subject status transitions. The subject_status_history table records every status trans']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Subject status transitions (screening, enrolled, randomized, completed, withdrawn) are enrollment-specific lifecycle events. A subject may be re-screened across multiple enrollments, so status history']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: Subject status transitions (screening, randomization, treatment, follow-up, completion) occur within defined study epochs. FK to study_epoch enables CONSORT diagram epoch-level subject flow analysis a']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the subject status transition was recorded. Supports site-level enrollment flow analysis and monitoring.']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: subject_status_history.irb_iec_approval_required flag indicates some transitions require IRB/IEC notification (e.g., SAE-driven withdrawal, suspension). Linking to the specific approval record enables']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: GCP requires PI awareness of subject status transitions (withdrawal, discontinuation, death). Regulatory inspection readiness and delegation log audits require identifying the PI responsible for each ']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol deviation record associated with this status transition, when protocol_deviation_flag is True. Links to the compliance.protocol_deviation product for full deviation details.']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Status transitions triggered by protocol amendments (reconsent required, arm reassignment, eligibility re-evaluation) must reference the causative amendment for regulatory audit trails and IND/CTA rep']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this subject status transition occurred. Links the history record to the governing study protocol.']
  study_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_milestone. Business justification: Subject status transitions (first subject enrolled, last subject completed) directly trigger and confirm study milestone achievements in CTMS. Linking status history to the milestone it fulfills enabl']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: CONSORT flow diagrams and enrollment performance dashboards aggregate subject status transitions at the study_site level. study_site provides study-specific enrollment targets and monitoring context n']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: ICH E6 R2 audit trail requires status transitions (Randomized, Re-randomized, Unblinded) to be traceable to the specific IRT event that triggered them. randomization_number, treatment_arm_code, treatm']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Subject status transitions are frequently triggered by visit events — for example, a subject completing a screening visit transitions to \'Screened\', completing a baseline visit transitions to \'Enrolle']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (participant) whose enrollment lifecycle status changed. Core party reference for this event log record.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the status transition was formally approved or electronically signed by the authorized user. Distinct from transition_timestamp (when the event occurred) and created_timestamp (when the record was created).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional clinical or administrative comments related to this status transition, entered by the site staff or sponsor. Supports contextual documentation for audit trail completeness per ALCOA+ standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this status history record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements. Distinct from transition_timestamp (when the clinical event occurred).']
  disposition_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the disposition event type associated with this status transition. Used for regulatory reporting in the CDISC SDTM DS domain and for subject flow summaries in Clinical Study Reports (CSRs). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screen-failure|early-termination|completion|randomization|enrollment|withdrawal|protocol-deviation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status transition required prior approval from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) (e.g., re-consent due to protocol amendment). True = IRB/IEC approval was required.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject\'s treatment assignment was blinded at the time of this status transition. True = blinded (treatment identity concealed); False = unblinded (treatment identity known). Critical for maintaining trial integrity and access control.']
  is_terminal_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the new_status represents a terminal (end-state) disposition for the subject in this study. True = the subject has reached a final state (completed, withdrawn, screen-failed, lost-to-follow-up) with no further expected transitions.']
  itt_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was included in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population at the time of this status transition. Supports biostatistical analysis population tracking per the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  new_status varchar [note: 'The subject\'s enrollment lifecycle status after this transition event. Represents the current state the subject entered as a result of this change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre-screened|screened|screen-failed|enrolled|randomized|on-treatment|completed|withdrawn|lost-to-follow-up|discontinued — promote to reference product]']
  pp_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was included in the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population at the time of this status transition. Supports biostatistical analysis population tracking per the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  prior_status varchar [note: 'The subject\'s enrollment lifecycle status immediately before this transition event. Together with new_status, defines the state transition vector for longitudinal subject flow analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre-screened|screened|screen-failed|enrolled|randomized|on-treatment|completed|withdrawn|lost-to-follow-up|discontinued — promote to reference product]']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation (PD) was associated with or triggered this status transition. True = a protocol deviation is linked to this transition event. Supports GCP compliance monitoring and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  safety_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was included in the safety analysis population at the time of this status transition (i.e., received at least one dose of investigational product). Supports safety reporting and TEAE analysis.']
  screen_failure_reason varchar [note: 'Specific reason why the subject failed screening and did not proceed to enrollment. Populated only when new_status = \'screen-failed\'. Used for site feasibility analysis, protocol amendment decisions, and enrollment optimization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this status transition record was sourced. Either Oracle Clinical One (study design and subject management) or Medidata Rave (EDC and RTSM). Supports data lineage tracking in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_key varchar [note: 'The native primary key or unique identifier of this status transition record in the originating source system (Oracle Clinical One or Medidata Rave). Enables traceability back to the system of record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  study_day varchar [note: 'The relative study day on which the status transition occurred, calculated from the subject\'s reference start date (typically first dose date). Negative values indicate pre-treatment days. Aligns with CDISC SDTM timing variable conventions.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric subject identifier assigned at enrollment (e.g., site-sequential number such as \'001-001\'). Used in regulatory submissions, eCRFs, and audit trails to identify the participant without revealing personal identity. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave.']
  transition_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the subject\'s status transition occurred in the real-world clinical context. This is the principal business event date used for enrollment timelines, regulatory reporting, and subject flow analysis. Distinct from the system capture timestamp.']
  transition_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the clinical or administrative reason for the subject status change (e.g., \'Subject met all inclusion/exclusion criteria\', \'Subject withdrew consent\', \'Adverse event led to discontinuation\'). Supports regulatory audit trails and disposition analysis.']
  transition_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized coded value for the transition reason, aligned with CDISC SDTM DS domain disposition event codes (e.g., \'COMPLETED\', \'WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT\', \'ADVERSE EVENT\', \'SCREEN FAILURE\'). Enables structured analysis and regulatory submission mapping.']
  transition_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this status transition within the subject\'s complete enrollment lifecycle history. Enables chronological ordering of all transitions for a given subject within a study. Starts at 1 for the first recorded transition.']
  transition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone) at which the subject status transition was recorded in the source system (Oracle Clinical One or Medidata Rave). Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation standards.']
  triggered_by_system varchar [note: 'Identifies whether the status transition was triggered by a human user or an automated system process, and which source system initiated it (Oracle Clinical One, Medidata Rave, IRT/RTSM). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  triggered_by_user_role varchar [note: 'The clinical role of the user who triggered this status transition (e.g., CRC - Clinical Research Coordinator, CRA - Clinical Research Associate, PI - Principal Investigator). Supports audit trail completeness and role-based accountability per GCP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CRC|CRA|PI|sub-investigator|sponsor|system|data-manager — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this status history record was last modified in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports change tracking, audit trail completeness, and data quality monitoring per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ standards.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Specific reason for subject withdrawal or discontinuation from the study. Populated when new_status is \'withdrawn\' or \'discontinued\'. Categorized per CDISC SDTM DS domain controlled terminology (e.g., \'ADVERSE EVENT\', \'WITHDRAWAL BY SUBJECT\', \'LOST TO FOLLOW-UP\').']

  Note: 'Lifecycle history table tracking every status transition of a trial subject across the enrollment lifecycle (e.g., pre-screened → screened → screen-failed → enrolled → randomized → on-treatment → completed → withdrawn). Captures prior status, new status, transition date, transition reason, and the user/system that triggered the change. Enables longitudinal subject flow analysis and regulatory audit trails. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave.'
}

Table subject.subject_contact {
  subject_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the subject contact record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the subject is enrolled. Used to scope contact data to the correct site for monitoring and follow-up.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this subject is enrolled. Scopes the contact record to a specific trial.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose contact information is stored in this record. Links to the subject master entity.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the subject\'s home address (street number and street name). Used for postal communications, visit logistics, and safety follow-up mailings.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the subject\'s home address (apartment, suite, unit, floor, or building name). Supplements address_line1 for complete postal address.']
  address_verified_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject\'s home address was last verified by site staff. Supports ALCOA+ data quality requirements and ensures contact information is current for safety follow-up.']
  address_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject\'s home address has been verified by site staff or through an address validation service. True = address confirmed; False = address unverified or pending confirmation.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the subject\'s home address. Used for geographic analysis of subject distribution and postal communications.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject granted consent for contact communications. Required for GDPR and HIPAA audit trails to demonstrate lawful basis for processing contact data.']
  consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has provided explicit consent to be contacted for study-related communications (visit reminders, follow-up calls). True = consent granted; False = consent not granted or withdrawn. Governed by GDPR and HIPAA privacy requirements.']
  consent_withdrawn_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject withdrew consent for contact communications. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Once populated, all non-safety-critical outreach must cease per GDPR right to withdraw.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the subject contact record. \'active\' indicates the contact details are current and in use; \'inactive\' indicates superseded or no longer valid; \'withdrawn\' indicates the subject has withdrawn consent for contact; \'pending_verification\' indicates details awaiting confirmation; \'deceased\' indicates the subject is deceased and contact is no longer applicable.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the subject\'s home address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Determines applicable data privacy regulations (GDPR, HIPAA) and communication rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this subject contact record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) principles.']
  do_not_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Hard stop flag indicating the subject must not be contacted under any circumstances (excluding mandatory safety communications). True = do not contact; False = contact permitted per consent settings. Overrides all other contact preferences.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this contact record is considered valid and active. Supports temporal versioning of contact information to maintain a history of address and phone changes over the course of a long-running trial.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date on which this contact record ceased to be valid (e.g., subject moved, changed phone number). Null for the currently active record. Enables Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD) Type 2 history tracking of contact information.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the subject. Used for electronic communications including visit reminders, eConsent notifications, and patient engagement via Clinpal.']
  email_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has specifically consented to receive communications via email. True = email contact permitted; False = email contact not permitted. Distinct from general contact consent as email may carry additional regulatory requirements.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the subject\'s designated emergency contact person. Used by site staff to reach a responsible party in the event of a serious adverse event (SAE) or medical emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the subject\'s emergency contact. Used exclusively for safety-related communications and serious adverse event (SAE) follow-up.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact person to the subject (e.g., spouse, parent, legal guardian). Provides context for site staff when initiating emergency communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spouse|parent|child|sibling|friend|caregiver|legal_guardian|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful contact with the subject. Used to track subject engagement, identify subjects at risk of dropout, and support visit compliance monitoring.']
  last_contact_method varchar [note: 'Communication channel used during the most recent successful contact with the subject. Supports analysis of effective outreach strategies and visit compliance improvement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by site staff regarding the subject\'s contact preferences, special communication instructions, or relevant context (e.g., best time to call, language interpreter required). Classified as confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive subject information.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the subject\'s home address. Used for geographic analysis, site feasibility, and postal communications.']
  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Subject\'s stated preference for how they wish to be contacted by the site or sponsor. Governs outreach channel selection for visit reminders and safety follow-up. \'no_contact\' indicates the subject has requested no proactive outreach.']
  preferred_contact_time timestamp [note: 'Subject\'s preferred time of day to be contacted. Supports scheduling of outreach calls and reminders to maximize response rates and respect subject preferences.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code representing the subject\'s preferred language for communications (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'de\', \'zh-CN\'). Ensures communications are delivered in the subject\'s native language to support informed participation.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contacting the subject. Used for visit reminders, recruitment follow-up, and safety follow-up communications. Stored in international format where possible.']
  primary_phone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary phone number (e.g., mobile, home, work). Informs the appropriate time and method for outreach.']
  record_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact record indicating the role of the contact in relation to the subject. \'primary\' is the subject themselves; \'secondary\' is an alternate contact; \'emergency\' is for emergency use only; \'caregiver\' is a designated caregiver; \'legal_representative\' is a legally authorized representative.']
  safety_contact_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether safety-critical communications (e.g., SAE follow-up, urgent safety notifications) are permitted even when do_not_contact_flag is True. True = safety contact allowed regardless of general consent; False = all contact restricted. Supports regulatory obligation to notify subjects of safety findings.']
  sms_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject has specifically consented to receive communications via SMS/text message. True = SMS contact permitted; False = SMS contact not permitted.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this contact record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation between Clinpal (eConsent/patient engagement) and Oracle Siebel CTMS (clinical trial management).']
  source_system_contact_key varchar [note: 'Natural key or unique identifier of this contact record in the originating source system (Clinpal or Oracle Siebel CTMS). Enables bidirectional traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational system.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the subject\'s home address. Supports regulatory reporting and geographic stratification of subject populations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this subject contact record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles. Tracks when contact details were last updated by site staff.']

  Note: 'Master entity storing contact and communication details for a trial subject, including primary phone, secondary phone, email address, preferred contact method, emergency contact name and relationship, home address (street, city, state, postal code, country), and privacy consent flags for contact. Supports patient recruitment follow-up, visit reminders, and safety follow-up communications. Governed by GDPR and HIPAA data privacy requirements. Sourced from Clinpal and Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

Table subject.population_assignment {
  population_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each population assignment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: population_assignment is the operational bridge assigning subjects to analysis populations; it must reference the specific analysis_population_id being assigned. Without this FK, population_code/popul']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: Analysis population assignments (ITT, PP, Safety) are arm-specific. FK to study_arm enables arm-level population counts in CSR tables, SAP-defined population summaries, and interim analysis arm-strati']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the database lock event during which this population assignment was finalized. Ensures traceability of population assignments to a specific DBL milestone, critical for regulatory submission integrity.']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Analysis population assignments (ITT, PP, Safety) are made for a subject within the context of a specific enrollment. Adding enrollment_id to population_assignment properly scopes each assignment to i']
  estimand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the estimand definition (per ICH E9(R1)) that this population assignment supports. Links the population to the specific treatment effect being estimated, including intercurrent event handling strategy.']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: Population assignments are specific to analysis timepoints including interim analyses. The interim_analysis_flag and interim_analysis_number on population_assignment indicate this relationship exists;']
  primary_superseded_by_population_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer population assignment record that supersedes this one (e.g., after a protocol amendment or SAP update). Null if this is the current active assignment. Supports assignment version history.']
  randomization_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.randomization_assignment. Business justification: Population assignments (ITT, PP, Safety) are directly tied to a subject\'s randomization event — the treatment arm and randomization number determine which analysis populations a subject belongs to. Ad']
  randomization_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: ADaM analysis population datasets (ITT, PP, Safety) are stratified by treatment arm per SAP requirements. treatment_arm_code on population_assignment is denormalized; FK enables direct join to arm par']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) version that defines the criteria and rules governing this population assignment. Ensures population definitions are traceable to the approved SAP.']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: Population assignments (ITT, PP, safety flags) directly determine subject inclusion in SDTM/ADaM datasets for regulatory submission. Statistical programmers and biostatisticians must trace which SDTM ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this population assignment was determined. Links the assignment to the governing protocol and sponsor.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Protocol amendments can redefine analysis populations (e.g., change ITT definition). FK to study_protocol_amendment enables amendment-driven population re-assignment tracking and SAP version alignment']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the subject was enrolled, used for site-level population analytics and CONSORT flow reporting.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) receiving this population assignment. Core party reference for this transactional record.']
  adam_flag_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The CDISC ADaM-compliant flag value (\'Y\' or \'N\') for the population flag variable, conforming to CDISC controlled terminology. Directly used in ADaM dataset generation for regulatory submissions.']
  adam_population_flag_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM variable name corresponding to this population assignment (e.g., \'ITTFL\', \'SAFFL\', \'PPROTFL\', \'PKFL\'). Used to map Silver Layer records to SDTM/ADaM submission datasets for regulatory packages (NDA, BLA, MAA).']
  amendment_driven_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this population assignment was created or revised as a direct result of a protocol amendment. Supports impact analysis of protocol changes on analysis populations.']
  analysis_cutoff_date date [note: 'The data cutoff date used to determine subject eligibility for this population assignment. All subject data up to and including this date was considered in the population determination.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status of the population assignment record (draft, under_review, approved, rejected). Tracks the QC/QA review lifecycle before the assignment is finalized for regulatory submission.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the population assignment was formally approved by the authorized statistical reviewer. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the biostatistics team formally assigned the subject to this analysis population, typically at or after Database Lock (DBL). Critical business event date for audit trail and regulatory submission timelines.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this population assignment record. \'included\' = subject is a member of the population; \'excluded\' = subject is explicitly excluded; \'pending\' = under biostatistics review; \'superseded\' = replaced by a later assignment after protocol amendment.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the population assignment record was created or last confirmed in the SAS Drug Development system. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record audit requirements.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes from the biostatistician or statistical reviewer regarding this population assignment, including any edge cases, adjudication decisions, or sponsor queries resolved during the assignment process.']
  consort_flow_category varchar [note: 'Category used to classify this subject\'s disposition in the CONSORT flow diagram (e.g., randomized, allocated, lost_to_followup, discontinued, analyzed, excluded). Required for CSR Section 10 and regulatory submission disposition tables.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this population assignment record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the subject is explicitly excluded from this analysis population. Complements inclusion_flag to support CONSORT flow diagrams and regulatory disposition tables.']
  exclusion_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for exclusion from the analysis population when exclusion_flag is True (e.g., \'NO_DOSE\', \'MAJOR_PROTOCOL_DEVIATION\', \'CONSENT_WITHDRAWN\', \'ELIGIBILITY_CRITERIA_NOT_MET\'). Sourced from the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) exclusion criteria taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NO_DOSE|MAJOR_PROTOCOL_DEVIATION|CONSENT_WITHDRAWN|ELIGIBILITY_CRITERIA_NOT_MET|MISSING_PRIMARY_ENDPOINT|WRONG_DIAGNOSIS — promote to reference product]']
  exclusion_reason_description varchar [note: 'Verbatim or standardized narrative description of why the subject was excluded from this analysis population. Supports regulatory reviewer queries and CSR narrative sections.']
  inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the subject is included in this analysis population. True = subject meets all criteria and is included; False = subject is excluded. Directly maps to ADaM population flag variables (e.g., ITTFL, SAFFL, PPROTFL).']
  interim_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this population assignment was generated for an interim analysis rather than the final database lock. Interim assignments may differ from final assignments and must be tracked separately.']
  population_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the analysis population to which the subject is assigned (e.g., ITT = Intent-to-Treat, mITT = modified Intent-to-Treat, PP = Per-Protocol, SAF = Safety, PK = Pharmacokinetic, PD = Pharmacodynamic, FAS = Full Analysis Set). Used in ADaM datasets and CSR disposition tables. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ITT|mITT|PP|SAF|PK|PD|FAS|MITT|EVAL — promote to reference product]']
  population_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the analysis population (e.g., \'Intent-to-Treat Population\', \'Per-Protocol Population\', \'Safety Population\'). Used in CSR tables, TLF shells, and regulatory submission narratives.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The IRT/RTSM-assigned randomization number for the subject at the time of population assignment. Used to cross-reference with randomization_assignment records and validate treatment arm alignment in population analyses.']
  reviewer_role varchar [note: 'Role of the individual who performed or approved the population assignment (e.g., biostatistician, lead statistician, statistical programmer). Supports audit trail and responsibility tracking per GCP requirements.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain associated with the primary data supporting this population assignment (e.g., \'DM\' for Demographics, \'DS\' for Disposition, \'EX\' for Exposure). Used for regulatory submission dataset traceability.']
  sensitivity_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this population assignment is used in a sensitivity analysis rather than the primary analysis. Sensitivity analyses test robustness of primary population definitions per ICH E9(R1) estimand framework.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this population assignment originated (e.g., SAS Drug Development for biostatistics outputs, Oracle Clinical One for study design-driven assignments). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (SAS Drug Development job ID, Oracle Clinical One record key). Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The human-readable subject identifier (e.g., \'001-001\') as assigned by the site and recorded in the EDC system. Used for cross-referencing with eCRF data and regulatory submission datasets.']
  unique_subject_identifier varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM-compliant Unique Subject Identifier (USUBJID) combining study and subject identifiers (e.g., \'STUDY001-001-001\'). Required for regulatory submission datasets and cross-domain subject traceability. Classified as restricted PII as it can identify a trial participant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this population assignment record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Required for change tracking and regulatory audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Transactional record assigning a trial subject to one or more analysis populations (Intent-to-Treat/ITT, modified ITT/mITT, Per-Protocol/PP, Safety/SAF, Pharmacokinetic/PK) as determined by the biostatistics team during database lock. Captures population code, inclusion flag, exclusion flag with coded reason, assignment date, protocol amendment version, and statistical reviewer identity. Critical for CONSORT flow diagrams, CSR disposition tables, and regulatory submission datasets (SDTM, ADaM). Sourced from biostatistics SAS programming outputs and Oracle Clinical One.'
}

Table subject.demographic {
  demographic_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each subject demographic record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the demographic data product.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: SDTM DM domain requires ARM and ACTARM derivation from the authoritative study_arm definition. Baseline characteristic tables in CSR are stratified by arm. arm_code, arm_name, actual_arm_code, actual_']
  corrected_demographic_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on demographic (corrected_demographic_id)']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Demographic data (ADSL dataset) is finalized at database lock. Biostatistics requires the locked demographic snapshot for baseline characteristics tables in CSRs and regulatory submissions. This link ']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Demographic data is collected on the DM eCRF form. Linking demographic to ecrf_form enables SDV tracking, form version traceability, and SDTM DM domain generation. Clinical data managers require this ']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Demographic records in clinical trials are captured per enrollment (SDTM DM domain is study-specific). Adding enrollment_id to demographic properly links each demographic snapshot to its enrollment ev']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Baseline demographic data collection and SDV is PI-delegated per GCP. SDTM DM domain submissions and regulatory inspection readiness require identifying the PI responsible for demographic data collect']
  randomization_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: SDTM DM domain requires ARM and ACTARM per subject derived from the canonical treatment_arm definition. arm_code, arm_name, actual_arm_code, actual_arm_name on demographic are denormalized; FK enables']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this demographic record was collected. Links the subject demographics to the parent study context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the subject was enrolled and demographic data was collected.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Demographic data (age, sex, race, ethnicity, height, weight, BMI) is collected at a specific study visit — typically the screening visit or baseline visit. Linking the demographic record to the specif']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (participant) whose demographic data is captured in this record. Core linkage to the trial_subject master entity.']
  age_at_screening varchar [note: 'Subject\'s age in whole years at the time of the screening visit, derived from date of birth and screening date. Used for eligibility verification, stratification, and CDISC SDTM DM domain submission.']
  age_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the age_at_screening field. Typically YEARS for adult studies; MONTHS or WEEKS may be used for pediatric or neonatal studies per CDISC SDTM convention.']
  birth_year varchar [note: 'Subject\'s year of birth as a four-digit integer. Used when full date of birth is masked for privacy compliance but year-level granularity is required for age-cohort analyses and CDISC partial date handling.']
  bmi decimal(5,2) [note: 'Subject\'s body mass index calculated as weight (kg) divided by height squared (m²) at baseline. Stored as a baseline characteristic per CDISC ADaM ADSL convention; used in eligibility assessment and subgroup analyses.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or clarifications related to the subject\'s demographic data, such as explanations for partial dates, data corrections, or special circumstances noted by the site or data manager.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the country where the subject was enrolled. Used for geographic subgroup analyses, regulatory reporting by region, and CDISC SDTM DM domain.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the demographic record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+.']
  data_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current data entry and verification status of the demographic eCRF form in the EDC system. Tracks completeness and readiness for database lock (DBL) and SDTM submission.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the demographic data was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements across the lakehouse pipeline.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Subject\'s date of birth in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-dd). Used to calculate age at key study milestones and to verify age-based eligibility criteria. May be partially masked (year only) per privacy regulations.']
  dm_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential record number within the DM domain for a given subject, per CDISC SDTM convention. Used to uniquely identify each demographic record when multiple records exist per subject (e.g., re-screening scenarios).']
  edc_subject_key varchar [note: 'Source system record key for the subject\'s demographic record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Enables traceability from the silver layer back to the source EDC record for SDV and audit purposes.']
  epoch varchar [note: 'The study epoch (e.g., SCREENING, TREATMENT, FOLLOW-UP) during which the demographic data was collected, per CDISC SDTM Trial Epochs dataset. Used for temporal context in multi-epoch study designs.']
  ethnicity varchar [note: 'Subject\'s self-reported ethnic origin, coded per FDA and CDISC controlled terminology. Distinguishes Hispanic/Latino origin from non-Hispanic/Latino. Required for FDA demographic subgroup reporting in NDA/BLA submissions.']
  first_exposure_date date [note: 'Date of the subject\'s first exposure to the investigational product (IP). Defines the start of the treatment period and is the reference date for study day calculations in CDISC SDTM DM domain.']
  gender_identity varchar [note: 'Subject\'s self-reported gender identity, distinct from biological sex. Collected per evolving FDA guidance on inclusion of gender identity in clinical trials. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MALE|FEMALE|TRANSGENDER MALE|TRANSGENDER FEMALE|NON-BINARY|PREFER NOT TO ANSWER|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  height_cm decimal(6,2) [note: 'Subject\'s height in centimeters measured at baseline/screening visit. Used for body surface area (BSA) calculations, dose determination, and baseline characteristic tables in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).']
  informed_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject signed the informed consent form (ICF) prior to any study-related procedures. Anchors the CDISC SDTM DM.RFICDTC reference date and is required for regulatory compliance and ALCOA+ data integrity.']
  last_exposure_date date [note: 'Date of the subject\'s last exposure to the investigational product (IP). Used to define the end of the treatment period and calculate treatment duration for safety and efficacy analyses.']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Lock status of the demographic eCRF record in the EDC system. LOCKED records cannot be modified without an audit-trailed unlock. FROZEN records are pending final database lock (DBL).']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the study protocol in effect at the time the demographic data was collected. Ensures traceability of demographic assessments to the applicable protocol amendment.']
  query_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more open data queries (discrepancies) exist against this demographic record in the EDC system. True = at least one unresolved query; False = no open queries. Used for data cleaning tracking.']
  race varchar [note: 'Subject\'s self-reported racial category, coded per FDA and CDISC controlled terminology (e.g., WHITE, BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN, ASIAN, AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE, NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER, MULTIPLE, UNKNOWN, NOT REPORTED). Required for FDA demographic subgroup analyses. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WHITE|BLACK OR AFRICAN AMERICAN|ASIAN|AMERICAN INDIAN OR ALASKA NATIVE|NATIVE HAWAIIAN OR OTHER PACIFIC ISLANDER|MULTIPLE|UNKNOWN|NOT REPORTED — promote to reference product]']
  race_other_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of race when the subject selects \'OTHER\' or \'MULTIPLE\' as their racial category. Captures verbatim self-reported race for completeness in regulatory submissions.']
  reference_start_date date [note: 'The protocol-defined reference start date for the subject, used as the anchor for study day (STUDYDAY) calculations across all SDTM domains. May differ from first_exposure_date in some study designs (e.g., observational studies).']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code for this record. Always \'DM\' for demographic records. Included to support automated SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission pipelines.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Status of source data verification (SDV) for the demographic record, indicating whether the EDC data has been verified against source documents by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) during site monitoring.']
  sex varchar [note: 'Biological sex of the subject as recorded at screening, coded per CDISC controlled terminology. M=Male, F=Female, U=Unknown, UNDIFFERENTIATED=Intersex or not classifiable. Used in safety analyses, PK/PD modeling, and regulatory submissions.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Site-assigned subject number used to identify the participant within the study site. Typically formatted as a numeric or alphanumeric code assigned at screening or enrollment.']
  unique_subject_identifier varchar [note: 'Globally unique identifier for the subject across the study, constructed per CDISC SDTM convention (typically STUDYID-SITEID-SUBJID). Used as the primary cross-domain linkage key in SDTM submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the demographic record in the silver layer lakehouse. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail compliance.']
  weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Subject\'s body weight in kilograms measured at baseline/screening visit. Used for dose calculations, PK/PD modeling, BMI derivation, and baseline characteristic tables in the CSR.']

  Note: 'Captures subject demographic data including age, sex, race, ethnicity, height, weight, and other baseline characteristics collected at screening per CDISC DM domain standards.'
}

Table subject.medical_history {
  medical_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each medical history record in the clinical trial system. Primary key for the medical_history data product.']
  coding_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.coding_assignment. Business justification: Medical history conditions require MedDRA coding for SDTM MH domain submission. Linking medical_history directly to its coding_assignment record enables medical reviewers and data managers to verify c']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Medical history is captured on the MH eCRF form. Replacing the denormalized ecrf_form_name with a proper FK to ecrf_form supports SDV workflows, form version tracking, and SDTM MH domain generation — ']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Medical history is collected during the screening and enrollment process as part of eligibility assessment. Linking medical_history to the specific enrollment record provides the enrollment-episode co']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Medical history collection and SDV is a PI-delegated activity per GCP. Delegation log audits and regulatory inspections require traceability of which PI or sub-investigator collected and verified base']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this medical history record was collected. Links the record to the sponsoring protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the medical history was collected during screening or baseline assessment.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Medical history is collected during screening visits. medical_history carries a denormalized screening_visit_number (STRING) that should be normalized via FK to subject_visit. Adding subject_visit_id ']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose medical history is recorded. Core linkage to the trial_subject master record.']
  ae_related_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this medical history condition has been associated with or contributed to an adverse event (AE) reported during the study. Supports integrated safety analysis.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the medical history was collected or assessed by the investigator or CRC during the screening visit. Corresponds to the eCRF data entry date for this record.']
  coding_date date [note: 'The date on which the verbatim medical history condition was coded to a MedDRA term. Supports audit trail requirements for medical coding activities.']
  coding_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the MedDRA coding process for this medical history condition. Tracks whether the verbatim term has been coded, auto-coded, reviewed, or approved by the medical coder.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or additional clinical context provided by the investigator regarding this medical history condition. May include relevant clinical details not captured in structured fields.']
  concomitant_medication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject is receiving concomitant medication for this medical history condition at the time of screening. Supports concomitant medication reconciliation and safety analysis.']
  condition_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the type of medical history entry. Corresponds to SDTM MHCAT variable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MEDICAL HISTORY|SURGICAL HISTORY|FAMILY HISTORY|PSYCHIATRIC HISTORY|ALLERGY|OBSTETRIC HISTORY — promote to reference product]']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Current clinical status of the pre-existing medical condition at the time of data collection. Supports safety profiling and eligibility determination.']
  condition_subcategory varchar [note: 'Optional secondary classification of the medical history condition providing further granularity within the category (e.g., CARDIOVASCULAR within MEDICAL HISTORY). Corresponds to SDTM MHSCAT.']
  condition_verbatim varchar [note: 'The verbatim (as-reported) text of the pre-existing medical condition or medical history event as entered by the investigator or CRC in the eCRF. This is the raw, uncoded term prior to MedDRA coding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this medical history record was first created in the EDC system or lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  edc_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique record identifier assigned by the EDC system (Medidata Rave) for this medical history entry. Used for source data verification (SDV) and data reconciliation.']
  eligibility_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this medical history condition is relevant to the study\'s inclusion/exclusion eligibility criteria. Used by the medical monitor and data management team to assess protocol compliance.']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Data lock status of this medical history record in the EDC system. Locked records cannot be modified without an authorized unlock procedure, supporting database lock (DBL) integrity.']
  meddra_llt varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) that most closely maps to the verbatim condition text. Provides the most granular level of MedDRA coding hierarchy.']
  meddra_pt varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) assigned to the verbatim medical history condition during medical coding. Used for standardized regulatory reporting and safety analysis.']
  meddra_soc varchar [note: 'The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) associated with the coded medical history condition. Used for aggregate safety reporting and tabulation by body system.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'The version of the MedDRA dictionary used to code this medical history condition (e.g., 26.0). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coding.']
  mh_seq varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying each medical history record for a given subject within a study. Corresponds to the SDTM MHSEQ variable used in regulatory submissions.']
  ongoing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medical history condition was ongoing at the time of the subject\'s screening or baseline assessment. True = condition is still active; False = condition has resolved. Corresponds to SDTM MHENRF or MHONGO.']
  onset_date date [note: 'The date on which the pre-existing medical condition first began or was first diagnosed. May be partial (year only or year-month) per CDISC partial date conventions. Corresponds to SDTM MHSTDTC.']
  pre_existing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the condition was present prior to the subject\'s first dose of investigational product. Distinguishes pre-existing conditions from treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) in safety analysis.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'The version of the study protocol in effect at the time this medical history record was collected. Ensures traceability of data to the applicable protocol amendment.']
  query_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is at least one open data query (discrepancy) against this medical history record in the EDC system. Supports data cleaning and database lock readiness tracking.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the pre-existing medical condition resolved or ended. Null if the condition is ongoing at the time of assessment. Corresponds to SDTM MHENDTC.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain code for this record. For medical history records this is always \'MH\'. Used during SDTM dataset generation for regulatory submissions.']
  sdv_date date [note: 'The date on which source data verification was completed for this medical history record by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA).']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Status of source data verification for this medical history record. Indicates whether the CRA has verified the eCRF data against source documents at the investigational site.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Clinical severity grading of the pre-existing medical condition as assessed by the investigator. Supports safety profiling and eligibility assessment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this medical history record was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this medical history entry in the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave record OID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse and the EDC.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this medical history record was last modified in the EDC system or lakehouse. Required for audit trail compliance and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Records subject medical history and pre-existing conditions collected during screening, coded to MedDRA terms, with onset dates, resolution status, and relevance to study eligibility.'
}

Table subject.concomitant_medication {
  concomitant_medication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each concomitant medication record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the adverse event record that prompted initiation or modification of this concomitant medication, when ae_related_flag is True. Supports traceability between safety events and concomitant medication changes.']
  coding_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.coding_assignment. Business justification: Concomitant medications require WHO Drug Dictionary coding for SDTM CM domain. Direct FK from concomitant_medication to coding_assignment enables medical reviewers to verify drug coding status and sup']
  continuation_of_concomitant_medication_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on concomitant_medication (continuation_of_concomitant_medication_id)']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific eCRF form instance in Medidata Rave from which this concomitant medication record was sourced. Supports source data verification (SDV) and audit trail traceability per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Concomitant medications are collected within the context of a specific study enrollment. Adding enrollment_id to concomitant_medication properly scopes each medication record to its enrollment, enabli']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: PI review of prohibited medications and concomitant therapy is a protocol-mandated responsibility. Regulatory submissions and inspection readiness require PI-level accountability for concomitant medic']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this concomitant medication record was collected.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the concomitant medication was reported or collected.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: Concomitant medications are recorded and assessed at study visits. concomitant_medication carries denormalized visit_name (STRING) and visit_number (INT) that should be normalized via FK to subject_vi']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) for whom this concomitant medication is recorded.']
  ae_related_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concomitant medication was initiated or modified in response to an adverse event (AE). Supports causality assessment and safety narrative construction in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  cm_seq varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying each concomitant medication record for a given subject within a study, used for SDTM CM domain sequencing (CMSEQ). Enables ordering of multiple medication records per subject.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes entered by the investigator, CRC, or data manager regarding this concomitant medication record. May include clarifications on dose changes, interruptions, or special circumstances not captured in structured fields.']
  concomitant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medication was taken concomitantly (during the study treatment period) as opposed to only in the pre-study period. Supports classification of medications as prior vs. concomitant per SDTM CM domain conventions.']
  concomitant_medication_category varchar [note: 'Sponsor-defined category grouping for the concomitant medication (e.g., PRIOR, CONCOMITANT, PROHIBITED, RESCUE). Corresponds to SDTM CMCAT. Used for protocol-specific medication classification and reporting.']
  data_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this concomitant medication record was first entered into the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.']
  dose decimal(10,4) [note: 'The numeric dose amount of the concomitant medication administered per dose event. Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSE. Must be interpreted in conjunction with dose_unit.']
  dose_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical form of the concomitant medication (e.g., TABLET, CAPSULE, SOLUTION, PATCH, INJECTION). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSFRM. Supports pharmacokinetic and bioavailability analysis.']
  dose_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the concomitant medication is administered (e.g., QD, BID, TID, QID, PRN, weekly). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSFRQ. Uses CDISC controlled terminology for standardized reporting.']
  dose_route varchar [note: 'The route by which the concomitant medication is administered (e.g., ORAL, INTRAVENOUS, SUBCUTANEOUS, TOPICAL, INHALATION). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSROUTE. Uses CDISC controlled terminology.']
  dose_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the concomitant medication dose (e.g., mg, mcg, IU, mL). Corresponds to SDTM CMDOSU. Required for accurate dose interpretation and cross-study comparisons.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject stopped taking the concomitant medication. Corresponds to SDTM CMENDTC (date portion). Null if medication is ongoing at time of data collection. Used for duration calculations and temporal overlap analysis.']
  indication varchar [note: 'The clinical reason or medical condition for which the concomitant medication is being taken, as reported by the subject or investigator. Corresponds to SDTM CMINDC. Used for safety and drug-drug interaction analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this concomitant medication record was most recently modified in the source EDC system. Required for audit trail compliance per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  modified_drug_name varchar [note: 'Standardized or sponsor-modified version of the verbatim drug name after data cleaning, prior to WHO-DD coding. Corresponds to SDTM CMMODIFY. Used when the verbatim term is modified for coding purposes.']
  ongoing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the concomitant medication was still ongoing at the time of the last assessment or data collection. Corresponds to SDTM CMONGO. True when end_date is not yet known or medication continues beyond the observation window.']
  prior_therapy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the medication was taken prior to the start of the study (pre-existing medication) as opposed to being initiated during the study. Corresponds to SDTM CMPRESP/CMOCCUR context. Supports prior therapy analysis in efficacy and safety assessments.']
  prohibited_medication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this concomitant medication is classified as a prohibited medication per the study protocol. A True value may trigger a protocol deviation review. Critical for protocol compliance monitoring.']
  query_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is at least one open data discrepancy query against this concomitant medication record in the EDC system. Supports data management workflow tracking and database lock (DBL) readiness assessment.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current data management status of the concomitant medication record in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Indicates whether the record is active, has been deleted, amended after lock, or locked for database lock (DBL).']
  rescue_medication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this medication was administered as a rescue therapy per the study protocol (e.g., for breakthrough pain, hypoglycemia rescue). Rescue medication use is a key efficacy endpoint in many trials.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain code for this record. Always \'CM\' (Concomitant and Prior Medications) for records in this product. Used for SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission via eCTD.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Current source data verification (SDV) status for this concomitant medication record, as tracked by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) in the CTMS. Indicates whether the data has been verified against source documents.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the source EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave internal record key) for this concomitant medication entry. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this concomitant medication record was extracted (e.g., MEDIDATA_RAVE, ORACLE_CLINICAL_ONE). Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject began taking the concomitant medication. Corresponds to SDTM CMSTDTC (date portion). Used to determine temporal overlap with investigational product exposure and adverse event onset.']
