  study_day_end varchar [note: 'The study day on which the concomitant medication was stopped, calculated relative to the reference start date. Corresponds to SDTM CMENDY. Null if medication is ongoing. Used for duration and overlap calculations.']
  study_day_start varchar [note: 'The study day on which the concomitant medication was started, calculated relative to the reference start date (Day 1 = first dose of investigational product). Corresponds to SDTM CMSTDY. Enables temporal analysis relative to IP exposure.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'Sponsor-defined subcategory for further classification of the concomitant medication within its category (e.g., ANALGESIC, ANTIBIOTIC, ANTIHYPERTENSIVE). Corresponds to SDTM CMSCAT. Enables granular therapeutic class reporting.']
  verbatim_drug_name varchar [note: 'The medication name exactly as reported by the subject or investigator on the electronic Case Report Form (eCRF), prior to WHO-DD coding. Corresponds to SDTM CMTRT field. Preserved for source data traceability per ALCOA+ principles.']
  whodb_atc_code varchar [note: 'The Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code assigned by the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) for the concomitant medication. Enables therapeutic class-level analysis and drug-drug interaction assessment.']
  whodb_drug_name varchar [note: 'The preferred drug name as coded against the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD). Corresponds to SDTM CMDECOD. Enables standardized cross-study drug identification and safety signal detection.']
  whodb_version varchar [note: 'Version of the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) used for coding this concomitant medication record. Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coded data.']

  Note: 'Records medications taken by subjects before and during the trial, including drug name, indication, dose, route, start/stop dates, and WHO-DD coding.'
}

Table safety.adverse_event {
  adverse_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each adverse event record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for the adverse_event data product and is the SSOT reference for all AE occurrences across studies and subjects.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: AEs must be attributed to the treatment arm for SDTM ADAE dataset generation, blinded/unblinded safety reporting, and dose-response analysis. A clinical-research expert expects every AE record to iden']

The CTA governs safety reporting obligations in EU member states. A clinical research expert expects every AE to reference the governing CTA for EU ']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: adverse_event stores ctcae_grade as an INT (the grade number). Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table establishes referential integrity and enables navigation to the full CTCAE gr']
  dosing_regimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen.   ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Source data verification (SDV) and ALCOA audit trail require tracing each adverse event back to the specific eCRF AE form it was collected on. Regulatory inspectors expect this traceability link betwe']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: AEs are classified by study epoch (treatment vs. follow-up) for SDTM mapping and safety analysis. Regulatory submissions require epoch-level AE breakdowns; epoch context determines whether an AE is tr']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: Pharmacovigilance causality assessment and DSUR/PBRER generation require direct linkage from AE to the IMP product master. Regulatory submissions (ICSRs, expedited reports) must identify the exact IMP']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: AEs must be reported to the FDA under the IND. The IND number is required on expedited safety reports (7-day/15-day). A clinical research expert expects every AE to reference the governing IND for US ']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the adverse event was reported. Used for site-level safety monitoring and CRA oversight.']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: GxP regulations (21 CFR 312, EMA GMP) require batch-level traceability from adverse events for product quality investigations and potential recalls. Direct FK enables immediate batch identification du']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: adverse_event stores MedDRA codes (meddra_llt_code, meddra_pt_code, meddra_soc_code) as denormalized strings. Adding meddra_term_id FK to the meddra_term reference master establishes referential integ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'FK to study.study.study_id — Critical for study-level safety analysis — AEs must join to study for DSUR preparation, aggregate safety tables, and cross-study signal detection.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: AE records require the subject\'s authoritative randomization record for causality assessment, ICSR narrative population, and expedited reporting obligation determination. treatment_arm on adverse_even']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: AEs are reported at specific subject visits. Linking adverse_event to subject_visit_data enables visit-level data completeness review, SDV tracking, and lock/freeze status checks before safety databas']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: AEs are collected at specific study visits; SDTM AE domain requires AEVISIT mapping. Safety reviewers and data managers trace AE onset to the reporting visit for source data verification and visit-win']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: AE source documents (CRF pages, follow-up reports) must be filed in the eTMF per ICH E6 GCP requirements. Safety monitors and data managers need direct traceability from each AE record to its filed TM']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event. Core party reference linking the AE to the enrolled participant.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'Action taken with the investigational product in response to the adverse event, as recorded by the investigator. Drives dose modification tracking and safety signal analysis.']
  ae_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the adverse event record, reflecting whether the event is still active, has resolved, or resulted in death. Drives workflow routing in Oracle Argus Safety and expedited reporting decisions.']
  ae_term_verbatim varchar [note: 'The exact verbatim text of the adverse event as reported by the investigator or subject on the eCRF, prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for ALCOA+ compliance and audit trail integrity.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety upon initial AE entry. This is the business-facing identifier used in regulatory submissions, safety narratives, and cross-system reconciliation with the source system of record.']
  awareness_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor (or CRO acting on behalf of the sponsor) first became aware of the adverse event. This is the clock-start date for regulatory expedited reporting timelines per ICH E2A.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Investigator\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event. Determines whether the event qualifies as a suspected adverse reaction and drives SUSAR classification and expedited reporting.']
  concomitant_medication_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event may be related to concomitant medications taken by the subject, as assessed by the investigator. Supports differential causality analysis and confounding factor assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this adverse event record was first created in the source system (Oracle Argus Safety). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'Severity grade assigned per the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE), ranging from 1 (mild) to 5 (death). The primary severity measure used in oncology and other therapeutic areas for safety reporting and DSMB/DMC reviews.']
  dsmb_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse event has been reported to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent safety review. Supports DSMB/DMC meeting preparation and safety oversight documentation.']
  edc_subject_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The AE sequence number assigned within the EDC system (Medidata Rave eCRF) for the subject, used to reconcile the safety database record with the source eCRF entry during SDV and data review.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is the most current version of the adverse event. Used to filter the latest state of each AE without requiring a MAX(version_number) query, supporting efficient reporting and analytics.']
  is_expected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event is listed in the current Investigator\'s Brochure (IB) or product labeling as an expected/anticipated reaction. Unexpected serious adverse reactions trigger SUSAR reporting obligations.']
  is_serious boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition). Triggers expedited regulatory reporting obligations.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited regulatory reporting to health authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) within defined timelines (7 or 15 calendar days).']
  is_teae boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event is treatment-emergent, defined as an event that first occurred or worsened in severity after the first dose of investigational product. Used in TEAE incidence tables in the CSR and SAP-defined safety analyses.']
  meddra_llt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) numeric code assigned during medical coding, representing the most granular level of the MedDRA hierarchy closest to the verbatim term.']
  meddra_llt_name varchar [note: 'MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) text description corresponding to the LLT code. Used in safety listings, narratives, and regulatory submissions.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) numeric code, the primary level used for regulatory reporting, signal detection, and aggregate safety analysis.']
  meddra_pt_name varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) text description. The standardized medical term used in all regulatory submissions, DSMB/DMC reports, and safety narratives.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) numeric code representing the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for aggregate safety tabulations by body system in CSRs and regulatory submissions.']
  meddra_soc_name varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) text description. Used in safety tabulations, listings, and figures in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding this adverse event (e.g., \'26.0\'). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coded terms across dictionary versions.']
  medical_history_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event is considered related to or a continuation of a pre-existing medical history condition, as assessed by the investigator. Supports pre-existing condition confounding analysis.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text medical narrative describing the clinical course of the adverse event, including relevant medical history, treatment, and outcome. Required for SAE/SUSAR regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC safety reviews.']
  onset_date date [note: 'The date on which the adverse event first began, as reported by the investigator. This is the principal real-world event date used for duration calculations, expedited reporting timelines, and TEAE determination.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Final or current outcome of the adverse event as assessed by the investigator. Used in safety listings, aggregate safety tables, and regulatory submissions.']
  regulatory_report_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which an expedited safety report (e.g., SUSAR, IND Safety Report) must be submitted to the relevant health authority. Calculated from the date of first awareness per ICH E2A timelines (7-day for fatal/life-threatening, 15-day for other SUSARs).']
  regulatory_report_submitted_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the expedited safety report was submitted to the health authority. Used to calculate compliance with regulatory reporting timelines and track submission performance KPIs.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the initial AE report, a follow-up update with new clinical information, or the final report upon event resolution. Supports version history tracking as the clinical picture evolves.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the adverse event resolved or returned to baseline. Null if the event is ongoing. Used to calculate AE duration and determine outcome status.']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific ICH E2A seriousness criterion or criteria met by this SAE (e.g., death, life-threatening, hospitalization required, persistent disability, congenital anomaly, medically significant). Multiple criteria may apply. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|prolonged_hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]']
  severity varchar [note: 'Descriptive severity classification of the adverse event as assessed by the investigator (mild, moderate, severe, life-threatening, fatal). Distinct from CTCAE grade; used in non-oncology studies and as a complementary severity descriptor per ICH E2A.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this adverse event record was sourced (Oracle Argus Safety, Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Clinical One, or manual entry). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.']
  study_day_onset varchar [note: 'The study day (relative to first dose date) on which the adverse event began. Calculated per CDISC convention (Day 1 = first dose date). Used in time-to-event analyses and safety exposure-adjusted incidence calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this adverse event record was most recently modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this AE record, incremented with each follow-up update. Version 1 is the initial report; subsequent versions capture evolving clinical information. Enables temporal evolution tracking across the AE lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Core master record for every adverse event (AE) reported during a clinical trial, capturing onset date, resolution date, severity grade per CTCAE, seriousness flag, expectedness, relationship to investigational product, action taken, outcome, and follow-up version history. Each AE may progress through multiple follow-up updates as clinical picture evolves, with version tracking for each update. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety and EDC (Medidata Rave). This is the SSOT for all AE occurrences and their temporal evolution across studies and subjects.'
}

  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record.   imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master.   protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: SAE reporting obligations are protocol-version-specific — the protocol in effect at SAE onset determines expectedness assessments and reporting timelines per ICH E2A. Regulatory binders require SAE re']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which the SAE occurred. Used for regulatory reporting and safety signal aggregation across the study.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: SAE aggregate reporting and site risk tiering require linking SAEs to the study_site record, which holds per-protocol SAE counts, monitoring visit data, and risk tier. CRAs use this join during monito']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization.   subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: SAEs are captured during subject visits; safety reviewers and data managers must trace each SAE back to the exact eCRF visit data record for source data verification and regulatory inspection. No exis']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: SAEs must be traceable to the visit at which they were identified or reported for source data verification, SDTM AE domain mapping (VISITNUM), and regulatory inspection readiness. adverse_event alread']
  susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.susar. Business justification: When an SAE meets the criteria for unexpectedness and causality, it is escalated to a SUSAR. The serious_adverse_event record needs a direct FK to the resulting susar record to track this escalation. ']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: SAE source documents are essential TMF artifacts per ICH E6 and FDA 21 CFR 312. The existing source_document_reference is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to tmf_document enables inspection-read']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study subject (patient/participant) who experienced the SAE. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this transaction header per canonical attribute rules.']
  action_taken varchar [note: 'The action taken with the investigational product in response to the SAE (e.g., dose reduced, drug withdrawn, dose interrupted). This is a key regulatory field in ICSR submissions and clinical study reports.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety upon SAE intake. This is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER used for cross-system traceability, regulatory submissions, and sponsor communications.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE case is currently blinded (treatment assignment unknown) or unblinded (treatment assignment revealed for safety reporting purposes). Unblinding may be required for SUSAR reporting to regulatory authorities.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'The investigator\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the SAE. This is a critical regulatory field used to determine whether the event qualifies as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the SAE record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field per canonical transaction header requirements. Conforms to 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'The CTCAE severity grade assigned to the SAE, ranging from 1 (mild) to 5 (death). Grade 3 = severe, Grade 4 = life-threatening, Grade 5 = death. Used for oncology and other therapeutic area safety assessments.']
  dsmb_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SAE was reported to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent safety review. Required for studies with an active DSMB/DMC charter.']
  expectedness varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE is expected (listed in the Investigator\'s Brochure or product labeling) or unexpected (not listed or differing in nature/severity). Unexpected + serious + related = SUSAR, triggering expedited regulatory reporting.']
  follow_up_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which follow-up report this record represents (0 = initial report, 1 = first follow-up, 2 = second follow-up, etc.). Used to track the evolution of the SAE case over time as new information becomes available.']
  initial_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the SAE was first reported to the sponsor or CRO by the investigational site. This is Day 0 for calculating regulatory reporting timelines (e.g., 7-day or 15-day expedited reporting clock).']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of the SAE. Required under GCP and local regulations to ensure ongoing ethical oversight of the study.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SAE has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited regulatory reporting to health authorities (e.g., FDA 7/15-day reporting, EMA reporting). Derived from causality, expectedness, and seriousness criteria but stored as a business flag.']
  meddra_llt_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) code that most closely matches the verbatim SAE term. The LLT is the most granular level of MedDRA coding and is used to preserve the original reported term in a coded format.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code assigned to the SAE. The PT is the primary level of MedDRA used for regulatory reporting and is the standard coded term submitted in ICSRs and clinical study reports.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code assigned to the SAE during medical coding. Represents the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy, used for regulatory submissions and safety signal detection.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'The version of the MedDRA dictionary used to code this SAE (e.g., \'26.0\', \'26.1\'). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coding and compliance with current dictionary standards.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'The full medical narrative describing the SAE, including clinical course, relevant medical history, concomitant medications, investigational product details, and the investigator\'s assessment. This is the primary free-text field in the Argus Safety case and is included in regulatory submissions.']
  onset_date date [note: 'The date on which the serious adverse event first began or was first observed by the subject or investigator. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for the SAE transaction.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The clinical outcome of the serious adverse event at the time of the most recent report. \'recovered_with_sequelae\' indicates the subject recovered but with lasting effects. \'fatal\' must align with seriousness_death = true.']
  regulatory_report_due_date date [note: 'The calculated deadline by which the expedited regulatory report (e.g., IND Safety Report, SUSAR notification) must be submitted to the relevant health authority. Typically 7 calendar days for fatal/life-threatening SUSARs and 15 calendar days for other SUSARs.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of safety report generated for this SAE. \'expedited\' = 7/15-day SUSAR or IND safety report; \'periodic\' = included in DSUR/PSUR; \'spontaneous\' = post-marketing spontaneous report; \'literature\' = identified from published literature.']
  reporting_authority varchar [note: 'The health authority or regulatory body to which the SAE/SUSAR report was submitted (e.g., \'FDA\', \'EMA\', \'MHRA\', \'PMDA\', \'TGA\', \'Health Canada\'). May contain multiple authorities if reported to more than one. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health Canada|ANVISA|CDSCO — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the serious adverse event resolved, stabilized, or the subject died. Null if the event is ongoing at the time of reporting.']
  sae_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SAE case in Oracle Argus Safety. \'initial\' = first report received; \'follow_up\' = additional information received; \'closed\' = case fully resolved and closed; \'nullified\' = case determined not to meet SAE criteria and voided.']
  sae_term varchar [note: 'The verbatim (as-reported) description of the serious adverse event as recorded by the investigator or site staff in the source document or eCRF. This is the raw, uncoded term prior to MedDRA coding.']
  seriousness_congenital_anomaly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of resulting in a congenital anomaly or birth defect in the offspring of the subject.']
  seriousness_death boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of resulting in death of the subject. One of the six ICH E2A seriousness criteria that must be captured individually.']
  seriousness_disability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of resulting in persistent or significant disability or incapacity.']
  seriousness_hospitalization boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of requiring inpatient hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization.']
  seriousness_life_threatening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of being immediately life-threatening (i.e., the subject was at immediate risk of death at the time of the event).']
  seriousness_medically_important boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SAE meets the regulatory seriousness criterion of being an other medically important condition — events that may not be immediately life-threatening but may jeopardize the subject or require intervention to prevent one of the other serious outcomes.']
  sponsor_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor (or CRO acting on behalf of the sponsor) received the complete minimum information required to process the SAE. This date starts the regulatory reporting clock per ICH E2A.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The protocol-assigned subject identifier (e.g., site-subject number) for the patient who experienced the SAE. Restricted as it can be used to identify an individual participant in combination with study data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the SAE record was most recently modified in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field per canonical transaction header requirements. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) that meet regulatory seriousness criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition). Captures SAE narrative, seriousness criteria met, initial and follow-up report dates, blinded/unblinded status, and regulatory reporting obligations. Linked to the parent adverse_event record. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.'
}

Table safety.susar {
  susar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the SUSAR record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete regulatory safety event with a defined lifecycle, expedited reporting obligations, and submission tracking.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: A SUSAR is a specific type of adverse event that is both serious and unexpected. Every SUSAR originates from an underlying adverse event record. Adding adverse_event_id to susar establishes the direct']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: SUSARs require treatment arm identification for unblinding procedures and regulatory reporting. Health authority submissions (EudraVigilance, FDA) for SUSARs must specify the implicated treatment arm ']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: SUSARs in EU trials are reported under specific CTAs to EMA/national competent authorities per EU Clinical Trials Regulation. Linking susar to clinical_trial_authorization replaces the denormalized eu']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: susar stores ctcae_grade as an INT. Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table establishes referential integrity and enables navigation to the full CTCAE grade definition. The ctcae_g']
  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record. Business justification: SUSAR case narratives and E2B(R3) submissions require complete product exposure documentation including dispensing date, dose, and kit number. susar links to imp_master and subject_randomization but n']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: SUSARs are sourced from eCRF form data. EMA and FDA regulations require traceability from the SUSAR back to the source eCRF form; pharmacovigilance teams use this link during 7/15-day expedited report']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: SUSAR regulatory submissions to health authorities require enrollment context to confirm IMP identity, arm assignment, and protocol version. EudraVigilance and FDA expedited reporting workflows depend']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: SUSAR regulatory reporting (7/15-day clock) to health authorities requires authoritative IMP identification. susar.investigational_product_name is a denormalized plain column; FK to imp_master enables']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: SUSARs in US trials are reported to the FDA under the IND within 7 or 15 days. The IND governs the SUSAR reporting obligation. A clinical research expert expects SUSARs to reference the governing IND.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the SUSAR occurred. Used for site-level safety monitoring, regulatory notifications, and investigator communications.']
  irb_iec_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.irb_iec_approval. Business justification: When a SUSAR is identified, the sponsor must notify the specific IRB/IEC approval under which subjects are enrolled per ICH E6(R2) 4.11 and 21 CFR 312.32. susar has irb_iec_notified and irb_iec_notifi']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: SUSAR 7/15-day expedited reporting clock requires documentation of the precipitating lab finding that confirmed unexpectedness and seriousness. EudraVigilance and FDA submissions must reference the sp']

  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: susar stores MedDRA codes (meddra_soc_code, meddra_pt_code, meddra_llt_code) as strings.   study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this SUSAR occurred. Links the SUSAR to the sponsoring study for regulatory reporting and safety surveillance.']

  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization.   tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: SUSAR expedited reports are essential TMF documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 1, Section 11). Regulatory inspectors verify that each SUSAR submission is filed in the TMF; this FK enables that trac']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study subject (patient) who experienced the SUSAR. Links to the subject master record for patient-level safety surveillance and regulatory reporting.']
  ae_term varchar [note: 'Verbatim description of the adverse event as reported by the investigator or subject, prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for source data verification (SDV) and coding audit trail per ALCOA+ principles.']
  argus_case_reference varchar [note: 'System-generated case identifier from Oracle Argus Safety, the operational system of record for adverse event and SUSAR management. Used to trace the SUSAR back to its source case in Argus.']
  awareness_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor (or CRO acting on behalf of the sponsor) first became aware of the SUSAR. This date starts the regulatory reporting clock for 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting obligations under ICH E2A.']
  blind_break_date date [note: 'Date on which the treatment blind was broken for this subject. Populated only when blind_broken is True. Required for audit trail and regulatory documentation of unblinding events.']
  blind_broken boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment blind was broken for this subject in order to perform the causality assessment for this SUSAR. A blind break is a significant event requiring documentation and regulatory notification.']
  blind_status varchar [note: 'Blinding status of the study at the time of SUSAR reporting. Indicates whether the study was blinded, and if so, whether the blind was broken for this subject to determine treatment assignment for causality assessment.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric case number assigned by the sponsor or CRO for this SUSAR, used in all regulatory submissions and correspondence. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Investigator\'s or sponsor\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the serious adverse reaction. A SUSAR requires a positive causality assessment (related or probably/possibly related) to the investigational product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SUSAR record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this transaction header, supporting ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'CTCAE severity grade assigned to the adverse reaction (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Used for safety signal detection, DSMB/DMC reviews, and regulatory reporting.']
  deadline_15day date [note: 'Calculated deadline for 15-day expedited reporting to regulatory authorities for non-fatal/non-life-threatening SUSARs. Derived from the reporting clock start date plus 15 calendar days per ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR 312.32.']
  deadline_7day date [note: 'Calculated deadline for 7-day expedited reporting to regulatory authorities for fatal or life-threatening SUSARs. Derived from the reporting clock start date plus 7 calendar days per ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR 312.32.']
  dose_at_onset varchar [note: 'Dose of the investigational product administered to the subject at the time of the adverse reaction onset, including amount and unit (e.g., \'100 mg\'). Required for causality assessment and regulatory case narratives.']
  dsmb_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this SUSAR. Populated only when dsmb_notified is True.']
  dsmb_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this SUSAR. Required for studies with an active DSMB/DMC per ICH E6(R2) and study protocol.']
  eudravigilance_number varchar [note: 'Unique case identifier assigned by the EudraVigilance system upon submission of the SUSAR to the EMA. Used for tracking and referencing the case in EU regulatory correspondence and EudraVigilance database.']
  expectedness_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the serious adverse reaction is unexpected — i.e., not listed or not consistent with the nature, severity, or frequency described in the current Investigator\'s Brochure (IB). A SUSAR must be assessed as unexpected to qualify for expedited reporting.']
  fatal_date date [note: 'Date of subject death if the outcome of the SUSAR is fatal. Triggers the 7-day expedited reporting clock and is required for death narratives in regulatory submissions.']
  follow_up_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the follow-up report for this SUSAR case (e.g., 1 for first follow-up, 2 for second). Zero or null for initial reports. Tracks the version history of regulatory submissions for this case.']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of this SUSAR. Populated only when irb_iec_notified is True.']
  irb_iec_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of this SUSAR as required by GCP and local regulations.']
  meddra_llt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) code that most closely matches the verbatim adverse event term. Used for granular coding traceability and verbatim-to-PT mapping audit.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code assigned to this SUSAR. The PT is the primary level of MedDRA used for regulatory reporting, signal detection, and aggregate safety analysis in IND/NDA/BLA submissions.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code assigned to this SUSAR during medical coding. Represents the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy and is required for regulatory submissions and aggregate safety reporting.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding this SUSAR (e.g., 26.0, 26.1). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure reproducibility of coding and compliance with current MedDRA release requirements.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Medical narrative summarizing the SUSAR case, including patient demographics, event description, clinical course, causality assessment, and outcome. Required component of all expedited regulatory reports per ICH E2A.']
  onset_date date [note: 'Date on which the subject first experienced the serious adverse reaction. This is the principal real-world event time (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP) for the SUSAR and is the anchor for all regulatory reporting clocks per ICH E2A.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Clinical outcome of the serious adverse reaction at the time of reporting. Required for regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC safety reviews. Tracks resolution status of the event.']
  reporting_clock_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the regulatory reporting clock begins for this SUSAR. Typically the sponsor awareness date, but may be adjusted based on the date of unblinding or receipt of minimum reportable information per ICH E2A.']
  reporting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SUSAR regulatory reporting workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through submission to regulatory authority acknowledgement and case closure. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this transaction.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the investigational product was administered (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous). Required for regulatory case narratives and pharmacovigilance signal assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation — promote to reference product]']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'ICH E2A seriousness criterion met by this adverse reaction that qualifies it as a Serious Adverse Event (SAE). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant — promote to reference product]']
  submission_report_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this SUSAR submission is an initial report, a follow-up report with additional information, or a final report closing the case. Regulatory authorities require tracking of report versions per ICH E2A.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SUSAR record in Oracle Argus Safety. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for this transaction header, supporting audit trail and data integrity requirements.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs) — SAEs that are both unexpected (not in the Investigator Brochure) and causally related to the investigational product. Captures expectedness assessment, causality determination, expedited reporting clock start date, 7-day and 15-day reporting deadlines, submission status to FDA/EMA/PMDA, and blind-break details. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.'
}

Table safety.ae_meddra_coding {
  ae_meddra_coding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each MedDRA coding record assigned to an adverse event. Primary key for the ae_meddra_coding data product in the safety domain.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent adverse event record for which this MedDRA coding assignment was made. Links the coding record back to the originating AE in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  coding_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.coding_assignment. Business justification: MedDRA coding reconciliation process requires matching safety-side ae_meddra_coding records with EDC-side coding_assignment records. Regulatory submissions (SDTM AE domain) depend on reconciled coding']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: ae_meddra_coding stores ctcae_grade (INT) and ctcae_version (STRING). Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table normalizes the grade reference and enables navigation to the full CTCA']
  discrepancy_query_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.discrepancy_query. Business justification: MedDRA coding discrepancies generate EDC queries that must be resolved before database lock. Linking ae_meddra_coding to discrepancy_query enables data managers to track open coding queries against sa']
  ecrf_field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: MedDRA coding traceability requires identifying which specific eCRF field contained the verbatim AE term that was coded. This supports SDTM AE domain mapping and regulatory submission audit trails whe']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: ae_meddra_coding is the MedDRA coding assignment table and should reference the authoritative meddra_term reference master. Adding meddra_term_id (pointing to the PT-level term) establishes referentia']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which the adverse event and its MedDRA coding were recorded. Supports study-level aggregate safety reporting.']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: AE MedDRA coding is performed on verbatim terms captured in subject visit data records. Medical coders need to trace each coding assignment back to the source visit data for coding quality review, rec']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: MedDRA coding of AEs requires visit context for SDTM AE domain mapping (VISITNUM, VISIT) and for reconciliation with EDC source data at the visit level. Data managers performing coding reconciliation ']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event being coded. Enables subject-level safety queries and MedDRA-coded AE listings.']
  argus_case_reference varchar [note: 'The case identifier from Oracle Argus Safety that corresponds to this MedDRA coding record. Serves as the source system business key for traceability back to the originating safety case in the system of record.']
  argus_event_reference varchar [note: 'The event-level identifier from Oracle Argus Safety for the specific adverse event record within a case that this MedDRA coding applies to. Enables precise traceability to the source system event record.']
  coded_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the MedDRA code was assigned to the verbatim adverse event term. Used for coding timeliness metrics and regulatory submission audit trails.']
  coded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the MedDRA code was assigned. Provides granular audit trail for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety system timestamp.']
  coding_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment entered by the coder to document coding rationale, ambiguities, or special considerations for this MedDRA assignment. Supports coding consistency reviews and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  coding_method varchar [note: 'Indicates how the MedDRA code was assigned: \'auto\' for system-generated auto-coding by Oracle Argus Safety, \'manual\' for coder-assigned coding, \'auto_confirmed\' for auto-coded and confirmed by a coder, \'recoded\' for a previously coded term that was revised. Drives QC workflows and audit requirements.']
  coding_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the MedDRA coding assignment. \'pending\' indicates awaiting coding, \'coded\' indicates a code has been assigned, \'confirmed\' indicates QC-approved, \'rejected\' indicates coding was rejected and requires rework, \'under_review\' indicates currently in QC review. Drives coding workflow in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  confirmed_date date [note: 'Date on which the MedDRA coding assignment was confirmed (QC-approved) by a second reviewer or coding supervisor. Null if coding has not yet been confirmed. Supports dual-coder QC workflows.']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'Severity grade assigned to the adverse event using the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) scale (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Used in oncology and other therapeutic areas for standardized severity grading alongside MedDRA coding.']
  ctcae_version varchar [note: 'Version of the NCI CTCAE grading scale used for severity assessment (e.g., \'5.0\', \'4.03\'). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure consistent severity grading interpretation across study sites and time periods.']
  dictionary_language varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding (e.g., \'ENG\' for English, \'JPN\' for Japanese). Relevant for multi-regional studies where local language MedDRA versions may be used (e.g., PMDA submissions in Japan).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this MedDRA coding record version is considered effective. Used in conjunction with is_current_version and effective_to_date to implement SCD Type 2 versioning for coding history tracking.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this MedDRA coding record version is considered effective. Null for the current active record. Used with effective_from_date and is_current_version for SCD Type 2 coding history. Populated when a recoding event supersedes this record.']
  hlgt_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the High Level Group Term (HLGT), which sits between the HLT and SOC in the MedDRA hierarchy. Used for broad safety grouping in regulatory submissions.']
  hlgt_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive text of the MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) corresponding to hlgt_code. Supports broad-level safety grouping in aggregate analyses and CSR appendices.']
  hlt_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the High Level Term (HLT) in the MedDRA hierarchy above the PT. Used for grouping related PTs in aggregate safety analyses and signal detection.']
  hlt_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive text of the MedDRA High Level Term (HLT) corresponding to hlt_code. Supports mid-level grouping in safety tabulations and DSMB/DMC review packages.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the current (active) MedDRA coding assignment for the associated adverse event. Set to false for superseded coding records retained for historical audit trail. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD Type 2) versioning in the Silver layer.']
  is_sae boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event associated with this MedDRA coding record meets the regulatory definition of a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) per ICH E2A criteria (death, life-threatening, hospitalization, disability, congenital anomaly, or other medically important condition). Drives expedited reporting timelines.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), requiring expedited regulatory reporting (7-day or 15-day) to health authorities. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety case classification.']
  llt_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the Lowest Level Term (LLT) assigned to the verbatim adverse event term. The LLT is the most granular level of the MedDRA hierarchy and is the primary coding target. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety MedDRA dictionary.']
  llt_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive text of the MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) corresponding to llt_code. Provides the human-readable label for the most granular MedDRA coding level. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety MedDRA dictionary.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for this coding assignment (e.g., \'26.0\', \'26.1\'). Critical for regulatory submissions as coding must reference the specific MedDRA version in use at the time of the submission. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety dictionary configuration.']
  previous_pt_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA PT code that was previously assigned before a recoding event. Populated only when coding_method = \'recoded\'. Enables before/after comparison for coding change impact analysis and regulatory audit.']
  previous_soc_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA SOC code that was previously assigned before a recoding event. Populated only when coding_method = \'recoded\'. Enables assessment of whether a recoding changed the SOC-level classification, which may affect regulatory safety tables.']
  primary_soc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOC assignment is the primary (true) or secondary (false) SOC for the PT. MedDRA PTs may be linked to multiple SOCs; the primary SOC is used for standard regulatory safety tables. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.']
  pt_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the Preferred Term (PT) to which the LLT maps. The PT is the primary level used for regulatory reporting, safety queries, and signal detection. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.']
  pt_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive text of the MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) corresponding to pt_code. Used as the standard term in regulatory submissions, CIOMS forms, and safety narratives.']
  recoding_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by the coder when a previously assigned MedDRA code is revised (coding_method = \'recoded\'). Required for audit trail integrity and regulatory inspection readiness. Null when coding_method is not \'recoded\'.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which this MedDRA coding record was first created in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp at which this MedDRA coding record was last modified in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Tracks the most recent update for change detection, incremental processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit MedDRA numeric code for the System Organ Class (SOC), the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy. SOC is the primary axis for organizing adverse events in regulatory safety tables (e.g., Table 14.3.1 in ICH E3 CSR).']
  soc_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive text of the MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) corresponding to soc_code. Used as the primary grouping header in all regulatory safety tabulations, PSUR/PBRER narratives, and DSMB reports.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system from which this MedDRA coding record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer. Typically \'argus_safety\' for records originating from Oracle Argus Safety.']
  verbatim_term varchar [note: 'The exact, unmodified adverse event term as reported by the investigator or subject in the source document or eCRF prior to MedDRA coding. Preserved for ALCOA+ compliance and audit trail. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety verbatim field.']

  Note: 'Stores the MedDRA coding assignment for each adverse event, capturing the verbatim term reported, the coded Lowest Level Term (LLT), Preferred Term (PT), High Level Term (HLT), High Level Group Term (HLGT), and System Organ Class (SOC). Tracks coding version, coder identity, coding date, and auto-code vs. manual-code flag. Enables standardized MedDRA-based safety queries and regulatory submissions. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety.'
}

  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'The case number assigned by Oracle Argus Safety to the parent safety case containing this causality assessment. Enables direct traceability back to the source system for audit and regulatory purposes.']
  argus_workflow_state varchar [note: 'The workflow routing state of the causality assessment within Oracle Argus Safety at the time of data extraction (e.g., Open, Closed, Submitted, Archived).
