  assessment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the causality assessment was performed or last confirmed by the assessor. Used to track compliance with expedited reporting timelines (e.g., 7-day/15-day clock).']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this causality assessment record, typically generated by Oracle Argus Safety (e.g., CA-2024-00123). Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory correspondence.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the causality assessment record within Oracle Argus Safety. Tracks progression from initial draft through regulatory submission. \'Superseded\' indicates a newer revision exists.']
  assessor_role varchar [note: 'The professional role of the individual performing the causality assessment. Distinguishes between the Principal Investigator (PI) site-level assessment and the Sponsor Medical Monitor\'s independent assessment, as required by ICH E2A and GCP guidelines.']
  causality_category varchar [note: 'The assessor\'s determination of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event. Standard WHO/ICH causality categories used in Oracle Argus Safety and regulatory submissions. \'Not assessable\' applies when insufficient information is available.']
  causality_category_code varchar [note: 'Standardized short code for the causality category used in CDISC SDTM AE domain submissions and Oracle Argus Safety exports (UN=Unrelated, UL=Unlikely, PO=Possible, PR=Probable, DE=Definite, NA=Not Assessable).']
  concomitant_medication_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this causality assessment pertains to a concomitant medication (True) rather than the primary investigational product (False). Enables differentiation of IP-related versus concomitant drug-related assessments within the same AE case.']
  concomitant_medication_name varchar [note: 'Name of the concomitant medication being assessed for causal relationship when concomitant_medication_flag is True. Populated from WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) preferred drug name.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this causality assessment record was first created in the source system (Oracle Argus Safety). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  dechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome observed when the investigational product was withdrawn (dechallenged) following the adverse event. A positive dechallenge (AE resolved or improved upon withdrawal) supports a causal relationship. Captured per ICH E2A causality criteria.']
  dsmb_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the DSMB/DMC formally reviewed this causality assessment during a scheduled or ad hoc safety review meeting.']
  dsmb_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this causality assessment was reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) as part of a formal safety review. Supports DSMB/DMC meeting documentation and regulatory oversight.']
  expectedness varchar [note: 'Determination of whether the adverse event is listed in the current Investigator\'s Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI) as a known effect of the investigational product. Drives SUSAR classification and expedited reporting obligations.']
  expedited_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this causality assessment triggers an obligation for expedited regulatory reporting (7-day or 15-day report) to health authorities such as FDA, EMA, or PMDA based on SUSAR classification.']
  is_revised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this causality assessment is a revision of a previously submitted assessment. True when the assessor has changed their causality determination after initial submission, triggering regulatory follow-up reporting obligations.']
  rationale_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text clinical narrative provided by the assessor explaining the reasoning behind the causality determination. Includes consideration of temporal relationship, biological plausibility, dechallenge/rechallenge results, and alternative explanations. Required for regulatory submissions.']
  rechallenge_result varchar [note: 'Outcome observed when the investigational product was re-administered (rechallenged) after the adverse event resolved. A positive rechallenge (AE recurred) provides strong evidence of causal relationship. Captured per ICH E2A causality criteria.']
  report_due_date date [note: 'Calculated deadline date by which the expedited regulatory report (SUSAR/IND safety report) must be submitted to health authorities. Derived from the date of first awareness plus the applicable reporting timeline.']
  reporting_timeline_days varchar [note: 'The applicable expedited reporting timeline in calendar days from the date of first awareness of the SUSAR (typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening or 15 days for other serious unexpected reactions). Drives compliance monitoring.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision version number for this causality assessment. Starts at 1 for the original assessment and increments with each revision. Enables audit trail tracking of assessment changes over the case lifecycle.']
  signal_detection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this causality assessment has been flagged for inclusion in pharmacovigilance signal detection analysis within Oracle Argus Safety. Supports aggregate safety review and cumulative causality trend monitoring.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this causality assessment record was extracted. Primarily Oracle Argus Safety; may include Medidata Rave for EDC-sourced assessments or \'manual\' for paper-based entries.']
  sponsor_causality_category varchar [note: 'The Sponsor Medical Monitor\'s independent causality determination, captured separately from the investigator\'s assessment. When sponsor_causality_override is True, this field holds the sponsor\'s differing conclusion used for regulatory reporting.']
  sponsor_causality_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Sponsor Medical Monitor\'s causality determination differs from (overrides) the Investigator\'s assessment. When True, both assessments are retained and the sponsor\'s determination governs regulatory reporting obligations per ICH E2A.']
  susar_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse event has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) based on the combination of seriousness, causality (at least possible), and unexpectedness criteria. Triggers mandatory expedited regulatory reporting.']
  temporal_relationship varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the timing between investigational product administration and adverse event onset is consistent with a causal relationship. A key criterion in WHO and ICH causality assessment frameworks.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this causality assessment record was most recently modified in the source system. Tracks the latest change for audit trail compliance and Silver layer incremental load processing.']
  who_dd_drug_code varchar [note: 'WHO Drug Dictionary standardized drug code for the product (investigational or concomitant) being assessed. Enables cross-study signal detection and regulatory pharmacovigilance reporting.']

  Note: 'Records the investigator and sponsor medical monitor causality assessments for each adverse event relative to the investigational product and concomitant medications. Captures assessor role, assessment date, causality category (unrelated, unlikely, possible, probable, definite), rationale narrative, and whether the assessment was revised. Supports regulatory reporting and signal detection workflows in Oracle Argus Safety.'
}

Table safety.narrative {
  narrative_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety narrative record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this data product.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Safety narratives are written for SAEs and SUSARs, which are adverse events. While safety_narrative already has icsr_id → icsr → adverse_event, a direct adverse_event_id FK is essential for narratives']
  ae_meddra_coding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ae_meddra_coding. Business justification: Narratives include MedDRA coding information (PT, SOC, LLT codes) that is authoritatively stored in ae_meddra_coding. Adding ae_meddra_coding_id normalizes the narrative by removing the denormalized M']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Safety narratives for regulatory submissions (ICSRs, SAE reports) must include the treatment arm the subject was enrolled in. Medical writers and pharmacovigilance teams require arm context when autho']
  causality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.causality_assessment. Business justification: Clinical narratives document the causality assessment as a core component of the case summary. The narrative.causality_assessment field is a denormalized string; adding a FK to causality_assessment pr']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Narratives for EU trial safety reports are submitted under the CTA framework. The CTA governs the reporting obligation for narrative submissions in EU member states. A clinical research expert expects']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: safety_narrative stores ctcae_grade (INT) and ctcae_version (STRING). Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table normalizes the grade reference. The ctcae_grade INT and ctcae_version ']
  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record. Business justification: Safety case narratives (ICH E2B, CIOMS I) must describe product exposure including dose, dispensing date, kit number, and compliance. The dispensing_record is the source of truth for these narrative e']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: Safety narratives reference specific eCRF forms as primary source documents. Medical writers use eCRF form data to construct case narratives; this link supports source document traceability required f']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Safety narratives for regulatory submissions must document the subject\'s treatment assignment, protocol version, and dosing history — all sourced from the enrollment record. Medical writers and pharma']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent safety case (SAE or SUSAR) for which this narrative is authored. Links to the safety case master record in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Narratives for expedited safety reports are submitted under the IND. The IND number is required on all narrative submissions to the FDA. A clinical research expert expects narratives to reference the ']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.investigational_site. Business justification: Regulatory case narratives per ICH E2B(R3) and FDA guidance must identify the site where the event occurred. safety_narrative has no site FK despite describing site-specific events. New site_id column']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Medical monitor narrative authoring (ICH E2A/E2B) requires direct reference to specific lab result values to populate the clinical description section. Narrative generation systems pull lab data (valu']
  safety_narrative_tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Safety narratives are formal ICSR components filed as TMF documents. Medical writers and pharmacovigilance teams need to link the narrative record to its TMF-filed version for audit trail, version con']
  safety_report_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.safety_report_submission. Business justification: Narratives are the core content of safety report submissions (ICSRs, DSURs). The narrative is filed as part of the safety_report_submission to health authorities. A clinical research expert expects na']
  serious_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.serious_adverse_event. Business justification: Narratives are explicitly authored for SAEs and SUSARs. While narrative.adverse_event_id links to the base AE record, a direct FK to serious_adverse_event is needed to associate the narrative with the']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this safety narrative was generated.']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: Safety narratives are authored using source data from subject visit eCRF records. Medical writers and safety reviewers must trace narrative content back to the specific visit data record for source do']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: ICH E2A-compliant safety narratives must include the visit at which the AE was observed/reported. Linking narrative to subject_visit enables automated population of visit date, visit number, and study']
  susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.susar. Business justification: Narratives are explicitly authored for SUSARs as part of the expedited regulatory reporting process. While narrative.adverse_event_id provides an indirect link, a direct FK to susar enables retrieval ']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) associated with this safety narrative. Links to the subject master record.']
  ae_onset_date date [note: 'Date on which the adverse event first began, as reported by the investigator or subject. Critical for calculating reporting timelines and for CDISC SDTM AE domain mapping.']
  ae_outcome varchar [note: 'Coded outcome of the adverse event at the time of narrative authoring or case closure. Aligns with ICH E2B(R3) outcome codes and CDISC SDTM AE.AEOUT controlled terminology.']
  ae_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the adverse event resolved or stabilized. Null if the event is ongoing at the time of narrative authoring. Used to determine event duration and outcome status.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the medical monitor formally approved the final version of the safety narrative for regulatory submission. Represents the final lifecycle milestone before submission.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known case number assigned by Oracle Argus Safety to the adverse event case. Used as the primary business identifier for cross-system traceability and regulatory submissions.']
  authoring_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety narrative was initially authored or drafted. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and version history tracking.']
  case_summary varchar [note: 'High-level executive summary of the safety case, synthesizing the patient description, event, treatment, and outcome into a concise paragraph suitable for inclusion in the ICSR narrative field of E2B(R3) submissions.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety case for which this narrative is written. SAE = Serious Adverse Event; SUSAR = Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction; AE = Adverse Event; AESI = Adverse Event of Special Interest.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Medical monitor\'s or investigator\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event. Used in regulatory reporting and signal detection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety narrative record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  ctcae_grade varchar [note: 'Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) severity grade assigned to the adverse event (1=Mild, 2=Moderate, 3=Severe, 4=Life-threatening, 5=Death). Used for safety signal detection and regulatory reporting.']
  ctcae_version varchar [note: 'Version of the NCI Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events used for severity grading in this narrative (e.g., \'5.0\'). Ensures consistency in grading across study sites and regulatory submissions.']
  dsmb_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the DSMB or DMC reviewed this safety case. Populated only when dsmb_reviewed is True. Used to track safety oversight compliance.']
  dsmb_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety case and its narrative have been reviewed by the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC). True if reviewed; False if not yet presented to the DSMB/DMC.']
  e2b_message_reference varchar [note: 'Unique message identifier assigned to the ICSR transmission in E2B(R3) XML format as submitted via eCTD to health authorities. Enables traceability between the narrative record and the regulatory submission artifact.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the onset, nature, severity, and clinical course of the adverse event or serious adverse event, including signs, symptoms, and diagnostic findings. Core narrative section for ICSR submissions.']
  expedited_report_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the expedited safety report (ICSR) must be submitted to health authorities. Calculated from the date of first awareness per ICH E2A (7-day for fatal/life-threatening; 15-day for other serious unexpected).']
  expedited_report_submitted_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the expedited safety report (ICSR) was submitted to the relevant health authority via eCTD/E2B(R3) format. Used to verify compliance with regulatory reporting timelines.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the narrative was authored under blinded conditions (True) or after unblinding of treatment assignment (False). Critical for maintaining study integrity in double-blind trials; blinded narratives must not reveal treatment allocation.']
  medical_monitor_commentary varchar [note: 'Clinical commentary and causality assessment provided by the medical monitor, including their opinion on the relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event, and any recommendations for protocol amendments or safety actions.']
  medical_monitor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the medical monitor or safety physician who reviewed and approved the safety narrative. Required for regulatory audit trail and ICSR submission accountability.']
  narrative_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the safety narrative document. Tracks progression from initial authoring through medical monitor review and approval to regulatory submission. \'superseded\' indicates a newer version has replaced this record.']
  outcome_description varchar [note: 'Narrative section describing the resolution or current status of the adverse event, including recovery details, sequelae, or death information. Corresponds to the outcome field in E2B(R3) regulatory submissions.']
  regulatory_authority_notified varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of regulatory authorities to which this safety report was submitted (e.g., FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada|other — promote to reference product]']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this narrative supports an initial expedited report, a follow-up report with updated information, or a final report closing the case. Drives regulatory submission timelines under ICH E2A.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the medical monitor or safety physician completed their clinical review of the narrative. Distinct from the authoring date; marks the transition from draft to reviewed status.']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ICH E2A seriousness criteria met by the adverse event (e.g., death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant). Determines expedited reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: death|life_threatening|hospitalization|disability|congenital_anomaly|medically_significant|other — promote to reference product]']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or path to the source documents (e.g., hospital records, lab reports, investigator notes) used to author this narrative, as stored in Veeva Vault eTMF. Supports SDV and audit trail requirements.']
  subject_description varchar [note: 'Structured narrative section describing the patient\'s relevant medical history, demographics (age, sex, weight), concomitant medications, and pre-existing conditions relevant to the adverse event. Contains protected health information (PHI).']
  treatment_description varchar [note: 'Narrative section describing the investigational product(s) and concomitant medications administered, including dose, route, frequency, start and stop dates, and any dose modifications or interruptions relevant to the adverse event.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'Date on which the treatment assignment was unblinded for this subject in the context of this safety case. Populated only when is_blinded transitions to False. Relevant for causality re-assessment post-unblinding.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety narrative record was most recently modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail, version control, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the safety narrative document, incremented each time the narrative is revised. Version 1 is the initial draft; subsequent versions reflect amendments or follow-up updates.']

  Note: 'Stores the structured clinical narrative for SAEs and SUSARs, including case summary, patient description, event description, treatment details, outcome, and medical monitor commentary. Tracks narrative version, authoring date, review status, and approval by the medical monitor. Used in expedited regulatory reports (ICSRs) submitted via Oracle Argus Safety in eCTD/E2B(R3) format.'
}

Table safety.icsr {
  icsr_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source adverse event or serious adverse event record in the safety.adverse_event product from which this ICSR was generated.']
  ae_meddra_coding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ae_meddra_coding. Business justification: ICSRs require the specific MedDRA coding record used for the regulatory submission. The icsr table has denormalized meddra_pt_code, meddra_soc_code, meddra_version fields that duplicate data in ae_med']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: ICSRs submitted to health authorities per ICH E2B(R3) must identify the treatment arm (study arm section of E2B message). Regulatory submissions require arm-level attribution for causality assessment ']
  causality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.causality_assessment. Business justification: ICSRs must include the causality assessment as a mandatory regulatory field (ICH E2B standard). The icsr table has a narrative field for causality but no FK to the structured causality_assessment reco']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: ICSRs in EU trials are submitted under the CTA framework. The CTA defines the reporting obligation and authority for ICSR submissions in EU member states. A clinical research expert expects ICSRs to r']
  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record. Business justification: E2B(R3) ICSR submissions to health authorities require product exposure details: dose, dispensing date, kit number, and subject compliance. The dispensing_record is the authoritative source for these ']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: ICSRs are prepared and submitted under a governing expedited reporting SOP (per ICH E2B and FDA/EMA requirements). Linking each ICSR to its governing SOP enables compliance verification, SOP deviation']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: ICSRs (E2B reports) are compiled from eCRF form data. ICH E2B regulatory requirement mandates traceability from the ICSR back to the source eCRF form; pharmacovigilance teams use this link during ICSR']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) require this linkage to validate IMP ex']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: ICSRs are submitted to specific health authorities.   imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master.   investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product.   investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.investigational_site.   lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result.   reporting_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_reporting_obligation.   serious_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.serious_adverse_event. Business justification: ICSRs are the formal regulatory submission records for expedited safety reporting, primarily triggered by SAEs and SUSARs. While icsr.adverse_event_id links to the base AE, a direct FK to serious_adve']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this ICSR, linking the safety report to the sponsoring trial.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: ICSRs must be tracked against the study_site for site-level regulatory reporting metrics, IRB/IEC notification tracking, and CRO monitoring dashboards. icsr already has investigational_site_id but lac']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: ICSR (E2B/MedWatch) preparation requires the subject\'s randomization assignment — treatment arm and blinding status — to complete causality and drug exposure sections. Pharmacovigilance systems like A']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: ICSRs are compiled from subject visit eCRF data. E2B traceability requirements and health authority inspections require linking each ICSR to the specific visit data record from which the case informat']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_visit. Business justification: ICSRs require visit-level traceability for complete case narrative construction and SDTM AE domain mapping. The visit at which the reportable event was observed is a standard data element in E2B(R3) c']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: ICSRs are expedited regulatory submissions (7/15-day reports) that must be filed as essential documents in the TMF per ICH E6(R2) and GVP Module VI. TMF managers and regulatory affairs teams track the']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event triggering this ICSR.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory agency formally acknowledged receipt of the ICSR submission. For electronic submissions via EudraVigilance or FDA gateway, this is the date of the automated acknowledgement message (ACK).']
  acknowledgement_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference number or message ID returned by the regulatory agency in the acknowledgement (ACK) message confirming receipt of the ICSR. Used for reconciliation and proof of submission.']
  applicable_deadline_date date [note: 'The operative regulatory deadline date for this specific ICSR, reflecting the applicable reporting window (7-day or 15-day) based on the seriousness criteria and expectedness of the adverse event. Used for compliance monitoring.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'The unique case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety to the source safety case from which this ICSR was generated. Used for traceability back to the system of record.']
  awareness_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor or CRO first became aware of the adverse event that triggered this ICSR. This is the regulatory clock start date (Day 0) from which all expedited reporting deadlines (7-day and 15-day) are calculated per ICH E2A.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this ICSR record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  days_to_submission varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days elapsed between the awareness_date (clock start) and the actual submission_date. Used to assess compliance with 7-day and 15-day expedited reporting requirements. Negative values indicate early submission.']
  deadline_15day_date date [note: 'The calculated calendar date by which a serious unexpected adverse reaction (non-fatal/non-life-threatening SUSAR) must be submitted to the regulatory agency, computed as awareness_date + 15 calendar days per ICH E2A.']
  deadline_7day_date date [note: 'The calculated calendar date by which a fatal or life-threatening unexpected SUSAR must be submitted to the regulatory agency, computed as awareness_date + 7 calendar days per ICH E2A.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation documenting the reason why the ICSR was not submitted within the applicable regulatory deadline. Required for late submissions to support CAPA processes and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  dsmb_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this ICSR. Required for studies with an active DSMB charter.']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of this ICSR. Used for compliance tracking against GCP notification requirements.']
  irb_iec_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) overseeing the study was notified of this ICSR, as required by GCP and applicable regulations.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the most current version of the ICSR. True for the latest version; False for superseded versions retained for audit history.']
  is_fatal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event associated with this ICSR resulted in the death of the subject. Fatal cases trigger the 7-day expedited reporting clock per ICH E2A.']
  is_life_threatening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event associated with this ICSR was life-threatening (i.e., the subject was at immediate risk of death). Life-threatening cases trigger the 7-day expedited reporting clock per ICH E2A.']
  is_on_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ICSR was submitted to the regulatory agency on or before the applicable regulatory deadline date. True = compliant submission; False = late submission. Key KPI for regulatory compliance monitoring.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ICSR relates to a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR). SUSARs require expedited reporting to regulatory authorities and ethics committees under ICH E2A and EU CTR 536/2014.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'The structured free-text clinical narrative summarizing the adverse event, patient information, treatment details, and outcome as included in the ICSR submission. Corresponds to ICH E2B(R3) H.1 narrative section.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided by the regulatory agency when an ICSR submission is rejected (e.g., data validation failure, duplicate case, missing mandatory fields). Populated only when submission_status = \'rejected\'.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known, unique regulatory report identifier assigned to this ICSR submission (e.g., the E2B message identifier or MedWatch report number). Used by health authorities to track and acknowledge the submission.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this ICSR is an initial submission, a follow-up update with new information, or a nullification (withdrawal) of a previously submitted report. Aligns with ICH E2B(R3) A.1.6.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this ICSR record. Version 1 is the initial report; subsequent versions represent follow-up or amended submissions for the same case.']
  sender_identifier varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the organization or individual sending the ICSR to the regulatory agency, as defined in ICH E2B(R3) A.3.1. Typically the sponsor or CRO EudraVigilance/FDA sender ID.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this ICSR record was sourced. Primarily Oracle Argus Safety case submission module; \'Manual\' for records entered outside the system.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the ICSR was transmitted to the regulatory agency. Used to calculate days-to-submission and determine on-time compliance against the applicable deadline.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The technical format or channel used to transmit the ICSR to the regulatory agency. E2B XML is the standard electronic format per ICH E2B(R3); MedWatch and CIOMS I are paper/PDF formats used for specific agencies or fallback scenarios.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the ICSR submission lifecycle. Tracks progression from internal drafting through regulatory acknowledgement or rejection.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone) at which the ICSR was electronically transmitted to the regulatory agency gateway. Provides audit-grade precision beyond the submission date for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this ICSR record was most recently modified in Oracle Argus Safety or the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports change tracking and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']

  Note: 'Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) — the formal regulatory submission record for expedited safety reporting to health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA). Captures report type (initial, follow-up, nullification), submission date, acknowledgement receipt, regulatory agency, submission method (E2B XML, MedWatch, CIOMS), report version, submission status, clock start date (date of first awareness), calculated 7-day and 15-day deadline dates, actual submission date, days-to-submission, on-time compliance flag, and reason for delay if late. Enables compliance monitoring against ICH E2A and regional reporting requirements. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety case submission module.'
}

Table safety.signal {
  signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety signal record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the adverse event case included in this signal\'s case series']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: Safety signals are detected within a defined analysis population (e.g., safety population, ITT). DSMB and regulatory signal evaluation reports must specify which population the signal was observed in;']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Safety signals are detected and evaluated at the treatment arm level — a signal in the high-dose arm vs. placebo arm drives different regulatory actions. DSMB signal reports and pharmacovigilance anal']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Safety signals in EU trials may require CTA amendments or urgent safety measures under the CTA framework. A clinical research expert expects signals to reference the governing CTA for EU regulatory ac']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Safety signals are detected within specific study epochs — a signal emerging during the treatment epoch vs. follow-up has different causality implications. DSMB data packages and signal evaluation rep']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Safety signals are reported to specific health authorities (FDA, EMA). The health authority determines the reporting format and timeline. A clinical research expert expects signals to reference the he']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: Signal detection and management is product-centric; pharmacovigilance teams aggregate signals by IMP for DSUR Section 16 and PBRER. safety_signal.investigational_product_name is denormalized; FK to im']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Safety signals identified during a trial must be reported to the FDA under the IND. Signal management and expedited reporting are governed by the IND safety reporting framework. A clinical research ex']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Safety signals are tracked per study-level investigational product record. study.investigational_product contains study-specific IP details (dose form, strength, IND reference) needed for signal evalu']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Safety signal detection and evaluation must determine whether a signal is batch-specific (manufacturing defect) or product-wide. Pharmacovigilance signal management systems link signals to specific ba']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: safety_signal stores MedDRA PT and SOC codes and names as strings. Adding meddra_term_id FK to the meddra_term reference master normalizes the terminology reference. meddra_pt_name and meddra_soc_name']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: Safety signals are detected from SDTM AE/SAE datasets. Signal detection workflows (e.g., disproportionality analysis) reference specific SDTM dataset versions; this link is required for signal documen']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: Safety signal detection and benefit-risk evaluation reference the specific SAP-defined statistical endpoint being monitored. Regulatory signal evaluation reports and DSMB packages must cite which endp']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this safety signal. Links the signal to the study protocol under which the signal was detected.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Safety signal detection and management reports are required TMF artifacts per ICH E2E and EMA pharmacovigilance guidelines. Each signal record must link to its filed TMF document for inspection readin']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Safety signals are detected and evaluated at treatment-arm level (active vs. placebo disproportionality). Signal evaluation reports and DSMB referrals require arm-level attribution. safety_signal has ']
  argus_signal_reference varchar [note: 'Native signal identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety signal detection and evaluation module. Used for traceability back to the source system of record.']
  benefit_risk_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the benefit-risk assessment conducted as part of signal evaluation. Captures the medical monitor\'s or safety committee\'s conclusion on whether the signal alters the overall benefit-risk profile of the investigational product.']
  case_count varchar [note: 'Total number of adverse event cases contributing to the safety signal at the time of detection or most recent evaluation. Represents the volume of evidence supporting the signal.']
  case_weight decimal [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this case in quantitative pharmacovigilance signal metrics (e.g., PRR, ROR, BCPNN). Reflects the case\'s contribution to the disproportionality statistic. Null for signals detected via clinical review rather than quantitative methods.']
  contribution_date date [note: 'Date on which this adverse event case was formally added to the signal case series during signal evaluation. Supports audit trail and evaluation timeline reconstruction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety signal record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_reviewed_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the data sources and datasets reviewed during signal evaluation, including case narratives, aggregate safety data, literature, and clinical trial data from Medidata Rave or SAS Drug Development outputs.']
  detection_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety signal was first identified or flagged, either by automated detection algorithms in Oracle Argus Safety or by a medical monitor during clinical review. Critical for regulatory reporting timelines.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to detect the safety signal. Includes quantitative pharmacovigilance methods such as Proportional Reporting Ratio (PRR), Reporting Odds Ratio (ROR), Bayesian Confidence Propagation Neural Network (BCPNN), disproportionality analysis, clinical review, or literature review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: disproportionality_analysis|PRR|ROR|BCPNN|clinical_review|literature_review|spontaneous_report|data_mining — promote to reference product]']
  dsmb_recommendation varchar [note: 'Formal recommendation issued by the DSMB/DMC following their review of the safety signal. Drives sponsor and investigator actions regarding study continuation, protocol modification, or suspension.']
  dsmb_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the DSMB/DMC reviewed this safety signal. Null if not yet reviewed or not referred. Used to track DSMB meeting schedules and signal review timelines.']
  evaluation_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal evaluation of the safety signal was completed and a final disposition was reached. Null if evaluation is still in progress.']
  evaluation_cycle_number varchar [note: 'Sequential cycle number for the current evaluation of this safety signal. Supports multiple evaluation cycles as new data emerges (e.g., Cycle 1 = initial evaluation, Cycle 2 = re-evaluation after new cases). Enables tracking of iterative signal assessments.']
  evaluation_due_date date [note: 'Target due date for completing the signal evaluation, based on signal priority and applicable regulatory timelines (e.g., 30 days for high-priority signals per EMA GVP Module IX). Used for SLA tracking and escalation.']
  evaluation_plan varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the planned approach for evaluating the safety signal, including data sources to be reviewed, analyses to be conducted, and timelines. Documented in Oracle Argus Safety evaluation module.']
  evaluation_start_date date [note: 'Date on which formal evaluation of the safety signal was initiated. Used to track evaluation timelines against regulatory and internal SOP requirements.']
  final_disposition varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the signal evaluation process. Indicates whether the signal was validated as a new risk, validated as a change in known risk, refuted as not a true signal, requires further monitoring, or requires no action. Populated upon evaluation completion.']
  inclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative or coded rationale explaining why this adverse event case was included in the signal case series. Examples: confirmed causality, meets Brighton Collaboration criteria, index case for disproportionality, regulatory authority query. Supports signal evaluation documentation requirements.']
  is_dsmb_referred boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety signal has been referred to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent review. True = referred; False = not referred.']
  is_expedited_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety signal triggers expedited regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., 7-day or 15-day IND safety report to FDA, or SUSAR report to EMA). True = expedited reporting required; False = routine reporting.']
  is_index_case boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this adverse event was the sentinel (first) case that triggered initial detection of the safety signal. Only one case per signal should carry this flag as true.']
  is_susar_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety signal is associated with one or more Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions (SUSARs). True = SUSAR-related; False = not SUSAR-related. Relevant for expedited regulatory reporting obligations.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code representing the primary adverse event term associated with the safety signal. Standardized coding per MedDRA hierarchy for regulatory submissions and signal tracking.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code for the primary adverse event term associated with the safety signal. Used for signal categorization and aggregate safety reporting.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding the adverse event terms associated with this safety signal (e.g., 26.1). Required for regulatory submissions to ensure coding consistency.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the safety signal based on clinical seriousness, signal strength, and potential public health impact. Drives evaluation timelines and resource allocation.']
  regulatory_action_recommended varchar [note: 'Regulatory action recommended as a result of signal evaluation. Options include label update (update Investigator Brochure or product label), Direct Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC), study suspension, protocol amendment, risk minimization measures, no action required, or further monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: label_update|DHPC|study_suspension|protocol_amendment|risk_minimization|no_action|further_monitoring — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_action_status varchar [note: 'Current implementation status of the recommended regulatory action. Tracks whether the recommended action (e.g., label update, protocol amendment) has been initiated, completed, or determined not required.']
  regulatory_report_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the regulatory safety report (e.g., IND safety report, SUSAR report) must be submitted to the relevant health authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, etc.) based on the signal\'s seriousness and expectedness.']
  signal_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature of the safety signal, including the observed drug-event association, population affected, and clinical context. Supports medical monitor review and regulatory reporting.']
  signal_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the safety signal (e.g., SIG-2024-00123). Used in regulatory correspondence, DSMB/DMC reports, and cross-functional communications.']
  signal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safety signal in the evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initial detection through evaluation to final disposition. Values: new (newly detected, not yet evaluated), under_evaluation (active evaluation in progress), validated (confirmed as a real signal), refuted (determined not to be a true signal), ongoing_monitoring (validated but under continued surveillance), closed (evaluation complete, no further action required).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this safety signal record originated (e.g., Oracle Argus Safety signal detection module, Medidata Rave EDC, SAS Drug Development). Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  strength_lower_ci decimal(10,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval for the signal strength metric value. A lower CI > 1 (for PRR/ROR) or > 0 (for IC) typically indicates a statistically significant signal.']
  strength_metric_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quantitative disproportionality metric used to measure signal strength (e.g., PRR - Proportional Reporting Ratio, ROR - Reporting Odds Ratio, BCPNN IC - Information Component, EBGM - Empirical Bayes Geometric Mean). Null if signal was detected by clinical review only.']
  strength_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the signal strength metric (e.g., PRR = 3.45, ROR = 4.12, IC = 2.1). Used to quantify the degree of disproportionate reporting and support signal validation decisions.']
  title varchar [note: 'Concise human-readable title describing the safety signal (e.g., \'Potential hepatotoxicity signal with Study Drug X\'). Used in dashboards, DSMB/DMC agendas, and regulatory submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety signal record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Master record for identified safety signals and their full evaluation lifecycle. Captures signal description, detection method (disproportionality analysis, PRR, ROR, BCPNN, clinical review, literature), detection date, data source, signal strength metrics, assigned medical monitor, evaluation plan, data reviewed, benefit-risk assessment summary, regulatory action recommended (label update, DHPC, study suspension, protocol amendment), evaluation completion date, DSMB/DMC referral flag, current status (new, under evaluation, validated, refuted, ongoing monitoring), and final disposition. Supports multiple evaluation cycles as new data emerges. Managed in Oracle Argus Safety signal detection and evaluation modules.'
}

Table safety.signal_evaluation {
  signal_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each signal evaluation record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the signal_evaluation data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures the formal evaluation workflow lifecycle for a detected safety signal.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: Signal evaluations specify which analysis population (safety population, subgroup) the signal was detected in. DSMB and regulatory signal evaluation reports require this population reference; it deter']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Signal evaluations are conducted per treatment arm to determine if a specific intervention is causing the signal. Benefit-risk assessments and regulatory action recommendations in signal_evaluation re']

A clinical research expert expects signal evaluations to reference the governing CTA.']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: signal_evaluation stores ctcae_grade as a STRING (not INT like other tables), which is a non-standard representation. Adding ctcae_grade_id FK to the ctcae_grade reference table normalizes this refere']
  dsmb_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.dsmb_review. Business justification: Signal evaluations are often reviewed and discussed at DSMB/DMC meetings.   treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Signal evaluations assess whether a safety signal is arm-specific, which is a core regulatory requirement for signal management (ICH E2E). signal_evaluation has investigational_product_name as a plain']
  argus_signal_evaluation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this signal evaluation as assigned by Oracle Argus Safety. Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory correspondence. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this TRANSACTION_HEADER.']
  benefit_risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the benefit-risk assessment conducted during this evaluation cycle, documenting the clinical judgment on the balance between therapeutic benefit and identified safety risk.']
  benefit_risk_conclusion varchar [note: 'Formal conclusion of the benefit-risk assessment for this evaluation cycle. Drives downstream regulatory action decisions.']
  case_count_reviewed varchar [note: 'Total number of adverse event cases reviewed during this signal evaluation cycle. Provides a quantitative measure of the evidence base examined.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the safety signal, using WHO-UMC or investigator causality criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this signal evaluation record was first created in the system. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_sources_reviewed varchar [note: 'Description of the data sources reviewed during this evaluation, such as clinical trial databases, spontaneous report databases (FAERS, EudraVigilance), published literature, and DSMB/DMC reports.']
  dsmb_dmc_recommendation varchar [note: 'Formal recommendation issued by the DSMB or DMC following their independent review of the referred signal evaluation. Populated only when a DSMB/DMC review has been completed.']
  dsmb_dmc_referral_date date [note: 'Date on which the signal evaluation was formally referred to the DSMB or DMC for independent safety review. Populated only when is_dsmb_dmc_referral is True.']
  evaluation_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal signal evaluation was completed and conclusions were finalized. Marks the end of the evaluation lifecycle for this cycle.']
  evaluation_cycle_number varchar [note: 'Sequential cycle number for this evaluation of the signal (e.g., 1 for initial evaluation, 2 for re-evaluation after new data). Supports tracking of iterative evaluation rounds for the same signal.']
  evaluation_initiated_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal signal evaluation process was initiated. Serves as the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this transaction, marking the start of the regulatory evaluation clock.']
  evaluation_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome determination of the signal evaluation cycle. Indicates whether the signal was confirmed as a new safety finding, refuted by the evidence, or requires ongoing monitoring.']
  evaluation_plan_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the evaluation plan, including the scope of data to be reviewed, analytical approach, and objectives for this evaluation cycle.']
  evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the signal evaluation workflow. Tracks progression from initiation through completion. LIFECYCLE_STATUS for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  expedited_report_due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the expedited safety report (e.g., SUSAR) must be submitted to the relevant authority. Calculated from the awareness date per ICH E2A timelines (7 or 15 calendar days).']
  expedited_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this signal evaluation triggers an expedited regulatory reporting obligation (e.g., SUSAR 7-day or 15-day report). True = expedited reporting required.']
  health_authority_query_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) for any formal query or request for information related to this signal evaluation. Enables traceability of regulatory correspondence.']
  investigational_product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or drug substance associated with the safety signal under evaluation.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the current active version of the signal evaluation. True = current version; False = superseded version. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) pattern in the Silver Layer.']
  is_dsmb_dmc_referral boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this signal evaluation has been referred to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) for independent review. True = referred; False = not referred.']
  is_susar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety signal under evaluation has been classified as a Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR), triggering specific expedited reporting obligations under ICH E2A.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term code representing the primary adverse event or medical concept under evaluation. Used for standardized coding and regulatory reporting alignment.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class code for the primary adverse event under evaluation. Supports aggregate safety reporting by organ system.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding the signal evaluation (e.g., 26.0). Ensures reproducibility and regulatory traceability of coding decisions.']
  protocol_amendment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this signal evaluation has resulted in a recommendation to amend the study protocol (e.g., dose modification, additional safety monitoring, exclusion criteria update).']
  regulatory_action_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for the recommended regulatory action, documenting the clinical and scientific rationale supporting the recommendation.']
  regulatory_action_recommended varchar [note: 'Formal regulatory action recommended as an outcome of this signal evaluation. Options include label update, Direct Healthcare Professional Communication (DHPC), study suspension, protocol amendment, Risk Management Plan (RMP) update, or urgent safety measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_action|label_update|dhpc|study_suspension|protocol_amendment|rmp_update|urgent_safety_measure|additional_pharmacovigilance — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_authority_notified varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary regulatory authority notified as part of this signal evaluation outcome. For multi-authority notifications, use \'multiple\' and document details in the regulatory action justification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|multiple|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  risk_minimization_measure varchar [note: 'Risk minimization measure recommended or implemented as a result of this signal evaluation. Supports Risk Management Plan (RMP) updates and regulatory commitments.']
  signal_detection_method varchar [note: 'Statistical or clinical methodology used to detect the safety signal prior to this evaluation. Examples include disproportionality analysis (PRR, ROR), data mining algorithms, or clinical case series review.']
  signal_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the safety signal that triggered this evaluation. Identifies whether the signal arose from spontaneous adverse event reports, clinical trial data, published literature, a regulatory authority communication, or DSMB/DMC review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: spontaneous_reports|clinical_trial_data|literature|regulatory_authority|dsmb_dmc|eudravigilance|faers|internal_review — promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this signal evaluation record originated. Primarily Oracle Argus Safety for adverse event signal management and regulatory case submission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this signal evaluation record was last modified. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for TRANSACTION_HEADER role. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this evaluation record, incremented each time the evaluation is updated or revised. Supports audit trail and version control per ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal evaluation workflow for a safety signal, including evaluation plan, data reviewed, benefit-risk assessment, regulatory action recommended (label update, DHPC, study suspension, protocol amendment), evaluation completion date, and DSMB/DMC referral flag. Each signal may have multiple evaluation cycles as new data emerges.'
}
