  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: DSMB reviews operate under a specific blinding configuration defining the committee\'s unblinded access level. Audit trails for GCP compliance require documenting which blinding_config governed each DS']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: DSMB reviews in EU trials operate under the CTA framework. DSMB findings may require CTA amendments or urgent safety measures. A clinical research expert expects DSMB reviews to reference the governin']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: DSMB data packages are prepared from a specific database lock/data cut. Traceability from DSMB review to the formal database_lock_event is required for audit trail and regulatory inspection; dsmb_revi']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: DSMB reviews are scheduled at specific study epochs (end of treatment, interim analysis points). Linking dsmb_review to epoch enables tracking of which epoch\'s data was reviewed and supports study_mil']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: DSMB reviews may result in IND safety reporting requirements or IND amendments. The IND governs whether DSMB findings require FDA notification. A clinical research expert expects DSMB reviews to refer']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal interim analysis record associated with this DSMB/DMC review, if applicable. Links safety governance to the statistical interim analysis conducted per the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_plan. Business justification: DSMB reviewers require the monitoring plan to assess data quality, SDV coverage, and monitoring approach when evaluating unblinded safety data. The DSMB charter typically references the monitoring pla']
  primary_dsmb_tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Document identifier for the official DSMB/DMC meeting minutes stored in the electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) system (Veeva Vault). Provides traceability to the formal meeting record. Classified confidential as minutes contain unblinded safety deliberations.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: DSMB charter and stopping rules are protocol-version-specific. The protocol version in effect at time of DSMB review determines the stopping criteria being evaluated. Regulatory submissions of DSMB re']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: DSMB unblinded safety reviews are conducted against a specific randomization list version. The unblinded statistician must reference the exact rand_list used during the review period to validate subje']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: DSMB reviews are conducted against a specific randomization scheme version governing blinding level, stratification, and allocation. The DSMB charter and data package are scheme-version-specific. dsmb']
  reconciliation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.reconciliation. Business justification: DSMB data packages require formal data reconciliation sign-off before unblinded data is presented. Linking the DSMB review to the reconciliation record documents the data quality status at the time of']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: DSMB review reports and recommendations are submitted to regulatory authorities and must be filed in regulatory binders. The existing submission_id links to the regulatory domain but not directly to t']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: DSMB reviews that identify safety issues trigger protocol amendments (protocol_amendment_required flag on dsmb_review). Linking to the resulting protocol amendment enables safety governance traceabili']
  review_committee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safety oversight committee (DSMB, DMC, IDMC, or internal SRC) that conducted this review. Links to the committee master record.']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: DSMB reviews are conducted per the SAP and statistical charter version in effect at the time of review. The unblinded statistician presents data per SAP specifications; regulatory submissions require ']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: DSMB reviews are conducted using unblinded SDTM datasets provided in the data package. Linking the DSMB review to the specific SDTM dataset version used is required for audit trails, reproducibility o']
  signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_signal. Business justification: dsmb_review has safety_signal_identified (BOOLEAN) and safety_signal_description (STRING) but no FK to the safety_signal record. A DSMB review may be triggered by or identify a specific safety signal.']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: DSMB charters define stopping rules based on primary statistical endpoints. Each DSMB review formally assesses whether efficacy or safety stopping boundaries have been crossed; linking to the statisti']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this DSMB/DMC review was conducted. Links the safety oversight record to the parent study master.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: DSMB reviews requiring regulatory notification generate formal submissions. Linking dsmb_review to its regulatory submission enables tracking of sponsor regulatory responses to DSMB outcomes, supporti']
  argus_safety_case_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this DSMB/DMC review to related safety cases or aggregate safety reports in Oracle Argus Safety. Enables cross-referencing between safety governance decisions and individual case safety reports.']
  charter_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the DSMB/DMC charter document in effect at the time of this review (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Ensures traceability to the governing charter under which the review was conducted.']
  closed_session_held boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a closed (confidential, unblinded) session was held during the DSMB/DMC meeting, attended only by committee members and the unblinded statistician (True = closed session held; False = no closed session).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DSMB/DMC review record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  data_cutoff_date date [note: 'The date through which clinical data was included in the unblinded safety data package reviewed by the DSMB/DMC. Defines the temporal scope of the safety data under review.']
  data_package_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the unblinded safety data package provided to the DSMB/DMC for this review. Ensures traceability between the committee\'s recommendation and the exact data snapshot reviewed.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor implemented the DSMB/DMC recommendation (e.g., date study was suspended, protocol amendment was issued, or continuation was confirmed). Used for regulatory timeline compliance tracking.']
  is_independent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee is fully independent of the sponsor and investigator team (True = independent; False = internal/non-independent). Independence is a key regulatory requirement for unblinded safety reviews.']
  meeting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the DSMB/DMC review meeting was held. This is the principal business event date for the review record and is used for regulatory timeline tracking.']
  meeting_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this DSMB/DMC meeting within the study (e.g., 1 = first meeting, 2 = second meeting). Used to track the progression of safety oversight across the study lifecycle.']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DSMB/DMC meeting format and trigger. Scheduled = pre-planned per charter; Ad hoc = convened outside regular schedule; Emergency = triggered by urgent safety signal; Teleconference = remote meeting; Written review = asynchronous document review without live meeting.']
  members_present_count varchar [note: 'Number of DSMB/DMC committee members who attended and participated in this review meeting. Used to verify quorum compliance and document meeting validity.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled DSMB/DMC review meeting as recommended by the committee or defined in the charter. Used for proactive scheduling and safety oversight continuity planning.']
  open_session_held boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an open (non-confidential) session was held during the DSMB/DMC meeting, typically attended by the sponsor and investigators (True = open session held; False = closed session only). Standard DSMB meetings include both open and closed sessions.']
  protocol_amendment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSMB/DMC recommendation requires a protocol amendment to be submitted to the IRB/IEC and regulatory authorities (True = amendment required; False = no amendment required). Triggers the protocol amendment workflow.']
  quorum_achieved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required quorum of DSMB/DMC members was present for the meeting to be valid per the committee charter (True = quorum met; False = quorum not met). Meetings without quorum may require rescheduling or written ratification.']
  recommendation varchar [note: 'The formal recommendation issued by the DSMB/DMC following the review. Continue = proceed without changes; Continue with modification = proceed with protocol or safety monitoring amendments; Suspend = temporary halt pending further review; Terminate = permanent study discontinuation; Pause enrollment = halt new enrollment while ongoing subjects continue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continue|continue_with_modification|suspend|terminate|pause_enrollment|other — promote to reference product]']
  recommendation_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the DSMB/DMC justifying the formal recommendation. Documents the safety findings, benefit-risk assessment, and reasoning that led to the committee\'s decision.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority was notified of the DSMB/DMC recommendation or safety findings. Populated only when regulatory_notification_required is True. Used for compliance with expedited reporting timelines.']
  regulatory_notification_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the regulatory authority must be notified per applicable regulations (e.g., 7-day or 15-day reporting window). Enables proactive compliance monitoring and escalation.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSMB/DMC recommendation or safety findings from this review require expedited regulatory notification to health authorities (True = notification required; False = no notification required).']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DSMB/DMC review record. Scheduled = meeting planned but not yet held; In progress = meeting underway or deliberations ongoing; Completed = recommendation issued and documented; Cancelled = meeting cancelled; Postponed = meeting rescheduled.']
  safety_signal_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSMB/DMC identified a new or emerging safety signal during this review that requires further investigation or monitoring (True = signal identified; False = no new signal). Triggers downstream signal management workflow in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this DSMB/DMC review record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary source is Oracle Argus Safety for safety governance records.']
  sponsor_response varchar [note: 'The sponsor\'s formal response to the DSMB/DMC recommendation. Accepted = sponsor will implement as recommended; Accepted with modification = sponsor accepts intent but modifies implementation; Rejected = sponsor disagrees and provides counter-rationale; Under review = sponsor is evaluating; Pending = response not yet provided.']
  sponsor_response_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the sponsor\'s response to the DSMB/DMC recommendation, particularly required when the sponsor rejects or modifies the recommendation. Documents the sponsor\'s benefit-risk reasoning.']
  stopping_rule_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specific pre-specified stopping rule that was triggered, if applicable (e.g., \'Futility boundary crossed at IA2\', \'Excess SAE rate in treatment arm exceeds 15%\'). Populated only when stopping_rule_triggered is True.']
  stopping_rule_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-specified stopping rule defined in the DSMB charter or Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) was triggered at this review (True = stopping rule met; False = no stopping rule triggered). Critical for regulatory audit trail.']
  study_phase_reviewed varchar [note: 'Clinical development phase of the study at the time of the DSMB/DMC review. Determines the regulatory framework and risk thresholds applicable to the safety assessment.']
  subjects_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects enrolled in the study at the time of the DSMB/DMC data cutoff. Provides context for the safety data volume reviewed and is a key metric in the committee\'s benefit-risk assessment.']
  subjects_exposed_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who received at least one dose of investigational product at the time of the data cutoff. Used for exposure-adjusted safety rate calculations in the DSMB/DMC review.']
  unblinded_statistician_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the independent unblinded statistician was present at the DSMB/DMC meeting to present the unblinded safety data package (True = present; False = not present). Required for valid closed-session deliberations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this DSMB/DMC review record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']

  Note: 'Master record for Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) / Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) safety governance, covering both committee definition and individual review meetings. Captures committee name, type (DSMB, DMC, IDMC, internal SRC), charter version, member roster, independence status, meeting date, meeting type (scheduled, ad hoc, emergency), study phase reviewed, unblinded data package version, interim analysis reference, formal recommendations issued (continue, modify, suspend, terminate), recommendation rationale, sponsor response, implementation date, and regulatory notification requirements. This is the SSOT for safety oversight committee governance and their formal outputs.'
}

Table safety.dsmb_recommendation {
  dsmb_recommendation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal DSMB/DMC recommendation record in the safety domain. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete formal recommendation event issued by the DSMB/DMC following a safety review meeting.']
  affected_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: DSMB stopping rules and arm-suspension recommendations target specific treatment arms. affected_treatment_arms on dsmb_recommendation is a plain text denormalization of treatment_arm. Operational impl']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record created in MasterControl in response to this DSMB/DMC recommendation. Links the safety recommendation to the quality management system for formal remediation tracking.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: DSMB recommendations in EU trials may require CTA amendments or urgent safety measures under the CTA framework. A clinical research expert expects DSMB recommendations to reference the governing CTA.']
  dsmb_review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent DSMB/DMC safety review meeting that generated this recommendation. A single review meeting may produce one or more recommendations.']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: DSMB recommendations often apply to specific epochs — e.g., stop the treatment epoch, extend follow-up epoch, or modify dosing in the next epoch. Epoch-level recommendations drive protocol amendments ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: DSMB recommendations requiring regulatory notification must reference the specific health authority to be notified. The dsmb_recommendation has regulatory_notification_required and regulatory_agencies']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: DSMB recommendations may require IND amendments or expedited safety reports to the FDA. The IND is the regulatory vehicle for implementing DSMB recommendations in US trials. A clinical research expert']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: Each DSMB recommendation is issued in response to a specific interim analysis. Regulatory submissions and TMF require traceability from recommendation to the triggering interim analysis; this link ena']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: DSMB recommendations frequently trigger protocol amendments (protocol_amendment_required flag exists on dsmb_recommendation). Linking to the resulting protocol amendment enables safety governance trac']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: DSMB recommendations for sample size re-estimation, stopping rule changes, or additional analyses require SAP amendments. Tracking which SAP version a recommendation targets is required for sponsor re']
  signal_evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.signal_evaluation. Business justification: DSMB recommendations are often issued in response to specific signal evaluations presented at the DSMB meeting. The dsmb_recommendation has signal_id → signal but no FK to the specific signal_evaluati']
  signal_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific safety signal or aggregate safety finding in Oracle Argus Safety that prompted or informed this DSMB/DMC recommendation. Nullable when the recommendation arises from routine scheduled review rather than a specific signal.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this DSMB/DMC recommendation was issued. Links the recommendation to the study protocol and sponsor context.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: DSMB recommendations requiring regulatory notification generate formal regulatory submissions. Linking the recommendation to its submission enables tracking of sponsor regulatory responses to DSMB dec']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF for the official DSMB/DMC recommendation letter or minutes filed in the Trial Master File. Ensures regulatory-compliant document traceability per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.']
  affected_subject_population varchar [note: 'Description of the subject population subgroup to which this recommendation applies (e.g., all enrolled subjects, subjects in a specific age cohort, subjects with a particular comorbidity). Supports targeted safety actions.']
  argus_safety_case_number varchar [note: 'Case number assigned in Oracle Argus Safety to the safety case or aggregate safety report associated with this DSMB/DMC recommendation. Enables traceability between the recommendation and the underlying pharmacovigilance case management record.']
  benefit_risk_summary varchar [note: 'Concise summary of the DSMB/DMC benefit-risk assessment that underpins the recommendation, capturing the committee\'s evaluation of the investigational product\'s benefit profile relative to observed safety risks at the time of review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this DSMB recommendation record was first captured in the data platform. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements and audit trail compliance.']
  dissenting_opinion varchar [note: 'Narrative capturing any dissenting opinion(s) from DSMB/DMC members who did not agree with the majority recommendation. Documented for regulatory transparency and governance purposes when vote_outcome is \'split\' or \'majority\'.']
  follow_up_review_date date [note: 'Target date for the follow-up DSMB/DMC review meeting required to assess the outcome of actions taken in response to this recommendation. Populated when follow_up_review_required is True.']
  follow_up_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recommendation requires a follow-up DSMB/DMC review meeting to assess implementation effectiveness or monitor evolving safety data. Drives scheduling of unscheduled interim reviews.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor implemented the actions required by the DSMB/DMC recommendation (e.g., protocol amendment effective date, dose modification start date, study suspension date). Critical for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  implementation_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory or charter-mandated deadline by which the sponsor must implement the DSMB/DMC recommendation. Supports compliance monitoring and escalation workflows.']
  informed_consent_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DSMB/DMC recommendation necessitates an update to the Informed Consent Form (ICF) to reflect new safety information. Triggers re-consent workflow in Clinpal eConsent system.']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the IRB/IEC was formally notified of this DSMB/DMC recommendation. Required for audit trail and compliance verification against ICH E6(R2) and applicable regulations.']
  irb_iec_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DSMB/DMC recommendation requires notification to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) overseeing the study. Required for significant safety findings and protocol changes.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this record represents the current active version of the recommendation. Set to False for superseded or amended versions retained for audit trail purposes.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the DSMB/DMC formally issued this recommendation to the sponsor following the safety review meeting. This is the principal real-world business event date for the recommendation lifecycle.']
  protocol_amendment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DSMB/DMC recommendation requires a formal protocol amendment to be submitted to regulatory authorities and IRB/IEC for approval before implementation.']
  rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the scientific, clinical, and safety evidence basis for the DSMB/DMC recommendation. Includes reference to interim efficacy data, safety data, benefit-risk assessment, and any stopping rules triggered. Required for regulatory submissions and CSR.']
  recommendation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this recommendation, typically formatted as a sequential reference within the study (e.g., REC-2024-001). Used for regulatory correspondence, sponsor communications, and TMF filing.']
  recommendation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the DSMB/DMC recommendation from initial issuance through sponsor acceptance or rejection and final closure. Drives workflow routing in Oracle Argus Safety and Oracle Siebel CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|under_review|accepted|rejected|superseded|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recommendation_type varchar [note: 'Formal classification of the DSMB/DMC recommendation issued following the safety review. Drives sponsor response obligations and regulatory notification requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: continue_unchanged|protocol_amendment_required|dose_modification|study_suspension|early_termination|other — promote to reference product]']
  recommendation_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this recommendation record, incremented when the recommendation is amended or superseded. Supports version control and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  regulatory_agencies_notified varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of health authority agencies notified of this recommendation (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Supports multi-jurisdictional regulatory compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the sponsor submitted the required regulatory notification to health authorities regarding this DSMB/DMC recommendation. Used to verify compliance with expedited reporting timelines.']
  regulatory_notification_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the sponsor must notify health authorities of this DSMB/DMC recommendation and any resulting study actions. Typically 7 or 15 calendar days depending on jurisdiction and recommendation type.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this DSMB/DMC recommendation triggers a mandatory regulatory notification obligation to health authorities (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA). True for recommendations involving study suspension, early termination, or significant safety findings.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the DSMB/DMC review that generated this recommendation, distinguishing between planned interim analyses, unscheduled safety reviews triggered by emerging signals, annual reviews, and final study reviews.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this DSMB recommendation record was sourced or ingested into the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sponsor_response varchar [note: 'Formal response of the study sponsor to the DSMB/DMC recommendation, indicating whether the recommendation has been accepted, rejected, or partially accepted. Drives implementation and regulatory notification workflows.']
  sponsor_response_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor formally communicated their response (acceptance, rejection, or partial acceptance) to the DSMB/DMC recommendation. Used to calculate response timeliness against regulatory and charter obligations.']
  sponsor_response_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the sponsor for their response to the DSMB/DMC recommendation, particularly required when the recommendation is rejected or only partially accepted. Documented in the TMF.']
  stopping_rule_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specific pre-specified stopping rule that was triggered, including the boundary type (efficacy, futility, safety), the statistical threshold, and the observed test statistic or safety metric that crossed the boundary.']
  stopping_rule_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-specified stopping rule defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) or DSMB charter was formally triggered as the basis for this recommendation. True when a boundary (efficacy, futility, or safety) was crossed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to this DSMB recommendation record. Required for change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  vote_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the DSMB/DMC committee vote that produced this recommendation, indicating whether the decision was unanimous, by majority, or resulted in a split vote. Relevant for regulatory transparency and committee governance documentation.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for formal DSMB/DMC recommendations issued following a safety review meeting. Captures recommendation type (continue unchanged, protocol amendment required, dose modification, study suspension, early termination), rationale, sponsor response, implementation date, and regulatory notification requirement. Each DSMB review may produce one or more recommendations.'
}

Table safety.reporting_obligation {
  reporting_obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory reporting obligation record in the safety_reporting_obligation reference master.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: EU safety reporting obligations are tied to specific CTAs under EudraCT/CTIS. Linking to clinical_trial_authorization replaces denormalized eudract_number and ctis_number and enables CTA-level safety ']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: Each regulatory reporting obligation (7-day, 15-day SUSAR, DSUR) is governed by a specific compliance SOP defining the process. reporting_obligation has document_sop_id pointing to document domain but']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Safety reporting obligations are defined per health authority (FDA 7/15-day rules, EMA rules). Linking to health_authority normalizes the agency reference, replaces denormalized agency_name, and enabl']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: Safety reporting obligations are defined per IMP per regulatory authority (IND, IMPD, CTA). safety_reporting_obligation.investigational_product_name is denormalized; FK to imp_master enables automated']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Safety reporting obligations are tied to specific IND applications (IND number is on the obligation). Linking to ind_application replaces the denormalized ind_number and enables IND-level safety repor']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Reporting obligations are IP-specific at the study level — different IPs in a combination study may have different reporting requirements. study.investigational_product provides the study-specific IP ']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Safety reporting obligations are defined at the protocol level — the protocol version determines applicable ICH E2A/E2B rules, reporting timelines, and expectedness criteria. Regulatory operations tea']
  reference_safety_tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Each reporting obligation is anchored to a Reference Safety Information (RSI) document (e.g., IB, SmPC) filed in the TMF. The role prefix \'reference_safety\' distinguishes this from generic TMF links. ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this reporting obligation applies. Enables study-specific obligation configuration and deadline tracking in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  superseded_by_obligation_reporting_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer safety_reporting_obligation record that supersedes this one when regulatory requirements are updated. Enables traceability of obligation version history and ensures Oracle Argus Safety applies the current rule.']
  agency_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regulatory agency that is the recipient of the safety report (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, HEALTH_CANADA). Determines submission routing in Oracle Argus Safety. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|HEALTH_CANADA|ANVISA|CDSCO|SFDA|COFEPRIS — promote to reference product]']
  applicable_regulation varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory citation governing this reporting obligation (e.g., \'21 CFR 312.32\', \'EU CTR Art. 42\', \'ICH E2A\', \'ICH E2F\', \'ISO 14155 Clause 7.10\'). Used for regulatory correspondence, audit trails, and compliance documentation in Veeva Vault.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this reporting obligation configuration was formally approved by the responsible regulatory affairs or pharmacovigilance authority. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance and SOP-controlled change management.']
  argus_report_type_code varchar [note: 'Oracle Argus Safety system report type code (e.g., \'EXP-7\', \'EXP-15\', \'DSUR-ANNUAL\') that maps this obligation to the Argus Safety case classification and output configuration. Used for automated case routing and report generation.']
  argus_workflow_template varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Oracle Argus Safety workflow template configured for this reporting obligation. Maps the obligation to the automated case processing, routing, and deadline calculation workflow in Argus Safety.']
  causality_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether this obligation applies to events assessed as related to the investigational product, unrelated, or all events regardless of causality. Drives Oracle Argus Safety auto-routing logic for expedited reports.']
  clock_day_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the reporting clock counts calendar days or business days. Most regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) use calendar days for expedited safety reports per ICH E2A.']
  clock_start_event varchar [note: 'Defines the business event that starts the regulatory reporting clock (e.g., AWARENESS_DATE — day sponsor becomes aware of the event; RECEIPT_DATE — day report is received from site). Critical for automated deadline calculation in Oracle Argus Safety per ICH E2A Day 0 rules.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction in which this reporting obligation applies (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, JPN). Drives country-specific regulatory deadline rules in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reporting obligation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  dsmb_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) must be notified in addition to the regulatory agency when this obligation is triggered. Drives parallel notification workflows in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this reporting obligation ceases to be effective. Null for open-ended obligations. Used to manage regulatory transitions and superseded obligation versions in Oracle Argus Safety configuration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this reporting obligation becomes effective and applicable to new safety events. Used to manage regulatory changes (e.g., transition from EudraCT to CTIS under EU CTR 536/2014) and version-controlled obligation records.']
  escalation_alert_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the regulatory deadline at which an escalation alert is triggered in Oracle Argus Safety to notify the safety team of an approaching submission deadline. Enables proactive compliance management.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety event category that triggers this reporting obligation (e.g., SUSAR_FATAL, SUSAR_NON_FATAL, SAE, IND_SAFETY_REPORT, DSUR). Drives the applicable reporting clock and submission format in Oracle Argus Safety. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUSAR_FATAL|SUSAR_NON_FATAL|SAE|AE|IND_SAFETY_REPORT|DSUR|PSUR|CIOMS_LINE_LISTING|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  expectedness_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether this obligation applies to unexpected adverse reactions (not listed in the Reference Safety Information), expected reactions, or all events. Unexpected serious adverse reactions (SUSARs) trigger expedited reporting under ICH E2A.']
  fatality_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies whether this obligation applies specifically to fatal events (7-day clock), non-fatal events (15-day clock), or all events regardless of outcome. Fatal SUSARs require 7-day expedited reporting under ICH E2A and 21 CFR 312.32.']
  follow_up_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days within which a follow-up report must be submitted after new significant information becomes available following the initial expedited report. Typically 15 days per ICH E2A for follow-up SUSARs.']
  follow_up_report_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up report must be submitted after the initial expedited report when additional information becomes available. Follow-up reports are required under ICH E2A when the initial report was incomplete.']
  grace_period_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours of grace period allowed beyond the regulatory deadline before a compliance breach is flagged in Oracle Argus Safety. Accounts for weekends, public holidays, and system downtime. Typically 0 for strict regulatory deadlines.']
  investigator_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all Principal Investigators (PIs) participating in the study must be notified of the safety finding when this obligation is triggered. Drives investigator notification letter generation in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  irb_iec_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) must be notified when this reporting obligation is triggered. Drives parallel IRB/IEC notification workflows in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this reporting obligation rule (e.g., SUSAR-FATAL-7D-FDA, SAE-15D-EMA). Used as the business key in Oracle Argus Safety deadline configuration and compliance dashboards.']
  obligation_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the reporting obligation (e.g., \'SUSAR Fatal 7-Day Expedited Report to FDA\', \'Annual Development Safety Update Report to EMA\'). Used in compliance monitoring dashboards and regulatory correspondence.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this reporting obligation record (ACTIVE — currently enforced; INACTIVE — no longer applicable; SUPERSEDED — replaced by a newer version; DRAFT — under review). Controls whether Oracle Argus Safety applies this obligation to new cases.']
  periodic_report_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which periodic safety reports (e.g., DSUR, PSUR) must be submitted under this obligation. Applicable only when reporting_category is PERIODIC or ANNUAL. Drives scheduled report generation in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  regulatory_basis_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the regulatory basis and rationale for this reporting obligation, including relevant guideline sections, agency guidance documents, and any country-specific deviations from ICH standards. Supports regulatory audit and inspection readiness.']
  reporting_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this obligation requires expedited (immediate) reporting or periodic/annual reporting. Expedited applies to SUSARs and fatal SAEs; periodic applies to DSURs and PSURs. Drives workflow prioritization in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  reporting_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from the clock start event within which the safety report must be submitted to the regulatory agency (e.g., 7 for fatal SUSARs, 15 for non-fatal SUSARs and SAEs, 30 for IND annual reports). Drives automated deadline calculation in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  seriousness_scope varchar [note: 'Defines whether this obligation applies to serious adverse events (SAEs), non-serious adverse events, or all events. Serious events meeting ICH E2A criteria trigger expedited reporting obligations.']
  sponsor_protocol_number varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned protocol number for the clinical study associated with this reporting obligation. Used to cross-reference obligations to the study protocol in Oracle Argus Safety and Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'Required electronic or paper format for the safety report submission (e.g., E2B_R3 for ICH-compliant electronic submissions via EudraVigilance/FDA FAERS, MEDWATCH_3500A for FDA paper/electronic, CIOMS_I for international expedited reports). Drives Oracle Argus Safety output configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: E2B_R3|E2B_R2|MEDWATCH_3500A|CIOMS_I|DSUR|PSUR|NARRATIVE|CTD_MODULE_5|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  submission_gateway varchar [note: 'Electronic gateway or portal through which the safety report is transmitted to the regulatory agency (e.g., EUDRAVIGILANCE for EMA, FDA_FAERS for FDA). Drives Oracle Argus Safety transmission routing configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EUDRAVIGILANCE|FDA_FAERS|MHRA_YELLOW_CARD|PMDA_GATEWAY|TGA_DATABASE|HEALTH_CANADA_MHPD|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this reporting obligation record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles for regulatory-grade data management.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of this reporting obligation record, incremented when regulatory requirements change (e.g., new ICH guideline, updated agency regulation). Supports version-controlled obligation management and audit trail in Veeva Vault.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining regulatory reporting timelines and obligations for each study-country-agency combination. Captures event type (SUSAR fatal, SUSAR non-fatal, SAE, annual DSUR, IND safety report), reporting clock duration (7-day, 15-day, 30-day, annual), applicable regulation (21 CFR 312.32, EU CTR Art. 42, ICH E2A), agency recipient, submission format (E2B R3, MedWatch 3500A, CIOMS I), and whether expedited or periodic reporting applies. Drives automated deadline calculation in Oracle Argus Safety and enables compliance monitoring dashboards.'
}

Table safety.expedited_report_deadline {
  expedited_report_deadline_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each expedited safety report deadline tracking record in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent adverse event or serious adverse event record for which this expedited report deadline is being tracked.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Expedited safety reports (7/15-day) submitted to health authorities must identify the treatment arm. Regulatory operations teams track arm-level expedited report compliance; arm attribution is require']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_capa. Business justification: Missed expedited reporting deadlines are GCP deviations that mandatorily trigger CAPAs in pharmacovigilance quality systems. Linking the deadline record to the resulting CAPA enables root cause tracki']
  causality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.causality_assessment. Business justification: The causality assessment determines whether expedited reporting is required (expedited_report_required flag on causality_assessment). The expedited_report_deadline has a causality_assessment denormali']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: EU expedited report deadlines are tracked per CTA under EudraCT/CTIS. Linking to clinical_trial_authorization replaces the denormalized eudract_number and enables CTA-level expedited reporting deadlin']
  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record. Business justification: Expedited reporting clock calculation requires the dispensing date as the product exposure reference point. The dispensing_record provides the authoritative dispensing timestamp needed to validate rep']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Expedited reporting clock-start and applicable timelines depend on the subject\'s enrollment context (IMP, arm, protocol version) to determine which reporting obligations apply. Pharmacovigilance opera']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Expedited report deadlines are defined per regulatory agency (FDA 7-day, EMA 15-day). Linking to health_authority normalizes the agency reference and enables deadline tracking by regulatory framework,']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.icsr. Business justification: Each expedited report deadline record tracks the submission deadline for a specific ICSR. expedited_report_deadline has icsr_number as a STRING reference which should be normalized to a proper FK. Add']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Expedited report deadlines are tracked per IND application per FDA requirements. Linking to ind_application replaces the denormalized ind_number and enables IND-level expedited reporting deadline comp']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Expedited reporting deadlines are determined by protocol-defined obligations and the protocol version in effect at AE onset. The protocol version determines expectedness (listed vs. unlisted) which dr']
  reporting_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_reporting_obligation. Business justification: Each expedited report deadline is calculated based on a specific regulatory reporting obligation (e.g., 7-day or 15-day rule for a specific agency). safety_reporting_obligation defines the clock_start']
  safety_report_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.safety_report_submission. Business justification: The expedited_report_deadline tracks whether the safety_report_submission was filed on time. This is the core compliance link between deadline management and actual submission. A clinical research exp']
  serious_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.serious_adverse_event. Business justification: Expedited report deadlines are calculated for SAEs that meet expedited reporting criteria. The expedited_report_deadline has adverse_event_id but not serious_adverse_event_id. A direct FK to serious_a']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which the adverse event and associated expedited report were generated.']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Data managers and safety teams need to trace the triggering visit data record for dea']
  susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.susar. Business justification: SUSARs have strict 7-day (fatal/life-threatening) and 15-day expedited reporting deadlines under ICH E2A and EU CTR. The expedited_report_deadline table tracks these deadlines but has no direct FK to ']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Expedited safety reports submitted to health authorities must be filed in the TMF as essential documents. Linking each deadline record to its filed TMF document enables inspection-ready tracking of su']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) who experienced the adverse event requiring expedited reporting.']
  applicable_deadline_date date [note: 'The operative deadline date for this specific expedited report submission, reflecting the applicable_deadline_days (7 or 15) from the awareness_date. This is the primary compliance target date for monitoring and alerting.']
  applicable_deadline_days varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days within which the expedited report must be submitted to the applicable regulatory agency (typically 7 or 15 days). Determined by the seriousness, expectedness, and causality of the adverse event per ICH E2A.']
  argus_case_number varchar [note: 'The unique case identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety for the Individual Case Safety Report (ICSR) associated with this deadline record. Used to cross-reference the source safety system.']
  argus_workflow_status varchar [note: 'The current workflow processing status of the ICSR case within Oracle Argus Safety, reflecting the internal case processing lifecycle from initial data entry through medical review, QC, approval, and submission.']
  awareness_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor or CRO first became aware of the adverse event meeting expedited reporting criteria. This is the regulatory clock start date (Day 0) from which all reporting deadlines are calculated, per ICH E2A.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'The investigator\'s or sponsor\'s assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse event. Causality is a key determinant of SUSAR classification and expedited reporting obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this expedited report deadline tracking record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days by which the submission exceeded the applicable regulatory deadline. Positive value indicates late submission; zero or null indicates on-time or not yet due. Used for CAPA tracking and regulatory risk assessment.']
  days_to_submission varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days elapsed between the awareness_date (clock start) and the actual submission_date. Populated only after submission. Used for compliance KPI reporting and trend analysis against ICH E2A timelines.']
  deadline_15_day date [note: 'Calculated deadline date for 15-day expedited reporting, applicable to non-fatal/non-life-threatening unexpected SUSARs and other serious unexpected adverse reactions. Computed as awareness_date + 15 calendar days per ICH E2A.']
  deadline_7_day date [note: 'Calculated deadline date for 7-day expedited reporting, applicable to fatal or life-threatening unexpected SUSARs. Computed as awareness_date + 7 calendar days per ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR Part 312.32.']
  deadline_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the expedited report deadline. Pending = report not yet submitted and deadline not passed; Submitted_on_time = submitted within required days; Submitted_late = submitted after deadline; Overdue = deadline passed without submission; Waived = regulatory waiver granted; Not_applicable = reporting exemption applies.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason explaining why the expedited report was submitted after the applicable regulatory deadline. Required for CAPA documentation and regulatory inspection readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incomplete_clinical_info|pending_causality_assessment|medical_review_delay|translation_required|system_outage|sponsor_approval_delay|other — promote to reference product]']
  delay_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the specific circumstances that caused the expedited report to be submitted after the regulatory deadline. Supports CAPA documentation and regulatory inspection responses.']
  dsmb_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was formally notified of this expedited safety report.']
  dsmb_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was notified of this expedited safety report. Required for studies with an active DSMB/DMC per the study protocol.']
  follow_up_due_date date [note: 'The target date by which a follow-up expedited safety report must be submitted to the regulatory agency with updated case information. Applicable when follow_up_required is True.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up expedited safety report is required to provide additional or updated information to the regulatory agency after the initial submission, per ICH E2A guidance.']
  investigator_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) was notified of the SUSAR or expedited safety report submission, as required by ICH E2A and GCP guidelines. Supports site-level compliance tracking.']
  irb_iec_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was notified of the SUSAR or expedited safety report, as required by GCP and applicable regulations.']
  is_fatal_or_life_threatening boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event is classified as fatal or life-threatening, which triggers the 7-day expedited reporting requirement rather than the standard 15-day timeline per ICH E2A.']
  is_on_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the expedited safety report was submitted on or before the applicable regulatory deadline. True = submitted within the required timeframe; False = submitted late or not yet submitted past deadline. Core compliance KPI.']
  is_unexpected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the adverse event is unexpected (not consistent with the Investigator Brochure or current labeling), which is a key criterion for SUSAR classification and expedited reporting obligations.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority to which the expedited safety report must be submitted (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Determines the applicable reporting timeline and format requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|ANVISA|Other — promote to reference product]']
  report_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this deadline record pertains to an initial expedited report submission, a follow-up report with updated information, or a final report closing the case.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the expedited safety report type. SAE = Serious Adverse Event report; SUSAR = Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction; USADE = Unexpected Serious Adverse Device Effect; IND_Safety = IND Safety Report; BLA_Safety = Biologics License Application Safety Report.']
  reporting_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the jurisdiction in which the expedited report must be filed. Drives country-specific regulatory timeline requirements.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the adverse event by the investigator or CRO. May differ from awareness_date if the CRO received information before the sponsor. Relevant for sponsor-level clock start determination.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the expedited safety report was submitted to the regulatory agency. Null if the report has not yet been submitted. Used to calculate days-to-submission and determine on-time compliance.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The electronic or paper-based method used to transmit the expedited safety report to the regulatory agency (e.g., eCTD via FDA ESG, E2B(R3) via EMA EVWEB, MedWatch 3500A, paper submission).']
  submission_reference_number varchar [note: 'The acknowledgement or tracking number returned by the regulatory agency upon receipt of the expedited safety report submission (e.g., FDA ESG acknowledgement number, EMA EVWEB submission ID). Confirms successful receipt.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the expedited safety report was electronically transmitted to the regulatory agency, including timezone offset. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp at which this expedited report deadline tracking record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  waiver_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal regulatory waiver or exemption has been granted for this expedited report, allowing submission outside the standard 7- or 15-day timeline. When True, waiver_reference must be populated.']
  waiver_reference varchar [note: 'The reference number or description of the regulatory waiver or exemption granted for this expedited report submission. Populated only when waiver_granted is True.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the calculated and actual submission deadlines for each expedited safety report (ICSR/SUSAR). Captures clock start date (date of first awareness), 7-day and 15-day deadline dates, actual submission date, days-to-submission, on-time flag, reason for delay if late, and regulatory agency. Enables compliance monitoring against ICH E2A and regional reporting requirements.'
}
