  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this DSUR was formally approved by the sponsor\'s qualified medical officer or pharmacovigilance responsible person, as recorded in the document management system.']
  argus_periodic_report_reference varchar [note: 'The internal report identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety periodic reporting module, used to trace this DSUR back to its source system record.']
  benefit_risk_conclusion varchar [note: 'The sponsor\'s overall benefit-risk conclusion for the investigational product based on the cumulative safety data reviewed in this DSUR, as required by ICH E2F.']
  benefit_risk_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative text summarizing the sponsor\'s benefit-risk assessment for the reporting period, including key safety findings, emerging signals, and overall conclusions as documented in the DSUR.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this DSUR record was first created in the system, providing the audit trail creation anchor per ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  cumulative_sae_count varchar [note: 'Total number of serious adverse events (SAEs) reported for the investigational product from program inception through the end of the reporting period, as captured in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  cumulative_subject_exposure_count varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects exposed to the investigational product from the inception of the development program through the end of the reporting period, representing the cumulative safety exposure denominator.']
  cumulative_subject_exposure_days varchar [note: 'Total patient-days of exposure to the investigational product accumulated across all studies from program inception through the reporting period end date, used as the denominator for incidence rate calculations.']
  cumulative_susar_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SUSARs (Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reactions) reported from program inception through the reporting period end date, a key regulatory safety metric.']
  data_lock_date date [note: 'The database lock date on which safety data was frozen for inclusion in this DSUR. No new safety data is incorporated after this date for the current reporting period.']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'The clinical development phase of the investigational product at the time of this DSUR (e.g., Phase I, Phase II, Phase III), which contextualizes the safety data and benefit-risk assessment.']
  dibd date [note: 'The Development International Birth Date — the date of first approval of a Clinical Trial Application (CTA) or equivalent in any country, used as the anchor date for annual DSUR reporting cycles.']
  dsmb_review_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) review was conducted during the reporting period, with findings incorporated into this DSUR.']
  fatal_sae_count varchar [note: 'Number of serious adverse events with a fatal outcome reported during the reporting period, a critical safety metric for benefit-risk assessment and DSMB/DMC review.']
  health_authority_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the DSUR submission, used for regulatory compliance tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  health_authority_query_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority issued a query or request for additional information in response to this DSUR submission, triggering a response workflow.']
  medical_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the qualified medical officer or pharmacovigilance physician who performed the medical review and sign-off of this DSUR prior to submission.']
  new_safety_signal_identified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a new safety signal for the investigational product was identified during the reporting period, triggering additional signal management activities and potential regulatory notifications.']
  ongoing_study_count varchar [note: 'Number of clinical studies with the investigational product that were ongoing during the reporting period, providing context for the scope of the safety data.']
  period_sae_count varchar [note: 'Number of serious adverse events (SAEs) reported during the current annual reporting period, used to assess period-over-period safety trends.']
  period_subject_exposure_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects exposed to the investigational product during the current annual reporting period only, used to assess period-specific safety signal rates.']
  period_susar_count varchar [note: 'Number of SUSARs reported during the current annual reporting period, used for expedited reporting compliance monitoring and signal detection.']
  reference_safety_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of reference safety information document used to assess expectedness of adverse reactions in this DSUR (e.g., IB = Investigator\'s Brochure, CCSI = Company Core Safety Information, SmPC = Summary of Product Characteristics, USPI = US Prescribing Information, RSI = Reference Safety Information).']
  reference_safety_document_version varchar [note: 'Version number or edition of the reference safety document (e.g., Investigator\'s Brochure edition) used as the basis for expectedness assessment in this DSUR.']
  regulatory_action_taken varchar [note: 'Significant regulatory or safety action taken during the reporting period as a result of safety findings (e.g., protocol amendment, Investigator\'s Brochure update, study suspension). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|protocol_amendment|ibr_update|study_suspension|study_termination|rmp_update|label_update|dhcp_letter — promote to reference product]']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known sequential report number assigned to this DSUR (e.g., DSUR-2024-001), used for regulatory tracking and cross-referencing across submissions.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the DSUR document, tracking its progression from authoring through regulatory acceptance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|submitted|accepted|rejected|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the DSUR document (e.g., 1.0, 2.0), incremented upon amendments or corrections to a previously submitted report.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the annual reporting period covered by this DSUR, marking the data cut-off for safety data included in the report.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the annual reporting period covered by this DSUR, typically the Development International Birth Date (DIBD) anniversary.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual date on which this DSUR was submitted to the primary health authority, used for regulatory compliance tracking and on-time submission KPI monitoring.']
  submission_due_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which this DSUR must be submitted to health authorities, typically 60 days after the reporting period end date per ICH E2F.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'The electronic or paper format used for the DSUR submission to health authorities (e.g., eCTD = Electronic Common Technical Document, NeeS = Non-eCTD Electronic Submission).']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The clinical therapeutic area or disease indication for which the investigational product is being developed (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology), used for safety signal contextualization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this DSUR record was last modified in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage tracking per ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Development Safety Update Report (DSUR) — the annual periodic safety report submitted to health authorities summarizing cumulative safety data for an investigational product across all ongoing studies. Captures reporting period, data lock date, cumulative subject exposure, SAE line listing summary, benefit-risk conclusion, and submission dates per agency. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety periodic reporting module.'
}

Table safety.ctcae_grade {
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each CTCAE grade definition record in the reference table. This is the primary key for the ctcae_grade product. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — a controlled NCI-published code list of severity grades; per-role minimum categories are exempt.]']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: ctcae_grade stores meddra_pt_code, meddra_pt_name, meddra_soc_code, meddra_version as strings representing the MedDRA mapping for each CTCAE grade. Adding meddra_term_id FK to the meddra_term referenc']
  ae_term varchar [note: 'The official CTCAE adverse event term name as published by NCI (e.g., \'Atrial fibrillation\', \'Nausea\', \'Peripheral neuropathy\'). This is the standardized clinical term used to classify and report adverse events across all clinical trials. Corresponds to the AETERM or AEDECOD field in CDISC SDTM AE domain and is the primary human-readable label for this grading record.']
  applicable_population varchar [note: 'Specifies the patient population for which this CTCAE grade definition is applicable (e.g., \'All\', \'Pediatric\', \'Adult\', \'Oncology\'). Some CTCAE terms and grade criteria differ by population (e.g., pediatric-specific grading criteria). Used to filter applicable grade definitions when assigning severity in studies with specific population constraints.']
  cdisc_codelist_code varchar [note: 'The CDISC controlled terminology codelist code associated with this CTCAE grade entry (e.g., the AETOXGR codelist code). Enables direct mapping to CDISC SDTM submission datasets and ADaM analysis datasets produced in SAS Drug Development. Required for eCTD regulatory submissions to FDA, EMA, and PMDA.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'A brief description of what changed in this grade definition relative to the prior CTCAE version (e.g., \'Grade 3 criteria updated to include specific lab threshold\', \'New term added in v5.0\', \'Grade description clarified\'). Supports data managers and regulatory affairs teams in assessing the impact of CTCAE version upgrades on ongoing studies and in preparing regulatory submission change justifications.']
  clinical_criteria varchar [note: 'Supplementary clinical criteria, laboratory thresholds, or objective measurement parameters that define the boundary conditions for assigning this specific grade to the AE term (e.g., specific lab value ranges, vital sign thresholds, functional impairment descriptors). Distinct from the grade_description in that it captures the measurable/objective criteria rather than the narrative description. Used by clinical staff for precise grade assignment and by biostatisticians for consistency validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this CTCAE grade reference record was first loaded into the data lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, ALCOA+ principles, and GDPR Article 5 data integrity obligations. Used by data management teams to track when new CTCAE version data was ingested.']
  ctcae_code varchar [note: 'Official NCI-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the CTCAE adverse event term within a given version. Used as the primary business identifier for cross-system referencing and CDISC SDTM mapping (AETOXGR domain). Enables unambiguous linkage between AE records and the reference grading criteria.']
  ctcae_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the CTCAE publication from which this grade definition originates (e.g., \'v5.0\', \'v4.03\'). Critical for ensuring AE severity grading is applied against the correct version mandated by the study protocol. New CTCAE versions are released by the National Cancer Institute (NCI) and may alter grade descriptions or add/remove terms.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this CTCAE grade definition was superseded, retired, or replaced by a newer version (nullable for currently active records). Used in conjunction with effective_start_date to implement bi-temporal versioning of the reference table, ensuring that AE records can always be validated against the grade criteria that were in effect at the time of the adverse event.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this CTCAE grade definition became effective and available for use in clinical trial AE grading (typically the official NCI publication date of the CTCAE version). Used to determine which version of a grade definition was applicable at the time of an AE occurrence, supporting retrospective audit and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  grade varchar [note: 'Numeric severity grade (1 through 5) as defined by CTCAE. Grade 1 = Mild; Grade 2 = Moderate; Grade 3 = Severe; Grade 4 = Life-threatening or disabling; Grade 5 = Death related to AE. This is the core grading dimension of the reference table and is used to populate the AETOXGR field in CDISC SDTM submissions and to trigger SAE/SUSAR reporting workflows in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  grade_description varchar [note: 'Full verbatim clinical description of the severity grade for the specific AE term as published in the CTCAE by NCI (e.g., for \'Nausea\' Grade 2: \'Moderate nausea; oral intake decreased without significant weight loss, dehydration or malnutrition\'). This is the authoritative clinical criteria text used by investigators and CRAs to assign the correct grade during AE assessment. Stored verbatim to preserve regulatory integrity.']
  grade_label varchar [note: 'Standardized textual label corresponding to the numeric CTCAE grade (e.g., \'Mild\' for Grade 1, \'Moderate\' for Grade 2, \'Severe\' for Grade 3, \'Life-Threatening\' for Grade 4, \'Death\' for Grade 5). Provides a human-readable severity descriptor for use in clinical reports, DSMB/DMC safety narratives, and regulatory submissions without requiring a lookup of the numeric grade.']
  grading_notes varchar [note: 'Additional clinical notes, caveats, or guidance provided by NCI for applying this specific grade to the AE term (e.g., \'Also consider Anemia\', \'See also Cardiac disorders: Left ventricular systolic dysfunction\'). Captures the \'Also consider\' and \'Navigational Note\' annotations present in the CTCAE publication that guide investigators in differential grading decisions.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CTCAE grade definition record is currently active and available for use in new AE grading assignments. Set to False when a term or grade is retired, superseded by a newer CTCAE version, or deprecated. Enables version-controlled filtering so that active studies use only current, approved grading criteria while historical records remain accessible for audit.']
  is_oncology_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CTCAE adverse event term and grade definition is specific to oncology/cancer treatment contexts. CTCAE was originally developed for oncology trials; some terms are exclusively relevant to chemotherapy, radiation, or immunotherapy adverse effects. Used to filter applicable grading criteria for oncology versus non-oncology study protocols.']
  is_serious_by_grade boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CTCAE grade level is inherently classified as a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) based on regulatory definitions (typically Grade 3 and above may trigger SAE criteria, Grade 4–5 are always serious). Used to automate SAE flagging logic in Oracle Argus Safety and EDC systems (Medidata Rave) when a grade is recorded, supporting expedited reporting timelines under ICH E2A and FDA 21 CFR 312.32.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code that most closely corresponds to this CTCAE adverse event term, enabling cross-coding between CTCAE and MedDRA terminologies. Used for regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) that require MedDRA coding, and for signal detection in Oracle Argus Safety. Alignment maintained per current MedDRA version used in the study.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) numeric code corresponding to the primary SOC classification of this CTCAE term\'s MedDRA PT cross-reference. Enables integrated safety tabulations that span both CTCAE-graded AE data and MedDRA-coded data in regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC reports.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for the cross-reference mapping between this CTCAE term and its corresponding MedDRA PT/SOC codes (e.g., \'26.0\', \'25.1\'). MedDRA is updated biannually; version tracking ensures regulatory submissions reference the correct dictionary version as required by FDA, EMA, and PMDA.']
  nci_concept_code varchar [note: 'The NCI Thesaurus (NCIt) concept identifier (e.g., \'C54767\') for this CTCAE adverse event term. Provides a stable, version-independent identifier from the NCI controlled vocabulary that persists across CTCAE versions. Used for ontology-based data integration, CDISC terminology alignment, and cross-study analytics in SAS Drug Development and AI/ML safety signal detection pipelines.']
  nci_publication_date date [note: 'The official publication date of the CTCAE version from which this grade definition originates, as released by the National Cancer Institute (NCI) Cancer Therapy Evaluation Program (CTEP). Provides the authoritative reference date for regulatory submissions and audit trails, distinct from the effective_start_date which may reflect internal adoption timing.']
  prior_version_code varchar [note: 'The CTCAE term code from the immediately preceding CTCAE version that this record supersedes or corresponds to, enabling version-to-version lineage tracking. Used by data managers and biostatisticians to reconcile AE grading data collected under different CTCAE versions within the same study or across studies in integrated safety analyses.']
  reporting_timeline_days varchar [note: 'The maximum number of calendar days within which an AE at this grade must be reported to regulatory authorities upon sponsor awareness (e.g., 7 days for fatal/life-threatening unexpected SUSARs, 15 days for other serious unexpected reactions, null for non-expedited grades). Drives automated due-date calculation in Oracle Argus Safety and regulatory_report_due_date population on the adverse_event product.']
  requires_expedited_reporting boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an AE recorded at this CTCAE grade level triggers mandatory expedited regulatory reporting obligations (e.g., 7-day or 15-day IND safety reports to FDA, EMA, PMDA). Derived from regulatory guidance but stored as a reference attribute to drive automated reporting workflow triggers in Oracle Argus Safety and CTMS. Supports compliance with FDA 21 CFR 312.32, EMA GVP Module VI, and ICH E2A timelines.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The two-letter CDISC SDTM domain code to which this CTCAE grade reference applies (e.g., \'AE\' for Adverse Events domain). Supports SDTM dataset construction in SAS Drug Development and ensures correct domain-level mapping during regulatory submission preparation.']
  sdtm_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM variable name to which this CTCAE grade value maps in regulatory submission datasets (e.g., \'AETOXGR\' for the AE domain toxicity grade). Used by SAS Drug Development programmers and CDISC data managers to ensure correct SDTM dataset population and FDA/EMA submission compliance.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'NCI-assigned code for the System Organ Class (SOC) category within the CTCAE hierarchy. Enables programmatic grouping and filtering of adverse event terms by organ system for safety tabulations, DSMB/DMC reports, and regulatory submissions. Distinct from MedDRA SOC codes but may be cross-referenced.']
  soc_name varchar [note: 'The System Organ Class (SOC) category name as defined in the CTCAE, representing the highest-level anatomical or physiological grouping of adverse event terms (e.g., \'Cardiac disorders\', \'Gastrointestinal disorders\', \'Nervous system disorders\'). Aligns with MedDRA SOC hierarchy for cross-coding purposes. Used for safety signal detection and aggregate reporting by organ system.']
  source_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section, page, or table in the official NCI CTCAE publication document from which this grade definition was extracted (e.g., \'CTCAE v5.0, Section 2, Table: Cardiac Disorders, p.12\'). Supports regulatory inspection readiness and audit trail requirements by providing a direct link to the authoritative source document stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system or authoritative source from which this CTCAE grade reference record was loaded (e.g., \'Oracle Argus Safety\' where CTCAE codes are maintained, \'SAS Drug Development\' for CDISC-aligned reference data, \'NCI CTCAE\' for direct NCI publication ingestion). Supports data lineage tracking and Silver layer provenance requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent update to this CTCAE grade reference record in the data lakehouse Silver layer. Tracks corrections, enrichments, or metadata updates applied after initial load. Required for audit trail completeness under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  veeva_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF for the official NCI CTCAE source document associated with this version. Enables direct navigation from the reference record to the controlled source document in the electronic Trial Master File, supporting audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness per 21 CFR Part 11 and EU CTR 536/2014.']

  Note: 'Reference table for CTCAE (Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events) severity grading definitions. Captures CTCAE version, SOC category, adverse event term, grade (1–5), grade description, and clinical criteria. Used to standardize severity grading across all AE records. Infrequently updated when new CTCAE versions are released by NCI.'
}

Table safety.meddra_term {
  meddra_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each MedDRA term record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — _canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a standardized regulatory reference code list (MedDRA hierarchy) maintained by MSSO/ICH; per-role minimums for REFERENCE_LOOKUP are exempt.]']
  argus_dict_reference varchar [note: 'The internal dictionary record identifier assigned by Oracle Argus Safety when this MedDRA term is loaded into the Argus dictionary module. Enables direct cross-reference between the lakehouse silver layer and the Oracle Argus Safety system of record for adverse event coding reconciliation and audit.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Describes the nature of the change applied to this term in the current MedDRA version: \'new\' (first introduced), \'modified\' (name or hierarchy changed), \'retired\' (removed from active use), \'split\' (one term divided into multiple), \'merged\' (multiple terms combined), or \'reinstated\' (previously retired term reactivated). Critical for managing dictionary upgrade impacts in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this MedDRA term record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and ALCOA+ data integrity principles for the clinical data management platform.']
  ctcae_grade_mapping varchar [note: 'The CTCAE v5.0 term name that corresponds to this MedDRA PT, where a direct mapping exists. Enables cross-referencing between MedDRA coding and CTCAE severity grading used in oncology and other therapeutic area studies. Null where no CTCAE mapping exists for this term.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this MedDRA term is officially effective and available for use in adverse event coding and regulatory submissions, corresponding to the MSSO release date of the version in which it was introduced. Supports version-gating logic in Oracle Argus Safety and study-level coding version controls.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'The level within the five-tier MedDRA hierarchy to which this term belongs: LLT (Lowest Level Term), PT (Preferred Term), HLT (High Level Term), HLGT (High Level Group Term), or SOC (System Organ Class). Determines how the term is used in adverse event coding, CTCAE mapping, and regulatory aggregate reporting.']
  hlgt_code varchar [note: 'The 8-digit MedDRA code for the High Level Group Term (HLGT) in the primary SOC path for this term. Populated for LLT, PT, HLT, and HLGT level terms to support aggregate safety analysis and regulatory tabulations at the HLGT level.']
  hlgt_name varchar [note: 'The official MedDRA name of the High Level Group Term (HLGT) in the primary SOC path. Denormalized for direct use in safety tabulations and signal detection analytics without requiring a hierarchy traversal.']
  hlt_code varchar [note: 'The 8-digit MedDRA code for the High Level Term (HLT) in the primary SOC path for this term. Populated for LLT, PT, and HLT level terms to support aggregate safety analysis at the HLT level in regulatory submissions and DSMB/DMC reports.']
  hlt_name varchar [note: 'The official MedDRA name of the High Level Term (HLT) in the primary SOC path. Denormalized for direct use in safety tabulations and signal detection analytics.']
  introduced_version varchar [note: 'The MedDRA version in which this term was first introduced into the dictionary. Supports historical analysis of term availability across study timelines and ensures correct version-gating when coding adverse events from legacy studies.']
  is_llt_current boolean [note: 'Applicable to LLT-level terms only. Indicates whether the LLT is a \'current\' LLT (true) — meaning it maps to a PT with the same name — or a \'non-current\' LLT (false) that maps to a PT with a different name. Current LLTs are preferred for new coding; non-current LLTs are retained for historical traceability per MSSO policy.']
  is_primary_soc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term\'s association to a System Organ Class (SOC) is the primary (true) or secondary (false) SOC linkage. MedDRA allows a PT to be linked to multiple SOCs; the primary SOC is used for regulatory aggregate safety tabulations, DSMB/DMC safety reviews, and CTCAE-aligned reporting.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code for the language of this term record (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'ja\' for Japanese, \'de\' for German). MedDRA is published in multiple languages; this field supports multi-language implementations required for submissions to PMDA (Japanese), EMA (EU languages), and Health Canada (French/English).']
  llt_code varchar [note: 'The 8-digit MedDRA code for the Lowest Level Term (LLT). For LLT-level records this equals term_code. For higher-level terms this field is null. LLTs capture verbatim-level medical concepts and are the entry point for verbatim-to-MedDRA coding in Oracle Argus Safety and EDC systems.']
  llt_name varchar [note: 'The official MedDRA name of the Lowest Level Term (LLT). For LLT-level records this equals term_name. Null for higher-level terms. Used in verbatim coding workflows and auto-coding dictionaries in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  load_date date [note: 'The date on which this MedDRA term record was loaded into the lakehouse silver layer from the MSSO distribution files. Used to track the effective date of each biannual dictionary update and to support audit trails required under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'The biannual MedDRA release version in which this term was introduced or last updated (e.g., \'27.0\', \'26.1\'). Critical for regulatory submissions requiring version-specific coding, database lock (DBL) documentation, and CDISC SDTM dataset compliance. MedDRA releases occur twice yearly (March and September).']
  pt_code varchar [note: 'The 8-digit MedDRA code for the Preferred Term (PT) to which this term maps. For PT-level records this equals term_code. For LLT-level records this is the parent PT code. Used as the primary coding level in Oracle Argus Safety adverse event records, CDISC SDTM (AEDECOD), and regulatory case submissions (ICH E2B).']
  pt_name varchar [note: 'The official MedDRA name of the Preferred Term (PT) to which this term maps. Denormalized for direct use in adverse event coding displays, safety narratives, CDISC SDTM datasets, and regulatory submissions without requiring a hierarchy join.']
  rave_dict_entry_reference varchar [note: 'The dictionary entry identifier assigned by Medidata Rave EDC when this MedDRA term is configured in the Rave coding dictionary for eCRF verbatim-to-MedDRA auto-coding. Supports reconciliation between EDC-coded adverse events and the master MedDRA reference in the lakehouse.']
  replacement_term_code varchar [note: 'The 8-digit MedDRA code of the term that replaces this term when it has been retired, split, or merged. Null for active terms and retired terms with no direct replacement. Supports automated re-coding workflows in Oracle Argus Safety during biannual MedDRA version upgrades.']
  retired_version varchar [note: 'The MedDRA version in which this term was retired, if applicable. Null for active terms. Used to enforce coding rules in Oracle Argus Safety and to flag terms that should not be applied to new adverse event records after the specified version.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which this MedDRA term was officially retired by MSSO, corresponding to the release date of the version in which it was retired. Null for active terms. Used to enforce coding restrictions in Oracle Argus Safety and to flag terms in legacy study datasets.']
  smq_membership varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of Standardised MedDRA Query (SMQ) names to which this PT belongs. SMQs are validated, topic-focused groupings of MedDRA terms used for signal detection, aggregate safety analysis, and regulatory queries (e.g., \'Anaphylactic reaction\', \'Drug-induced liver injury\'). Populated for PT-level terms only.']
  smq_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this term\'s SMQ membership is classified as \'broad\' (higher sensitivity, more inclusive) or \'narrow\' (higher specificity, more restrictive) within the associated SMQ. Used to configure signal detection algorithms in Oracle Argus Safety and to define the scope of safety queries in regulatory submissions.']
  soc_abbreviation varchar [note: 'The standardized two-to-four letter abbreviation for the System Organ Class (SOC) as defined by MSSO (e.g., \'CARD\' for Cardiac disorders, \'NERV\' for Nervous system disorders). Used in safety tabulation headers, DSMB/DMC slide decks, and abbreviated regulatory reports.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'The 8-digit MedDRA code for the System Organ Class (SOC) to which this term is linked (primary or secondary). Populated for all hierarchy levels to enable direct SOC-level filtering in safety tabulations, signal detection, and regulatory submissions without requiring a hierarchy traversal join.']
  soc_name varchar [note: 'The official MedDRA name of the System Organ Class (SOC) associated with this term. Denormalized for direct use in safety tabulations, DSMB/DMC reports, and CDISC SDTM datasets (AEBODSYS variable) without requiring a hierarchy join.']
  source_file_name varchar [note: 'The name of the MSSO-distributed MedDRA ASCII flat file from which this term record was loaded (e.g., \'llt.asc\', \'pt.asc\', \'hlt.asc\', \'hlgt.asc\', \'soc.asc\'). Supports data lineage traceability and ETL audit for the biannual MedDRA dictionary update process in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'The official 8-digit numeric MedDRA term code assigned by the Maintenance and Support Services Organization (MSSO) uniquely identifying this term within the MedDRA hierarchy. Used as the primary lookup key in Oracle Argus Safety for adverse event coding and regulatory submissions.']
  term_name varchar [note: 'The official MedDRA term label as published by MSSO for this term code. Represents the standardized medical concept name used in adverse event coding, regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA), and safety signal detection across all five hierarchy levels.']
  term_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the MedDRA term is currently active and available for coding or has been retired in a subsequent MedDRA version release. Retired terms are retained for historical traceability of previously coded adverse events but must not be used for new coding in Oracle Argus Safety.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the term as \'standard\' (fully endorsed MedDRA term) or \'non-standard\' (a term that has been superseded, split, or merged and retained for backward compatibility). Drives coding validation rules in Oracle Argus Safety and Medidata Rave auto-coding configurations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this MedDRA term record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer, typically triggered by a biannual MedDRA version release load. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11, and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  who_art_mapping varchar [note: 'The corresponding WHO Adverse Reaction Terminology (WHO-ART) preferred term code or name for this MedDRA term, where a legacy mapping exists. Supports legacy study migration and cross-dictionary reconciliation for studies coded under WHO-ART prior to MedDRA adoption.']

  Note: 'Reference master for the MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) terminology hierarchy, storing all active terms across all five levels: LLT (Lowest Level Term), PT (Preferred Term), HLT (High Level Term), HLGT (High Level Group Term), and SOC (System Organ Class). Captures term code, term name, level, primary SOC flag, MedDRA version, and active/retired status. Updated with each biannual MedDRA release.'
}

Table safety.review_committee {
  review_committee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the safety review committee record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Review committees in EU trials operate under the CTA framework. The CTA governs the committee\'s mandate and reporting obligations in EU member states. A clinical research expert expects review committ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Safety review committees (DSMBs) report to specific regulatory authorities per their charter. Linking to health_authority normalizes the regulatory_authority_notified field and enables governance trac']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: DSMBs/review committees are constituted under the IND protocol. The IND references the DSMB charter and governs the committee\'s regulatory reporting obligations. A clinical research expert expects rev']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_study. Business justification: DSMB/DMC review committees are established per study — the committee charter, membership, and meeting schedule are study-specific. Linking review_committee to study_study enables study-level committee']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the charter document as stored in Veeva Vault eTMF. Enables direct retrieval of the governing charter for audit and regulatory inspection purposes.']
  argus_committee_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for this committee as configured in Oracle Argus Safety for routing SAE/SUSAR reports and safety data packages to the committee. Enables traceability between safety case management and committee oversight.']
  charter_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the current version of the committee charter was formally approved by the sponsor or designated authority. Establishes the effective date of the committee\'s operating mandate.']
  charter_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current charter version expires and requires renewal or amendment. Null if the charter is open-ended and tied to study completion. Used for compliance tracking in MasterControl.']
  charter_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the committee\'s governing charter document as stored in Veeva Vault eTMF. The charter defines the committee\'s mandate, operating procedures, decision-making authority, and confidentiality obligations.']
  charter_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the currently approved committee charter document (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Tracks charter amendments over the committee lifecycle. Linked to the document in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  closed_session_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee charter mandates a closed/executive session restricted to independent committee members and the unblinded statistician, excluding sponsor representatives. Required for independent DMC/DSMB per FDA guidance.']
  committee_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric business identifier for the committee used in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Oracle Argus Safety for cross-system referencing and reporting (e.g., \'DMC-2024-001\', \'SRC-ONCOLOGY-01\').']
  committee_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the safety review committee as defined in its charter (e.g., \'Study XYZ Data Monitoring Committee\', \'Global Safety Review Committee\'). Used as the primary human-readable identifier across CTMS and safety systems.']
  committee_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the committee. \'active\' = operational and conducting reviews; \'inactive\' = temporarily not meeting; \'dissolved\' = formally disbanded at study close-out; \'suspended\' = operations halted pending investigation; \'pending_charter\' = established but charter not yet approved.']
  committee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the committee by its regulatory and operational role. SRC = internal Safety Review Committee; DSMB = Data Safety Monitoring Board; DMC = Data Monitoring Committee; IDMC = Independent Data Monitoring Committee; ISMC = Independent Safety Monitoring Committee. Drives applicable regulatory obligations and independence requirements.']
  confidentiality_agreement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all committee members are required to execute a confidentiality agreement prior to receiving unblinded study data. Mandatory for independent committees with unblinded access per GCP and sponsor SOPs.']
  conflict_of_interest_policy varchar [note: 'Identifies the conflict-of-interest (COI) policy framework governing committee member disclosures and recusal procedures. \'sponsor_policy\' = governed by sponsor\'s COI SOP; \'charter_defined\' = COI rules embedded in the committee charter; \'external_standard\' = governed by an external professional body standard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this committee record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  ctms_committee_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for this committee record in Oracle Siebel CTMS, used for meeting scheduling, site notification workflows, and cross-system reconciliation between the CTMS and the lakehouse.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the committee was formally dissolved, typically at study close-out or database lock (DBL). Null if the committee is still active. Required for TMF completeness at study archival.']
  establishment_date date [note: 'Date on which the committee was formally established and constituted by the sponsor. Marks the start of the committee\'s operational lifecycle and is recorded in the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  governing_regulation varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework or guideline under which the committee operates (e.g., \'21 CFR Part 312\', \'EU CTR 536/2014\', \'ICH E6(R2)\', \'ISO 14155\'). Determines reporting obligations and procedural requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 21_CFR_312|EU_CTR_536_2014|ICH_E6R2|ISO_14155|PMDA_GCP|TGA_GCP — promote to reference product]']
  independent_statistician_assigned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an independent (unblinded) statistician has been formally assigned to support the committee with unblinded data analysis. Required for DSMB/DMC operations per ICH E9(R1) and FDA guidance.']
  is_independent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee operates independently from the sponsor and investigators (True) or is an internal sponsor committee (False). Independence is a regulatory requirement for DMC/DSMB/IDMC roles per ICH E6(R2) and FDA guidance.']
  last_meeting_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed committee meeting. Used for monitoring meeting cadence compliance against the charter-defined frequency and for DSMB/DMC oversight tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  meeting_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the committee convenes for safety reviews as defined in the charter. \'per_protocol\' indicates frequency is defined by study protocol milestones (e.g., after every N subjects enrolled). Drives meeting scheduling in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  meeting_minutes_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal written minutes are required for each committee meeting per the charter. Minutes are a GCP requirement for DMC/DSMB meetings and must be archived in the eTMF.']
  next_meeting_date date [note: 'Date of the next scheduled committee meeting as planned in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for advance preparation of safety data packages and member notification workflows.']
  open_session_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee charter mandates an open session (attended by sponsor representatives) in addition to the closed/executive session (independent members only). Standard DMC practice per FDA guidance.']
  outstanding_recommendations_count varchar [note: 'Number of committee recommendations that have been issued but not yet formally resolved or closed by the sponsor. A non-zero value triggers escalation workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS and MasterControl CAPA tracking.']
  primary_therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The primary therapeutic area or disease indication for which the committee provides safety oversight (e.g., \'Oncology\', \'Cardiovascular\', \'Neurology\'). Used for committee expertise matching and portfolio-level safety analytics.']
  quorum_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum number of committee members required to be present for a meeting to be valid and for decisions to be binding, as specified in the charter. Enforced during meeting scheduling and minutes review.']
  regulatory_authority_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the relevant regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA) has been formally notified of the committee\'s establishment and composition, as required for certain study types and jurisdictions.']
  reporting_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the organizational body or individual to whom the committee formally reports its recommendations (e.g., \'Sponsor Medical Officer\', \'Executive Steering Committee\', \'IRB/IEC\'). Defines the escalation path for safety recommendations.']
  safety_data_package_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the unblinded safety data package is prepared and distributed to committee members prior to meetings. Governs data preparation schedules in SAS Drug Development and Oracle Argus Safety.']
  sponsor_liaison_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the sponsor\'s designated liaison or medical monitor who serves as the primary point of contact between the sponsor organization and the committee. This individual typically attends open sessions but is excluded from closed sessions.']
  statistical_charter_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee charter includes a statistical analysis plan (SAP) or statistical charter defining interim analysis rules, stopping boundaries (e.g., O\'Brien-Fleming, Lan-DeMets), and alpha-spending functions. Critical for DSMB/DMC oversight of adaptive trials.']
  stopping_rules_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the charter defines pre-specified stopping rules for efficacy, futility, or safety that the committee is empowered to invoke. Presence of stopping rules is a key regulatory expectation for DSMB/DMC charters.']
  tmf_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the TMF section or zone in Veeva Vault eTMF where committee-related documents (charter, member CVs, meeting minutes, recommendations) are filed. Aligns with the DIA Reference Model for TMF structure.']
  total_meetings_held varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of formal committee meetings held since the committee\'s establishment. Used for lifecycle tracking, regulatory inspection readiness, and TMF completeness verification.']
  unblinded_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the committee has access to unblinded (treatment-arm-identified) safety and efficacy data during its reviews. Unblinded access is standard for independent DMC/DSMB; internal SRCs may operate on blinded data. Governs data preparation workflows in SAS Drug Development.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this committee record in the lakehouse silver layer. Required for change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Master record for internal sponsor Safety Review Committees (SRCs) and external Data Monitoring Committees (DMCs) overseeing study safety. Captures committee name, type (internal SRC, DSMB, DMC, IDMC), charter version, meeting frequency, member roster, independence status, and associated studies. Distinct from the dsmb_review entity which captures individual meeting records.'
}

Table safety.communication {
  communication_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each formal safety communication record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']

Business justification: Urgent safety measures and safety communications frequently trigger blinding modifications or reference the current blinding configuration (e.g., requiring blind break, updating unblinding procedures)']

Business justification: Safety communications in EU trials (urgent safety measures) must be filed under the CTA. The CTA governs the notification requirements for urgent safety measures. Linking to health_authority normalizes the regulatory_authority_code field and enables tracking of safety communications by authority']

  parent_communication_safety_communication_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original (initial) safety communication record when this record is a follow-up communication. Null for initial communications. Enables reconstruction of the full communication chain for a given safety event.']

GCP requires PI acknowledgement tracking per communication. saf']

Business justification: Safety communications (urgent safety measures) frequently trigger protocol amendments (protocol_amendment_required flag on safety_communication).   reporting_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.reporting_obligation. The reporting_obligation t']
  signal_evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the safety signal evaluation record that triggered this communication, where applicable. Null if triggered by a SUSAR or DSMB/DMC recommendation.']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Safety communications (urgent safety measures, safety letters, investigator notifications) must be filed in site files per ICH E6 GCP. Linking safety_communication to site_file enables site-level fili']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this safety communication was issued. Links the communication to the relevant protocol and investigational product context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site (Principal Investigator site) that is the primary recipient of this safety communication, where the communication is site-specific. Null for communications addressed to health authorities or broad HCP audiences.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Urgent safety communications (Dear Investigator Letters, urgent safety measures) require regulatory submission. Linking safety_communication to its submission replaces the denormalized regulatory_subm']

Business justification: Safety communications (urgent safety measures, investigator notifications for SUSARs) are essential TMF documents per ICH E6(R2) Section 8. TMF managers must file each safety communication and link it']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Urgent safety measures and investigator safety communications are frequently arm-specific (e.g., dose reduction for active arm only). safety_communication has no FK to treatment_arm. Regulatory agenci']
  acknowledgement_received_date date [note: 'Date on which formal acknowledgement of receipt was received from the primary recipient. Null if acknowledgement has not yet been received or is not required. Used for compliance tracking and site monitoring follow-up.']
  acknowledgement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal written acknowledgement from the recipient (investigator, IRB/IEC, or health authority) is required for this safety communication. Drives acknowledgement tracking and overdue escalation workflows.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the acknowledgement tracking for this safety communication. PENDING: acknowledgement required but not yet received; RECEIVED: acknowledgement confirmed; OVERDUE: acknowledgement deadline passed without receipt; NOT_REQUIRED: no acknowledgement required for this communication type.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety communication was formally approved by the authorized signatory for issuance. Distinct from issue_date; approval may precede issuance by hours or days in multi-step workflows.']
  argus_communication_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned by Oracle Argus Safety to this safety communication record. Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory audit trails.']
  communication_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the safety communication record within the Oracle Argus Safety and Veeva Vault workflow. DRAFT: in preparation; PENDING_APPROVAL: awaiting sponsor/medical review sign-off; APPROVED: cleared for distribution; ISSUED: formally distributed to recipients; SUPERSEDED: replaced by a follow-up communication; WITHDRAWN: retracted prior to or after issuance.']
  communication_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formal safety communication. SUSAR_NOTIFICATION: expedited SUSAR report to investigators/IRBs; URGENT_SAFETY_MEASURE: immediate action to protect subjects; DHPC: Dear Healthcare Professional Communication; SAFETY_LETTER: general safety update letter; DSMB_RECOMMENDATION: communication of Data Safety Monitoring Board/Data Monitoring Committee recommendation; PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_ALERT: safety-relevant protocol deviation notice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SUSAR_NOTIFICATION|URGENT_SAFETY_MEASURE|DHPC|SAFETY_LETTER|DSMB_RECOMMENDATION|PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_ALERT — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety communication record was first created in Oracle Argus Safety or Veeva Vault. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements and audit trail compliance.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism used to distribute this safety communication to recipients. eCTD_PORTAL: submitted via electronic Common Technical Document gateway; EMAIL: direct email distribution; POSTAL: physical mail; FAX: facsimile; CTMS_PORTAL: distributed via Oracle Siebel CTMS investigator portal; HAND_DELIVERY: in-person delivery at site.']
  dsmb_dmc_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the DSMB/DMC was formally notified of the safety event. Null if dsmb_dmc_notified is False. Used for DSMB/DMC meeting preparation and safety oversight audit trails.']
  dsmb_dmc_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) was formally notified of the safety event underlying this communication. Supports DSMB/DMC oversight tracking.']
  follow_up_number varchar [note: 'Sequential follow-up number for this communication, where 0 or null indicates the initial communication and integers ≥1 indicate subsequent follow-up communications providing updated safety information on the same triggering event.']
  icf_update_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety communication requires an update to the Informed Consent Form (ICF) to reflect new safety information. When True, re-consenting of enrolled subjects may be required per IRB/IEC direction.']
  is_expedited_report boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety communication constitutes an expedited regulatory report (7-day or 15-day) as defined by ICH E2A and applicable regional regulations. True = expedited report required and submitted; False = routine or non-expedited communication.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the safety communication was formally issued and distributed to recipients. This is the principal real-world event date used for regulatory reporting clock calculations and compliance tracking.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term code for the primary adverse event or safety finding that is the subject of this communication. Enables standardized cross-study and cross-product safety signal analysis.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA dictionary used for coding the safety finding in this communication. Required for regulatory submissions to ensure consistent terminology versioning.']
  narrative varchar [note: 'Full narrative text body of the safety communication, describing the safety finding, its clinical significance, recommended actions for investigators, and any required subject management steps. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety case narrative or Veeva Vault document content.']
  protocol_amendment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety communication necessitates a formal protocol amendment. When True, a protocol amendment workflow must be initiated in Oracle Clinical One or Veeva Vault to update the study protocol.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Category of the primary recipient(s) of this safety communication. INVESTIGATOR: Principal Investigator at a clinical site; IRB_IEC: Institutional Review Board or Independent Ethics Committee; HEALTH_AUTHORITY: regulatory agency (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.); SPONSOR: pharmaceutical/biotech sponsor organization; DSMB_DMC: Data Safety Monitoring Board or Data Monitoring Committee; HEALTHCARE_PROFESSIONAL: broader HCP audience for DHPCs.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Specific action(s) recommended to the recipient as a result of this safety communication, such as enhanced subject monitoring, protocol amendment implementation, informed consent update, or study suspension. Derived from the safety assessment and sponsor medical review.']
  regulatory_deadline_date date [note: 'The calculated deadline date by which this safety communication must be submitted to the relevant health authority, based on the reporting clock start date and applicable regulatory timeline (7-day for fatal/life-threatening SUSAR; 15-day for other serious unexpected reactions).']
  reporting_clock_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory reporting clock was initiated for this safety communication, typically the sponsor\'s date of first awareness of the triggering safety event. Determines the 7-day or 15-day expedited reporting deadline.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this safety communication record was sourced for the Silver Layer. ARGUS_SAFETY: Oracle Argus Safety (primary pharmacovigilance system); VEEVA_VAULT: Veeva Vault eTMF/regulatory module; SIEBEL_CTMS: Oracle Siebel CTMS; MANUAL: manually entered record.']
  study_suspension_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety communication is associated with a full or partial suspension of the clinical study or specific study arms. When True, study suspension actions must be tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  subject_line varchar [note: 'The formal subject or title of the safety communication document as it appears in the issued letter or notification. Used for document identification, TMF filing, and recipient reference.']
  trigger_event_type varchar [note: 'The category of safety event or business process that initiated this communication. Distinguishes whether the communication was driven by a SUSAR, a detected safety signal, a DSMB/DMC review outcome, a health authority request, a protocol amendment, or a risk minimization measure.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this safety communication record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per ALCOA+ principles.']
  urgent_safety_measure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this communication constitutes or accompanies an Urgent Safety Measure (USM) as defined under EU CTR 536/2014, requiring immediate protective action for trial subjects prior to formal protocol amendment approval.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module) where the finalized safety communication document is archived. Enables direct retrieval from the electronic Trial Master File.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for formal safety communications issued to investigators, IRBs/IECs, and health authorities, including urgent safety measures, Dear Healthcare Professional Communications (DHPCs), and safety letters. Captures communication type, trigger event (SUSAR, signal, DSMB recommendation), recipient type, issue date, distribution method, and acknowledgement tracking. Sourced from Oracle Argus Safety and Veeva Vault.'
}

  coding_dictionary varchar [note: 'Standardized medical coding dictionary applied to verbatim terms collected on this form. MedDRA is used for adverse events and medical history; WHO-DD for drug names. Applicable when medical_coding_required is True.']

  edit_check_count varchar [note: 'Number of programmed edit checks (validation rules) associated with this eCRF form in the Medidata Rave study build. Indicates the complexity of data validation logic applied to the form.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this eCRF form version was retired or superseded by a newer version. Null for currently active forms. Supports audit trail and form version history.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this eCRF form version is effective and available for data collection in the deployed study. Supports form versioning and amendment tracking.']
  field_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data fields (eCRF fields) contained within this form definition. Used for form complexity assessment, UAT planning, and data management workload estimation.']
  form_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the eCRF form\'s purpose, the clinical data it captures, and any special instructions for data entry personnel (CRCs/site staff).']
  form_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the eCRF form as defined in Medidata Rave (e.g., \'Adverse Events\', \'Vital Signs\', \'Demographics\'). Used for display and navigation in the EDC system.']
  form_oid varchar [note: 'Unique object identifier (OID) for the eCRF form as defined in the ODM-compliant Medidata Rave study design. Used for CDISC ODM data exchange and SDTM mapping. Externally referenceable identifier.']
  form_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence position of this form within its assigned visit or folder in the Medidata Rave EDC navigation. Controls the order in which forms are presented to data entry personnel.']
  form_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eCRF form definition within the EDC system. Draft forms are under development; active forms are deployed for data collection; inactive/retired forms are no longer used.']
  form_type varchar [note: 'Categorization of the eCRF form by its structural and scheduling behavior in Medidata Rave. Visit-based forms appear at scheduled visits; log/repeating forms allow multiple instances per subject; unscheduled forms capture ad hoc data; common forms are shared across visits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visit_based|unscheduled|log|repeating|common|standalone — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  form_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the eCRF form design (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\', \'Draft\'). Tracks design iterations and amendments to the form across study lifecycle.']
  help_text varchar [note: 'Instructional text displayed to site staff when accessing this eCRF form in the EDC system. Provides guidance on data collection procedures, definitions, and completion requirements.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this eCRF form is mandatory for all subjects at the assigned visit(s). Mandatory forms must be completed or explicitly marked as not applicable before database lock.']
  is_repeating boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eCRF form supports multiple instances (log lines) per subject per visit, such as Adverse Events or Concomitant Medications forms. True for log/repeating forms in Medidata Rave.']
  is_subject_level boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this form is collected once per subject across the entire study (e.g., Demographics, Medical History) rather than at each visit. Drives form placement in the EDC navigation structure.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code for the language in which this eCRF form is presented to site staff (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'de\' for German). Supports multi-language global study deployments.']
  medical_coding_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data collected on this form requires medical coding using standardized dictionaries (MedDRA for adverse events/medical history, WHO-DD for concomitant medications). Drives coding workflow assignment.']
  primary_endpoint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eCRF form captures data for the study\'s primary efficacy or safety endpoint. Primary endpoint forms receive heightened data quality scrutiny, 100% SDV, and priority query resolution.']
  protocol_section_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section(s) of the study protocol that this eCRF form supports (e.g., \'Section 8.2 - Safety Assessments\'). Ensures traceability between protocol requirements and data collection instruments.']
  query_auto_generation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EDC system is configured to automatically generate data queries (discrepancies) for this form based on programmed edit checks and validation rules.']
  rave_form_reference varchar [note: 'Native form identifier as assigned by the Medidata Rave EDC source system. Used for cross-referencing with the operational EDC system and data lineage tracking from source to lakehouse.']
  requires_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eCRF form requires an electronic signature (e.g., Principal Investigator sign-off) upon completion. Governs 21 CFR Part 11 compliant e-signature workflows in Medidata Rave.']
  safety_form_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eCRF form is designated as a safety data collection instrument (e.g., Adverse Events, Serious Adverse Events, Concomitant Medications). Safety forms are subject to expedited review and regulatory reporting timelines.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code to which this eCRF form maps (e.g., \'AE\' for Adverse Events, \'VS\' for Vital Signs, \'DM\' for Demographics, \'LB\' for Laboratory). Drives SDTM transformation and regulatory submission dataset generation.']
  sdv_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of data points on this form subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) as defined in the monitoring plan. Supports risk-based monitoring strategies (e.g., 100%, 50%, 10%).']
  sdv_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) is required for data entered on this form. Critical forms (e.g., primary endpoints, safety data) typically require 100% SDV; others may be risk-based.']
  signature_role varchar [note: 'The role required to provide the electronic signature on this eCRF form (e.g., Principal Investigator). Applicable only when requires_signature is True.']
  sponsor_form_number varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned form number or code used in the paper CRF or sponsor\'s internal document management system. Enables cross-referencing between the EDC form and sponsor documentation (e.g., annotated CRF).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this eCRF form definition record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and ALCOA+ compliance.']

  Note: 'Master definition of Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) designs built in Medidata Rave EDC. Captures form name, version, study association, form type (visit-based, unscheduled, log/repeating), completion rules, CDISC SDTM domain mapping, data entry mode (single/double entry), and activation status. Each form contains one or more ecrf_fields and is deployed via an edc_study_build. Serves as the authoritative catalog of all eCRF instruments deployed across studies.'
}

Table data.ecrf_field {
  ecrf_field_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each eCRF field definition record in Medidata Rave. Primary key for the ecrf_field data product.']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent eCRF form to which this field belongs. Links the field to its containing form within the study design hierarchy in Medidata Rave.']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: IE eCRF fields directly capture eligibility criterion assessments. eligibility_criterion has edc_field_oid and sdtm_ie_testcd attributes confirming this mapping. Linking enables automated IE domain SD']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: Individual eCRF fields map to specific study endpoints (e.g., HAMD-17 total score field maps to primary efficacy endpoint). CRF annotation, SAP traceability, and define.xml generation all require fiel']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this eCRF field is defined. Enables study-level governance and version control of field definitions.']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: Each eCRF field belongs to a visit procedure (e.g., blood pressure field belongs to vital signs procedure). Procedure-level CRF annotation, SDV planning, and SDTM domain assignment all require this di']
  alcoa_attribute varchar [note: 'Primary ALCOA+ data integrity attribute that this field is designed to support (e.g., contemporaneous for date/time fields, attributable for signature fields, accurate for validated numeric fields). Supports ALCOA+ compliance tracking at the field level. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: attributable|legible|contemporaneous|original|accurate|complete|consistent|enduring|available — promote to reference product]']
  cdash_variable varchar [note: 'CDISC CDASH variable name that this eCRF field implements, representing the standardized data collection variable. Ensures harmonized data collection aligned with CDISC CDASH standards for cross-study comparability.']
  codelist_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the controlled terminology codelist bound to this field (e.g., NY, SEX, RACE, ETHNIC). Provides context for the permissible values without requiring a codelist lookup.']
  codelist_oid varchar [note: 'Object identifier (OID) of the controlled terminology codelist bound to this field in Medidata Rave. Applicable for codelist-type fields; references CDISC Controlled Terminology or sponsor-defined codelists governing permissible values.']
  coding_dictionary varchar [note: 'Medical coding dictionary applied to this field for verbatim-to-coded term mapping (e.g., MedDRA for adverse events, WHO-DD for concomitant medications, CTCAE for toxicity grading). Drives the medical coding workflow in the Data Management process.']
  coding_dictionary_version varchar [note: 'Version of the medical coding dictionary applied to this field (e.g., MedDRA 26.1, WHO-DD March 2024). Ensures reproducibility of coded terms across study lifecycle and regulatory submissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this eCRF field definition record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance tracking for field-level configuration history.']
  default_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Pre-populated default value for this field as configured in Medidata Rave. Reduces data entry burden for fields with a common expected value while remaining editable by site staff.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the eCRF field definition was superseded or retired. Null for currently active field definitions. Supports temporal versioning and audit trail for field-level changes.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the eCRF field definition is effective and active for data collection. Used to manage field versioning across protocol amendments and study lifecycle.']
  field_data_type varchar [note: 'Data type of the field as configured in Medidata Rave, determining the kind of value that can be entered. Drives validation rule applicability and CDISC SDTM variable type mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: text|integer|float|date|datetime|codelist|checkbox|file|log — promote to reference product]']
  field_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable label displayed to the data entry user on the eCRF in Medidata Rave. Represents the question or prompt as seen by the Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC) or site staff.']
  field_length varchar [note: 'Maximum character length allowed for text entry in this field. Constrains free-text input to prevent data truncation during SDTM transformation and downstream database storage.']
  field_name varchar [note: 'Programmatic name of the eCRF field as configured in Medidata Rave. Must be unique within the form and conform to naming conventions for EDC build and CDISC mapping.']
  field_oid varchar [note: 'Unique object identifier (OID) for the field as defined in Medidata Rave and conforming to ODM XML standards. Used for cross-system referencing and CDISC ODM data exchange.']
  field_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence position of the field within its parent eCRF form, determining the display order presented to data entry users. Used for form layout and navigation.']
  field_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the eCRF field definition. Controls whether the field is available for data entry in active studies. Draft fields are under design review; retired fields are no longer used in new study builds.']
  help_text varchar [note: 'Instructional or contextual help text displayed to data entry users for this field in Medidata Rave. Provides guidance on how to complete the field, acceptable formats, and clinical definitions to ensure data quality.']
  is_audit_trail_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether field-level audit trail tracking is enabled for this eCRF field, capturing all data entry, modification, and deletion events with user, timestamp, and reason for change. Mandatory for ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  is_key_field boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field is designated as a key field in Medidata Rave, used for record identification and deduplication within a log or repeating form. Key fields are critical for data integrity in multi-record forms.']
  is_log_field boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field belongs to a log (repeating) form in Medidata Rave, allowing multiple rows of data entry per subject visit (e.g., concomitant medications log, adverse events log).']
  is_read_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field is read-only and cannot be directly edited by site users. Read-only fields are typically auto-populated by the system or derived from other data points.']
  is_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether data entry for this field is mandatory before the eCRF can be submitted. Required fields enforce ALCOA+ completeness compliance and are enforced by Medidata Rave validation.']
  is_sdv_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field is designated for Source Data Verification (SDV) by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) during monitoring visits. Drives risk-based monitoring plans and SDV scope configuration.']
  is_sponsor_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field is a sponsor-defined custom field beyond the standard CDISC CDASH variables. Sponsor-defined fields require additional SDTM mapping documentation for regulatory submissions.']
  is_visible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the field is currently visible to data entry users on the eCRF. Invisible fields may be conditionally hidden by edit check logic or used for internal system calculations.']
  max_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum permissible numeric value for this field as defined in the validation rules. Applicable to numeric and date fields; values above this threshold trigger a data query in Medidata Rave.']
  min_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum permissible numeric value for this field as defined in the validation rules. Applicable to numeric and date fields; values below this threshold trigger a data query in Medidata Rave.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or user identifier of the individual who last modified this eCRF field definition in Medidata Rave. Supports ALCOA+ attributability and change control audit trail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this eCRF field definition record. Tracks configuration changes for audit trail purposes and supports ALCOA+ compliance.']
  query_generation_mode varchar [note: 'Specifies how data queries (discrepancies) are generated for this field in Medidata Rave: automatic (system-generated by edit checks), manual (raised by data managers or monitors), or none (no query generation configured).']
  rave_field_type varchar [note: 'Medidata Rave-specific UI control type for this field, determining the data entry widget rendered on the eCRF (e.g., text box, radio button, dropdown list, checkbox). Distinct from the logical data type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: text|longtext|integer|float|date|datetime|checkbox|radiobutton|dropdownlist|file|signature|label — promote to reference product]']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code (e.g., DM, AE, LB, VS, CM) to which this eCRF field maps. Drives automated CDISC SDTM transformation in SAS Drug Development and regulatory submission dataset generation.']
  sdtm_variable varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM variable name (e.g., AEDECOD, LBSTRESN, VSORRES) to which this eCRF field is mapped. Enables automated CDISC SDTM dataset generation for regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA).']
  significant_digits varchar [note: 'Number of significant digits (decimal places) permitted for numeric fields. Ensures precision alignment with laboratory reporting standards and SDTM numeric variable specifications.']
  source_system_field_reference varchar [note: 'Native field identifier as assigned by Medidata Rave EDC. Enables traceability between the Silver Layer data product and the originating EDC system record for reconciliation and lineage purposes.']
  unit_dictionary varchar [note: 'Name of the unit dictionary or unit codelist bound to this field in Medidata Rave (e.g., SI units, lab units). Governs permissible units of measure for numeric fields and supports SDTM --ORRESU and --STRESU variable mapping.']
  validation_rule_expression varchar [note: 'Edit check or validation rule expression applied to this field in Medidata Rave (e.g., range checks, cross-field consistency checks, date logic). Defines the programmatic logic that triggers data queries when violated.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this eCRF field definition, incremented when the field configuration is modified after study go-live. Supports amendment tracking and ensures traceability of field-level changes per protocol amendment.']

  Note: 'Master definition of individual data fields within eCRF forms in Medidata Rave. Captures field name, label, data type (text, numeric, date, codelist), CDISC variable mapping (SDTM variable, domain), validation rules, required flag, controlled terminology binding, and field-level audit configuration. Supports ALCOA+ compliance at the field level.'
}

This join is required']
