True = fully compliant; False = one or more ALCOA+ criteria not met.']
  cdisc_sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain code to which this eCRF form maps (e.g., \'DM\' for Demographics, \'VS\' for Vital Signs, \'AE\' for Adverse Events, \'LB\' for Laboratory). Used to drive SDTM transformation and regulatory submission packaging in SAS Drug Development.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the eCRF form in Medidata Rave. Reflects the data entry and review workflow: incomplete (data entry in progress), complete (all required fields entered), frozen (data frozen pending review), locked (data locked post-database lock), signed (investigator signature applied). This is the primary lifecycle status for this transactional entity.']
  data_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of required eCRF fields that have been populated for this subject visit form, calculated as (completed fields / total required fields) × 100. Key metric for data quality monitoring and site performance KPIs. Range: 0.00 to 100.00.']
  data_entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when data was first entered into this eCRF form in Medidata Rave. Represents the ALCOA+ \'Contemporaneous\' requirement — data must be recorded at the time of the observation. This is the record creation audit timestamp.']
  data_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the medical/data management review of this subject visit form. Tracks the review workflow from initial data entry through medical monitor approval. Values: not_started, in_review, reviewed, approved, requires_correction.']
  edc_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by Medidata Rave EDC to this subject visit form instance. Used for traceability back to the source EDC system and for audit trail reconciliation.']
  entered_by varchar [note: 'The username of the Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC) or site staff member who initially entered data into this eCRF form in Medidata Rave. Satisfies ALCOA+ \'Attributable\' requirement — data must be traceable to the person who recorded it.']
  freeze_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this subject visit form has been frozen in Medidata Rave (True = frozen; False = not frozen). Freezing is a pre-lock step that prevents further data entry while allowing review. Distinct from lock_status.']
  freeze_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this subject visit form was frozen in Medidata Rave. Null if the form has not been frozen. Supports the pre-database lock workflow audit trail.']
  investigator_signature_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Principal Investigator (PI) or sub-investigator electronic signature on this eCRF form. Required for regulatory compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Values: not_required, pending, signed, rejected.']
  investigator_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the Principal Investigator (PI) or sub-investigator applied their electronic signature to this eCRF form. Null if not yet signed. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The username of the user who most recently modified this eCRF form record in Medidata Rave. Supports ALCOA+ \'Attributable\' requirement for all data changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this eCRF form record was most recently modified in Medidata Rave. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and change tracking for data management workflows.']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the database lock state of this subject visit form. Unlocked = data entry and editing permitted; locked = form locked as part of the database lock (DBL) workflow; hard_locked = irreversibly locked post-database lock for regulatory submission.']
  lock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this subject visit form was locked in Medidata Rave as part of the database lock (DBL) workflow. Null if the form has not been locked. Critical for regulatory audit trail.']
  locked_by varchar [note: 'The username of the data management user who applied the lock to this subject visit form in Medidata Rave. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records audit trail.']
  medical_coding_status varchar [note: 'Status of medical coding for verbatim terms on this eCRF form using MedDRA (adverse events, medical history) or WHO Drug Dictionary (concomitant medications). Values: not_applicable (no codeable terms), pending, coded, approved, rejected.']
  missing_data_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more required data fields are missing or unanswered on this eCRF form for the subject visit. True = missing data present; False = all required fields populated. Supports ALCOA+ compliance tracking.']
  missing_field_count varchar [note: 'The number of required eCRF fields that are currently unanswered or missing for this subject visit form. Provides granular data completeness tracking beyond the binary missing_data_flag.']
  open_query_count varchar [note: 'The number of currently open data queries (discrepancies) raised against this subject visit form in Medidata Rave. Used to track data cleaning progress and site responsiveness.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation has been identified in association with this subject visit data record (True = deviation present; False = no deviation). Supports compliance monitoring and regulatory reporting.']
  rave_page_repeat_key varchar [note: 'The repeat sequence key for repeating forms (log pages) in Medidata Rave, used when a subject has multiple instances of the same form within a single visit (e.g., multiple adverse event entries). Value is 1 for non-repeating forms.']
  sdv_date date [note: 'The date on which source data verification (SDV) was completed for this subject visit form by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA). Null if SDV has not been performed.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Current status of source data verification (SDV) for this subject visit form. Reflects whether the CRA has verified the eCRF data against source documents at the site. Values: not_required (SDV not mandated per monitoring plan), pending (SDV not yet performed), partial (some fields verified), verified (all required fields SDV-confirmed), overdue (SDV past due per monitoring plan).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this subject visit data was captured. Primarily Medidata Rave for EDC-based studies; may be Oracle Clinical One or paper CRF for legacy or hybrid studies.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the subject within the study (e.g., site-subject number such as \'001-0042\'). Used in eCRF, regulatory submissions, and CDISC SDTM USUBJID derivation. Directly identifies a trial participant.']
  total_query_count varchar [note: 'The total number of data queries (discrepancies) ever raised against this subject visit form, including open, answered, and closed queries. Supports data quality trend analysis.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the subject attended the study visit and clinical data was collected. Represents the principal real-world event date. Maps to SVSTDTC in CDISC SDTM.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'The protocol-defined name of the study visit as configured in Medidata Rave (e.g., \'Screening\', \'Baseline\', \'Week 4\', \'End of Treatment\'). Maps to VISITDESC in CDISC SDTM.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'The sequential numeric identifier of the study visit as defined in the protocol schedule of assessments. Maps to VISITNUM in CDISC SDTM. Used for ordering and analysis.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the visit as defined in the protocol (e.g., scheduled per protocol, unscheduled/ad hoc, early termination, follow-up, telephone contact). Drives protocol deviation detection and data completeness expectations.']
  visit_window_end_date date [note: 'The latest acceptable calendar date for this protocol-defined visit per the study schedule of assessments. Used to assess whether the visit occurred within the protocol-specified window.']
  visit_window_start_date date [note: 'The earliest acceptable calendar date for this protocol-defined visit per the study schedule of assessments. Used to assess whether the visit occurred within the protocol-specified window.']
  visit_within_window boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual visit date falls within the protocol-defined visit window (visit_window_start_date to visit_window_end_date). True = within window; False = outside window (potential protocol deviation).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of all eCRF data collected for a subject at a specific study visit in Medidata Rave. Captures subject identifier, visit name/number, visit date, form completion status (incomplete, complete, frozen, locked), data completeness percentage, missing data flags, data entry timestamp, entered-by user, source data verification (SDV) status, and lock status. Represents the primary unit of clinical data collection linking subjects to their visit-level form submissions. Completion status subsumes missing data tracking previously handled separately.'
}

  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) form definition that contains this field. Identifies the form context (e.g., Adverse Event form, Vital Signs form) within Medidata Rave.']
  irt_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.irt_transaction. This is distinct from the existing field_entry']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. This link is a core GCP requirement enabling monitors to verify ']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Specific field entry values (e.g., out-of-window visit, prohibited medication) are the source evidence for protocol deviations.   study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this field entry was captured. Links the field entry to its parent study context for filtering, reporting, and regulatory submission.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which this field entry was recorded. Supports site-level data quality monitoring and CRA (Clinical Research Associate) review workflows.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Adding s']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which this field entry was collected. Provides temporal context for the data point within the subject\'s study timeline.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) whose data this field entry represents. Enables subject-level audit trail and source data verification (SDV) traceability.']
  validation_rule_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the edit check or validation rule in Medidata Rave that was triggered by this field entry. Supports data validation rule management, discrepancy tracking, and DMP (Data Management Plan) compliance reporting.']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: Each field entry corresponds to a specific visit procedure (lab draw, vital sign measurement). SDV tracking at the procedure level is a core GCP requirement. Procedure-level data entry completeness an']
  alcoa_accurate boolean [note: 'ALCOA+ compliance flag indicating whether the field entry value has been verified as accurate against the source document or original observation. Set to true upon successful SDV or data review confirmation.']
  alcoa_attributable boolean [note: 'ALCOA+ compliance flag indicating whether the field entry is attributable to a specific, identified individual (i.e., the entered_by_user is known and authenticated). One of the core ALCOA+ data integrity principles required by FDA and ICH.']
  alcoa_contemporaneous boolean [note: 'ALCOA+ compliance flag indicating whether the field entry was recorded at the time the observation was made (i.e., entry_timestamp is within an acceptable window of the actual observation date). Supports data integrity and GCP compliance monitoring.']
  alcoa_legible boolean [note: 'ALCOA+ compliance flag indicating whether the field entry value is legible and readable. Particularly relevant for transcribed or scanned source data. Supports data integrity assessments during regulatory inspections.']
  alcoa_original boolean [note: 'ALCOA+ compliance flag indicating whether this field entry represents the original (first) recorded value, as opposed to a transcription or copy. Distinguishes primary source data from derived or transferred data.']
  audit_trail_ref varchar [note: 'The unique reference identifier linking this field entry to its corresponding audit trail record in Medidata Rave. Enables direct cross-referencing with the EDC system\'s immutable audit log for regulatory inspection and SDV activities.']
  coded_term varchar [note: 'The standardized dictionary term assigned to this field entry after medical coding (e.g., MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) for adverse events, WHO-DD drug name for medications). Null for fields not requiring coding.']
  coding_dictionary varchar [note: 'The standardized medical coding dictionary used to code this field entry (e.g., MedDRA for adverse events and medical history, WHO-DD for concomitant medications). Null or \'not_applicable\' for fields not requiring coding.']
  data_entry_type varchar [note: 'Classifies how the field value was populated in the EDC system. Distinguishes between manual site entry, system auto-population (e.g., calculated fields), data migration, or external data import (e.g., lab data transfer). Supports ALCOA+ \'Original\' assessment.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the field entry within the EDC system lifecycle. Drives data review, query management, and database lock (DBL) readiness assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entered|modified|deleted|frozen|locked|sdv_complete — promote to reference product]']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (with timezone) at which the field value was originally entered into the EDC system by the site user. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous data entry verification and audit trail analysis.']
  entry_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual data value entered into the eCRF field by the site user. Stored as STRING to accommodate all field types (numeric, text, date, coded). This is the primary clinical data payload and is classified as Protected Health Information (PHI) under HIPAA.']
  field_data_type varchar [note: 'The data type of the eCRF field as defined in the study build (e.g., text, numeric, date, coded list). Governs validation rules applied to the entry_value and supports downstream SDTM transformation logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: text|numeric|date|datetime|coded|checkbox|file — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  has_open_query boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field entry currently has at least one open data query (discrepancy) requiring resolution. Supports real-time data cleaning dashboards and DBL readiness tracking.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field entry has been frozen, preventing further modification by site users. Freezing is a pre-lock step in the database lock (DBL) workflow to protect data integrity prior to statistical analysis.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field entry has been locked as part of the Database Lock (DBL) process. Locked fields are immutable and represent the final, analysis-ready dataset. Supports ALCOA+ \'Enduring\' and \'Available\' principles.']
  is_missing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this field entry represents a missing data point (i.e., the field was expected but no value was entered or a missing reason code was provided). Supports data completeness reporting and ALCOA+ compliance tracking.']
  locked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this field entry was locked as part of the Database Lock (DBL) process. Null if the field has not yet been locked. Provides a precise audit record of the lock event for regulatory submissions.']
  medical_coding_status varchar [note: 'The medical coding status for fields requiring standardized coding (e.g., adverse event terms coded to MedDRA, concomitant medications coded to WHO-DD). Tracks whether the verbatim term has been coded to a standard dictionary term.']
  missing_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code explaining why a field value is missing (e.g., NOT_DONE, UNKNOWN, NA). Aligns with CDISC CDASH missing data handling conventions and supports statistical analysis plan (SAP) imputation strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NOT_DONE|UNKNOWN|NA|NOT_APPLICABLE|TECHNICAL_FAILURE|SUBJECT_REFUSED — promote to reference product]']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or system identifier of the EDC user who last modified this field entry. Null if the field has never been modified. Required for complete audit trail attribution under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time of the most recent modification to this field entry. Null if the field has never been modified after initial entry. Part of the complete audit trail required under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  previous_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the field prior to the most recent modification. Captured as part of the ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) audit trail to support source data verification (SDV) and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  rave_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by Medidata Rave EDC to the parent data record (row) containing this field entry. Used for cross-referencing with the source EDC system and audit trail reconciliation.']
  reason_for_change varchar [note: 'The free-text justification provided by the site user when modifying a previously entered field value. Mandatory for all post-entry modifications per GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements. Supports ALCOA+ \'Accurate\' principle.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain (two-letter code) to which this field belongs (e.g., AE for Adverse Events, VS for Vital Signs, LB for Laboratory). Supports SDTM dataset construction and regulatory submission packaging.']
  sdtm_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM variable name to which this field maps (e.g., AESTDTC, VSORRES, LBSTRESC). Enables automated SDTM transformation and regulatory submission dataset generation via SAS Drug Development.']
  sdv_by_user varchar [note: 'The username of the CRA or authorized reviewer who performed Source Data Verification (SDV) on this field entry. Satisfies ALCOA+ \'Attributable\' requirement for the verification action.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'The current Source Data Verification (SDV) status of this field entry. Indicates whether a CRA has verified the entered value against the original source document (e.g., medical record). Critical for GCP compliance and monitoring visit reporting.']
  sdv_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the CRA completed Source Data Verification (SDV) for this field entry. Null if SDV has not been performed. Supports monitoring visit close-out and audit trail completeness.']
  validation_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the edit check or validation rule applied to this field entry at the time of data entry. A \'fail\' result triggers a data query. Supports data quality monitoring and DMP compliance.']

  Note: 'Atomic-level transactional record capturing a single field-level data entry in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Stores the field value, entry timestamp, entered-by user, reason for change, previous value, audit trail reference, and ALCOA+ compliance flags (attributable, legible, contemporaneous, original, accurate). Supports full audit trail and source data verification (SDV) requirements.'
}

Table data.discrepancy_query {
  discrepancy_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each discrepancy query record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the discrepancy_query data product.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_deviation. Business justification: Discrepancy queries raised during data review frequently identify or are associated with protocol deviations. Data managers and monitors need to link queries to deviations for CAPA tracking, query clo']
  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record. Business justification: Data queries raised against dispensing eCRF fields must be traceable to the RTSM dispensing record to enable query resolution and IMP accountability reconciliation. CRAs and data managers use this lin']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Query management is governed by a named query management SOP (GCP requirement). CDMs and data managers must follow the specific SOP for raising, responding to, and closing queries. Regulatory inspecto']
  ecrf_field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: discrepancy_query is raised against a specific eCRF field. ecrf_field is the master field definition. Adding ecrf_field_id normalizes the relationship and allows removal of the denormalized ecrf_field']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: discrepancy_query is raised against data in a specific eCRF form. ecrf_form is the master form definition. Adding ecrf_form_id normalizes the relationship and allows removal of the denormalized ecrf_f']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: Queries on primary endpoint data fields are flagged as critical and prioritized. Endpoint-level query rate reporting is a standard data quality KPI for sponsor oversight. Linking queries to endpoints ']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigative site where the subject data entry originated, enabling site-level query metrics and monitoring.']
  irt_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.irt_transaction. Business justification: IRT reconciliation queries — a named clinical data management activity — require discrepancy queries to link directly to the IRT transaction in dispute (e.g., dispensing date mismatch, wrong kit recor']
  kit_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.kit_inventory. Business justification: Queries raised about kit numbers, expiry dates, or dispensing quantities in the EDC must link to the specific kit inventory record. This supports IMP accountability audit trails and enables supply tea']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: EDC discrepancy queries raised against lab values (out-of-range, missing results, transcription errors) must reference the source lab result for data managers to compare EDC-entered value against the ']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Queries are raised on shipment-related eCRF data (receipt date, kit condition on arrival, temperature excursion acknowledgement). CRAs and data managers link these queries directly to the shipment rec']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) against which this discrepancy query was raised. Links the query to its parent study context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: Query management reports and site performance dashboards are always study-site specific — a site may participate in multiple studies with different query profiles. discrepancy_query already has invest']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Discrepancy queries raised against randomization visit data (wrong stratification factor, kit number mismatch, eligibility discrepancy) must link directly to the subject_randomization record. CRAs and']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: A discrepancy query is raised against eCRF data collected at a specific subject visit. While discrepancy_query already references ecrf_form_id, ecrf_field_id, and trial_subject_id, it lacks a direct F']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose eCRF data entry triggered this discrepancy query. Central to subject-level data cleaning workflows.']
  validation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.validation_rule. Business justification: discrepancy_query has edit_check_name indicating the validation rule (edit check) that triggered the query. validation_rule is the master definition of programmatic edit checks. Adding validation_rule']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: Queries are raised against data collected during specific visit procedures. Procedure-level query rate analysis is a standard data quality KPI in clinical operations. Linking queries to procedures ena']
  assigned_to_user varchar [note: 'Username of the site user (CRC or PI) to whom the query is currently assigned for response. Drives site-facing query worklist in Medidata Rave.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the discrepancy query was cancelled (e.g., raised in error, duplicate query, data corrected via another mechanism). Required for audit trail integrity.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the discrepancy query was cancelled (withdrawn) in Medidata Rave. Populated only when query_status = cancelled.']
  closed_by_user varchar [note: 'Username of the DM or reviewer who closed the discrepancy query after verifying the site response and confirming data correction in Medidata Rave.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the discrepancy query was formally closed (resolved and accepted) by the data management team. Used to measure total query lifecycle duration and DBL readiness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discrepancy query record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer data product. Audit trail field for data lineage and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  data_value_corrected varchar [note: 'The corrected data value entered in the eCRF field after query resolution. Populated when the site updates the data entry in response to the query. Supports data change audit trail.']
  data_value_original varchar [note: 'The original data value entered in the eCRF field at the time the query was raised. Preserved for audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance to document the pre-correction state.']
  days_open varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the query has been in open or answered status since raised_timestamp. Operational metric used for site performance monitoring and query aging reports. Recalculated daily.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the query was escalated. Populated only when is_escalated = true. Used to track escalation response times and site performance.']
  is_dbl_blocking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this open query is blocking the Database Lock (DBL) process. DBL-blocking queries must be resolved before the study database can be locked for statistical analysis.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discrepancy query has been escalated due to non-response, repeated failure to resolve, or critical data impact. Triggers escalation workflow in CTMS and monitoring processes.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discrepancy query relates to safety data (e.g., Adverse Events, SAEs, concomitant medications). Safety-related queries require expedited resolution per GCP and regulatory requirements.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the discrepancy query, indicating urgency of resolution. Critical queries may relate to safety data, primary endpoints, or DBL-blocking issues.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the study protocol in effect at the time the query was raised. Relevant for queries related to protocol deviations or version-specific data collection requirements.']
  query_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the data issue driving the query (e.g., missing data, out-of-range value, inconsistency, protocol deviation, transcription error, coding discrepancy). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missing_data|out_of_range|inconsistency|protocol_deviation|transcription_error|coding_discrepancy|illegible|other — promote to reference product]']
  query_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for the discrepancy query as assigned by Medidata Rave EDC (e.g., QRY-0001234). Used in site communications, monitoring reports, and audit trails.']
  query_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the discrepancy query within the data cleaning workflow. Open = awaiting site response; Answered = site has responded, pending review; Closed = resolved and accepted; Cancelled = query withdrawn.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'Full text of the discrepancy query message as displayed to the site. Describes the data issue and requests specific corrective action or clarification from the investigator.']
  query_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the query origin: manual = CRA/DM-raised; system = auto-generated by Medidata Rave edit check; review = raised during medical or data review; reconciliation = raised from external data source comparison (e.g., lab, SAE reconciliation).']
  raised_by_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the person or system that raised the query (e.g., CRA - Clinical Research Associate, DM - Data Manager, medical reviewer, system for automated edit checks). Supports workload and quality analytics by role.']
  raised_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the discrepancy query was first raised in Medidata Rave EDC. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Critical for ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation compliance.']
  rave_query_reference varchar [note: 'Native query identifier from Medidata Rave EDC source system. Enables traceability back to the originating system record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  reopen_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this query has been re-opened after being answered or closed, indicating iterative resolution cycles. High reopen counts signal site data quality issues or unclear query text.']
  resolution_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between raised_timestamp and closed_timestamp. Measures end-to-end query resolution efficiency; key data management KPI for study performance dashboards.']
  response_by_user varchar [note: 'Username of the site staff member (CRC or PI) who submitted the query response in Medidata Rave. Required for ALCOA+ attributability.']
  response_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days between raised_timestamp and response_timestamp. Measures site responsiveness to data queries; used in site performance KPI reporting.']
  response_text varchar [note: 'Site\'s textual response to the discrepancy query, entered by the CRC or PI in Medidata Rave. Captures the explanation, correction, or clarification provided by the site.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the site submitted their response to the discrepancy query in Medidata Rave. Used to calculate query response cycle time KPIs.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this discrepancy query originated. Primarily Medidata Rave EDC; may include Oracle Clinical One for studies using that platform.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this discrepancy query record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer data product. Supports incremental data processing and change detection.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'Name of the study visit (e.g., Screening, Week 4, End of Treatment) associated with the eCRF data entry that triggered the query. Supports visit-level data quality reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of ALL data queries (discrepancies) raised against eCRF data entries in Medidata Rave, serving as the single source of truth for data quality issues regardless of origin. Captures query text, query type (manual CRA-raised, system-generated from edit checks, review-originated from medical/data review, reconciliation-originated from external data comparison), raised-by user, raised date, assigned-to site/user, response text, resolution status (open, answered, closed, cancelled), resolution date, query category, and escalation flag. Central to the data cleaning and discrepancy resolution workflow; all data quality findings from any source are consolidated here.'
}

Business justification: Validation rules are defined at the procedure level (e.g., lab result range checks for a specific lab procedure).   activation_date date [note: 'The date on which this validation rule was activated (deployed to production) in Medidata Rave EDC. Marks the start of the rule\'s operational period. Used for change control documentation and audit trail.']

  cdisc_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM or CDASH variable name that this validation rule maps to (e.g., AESTDTC, VSVAL, CMTRT). Enables traceability from the edit check to the CDISC submission variable for regulatory compliance and SDTM transformation validation.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Documented business or regulatory justification for the most recent change to this validation rule (e.g., protocol amendment, data quality finding, regulatory feedback). Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and change control compliance.']
  country_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes (e.g., USA,GBR,DEU) indicating the countries where this validation rule applies. Null if the rule applies globally. Supports country-specific regulatory compliance (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, Health Canada).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this validation rule record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'The date on which this validation rule was deactivated or retired in Medidata Rave EDC. Null if the rule is currently active. Required for version history tracking and regulatory audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this version of the validation rule is considered effective for data collection purposes. May differ from activation_date if the rule was pre-configured before deployment. Supports temporal validity tracking.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this version of the validation rule is effective. Null for open-ended rules. Enables temporal validity management and supports retroactive data impact analysis when rules are superseded.']
  generates_query boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this validation rule automatically generates a data query (discrepancy) in Medidata Rave when triggered (True) or only performs a silent validation without raising a query (False). Drives query management workflow volume.']
  is_cdisc_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this validation rule has been verified as compliant with CDISC CDASH/SDTM data standards. True = rule aligns with CDISC requirements; False = rule is study-specific or non-standard. Used in regulatory submission readiness assessments.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this validation rule is mandatory per the study protocol or regulatory requirement (True) or optional/advisory (False). Mandatory rules cannot be waived without formal change control and regulatory justification.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the person who last modified this validation rule record. Required for ALCOA+ attributability and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this validation rule record was last updated. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail for change history tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, implementation guidance, known limitations, or contextual information about this validation rule. Supports Data Management team knowledge management and DMP documentation.']
  previous_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the immediately preceding version of this validation rule, enabling version chain traceability. Supports audit trail reconstruction and change impact analysis per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'The standardized query message text that is automatically generated and displayed to the site user (CRC/PI) in Medidata Rave when this validation rule fires. Instructs the site on the data discrepancy and required corrective action.']
  rave_edit_check_reference varchar [note: 'The native edit check identifier as assigned by Medidata Rave EDC (source system key). Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for reconciliation, UAT, and system integration purposes.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the validation rule by its logical type: range_check (numeric bounds), cross_form (inter-form consistency), date_logic (temporal relationships), required_field (mandatory data presence), consistency_check (intra-form logic), coding_check (MedDRA/WHO-DD coding validation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: range_check|cross_form|date_logic|required_field|consistency_check|coding_check|derived_field|format_check — promote to reference product]']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the validation rule within the EDC system (e.g., EC_AESTDT_001). Used as the business identifier for cross-referencing in DMP, SAP, and audit documentation.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the validation rule\'s business purpose, the data quality condition it enforces, and the expected corrective action when triggered. Supports DMP documentation and regulatory audit readiness.']
  rule_expression varchar [note: 'The programmatic logic or expression defining the edit check condition as coded in Medidata Rave (e.g., OpenRave expression syntax or Rave Edit Check language). Captures the exact computational rule used to evaluate data validity.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or short title of the validation rule as configured in Medidata Rave EDC (e.g., \'AE Start Date Cannot Be After End Date\'). Used for display in query management and DMP documentation.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the validation rule in Medidata Rave EDC: draft (in development, not yet deployed), active (deployed and executing), inactive (temporarily disabled), retired (permanently decommissioned), under_review (pending change control review). Governs whether the rule fires during data entry.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the technical implementation type of the validation rule in Medidata Rave: edit_check (standard Rave edit check), derivation (computed/derived field rule), custom_function (OpenRave custom function), system_check (system-level validation). Determines the execution engine and maintenance approach.']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the validation rule following semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0). Tracks the change history of the rule expression, severity, or configuration. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and change control compliance.']
  scope varchar [note: 'The level at which the validation rule operates within the Medidata Rave data hierarchy: field (single data point), form (entire eCRF page), folder (visit/event folder), study (cross-visit/global). Determines the breadth of data evaluated by the rule.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the validation rule determining how the EDC system responds when the rule fires: error (blocks data entry/save), warning (alerts user but allows save), informational (advisory only). Governs automated query generation behavior in Medidata Rave.']
  trigger_condition varchar [note: 'The EDC system event that causes this validation rule to execute: on_save (when the eCRF page is saved), on_entry (during data entry), on_change (when a field value changes), on_activation (when a form is activated), on_signature (at electronic signature), batch (scheduled batch execution). Determines when automated queries are raised.']
  uat_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which UAT was completed for this validation rule. Required for computer system validation documentation per 21 CFR Part 11 and FDA/EMA GCP expectations prior to production deployment.']
  uat_status varchar [note: 'Current User Acceptance Testing (UAT) status of this validation rule: not_started, in_progress, passed, failed, waived. UAT must be completed before a rule can be activated in production per GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 validation requirements.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the Data Manager who originally authored and configured this validation rule in Medidata Rave EDC. Supports ALCOA+ attributability requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']

  Note: 'Master definition of programmatic data validation rules (edit checks) configured in Medidata Rave EDC. Captures rule name, rule expression/logic, trigger condition, associated form and field, severity level (error, warning), rule category (range check, cross-form, date logic, required field), CDISC compliance flag, activation status, and version history. Governs automated query generation.'
}

  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment.   study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this Data Management Plan governs all data management activities.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Regulatory document control and TMF completeness: the Data Management Plan is a controlled document versioned with protocol amendments. Inspectors require traceability between DMP versions and the pro']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_plan. Business justification: The Data Management Plan must align data collection strategy with the supply plan — blinding type, kit types, and dispensing visit windows defined in the supply plan directly drive DMP authoring decis']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The DMP is a TMF essential document (DIA TMF Zone 2). Regulators verify its eTMF filing during inspections. This FK replaces the denormalized tmf_document_reference plain-text column, linking the DMP ']
  adam_ig_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide used for this study. Governs the structure of analysis datasets produced from SDTM data for biostatistical reporting.']
  alcoa_compliance_approach varchar [note: 'Description of the approach and controls implemented to ensure ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate, plus Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available) data integrity compliance as defined in the DMP.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for the creation of a new version of the DMP. Null for the initial version (1.0). Documents the business or regulatory driver for the amendment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the DMP received final approval signature. Marks the transition from draft to approved status.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle approval state of the Data Management Plan. Controls whether the DMP is operationally active and governs data management activities for the study.']
  autocoding_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated medical coding (auto-coding) is enabled for this study in the coding platform. When true, verbatim terms are automatically matched to dictionary terms without manual intervention.']
  coding_platform varchar [note: 'Software platform used to perform MedDRA and WHO-DD medical coding for this study as specified in the DMP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DMP record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance requirements.']
  critical_data_fields_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMP includes a defined list of critical data fields (primary and key secondary endpoints) subject to enhanced data review and 100% SDV requirements.']
  data_collection_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary strategy for clinical data collection as defined in the DMP. Specifies whether data is collected via eCRF only, eCRF supplemented by external data transfers, paper CRF, or a hybrid approach.']
  data_privacy_framework varchar [note: 'Primary data privacy regulatory framework applicable to subject data collected in this study, as identified in the DMP. Determines pseudonymization, data transfer, and retention requirements.']
  data_retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years that clinical study data must be retained after study completion or database lock, as mandated by applicable regulations and specified in the DMP.']
  data_review_plan_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the data review plan as defined in the DMP, describing the approach for central data review, medical review, and programmatic data checks prior to database lock.']
  dbl_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the database was locked (DBL) for this study. Null until lock is achieved. Compared against target date for KPI reporting.']
  dbl_criteria varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the criteria that must be satisfied before the database can be locked (DBL), as defined in the DMP. Includes query resolution thresholds, outstanding discrepancy limits, and data review completion requirements.']
  dbl_target_date date [note: 'Planned target date for achieving database lock (DBL) as specified in the DMP. Used for study timeline planning and milestone tracking.']
  dmp_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the DMP, typically formatted as DMP-{StudyCode}-{SequenceNumber}. Used in regulatory submissions, TMF indexing, and cross-system references.']
  edc_study_environment varchar [note: 'Deployment environment of the EDC system for this study (production, UAT, training). Ensures data management operations reference the correct validated environment.']
  edc_study_name varchar [note: 'Name of the study as configured within the EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave study name). Used to cross-reference DMP governance with the operational EDC configuration.']
  edc_system varchar [note: 'Name of the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system configured for this study as specified in the DMP. Governs which system is the authoritative source for eCRF data collection.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the Data Management Plan becomes operationally binding for the study. Aligns with the date of final approval signature.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the DMP is superseded or retired. Null for the currently active version. Used to manage version history and ensure the correct DMP governs active operations.']
  external_data_sources varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative description of external data sources feeding into the study database (e.g., central laboratory, ECG vendor, PK laboratory, ePRO, wearable devices) as specified in the DMP.']
  external_data_transfer_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study requires ingestion of external data transfers (e.g., central lab, ECG, PK, imaging) as specified in the DMP. When true, external data transfer specifications must be documented.']
  meddra_version varchar [note: 'Version of the MedDRA coding dictionary mandated by this DMP for adverse event and medical history coding (e.g., 26.0). Ensures consistent regulatory coding across the study lifecycle.']
  part11_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EDC system and data management processes for this study have been confirmed as compliant with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic records and electronic signatures.']
  pseudonymization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to pseudonymize subject data in the EDC and downstream datasets as specified in the DMP, ensuring subject identity is protected in compliance with GDPR and HIPAA.']
  query_management_convention varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the query management approach defined in the DMP, including rules for raising, routing, escalating, and closing data queries in the EDC system.']
  sdtm_ig_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide (SDTM IG) used for this study (e.g., 3.3, 3.4). Determines the domain structures and controlled terminology applied in SDTM dataset generation.']
  sdtm_mapping_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC SDTM mapping specification applied to this study as defined in the DMP. Governs the transformation of raw EDC data to SDTM-compliant datasets for regulatory submission.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'Regulatory phase of the clinical study governed by this DMP (e.g., Phase I, Phase II, Phase III, Phase IV). Determines the complexity and regulatory rigor of data management requirements.']
  unblinding_procedure_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DMP documents the procedure for emergency and planned unblinding of randomization codes, including roles, authorization levels, and audit trail requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this DMP record was last modified in the system. Used for change tracking and audit trail compliance.']
  validation_check_count varchar [note: 'Total number of programmatic data validation checks (edit checks) defined in the EDC system for this study as documented in the DMP. Indicates the scope of automated data quality controls.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Data Management Plan document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented upon each approved amendment to the DMP.']
  whodd_version varchar [note: 'Version of the WHO Drug Dictionary (WHO-DD) mandated by this DMP for concomitant medication coding. Ensures standardized drug coding for regulatory submissions.']

  Note: 'Master record for the Data Management Plan (DMP) governing all data management activities for a clinical study. Captures DMP version, effective date, study phase, EDC system configuration, data collection strategy, coding dictionaries and versions (MedDRA, WHO-DD), database lock criteria and procedures, data review plan, query management conventions, external data transfer specifications, responsible data manager, and approval status. Serves as the single authoritative governance document referenced by all data management operations on a study.'
}

Table data.coding_assignment {
  coding_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each medical coding assignment record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: Blinded medical coding is a named GCP requirement — coders must be blinded to treatment assignment when coding AEs and medications to prevent bias. The blinding_config defines coder blinding rules. Th']
  discrepancy_query_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an active or resolved data query raised against this coding assignment. Populated when the coding status is \'queried\'. Supports query management and discrepancy resolution workflows.']
  dispensing_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.dispensing_record. Business justification: Concomitant medication coding (WHO-DD) is performed against actual dispensing events. Medical coders link verbatim drug terms to coded terms by referencing the dispensing record. This is a standard CM']
  dmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: The Data Management Plan (DMP) governs all coding activities including coding_platform, autocoding_enabled, meddra_version, and whodd_version. coding_assignment captures the actual execution of coding']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Medical coding (MedDRA/WHODrug) is governed by a specific coding SOP. coding_assignment has compliance_sop_id (compliance domain) but needs a document_sop_id for the document-level coding procedure SO']
  ecrf_field_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific eCRF field (data point) in Medidata Rave from which the verbatim term was collected. Provides traceability back to the source data capture point.']
  field_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.field_entry. Business justification: coding_assignment applies medical coding to a verbatim term collected in a specific eCRF field entry. field_entry is the atomic data entry record. Adding field_entry_id links the coding assignment to ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this coding assignment was made. Links the coding record to its parent study context.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose verbatim term is being coded. Enables subject-level safety and coding analytics.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the coding assignment was formally approved by an authorized reviewer. Used for regulatory submission readiness tracking and coding cycle time analytics.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The regulatory approval state of the coding assignment, distinct from the workflow coding status. Indicates whether the coding has been formally approved by an authorized reviewer for inclusion in regulatory submissions and SDTM datasets.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone) when the coding assignment was approved by an authorized reviewer. Provides granular audit trail for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  assigned_code varchar [note: 'The standardized dictionary code assigned to the verbatim term. For MedDRA, this is the numeric PT code (e.g., 10047700). For WHO-DD, this is the drug record number. This is the primary coded output used in regulatory submissions and safety analyses.']
  autocoding_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'The algorithmic confidence score (0.0000 to 1.0000) returned by the autocoding engine when automatically matching the verbatim term to a dictionary code. Scores below a configured threshold require manual review. Null for manually coded terms.']
  coding_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the verbatim term was coded (code assigned). Used for coding timeliness metrics, regulatory timelines, and ALCOA+ contemporaneity compliance.']
  coding_dictionary varchar [note: 'The medical coding dictionary used to assign the standardized code to the verbatim term. MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) is used for adverse events and medical history; WHO-DD (WHO Drug Dictionary) is used for concomitant medications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MedDRA|WHO-DD|SNOMED-CT|ICD-10|CTCAE|NCI-Thesaurus — promote to reference product]']
  coding_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain to which this coding assignment belongs. Determines the regulatory submission context. AE=Adverse Events, CM=Concomitant Medications, MH=Medical History, DS=Disposition, DV=Protocol Deviations, EX=Exposure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AE|CM|MH|DS|DV|EX|LB|VS|PR — promote to reference product]']
  coding_flag varchar [note: 'A quality control flag applied to the coding assignment indicating its readiness for database lock (DBL) and regulatory submission. \'clean\' = no issues; \'flagged\' = requires attention; \'review_required\' = pending medical review; \'locked\' = included in database lock.']
  coding_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the coding assignment was made. \'autocoding\' indicates the EDC system (Medidata Rave) automatically matched the verbatim term; \'manual\' indicates a coder manually assigned the code; \'assisted\' indicates a system suggestion accepted by a coder; \'recoded\' indicates a previously coded term was reassigned.']
  coding_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the coding assignment. \'pending\' = awaiting coding; \'coded\' = code assigned but not yet approved; \'approved\' = coding reviewed and approved by authorized coder or supervisor; \'rejected\' = coding rejected and requires rework; \'queried\' = a data query has been raised; \'recoded\' = previously approved code has been revised.']
  coding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone) when the coding assignment was created or last modified in the source system. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneity requirements and audit trail reconstruction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this coding assignment record was first created in the source system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer.']
  dbl_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this coding assignment was included in the final database lock (DBL) snapshot for regulatory submission. True = included in DBL; False = excluded or post-lock amendment. Critical for submission integrity and audit trail.']
  dictionary_version varchar [note: 'The specific version of the coding dictionary applied at the time of coding (e.g., MedDRA 26.1, WHO-DD March 2024). Regulatory submissions require explicit dictionary version documentation to ensure reproducibility and consistency.']
  field_name varchar [note: 'The specific eCRF field name within the form from which the verbatim term was captured (e.g., \'AETERM\', \'CMTRT\'). Enables precise source data traceability per ALCOA+ and SDTM variable mapping.']
  form_name varchar [note: 'The name of the eCRF form (e.g., \'Adverse Events\', \'Concomitant Medications\') in Medidata Rave from which the verbatim term was collected. Supports data lineage and source data verification (SDV) traceability.']
  high_level_group_term varchar [note: 'The MedDRA High Level Group Term (HLGT) associated with the assigned Preferred Term. Second-highest level in the MedDRA hierarchy, used for broader safety signal grouping and regulatory tabulations.']
  high_level_term varchar [note: 'The MedDRA High Level Term (HLT) grouping the assigned Preferred Term. Intermediate level in the MedDRA hierarchy between PT and HLGT, used in aggregate safety analyses and signal detection.']
  is_primary_soc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this coding assignment uses the primary SOC classification for the assigned Preferred Term. MedDRA allows a PT to belong to multiple SOCs; the primary SOC is used for regulatory safety tabulations. True = primary SOC; False = secondary SOC.']
  is_serious_ae boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the verbatim term being coded is associated with a Serious Adverse Event (SAE). True = the source record is an SAE. Drives expedited regulatory reporting timelines (e.g., 7-day/15-day reporting per ICH E2A) and SUSAR assessment workflows.']
  lowest_level_term varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Lowest Level Term (LLT) that most closely matches the verbatim term. The LLT is the most granular level of the MedDRA hierarchy and is used to capture the precise clinical concept before mapping to the PT.']
  medical_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the medical reviewer completed their review of the coding assignment. Null if medical review is not required or not yet performed for this record.']
  preferred_term varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) or WHO-DD preferred drug name assigned to the verbatim term. The PT is the primary level of the MedDRA hierarchy used in regulatory safety reporting and aggregate analyses.']
  prior_assigned_code varchar [note: 'The dictionary code that was previously assigned before a recoding event. Preserved for audit trail purposes to document the history of coding changes per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements. Null for initial coding assignments.']
  prior_preferred_term varchar [note: 'The MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) that was previously assigned before a recoding event. Preserved alongside prior_assigned_code for complete audit trail documentation of coding history changes.']
  pt_code varchar [note: 'The numeric MedDRA code for the Preferred Term (PT). Used in SDTM datasets (AEPTCD) and regulatory submissions. Distinct from the PT text label to support code-based lookups and dictionary version migrations.']
  recoding_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by the coder when a previously approved coding assignment is revised (recoded). Required for audit trail integrity and regulatory inspection readiness. Null for initial coding assignments.']
  sdtm_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM variable name to which this coding assignment maps (e.g., AEDECOD for AE Preferred Term, AEBODSYS for AE SOC, CMDECOD for CM decoded name). Supports automated SDTM dataset generation and regulatory submission preparation.']
  soc_code varchar [note: 'The numeric MedDRA code for the System Organ Class (SOC). Used in SDTM datasets (AESOCCD) and regulatory submissions to uniquely identify the SOC category.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this coding assignment originated (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC coding module). Supports data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation.']
  system_organ_class varchar [note: 'The MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) associated with the assigned Preferred Term. The SOC is the highest level of the MedDRA hierarchy and is used for aggregate safety tabulations in clinical study reports and regulatory submissions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this coding assignment record was last modified in the source system. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  verbatim_term varchar [note: 'The exact, unmodified text as entered by the investigator or site staff in the eCRF prior to coding. Preserved verbatim per ALCOA+ principles to maintain data integrity and auditability. May contain patient-reported or clinician-reported language.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'The name or label of the study visit during which the verbatim term was collected (e.g., \'Screening\', \'Week 4\', \'End of Treatment\'). Provides temporal context for the coded term within the study schedule.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of medical coding assignments applied to verbatim terms collected in eCRFs. Captures verbatim term, coding dictionary (MedDRA or WHO-DD), dictionary version, assigned code, preferred term (PT), system organ class (SOC), lowest level term (LLT), coding method (autocoding, manual), coder user ID, coding date, approval status, and coding flag. Supports regulatory-grade medical coding workflows.'
}
