  dataset_name varchar [note: 'Two- to four-character CDISC SDTM domain abbreviation identifying the dataset (e.g., AE for Adverse Events, CM for Concomitant Medications, DM for Demographics, LB for Laboratory, VS for Vital Signs, EX for Exposure). Must conform to CDISC SDTM domain naming conventions.']
  dataset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SDTM dataset within the data management and submission workflow. Progresses from Draft through QC Complete, Frozen, Locked, and Submitted. Superseded indicates a prior version replaced by a newer submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|In Review|QC Complete|Frozen|Locked|Submitted|Superseded — promote to reference product]']
  define_xml_reference varchar [note: 'File path or URI reference to the define.xml document that describes the metadata for this SDTM dataset within the eCTD submission package. Enables direct navigation to the dataset metadata specification.']
  define_xml_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC define.xml metadata standard used to describe this dataset within the submission package (e.g., \'2.0\', \'2.1\'). The define.xml is a mandatory component of FDA and EMA electronic submissions.']
  domain_class varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM observation class to which this dataset belongs. Classifies the dataset into one of the standard SDTM domain classes: Events (e.g., AE, DS), Interventions (e.g., CM, EX), Findings (e.g., LB, VS, EG), Special Purpose (e.g., DM, CO), Relationship (e.g., RELREC, SUPPQUAL), or Trial Design (e.g., TA, TE, TV).']
  generation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this SDTM dataset was generated from source EDC data. Used for audit trail and submission package dating per ALCOA+ contemporaneous principle.']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the SDTM dataset was generated, including timezone offset. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  is_split_dataset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dataset has been split into multiple physical files due to size constraints (e.g., LB domain split by lab panel). True = dataset is one part of a split; False = single complete dataset file.']
  is_submission_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SDTM dataset has passed all QC checks, Pinnacle 21 validation, and internal review gates and is approved for inclusion in a regulatory submission package. True = ready for submission; False = not yet approved.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing dataset-specific annotations, known issues, deviation justifications, or reviewer instructions that do not fit into structured fields. Supports ALCOA+ documentation completeness.']
  pinnacle21_error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors (severity: Error) identified during the Pinnacle 21 validation run. Errors must be resolved or formally justified before regulatory submission.']
  pinnacle21_run_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Pinnacle 21 validation was executed against this dataset. Supports audit trail and ensures validation currency relative to dataset generation.']
  pinnacle21_validation_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the Pinnacle 21 Community or Enterprise conformance validation run against this SDTM dataset. Regulatory agencies (FDA, PMDA) require Pinnacle 21 validation reports as part of the submission package.']
  pinnacle21_warning_count varchar [note: 'Number of warnings identified during the Pinnacle 21 validation run. Warnings require documented justification in the Reviewer\'s Guide or ADRG if not resolved.']
  qc_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the QC review of this SDTM dataset was completed and signed off. Required for audit trail documentation per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current quality control review status for this SDTM dataset. Tracks progression through the double-programming or independent QC review process required before regulatory submission.']
  record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of observation records contained in this SDTM dataset at the time of generation or last update. Used for reconciliation, QC verification, and submission package validation.']
  reviewer_guide_reference varchar [note: 'File path or document reference to the Analysis Data Reviewer\'s Guide (ADRG) or Study Data Reviewer\'s Guide (SDRG) section that documents dataset-specific conventions, deviations, and reviewer instructions for this SDTM domain.']
  sas_dataset_path varchar [note: 'File system path or storage location of the SAS transport file (XPT format) for this SDTM dataset. FDA requires submission datasets in SAS Version 5 transport format (.xpt).']
  sdtm_ig_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide used to generate this dataset (e.g., \'3.3\', \'3.4\'). Critical for regulatory submission conformance validation and Pinnacle 21 checks.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which the source data for this SDTM dataset was extracted (e.g., Medidata Rave for EDC data, Oracle Argus Safety for safety data, SAS Drug Development for transformed outputs).']
  split_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the order of this file within a split dataset series (e.g., 1 for LB1, 2 for LB2). Null when is_split_dataset is False.']
  sponsor_dataset_name varchar [note: 'Sponsor-specific internal name or alias for this dataset, which may differ from the standard CDISC domain abbreviation in cases of custom or extended domains. Used for internal tracking and sponsor communication.']
  subject_count varchar [note: 'Number of unique study subjects (USUBJID) represented in this SDTM dataset. Supports population-level QC checks and reconciliation against the DM domain.']
  submission_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the regulatory submission package in which this dataset is included (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Tracks dataset evolution across multiple submission cycles for the same study.']
  suppqual_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a supplemental qualifier dataset (SUPP--) accompanies this SDTM domain dataset to capture non-standard variables. True = a corresponding SUPP-- dataset exists; False = no supplemental dataset.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SDTM dataset record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.']
  variable_count varchar [note: 'Total number of variables (columns) in this SDTM dataset. Used for define.xml completeness verification and to ensure compliance with CDISC domain variable requirements.']
  xpt_file_size_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Size of the SAS transport (.xpt) file in kilobytes. Used for submission package size management and to flag datasets approaching the 5GB FDA file size limit.']

  Note: 'Master record for CDISC SDTM (Study Data Tabulation Model) dataset definitions and their submission-ready outputs. Captures dataset name (e.g., AE, CM, DM, LB, VS), SDTM domain class (Events, Interventions, Findings), SDTM IG version, study identifier, submission version, generation date, record count, Pinnacle 21 validation status, define.xml reference, submission-ready flag, and responsible programmer. Each dataset contains one or more sdtm_mapping records that define the source-to-target transformation rules. Serves as the header record governing CDISC dataset generation and regulatory submission packaging.'
}

Table data.sdtm_mapping {
  sdtm_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a single source-to-target SDTM variable mapping rule record within the Clinical Trials data management system. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — this record represents one variable-level mapping line within a parent SDTM dataset specification.']
  dmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: The DMP specifies sdtm_mapping_version and sdtm_ig_version, which directly govern the SDTM mapping rules. sdtm_mapping already captures sdtm_ig_version and mapping_version as attributes, but lacks a d']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are created and maintained per a data standards SOP. sdtm_mapping has compliance_sop_id (compliance domain) but needs document_sop_id for the document-level SDTM mapping pr']
  dosing_regimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen. Business justification: SDTM EX domain mappings reference dosing regimens — regimen_code and sdtm_ex_mapping_notes on dosing_regimen confirm this. Linking sdtm_mapping to dosing_regimen enables EX domain traceability and exp']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: SDTM IE domain mappings directly reference eligibility criteria — each IE record maps to a specific criterion. Linking sdtm_mapping to eligibility_criterion is required for IE domain define.xml genera']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: SDTM mappings are directly tied to endpoints — adam_parameter_code and sdtm_domain on endpoint align with sdtm_variable_name on sdtm_mapping. Endpoint-level SDTM traceability is required for SAP align']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: SDTM LB domain mappings are panel-specific. SDTM programmers must know which lab panel a mapping applies to for define.xml generation and LB dataset construction. A direct FK avoids traversal through ']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are governed by the SAP, which defines required SDTM domains, variables, and submission versions. Programmers reference the SAP when authoring and validating SDTM mappings ']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent SDTM dataset specification record that owns this mapping rule. Establishes the header/detail relationship between the dataset-level specification and this individual variable mapping line.']
  source_ecrf_field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: sdtm_mapping defines transformation rules from raw EDC eCRF fields to SDTM variables. source_field_name and source_field_label are denormalized references to the source eCRF field. Adding source_ecrf_']
  source_ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_form. Business justification: sdtm_mapping defines transformation rules from raw EDC eCRF fields to SDTM variables. source_form_name is a denormalized reference to the source eCRF form. Adding source_ecrf_form_id normalizes this r']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this SDTM mapping rule is defined. Enables study-scoped filtering and traceability of all mapping specifications back to the originating protocol.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are submission-specific — NDA, MAA, and JNDA submissions may require different mapping versions and controlled terminology. Regulatory data managers need to know which subm']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: SDTM mapping specifications are filed in the TMF as data management plan supporting documents. TMF completeness assessments require mapping specifications to be traceable to TMF filings. Supports insp']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: SDTM ARM/ACTARM variable mappings (TA, ADSL domains) are directly sourced from treatment_arm definitions. Regulatory submission requires SDTM mappings to reference the exact treatment arm record to ge']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: SDTM mappings trace source data to SDTM variables at the procedure level. Visit procedures define what data is collected and in which SDTM domain. Procedure-level SDTM traceability is a core requireme']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this mapping rule was formally approved and promoted to \'approved\' status by the authorized reviewer. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this mapping lifecycle transition. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification for the most recent change to this mapping rule (e.g., \'Protocol Amendment 3 — new AE severity scale\', \'Corrected derivation logic per QC review\'). Required when mapping_status transitions or derivation_algorithm is modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  coding_dictionary varchar [note: 'Medical coding dictionary used for this mapping when derivation_type is \'coded\'. MedDRA (Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities) for adverse events and medical history; WHO-DD (WHO Drug Dictionary) for concomitant medications; CTCAE (Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events) for severity grading. \'none\' if no coding dictionary applies.']
  coding_dictionary_version varchar [note: 'Version of the medical coding dictionary applied for this mapping (e.g., MedDRA v26.1, WHO-DD March 2023). Ensures reproducibility of coded terms across transformation runs and supports regulatory submission traceability.']
  controlled_terminology_codelist varchar [note: 'Name of the CDISC Controlled Terminology code list applied to this SDTM variable (e.g., \'AESEV\', \'NCOMPLT\', \'SEX\', \'RACE\'). Null if no controlled terminology applies. Governs permissible values for the target variable in regulatory submissions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SDTM mapping rule record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance. Immutable after initial record creation.']
  ct_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC Controlled Terminology package applied for this mapping (e.g., \'2023-09-29\'). Ensures reproducibility of coded values across transformation runs and regulatory submissions. Critical for FDA/EMA submission compliance.']
  define_xml_comment varchar [note: 'Supplementary comment text to be included in the CDISC Define-XML v2.1 submission package for this variable. Provides additional context for FDA/EMA reviewers about the derivation logic, data source, or special handling. Appears in the define.xml ItemDef Comment attribute.']
  define_xml_origin varchar [note: 'Origin attribute value for this variable as it will appear in the CDISC Define-XML v2.1 submission metadata. Indicates the source of the data: \'CRF\' (collected on eCRF), \'Derived\' (algorithmically derived), \'Assigned\' (assigned by sponsor/CRO), \'Protocol\' (defined in protocol), \'eDT\' (electronic data transfer), \'Predecessor\' (derived from prior SDTM variable).']
  derivation_algorithm varchar [note: 'Full specification of the transformation logic or derivation rule applied to produce the target SDTM variable value from the source field(s). Includes SAS code snippets, conditional logic, date calculations, concatenation rules, and multi-source merge logic. For direct mappings, states \'Direct mapping from {source_field_name}\'. Supports reproducible and auditable CDISC data transformation per ALCOA+ principles.']
  derivation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the mapping derivation method: \'direct\' (verbatim copy from source), \'derived\' (computed from one or more source fields), \'assigned\' (constant or protocol-defined value), \'predecessor\' (derived from a previously mapped SDTM variable), \'computed\' (algorithmic calculation), \'coded\' (medical coding via MedDRA or WHO-DD lookup).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this version of the mapping rule is no longer active and has been superseded or deprecated. Null for currently active mapping rules. Supports temporal versioning of mapping specifications across protocol amendments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the mapping rule is effective and applicable to SDTM transformation runs. Supports version-controlled mapping specifications where different versions apply to different data collection periods or protocol amendments.']
  is_multi_source boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SDTM variable mapping requires data from more than one source field or form (True) or derives from a single source field (False). When True, the derivation_algorithm field documents all contributing source fields and the combination logic.']
  is_predecessor_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the predecessor variable dependency documented in predecessor_variable is a hard requirement (True) that must be resolved before this mapping executes, or an informational dependency (False). Governs transformation program execution sequencing.']
  is_sensitive_variable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target SDTM variable contains sensitive subject data requiring special handling under GDPR, HIPAA, or sponsor data privacy agreements (True). Examples include subject identifiers, dates of birth, and geographic data. Governs data masking and de-identification procedures in submission datasets.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SDTM mapping rule record was most recently updated. Updated on every change to the mapping rule, derivation logic, or status. Supports change tracking and ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  mapping_rule_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code identifying this mapping rule within the study\'s SDTM mapping specification document. Follows the pattern MAP-{DOMAIN}-{VARIABLE}-{SEQUENCE}. Used in Data Management Plans (DMP) and CDISC submission packages.']
  mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this SDTM mapping rule. Controls which version is active for production CDISC transformation runs. \'approved\' rules are eligible for DBL and regulatory submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|deprecated|superseded|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  mapping_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this mapping rule in major.minor format (e.g., 1.0, 2.3). Incremented when the transformation logic, source field, or controlled terminology mapping changes. Supports version-controlled, auditable CDISC data transformation per ALCOA+ principles.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional programmer or reviewer notes about this mapping rule, including special handling instructions, known data quality issues, sponsor-specific requirements, or cross-references to related mapping rules. Not included in regulatory submission artifacts.']
  predecessor_variable varchar [note: 'Name of the SDTM variable (within the same or a different domain) that must be derived before this mapping can be executed. Documents derivation dependencies for sequencing transformation programs. Format: \'{DOMAIN}.{VARIABLE}\' (e.g., \'AE.AEDECOD\'). Null if no predecessor dependency exists.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Data Management or Biostatistics reviewer who reviewed and approved this mapping rule. Supports dual-review traceability and ALCOA+ compliance for the SDTM mapping specification document.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'Two-letter CDISC SDTM domain code identifying the target dataset domain (e.g., DM, AE, LB, VS, CM, EX, MH, DS, SV, TU). Determines the SDTM class and required/expected variable set for this mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DM|AE|LB|VS|CM|EX|MH|DS|SV|TU|EC|FA|RS|TR|TU|PR|CE|DD|HO|ML|PC|PP|RE|RP|SR|SS|TI|TS|TV — promote to reference product]']
  sdtm_ig_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide that governs this mapping rule (e.g., \'SDTMIG v3.4\', \'SDTMIG-AP v1.0\'). Ensures regulatory submission compliance with the correct CDISC standard version as required by FDA and EMA data submission guidelines.']
  sdtm_variable_core varchar [note: 'CDISC core designation indicating whether the target SDTM variable is Required (Req), Expected (Exp), or Permissible (Perm) for the domain. Required variables must be populated; Expected variables should be populated when data is collected; Permissible variables are optional.']
  sdtm_variable_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive label for the target SDTM variable as defined in the CDISC SDTM Implementation Guide (e.g., \'Dictionary-Derived Term\', \'Numeric Result/Finding in Standard Units\'). Used in SAS dataset metadata and regulatory submission define.xml.']
  sdtm_variable_name varchar [note: 'Name of the target CDISC SDTM variable within the specified domain (e.g., AEDECOD, LBSTRESN, VSORRES). Must conform to CDISC SDTM naming conventions: uppercase, 2–8 characters, domain prefix for most variables. This is the column name in the final SDTM dataset submitted to regulatory agencies.']
  sdtm_variable_role varchar [note: 'CDISC-defined role of the target SDTM variable within its domain class (Identifier, Topic, Qualifier, Timing, Rule). Determines how the variable functions within the SDTM dataset structure and governs its position and usage in regulatory submissions.']
  sdtm_variable_type varchar [note: 'CDISC-defined data type of the target SDTM variable. \'Char\' for character/text variables and \'Num\' for numeric variables, as specified in the SDTM Implementation Guide and define.xml metadata. Governs SAS dataset column type in regulatory submissions.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this mapping rule within the parent SDTM dataset specification. Governs the order in which variables appear in the SDTM dataset and define.xml, and the execution order of transformation programs in SAS Drug Development.']
  source_dataset_name varchar [note: 'Name of the raw/staging dataset (SAS dataset or EDC extract table) that contains the source field prior to SDTM transformation (e.g., \'RAW_AE\', \'RAW_VS\'). Used in SAS Drug Development transformation programs to identify the input dataset for each mapping rule.']
  suppqual_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variable is mapped to a CDISC SDTM Supplemental Qualifier (SUPP--) dataset rather than the parent domain dataset (True). SUPPQUAL datasets hold non-standard variables that cannot be accommodated in the standard domain structure.']
  suppqual_qnam varchar [note: 'Short variable name (QNAM) used in the SUPPQUAL dataset for this non-standard variable. Populated only when suppqual_flag is True. Must be uppercase, 1–8 characters, and unique within the SUPPQUAL dataset for the domain.']
  validation_error_code varchar [note: 'Pinnacle 21 / CDISC conformance rule error or warning code associated with the most recent validation failure for this mapping (e.g., \'SD0083\', \'SD0006\'). Null when validation_status is \'passed\' or \'not_validated\'. Supports targeted remediation by the CDISC programmer.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the CDISC Pinnacle 21 / OpenCDISC validation check result for this mapping rule. \'passed\' indicates no conformance errors; \'failed\' indicates outstanding validation errors requiring remediation before DBL; \'waived\' indicates a documented exception approved by the Data Management team.']

  Note: 'Detail-level record defining a single source-to-target transformation rule between a raw EDC eCRF field and a CDISC SDTM variable within a parent sdtm_dataset. Captures source form, source field, target SDTM domain, target variable name, transformation logic/derivation rule (including multi-source derivations), controlled terminology mapping, predecessor derivation dependencies, mapping version, validation status, and responsible programmer. Represents one variable-level mapping within the complete dataset specification. Supports reproducible, auditable, and version-controlled CDISC data transformation.'
}

  edc_study_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build.   epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: External transfers are epoch-scoped for interim analysis data cuts and end-of-epoch data deliveries. Epoch-level transfer management and data cut planning require direct epoch linkage on external tran']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigative site associated with the transferred data, where applicable (e.g., central lab data linked to a specific site).']

TMF managers must file and track transfer agreements. external_transfer already has document']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: External data transfers are triggered by specific visit procedures (central lab transfer after PK sampling visit). Procedure-level transfer tracking and reconciliation require knowing which visit proc']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal acknowledgement of receipt was received from the target system or recipient.']
  acknowledgement_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal acknowledgement of receipt was received from the target system or recipient following the transfer. Supports ALCOA+ traceability.']
  cdisc_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM or ADaM domain code applicable to the transferred data (e.g., LB for laboratory, PC for pharmacokinetic concentration, EG for ECG). Governs downstream SDTM transformation.']
  checksum_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to generate the checksum value for file integrity verification (e.g., MD5, SHA-256).']
  checksum_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cryptographic checksum (e.g., MD5 or SHA-256 hash) of the transferred file, used to verify file integrity and detect corruption or tampering during transmission.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for data manager notes, operational remarks, or contextual information about this transfer that does not fit structured fields.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this external transfer record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements.']
  data_cut_date date [note: 'The data cut-off date up to which records are included in this transfer. Critical for interim analyses, database lock (DBL) workflows, and regulatory submissions.']
  dbl_impacted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transfer is on the critical path for a database lock (DBL) milestone. Transfers flagged as DBL-impacted require prioritized reconciliation and sign-off.']
  discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Number of data discrepancies identified during reconciliation between the transferred data and the expected source data. Zero indicates a clean transfer.']
  encryption_method varchar [note: 'Encryption or secure transmission method applied to the data file during transfer (e.g., PGP encryption, SFTP, HTTPS). Supports data security and regulatory compliance.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any errors, failures, or exceptions encountered during the transfer, validation, or reconciliation process. Populated when transfer_status is \'failed\'.']
  expected_record_count varchar [note: 'Number of records expected in the transfer as defined in the transfer specification or agreed with the vendor. Compared against actual record_count during reconciliation.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the transferred data file (e.g., SAS XPT for CDISC submissions, CSV for flat file transfers, HL7 for lab/clinical messaging). Governs parsing and validation approach.']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the data file or package transferred, as received or sent. Used for traceability and reconciliation against transfer specifications.']
  file_size_kb decimal(18,3) [note: 'Size of the transferred data file in kilobytes. Used for transfer validation, completeness checks, and anomaly detection (e.g., unexpectedly small or large files).']
  record_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data records contained in the transferred file or dataset. Used for reconciliation against expected counts from the source vendor.']
  regulatory_submission_relevant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transferred data is intended for inclusion in a regulatory submission (e.g., NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD). Triggers additional QC and traceability requirements.']
  retry_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this transfer was retried following an initial failure. Supports operational monitoring and escalation decisions.']
  sdv_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether source data verification (SDV) is required for the transferred data as part of the monitoring plan. Relevant for central lab and PK/PD data.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the originating system from which data is being transferred (e.g., LabCorp LIMS, Covance Central Lab, BioClinica ECG, Medidata Rave EDC, ePRO vendor platform).']
  target_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the destination system receiving the transferred data (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC, SAS Drug Development, sponsor data warehouse).']
  transfer_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the external data transfer was executed. This is the principal real-world business event date for the transfer.']
  transfer_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether data is flowing into the clinical database (inbound) from an external source, or being sent out (outbound) to an external recipient.']
  transfer_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency of this type of external data transfer as agreed in the data transfer specification (e.g., weekly lab data feeds, one-time PK data transfer).']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the transfer event (e.g., EXT-STUDY001-202401). Used for cross-system traceability and audit references.']
  transfer_protocol varchar [note: 'Network or file transfer protocol used to transmit the data (e.g., SFTP, FTPS, HTTPS, AS2, or manual upload). Governs technical integration and security posture.']
  transfer_spec_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the data transfer specification document in effect at the time of this transfer. Ensures traceability to the correct specification version.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the external data transfer workflow, from initiation through completion or failure.']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the external data transfer was initiated or completed, supporting ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the external data transfer by data category. Identifies the nature of the third-party data being transferred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: central_lab|pk_pd|epro_ecoa|ecg|medical_imaging|wearable_device|other — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this external transfer record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the data validation process applied to the transferred file, including format checks, range checks, and completeness checks per the data validation plan.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of external data transfers into or out of the clinical database, including central lab results (LabCorp, Covance), PK/PD concentration data, ePRO/eCOA data, ECG data, medical imaging, and wearable/device data. Captures transfer direction (inbound/outbound), source system, target system, transfer date, file format (SAS XPT, CSV, HL7), record count, validation status, reconciliation status, responsible data manager, and transfer specification reference. Governs third-party data integration and ensures all external data is traceable and reconciled.'
}

Table data.edc_study_build {

  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual(s) who provided formal sign-off approval for this EDC study build. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail.']
  build_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the EDC study build configuration was completed and handed over for UAT. Marks the end of the development phase.']
  build_environment varchar [note: 'The Medidata Rave environment in which this build configuration resides: development (active build/configuration), UAT (User Acceptance Testing validation), or production (live data collection).']
  build_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the EDC study build configuration, typically including the study acronym and build purpose (e.g., STUDY-001 Phase III Production Build).']
  build_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing key decisions, known issues, deviations from standard build practices, or special configurations relevant to this EDC study build version.']
  build_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this specific EDC study build version in Medidata Rave (e.g., BUILD_001, v2.3.1). Used for traceability and change control documentation.']
  build_start_date date [note: 'The date on which active development/configuration of this EDC study build commenced in Medidata Rave. Used for project planning and build timeline tracking.']
  build_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the EDC study build configuration. Tracks progression from initial development through UAT, approval, deployment, and eventual retirement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_development|in_uat|uat_complete|approved|deployed|retired — promote to reference product]']
  build_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the build change type: initial (first build for the study), amendment (protocol amendment-driven rebuild), patch (minor correction), or hotfix (urgent production fix).']
  build_version varchar [note: 'Semantic version number of the EDC study build (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Incremented with each approved change to the build configuration to support audit trail and change control.']
  cdash_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC CDASH standard used to design the eCRF data collection fields in this EDC study build. Ensures harmonized data collection aligned with regulatory submission requirements.']
  cdisc_sdtm_mapping_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC SDTM mapping specification applied to this EDC study build. Governs how collected eCRF data is transformed into SDTM-compliant datasets for regulatory submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EDC study build record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.']
  crf_count varchar [note: 'Total number of electronic Case Report Forms (eCRFs) configured in this EDC study build. Provides a high-level measure of build scope and complexity.']
  derivation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of programmed data derivations (auto-calculated fields) configured in this EDC study build. Derivations reduce manual data entry and support data consistency.']
  econsent_integrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EDC study build is integrated with an eConsent platform (e.g., Clinpal) for electronic Informed Consent Form (ICF) management and subject consent tracking.']
  edit_check_count varchar [note: 'Total number of programmed edit checks (data validation rules) configured in this EDC study build. Edit checks enforce data quality and ALCOA+ compliance at point of entry.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'The date on which the EDC study build was deployed to the production environment and made available for live clinical data collection at study sites.']
  lab_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EDC study build has automated laboratory data integration configured (e.g., central lab data feeds into Medidata Rave). Reduces manual transcription and supports ALCOA+.']
  part11_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EDC study build has been validated and confirmed compliant with 21 CFR Part 11 requirements for electronic records and electronic signatures.']
  randomization_integrated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this EDC study build is integrated with the Medidata Rave RTSM (Randomization and Trial Supply Management) module for subject randomization and IRT functionality.']
  rave_study_name varchar [note: 'The exact study name as configured and registered within the Medidata Rave EDC system. Used for system-level identification and integration with downstream CDISC SDTM pipelines.']
  rave_study_uuid varchar [note: 'Universally unique identifier assigned to the study within Medidata Rave. Used for system-to-system integration, API calls, and cross-environment traceability.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which this EDC study build version was retired and superseded by a newer build version. Null if the build is still active.']
  sponsor_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor provided formal approval for the EDC study build. Null if sponsor approval is not required or not yet received.']
  sponsor_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal sponsor sign-off is required for this EDC build before production deployment. Driven by the study contract and Data Management Plan.']
  uat_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which UAT was formally completed and signed off. Prerequisite for build approval and production deployment.']
  uat_defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects identified during UAT for this build version. Used to assess build quality and readiness for production deployment.']
  uat_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EDC study build successfully passed all UAT test cases without critical defects. True = UAT passed; False = UAT failed or has open critical defects.']
  uat_start_date date [note: 'The date on which User Acceptance Testing of the EDC study build commenced. UAT validates that the build meets protocol requirements before production deployment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EDC study build record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Master record for the EDC study build configuration in Medidata Rave. Captures study build version, build environment (development, UAT, production), build owner, build date, UAT completion status, go-live date, protocol version linked, amendment history, and build approval sign-off. Governs the lifecycle of the EDC system configuration for each study.'
}

Business justification: Reconciliation of external data (central lab, PK samples) is tied to specific visit procedures. Procedure-level reconciliation tracking — knowing which procedure\'s data is being reconciled — is a stan']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for any Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiated as a result of systemic discrepancies identified during this reconciliation. Links to the CAPA management system (MasterControl).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  critical_discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrepancies classified as critical (e.g., safety-impacting, primary endpoint data, regulatory submission data). Critical discrepancies require mandatory resolution before DBL.']
  dataset_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific CDISC SDTM domain or dataset (e.g., AE, LB, CM, VS) being reconciled. Provides dataset-level traceability for SDTM submission readiness.']
  dbl_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation is a mandatory prerequisite for Database Lock (DBL). When True, outstanding discrepancies must be resolved or formally waived before DBL can proceed.']
  discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Total number of data discrepancies identified between the source and target systems during this reconciliation exercise. Includes all open, resolved, and outstanding discrepancies.']
  discrepancy_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of records with at least one discrepancy relative to total records compared (discrepancy_count / total_records_compared * 100). Key quality KPI for data management performance reporting and study risk assessment.']
  escalation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for escalation, including the nature of critical discrepancies, impacted data domains, and recommended corrective actions. Populated only when escalation_required is True.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation outcome requires escalation to the study team, sponsor, or Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) due to critical unresolved discrepancies or systemic data quality issues.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Cadence at which this type of reconciliation is performed for the study, as defined in the DMP. Drives scheduling and resource planning for data management activities.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for data manager comments, observations, or contextual information about the reconciliation activity, including any known data transfer delays, system outages, or protocol amendments affecting the comparison.']
  outstanding_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrepancies that remain unresolved at the time of the reconciliation record. A non-zero value blocks DBL (Database Lock) unless formally justified and documented.']
  period_end date [note: 'End date of the data window covered by this reconciliation activity. Defines the upper bound of the data extract or comparison period.']
  period_start date [note: 'Start date of the data window covered by this reconciliation activity. Defines the lower bound of the data extract or comparison period.']
  planned_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the reconciliation activity as defined in the Data Management Plan (DMP) or study timeline. Enables variance tracking between planned and actual reconciliation dates.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the reconciliation activity was formally executed. Principal business event date used for scheduling, DMP milestone tracking, and pre-DBL (Database Lock) compliance verification.']
  reconciliation_method varchar [note: 'Approach used to perform the reconciliation — fully manual review, automated system comparison, or a hybrid semi-automated process. Informs quality risk assessment and resource planning.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the reconciliation activity, used in Data Management Plans (DMP), audit trails, and cross-system references. Typically formatted as REC-{StudyCode}-{Sequence}.']
  reconciliation_scope varchar [note: 'Granularity level at which the reconciliation is performed — whether it covers the full study, a specific site, individual subjects, a visit window, or a specific dataset. Determines the breadth of the reconciliation exercise.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reconciliation activity, from initiation through sign-off. Drives workflow progression and DBL (Database Lock) readiness gates.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reconciliation activity by the category of external data source being reconciled against the EDC (Electronic Data Capture) system. Drives workflow routing and DMP compliance checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: central_lab|safety_database|irt_rtsm|epro|external_vendor|edc_internal — promote to reference product if additional types are anticipated]']
  resolved_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrepancies that have been investigated and resolved (closed) as of the reconciliation sign-off date. Used to assess reconciliation completeness.']
  signoff_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the individual who formally approved and signed off the reconciliation activity. Supports electronic signature audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  signoff_status varchar [note: 'Formal approval status of the reconciliation activity by the responsible data manager and/or study team. Approved sign-off is required before the reconciliation can be considered complete for DBL purposes.']
  signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the reconciliation was formally signed off. Provides the precise audit timestamp for regulatory inspection readiness per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  source_data_extract_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the data extract was pulled from the source system for reconciliation purposes. Critical for ALCOA+ contemporaneity and audit trail integrity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating operational system providing data for reconciliation (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Argus Safety, IRT/RTSM, ePRO platform, central laboratory LIMS). Identifies the system-of-record on the sending side.']
  target_data_extract_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the data extract was pulled from the target system for reconciliation comparison. Paired with source_data_extract_timestamp to establish the comparison window.']
  target_system varchar [note: 'Name of the receiving or comparison operational system against which source data is reconciled (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Argus Safety, SAS Drug Development). Identifies the system-of-record on the receiving side.']
  tool varchar [note: 'Name of the software tool or system used to perform the reconciliation (e.g., Medidata Rave, SAS macro, custom script, Oracle Clinical One). Supports audit trail and reproducibility documentation.']
  total_records_compared varchar [note: 'Total count of data records (e.g., subject visits, lab results, AE entries) included in the reconciliation comparison between source and target systems. Denominator for discrepancy rate calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reconciliation record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail integrity, and Silver Layer incremental load processing.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the reconciliation activity, incremented when a reconciliation is re-executed (e.g., after additional data is received or discrepancies are resolved). Supports iterative reconciliation tracking.']
  waived_count varchar [note: 'Number of discrepancies formally waived with documented justification (e.g., clinically insignificant, data not required for analysis). Supports transparent DBL decision-making.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of reconciliation activities performed between EDC data and external data sources (central lab, safety database, IRT/RTSM, ePRO). Captures reconciliation type, source system, target system, reconciliation date, discrepancy count, resolved count, outstanding count, responsible data manager, and reconciliation sign-off status. Ensures cross-system data consistency prior to database lock.'
}

Table laboratory.lab_panel {
  lab_panel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory test panel or battery record in the clinical trials lab catalog.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: GCP requires lab panels to be executed per validated SOPs. Traceability from panel definition to its governing compliance SOP is mandatory for audit readiness and inspection. A domain expert would exp']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: PD and PK panels (flagged by is_pd_panel, is_pk_panel, is_biomarker_panel) are designed for specific IMPs. Regulatory submissions and SAPs require traceability of pharmacodynamic/biomarker panels to t']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: IND applications specify required safety laboratory assessments (e.g., FDA-mandated hematology/chemistry panels). Regulatory and lab operations teams must know which IND application mandates each pane']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Lab panels with regulatory_submission_required=true must be traceable to the specific NDA/BLA/IND submission (SDTM LB domain) that includes them. Regulatory affairs teams track which submission valida']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Lab panel method validation reports are essential TMF documents (ICH E6 requires filed analytical method validations). lab_panel.method_validation_status and regulatory_submission_required confirm thi']
  adam_dataset_mapping varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM dataset name to which this panel\'s results are mapped for statistical analysis (e.g., ADLB for laboratory data, ADPC for PK concentration data, ADPP for PK parameter data). Used by SAS Drug Development for ADaM dataset generation.']
  applicable_study_phases varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of clinical trial phases for which this panel is applicable (e.g., \'Phase I|Phase II|Phase III\'). Guides protocol designers in selecting appropriate panels for the study phase. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phase_i|phase_i_ii|phase_ii|phase_ii_iii|phase_iii|phase_iv|first_in_human — promote to reference product]']
  assay_method varchar [note: 'Primary analytical method or technology platform used to perform the panel (e.g., LC-MS/MS, ELISA, Flow Cytometry, Immunoturbidimetry, PCR, Automated Hematology Analyzer). Relevant for bioanalytical method validation per ICH M10.']
  cdisc_lbcat_code varchar [note: 'Standardized CDISC SDTM LBCAT value for this panel\'s category as defined in the CDISC controlled terminology (e.g., \'HEMATOLOGY\', \'CHEMISTRY\', \'URINALYSIS\'). Required for SDTM dataset compliance in regulatory submissions.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of collection tube or container required for specimen collection (e.g., EDTA tube, SST tube, Lithium Heparin tube, Urine cup). Critical for pre-analytical quality and chain of custody. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: edta|sst|lithium_heparin|sodium_citrate|fluoride_oxalate|urine_cup|cryovial — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lab panel definition record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  critical_value_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any analyte in this panel has defined critical (panic) values that require immediate notification to the investigator or site per laboratory SOP. Drives alert workflows in the EDC and CTMS.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this version of the lab panel definition is no longer valid for new protocol inclusions. Null for currently active panel versions. Supports panel retirement and version management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the lab panel definition is valid and available for protocol inclusion. Supports temporal validity management of the panel catalog.']
  fasting_duration_hours decimal(4,1) [note: 'Minimum number of hours the subject must fast before specimen collection when fasting is required. Null when fasting_required is False.']
  fasting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject must be in a fasted state (typically ≥8 hours) prior to specimen collection for this panel. Relevant for lipid panels, glucose, and insulin assays. Drives patient instructions and visit scheduling.']
  is_biomarker_panel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel includes exploratory or validated biomarker assessments (e.g., genomic, proteomic, or metabolomic markers). Biomarker panels may require additional consent and specialized sample handling.']
  is_pd_panel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel is a Pharmacodynamics (PD) panel measuring the biological effect of the drug. PD panels may map to CDISC SDTM LB or FA domains depending on the analyte type.']
  is_pk_panel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel is a Pharmacokinetics (PK) panel used to measure drug concentration over time. PK panels map to CDISC SDTM PC domain and require specialized bioanalytical assay management.']
  is_safety_panel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this panel is designated as a safety monitoring panel (e.g., liver function, renal function, hematology) required for adverse event assessment and DSMB/DMC review.']
  method_validation_status varchar [note: 'Validation status of the analytical method used for this panel per ICH M10 and FDA/EMA guidance. \'validated\' methods have full regulatory-grade validation; \'qualified\' methods have partial validation; \'exploratory\' methods are research-grade only.']
  panel_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the panel by clinical discipline or analyte group (e.g., Hematology, Clinical Chemistry, Coagulation, Urinalysis, Immunology, Pharmacokinetics, Biomarker). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hematology|clinical_chemistry|coagulation|urinalysis|immunology|pharmacokinetics|biomarker|microbiology|serology|endocrinology — promote to reference product]']
  panel_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, sponsor- or CRO-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the lab panel within the study catalog (e.g., CHEM20, CBC, LFT). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and CDISC SDTM LBTESTCD mapping.']
  panel_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the laboratory test panel or battery (e.g., \'Complete Blood Count\', \'Comprehensive Metabolic Panel\', \'Liver Function Tests\'). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBTEST.']
  panel_short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated display name for the panel used in eCRF headers, reports, and dashboards where space is constrained (e.g., \'CBC\', \'CMP\', \'LFT\').']
  panel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lab panel definition in the catalog. \'active\' panels are available for protocol inclusion; \'retired\' panels are no longer used in new studies; \'draft\' panels are pending validation.']
  panel_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the panel category providing finer granularity (e.g., within Clinical Chemistry: \'Liver Function\', \'Renal Function\', \'Lipid Panel\'). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSCAT.']
  reference_range_source varchar [note: 'Source of the normal reference ranges applied to results from this panel. Central lab-derived ranges are preferred for regulatory submissions; sponsor-defined ranges may be used for novel biomarkers.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether results from this panel must be included in regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). Drives data management and CDISC SDTM/ADaM transformation requirements.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code to which this panel\'s results are mapped for regulatory submission (e.g., LB for Laboratory Test Results, PC for Pharmacokinetics Concentrations, PP for Pharmacokinetics Parameters). Required for FDA/EMA eCTD submissions.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Required temperature condition for shipping specimens from the clinical site to the central laboratory. Governs cold chain logistics, packaging requirements, and IATA compliance for biological specimens.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this lab panel definition was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave, Oracle Clinical One, LIMS). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer provenance tracking.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special pre-analytical or handling requirements for the specimen (e.g., \'Protect from light\', \'Process within 30 minutes\', \'Do not freeze\', \'Centrifuge at 2000g for 10 minutes\'). Communicated to site staff via lab manual.']
  specimen_collection_method varchar [note: 'Standardized method used to collect the specimen (e.g., Venipuncture, Finger Stick, Midstream Catch, Lumbar Puncture). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBMETHOD. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venipuncture|finger_stick|midstream_catch|lumbar_puncture|biopsy|swab|catheterization — promote to reference product]']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Primary biological specimen type required for this panel (e.g., Whole Blood, Serum, Plasma, Urine, CSF, Tissue Biopsy). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSPEC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: whole_blood|serum|plasma|urine|csf|tissue|saliva|stool|bone_marrow|synovial_fluid — promote to reference product]']
  specimen_volume_ml decimal(8,3) [note: 'Minimum volume of specimen required in millilitres to perform all analytes in the panel. Used for phlebotomy planning, patient burden assessment, and paediatric study feasibility.']
  stability_hours_ambient decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum number of hours the collected specimen remains analytically stable at ambient temperature (15–25°C) before processing or freezing is required. Critical for chain of custody and pre-analytical quality management.']
  stability_hours_refrigerated decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum number of hours the collected specimen remains analytically stable when stored refrigerated (2–8°C). Used to define shipping and processing windows for central lab specimens.']
  stat_turnaround_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Expedited turnaround time in hours for urgent (STAT) requests, typically triggered by safety events or critical value alerts. Distinct from standard SLA turnaround time.']
  storage_temperature varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature condition for the specimen prior to analysis (e.g., ambient, refrigerated 2–8°C, frozen –20°C, frozen –80°C). Governs cold chain logistics and sample chain of custody.']
  turnaround_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contracted or target turnaround time in hours from specimen receipt at the laboratory to result reporting. Used for SLA monitoring, site management, and safety decision timelines. Critical for SAE-related panels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lab panel definition record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the lab panel definition (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Tracks changes to panel specifications over time to support audit trails and protocol amendment management.']
  visit_timepoints varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of protocol visit names or codes at which this panel is scheduled (e.g., \'Screening, Day 1, Week 4, Week 8, End of Treatment, Follow-Up\'). Supports protocol schedule of assessments design and EDC form configuration.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a laboratory test panel or battery used in clinical trials. Captures panel code, name, specimen type requirements, analyte list, applicable study phases, CDISC SDTM domain mapping (LB), turnaround time SLA, and whether the panel is central or local lab. Serves as the catalog of all lab assessments available for protocol inclusion.'
}

Table laboratory.lab_facility {
  lab_facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory facility record. Primary key for the lab_facility data product. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the laboratory facility. Used for specimen shipment labels, regulatory correspondence, and audit documentation.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, floor, building) for the laboratory facility. Supplements address_line1 for complete mailing and shipment address.']
  biobanking_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility has certified long-term specimen storage (biobanking) capabilities, including controlled-temperature archival storage. Relevant for studies requiring retained samples for future exploratory analyses.']
  cap_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation number issued by the College of American Pathologists (CAP) confirming the laboratory meets rigorous quality standards. Widely recognized by sponsors and regulatory agencies as evidence of laboratory quality. Null if not CAP-accredited.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the laboratory facility is physically located. Used for geographic reporting, logistics planning, and specimen shipment routing.']
  clia_number varchar [note: 'Federal CLIA certificate number issued by CMS to U.S. clinical laboratories authorizing them to perform testing on human specimens. Required for regulatory compliance and sponsor qualification audits. Null for non-U.S. facilities.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the laboratory facility is physically located (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives regulatory jurisdiction determination and applicable accreditation standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory facility record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  dmpk_analysis_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility is equipped and validated to perform Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics (DMPK) analyses, including metabolite profiling and enzyme characterization studies.']
  edi_lab_code varchar [note: 'Standardized laboratory code used in electronic data interchange (EDI) transmissions between the laboratory and EDC/CTMS systems. Aligns with CDISC LAB data standards for lab results transfer.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique business identifier for the laboratory facility (e.g., \'LAB-CENT-001\'). Used in study protocols, eCRFs, and regulatory submissions to reference the facility. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Official legal or operating name of the laboratory facility as registered with accreditation bodies and regulatory authorities. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the laboratory facility by its operational role in clinical trials. Central labs process specimens from multiple sites; local labs are site-affiliated; bioanalytical labs perform assay development and sample analysis; PK/DMPK labs focus on pharmacokinetics and drug metabolism. Serves as CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.']
  flow_cytometry_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility has validated flow cytometry capabilities for immunophenotyping and cell-based assays, commonly required in oncology and immunology trials.']
  gcp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility holds a current Good Clinical Practice (GCP) certification or has been qualified under GCP guidelines by the sponsor or CRO. Required for laboratories participating in regulated clinical trials.']
  genomics_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility is equipped to perform genomic analyses (e.g., PCR, NGS, genotyping) relevant to pharmacogenomics endpoints in clinical trials.']
  glp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility is certified under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations, required for non-clinical safety studies and certain bioanalytical work supporting regulatory submissions.']
  iso_15189_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued by a national accreditation body (e.g., UKAS, DAkkS, COFRAC) confirming the laboratory conforms to ISO 15189 requirements for medical laboratories. Recognized internationally for cross-border clinical trial operations.']
  lims_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this facility within its Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS). Used for electronic data exchange, specimen tracking, and results transmission to EDC systems such as Medidata Rave.']
  lims_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the LIMS software vendor used by the facility (e.g., LabVantage, STARLIMS, LabWare). Relevant for EDC integration planning and data transfer agreement scoping.']
  medical_director_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the licensed physician or doctoral scientist serving as the laboratory\'s Medical Director, as required by CLIA and CAP accreditation. Accountable for overall laboratory quality and regulatory compliance.']
  network_affiliation varchar [note: 'Name of the central laboratory network or parent organization to which this facility belongs (e.g., \'Covance\', \'Q2 Solutions\', \'BioReference\', \'PPD\'). Used for contract management, network-level performance reporting, and escalation routing.']
  normal_hours_of_operation varchar [note: 'Description of the laboratory facility\'s standard operating hours (e.g., \'Mon-Fri 07:00-19:00 EST\'). Used for specimen shipment scheduling, turnaround time SLA definition, and site coordinator guidance.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the laboratory facility indicating whether it is actively accepting specimens for clinical trial studies. Drives eligibility for new study assignments and site qualification workflows. Serves as LIFECYCLE_STATUS per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.']
  pk_analysis_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory facility is equipped and validated to perform pharmacokinetic (PK) sample analysis, including bioanalytical assays for drug concentration measurement.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the laboratory facility\'s physical address. Used for specimen shipment logistics and geographic analysis.']
  preferred_courier varchar [note: 'Name of the preferred or contracted courier service for specimen shipment to this facility (e.g., \'World Courier\', \'Marken\', \'FedEx Custom Critical\'). Used for logistics planning and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary operational contact at the laboratory facility. Used for study coordination communications, specimen shipment notifications, and results transmission alerts.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary operational contact person at the laboratory facility (e.g., Laboratory Director or Operations Manager). Used for study coordination, issue escalation, and regulatory correspondence.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the laboratory facility\'s operational contact. Used for urgent specimen handling queries, critical value notifications, and logistics coordination.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor or CRO qualification of the laboratory facility expires, requiring re-qualification before the facility can be assigned to new studies.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Sponsor or CRO qualification status of the laboratory facility, reflecting the outcome of vendor qualification audits and ongoing performance reviews. Determines whether the facility may be assigned to new studies.']
  regulatory_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional regulatory certifications held by the facility beyond primary accreditation (e.g., \'FDA-registered, EMA-compliant, PMDA-approved, NY State DOH\'). Supports multi-jurisdictional study eligibility assessment.']
  specimen_capacity_per_day varchar [note: 'Maximum number of specimens the facility can process per business day under normal operating conditions. Used for capacity planning, site feasibility assessments, and study logistics. Serves as MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE per MASTER_RESOURCE canonical requirements.']
  specimen_receipt_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format, local facility time) by which specimens must be received to be processed on the same business day. Critical for study protocol logistics and turnaround time commitments.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region in which the laboratory facility is physically located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction mapping and logistics.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the laboratory facility\'s local time (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for specimen receipt timestamp normalization, turnaround time calculations, and critical value notification SLA tracking.']
  turnaround_time_standard_hours varchar [note: 'Standard turnaround time in hours from specimen receipt to results availability for routine panels. Used in study feasibility assessments and service level agreement (SLA) monitoring.']
  turnaround_time_stat_hours varchar [note: 'Turnaround time in hours for STAT (urgent) specimen processing. Used for safety monitoring protocols where rapid results are required for subject safety decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the laboratory facility record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']

  Note: 'Master record for central and local laboratory facilities performing specimen analysis for clinical trials. Captures facility name, type (central/local/bioanalytical/PK), CLIA/CAP/ISO 15189 accreditation status, accreditation expiry, regulatory certifications, geographic location, contact details, LIMS system identifier, and operational status. SSOT for lab facility identity and certification.'
}

Table laboratory.specimen {
  specimen_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each biological specimen record in the laboratory data product. Primary key for the specimen entity.']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the central or local laboratory responsible for analyzing this specimen. Used for lab-level quality monitoring, accreditation tracking, and result attribution.']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: A specimen is collected for a specific laboratory panel (e.g., a CBC panel, chemistry panel). The assay_panel_code STRING column on specimen is a denormalized reference to lab_panel. Adding lab_panel_']
  parent_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent specimen from which this aliquot was derived. Null for primary specimens. Enables full chain-of-custody traceability from primary collection through all downstream aliquots.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record under which this specimen was transported to the central laboratory or biorepository. Enables chain-of-custody tracking and cold-chain compliance verification.']
  specimen_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.specimen_collection. Business justification: A specimen is produced by a specimen collection event. The specimen_collection table records the scheduled or unscheduled collection event, while specimen records the physical biological material coll']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical site where the specimen was collected. Used for site-level quality monitoring and logistics.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Specimens are collected at protocol-defined visits; linking to the visit schedule enables deviation detection (collection outside visit window), SDTM domain mapping, and protocol compliance reporting.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which this specimen was collected. Enables visit-level specimen tracking.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (participant) from whom this specimen was collected. Core linkage for subject-level traceability.']
  actual_timepoint_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Actual elapsed time in hours from the reference event (e.g., dose administration) to specimen collection. Critical for pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) analysis and nominal time deviation assessment.']
  aliquot_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the order of this aliquot within the set of aliquots derived from the same parent specimen (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Null for primary specimens. Used for aliquot management and laboratory workflow.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Unique barcode or label identifier affixed to the specimen container at the point of collection. Used for physical tracking and LIMS accession matching. Serves as the primary operational identifier in the laboratory.']
  chain_of_custody_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the specimen\'s chain of custody documentation. \'Intact\' indicates unbroken custody from collection to current location; \'broken\' indicates a gap requiring investigation. Critical for regulatory and legal defensibility of specimen data.']
  collection_body_site varchar [note: 'Anatomical body location from which the specimen was collected (e.g., left antecubital vein, right arm, lumbar region). Required for specimens where site of collection affects result interpretation.']
  collection_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen was collected from the subject. Principal real-world event timestamp for the specimen. Used for PK/PD time-point calculations, protocol compliance, and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  collection_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation occurred during specimen collection (True) or not (False). Triggers deviation documentation workflow and may affect specimen usability for primary analysis.']
  collection_kit_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the collection kit used for specimen collection. Required for traceability of kit materials, deviation investigation, and supply chain accountability.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'Procedure or technique used to collect the specimen (e.g., venipuncture, fingerstick, lumbar puncture, biopsy, swab). Affects pre-analytical processing and result interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venipuncture|fingerstick|catheter|lumbar_puncture|biopsy|swab|midstream_void|other — promote to reference product]']
  collection_tube_type varchar [note: 'Type of collection tube or container used (e.g., EDTA, SST, lithium heparin, sodium citrate, PAXgene). Critical for pre-analytical integrity and analyte stability. Aligns with laboratory manual specifications.']
  condition_on_arrival varchar [note: 'Assessment of the specimen\'s physical condition upon receipt at the laboratory (e.g., acceptable, hemolyzed, lipemic, temperature excursion). Determines whether the specimen is suitable for analysis or requires rejection/re-collection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acceptable|hemolyzed|lipemic|icteric|insufficient_volume|temperature_excursion|damaged|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this specimen record was first created in the system. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, and data lineage tracking.']
  destruction_date date [note: 'Date on which the specimen was destroyed or disposed of, in accordance with the protocol-defined retention period and applicable regulations. Required for regulatory accountability and chain-of-custody closure.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specimen within the laboratory workflow. Drives operational actions and regulatory accountability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending_receipt|received|in_analysis|analyzed|stored|shipped|destroyed|rejected|lost — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the specimen is no longer considered analytically valid for its intended assay(s). Determined by specimen type, storage conditions, and analyte stability data. Used to flag specimens requiring re-collection or exclusion.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Fasting status of the subject at the time of specimen collection (fasted, non-fasted, unknown). Required for metabolic panels, lipid profiles, and glucose assays where fasting state affects reference ranges.']
  freeze_thaw_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the specimen has undergone. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles can degrade analyte integrity; this field is used to assess specimen quality and flag specimens that may yield unreliable results.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specimen record represents the primary (original) specimen (True) or an aliquot derived from a parent specimen (False). Drives chain-of-custody logic and parent-child specimen relationships.']
  lims_accession_number varchar [note: 'Accession number assigned by the Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) upon receipt of the specimen at the central or local laboratory. Used for laboratory workflow tracking and result linkage.']
  pre_analytical_processing varchar [note: 'Description of pre-analytical processing steps performed on the specimen prior to analysis or storage (e.g., centrifugation speed/duration, temperature, time to processing). Critical for result interpretation and protocol compliance.']
  processing_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which pre-analytical processing of the specimen was completed. Used to calculate time-from-collection-to-processing, a key pre-analytical quality metric per laboratory manual requirements.']
  receipt_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the specimen was received at the central or local laboratory. Used to calculate transit time, assess cold-chain compliance, and trigger LIMS accession workflows.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for specimen rejection when disposition_status is \'rejected\' (e.g., insufficient volume, wrong tube type, temperature excursion, unlabelled, clotted). Required for deviation tracking and re-collection decisions.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Biological matrix type of the specimen collected from the subject (e.g., blood, urine, plasma, serum, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), tissue). Drives pre-analytical processing rules and reference range selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|urine|plasma|serum|csf|tissue|saliva|stool|other — promote to reference product]']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location of the specimen within the laboratory or biorepository (e.g., freezer ID, rack, box, position). Required for specimen retrieval, inventory management, and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required storage temperature for this specimen in degrees Celsius (e.g., -80, -20, 2-8, ambient). Used to verify cold-chain compliance and specimen integrity throughout the chain of custody.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable subject identifier (e.g., site-subject number) as assigned in the clinical trial management system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and regulatory reporting. Classified as restricted PII as it can identify a trial participant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this specimen record was most recently modified. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail, 21 CFR Part 11 compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  volume_collected_ml decimal(10,4) [note: 'Volume of biological material collected from the subject, expressed in millilitres (mL). Used to verify adequacy for planned assays, assess protocol compliance, and support chain-of-custody documentation.']
