  Note: 'Master record for each biological specimen collected from a trial subject, including collection event details. Captures specimen barcode/ID, subject ID, visit reference, collection date/time, specimen type (blood, urine, CSF, tissue), collection tube type, volume collected, collector ID, collection site (body location), collection method, collection kit lot, pre-analytical processing performed, collection deviations, primary/aliquot flag, parent specimen reference, receipt status, condition on arrival, LIMS accession number, and current disposition status. SSOT for specimen identity, collection event, receipt, and chain of custody.'
}

Table laboratory.lab_result {
  lab_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each laboratory test result record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the lab_result data product.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: Safety lab results must be analyzed by treatment arm for DSMB safety reviews, regulatory submissions, and ADaM ADLB dataset generation. SDTM LB domain includes ARM variable; arm-level lab safety summa']
  critical_value_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.critical_value. Business justification: When a lab result triggers a critical value alert (critical_value_flag = TRUE), it should reference the specific critical_value threshold definition that was breached. Adding critical_value_id creates']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: Lab results are graded per CTCAE criteria to determine AE reporting obligations. Formalizing lab_result→ctcae_grade supports the regulatory process of identifying reportable lab-based AEs. Domain expe']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Lab results are locked at database lock; each lab_result must be attributable to the database_lock_event that froze it for statistical analysis. This supports audit trails for regulatory submissions, ']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: For subjects with multiple enrollment episodes (screen failure then re-screen), lab_result must be tied to the correct enrollment to support accurate population assignment (ITT/PP/safety) and SDTM LB ']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: SDTM LB domain requires epoch assignment for all lab results. Safety monitoring by epoch (screening vs treatment vs follow-up) is standard in DSMB reports and regulatory submissions. ADaM ADLB dataset']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the central or local laboratory that performed the analysis and reported this result. Supports lab certification and accreditation tracking.']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory panel definition that groups this analyte test within a scheduled assessment (e.g., hematology panel, chemistry panel, urinalysis).']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Lab results must be evaluated against protocol-defined CTCAE grading rules and reference ranges for the specific protocol version. SDTM LB domain submissions require protocol traceability. Regulatory ']
  reference_range_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.reference_range. Business justification: A lab result is evaluated against a specific reference range definition. The reference_range_source STRING column is a denormalized pointer to the reference_range master. Adding reference_range_id cre']
  specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological specimen from which this laboratory result was derived. Links to the specimen/sample chain-of-custody record.']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: Lab results are the primary source observations for statistical endpoints (e.g., hematology abnormality rates, liver enzyme elevations). Regulatory submissions require traceability from individual lab']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this laboratory result was generated. Enables study-level aggregation and regulatory submission.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: ICH E6 GCP and regulatory submissions require lab results to be traceable to the protocol amendment version active at collection time. This determines which reference ranges, eligibility criteria, and']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the specimen was collected or the local laboratory is located.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Lab results must be mapped to protocol-defined visit definitions for SDTM LB domain generation, visit-window compliance reporting, and primary endpoint analysis. Linking results to the scheduled visit']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: SDTM LB domain and ADaM safety analysis require lab results linked to treatment arm assignment. DSMB safety reviews and treatment-arm-stratified safety lab reporting (ICH E3, CDISC standards) depend o']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the specimen was collected. Supports visit-level data review and SDTM VISITNUM mapping.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) from whom the specimen was collected. Core linkage for subject-level safety and efficacy analysis.']
  collection_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the specimen was collected from the subject. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBDTC. Principal business event timestamp for the laboratory result. Critical for PK/PD analysis, time-from-dose calculations, and protocol deviation assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this laboratory result record was first created in the data management system. Audit trail field required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  critical_value_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result meets the laboratory\'s critical (panic) value threshold requiring immediate clinical notification. True triggers safety review workflow and potential SAE/AE assessment per protocol.']
  data_origin varchar [note: 'Source system or method by which this laboratory result was entered into the data management system. EDC: entered via Medidata Rave eCRF; LAB_DIRECT: electronic lab data transfer; MANUAL: manual data entry; MIGRATION: legacy data migration; BIOANALYTICAL: bioanalytical lab system feed.']
  dbl_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result was included in the locked database at the time of Database Lock (DBL). False indicates the result was added or modified post-lock (requires formal database unlock procedure). Critical for regulatory submission integrity.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was fasting at the time of specimen collection. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBFAST. Critical for metabolic analytes (glucose, lipids) where fasting status affects reference ranges and result interpretation.']
  lbcat varchar [note: 'High-level CDISC SDTM category grouping the laboratory test (e.g., HEMATOLOGY, CHEMISTRY, URINALYSIS, COAGULATION, IMMUNOLOGY). Used for data review panels and SDTM LB dataset partitioning.']
  lbscat varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM subcategory providing finer classification within LBCAT (e.g., LIVER FUNCTION TESTS within CHEMISTRY, RED BLOOD CELL within HEMATOLOGY). Supports granular data review and reporting.']
  method varchar [note: 'Analytical method or instrument used to perform the laboratory test (e.g., ENZYMATIC, IMMUNOASSAY, FLOW CYTOMETRY, PCR, HPLC). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBMETHOD. Relevant for bioanalytical assay validation and cross-lab comparability.']
  normal_range_lower decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the laboratory\'s normal reference range for this analyte, in standard units. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSTNRLO. Used for out-of-range flagging and shift table generation.']
  normal_range_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the laboratory\'s normal reference range for this analyte, in standard units. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSTNRHI. Used for out-of-range flagging and shift table generation.']
  out_of_range_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of whether the result falls outside the normal reference range. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBNRIND. Values: NORMAL (within range), LOW (below lower bound), HIGH (above upper bound), LOW LOW (critically low), HIGH HIGH (critically high).']
  query_open_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether there is at least one open data query (discrepancy) associated with this laboratory result in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Supports data management workflow and DBL readiness tracking.']
  result_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment from the laboratory or data manager regarding this result (e.g., hemolyzed sample, lipemic specimen, instrument flag, repeat analysis note). Supports data review and query resolution.']
  result_received_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory result was received by the sponsor/CRO data management system (EDC or CTMS). Used for turnaround time monitoring and critical value notification tracking.']
  result_reported_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the laboratory formally reported and released the result. Distinct from collection and receipt timestamps. Used for lab turnaround time KPI calculation and audit trail.']
  result_seq_num varchar [note: 'Sequential number identifying the order of this result record within the subject\'s laboratory data. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSEQ variable for dataset generation.']
  result_standard_unit varchar [note: 'Standardized unit of measure for the numeric result value after conversion. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESU. Enables cross-laboratory and cross-study comparisons in statistical analysis.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the laboratory result indicating its data quality and review state. PRELIMINARY: initial unverified result; FINAL: verified and released; CORRECTED: amended after final release; CANCELLED: voided result; AMENDED: modified with audit trail.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the original reported result value as received from the laboratory. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBORRESU (e.g., g/dL, mmol/L, U/L, 10^9/L).']
  result_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original reported result value as received from the laboratory, stored as STRING to accommodate numeric, text, and operator-prefixed values (e.g., \'<0.5\', \'>1000\', \'POSITIVE\', \'NEG\'). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBORRES.']
  result_value_numeric decimal(18,6) [note: 'Standardized numeric result value after unit conversion to the standard unit. NULL for non-numeric results. Maps to CDISC SDTM LBSTRESN. Used for statistical analysis, shift tables, and CTCAE grading.']
  sdv_status varchar [note: 'Status of source data verification (SDV) for this laboratory result, performed by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) against source documents. Supports risk-based monitoring and GCP compliance.']
  study_day varchar [note: 'Relative study day of specimen collection, calculated from the subject\'s reference start date (Day 1 = first dose). Maps to CDISC SDTM LBDY. Negative values indicate pre-treatment assessments. Used for time-course analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this laboratory result record was last modified. Audit trail field required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. Tracks corrections, amendments, and status changes throughout the data lifecycle.']
  usubjid varchar [note: 'Globally unique subject identifier in the format STUDYID-SITEID-SUBJID, as defined by CDISC SDTM. Required for regulatory submission datasets and cross-study subject traceability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a laboratory test result for a specific analyte from a subject specimen. Captures analyte name, LOINC code, result value, result unit, normal range lower/upper bound, reference range source, result status (preliminary/final/corrected/cancelled), out-of-range flag, critical value flag, CTCAE grade, CDISC SDTM LBTESTCD/LBTEST mapping, and reporting lab. Core entity for clinical data review, safety monitoring, and SDTM LB dataset generation.'
}

Table laboratory.specimen_collection {
  specimen_collection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each specimen collection event record in the clinical trial laboratory domain. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: Safety and PD specimen collections are arm-specific in randomized trials. Arm-level collection compliance reporting, DSMB safety reviews, and SDTM LB domain generation require linking collections to t']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the eCRF (Electronic Case Report Form) form in the EDC system (Medidata Rave) where this specimen collection event was recorded. Supports data lineage and source data verification (SDV).']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: SDTM LB dataset construction and enrollment-episode reconciliation require linking specimen collections to the specific enrollment record. For re-screened subjects with multiple enrollment episodes, t']
  lab_kit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_kit. Business justification: A specimen collection event uses a specific laboratory collection kit supplied to the site. The collection_kit_lot_number and collection_kit_expiry_date on specimen_collection are denormalized from th']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protocol-defined laboratory panel or test battery that this specimen collection is intended to fulfill.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Specimen collections must be evaluated against the protocol version in effect at time of collection for protocol deviation assessment and regulatory audit. When amendments change collection requiremen']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this specimen collection event occurred. Links the collection to the governing protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: When protocol amendments change specimen collection requirements, collections after the amendment effective date follow new rules. Tracing collections to the governing amendment version is required fo']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical site (investigative site) where the specimen collection event took place.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Specimen collections are planned against protocol-defined visit schedules. Linking actual collections to the scheduled visit definition enables visit-window deviation detection, protocol deviation rep']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Specimen collection compliance requires confirming subject\'s randomization status and treatment arm at collection time — especially for PK/PD assessments where collection timing is dose-relative. Prot']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the specimen was collected. Links collection to the visit schedule.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) from whom the specimen was collected. Core party reference for this transactional event.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'Barcode or unique label identifier affixed to the specimen tube or container at the time of collection. Used for automated specimen tracking and laboratory information system (LIS) integration.']
  chain_of_custody_number varchar [note: 'Unique tracking number assigned to document the chain of custody for the specimen from collection through laboratory receipt. Ensures specimen integrity and traceability for regulatory and legal purposes.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The clinical method or procedure used to collect the specimen from the subject (e.g., venipuncture, fingerstick, lumbar puncture, biopsy). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBMETHOD. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venipuncture|fingerstick|catheter|lumbar_puncture|biopsy|swab|urine_void|other — promote to reference product]']
  collection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this specimen collection event, typically generated by the EDC or CTMS (e.g., \'SC-0042-001\'). Used for cross-system traceability and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  collection_site_anatomy varchar [note: 'The anatomical location on the subject\'s body from which the specimen was collected (e.g., \'Left Antecubital Vein\', \'Right Arm\', \'Lumbar Region\'). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBLOC.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specimen collection event. Indicates whether the collection was completed as planned, partially completed, missed, or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|collected|missed|cancelled|partial|failed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time at which the specimen was collected from the subject. This is the principal real-world event timestamp per ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) requirements. Distinct from scheduled collection time.']
  collector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the CRC or phlebotomist who performed the specimen collection. Denormalized for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness. Classified as confidential as it identifies site personnel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specimen collection record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  fasting_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the subject was in a fasted state at the time of specimen collection, as required by certain protocol-defined laboratory assessments (e.g., lipid panels, glucose). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBFAST.']
  has_deviation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation or collection irregularity was recorded for this specimen collection event. True = deviation present; False = no deviation. Triggers downstream deviation management workflow.']
  hemolysis_grade varchar [note: 'Visual or instrument-assessed grade of hemolysis (red blood cell rupture) in the collected blood specimen. Hemolysis can interfere with laboratory assay results and may require recollection.']
  is_unscheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specimen collection was an unscheduled (ad hoc) event not defined in the original study protocol visit schedule. True = unscheduled; False = protocol-scheduled.']
  lipemia_grade varchar [note: 'Visual or instrument-assessed grade of lipemia (excess lipids causing turbidity) in the collected specimen. Lipemia can interfere with photometric assay results.']
  nominal_time_point varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined nominal time point label for PK/PD sample collection (e.g., \'Pre-dose\', \'0.5h\', \'1h\', \'2h\', \'4h\', \'8h\', \'24h\'). Used to align actual collection times to protocol-scheduled PK sampling windows.']
  pre_analytical_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded description of the pre-analytical processing instructions followed after collection (e.g., \'Centrifuge within 30 min at 2000 rpm\', \'Store at -80°C\', \'Protect from light\'). Documents protocol compliance for sample integrity.']
  processing_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which pre-analytical processing of the specimen was completed at the collection site (e.g., centrifugation, aliquoting). Used to calculate processing turnaround time and assess sample integrity.']
  recollection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why a repeat specimen collection is required. Populated when recollection_required = True. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: insufficient_volume|hemolysis|lipemia|clotted|mislabeled|lost_in_transit|expired_kit|other — promote to reference product]']
  recollection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a repeat specimen collection is required due to inadequate sample, collection deviation, or laboratory rejection. True = recollection needed; False = no recollection required.']
  sample_adequacy varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the collected specimen volume and quality are sufficient to perform all protocol-required laboratory analyses. Determined at the time of collection or upon receipt at the laboratory.']
  scheduled_collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The protocol-defined date and time at which the specimen collection was planned to occur per the study visit schedule. Used to calculate collection timing deviations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this specimen collection record originated (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC, Oracle Clinical One). Supports data lineage and integration traceability.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the biological specimen collected (e.g., \'Whole Blood\', \'Serum\', \'Plasma\', \'Urine\', \'CSF\', \'Tissue Biopsy\', \'Saliva\'). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM LBSPEC. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Whole Blood|Serum|Plasma|Urine|CSF|Tissue Biopsy|Saliva|Stool|Bone Marrow — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius at which the specimen was stored at the collection site prior to shipment to the central laboratory. Deviations from protocol-specified storage conditions may compromise sample integrity.']
  time_from_dose_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in hours between the subject\'s most recent investigational product dose and the specimen collection. Critical for PK (Pharmacokinetics) and DMPK (Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics) sample timing analysis.']
  tube_count varchar [note: 'The total number of tubes, vials, or containers collected during this specimen collection event. Used for chain-of-custody tracking and sample accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specimen collection record was last modified. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  volume_collected_ml decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of specimen collected from the subject, expressed in millilitres (mL). Critical for PK/PD studies and for verifying adequacy of sample for planned assays.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume collected. Typically mL for blood/urine, but may be µL for microsampling or g for tissue. Ensures unambiguous interpretation of volume_collected_ml.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a scheduled or unscheduled specimen collection event at a clinical site or subject visit. Captures collection event date/time, study visit reference, protocol-scheduled vs. unscheduled flag, collector ID (CRC/phlebotomist), collection kit lot number, number of tubes/containers collected, collection deviations, and pre-analytical processing instructions followed. Links specimen collection to the study visit schedule.'
}

Table laboratory.specimen_shipment {
  specimen_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the specimen shipment record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Cross-border specimen shipments require a valid CTA and import permit in the destination country. specimen_shipment.import_permit_number is a denormalized CTA reference. Regulatory operations teams mu']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.investigational_site. Business justification: Specimen logistics operations require direct linkage to the originating investigational site for courier scheduling, address resolution, and time-zone-aware pickup windows. origin_site_number is a den']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination central or reference laboratory facility receiving the shipment. Supports routing and receipt confirmation.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which the specimens were collected and shipped. Links shipment to the sponsoring protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical site (origin) from which the specimens were dispatched. Supports chain-of-custody traceability back to the collecting site.']
  actual_receipt_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the receiving laboratory confirmed physical receipt of the shipment. Used to calculate transit duration and assess specimen integrity against stability windows.']
  airbill_number varchar [note: 'Air waybill or bill of lading number issued by the carrier for international or air freight shipments. Required for customs clearance and IATA dangerous goods documentation.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the specimens included in this shipment were collected from study subjects. Used to calculate specimen age at receipt and assess stability compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or observations recorded by site staff, courier, or laboratory personnel regarding the shipment (e.g., packaging condition on arrival, partial delivery notes).']
  courier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or courier service responsible for transporting the shipment (e.g., World Courier, FedEx Clinical, Marken). Supports vendor performance tracking.']
  courier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier selected for the shipment (e.g., overnight, two-day, international priority). Determines expected transit time and cost classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this specimen shipment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the customs or import/export declaration filed for international specimen shipments. Required for cross-border regulatory compliance and specimen release at destination.']
  destination_lab_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the receiving central or reference laboratory. Denormalized for manifest documentation and rapid chain-of-custody reporting.']
  dry_ice_weight_kg decimal(6,3) [note: 'Weight of dry ice (in kilograms) included in the shipment package. Required for IATA dangerous goods declaration when dry ice is used as a refrigerant. Applicable only when temperature_condition = dry_ice.']
  excursion_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration in minutes during which the shipment temperature was outside the acceptable range. Used by the laboratory to assess specimen viability and document deviation impact.']
  excursion_max_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Highest temperature (in degrees Celsius) recorded by the temperature monitor/data logger during transit. Populated only when a temperature excursion is detected. Supports specimen impact assessment.']
  excursion_min_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lowest temperature (in degrees Celsius) recorded by the temperature monitor/data logger during transit. Populated only when a temperature excursion is detected. Supports specimen impact assessment.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Planned date on which the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination laboratory, as agreed with the courier at time of dispatch. Used to trigger overdue alerts.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains materials classified as hazardous (e.g., Category B biological substances, dry ice). True = hazardous declaration required; False = non-hazardous. Drives IATA dangerous goods documentation requirements.']
  lab_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the designated contact person at the receiving laboratory responsible for accepting and processing the shipment. Used for courier delivery confirmation and discrepancy resolution.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the shipment, indicating the thermal protection and containment method. Relevant for regulatory compliance with IATA P650 and specimen integrity documentation.']
  protocol_visit_name varchar [note: 'Name or label of the study protocol visit (e.g., Day 1, Week 4, End of Treatment) associated with the specimen collection event. Supports SDTM domain mapping and visit-level reconciliation.']
  receipt_confirmed_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the laboratory staff member who confirmed receipt of the shipment. Part of the chain-of-custody audit trail per ALCOA+ principles.']
  received_specimen_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of specimens confirmed by the receiving laboratory upon opening the shipment. Compared against specimen_count to identify discrepancies or missing specimens.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the shipment was rejected at the receiving laboratory (e.g., temperature excursion, broken specimens, incorrect labeling, missing manifest). Populated only when shipment_status = rejected.']
  shipment_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the shipment was handed over to the courier at the originating clinical site. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the specimen shipment. Drives chain-of-custody workflow and exception management. Values: pending (prepared, not yet dispatched), in_transit (with courier), delivered (courier confirmed delivery), received (lab confirmed receipt), rejected (refused at destination), cancelled (shipment voided).']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment by its clinical purpose. Values: routine (standard scheduled collection), urgent (expedited for safety or protocol reasons), pk_sample (Pharmacokinetics samples requiring strict timing), biomarker (exploratory biomarker specimens), backup (duplicate aliquots for archival), reshipment (replacement for a failed prior shipment).']
  specimen_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual specimen tubes, aliquots, or containers included in this shipment as declared on the manifest. Used for reconciliation at receipt.']
  subject_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct study subjects whose specimens are included in this shipment. Supports subject-level reconciliation and enrollment tracking at the laboratory.']
  temperature_condition varchar [note: 'Required temperature storage and transport condition for the shipment as specified in the laboratory manual. Critical for specimen integrity. Values: ambient (15–25°C), refrigerated (2–8°C), frozen (-20°C), dry_ice (-78°C), liquid_nitrogen (-196°C).']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation outside the acceptable range) was detected during transit. True = excursion occurred; False = no excursion. Triggers specimen usability assessment workflow.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the acceptable temperature range (in degrees Celsius) for this shipment as defined in the study laboratory manual. Used to evaluate temperature excursion severity.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the acceptable temperature range (in degrees Celsius) for this shipment as defined in the study laboratory manual. Used to evaluate temperature excursion severity.']
  temperature_monitor_code varchar [note: 'Serial number or identifier of the temperature data logger or indicator device placed in the shipment. Enables retrieval of the full temperature log for excursion investigation and regulatory documentation.']
  temperature_monitor_type varchar [note: 'Type of temperature monitoring device included in the shipment. Data_logger provides continuous electronic recording; indicator_card provides a visual pass/fail indicator; none indicates no monitoring device was used.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment package, enabling real-time logistics monitoring and proof-of-delivery confirmation.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations dangerous goods classification number for the shipment contents (e.g., UN3373 for Category B biological substances, UN1845 for dry ice). Required on shipping labels and documentation when hazardous_material_flag is True.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this specimen shipment record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks lakehouse.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the shipment of specimens from a clinical site to a central or reference laboratory. Captures shipment manifest ID, origin site, destination lab facility, courier name, tracking number, shipment date/time, expected arrival date, actual receipt date, temperature condition (ambient/refrigerated/frozen/dry ice), number of specimens, shipment status, and any temperature excursion flags. Supports chain of custody and specimen integrity tracking.'
}

Table laboratory.reference_range {
  reference_range_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each reference range record in the laboratory master. Primary key for the reference_range data product.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: Reference range establishment, approval, and version changes must follow SOPs per GCP requirements. Regulatory submissions require documentation that reference ranges were set per validated procedures']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: Reference ranges define CTCAE grading thresholds (Grade 1-4 numeric cutoffs are present as columns). Linking to the ctcae_grade master ensures consistent grading criteria between lab reference ranges ']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the central or local laboratory facility that owns and applies this reference range. Links to the lab facility master.']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: Reference ranges are often defined in the context of a specific lab panel (e.g., a chemistry panel has specific normal ranges for its analytes). The panel_code STRING column on reference_range is a de']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Sponsor-defined reference ranges are specified per protocol version for SDTM/ADaM dataset generation. Protocol-version-specific normal range application is required for regulatory submissions; the exi']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: FDA/EMA submissions require that reference ranges used for out-of-range flagging and CTCAE grading match those specified in the SAP. Linking reference_range to the governing SAP version enables audit ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical study for which this reference range is specifically applicable. Null indicates a lab-standard range applicable across all studies at the facility.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Reference ranges are versioned when protocol amendments change lab parameters, eligibility thresholds, or CTCAE grading criteria. Linking reference_range to study_protocol_amendment is required for re']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Sponsor-defined reference ranges are essential TMF documents filed for regulatory submissions and inspections. reference_range.regulatory_submission_required and submission_id confirm regulatory filin']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Arm-specific reference ranges are required in dose-escalation and oncology trials where different treatment arms have distinct normal value thresholds. DSMB safety reports and unblinded lab analyses s']
  age_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum age in years of the subject population for which this reference range applies. Null indicates no upper age bound.']
  age_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum age in years of the subject population for which this reference range applies. Used to stratify ranges by pediatric, adult, or geriatric populations.']
  age_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the age range fields (age_min, age_max). Defaults to years for adults; may be months, weeks, or days for neonatal or pediatric populations.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval lifecycle status of this reference range version. Only approved ranges are used for out-of-range flagging and CTCAE grading in clinical trial lab results.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reference range version was formally approved by the authorized laboratory or sponsor personnel. Part of the GCP-required audit trail.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Coded reason explaining why this reference range version was created or updated. Required for GCP audit trail. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: instrument_change|reagent_lot_change|population_recalibration|method_change|accreditation_update|sponsor_request|error_correction|other — promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional detail on the reason for the reference range update. Supplements the coded change_reason field for regulatory audit trail documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reference range record was first created in the system. Part of the mandatory GCP audit trail for laboratory data per ALCOA+ principles.']
  critical_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'The critical (panic) high threshold for the analyte. Results at or above this value require immediate clinical notification per laboratory SOP and GCP safety reporting requirements.']
  critical_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'The critical (panic) low threshold for the analyte. Results at or below this value require immediate clinical notification per laboratory SOP and GCP safety reporting requirements.']
  ctcae_grade1_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 1 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.']
  ctcae_grade2_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 2 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.']
  ctcae_grade3_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 3 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.']
  ctcae_grade4_upper decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper boundary value corresponding to CTCAE Grade 4 toxicity for this analyte. Used for automated CTCAE grading of laboratory results in safety and adverse event reporting.']
  ctcae_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CTCAE grading criteria used to define the CTCAE grade thresholds for this analyte (e.g., 5.0, 4.03). Required for regulatory submissions and safety reporting consistency.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this reference range version ceased to be effective. Null indicates the range is currently active. Populated when a new version supersedes this record.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this reference range version became or becomes effective for use in clinical trial lab result evaluation and out-of-range flagging.']
  ethnicity_applicability varchar [note: 'Ethnic or racial population for which this reference range is specifically calibrated (e.g., ALL for universal, or specific population codes per CDISC). Null or ALL indicates the range applies universally regardless of ethnicity.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific laboratory instrument or analyzer for which this reference range was established. Instrument-specific ranges are required when different analyzers produce systematically different results.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the currently active version of the reference range for the given analyte, lab, and stratification combination. True = current; False = historical version.']
  is_sponsor_defined boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this reference range was explicitly defined or mandated by the clinical trial sponsor, as opposed to being the laboratory\'s standard range. Sponsor-defined ranges take precedence for trial-specific out-of-range flagging.']
  lab_accreditation_standard varchar [note: 'Accreditation standard under which the laboratory facility that established this reference range is certified (e.g., CAP, CLIA, ISO 15189). Relevant for regulatory submissions and sponsor qualification of central labs.']
  loinc_code varchar [note: 'LOINC code uniquely identifying the laboratory observation. Used for interoperability across lab systems, CDISC submissions, and regulatory reporting.']
  lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower boundary of the normal reference range for the analyte. Values below this threshold are flagged as abnormally low. Corresponds to LBORNRLO in CDISC SDTM.']
  method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the analytical method or instrument platform used to generate results evaluated against this reference range (e.g., colorimetric, immunoassay, HPLC). Reference ranges may differ by method.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this reference range record was last modified. Part of the mandatory GCP audit trail for laboratory data per ALCOA+ principles.']
  prior_lower_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower normal limit value from the immediately preceding version of this reference range. Retained for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per GCP requirements.']
  prior_upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper normal limit value from the immediately preceding version of this reference range. Retained for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per GCP requirements.']
  range_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this reference range is sponsor-defined (provided by the clinical trial sponsor), lab-standard (established by the laboratory facility), derived from a regulatory guideline, or from published literature.']
  reagent_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the reagent kit or calibrator associated with this reference range version. Captured when a reagent lot change triggers a reference range update per laboratory SOPs.']
  sex_applicability varchar [note: 'Biological sex for which this reference range is applicable. Use M for male, F for female, BOTH for sex-independent ranges, or UNKNOWN when sex stratification is not defined.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this reference range was sourced or ingested (e.g., Medidata Rave, Oracle Clinical One, central lab LIMS). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen for which this reference range is applicable (e.g., serum, plasma, whole blood, urine). Corresponds to LBSPEC in CDISC SDTM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: serum|plasma|whole_blood|urine|csf|tissue|saliva|other — promote to reference product]']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measurement for the reference range limits and analyte result (e.g., g/dL, mmol/L, U/L, mg/dL). Corresponds to LBORRESU in CDISC SDTM. Should align with UCUM (Unified Code for Units of Measure).']
  upper_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper boundary of the normal reference range for the analyte. Values above this threshold are flagged as abnormally high. Corresponds to LBORNRHI in CDISC SDTM.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer version number of this reference range definition. Incremented each time the range is updated. Supports full version history and audit trail per GCP requirements.']

  Note: 'Master definition of laboratory reference ranges (normal ranges) for analytes, with full version history. Captures analyte/LOINC code, lower and upper normal limits, unit of measure, sex, age range, ethnicity applicability, specimen type, lab facility source, effective date range, version number, prior range values, reason for update (instrument change/reagent lot change/population recalibration), approval status, and whether the range is sponsor-defined or lab-standard. Used for out-of-range flagging and CTCAE grading in lab results. Maintains audit trail for reference range changes per GCP and regulatory requirements.'
}

Table laboratory.critical_value {
  critical_value_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each critical (panic) value threshold record in the laboratory reference master.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: Critical value notification procedures are operationalized through SOPs defining escalation pathways and SLA timelines. critical_value has escalation_pathway and notification_sla_minutes. Linking to t']
  ctcae_grade_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.ctcae_grade. Business justification: Critical value thresholds are defined in terms of CTCAE grades to determine AE reporting requirements (ae_reporting_required flag). Linking critical_value to ctcae_grade formalizes the threshold-to-gr']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Critical value thresholds are defined per regulatory authority guidelines (FDA CTCAE grading, EMA safety reporting requirements). The critical_value table has regulatory_authority_basis as plain text.']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: IMP-specific toxicity profiles require IMP-specific critical value thresholds (e.g., hepatotoxic IMPs have tighter ALT critical limits). Safety monitoring plans and pharmacovigilance SOPs define criti']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the specific laboratory facility to which this critical value threshold applies, when lab_facility_scope is \'specific_facility\'. Null when the threshold applies globally or to a facility class.']
  meddra_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.meddra_term. Business justification: Critical lab values trigger AE reporting; the MedDRA coding of the lab finding is required for ICSR and expedited report submissions. Linking critical_value to meddra_term formalizes the MedDRA classi']
  superseded_by_critical_value_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the critical_value_id of the newer threshold record that replaces this one when threshold_status is \'superseded\'. Enables version chain traceability for audit and regulatory inspection.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Critical value threshold definitions and notification procedures are regulatory-required documents filed in the eTMF. critical_value.source_reference_document is a denormalized text reference to this ']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: In dose-escalation and oncology studies, critical value alert thresholds are arm-specific — higher-dose arms require tighter critical value limits per regulatory safety monitoring plans. Linking criti']
  ae_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a critical value breach automatically triggers an Adverse Event (AE) or Serious Adverse Event (SAE) report in Oracle Argus Safety. True = AE/SAE workflow initiated; False = clinical notification only.']
  age_group_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies the patient age group for which this critical value threshold applies. Different thresholds may be defined for pediatric, adult, and geriatric populations.']
  analyte_category varchar [note: 'Clinical category of the analyte (e.g., Hematology, Chemistry, Coagulation, PK/PD, DMPK). Used for grouping critical value alerts by clinical domain and routing to appropriate clinical reviewers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hematology|chemistry|coagulation|urinalysis|microbiology|immunology|pk_pd|dmpk|other — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this critical value threshold was formally approved by the designated clinical reviewer. Required for GCP audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  argus_safety_rule_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding automated safety rule configured in Oracle Argus Safety that is triggered when this critical value threshold is breached. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lab threshold master and the safety system.']
  bioanalytical_method varchar [note: 'Name or code of the validated bioanalytical method used to measure the analyte (e.g., LC-MS/MS, ELISA, flow cytometry). Relevant for PK/PD and DMPK analytes where method-specific critical thresholds may apply.']
  clinical_rationale varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the clinical or scientific rationale for the defined critical value thresholds. Documents the medical justification for regulatory inspection readiness and protocol defense.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this critical value threshold record was first created in the system. Required for ALCOA+ data integrity and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.']
  critical_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'The upper critical (panic) value threshold for the analyte. A result at or above this value triggers an immediate clinical notification workflow per the defined notification SLA.']
  critical_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lower critical (panic) value threshold for the analyte. A result at or below this value triggers an immediate clinical notification workflow per the defined notification SLA.']
  edc_alert_rule_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automated edit check or alert rule configured in Medidata Rave EDC that fires when a lab result breaches this critical value threshold. Supports automated critical value flagging at point of data entry.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this critical value threshold ceases to be effective. Null indicates the threshold is currently active with no defined expiry. Supports temporal versioning and audit trail.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this critical value threshold becomes effective and is applied in automated alerting workflows. Supports temporal versioning of threshold definitions.']
  escalation_pathway varchar [note: 'Defined escalation route for communicating a critical value to the appropriate clinical personnel (e.g., PI direct, CRC then PI, Medical Monitor, Sponsor Safety team, DSMB). Governs the automated notification routing logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pi_direct|crc_then_pi|medical_monitor|sponsor_safety|dsmb|site_medical_director|other — promote to reference product]']
  escalation_tier_2_minutes varchar [note: 'Number of minutes after initial notification attempt before escalating to the secondary contact in the escalation pathway if no acknowledgement is received.']
  fasting_state_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this critical value threshold applies only to specimens collected under fasting conditions. True = fasting required for threshold applicability; False = non-fasting or state-independent.']
  lab_facility_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the applicability of this critical value threshold to laboratory facilities: all facilities, central lab only, local lab only, or a specific named facility. Drives which lab facilities apply this threshold in automated alerting workflows.']
  loinc_code varchar [note: 'Standardized LOINC code uniquely identifying the laboratory observation or test. Enables interoperability across laboratory systems and regulatory submissions per CDISC standards.']
  notification_sla_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable time in minutes from critical value detection to confirmed clinical notification. Drives automated alerting SLA monitoring and compliance reporting. Typical values range from 15 to 60 minutes per CAP and CLIA requirements.']
  regulatory_authority_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority or guideline that mandates or informs this critical value threshold definition (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, CAP, CLIA). Supports multi-jurisdictional compliance documentation.']
  requires_repeat_confirmation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a repeat specimen analysis is required to confirm the critical value before initiating the notification workflow. True = repeat confirmation required; False = single result triggers notification.']
  sex_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies the biological sex for which this critical value threshold applies. Some analytes (e.g., hemoglobin, creatinine) have sex-specific reference and critical ranges.']
  specimen_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological specimen from which the analyte is measured (e.g., blood, urine, plasma, serum). Critical value thresholds may differ by specimen type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|urine|plasma|serum|csf|tissue|saliva|other — promote to reference product]']
  test_system_code varchar [note: 'Internal or sponsor-defined code for the specific laboratory test or panel within the clinical trial management system (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC lab test code). Used for system-to-system mapping.']
  threshold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the critical value threshold record. Active = in use for alerting; Inactive = retired; Superseded = replaced by a newer version; Under Review = pending clinical or regulatory review.']
  threshold_type varchar [note: 'Defines whether the critical value threshold is an absolute numeric value, a delta change from baseline, or a percent change from a prior result. Determines the comparison logic applied during automated alerting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measurement for the critical value thresholds (e.g., mmol/L, g/dL, 10^9/L, mg/dL). Must align with the unit used in the corresponding lab result to enable automated threshold comparison.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this critical value threshold record. Required for ALCOA+ data integrity and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this critical value threshold record (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented when threshold values, SLA, or escalation pathway are modified. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining critical (panic) value thresholds for laboratory analytes that require immediate clinical notification. Captures analyte, LOINC code, critical low threshold, critical high threshold, unit, notification SLA (minutes), escalation pathway, and lab facility applicability. Drives automated critical value alerting workflows and safety reporting integration with Oracle Argus Safety.'
}

Table laboratory.pk_sample {
  pk_sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each pharmacokinetic (PK) or pharmacodynamic (PD) sample record in the master PK sampling registry. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete, time-critical biological sample collection event tied to a dosing schedule.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: PK samples are collected during AE investigations to measure drug concentration at time of reaction (exposure-response analysis for causality). Linking pk_sample to adverse_event supports the PK/safet']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: PK sample analysis requires arm-level stratification for exposure comparisons and dose-response modeling. Arm assignment determines the dosing regimen applied, which is essential for NCA and compartme']
  bioanalytical_assay_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bioanalytical assay assigned to analyze this PK sample. Links to the assay definition record containing the validated method, analyte(s), LLOQ, ULOQ, and laboratory performing the analysis.']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: PK sampling is enrollment-episode-specific (dose regimen, cohort, stratification). Linking pk_sample to enrollment enables NCA dataset construction by enrollment cohort and supports PK/PD analysis str']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: PK sampling is epoch-specific (single-dose, multiple-dose, steady-state epochs); NCA and compartmental modeling require epoch-level stratification. SDTM PC domain requires epoch assignment. The existi']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: DMPK analysts require direct traceability from PK samples to the IMP manufacture batch administered. Batch-specific PK variability analysis, stability investigations, and GCP audit trails for PK studi']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: PK sampling deviations (wrong timepoint, missed sample, incorrect volume) are protocol deviations that directly affect PK analysis populations. Direct FK from pk_sample to protocol_deviation enables P']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: PK sampling plans are defined in the protocol; direct protocol_id on pk_sample supports SDTM PC domain submission traceability, regulatory inspection readiness, and multi-protocol study disambiguation']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record under which this PK sample was transported to the central or bioanalytical laboratory. Enables chain-of-custody tracking and temperature excursion investigation at the shipment level.']
  specimen_id varchar [note: 'The internal sample identifier assigned by the bioanalytical or central laboratory upon receipt. Used for cross-referencing between the clinical trial management system and the laboratory information management system (LIMS). Enables reconciliation between EDC and lab data.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under whose protocol this PK sample was collected. Links the sample to the governing study protocol, sponsor, and regulatory submission context.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: PK sampling schedules change with protocol amendments (dose levels, timepoints, windows). Tracing which amendment governed a PK sample is required for regulatory submission audit trails and for correc']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the PK sample was collected. Used for site-level PK compliance monitoring and logistics tracking.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: PK sampling is scheduled per protocol visit window. Linking pk_sample to study_visit_schedule enables SDTM PC domain mapping, sampling window compliance checks (within_sampling_window flag), and devia']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: PK sampling schedules are treatment-arm-specific (dose level, nominal timepoints). Direct FK to subject_randomization supports PK schedule compliance verification — confirming correct sampling windows']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled clinical visit during which this PK sample was collected. Enables linkage to visit-level data including dosing records and clinical assessments.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) from whom this PK sample was collected. Core party reference linking the sample to the enrolled participant record.']
  actual_time_post_dose_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Calculated elapsed time in hours between the actual dose administration time and the actual sample collection time. Distinct from nominal_time_hours; used as the primary time variable in PK concentration-time profiles and non-compartmental analysis (NCA). Negative values indicate pre-dose collections.']
  analyte_name varchar [note: 'The name of the drug compound, metabolite, or biomarker being measured in this PK sample (e.g., parent compound, active metabolite, total drug). Corresponds to CDISC SDTM PC.PCTEST. Multiple analytes per sample are handled at the result level; this field captures the primary analyte assignment.']
  collection_method varchar [note: 'The method used to collect the biological sample from the subject. Relevant for bioanalytical method validation and potential pre-analytical variability assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: venipuncture|finger_prick|catheter|indwelling_cannula|spot_urine|timed_urine|dried_blood_spot|lumbar_puncture|buccal_swab — promote to reference product]']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time the PK sample was collected from the subject, recorded to the nearest minute. This is the principal real-world event timestamp and is critical for calculating actual time post-dose (ATPT) used in PK analysis. Must comply with ALCOA+ contemporaneous recording requirements.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'The type of collection container or tube used for the PK sample. Determines anticoagulant/additive present, which affects matrix type (plasma vs serum) and bioanalytical method compatibility. Critical for pre-analytical quality control. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: edta_tube|lithium_heparin_tube|sst_tube|plain_tube|urine_cup|cryovial|dbs_card — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this PK sample record was first created in the source system (EDC or LIMS). Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Represents the ALCOA+ \'contemporaneous\' requirement.']
  dose_administration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time of the reference dose administration used to calculate actual time post-dose for this sample. Sourced from the dosing record in the EDC (Medidata Rave). Required for accurate ATPT calculation per CDISC SDTM PC domain conventions.']
  dose_number varchar [note: 'The sequential dose number relative to which this PK sample was collected (e.g., 1 for first dose, steady-state dose number). Critical for distinguishing single-dose PK from multiple-dose/steady-state PK profiles in DMPK analysis.']
  edc_form_reference varchar [note: 'The reference identifier of the eCRF form in the EDC system (Medidata Rave) where this PK sample collection event was recorded. Enables direct traceability from the silver-layer data product back to the source EDC record for SDV and audit purposes.']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for excluding this PK sample from the primary analysis dataset. Populated only when sample_exclusion_flag is True. Examples include \'Outside sampling window\', \'Haemolysed sample\', \'Vomiting within 2h of dose\', \'Bioanalytical failure\'. Required for SAP-defined exclusion criteria documentation.']
  freeze_thaw_cycles varchar [note: 'The number of freeze-thaw cycles the PK sample has undergone. Excessive freeze-thaw cycles can degrade analyte stability and invalidate bioanalytical results. Tracked against the validated freeze-thaw stability limit established during bioanalytical method validation.']
  lab_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time the PK sample was received and logged at the bioanalytical or central laboratory. Used to calculate total sample transit time and to verify cold-chain compliance. Part of the chain-of-custody audit trail.']
  nominal_time_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric representation of the nominal time point in hours relative to dose administration (e.g., -0.0833 for pre-dose, 0.5, 1.0, 2.0). Negative values indicate pre-dose collections. Used for PK modeling, NCA (non-compartmental analysis), and SDTM/ADaM dataset generation in SAS Drug Development.']
  nominal_time_point varchar [note: 'The protocol-specified nominal time point for sample collection relative to dose administration (e.g., \'Pre-dose\', \'0.5h\', \'1h\', \'2h\', \'4h\', \'8h\', \'24h\'). Used as the planned time point label in PK analysis and SDTM PC domain mapping. Stored as a string to accommodate mixed formats (pre-dose, fractional hours, day-relative).']
  pre_dose_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample is a pre-dose (trough/baseline) collection. True = pre-dose sample; False = post-dose sample. Pre-dose samples are used to assess trough concentrations, baseline drug levels, and accumulation ratios in multiple-dose PK studies.']
  processing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing stage of the PK sample from collection through shipment to the bioanalytical laboratory. Distinct from sample_status (overall lifecycle); this tracks the physical processing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|processed|centrifuged|aliquoted|frozen|shipped|received_at_lab|failed|destroyed — promote to reference product]']
  processing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time the sample was processed (e.g., centrifuged and aliquoted) after collection. Time from collection to processing is a critical pre-analytical variable that can affect analyte stability and bioanalytical results.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation was associated with this PK sample collection event (e.g., collection outside the acceptable sampling window, incorrect tube used, missed collection). True = deviation present; False = no deviation. Triggers deviation reporting workflow.']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the PK sample, typically printed on the sample tube label and used across CTMS, LIMS, and bioanalytical systems for chain-of-custody tracking. Corresponds to the specimen barcode or accession number.']
  sample_condition_on_receipt varchar [note: 'Assessment of the physical condition of the PK sample upon receipt at the bioanalytical laboratory. Conditions such as haemolysis, lipaemia, or temperature excursion may affect analyte measurement and trigger sample rejection or result flagging. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: acceptable|haemolysed|lipemic|insufficient_volume|temperature_excursion|damaged|missing — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sample_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PK sample has been excluded from the primary PK analysis dataset. True = excluded; False = included. Exclusions may be due to protocol deviations, sample integrity issues, or bioanalytical failures. Exclusion reason is captured in exclusion_reason.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PK sample from scheduled collection through bioanalytical reporting. Drives workflow routing in LIMS and CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|collected|received|in_transit|received_at_lab|analyzed|reported|rejected|missing|cancelled — promote to reference product if additional statuses are required]']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'The biological matrix type of the PK sample. Determines the bioanalytical method applied and the reference range for analyte concentration. Plasma and serum are most common for small molecule PK; urine for renal clearance studies; CSF for CNS penetration studies.']
  sampling_window_end_hours decimal(8,4) [note: 'The protocol-defined acceptable latest collection time in hours relative to dose for this nominal time point. Together with sampling_window_start_hours, defines the acceptable window for protocol compliance assessment.']
  sampling_window_start_hours decimal(8,4) [note: 'The protocol-defined acceptable earliest collection time in hours relative to dose for this nominal time point. Used to assess whether the actual collection time falls within the acceptable PK sampling window for protocol compliance determination.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM domain to which this PK sample record maps for regulatory submission. PC = Pharmacokinetics Concentrations; PP = Pharmacokinetics Parameters. Enables automated SDTM dataset generation in SAS Drug Development.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The temperature in degrees Celsius at which the PK sample is stored prior to shipment or analysis. Critical for analyte stability monitoring; deviations from protocol-specified storage conditions (e.g., -70°C, -20°C) must be documented and assessed for impact on sample integrity.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The protocol-assigned subject number (e.g., site-subject identifier such as 001-001) used to identify the participant in regulatory submissions and PK analysis datasets. Classified as restricted PII as it can be used to trace back to an individual participant.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time this PK sample record was most recently modified. Required for audit trail and data integrity monitoring under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Used to detect unauthorized or unexplained data changes.']
  volume_collected_ml decimal(8,3) [note: 'The actual volume of biological sample collected from the subject, expressed in millilitres. Used for sample sufficiency assessment, bioanalytical aliquoting planning, and protocol compliance verification against the required volume specified in the PK sampling schedule.']
  volume_required_ml decimal(8,3) [note: 'The protocol-specified minimum volume of sample required for the PK assay at this time point, expressed in millilitres. Used to assess whether the collected volume meets protocol requirements and to flag insufficient sample events.']
  within_sampling_window boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the actual sample collection time falls within the protocol-defined acceptable PK sampling window for this nominal time point. True = within window (compliant); False = outside window (potential protocol deviation). Used in PK analysis inclusion/exclusion decisions and protocol deviation reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) samples collected per the study PK sampling schedule. Captures nominal and actual collection time relative to dose (pre-dose, post-dose hours), dose number, PK sampling window compliance, sample type (plasma/serum/whole blood/urine), volume collected, and DMPK assay assignment. Distinct from routine lab specimens due to time-critical nominal time point tracking required for PK analysis.'
}

Table laboratory.bioanalytical_assay {
  bioanalytical_assay_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the bioanalytical assay record in the lakehouse. Primary key. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a validated analytical method owned and managed by the bioanalytical laboratory.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: GLP/GCP bioanalytical assays must reference their validated method SOP. Regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA bioanalytical section) and FDA/EMA inspections require direct traceability from the assay record']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: FDA/EMA bioanalytical method validation guidance requires each validated assay to be linked to the specific IMP it measures. Regulatory submissions (NDA/MAA) require assay-to-drug traceability. DMPK t']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Bioanalytical assays are validated to measure specific investigational products. Regulatory submissions (IND/NDA bioanalytical method validation sections) require tracing which validated assay measure']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: A bioanalytical assay is validated and performed at a specific laboratory facility. The existing STRING columns responsible_lab_name and lab_accreditation_number are denormalized references to lab_fac']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: The SAP governs bioanalytical methods, LLOQ/ULOQ thresholds, and acceptance criteria used in PK statistical analyses. Regulatory submissions require the SAP to reference the validated bioanalytical_as']
  validation_tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Bioanalytical validation reports are TMF documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 3, Section 3.2). Regulatory submissions require direct navigation from the assay record to the filed validation report.']
  accuracy_percent decimal(6,3) [note: 'Mean accuracy of the assay expressed as percent of nominal concentration (%Nominal) at QC levels during validation. Regulatory acceptance criterion is typically 85–115% (80–120% at LLOQ). Stored as the worst-case value across QC levels.']
  analyte_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the analyte type measured by the assay. Distinguishes small molecule drugs (PK), large molecule biologics, biomarkers (PD), immunogenicity (anti-drug antibodies), metabolites, and endogenous compounds. Drives regulatory submission strategy and validation requirements.']
  analyte_name varchar [note: 'Name of the substance being measured by the assay (e.g., drug compound, metabolite, biomarker, anti-drug antibody). Directly links the assay to the pharmacokinetic (PK), pharmacodynamic (PD), or immunogenicity endpoint it supports.']
  assay_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code assigned to the assay by the bioanalytical laboratory or sponsor (e.g., \'BA-LC-001\', \'ELISA-TNF-003\'). Used as the business identifier across CTMS, EDC, and regulatory submissions. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  assay_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the bioanalytical assay as used in study protocols, validation reports, and regulatory submissions (e.g., \'LC-MS/MS Assay for Compound XYZ in Human Plasma\'). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE IDENTITY_LABEL category.']
  assay_purpose varchar [note: 'Primary scientific purpose the assay serves within the clinical or nonclinical program. PK (Pharmacokinetics) assays measure drug concentration over time; PD (Pharmacodynamics) assays measure biological effect; Immunogenicity assays detect anti-drug antibodies; DMPK (Drug Metabolism and Pharmacokinetics) assays support metabolite profiling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PK|PD|Immunogenicity|Safety Biomarker|Efficacy Biomarker|DMPK|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  assay_type varchar [note: 'Analytical technology platform used to perform the assay. Common types include LC-MS/MS (Liquid Chromatography-Tandem Mass Spectrometry), ELISA (Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay), ECL (Electrochemiluminescence), PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction), Flow Cytometry, and LBA (Ligand Binding Assay). Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LC-MS/MS|ELISA|ECL|PCR|Flow Cytometry|LBA|HPLC|GC-MS|Western Blot|IHC|CLIA|RIA — promote to reference product]']
  calibration_curve_model varchar [note: 'Mathematical regression model used to fit the calibration curve for back-calculation of analyte concentrations from instrument response. Common models include Linear (1/x or 1/x² weighting), Quadratic, 4-Parameter Logistic (4PL), and 5-Parameter Logistic (5PL) for LBA methods.']
  cdisc_test_code varchar [note: 'Standardized test code from the CDISC Controlled Terminology for the SDTM PC (Pharmacokinetics Concentrations) or LB (Laboratory Test Results) domain. Enables direct mapping of assay results to CDISC SDTM datasets for regulatory submission.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bioanalytical assay record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) data integrity principles.']
  cut_point_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method used to establish the screening and confirmatory cut points for immunogenicity (anti-drug antibody) assays (e.g., \'Parametric 5th percentile\', \'Non-parametric 5th percentile\'). Applicable only to immunogenicity assays; null for PK/PD assays.']
  dilution_integrity_factor decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum validated dilution factor that can be applied to samples exceeding the ULOQ while maintaining assay accuracy and precision within acceptance criteria. Samples requiring dilution beyond this factor must be flagged.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the assay validation is no longer considered current and re-validation or re-qualification is required. Relevant for long-running studies where assay reagents, reference standards, or critical reagents may expire.']
  gcp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay is performed under Good Clinical Practice (GCP) standards as required for clinical trial sample analysis. True = GCP-compliant; False = non-GCP. Distinct from GLP compliance — clinical samples require GCP; nonclinical samples require GLP.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assay was validated and is performed under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) regulations. GLP compliance is required for nonclinical safety studies submitted to regulatory agencies. True = GLP-compliant; False = non-GLP.']
  inter_run_precision_cv decimal(6,3) [note: 'Between-run precision expressed as percent coefficient of variation (%CV) across multiple analytical runs during validation. Regulatory acceptance criterion is typically ≤15% (%CV). Stored as the worst-case value across QC levels.']
  internal_standard varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal standard (IS) used in the assay for quantification normalization (e.g., stable isotope-labeled analog, structural analog). Critical for LC-MS/MS and other chromatographic methods to correct for matrix effects and extraction variability.']
  intra_run_precision_cv decimal(6,3) [note: 'Within-run precision expressed as percent coefficient of variation (%CV) at the QC concentration levels, as determined during validation. Regulatory acceptance criterion is typically ≤15% (%CV), or ≤20% at LLOQ. Stored as the worst-case value across QC levels.']
