  lloq decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower Limit of Quantification — the lowest analyte concentration that can be reliably quantified with acceptable accuracy and precision per the validated method. Expressed in the unit defined by lloq_uloq_unit. Critical for PK/PD data interpretation and regulatory submission. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE category.']
  lloq_uloq_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the LLOQ and ULOQ values (e.g., \'ng/mL\', \'pg/mL\', \'µg/mL\', \'nM\'). Must be consistent with the unit reported in PK/PD concentration data submitted to regulatory agencies.']
  long_term_stability_days varchar [note: 'Number of days for which analyte stability in the biological matrix at long-term storage conditions (typically -20°C or -70°C) has been validated. Samples stored beyond this period require re-validation of stability before results can be reported.']
  matrix_effect_assessed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether matrix effect (ion suppression/enhancement for LC-MS/MS; non-specific binding for LBA) was formally evaluated during validation. True = matrix effect experiments conducted; False = not assessed.']
  matrix_type varchar [note: 'Biological matrix in which the analyte is measured (e.g., plasma, serum, whole blood, urine, cerebrospinal fluid). The assay is validated specifically for the declared matrix; use in a different matrix requires re-validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Plasma|Serum|Whole Blood|Urine|CSF|Tissue|Saliva|Feces|Synovial Fluid|Bronchoalveolar Lavage|Dried Blood Spot — promote to reference product]']
  parallelism_assessed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parallelism (serial dilution of incurred samples producing results parallel to the calibration curve) was assessed. Relevant primarily for LBA and immunogenicity assays. True = parallelism assessed; False = not assessed.']
  reference_standard_lot varchar [note: 'Lot number of the certified reference standard used to prepare calibration standards and quality control (QC) samples. Traceability of the reference standard lot is required for regulatory submissions and audit purposes.']
  sample_volume_required_ul decimal(10,3) [note: 'Minimum volume of biological matrix (in microliters) required per sample aliquot for a single assay run. Used for specimen collection planning, biobank management, and feasibility assessment of retrospective analyses.']
  selectivity_assessed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether selectivity (absence of interference from endogenous matrix components) was formally assessed during validation. True = selectivity experiments conducted and documented; False = not assessed. Required for full validation per FDA/EMA/ICH M10.']
  species varchar [note: 'Biological species for which the assay has been validated. Assays validated in human matrices may differ from those validated in preclinical animal species used in DMPK studies. Drives applicability to clinical vs. nonclinical programs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Human|Rat|Mouse|Dog|Monkey|Rabbit|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sponsor_assay_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned identifier for the assay as referenced in the study protocol, SAP, or regulatory submission. May differ from the CRO\'s internal assay code. Required for cross-referencing sponsor documents with CRO laboratory records.']
  stability_freeze_thaw_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles for which analyte stability in the biological matrix has been validated. Samples subjected to more freeze-thaw cycles than validated must be flagged or excluded from analysis.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature and conditions for samples analyzed by this assay (e.g., \'-70°C ± 10°C\', \'-20°C\', \'2–8°C\'). Defines the chain-of-custody storage requirement for clinical samples to maintain analyte integrity.']
  superseded_by_assay_code varchar [note: 'Assay code of the newer version that replaces this assay when it is retired or superseded. Supports version lineage tracking and ensures historical sample results can be linked to the correct assay version.']
  uloq decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper Limit of Quantification — the highest analyte concentration that can be reliably quantified within the validated dynamic range. Samples above ULOQ must be diluted and re-assayed. Expressed in the unit defined by lloq_uloq_unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bioanalytical assay record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Tracks when assay parameters, validation status, or metadata were last changed.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the bioanalytical method validation was formally completed and the validation report was approved. Marks the point from which the assay is considered fit for regulated use in clinical sample analysis.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory validation tier of the assay. \'Validated\' indicates full GLP/GCP-compliant validation per FDA/EMA/ICH M10 guidelines; \'Qualified\' indicates fit-for-purpose qualification; \'Exploratory\' indicates early-phase research use only; \'In Validation\' indicates validation in progress; \'Retired\' indicates the assay is no longer in use. Satisfies MASTER_RESOURCE LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the assay method (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Incremented when the assay undergoes method changes requiring partial or full re-validation. Enables traceability of which assay version was used for each study\'s sample analysis.']
  weighting_factor varchar [note: 'Statistical weighting factor applied to the calibration curve regression to minimize heteroscedasticity (e.g., \'1/x\', \'1/x²\', \'none\'). Documented in the validation report and applied consistently during sample analysis.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a validated bioanalytical assay used to quantify drug concentrations, biomarkers, or immunogenicity in clinical trial samples. Captures assay name, analyte measured, assay type (LC-MS/MS, ELISA, ECL, PCR, flow cytometry), validation status (validated/qualified/exploratory), LLOQ, ULOQ, matrix type, regulatory submission reference, and responsible bioanalytical lab. Supports DMPK and PK/PD data generation.'
}

Table laboratory.bioanalytical_result {
  bioanalytical_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each bioanalytical or biomarker assay result record in the clinical trial laboratory data product. Serves as the primary key for the silver-layer lakehouse table.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: PK/PD safety analysis links drug exposure data (bioanalytical results) to adverse events to assess exposure-response relationships. This is a core pharmacokinetic safety analysis required for NDA/BLA ']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: Bioanalytical results are attributed to PK analysis populations (PK-evaluable, full PK set) for NCA and modeling analyses. ADaM ADPC dataset derivation requires each bioanalytical_result to reference ']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: PK/PD exposure analysis by treatment arm is a core pharmacokinetic reporting requirement. Arm-level PK parameter comparisons (e.g., AUC, Cmax by dose group) are required for regulatory submissions and']
  bioanalytical_assay_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.bioanalytical_assay. Business justification: A bioanalytical result is produced by executing a specific validated bioanalytical assay. The result record carries analyte_name, analyte_code, and method_id as denormalized strings that are authorita']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: Bioanalytical/PK concentration results directly reveal treatment assignment in blinded studies. Blinding_config governs access restrictions for unblinded statisticians vs. blinded operations staff. Di']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Bioanalytical results are locked at database lock for PK analysis. Traceability from bioanalytical_result to database_lock_event is required for regulatory PK submissions, confirming that the PK datas']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Bioanalytical results (SDTM PC domain) require enrollment-episode context for correct dataset mapping and for subjects with multiple enrollment episodes. Enrollment captures cohort, stratification, an']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: PK/PD results must be epoch-stratified for NCA (single-dose vs steady-state epochs) and SDTM PC domain mapping. Epoch assignment is required for ADaM ADPC dataset generation and regulatory PK report t']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: A bioanalytical result is generated at a specific laboratory facility. The existing laboratory_code STRING column is a denormalized reference to lab_facility. Adding lab_facility_id normalizes this, e']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: FDA/EMA bioanalytical method validation guidance requires traceability from drug concentration results to the specific IMP manufacture batch. Inter-batch PK variability investigations and batch-relate']
  pk_sample_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the biological specimen record from which this assay result was derived. Supports chain-of-custody traceability from collection through analysis.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Bioanalytical results failing acceptance criteria (ISR failure, calibration curve rejection) or collected outside protocol windows generate protocol deviations affecting DMPK analysis datasets. Linkin']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Bioanalytical results with QC failures, calibration curve rejections, or data integrity issues trigger quality events in the QMS. Direct FK from bioanalytical_result to quality_event supports GLP comp']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: Bioanalytical results (drug concentration measurements) are the source data for PK statistical endpoints (AUC, Cmax, t½). Regulatory PK submissions require direct traceability from bioanalytical_resul']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this bioanalytical result was generated. Links the result to the governing protocol, sponsor, and regulatory context.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Bioanalytical results must be traceable to the protocol amendment active at analysis time for regulatory submission (NDA/BLA bioanalytical data packages). Amendment changes affect assay methods, accep']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: PK/PD bioanalytical results must be mapped to protocol-defined sampling visits for SDTM PC/PP domain generation and NCA analysis. Linking results to the scheduled visit definition enables sampling-win']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: PK/bioanalytical analysis (NCA, population PK, exposure-response) requires treatment arm and dose level from randomization. ADaM ADPC dataset construction and PK reporting to regulators depend on link']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (participant) from whom the bioanalytical sample was collected. Enables subject-level PK/PD profiling and safety linkage.']
  actual_time_point decimal(10,4) [note: 'The actual elapsed time (in hours post-dose) at which the sample was collected, calculated from the dose administration timestamp and sample collection timestamp. Used in non-compartmental and compartmental PK analysis.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the bioanalytical assay was performed and the result was generated by the laboratory instrument or system. Distinct from collection timestamp; used for audit trail and turnaround time tracking.']
  assay_platform varchar [note: 'Analytical technology platform used to generate this result. Determines method-specific acceptance criteria and regulatory validation requirements. Values include LC-MS/MS (liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry), ELISA, ECL (electrochemiluminescence), PCR, FLOW_CYTOMETRY, NGS (next-generation sequencing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LC-MS/MS|ELISA|ECL|PCR|FLOW_CYTOMETRY|NGS|IHC|RIA|WESTERN_BLOT — promote to reference product]']
  assay_run_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned by the bioanalytical laboratory to the analytical run (batch) in which this sample was processed. Used to link results to calibration curve and QC sample performance for the run. Maps to PCRUNID in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  calibration_curve_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calibration curve for the analytical run met pre-defined acceptance criteria (e.g., ≥75% of calibrators within ±15% of nominal, ±20% at LLOQ). A failed calibration curve invalidates all results from the run.']
  categorical_result varchar [note: 'Qualitative or categorical result for assays that produce non-numeric outcomes (e.g., POSITIVE/NEGATIVE for immunogenicity, BELOW_LLOQ for concentrations below the lower limit of quantification). Null when numeric_result is populated. Maps to PCSTRESC in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the biological sample was collected from the subject. The principal real-world event time for this bioanalytical result record. Maps to PCDTC in CDISC SDTM PC domain. Conforms to ISO 8601 format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bioanalytical result record was first created in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  dilution_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'The dilution factor applied to the sample prior to analysis when the original concentration exceeded the ULOQ. The reported result incorporates this factor. Maps to PCDILIND/PCDILUTN in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this result has been excluded from the primary PK/PD analysis dataset (e.g., due to protocol deviation, sample integrity issue, or statistical outlier determination per the Statistical Analysis Plan).']
  exclusion_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for excluding this result from the PK/PD analysis dataset. Required when exclusion_flag is true. Examples: \'Sample collected outside protocol window\', \'Hemolyzed sample\', \'Calibration curve failure\', \'Subject vomited within 2 hours of dose\'.']
  freeze_thaw_cycles varchar [note: 'Number of freeze-thaw cycles the sample underwent prior to analysis. Stability data from method validation defines the maximum allowable cycles; exceedance may invalidate the result.']
  isr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sample was selected for Incurred Sample Reanalysis (ISR), a regulatory requirement to demonstrate reproducibility of results in authentic study samples. ISR is mandatory for pivotal PK studies submitted to FDA and EMA.']
  isr_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the Incurred Sample Reanalysis (ISR) assessment. PASS indicates the original and repeat results agree within ±20% of their mean for ≥67% of ISR samples. FAIL triggers investigation. NOT_APPLICABLE when isr_flag is false.']
  lloq decimal(18,6) [note: 'The lowest concentration of analyte in a sample that can be quantitatively determined with acceptable precision and accuracy for this validated method and run. Used to flag results as BLQ (below limit of quantification). Maps to PCLLOQ in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  matrix_type varchar [note: 'Type of biological matrix from which the sample was derived and analyzed. Determines applicable method validation parameters and reference ranges. Maps to PCSPEC in CDISC SDTM PC domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PLASMA|SERUM|WHOLE_BLOOD|URINE|CSF|TISSUE|SALIVA|FECES|SYNOVIAL_FLUID — promote to reference product]']
  nominal_time_point decimal(10,4) [note: 'The protocol-specified time point (in hours post-dose) at which the sample was scheduled to be collected (e.g., 0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 4.0, 8.0, 24.0). Used as the reference for PK modeling and deviation calculation. Maps to PCTPTNUM in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  numeric_result decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantitative measured concentration or signal value for the analyte in this sample. Null when result is categorical (e.g., positive/negative for immunogenicity). Maps to PCSTRESN in CDISC SDTM PC domain. This is the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT for the TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  qc_sample_performance varchar [note: 'Overall acceptance outcome for the QC samples (low, medium, high QC) within the analytical run. PASS indicates ≥67% of QC samples within ±15% of nominal with at least 50% at each level passing. FAIL invalidates the run. MARGINAL triggers scientific review.']
  result_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the bioanalytical result type: PK_CONCENTRATION (pharmacokinetic drug/metabolite concentration), BIOMARKER (pharmacodynamic or disease biomarker), IMMUNOGENICITY (anti-drug antibody or neutralizing antibody), EXPLORATORY (hypothesis-generating biomarker), PD_MARKER (pharmacodynamic endpoint marker). Drives downstream CDISC domain routing (PC vs PP vs ADPC).']
  result_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment entered by the bioanalytical scientist or laboratory reviewer providing additional context for the result (e.g., instrument flags, unusual chromatography, sample appearance notes). Maps to PCORRES or PCCOMMENT in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  result_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the validated bioanalytical result was formally reported and released by the laboratory to the sponsor or data management team. Marks the transition from laboratory-internal to reportable status.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Current disposition status of the bioanalytical result indicating whether it is reportable for inclusion in the clinical database, requires repeat analysis, or has been invalidated. Drives data flow into EDC and CDISC SDTM datasets.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the numeric result value (e.g., ng/mL, pg/mL, nM, U/mL). Standardized per CDISC controlled terminology. Maps to PCSTRESU in CDISC SDTM PC domain.']
  sample_stability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample met stability criteria at the time of analysis based on validated short-term, long-term, and freeze-thaw stability data. False indicates a potential stability failure that may affect result validity.']
  sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC Study Data Tabulation Model (SDTM) domain to which this result maps for regulatory submission. PC = Pharmacokinetics Concentrations, PP = Pharmacokinetics Parameters, MB = Microbiology, IS = Immunogenicity Specimen Assessments.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this bioanalytical result was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. LIMS refers to a Laboratory Information Management System.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable subject identifier (e.g., site-subject number such as 001-001) used in the clinical trial. Distinct from the surrogate subject_id; used for cross-system reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  time_deviation_hours decimal(10,4) [note: 'Deviation of the actual sample collection time from the nominal protocol-specified time point, expressed in hours (actual minus nominal). Positive values indicate late collection; negative values indicate early collection. Used to assess protocol compliance and flag samples for PK analysis exclusion.']
  uloq decimal(18,6) [note: 'The highest concentration of analyte in a sample that can be quantitatively determined with acceptable precision and accuracy for this validated method and run. Results above ULOQ require dilution and re-analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this bioanalytical result record. Required for audit trail compliance and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks silver layer.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a bioanalytical or biomarker assay result from a clinical trial PK/PD, DMPK, or exploratory biomarker sample. Captures assay run ID, sample ID, analyte/biomarker measured, concentration or categorical result value, unit/scale, assay platform (LC-MS/MS/ELISA/ECL/PCR/flow cytometry/NGS/IHC), calibration curve acceptance, QC sample performance, result status (reportable/non-reportable/repeat required), result category (PK concentration/biomarker/immunogenicity/exploratory), incurred sample reanalysis (ISR) flag, deviation from nominal time point, and data sharing consent flag. Feeds CDISC SDTM PC and PP domains. Consolidated SSOT for all specialized assay results beyond routine clinical lab testing.'
}
