  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: Lab accreditation activities (qualification assessments, renewal processes) must follow documented SOPs per GCP/GLP requirements. Linking accreditation records to the governing compliance SOP enables ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Lab accreditations are recognized by specific health authorities (FDA recognizes CAP/CLIA, EMA requires ISO 15189/GLP). Sponsors must demonstrate that lab accreditation meets the specific health autho']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: GCP requires sponsor to qualify labs for specific IMP bioanalytical analysis. lab_accreditation.scope_includes_pk_pd and scope_includes_dmpk indicate IMP-specific scope. Regulatory submissions and ven']
  primary_lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory facility for which this accreditation record applies. Links to the laboratory master record.']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Site files track lab_certification_on_file status. Linking lab_accreditation to site_file enables automated completeness checks — confirming which accreditation record satisfies the site file\'s lab ce']
  tertiary_lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'FK to laboratory.lab_facility']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Lab accreditation certificates are essential TMF documents (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 3). Regulatory inspectors require filed accreditation certificates in the eTMF. This FK links the accreditation']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the accrediting or certifying organization that issued the accreditation (e.g., CAP - College of American Pathologists, CLIA - Clinical Laboratory Improvement Amendments, ISO 15189, UKAS - UK Accreditation Service, DAkkS - Deutsche Akkreditierungsstelle). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CAP|CLIA|ISO 15189|UKAS|DAkkS|A2LA|COFRAC|INAB|NABH|NABL — promote to reference product]']
  accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the primary laboratory accreditation (CLIA, CAP, or ISO 15189) expires. Used to trigger renewal workflows and to validate facility eligibility for ongoing and new study assignments. Critical for regulatory compliance monitoring. [Moved from lab_facility: This scalar field on lab_facility collapses all authority-specific expiry dates into a single value, which is incorrect when a facility holds certifications from multiple health authorities each with independent expiry timelines. The per-authority expiry_date on facility_accreditation replaces this field with full fidelity.]']
  accreditation_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured description of the test menu and analytical disciplines covered by the accreditation (e.g., clinical chemistry, hematology, microbiology, PK/PD bioanalysis, DMPK assays, flow cytometry). Defines which tests the laboratory is qualified to perform under the accreditation.']
  accreditation_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version or edition of the accreditation standard under which the certificate was issued (e.g., ISO 15189:2022, ISO 15189:2012, CAP 2023 Checklist). Relevant for regulatory submissions and gap assessments when standards are updated.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the accreditation certificate. Drives eligibility of the laboratory for use in regulated clinical trials. Active status is required for regulatory submission documentation.']
  accreditation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the accreditation grant indicating the scope and conditions of the award (e.g., full accreditation, provisional, conditional, limited scope, renewal).']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-issued certificate or accreditation number assigned by the accrediting body (e.g., CAP number, CLIA ID, ISO certificate number). This is the primary business identifier used in regulatory submissions and audit documentation.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the accreditation as maintained by the issuing health authority. Drives facility eligibility for use in trials subject to that authority\'s jurisdiction. Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accreditation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority\'s certification or recognition of this facility became valid. Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the accreditation certificate expires and the laboratory must have completed renewal to remain qualified for use in regulated clinical trials. Critical for trial eligibility monitoring.']
  gcp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory has been assessed and confirmed as compliant with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) requirements for clinical laboratory operations supporting regulated clinical trials.']
  glp_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory operates under Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) principles as required for non-clinical safety studies submitted to regulatory agencies. GLP compliance is distinct from ISO/CAP accreditation.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent inspection conducted by this health authority at this facility. Authority-specific — the same facility may receive different outcomes from different authorities. Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the accreditation certificate was formally issued or granted by the accrediting body. Represents the effective start of the accreditation period.']
  lab_director_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the qualified laboratory director responsible for the accredited laboratory facility. Required for regulatory submissions and accreditation documentation. Classified as confidential business data.']
  lab_director_qualification varchar [note: 'Professional qualifications, credentials, or certifications held by the laboratory director (e.g., MD, PhD, FACB, FRCPA). Required to verify director eligibility under CLIA and accrediting body requirements.']
  lab_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the laboratory by operational role in the clinical trial (central laboratory, local/site laboratory, reference laboratory, bioanalytical laboratory, specialty laboratory). Determines applicable accreditation requirements and regulatory expectations.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or accreditation body inspection of the laboratory facility. Used to assess compliance currency and to schedule re-inspections per regulatory requirements. [Moved from lab_facility: A single last_inspection_date on lab_facility cannot represent the fact that FDA, EMA, and PMDA each conduct independent inspections on different schedules. The inspection history per authority belongs on the association record.]']
  last_inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent regulatory or accreditation inspection. Drives risk-based monitoring decisions, sponsor qualification status, and CAPA initiation for the facility. [Moved from lab_facility: Same rationale as last_inspection_date — a single outcome field cannot capture authority-specific inspection results. The inspection_outcome attribute on facility_accreditation provides per-authority granularity.]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, context, or clarifications related to the accreditation record that do not fit structured fields (e.g., special conditions, interim arrangements, liaison contact details).']
  previous_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number from the immediately preceding accreditation cycle. Supports continuity tracking and audit trail for regulatory submissions requiring historical accreditation evidence.']
  proficiency_testing_program varchar [note: 'Name of the external proficiency testing or external quality assessment (EQA) program in which the laboratory participates (e.g., CAP PT, UKNEQAS, EQAS, RCPA QAP). Supports accreditation body verification.']
  proficiency_testing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the laboratory\'s external proficiency testing (PT) or external quality assessment (EQA) program participation. Proficiency testing is a mandatory component of CLIA and CAP accreditation and demonstrates ongoing analytical competency.']
  qualification_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the CRO or sponsor conducted an internal laboratory qualification assessment (distinct from the accrediting body inspection). Supports vendor qualification and oversight requirements under GCP.']
  qualification_assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome of the internal CRO or sponsor laboratory qualification assessment. Determines whether the laboratory may be activated for use in clinical trials managed by the organization.']
  recognition_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of certification or regulatory recognition granted by this health authority to the facility (e.g., CLIA for FDA, GLP for EMA, GCP for PMDA). Sourced directly from detection-phase relationship_data.']
  regulatory_submission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this laboratory\'s accreditation record is currently eligible to be cited in regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTD/eCTD). Derived from accreditation status, expiry, and CAPA closure but stored as an explicit business flag for audit and reporting purposes.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the renewal application must be submitted to the accrediting body to avoid a lapse in accreditation status. May differ from expiry_date based on accrediting body lead-time requirements.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the accreditation renewal process. Tracks whether the laboratory has initiated, submitted, or completed renewal prior to certificate expiry to ensure uninterrupted trial eligibility.']
  scope_includes_dmpk boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accreditation scope explicitly covers drug metabolism and pharmacokinetics (DMPK) assays, relevant for early-phase and ADME studies.']
  scope_includes_pk_pd boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the accreditation scope explicitly covers pharmacokinetic (PK) and pharmacodynamic (PD) bioanalytical testing, which is required for studies with PK/PD endpoints.']
  sponsor_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinical trial sponsor formally approved the laboratory for use in their studies. Required for audit trail and regulatory submission documentation.']
  sponsor_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the laboratory has been formally approved by the clinical trial sponsor for use in their studies. Sponsor approval is a prerequisite for laboratory qualification in addition to regulatory accreditation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this accreditation record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking laboratory facility accreditation and certification status. Captures accreditation body (CAP, CLIA, ISO 15189, UKAS, DAkkS), certificate number, accreditation scope (test menu), issue date, expiry date, renewal status, inspection date, findings summary, and corrective action status. Ensures only qualified labs are used in regulated clinical trials and supports regulatory submission documentation.'
}

Table laboratory.lab_kit {
  lab_kit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory collection kit master record. Primary key for the lab_kit entity.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Lab kits are arm-specific in blinded and dose-escalation trials (different tube types, volumes, or panels per treatment arm). Linking lab_kit to arm enables arm-level kit supply planning, blinded kit ']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: Lab kits are prepared, stored, and used per SOPs governing collection procedures and chain of custody. GCP inspection readiness requires direct traceability from kit definition to its governing compli']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: A laboratory collection kit is supplied by and associated with a specific central laboratory facility. The central_lab_code STRING column is a denormalized reference to lab_facility. Adding lab_facili']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the laboratory panel definition that this kit is designed to support. Ensures correct collection materials are used per protocol-specified assays.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Lab kits are designed and versioned per protocol specification (tube types, volumes, handling instructions). Regulatory inspections and GCP audits require tracing kit versions to the protocol that man']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: IRT/RTSM systems configure lab kit assignments directly against randomization schemes — kit availability, block structure, and dispensing rules are scheme-dependent. Lab kit setup in IRT requires this']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this kit is designated. Links the kit to a specific protocol and sponsor.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Protocol amendments that change specimen collection requirements trigger new kit versions. Tracing kit versions to the driving amendment is required for amendment impact tracking, site resupply planni']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical site to which this kit is assigned or shipped. Supports site-level inventory tracking.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Lab kit instruction leaflets are essential TMF documents filed per ICH E6 GCP requirements. lab_kit.instruction_leaflet_version indicates a versioned document exists; this FK links the kit record to i']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Lab kits are arm-specific in blinded trials — active and placebo arms use different collection kits (e.g., PK kits only for active arm, different tube types per arm). IRT systems assign kits by treatm']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the kit design and components list was formally approved by the sponsor or quality team for use in the study. Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  biohazard_classification varchar [note: 'IATA/UN biohazard classification for shipping the collected specimen (Category A UN2814, Category B UN3373, Exempt). Determines packaging and shipping documentation requirements for specimen transport.']
  cdisc_lbspec_code varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM controlled terminology value for specimen type (LBSPEC variable), e.g., BLOOD, URINE, PLASMA, SERUM. Enables direct mapping to SDTM LB domain for regulatory submission datasets.']
  collection_timepoint varchar [note: 'Specific timepoint within a visit at which the specimen should be collected (e.g., pre-dose, 1h post-dose, 4h post-dose). Particularly relevant for PK/PD sampling schedules.']
  components_description varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of all components included in the kit (e.g., EDTA tubes, serum separator tubes, cryovials, dry ice packs, biohazard bags, requisition forms, instruction leaflets). Supports site training and deviation investigation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lab kit master record was first created in the system. Part of the mandatory audit trail for GCP compliance and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the kit must not be used for specimen collection. Expiry is driven by the stability of collection tubes, preservatives, and reagents. Sites must discard or return kits past this date.']
  fasting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject must be fasting prior to specimen collection using this kit. Fasting status affects reference ranges and result interpretation for metabolic and lipid panels.']
  instruction_leaflet_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the collection instruction leaflet included in the kit. Ensures site staff follow the correct collection procedure aligned to the current protocol version.']
  is_ancillary_kit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this kit is an ancillary/backup kit (True) rather than a primary protocol-required kit (False). Ancillary kits are used for repeat collections or unscheduled assessments.']
  kit_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the kit indicating the scientific purpose of the specimen collection (e.g., Pharmacokinetics (PK), Pharmacodynamics (PD), safety labs, biomarker, genetic). Aligns kit to the study\'s analytical objectives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pk|pd|safety|biomarker|genetic|microbiome|dmpk|other — promote to reference product]']
  kit_label_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639 language code for the primary language of the kit label and instruction leaflet (e.g., en, fr, de, ja). Ensures regulatory compliance for multi-country trials requiring local language labelling.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the kit, typically printed on the kit label and used for chain-of-custody tracking. Corresponds to the kit barcode or label ID in RTSM/IRT systems such as Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  kit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the kit. Drives inventory management workflows and ensures expired or quarantined kits are not used for specimen collection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|assigned|shipped|received|used|expired|quarantined|destroyed|returned — promote to reference product]']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the kit by specimen matrix type (e.g., blood, urine, tissue). Determines the collection containers and preservatives included. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: blood|urine|saliva|tissue|csf|stool|buccal_swab|plasma|serum|other — promote to reference product]']
  kit_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the kit design or configuration. Used to track protocol amendments that change collection requirements, ensuring sites use the correct kit version per the current protocol.']
  lab_accreditation_standard varchar [note: 'Accreditation standard under which the central laboratory processing specimens from this kit is certified (e.g., ISO 15189, CAP, CLIA, GCLP). Required for regulatory submissions and sponsor quality oversight.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot number assigned by the kit supplier or central laboratory. Enables traceability for quality investigations, recalls, and regulatory inspections. Critical for ALCOA+ compliance.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the kit was manufactured or assembled by the supplier. Used in conjunction with expiry date to calculate shelf life and manage inventory rotation.']
  preservative_type varchar [note: 'Type of preservative or additive included in the kit (e.g., EDTA, heparin, sodium fluoride, formalin, RNAlater). Critical for specimen stability and downstream analytical method compatibility.']
  protocol_visit_reference varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined visit name or visit window for which this kit is intended (e.g., Screening, Day 1, Week 4). Ensures the correct kit type is used at the correct study visit per the protocol schedule of assessments.']
  quantity_per_shipment varchar [note: 'Standard number of kits included in a single shipment to a site. Used for supply planning, purchase order generation, and site inventory management.']
  reorder_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity of kits at a site below which a resupply order should be triggered. Supports proactive inventory management to prevent site stock-outs.']
  requisition_form_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a laboratory requisition form (paper or pre-printed label) is included in the kit. Requisition forms are required by central labs for specimen processing and chain-of-custody documentation.']
  return_kit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the used kit or residual materials must be returned to the central laboratory or sponsor after specimen collection. Relevant for chain-of-custody and waste management compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this kit record originated (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One, Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the lakehouse.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements for the kit or collected specimen (e.g., protect from light, invert tube 8 times, centrifuge within 30 minutes, ship on dry ice). Critical for specimen integrity.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Categorical storage condition required for the kit (e.g., refrigerated 2–8°C, frozen −20°C, room temperature). Complements the numeric temperature range with a human-readable label for site staff.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the kit. Used alongside minimum temperature to define the acceptable storage range and trigger excursion alerts.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the kit. Ensures cold-chain integrity and preservative efficacy. Deviations must be documented per GCP.']
  supplier_kit_catalog_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own catalog or part number for this kit configuration. Used for reordering, purchase orders in SAP S/4HANA, and supplier quality documentation.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor or central laboratory that manufactured and supplied the kit. Used for procurement, quality investigations, and supplier qualification tracking.']
  tube_count varchar [note: 'Total number of collection tubes included in the kit. Used for inventory reconciliation and to verify completeness of kit contents upon receipt at the site.']
  tube_type varchar [note: 'Type of collection tube(s) included in the kit (e.g., EDTA K2, SST, lithium heparin, sodium citrate, PAXgene). Determines anticoagulant or additive present, which is critical for assay validity.']
  tube_volume_ml decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal volume in millilitres of each collection tube in the kit. Used to confirm adequate sample volume for the required assays and to flag insufficient sample at processing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the lab kit master record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for laboratory collection kits supplied to clinical sites for specimen collection. Captures kit type, kit lot number, components list (tubes, containers, preservatives, requisition forms), storage requirements, expiry date, kit version, and associated lab panel. Supports kit inventory management at sites and ensures correct collection materials are used per protocol.'
}

Table laboratory.lab_query {
  lab_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the laboratory data query record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Lab queries with safety_relevance_flag=true are raised to resolve data discrepancies that affect AE reporting. Linking lab_query to adverse_event formalizes the safety data cleaning process, enabling ']
  bioanalytical_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.bioanalytical_result. Business justification: Data queries in clinical trials can be raised against bioanalytical results (PK/PD, DMPK data) in the EDC, not only against standard lab results. The existing lab_result_id FK covers standard lab resu']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Lab queries must be resolved before database lock. The database_lock_event tracks outstanding_query_count and pre-lock QC checklist completion. Linking lab_query to database_lock_event enables the pre']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific laboratory result record in the lab_result product that this query was raised against. Core linkage between the query and the underlying data point.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Lab data queries are frequently raised to document or resolve protocol deviations affecting lab data (e.g., wrong collection timepoint, incorrect tube). Direct traceability from lab_query to protocol_']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Lab queries that reveal systematic data issues (e.g., multiple sites with same anomaly, instrument calibration failure) escalate to formal quality events. Linking lab_query to quality_event enables es']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this laboratory query was raised. Links the query to the parent protocol and sponsor context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site associated with the subject whose laboratory result triggered this query. Used for site-level query metrics and monitoring.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the specimen was collected and the lab result was generated, providing temporal context for the query.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose laboratory result is the subject of this query. Supports subject-level data quality tracking.']
  assigned_to_party_type varchar [note: 'Type of organizational party to whom the query is assigned for response — investigational site, central laboratory, local laboratory, sponsor, or CRO (Contract Research Organization). Determines routing and accountability.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the query was cancelled, if applicable (e.g., query raised in error, duplicate query, data issue resolved through another mechanism). Required for cancelled queries to maintain ALCOA+ audit trail integrity.']
  corrected_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The laboratory result value after correction, if a data change was made as a result of this query. Null if no correction was applied. Provides after-state for audit trail per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this lab_query record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Audit trail field for data lineage and ALCOA+ compliance. Distinct from raised_datetime which captures the business event time.']
  data_correction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resolution of this query resulted in a data value change in the source EDC or LIMS record. True = a data correction was made; False = original data confirmed or query cancelled without change. Key metric for data quality reporting.']
  dbl_blocking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this open query is blocking the Database Lock (DBL) for the study or subject. True = must be resolved before DBL can proceed; False = non-blocking. Critical for study close-out planning and timeline management.']
  ecrf_field_name varchar [note: 'Specific field name within the eCRF form that contains the queried laboratory data value (e.g., \'LBORRES\', \'LBORRESU\'). Enables precise identification of the data point in question for site response and EDC correction.']
  ecrf_form_name varchar [note: 'Name of the eCRF form in the EDC (Medidata Rave) on which the queried laboratory data field resides (e.g., \'Laboratory Results\', \'Hematology\'). Supports EDC navigation and query routing to the correct data entry form.']
  edc_query_code varchar [note: 'Native query identifier assigned by the source EDC system (Medidata Rave) or LIMS. Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver Layer record and the operational system of record for reconciliation and audit.']
  escalation_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the query was formally escalated. Null if no escalation has occurred. Used for escalation cycle time analytics and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this query has been formally escalated to a higher authority (e.g., from CRA to Clinical Lead, or from site to sponsor medical monitor) due to non-response, SLA breach, or data criticality. Triggers escalation workflow in CTMS.']
  original_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The laboratory result value as it existed at the time the query was raised, captured as a string to accommodate numeric, text, and coded values. Provides before-state for audit trail and data lineage per ALCOA+ requirements.']
  query_category varchar [note: 'Functional category indicating which business process or team initiated the query — data management programmatic check, medical monitor review, safety review, statistical review, or site-initiated clarification. Supports workload analytics and process improvement.']
  query_iteration_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this query has been re-opened or re-issued against the same laboratory result field, indicating the number of response-review cycles. A high iteration count signals persistent data quality issues at the site or lab.']
  query_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally visible query identifier assigned by the EDC (Medidata Rave) or LIMS system at the time of query creation. Used for cross-system tracking and communication with sites and labs (e.g., QRY-2024-00123).']
  query_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the query indicating urgency of resolution. Critical queries (e.g., those linked to SAE data or DBL-blocking issues) require immediate attention. Drives escalation and SLA enforcement.']
  query_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record in which this query originated — EDC (Medidata Rave), a Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS), Oracle Clinical One, or a manually entered query. Supports multi-system data quality governance.']
  query_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the laboratory data query. Drives workflow routing, SLA monitoring, and DBL readiness assessment. Open = awaiting response; Answered = response received, pending DM review; Closed = resolved and accepted; Cancelled = withdrawn.']
  query_text varchar [note: 'Full narrative text of the query as raised by the data manager or medical monitor, describing the data issue, the specific concern, and the action requested from the site or laboratory. Core ALCOA+ documentation field.']
  query_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason the query was raised against the laboratory result. Drives triage, routing, and resolution workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: missing_value|implausible_result|unit_mismatch|collection_time_deviation|reference_range_deviation|transcription_error|duplicate_entry|method_deviation — promote to reference product]']
  raised_by_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the person or process that raised the query (e.g., Data Manager, Medical Monitor, Clinical Research Associate (CRA), Statistician, or automated system check). Denormalized for reporting without requiring a user-role join.']
  raised_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when the query was formally raised and entered into the EDC or LIMS. Principal business event timestamp for the query lifecycle. Required for SLA calculation and ALCOA+ contemporaneity compliance.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Outcome action taken to resolve the query: data value was corrected in the EDC/LIMS, original data was confirmed as correct, query was withdrawn by the raiser, or no action was required. Critical for data audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  resolution_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when the query was formally closed or cancelled by the data manager after reviewing the site/lab response. Marks the end of the query lifecycle. Used for cycle time analytics and DBL readiness.']
  response_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) when the site or laboratory submitted their response to the query. Used for SLA compliance measurement and ALCOA+ contemporaneity tracking.']
  response_text varchar [note: 'Full narrative response provided by the site or laboratory in reply to the query, explaining the data discrepancy, confirming the value, or documenting a correction. Core ALCOA+ documentation field for audit trail.']
  safety_relevance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the queried laboratory result is associated with a safety event (SAE, AE, or SUSAR) or a critical value, requiring expedited resolution. True = safety-relevant; False = routine data quality query. Drives prioritization and safety reporting workflows.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the query response or resolution exceeded the defined SLA target days. True = SLA was breached; False = responded within SLA. Drives escalation workflows and site performance KPI reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this lab_query record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports incremental data processing, change detection, and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  usubjid varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM-compliant unique subject identifier (sponsor-study-subject concatenation) denormalized onto the query record for direct regulatory submission traceability and ALCOA+ audit compliance without requiring a join.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined visit name (e.g., Screening, Week 4, End of Treatment) during which the queried laboratory specimen was collected. Denormalized for query analytics and reporting without requiring a visit join.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a data query raised against a laboratory result in the EDC (Medidata Rave) or LIMS. Captures query ID, query type (missing value/implausible result/unit mismatch/collection time deviation), query text, raised by (data manager/medical monitor), raised date, assigned to (site/lab), response text, resolution date, and query status (open/answered/closed/cancelled). Supports ALCOA+ data quality compliance for lab data.'
}

Table laboratory.specimen_aliquot {
  specimen_aliquot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the specimen aliquot record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  bioanalytical_assay_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the validated bioanalytical assay method assigned to test this aliquot. Applicable for PK, PD, and biomarker aliquots.']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the central or local laboratory responsible for receiving, storing, or testing this aliquot.']
  parent_specimen_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary specimen from which this aliquot was derived. Establishes the parent-child chain of custody lineage.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment manifest under which this aliquot was transported to a testing or storage laboratory. Supports chain of custody and logistics tracking.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this aliquot was collected and is being managed.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical site where the parent specimen was collected and the aliquot originated.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject study visit at which the parent specimen was collected. Links aliquot to the clinical event context.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) from whom the parent specimen was collected. Supports subject-level sample accountability.']
  aliquot_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known unique barcode or label identifier assigned to this aliquot at the time of processing. Used for physical tracking and chain of custody.']
  aliquot_purpose varchar [note: 'Designated analytical or storage purpose for this aliquot. Drives routing to the appropriate laboratory or storage tier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_testing|pk_analysis|biomarker|backup|archive|exploratory — promote to reference product if additional purposes are added]']
  aliquot_seq_num varchar [note: 'Sequential integer identifying the order in which this aliquot was derived from the parent specimen (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Supports multi-aliquot tracking per specimen.']
  aliquot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the aliquot. Drives chain of custody accountability and biorepository management workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_storage|in_transit|in_testing|consumed|destroyed|lost — promote to reference product]']
  blinded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this aliquot is subject to study blinding (i.e., treatment assignment is masked). Governs access controls and prevents unblinding during active study phases.']
  box_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the box or tray within the rack where this aliquot is placed. Part of the three-dimensional storage coordinate system.']
  chain_of_custody_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a complete, unbroken chain of custody record exists for this aliquot from collection through current status. A value of False triggers a compliance review.']
  collection_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time the parent specimen was collected from the subject. Inherited from the parent specimen for chain of custody and stability calculations.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for laboratory technician or data manager notes regarding this aliquot (e.g., visible hemolysis, lipemia, unusual appearance, processing deviations).']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of physical container used to store the aliquot (e.g., cryovial, EDTA tube, serum separator tube, microcentrifuge tube). Affects stability and compatibility with analytical methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cryovial|edta_tube|sst_tube|microcentrifuge_tube|matrix_tube|capillary_tube — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this aliquot record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  data_origin varchar [note: 'Source system from which this aliquot record was ingested (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One, laboratory information management system). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  destruction_date date [note: 'Date on which the aliquot was destroyed or discarded, if applicable. Required for chain of custody closure and regulatory accountability.']
  destruction_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for aliquot destruction or discard. Required for regulatory accountability and chain of custody documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stability_expired|volume_insufficient|testing_complete|sponsor_request|protocol_deviation|lost|contaminated — promote to reference product]']
  freeze_thaw_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of freeze-thaw cycles this aliquot has undergone. Exceeding the validated limit may compromise analyte integrity and render the aliquot unsuitable for testing.']
  max_freeze_thaw_allowed varchar [note: 'Validated maximum number of freeze-thaw cycles permitted for this aliquot type before analyte stability is compromised. Defined in the bioanalytical method validation plan.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric position coordinate within the box (e.g., A1, B12, H08) identifying the exact slot where the aliquot is stored. Enables precise physical retrieval.']
  processing_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time the aliquot was separated from the parent specimen and prepared for storage or shipment. Critical for stability and pre-analytical variable tracking.']
  protocol_num varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned protocol number for the clinical study. Denormalized for direct regulatory reporting and specimen labeling without requiring a study join.']
  rack_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the rack within the storage unit where this aliquot is placed. Part of the three-dimensional storage coordinate system (rack/box/position).']
  stability_expiry_date date [note: 'Date beyond which the aliquot is considered analytically unstable based on validated long-term stability data. Triggers discard or re-evaluation workflows.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this aliquot (e.g., -80, -20, 4, 25). Used for stability monitoring and deviation assessment.']
  storage_unit_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage unit (freezer, refrigerator, liquid nitrogen tank) where this aliquot is currently placed. Supports biorepository location management.']
  storage_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage unit housing this aliquot (e.g., -80°C freezer, -20°C freezer, refrigerator, liquid nitrogen vapor phase). Determines stability expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: freezer|-80_freezer|-20_freezer|refrigerator|ln2_vapor|ln2_liquid|ambient — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this aliquot has experienced a documented temperature excursion outside the validated storage range. Triggers stability impact assessment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this aliquot record was last modified. Supports audit trail, change detection, and incremental data pipeline processing.']
  usubjid varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM-compliant unique subject identifier (sponsor-subject-site concatenation). Required for regulatory submission datasets and cross-domain subject traceability.']
  volume_ml decimal(10,4) [note: 'Volume of the aliquot in millilitres at the time of processing. Used for sufficiency assessment and inventory management.']
  volume_sufficient_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the remaining aliquot volume is sufficient to complete the assigned assay or testing requirement. Drives re-collection or backup aliquot retrieval workflows.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the aliquot volume. Typically mL for liquid specimens; mg or g for tissue aliquots.']

  Note: 'Master record for aliquots derived from a primary specimen for distribution to multiple testing labs or long-term storage, including storage placement tracking. Captures parent specimen ID, aliquot sequence number, volume, container type, storage unit (freezer/refrigerator/LN2 tank), rack/box/position coordinates, storage temperature, freeze-thaw cycle count, aliquot purpose (clinical testing/PK/biomarker/backup/archive), storage event history (placement/transfer/retrieval/destruction), and current status. Supports biorepository management, chain of custody, and long-term sample accountability.'
}

Table laboratory.panel_facility_qualification {
  panel_facility_qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the panel_facility_qualification association']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the laboratory facility that holds or is being evaluated for qualification to perform the referenced panel.']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the laboratory test panel or battery that is the subject of this facility qualification record.']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'The specific accreditation or certification standard (e.g., CLIA, CAP, ISO 15189, GLP) under which this facility-panel qualification is governed and auditable.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this qualification record is no longer valid. Supports historical tracking of past qualifications for regulatory audit trails.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this qualification record is valid and the facility is authorized to receive specimens for this panel.']
  lab_certification_standard varchar [note: 'Accreditation or certification standard under which the performing laboratory is certified for this panel (e.g., CAP, ISO 15189, CLIA, GLP, GCLP). Required for regulatory submission credibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cap|iso_15189|clia|glp|gclp|jci|ukas — promote to reference product] [Moved from lab_panel: lab_panel.lab_certification_standard describes the certification standard under which the panel is performed, but this standard is facility-specific — different facilities may hold different certifications for the same panel. This attribute belongs on the qualification association (as certification_standard), not on the panel master definition.]']
  lab_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this panel is performed at a central laboratory (single reference lab processing all specimens), a local laboratory (site-level lab), or both. Central labs provide standardized reference ranges; local labs may be used for safety monitoring. [Moved from lab_panel: lab_panel.lab_type indicates whether the panel is performed at a central or local lab, but this is a property of the facility-panel qualification (which type of facility is approved for this panel), not an intrinsic property of the panel definition itself. It should be derived from the qualified facility\'s facility_type rather than stored on the panel.]']
  qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the laboratory facility was formally qualified and approved to perform this panel, following successful method validation or proficiency testing.']
  qualification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current qualification lapses and renewal or re-qualification is required. Used to trigger proactive renewal workflows and block expired facilities from specimen routing.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the facility\'s qualification to perform this panel. Drives specimen routing eligibility — only \'active\' qualifications permit specimen assignment.']
  turnaround_time_hours decimal [note: 'Facility-specific turnaround time SLA in hours for this panel at this facility. Distinct from the panel-level SLA on lab_panel, as TAT varies by facility capacity, geography, and contractual terms.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the formal Qualification contract between a lab_panel and a lab_facility in clinical trial laboratory operations. It captures the operational approval record confirming that a specific laboratory facility is certified and validated to perform a specific test panel, including qualification status, validity dates, facility-specific turnaround time SLAs, and the governing certification standard. Each record is the authoritative gating artifact used during specimen routing and protocol setup to confirm a facility is approved to process a given panel.. Existence Justification: In clinical trial laboratory operations, sponsors and CROs formally qualify specific laboratory facilities to perform specific test panels before specimen routing can occur. A single lab panel (e.g., a PK panel) may be qualified at multiple central lab facilities across different geographies, and a single facility holds qualifications for many different panels. This qualification relationship is actively managed, has a defined lifecycle (qualification, expiry, renewal), and is the operational gating mechanism for specimen routing decisions.'
}

Table supply.imp_master {
  imp_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each investigational medicinal product master record within the supply domain. Serves as the primary key for all downstream supply chain, dispensing, and accountability references.']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: IND/IMPD covers the investigational product — regulatory affairs and supply teams must verify IND status before manufacturing and releasing IMP. IND annual reports require product-level linkage. `ind_']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: IMP master records are directly associated with regulatory submissions (IND/IMPD/CTA). imp_master has ind_impd_reference as plain text. Linking to regulatory_binder enables direct navigation from the ']
  special_designation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.special_designation. Business justification: Orphan drug, breakthrough therapy, and fast-track designations directly affect IMP supply strategy — expedited supply timelines, special labeling requirements, and fee waivers. Supply planners must kn']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: IMPD/IND dossiers and GMP certificates are ICH GCP essential TMF documents directly associated with each IMP master record. Regulatory inspectors expect direct traceability from the IMP master to its ']
  active_substance_name varchar [note: 'International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or chemical name of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) contained in the IMP. Used for regulatory submissions, pharmacovigilance coding, and WHO-DD cross-referencing.']
  atc_code varchar [note: 'WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code assigned to the active substance. Used for pharmacovigilance signal detection, WHO-DD cross-referencing, and regulatory reporting.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding configuration applicable to this IMP within the trial design: open-label (all parties know treatment), single-blind (subject blinded), double-blind (subject and investigator blinded), or triple-blind (subject, investigator, and sponsor blinded). Drives labelling, packaging, and RTSM randomisation configuration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the IMP master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under ALCOA+ principles, 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  destruction_method varchar [note: 'Approved method for destruction of unused, expired, or returned IMP. Required for accountability reconciliation, environmental compliance, and regulatory documentation of drug disposition.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which the IMP was formally discontinued from use in clinical trials. Populated when product_status transitions to \'discontinued\'. Used for supply chain close-out, accountability reconciliation, and regulatory reporting.']
  dispensing_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure used for dispensing the IMP to subjects at the site level (e.g., kit, vial, tablet, capsule). Aligns with RTSM dispensing records and site accountability logs. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kit|vial|tablet|capsule|syringe|bottle|patch — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Physical form of the IMP as administered to subjects (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, infusion, patch, oral solution). Drives dispensing instructions, packaging requirements, and cold-chain logistics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|infusion|patch|oral_solution|powder|cream|inhaler|suppository — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this IMP master record version is considered authoritative and approved for use in supply planning and dispensing. Supports version control and regulatory compliance for product specification changes.']
  formulation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the IMP formulation including excipients, concentration, and any special formulation characteristics (e.g., modified-release, lyophilised). Sourced from sponsor-provided specifications and IMPD. Supports pharmacist review and site dispensing guidance.']
  gmp_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the GMP certificate issued by the relevant regulatory authority for the manufacturing site producing this IMP. Required for regulatory submissions, batch release, and inspection readiness.']
  impd_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the IMPD or IND module that governs the current specifications for this IMP. Ensures traceability between the product master record and the regulatory submission version in effect. Supports inspection readiness and TMF completeness.']
  is_biological boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP is a biological product (e.g., monoclonal antibody, vaccine, gene therapy, cell therapy) as opposed to a small-molecule chemical entity. Drives regulatory pathway selection (BLA vs NDA/NDA), cold-chain requirements, and special handling protocols.']
  is_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP requires cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled storage and transport, typically 2–8°C or below). Drives cold-chain packaging selection, qualified shipper requirements, and temperature monitoring obligations.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP is classified as a controlled substance under applicable national regulations (e.g., DEA Schedule in the US, Misuse of Drugs Act in the UK). Triggers enhanced accountability, storage security, and regulatory reporting requirements.']
  is_orphan_designated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP has received orphan drug designation from FDA (ODD) or EMA (OMP), conferring special regulatory incentives and obligations. Relevant for regulatory strategy, supply planning, and sponsor reporting.']
  manufacturer_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the IMP is manufactured. Used for import/export regulatory compliance, customs documentation, and supply chain risk assessment.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the GMP-certified manufacturer responsible for producing the IMP. Required for regulatory submissions (IND/IMPD), batch release documentation, and supply chain traceability.']
  max_storage_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP. Used to define temperature excursion thresholds, configure temperature monitoring devices, and assess cold-chain compliance at depots and sites.']
  min_storage_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum permissible storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP. Used to define temperature excursion thresholds, configure temperature monitoring devices, and assess cold-chain compliance at depots and sites.']
  ndc_code varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned National Drug Code (NDC) for commercially procured comparators or approved products used in the trial. Applicable for US-sourced products. Used for procurement, accountability, and regulatory reporting.']
  packaging_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of the IMP packaging unit configuration as used in the trial (e.g., \'blister pack of 28 tablets\', \'vial 10 mL\', \'pre-filled syringe 1 mL\'). Drives kit assembly, labelling design, and RTSM dispensing unit definitions.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the IMP within the sponsor\'s product catalogue. Used as the externally-known business identifier across RTSM, EDC, and regulatory submissions. Maps to the product code field in Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the investigational medicinal product as defined by the sponsor. May be a code name, development compound name, or commercial name for comparators. Used in labelling, supply documentation, and regulatory submissions.']
  product_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the IMP master record within the supply domain. Active records are eligible for supply planning and dispensing. Inactive or discontinued records are retained for audit and historical accountability.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the product role within the clinical trial: IMP (the investigational compound under study), comparator (active reference product), placebo (inactive control), or auxiliary medicinal product (supportive agent not under investigation). Drives supply planning, labelling requirements, and regulatory classification.']
  regulatory_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory filing category under which the IMP is authorised for use in the clinical trial. Determines the applicable regulatory framework, submission requirements, and oversight obligations across jurisdictions (FDA IND, EMA IMPD/CTA, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|IMPD|CTA|NDA|BLA|MAA|exempt — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retest_period_months varchar [note: 'Period in months after which the IMP must be retested before continued use, applicable where a formal expiry date is not assigned. Relevant for bulk drug substances and certain investigational compounds. Supports expiry and retest date calculations in RTSM.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The path by which the IMP is delivered into the body (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical). Required for eCRF design, SDTM CM domain mapping, and regulatory labelling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]']
  rtsm_product_code varchar [note: 'The product identifier as configured in the Medidata Rave RTSM system. Used to reconcile the IMP master record with RTSM kit assignments, dispensing records, and inventory transactions. Source system reference for integration and lineage.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Approved shelf life of the IMP in months from the date of manufacture, as established by stability studies and documented in the IMPD/IND. Used for expiry management, resupply planning, and depot/site inventory accountability.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Indicates how the IMP is obtained for the clinical trial: manufactured (custom GMP production), commercially procured (purchased from market), over-the-counter, or imported. Drives procurement workflows, GMP documentation requirements, and supply chain planning.']
  sponsor_product_code varchar [note: 'The sponsor\'s internal product code or compound number for the IMP (e.g., development compound identifier such as \'ABC-123\'). Distinct from the CRO\'s product_code; used for sponsor-facing reporting, cross-study portfolio analytics, and regulatory correspondence.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage conditions for the IMP as specified in the product dossier and labelling (e.g., \'Store at 2–8°C\', \'Store below 25°C, protect from light\'). Drives cold-chain logistics, depot and site storage compliance, and temperature excursion management.']
  strength_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the active substance strength (e.g., mg, g, mcg, mg/mL, IU). Combined with strength_value to fully define the IMP dose strength for labelling, dispensing, and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|g|mcg|mg/mL|IU|%|mmol — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  strength_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric quantity of the active substance per dosage unit (e.g., 10, 500, 1.5). Must be interpreted in conjunction with strength_unit. Represents the principal quantitative fact about the IMP formulation. Used in dispensing records, RTSM kit assignment, and regulatory labelling.']
  units_per_kit varchar [note: 'Number of individual dosage units (tablets, vials, syringes, etc.) contained in one RTSM dispensing kit. Used for supply planning, kit assembly, and accountability reconciliation in Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the IMP master record. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements under ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for all investigational and comparator products used in clinical trials, including sponsor IMPs, active comparators, and placebos. Captures product identity, product type (IMP/comparator/placebo), formulation details, dosage form, strength, storage conditions (including cold-chain requirements), shelf life, sourcing strategy (manufactured vs. commercially procured), regulatory classification, and IND/IMPD reference. Serves as the SSOT for all drug product definitions within the supply domain. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and sponsor-provided specifications.'
}

  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment arm (e.g., active drug, placebo, comparator) that this kit definition is associated with. Drives randomization assignment logic in RTSM.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this kit definition was formally approved for use in manufacturing and dispensing. Required for GCP compliance and audit trail documentation.']
  blinding_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the blinding configuration applied to this kit definition. Determines whether the subject, investigator, and/or sponsor are masked to treatment assignment. Critical for maintaining trial integrity and regulatory compliance.']

  destruction_method varchar [note: 'The approved method for destruction of returned or expired kits of this definition type. Required for regulatory accountability documentation and environmental compliance.']

  kit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the kit definition template. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the kit by its therapeutic content category. Distinguishes between active drug kits, placebo kits, comparator kits, combination kits, device kits, and ancillary supply kits. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  label_language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) for the primary language of the kit label text (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'de\', \'ja\'). Required for multi-country studies with country-specific labeling.']
  label_text varchar [note: 'The regulatory-approved label text to be printed on physical kits manufactured from this definition. Must comply with applicable labeling regulations and may include study number, kit number, storage conditions, and investigational use statement.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the manufacturing or packaging site responsible for producing physical kits from this definition. Used for supply chain traceability and GMP compliance.']
  max_storage_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for kits of this definition. Used for cold-chain monitoring alerts and excursion detection.']
  min_storage_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for kits of this definition. Used for cold-chain monitoring alerts and excursion detection.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this kit definition record was last modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and change tracking for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  pack_size varchar [note: 'The number of individual dosage units (e.g., tablets, capsules, vials) contained in one physical kit of this definition. Used for supply planning, dispensing calculations, and accountability reconciliation.']
  pack_size_unit varchar [note: 'The unit descriptor for the pack size count (e.g., tablet, capsule, vial). Clarifies what each unit in the pack_size count represents. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|ampoule|syringe|patch|sachet|bottle — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  randomization_scheme varchar [note: 'The randomization methodology applied to kits of this definition type within the RTSM. Determines how kits are allocated to subjects during enrollment and randomization events.']
  retest_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from manufacture after which kits of this definition must be retested before continued use. Applicable for IMP components with defined retest periods rather than fixed expiry dates.']
  return_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether used or unused kits of this definition must be returned to the depot or sponsor for accountability reconciliation and destruction. Drives returns logistics workflows.']
  rtsm_kit_type_code varchar [note: 'The internal kit type code as configured in the Medidata Rave RTSM system. Used for system-to-system integration and reconciliation between the RTSM and downstream data systems.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The approved shelf life duration in days for kits manufactured from this definition, measured from the date of manufacture. Used to calculate expiry dates on physical kit instances and to trigger re-supply or destruction workflows.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for kits of this definition type. Drives cold-chain logistics planning, depot storage allocation, and site storage compliance monitoring.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this kit definition (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented when the kit definition is amended due to protocol changes, labeling updates, or manufacturing changes. Supports audit trail and regulatory traceability.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'The type of study visit at which this kit definition is intended to be dispensed (e.g., baseline, treatment, follow-up, unscheduled). Drives RTSM dispensing schedule configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: screening|baseline|treatment|follow_up|unscheduled|end_of_study|early_termination — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Defines randomization kit configurations used in RTSM (Medidata Rave). Captures kit type, treatment arm association, blinding level, component IMPs, pack size, label text, kit number range, and applicable study/protocol version. Serves as the template from which physical kits are manufactured and assigned. Distinct from kit_inventory which tracks physical instances.'
}

  batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing batch or lot number of the IMP contained in this kit. Essential for batch recall management, drug accountability, and regulatory submissions. Sourced from the Certificate of Analysis (CoA).']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the kit identity (treatment assignment) is blinded, unblinded, or open-label for this record. Critical for maintaining study integrity and controlling access to treatment assignment information.']
  cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the kit has maintained cold-chain compliance throughout its custody chain based on temperature monitoring data. A value of False triggers a quality investigation before dispensing.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this kit requires cold-chain storage and transportation (e.g., 2–8°C refrigeration or frozen conditions). Drives logistics planning, temperature monitoring requirements, and qualified packaging selection.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for site staff or supply chain personnel to record notes about this kit, such as reasons for quarantine, partial dispensing explanations, or deviation observations. Supports ALCOA+ documentation requirements.']
