  current_location_type varchar [note: 'Categorical indicator of where the kit physically resides in the supply chain at the current point in time. Used in conjunction with depot_id or site_id to determine precise location.']
  depot_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the kit was received at the supply depot from the manufacturer or another depot. Initiates the depot inventory record and chain-of-custody tracking.']
  destroyed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the kit was confirmed destroyed per the destruction certificate. Required for drug accountability reconciliation and regulatory close-out documentation.']
  destruction_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the official destruction certificate issued upon confirmed destruction of the kit. Required for drug accountability close-out and regulatory submission documentation.']
  dispensed_quantity varchar [note: 'The actual number of dosage units dispensed to the subject from this kit. Used for drug accountability reconciliation and compliance with protocol-specified dosing.']
  dispensed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this kit was physically dispensed to the subject by site staff. Captured in RTSM (Medidata Rave) as part of the chain-of-custody audit trail. Conforms to ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the investigational medicinal product within this kit as printed on the label. Kits must not be dispensed after this date. Used for expiry management, re-supply planning, and regulatory accountability.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the physical randomization kit by the RTSM (Medidata Rave). This is the primary business identifier used in drug accountability logs, dispensing records, and regulatory submissions.']
  kit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the kit within the supply chain. \'available\' = at depot or site, ready for assignment; \'assigned\' = allocated to a subject via RTSM but not yet dispensed; \'dispensed\' = physically given to subject; \'expired\' = past expiry date; \'returned\' = returned from site or subject; \'destroyed\' = destruction confirmed; \'quarantine\' = held pending investigation; \'lost\' = unaccounted. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|assigned|dispensed|expired|returned|destroyed|quarantine|lost — promote to reference product]']
  label_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the IMP label applied to this kit. Used to verify that the correct, IRB/IEC-approved label version was used at the time of dispensing and for regulatory label reconciliation.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the investigational medicinal product in this kit was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculation, batch traceability, and regulatory accountability.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'The version of the study protocol under which this kit was dispensed or assigned. Ensures that kit dispensing is traceable to the correct protocol amendment for regulatory compliance.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The unique randomization number assigned to the subject-kit pairing by the RTSM (IRT/IVRS) at the point of subject randomization. Links the kit to the randomization schedule for unblinding and accountability.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the kit must be retested or re-evaluated for continued use, particularly for products with conditional shelf-life extensions. Distinct from expiry_date; used for stability management.']
  returned_quantity varchar [note: 'The number of dosage units returned by the subject or site for this kit. Used in drug accountability reconciliation to calculate compliance and account for unused medication.']
  returned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the kit was returned by the subject or site to the depot or destruction facility. Part of the chain-of-custody audit trail for drug accountability.']
  rtsm_kit_code varchar [note: 'The source system identifier for this kit record as assigned by the RTSM module in Medidata Rave. Used for data lineage, reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the operational RTSM system, and audit trail traceability.']
  site_receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the kit was received and confirmed at the investigational site. Marks the transfer of custody from depot/logistics to site. Used for site inventory reconciliation.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for this kit as specified on the label and protocol. Drives warehouse and site storage assignment, cold-chain logistics, and temperature excursion monitoring.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion event has been recorded for this kit during storage or transit. When True, the kit must be quarantined and assessed by QA before dispensing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this kit inventory record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Tracks physical randomization kit instances across the supply chain — from manufacture through depot receipt, site shipment, dispensing, and destruction. Each record represents a unique kit number with current status (available, assigned, dispensed, expired, returned, destroyed), location (depot or site), expiry date, cold-chain compliance flag, and chain-of-custody timestamps. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.'
}

Table supply.depot {
  depot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the clinical supply depot. Primary key for the depot master record.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Depots serving as IMP import/distribution nodes must be authorized under the CTA for the relevant country. CTA-to-depot traceability is required for regulatory inspection readiness and to confirm that']
  country_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.country_registration. Business justification: Depots operating in a country must align with the country registration status of the IMP they store and distribute. Regulatory country registration governs which depots can hold and distribute product']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the depot facility. Used for logistics routing, regulatory filings, and cold-chain shipment documentation.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address detail (suite, building, floor) for the depot facility.']
  ambient_storage_capacity_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total ambient temperature storage capacity of the depot measured in cubic metres. Ambient storage is typically 15–25°C. Used for supply planning and capacity allocation across studies.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the depot facility is located. Used for geographic distribution planning and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  cold_chain_qualified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the depot has been formally qualified for cold-chain IMP handling, including validated temperature monitoring, qualified packaging, and trained personnel per WHO and GDP guidelines.']
  controlled_substance_licensed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the depot is licensed to store and handle controlled substances (narcotics, psychotropics) as defined by DEA Schedule (US), Home Office (UK), or equivalent national authority. Required for studies involving opioids, CNS agents, or other scheduled compounds.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the depot is located. Drives regulatory jurisdiction, import/export rules, and cold-chain routing logic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the depot master record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  ctms_depot_code varchar [note: 'Depot identifier as recorded in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the supply chain data product and clinical trial management records.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the depot was or is scheduled to be decommissioned and removed from the active clinical supply network. Null for currently active depots.']
  depot_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the depot within the clinical supply network. Used in RTSM (Randomization and Trial Supply Management) and CTMS for depot referencing across studies.']
  depot_name varchar [note: 'Full legal or operational name of the clinical supply depot as registered with regulatory authorities and used in the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  depot_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the depot. Active depots are approved for IMP storage and distribution. Suspended depots are temporarily halted pending investigation or re-qualification. Decommissioned depots are permanently closed.']
  depot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the depot within the clinical supply logistics network. Central depots serve as primary distribution hubs; regional depots serve geographic clusters; local depots are near investigational sites; satellite depots are temporary; returns depots handle IMP returns and destruction.']
  destruction_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the depot is authorized to perform on-site destruction of expired, returned, or unusable IMPs in compliance with GMP and environmental regulations.']
  erp_vendor_code varchar [note: 'Vendor or supplier identifier for the depot as registered in SAP S/4HANA. Used for purchase order processing, invoice matching, and financial reconciliation of depot service fees.']
  frozen_storage_capacity_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total frozen storage capacity (typically -20°C or -80°C) of the depot in cubic metres. Required for biologics, gene therapies, and other temperature-sensitive IMPs.']
  gmp_certificate_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the depot\'s GMP certificate expires. Triggers re-qualification workflows and may suspend depot operations if not renewed prior to expiry.']
  gmp_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate number issued by the regulatory authority confirming GMP compliance for the depot. Referenced in regulatory submissions and audit documentation.']
  gmp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the depot holds a current Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) certification as required for IMP storage and handling under ICH Q7/Q10 and applicable regional regulations.']
  import_license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the depot\'s import license for IMP receipt. Expired licenses block inbound international shipments.']
  import_license_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory import license number permitting the depot to receive IMP shipments from international origins. Required for cross-border clinical supply operations.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent GMP/GDP audit conducted at the depot by the sponsor, CRO, or regulatory authority. Audit outcomes are tracked in the quality management system (MasterControl).']
  last_qualification_date date [note: 'Date on which the depot last completed a formal qualification or re-qualification exercise (IQ/OQ/PQ). Qualification records are maintained in the eTMF and are subject to regulatory inspection.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next GMP/GDP audit at the depot. Used for audit planning and compliance calendar management.']
  next_qualification_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next depot qualification or re-qualification. Drives preventive maintenance and compliance calendar in the CTMS and quality management system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or depot-specific constraints relevant to clinical supply operations. Not intended for regulatory documentation.']
  operational_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the depot became operationally active for clinical supply activities. Used for lifecycle tracking and regulatory documentation.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the depot facility. Used for logistics routing and regulatory jurisdiction mapping.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary operational contact at the depot. Used for shipment alerts, RTSM notifications, and supply chain communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary operational contact person at the depot. Used for logistics coordination, shipment notifications, and regulatory correspondence.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the depot operational contact. Used for urgent logistics coordination and cold-chain incident escalation.']
  refrigerated_storage_capacity_m3 decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total refrigerated storage capacity (typically 2–8°C) of the depot in cubic metres. Critical for IMPs requiring cold-chain management.']
  returns_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the depot is authorized to receive returned Investigational Medicinal Products (IMPs) from investigational sites. Required for accountability reconciliation and destruction workflows.']
  rtsm_depot_code varchar [note: 'Depot identifier as configured in the RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM). Used to link depot master records to RTSM dispensing, randomization kit assignment, and resupply trigger logic.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the depot is located. Relevant for import/export licensing and regional regulatory compliance.']
  temperature_monitoring_system varchar [note: 'Name or model of the continuous temperature monitoring system installed at the depot (e.g., Sensitech TempTale, Berlinger ELPRO). Ensures 21 CFR Part 11 compliant electronic records of storage conditions.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the depot location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling shipment windows, expiry checks, and operational hours alignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the depot master record. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  wholesale_distribution_license varchar [note: 'License number authorizing the depot to distribute investigational medicinal products (IMPs) as a wholesale distributor. Required under EU GDP guidelines and equivalent national regulations.']
  wholesale_license_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the wholesale distribution license. Depot cannot legally distribute IMPs after this date without renewal.']

  Note: 'Master record for clinical supply depots (central and regional warehouses) that store and distribute IMPs. Captures depot name, geographic location, GMP certification status, temperature-controlled storage capabilities (ambient, refrigerated, frozen), qualified person details, regulatory licenses, and operational contact information. Depots are the primary nodes in the clinical supply logistics network.'
}

Table supply.site_inventory {
  site_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each site inventory record representing a stock position of an Investigational Medicinal Product (IMP) or kit type at a specific supply chain node (investigational site or depot).']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Site inventory is tracked per treatment arm for arm-specific stock management, reorder triggers, and IMP accountability. treatment_arm_code is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to study.arm enabl']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Site inventory is governed by the CTA for the country where the site operates. Accountability reconciliation reports and regulatory inspection readiness checks reference the CTA under which inventory ']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Investigational Medicinal Product (IMP) master record defining the drug substance, formulation, and dosage form tracked in this inventory position.']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: Interim analysis outcomes that revise enrollment targets or arm allocations directly trigger site-level inventory adjustments (revised minimum_stock_qty, reorder_point). In adaptive trials, site inven']
  kit_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the randomization kit type as defined in the RTSM (Randomization and Trial Supply Management) system (Medidata Rave). Identifies the specific kit configuration (e.g., treatment arm, dose level, pack size) tracked in this inventory record.']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Site inventory is tracked by batch (site_inventory.batch_number is denormalized). Each site inventory record represents stock of a specific manufacturing batch at a site. Adding manufacture_batch_id c']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Site inventory discrepancies, storage condition failures, or physical count variances trigger quality events. quality_event has study_site_id but no site_inventory_id. This link supports site-level IM']
  receipt_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Site inventory records are created or updated when a shipment is received at the investigational site. Adding receipt_shipment_id links the site inventory record to the inbound shipment. This enables ']
  site_risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.site_risk_assessment. Business justification: Site risk tier from site_risk_assessment directly drives minimum_stock_qty and reorder_point thresholds in site_inventory. Risk-based supply management requires tracing which risk assessment informed ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this inventory is being tracked. Links inventory records to the sponsoring protocol and enables study-level accountability reconciliation.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site or depot location where this inventory is physically held. Enables site-level stock position reporting and resupply trigger management.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Site inventory is managed per treatment arm for blinded stock control and RTSM resupply triggers. treatment_arm_code is a denormalized plain column; replacing with FK enforces referential integrity an']
  accountability_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP accountability reconciliation has been completed for this inventory position. Set to True when all received, dispensed, returned, expired, and destroyed quantities have been formally reconciled and signed off per GCP requirements.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s batch or lot number for the IMP stock held at this location. Required for regulatory traceability, recall management, and accountability reconciliation. Corresponds to the batch/lot number on the IMP label.']
  blinding_level varchar [note: 'Blinding level applicable to this inventory position, indicating whether the IMP identity is masked at this location. Drives labeling requirements and access controls for inventory data to preserve study integrity.']
  destruction_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether destruction of expired, returned, or quarantined IMP stock at this location has been formally authorized by the sponsor or designee. Required before physical destruction can proceed per GCP and regulatory requirements.']
  destruction_date date [note: 'Date on which IMP units or kits were destroyed at or from this location. Required for regulatory accountability documentation and study close-out reporting.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the IMP batch or kit stock held at this location, as stated on the product label. Used for expiry management, resupply planning, and to prevent dispensing of expired product to study subjects.']
  imp_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used to count IMP stock at this location (e.g., kit, vial, tablet, capsule, syringe, ampoule). Ensures consistent quantity reporting across different IMP formulations and packaging configurations.']
  inventory_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for this inventory position record, typically generated by the RTSM or CTMS system. Used in regulatory accountability documentation and audit trails.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this inventory position. \'active\' indicates stock is available for dispensing; \'quarantined\' indicates stock is held pending investigation or quality review; \'depleted\' indicates zero usable stock; \'closed\' indicates the position is no longer active post study close-out; \'suspended\' indicates dispensing is temporarily halted.']
  last_dispensing_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent IMP or kit dispensing event occurred at this location. Used to monitor site dispensing activity and identify inactive sites that may require supply reallocation.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory count was conducted at this location for this IMP or kit type. Used to assess inventory data currency and schedule the next physical count per the accountability reconciliation plan.']
  last_physical_count_qty varchar [note: 'Actual physical count of IMP units or kits recorded during the most recent physical inventory count at this location. Compared against the system quantity to identify discrepancies requiring investigation.']
  last_shipment_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent inbound IMP or kit shipment was received and confirmed at this location. Used to track supply chain activity and assess time since last replenishment.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supply chain node holding this inventory. Distinguishes between investigational sites (where IMP is dispensed to subjects), depots (regional or central storage), central pharmacies, and satellite depots. Drives resupply logic and accountability reporting.']
  maximum_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Maximum number of IMP units or kits permitted at this location to prevent overstocking, minimize expiry risk, and comply with storage capacity constraints. Used in supply planning and RTSM configuration.']
  minimum_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Absolute minimum number of IMP units or kits that must be maintained at this location to ensure uninterrupted subject dosing. Falling below this level triggers an urgent resupply escalation.']
  qty_adjusted varchar [note: 'Net inventory adjustment quantity applied to this position due to physical count discrepancies, data corrections, or reconciliation differences. Positive values indicate upward adjustments; negative values indicate downward adjustments.']
  qty_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of IMP units or kits currently reserved or allocated to specific subjects or randomization slots via the RTSM but not yet physically dispensed. Reduces available-for-dispensing stock.']
  qty_destroyed varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of IMP units or kits that have been destroyed at or from this location, including expired, damaged, or returned-and-destroyed stock. Required for regulatory accountability reconciliation.']
  qty_dispensed varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of IMP units or kits dispensed to study subjects at this location since the study began or since the last accountability reconciliation period. Sourced from dispensing records in the RTSM.']
  qty_expired varchar [note: 'Number of IMP units or kits that have passed their expiry date at this location and are no longer eligible for dispensing. Tracked separately for regulatory accountability and waste reporting.']
  qty_on_hand varchar [note: 'Current physical count of IMP units or kits available at this location, excluding quarantined, allocated, and reserved stock. Represents the net usable inventory at the time of the last update. Used as the primary metric for resupply trigger evaluation.']
  qty_quarantined varchar [note: 'Number of IMP units or kits currently placed in quarantine at this location due to quality concerns, temperature excursions, damaged packaging, or pending investigation. These units are not available for dispensing.']
  qty_received varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of IMP units or kits received at this location since the study began or since the last accountability reconciliation period. Sourced from inbound shipment confirmations in the RTSM.']
  qty_returned varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of IMP units or kits returned from subjects or from this site to the depot or sponsor for destruction or reconciliation. Used in accountability reconciliation calculations.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent IMP accountability reconciliation was completed and approved for this inventory position. Required for regulatory inspection readiness and study close-out documentation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory record was first created in the system, conforming to ALCOA+ principles of contemporaneous data capture. Used for audit trail and data lineage purposes.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements under ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11, and enables change detection in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold at which a resupply request should be triggered for this IMP or kit type at this location. When qty_on_hand falls at or below this value, the RTSM or supply planner initiates a replenishment shipment.']
  reorder_qty varchar [note: 'Standard replenishment quantity to be shipped when a resupply is triggered for this location and IMP/kit type. Defined in the supply plan and used by the RTSM to generate resupply orders automatically.']
  resupply_request_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent resupply request was initiated for this inventory position. Used to track supply chain responsiveness and expected delivery timelines.']
  resupply_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an active resupply request has been triggered for this inventory position. Set to True when qty_on_hand falls at or below the reorder_point and a resupply order has been initiated in the RTSM or supply management system.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the IMP batch must be retested to confirm continued stability and suitability for use. Applicable for products with defined retest periods rather than fixed expiry dates. Triggers stability testing workflows.']
  rtsm_location_code varchar [note: 'The location identifier assigned to this site or depot within the RTSM system (Medidata Rave). Used to reconcile inventory records between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational RTSM source system.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the IMP or kit at this location, as specified in the Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier (IMPD) or protocol. Drives cold-chain logistics planning and compliance monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_temperature|refrigerated|frozen|ultra_frozen|controlled_room_temperature|light_protected — promote to reference product if additional conditions are needed]']
  storage_condition_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current storage conditions at this location are compliant with the required storage condition specification for this IMP or kit type. Set to False when a temperature excursion or storage deviation is recorded, triggering quarantine and quality review.']
  temperature_excursion_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of recorded temperature excursion events for this inventory position at this location. Used to assess cold-chain compliance history and inform quarantine or destruction decisions.']

  Note: 'Tracks IMP and kit stock positions at supply chain nodes (investigational sites and depots). Each record represents current inventory levels by IMP/kit type at a specific location, including quantity on-hand, received, dispensed, allocated, quarantined, returned, and expired. Records storage condition compliance, last physical count date, and location type (site or depot). Enables accountability reconciliation, resupply trigger management, and regulatory inventory reporting. Linked to site or depot via location reference.'
}

Table supply.supply_plan {
  supply_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the investigational medicinal product (IMP) supply plan record within the clinical trial supply chain management system.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Supply plans are scoped to CTAs per country/region; the CTA defines authorized quantities, sites, and subject counts that directly drive supply planning parameters. Regulatory affairs and supply teams']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.country. Business justification: Supply plans are built per country for CTA compliance, import permit planning, and country-level enrollment forecasting. geographic_scope is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to study.country ena']
  country_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.country_registration. Business justification: Supply plans for each country must align with the country registration status of the IMP. Regulatory country registration determines whether product can be supplied to a country; supply planners refer']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Supply planning procedures (forecasting, buffer stock, overage calculation) are governed by SOPs. Supply plans require SOP traceability for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection. Clinical supply op']
  dosing_regimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen. Business justification: Supply quantity forecasting is directly calculated from the dosing regimen (dose frequency, treatment duration, number of subjects per arm). supply_plan.dosing_regimen_description is a denormalized fr']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Supply plans are scoped to study epochs (treatment, extension, follow-up) as IP requirements differ by epoch. Epoch-level supply forecasting is a standard clinical supply planning activity; planners n']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational medicinal product (IMP) being planned, linking to the product master record that defines formulation, dosage form, and strength.']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Supply plans are scoped to an IND — the IND defines the protocol coverage, phase, and product authorization that drives manufacturing quantity planning. Supply planners reference the IND when building']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Supply plans are built for a specific investigational product as defined in the study protocol (IND/IMPD reference, storage conditions, shelf life). Linking supply_plan to study.investigational_produc']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Supply plans are built against a specific protocol version — planned enrollment, dosing regimen, number of arms, and visit frequency all derive from the protocol. Regulatory supply forecasting require']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: Supply planning is directly driven by the randomization scheme — allocation ratios, treatment arm counts, block sizes, and stratification factors determine IMP quantities and overage. Supply planners ']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Supply plans are included in IND/CTA submissions (investigational product section). supply_plan links to ind_application and regulatory_strategy but not regulatory_binder. Regulatory affairs teams fil']
  regulatory_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.strategy. Business justification: Supply plans must align with the regulatory strategy — rolling review, accelerated approval, and adaptive pathways directly affect supply planning timelines, overage percentages, and manufacturing bat']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Supply plans are developed in conjunction with formal risk assessments covering shortage risk, expiry risk, and cold chain failure probability. Linking supply_plan to the governing risk_assessment ena']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: Supply planning directly references the SAP version in effect: dropout rate assumptions, analysis population definitions, and sample size targets in the SAP drive total units planned and buffer stock ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this supply plan is created. Links the supply plan to the study protocol, enrollment projections, and dosing regimen.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Supply plans are essential TMF documents filed under TMF Zone 5 (IP management) per DIA TMF Reference Model. Direct FK enables TMF completeness tracking for supply planning artifacts. Clinical TMF man']
  accountability_reconciliation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether drug accountability reconciliation is required under this supply plan, as mandated by GCP and regulatory requirements. Drives reconciliation workflow in RTSM and site monitoring activities.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the supply plan was formally approved by the sponsor supply team and/or CRO supply chain lead. Null if plan has not yet been approved.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding configuration of the study as it affects supply planning. Double-blind studies require matched placebo kits; open-label studies have simpler kit structures. Impacts kit design and labeling requirements.']
  buffer_stock_weeks decimal(5,1) [note: 'Target buffer stock expressed in weeks of supply to be maintained at depot and/or site level. Ensures continuity of supply during manufacturing lead times and logistics delays.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP requires cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled storage and transport) throughout the supply chain. Drives depot selection, packaging specifications, and logistics provider requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supply plan record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.']
  destruction_plan_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this supply plan version includes a returns and destruction plan for expired, unused, or damaged IMP. Required for GMP compliance and accountability reconciliation.']
  dropout_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Assumed subject dropout/discontinuation rate (as a percentage) applied in demand forecasting calculations. Accounts for subjects who withdraw consent, are lost to follow-up, or are discontinued per protocol.']
  expiry_management_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy applied for managing IMP expiry at depot and site level. FIFO (First In First Out), FEFO (First Expired First Out), manual review, or RTSM-managed automated expiry tracking.']
  forecasted_enrollment varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects projected to be enrolled over the planning horizon, derived from enrollment projections and site feasibility assessments. Primary driver of IMP demand forecasting.']
  horizon_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Typically extends to study completion or database lock (DBL) plus a buffer for returns and destruction.']
  horizon_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning horizon covered by this supply plan. Defines the beginning of the forecasted demand and manufacturing schedule window.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supply chain planners to document assumptions, constraints, protocol amendment impacts, or sponsor-specific instructions relevant to this supply plan version.']
  overage_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned overage percentage applied on top of net demand to account for wastage, damaged kits, protocol amendments, and supply chain uncertainties. Agreed with sponsor and documented in the supply agreement.']
  plan_date date [note: 'The principal business date on which this supply plan was formally prepared or issued. Represents the real-world event date distinct from system audit timestamps.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the supply plan, typically formatted as SP-{StudyCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}. Used in sponsor communications, RTSM configuration, and regulatory documentation.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supply plan. Draft indicates initial creation; under_review indicates sponsor/CRO review; approved indicates sign-off; active indicates currently governing supply operations; superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; cancelled indicates plan was voided.']
  planned_manufacturing_batches varchar [note: 'Total number of manufacturing batches planned to fulfill the forecasted IMP demand over the planning horizon, including buffer and overage quantities.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency or trigger basis for this supply plan update cycle. Typically monthly or per enrollment milestone as defined in the supply agreement with the sponsor.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'Randomization methodology used in the RTSM for kit assignment to subjects. Drives kit numbering, stratification factors, and supply allocation logic within Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  resupply_quantity varchar [note: 'Standard quantity of kits or units to be shipped per resupply order when the resupply trigger threshold is reached. Defined per site tier and country in the supply plan.']
  resupply_trigger_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum kit or unit quantity at depot or site level that triggers an automatic resupply order via RTSM (Interactive Response Technology). When inventory falls to or below this threshold, a resupply shipment is initiated.']
  rtsm_plan_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding supply plan configuration record within the Medidata Rave RTSM system. Enables traceability between the lakehouse supply plan record and the operational RTSM configuration.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Approved shelf life of the IMP in months from date of manufacture, as defined in the product specification and stability data. Critical for expiry management and resupply timing calculations.']
  site_tier_scope varchar [note: 'Site tier classification covered by this supply plan. Allows differentiated supply planning for high-enrolling versus low-enrolling sites, supporting optimized kit allocation and resupply strategies.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP as specified in the product dossier. Defines upper bound for cold-chain and ambient storage qualification.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,1) [note: 'Minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the IMP as specified in the product dossier. Used to define cold-chain logistics requirements and depot qualification criteria.']
  total_units_planned varchar [note: 'Total number of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials, kits) planned for manufacture and distribution over the planning horizon, inclusive of overage and buffer stock.']
  treatment_arms_count varchar [note: 'Number of treatment arms (including placebo/comparator) in the study protocol that require separate IMP supply planning. Drives kit type differentiation in RTSM randomization configuration.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which IMP supply quantities are expressed and tracked throughout the supply plan (e.g., kit, vial, tablet). Aligns with RTSM kit configuration and dispensing records. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kit|vial|tablet|capsule|syringe|blister_pack|bottle — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this supply plan record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Updated on every planning cycle revision or status change.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the supply plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3), incremented at each planning cycle or upon significant enrollment milestone updates. Supports version history and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Captures the drug supply planning record for a study, including forecasted demand by country/region and site tier, planned manufacturing batches, buffer stock targets, resupply trigger thresholds, overage assumptions, and planning horizon. Supports IMP demand forecasting driven by enrollment projections, protocol-defined dosing regimens, and dropout rates. Managed in coordination with sponsor supply teams and updated at defined planning cycles (typically monthly or per enrollment milestone).'
}

Table supply.manufacture_batch {
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the IMP manufacturing batch record in the clinical supply chain. Primary key for all downstream kit, inventory, and accountability traceability.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: QP batch release and disposition decisions reference the regulatory approval authorizing the product. Batch release status reports and GMP compliance checks require knowing which approval covers the b']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_capa. Business justification: Manufacturing batches with GMP deviations, out-of-spec stability results, or QP release failures trigger CAPAs. QA teams must track which batches are under open CAPAs before making release decisions. ']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Batches are manufactured and released under a specific CTA governing which member states can receive them. CTA-scoped batch release is a standard GMP/regulatory supply chain requirement; inspectors ve']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: Each manufacturing batch produces a specific IMP. manufacture_batch.imp_name and imp_code are denormalized from imp_master.product_name and product_code. Adding imp_master_id normalizes this relations']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Investigational batches are manufactured under an IND. The IND governs manufacturing standards, batch release criteria, and annual reporting of batch data. FDA inspectors and sponsors track which IND ']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: A manufacture batch is a physical production run of a specific investigational product. Cross-referencing the study-domain IP definition enables IMPD regulatory submissions, QP release validation, and']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: Manufacturing batch quantities and arm ratios are determined by the randomization scheme. CMC planning and batch release documentation reference the rand_scheme to validate that produced quantities ma']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_study. Business justification: Manufacture batches are produced for specific studies. GMP/GCP IMP accountability, IMPD regulatory submissions, and batch release documentation all require direct study attribution. Without this FK, b']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Batch records, certificates of analysis, and QP release documents are essential TMF documents (ICH E6 R2, DIA TMF Zone 5). manufacture_batch links to regulatory_binder but not tmf_document. TMF comple']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Manufacture batches are produced for specific treatment arms (active, comparator, placebo). is_comparator and is_placebo flags exist but no FK to treatment_arm. Per-arm batch tracking is required for ']
  available_quantity varchar [note: 'Current quantity of units from this batch that are available for allocation to depots or clinical sites, net of already allocated, dispensed, returned, and destroyed quantities. Used for supply planning and shortage risk monitoring.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric batch/lot number assigned by the manufacturing site, as printed on the IMP label and referenced in the Certificate of Analysis (CoA). This is the primary human-readable identifier used in regulatory submissions, sponsor communications, and supply chain reconciliation.']
  batch_size varchar [note: 'Total number of units (e.g., tablets, vials, kits) manufactured in this batch. Used for supply planning, yield calculations, and accountability reconciliation against dispensed and returned quantities.']
  batch_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size quantity (e.g., units, vials, tablets, kits). Required for unambiguous interpretation of batch_size in supply planning and accountability reports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: units|vials|tablets|capsules|kits|ampoules|syringes|patches — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch purpose: clinical (intended for subject dosing), validation (process validation), stability (stability study samples), reference (retained reference samples), reserve (emergency reserve). Determines downstream handling and allocation rules.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding classification of the IMP batch indicating whether the batch is open-label, single-blind, double-blind, or triple-blind. Determines labelling requirements, handling procedures, and unblinding protocols at clinical sites.']
  coa_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the GMP Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued by the manufacturing site for this batch. The CoA confirms the batch meets all release specifications. Links to the document management system (Veeva Vault eTMF).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this manufacture batch record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  cta_reference varchar [note: 'Clinical Trial Authorisation (CTA) reference number issued by the relevant national competent authority (e.g., EMA, MHRA) under which this IMP batch is authorised for use in the clinical trial.']
  destroyed_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of units from this batch that have been confirmed destroyed, including rejected units, expired returns, and reconciliation destructions. Required for GMP accountability reconciliation and regulatory reporting.']
  destruction_certificate_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the destruction certificate issued upon confirmed destruction of batch units. Required for GMP accountability reconciliation and regulatory audit trail. Links to document management system (Veeva Vault).']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the IMP in this batch (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection). Determines storage, handling, and dispensing requirements at clinical sites. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|infusion|patch|powder|solution|suspension|cream|gel|other — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the IMP batch must not be used, as determined by stability studies and printed on the IMP label. Critical for expiry management, site recall, and RTSM kit assignment logic to prevent dispensing of expired product.']
  gmp_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the GMP manufacturing authorisation or certificate issued by the relevant regulatory authority for the manufacturing site. Confirms the site is authorised to manufacture IMPs for clinical trials.']
  is_comparator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch is a comparator (active control or placebo) product rather than the investigational drug. True = comparator/placebo; False = investigational drug. Used for supply planning, kit assembly, and RTSM randomization configuration.']
  is_placebo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch contains placebo (no active ingredient). True = placebo batch; False = active drug batch. Distinct from is_comparator as a batch may be a comparator active drug. Used for blinded supply management and kit labelling.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'The date on which manufacturing of this IMP batch was completed, as recorded in the batch manufacturing record. Used as the reference point for shelf-life and expiry date calculation.']
  manufacturing_site_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the manufacturing site is located. Used for import/export regulatory compliance and supply chain risk assessment.']
  manufacturing_site_name varchar [note: 'Name of the GMP-certified facility where this IMP batch was manufactured. Used for regulatory traceability, site audit trails, and supply chain accountability.']
  packaging_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of the primary and secondary packaging configuration for this batch (e.g., \'30-count HDPE bottle\', \'10-pack blister card\', \'1 mL pre-filled syringe\'). Used for kit assembly planning and site dispensing instructions.']
  quarantine_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units from this batch currently held in quarantine pending QP release or investigation. Tracked separately from available quantity for GMP accountability.']
  recall_date date [note: 'Date on which the recall of this batch was initiated. Populated only when recall_flag is True. Used for regulatory reporting timelines and site notification tracking.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this batch has been subject to a product recall. True = batch recalled; False = no recall. Triggers urgent site notification, return logistics, and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the Qualified Person (QP) or authorized release authority formally released this batch for clinical use. Marks the transition from quarantine to released status and triggers downstream supply chain activities.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current GMP release status of the batch as determined by the Qualified Person (QP) or authorized release authority. Controls whether the batch may be shipped to depots or dispensed to subjects. Valid values: quarantine (awaiting QP release), released (approved for use), rejected (failed QC), recalled (post-release recall), destroyed (confirmed destruction), under_review (hold pending investigation).']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the batch must be retested to confirm continued compliance with specifications before further use. Applicable for APIs and some drug products where retest rather than expiry dating is used.']
  shelf_life_months varchar [note: 'Approved shelf life of the IMP batch in months, as determined by stability data and declared in the product specification. Used to calculate and validate the expiry date relative to the manufacture date.']
  source_system_batch_key varchar [note: 'Native batch record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM batch record ID, sponsor ERP batch key from SAP S/4HANA). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and ETL traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and operational systems.']
  sponsor_batch_reference varchar [note: 'Sponsor-assigned reference number for this batch as provided in the sponsor batch release documentation. May differ from the manufacturing site batch number. Used for sponsor reconciliation and regulatory submissions.']
  stability_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the ICH stability study protocol under which this batch is enrolled for ongoing stability monitoring. Used to track shelf-life extensions and expiry date updates.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for this IMP batch as specified in the product dossier and CoA. Drives cold-chain logistics planning, depot and site storage requirements, and temperature excursion monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: room_temperature|refrigerated|frozen|ultra_frozen|controlled_room_temperature|protect_from_light|other — promote to reference product]']
  storage_temp_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this IMP batch, as specified in the product specification. Used for cold-chain monitoring, temperature excursion assessment, and depot/site compliance verification.']
  storage_temp_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this IMP batch, as specified in the product specification. Used for cold-chain monitoring, temperature excursion assessment, and depot/site compliance verification.']
  strength varchar [note: 'Declared strength or concentration of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) per unit dose in this batch (e.g., \'100 mg\', \'5 mg/mL\'). Used for dose verification and dispensing accountability.']
  units_per_kit varchar [note: 'Number of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials) contained in each randomization kit assembled from this batch. Used by RTSM for kit assembly configuration and dispensing accountability calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this manufacture batch record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.']

  Note: 'Records IMP manufacturing batch details including batch number, manufacturing date, batch size, manufacturing site, GMP certificate of analysis reference, release status (quarantine, released, rejected), expiry date, and storage conditions. Serves as the upstream traceability anchor for all kit and inventory records. Sourced from sponsor-provided batch release documentation.'
}

Table supply.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each IMP/kit shipment record in the clinical supply chain. Primary key for the shipment data product.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: EU/EEA IMP shipments to sites require a valid CTA in the destination member state before dispatch. Supply operations teams verify CTA authorization status as a mandatory gate in the shipment release p']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_country. Business justification: Shipments must be blocked to countries under regulatory hold. Linking shipment to study.study_country enables automated regulatory hold checks before dispatch, supports country-level supply chain repo']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Shipment handling, cold-chain receipt, and temperature monitoring procedures are governed by SOPs (ICH E6 R2). Shipment records must reference the applicable handling SOP for GCP audit readiness and r']
  origin_depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.depot. Business justification: Shipments originate from depots (depot-to-site or depot-to-depot shipments). shipment.origin_node_name is a denormalized string. Adding origin_depot_id creates a proper FK to the originating depot. Nu']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination clinical site receiving the IMP shipment. Used for site-level inventory accountability and regulatory reconciliation.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The confirmed date on which the IMP shipment was received at the destination node. Used for delivery performance measurement, accountability reconciliation, and regulatory documentation.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the IMP shipment was confirmed received at the destination. Supports cold-chain total transit time calculations and ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Investigational Medicinal Product (IMP) manufacturing batch or lot number included in this shipment. Critical for product traceability, recall management, and regulatory accountability.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or courier responsible for transporting the IMP shipment (e.g., World Courier, Marken, DHL). Required for cold-chain accountability and deviation investigation.']
  cold_chain_logger_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the temperature data logger device placed in the shipment. Used to retrieve detailed temperature profiles from the logistics provider system for excursion investigation and regulatory documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the source RTSM system or lakehouse. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  customs_clearance_date date [note: 'Date on which customs clearance was obtained for the international IMP shipment. Used for transit time analysis and regulatory import documentation.']
  customs_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires customs clearance due to international cross-border transit. Drives import/export documentation requirements and regulatory import permits for IMP.']
  destination_node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the supply chain destination node (e.g., site name, depot name). Supports logistics documentation and regulatory accountability records.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Type of supply chain node to which the shipment is directed (e.g., clinical site, central depot, manufacturer). Supports supply chain network mapping and inventory accountability.']
  destruction_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Certificate number issued upon confirmed destruction of returned or expired IMP. Required for regulatory accountability reconciliation and GMP compliance documentation.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates the directional flow of the shipment: outbound (manufacturer/depot to site), inbound (site to depot/returns), or lateral (site to site). Supports supply chain node mapping and accountability.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'The date on which the IMP shipment was physically dispatched from the origin node. Represents the principal business event timestamp for outbound shipments. Used for lead-time calculations and regulatory documentation.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time the IMP shipment was dispatched from the origin node. Supports cold-chain elapsed time calculations and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  excursion_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total cumulative duration in minutes during which the shipment temperature was outside the specified storage condition range. Used for product impact assessment and quality disposition decisions.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'The planned date on which the IMP shipment is expected to arrive at the destination node. Used for site inventory planning, expiry management, and supply chain KPI monitoring.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the IMP batch included in this shipment. Used for expiry management, re-test date tracking, and prevention of expired product administration to subjects.']
  imp_description varchar [note: 'Description of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or comparator included in the shipment (e.g., drug name, formulation, strength). Supports accountability documentation and regulatory submissions.']
  import_permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory import permit or authorization number issued by the receiving country\'s health authority for the IMP shipment. Required for cross-border clinical supply and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the transfer of risk and responsibility between shipper and receiver for the IMP shipment. Governs insurance, customs, and cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  kit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of randomization kits included in this shipment as recorded in the RTSM (Medidata Rave). Core quantity field for inventory accountability and reconciliation.']
  max_recorded_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by the cold-chain data logger during transit. Used to assess whether the IMP was exposed to excessive heat or out-of-range conditions.']
  min_recorded_temp_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by the cold-chain data logger during transit. Used to assess whether the IMP was exposed to freezing or sub-optimal low temperatures.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special handling instructions, or comments related to the shipment (e.g., fragile contents, priority handling, deviation observations).']
  origin_node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the supply chain origin node (e.g., depot name, manufacturer facility name, or site name). Supports logistics documentation and regulatory accountability records.']
  origin_node_type varchar [note: 'Type of supply chain node from which the shipment originates (e.g., manufacturer, central depot, clinical site, CRO warehouse). Used for supply chain network mapping and accountability.']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment or its contents have been placed under quarantine pending quality investigation (e.g., following a temperature excursion or damaged packaging). Prevents dispensing of affected kits to subjects.']
  rtsm_shipment_ref varchar [note: 'Source system shipment reference identifier from the RTSM module (Medidata Rave). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational RTSM system.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable shipment reference number generated by the RTSM (Medidata Rave) or logistics provider. Used for cross-system reconciliation, carrier communication, and regulatory accountability documentation.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the IMP shipment within the clinical supply chain workflow. Drives accountability reconciliation and RTSM inventory updates. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|returned|destroyed|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment purpose within the clinical supply chain. Distinguishes initial site supply, resupply, site-to-site transfer, returns to depot, and emergency shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initial_supply|resupply|return|destruction|transfer|emergency — promote to reference product]']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage temperature condition for the IMP during transit and at the destination (e.g., refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen -20°C, ambient 15-25°C). Drives cold-chain logistics requirements and excursion assessment.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion (deviation from required storage condition) was detected during transit. Triggers quality investigation, product impact assessment, and potential quarantine of affected kits.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment. Used to monitor real-time transit status, confirm delivery, and investigate temperature excursions or delays.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements, change tracking, and data integrity compliance per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  waybill_number varchar [note: 'Air waybill (AWB) or bill of lading number issued by the carrier for the shipment. Used for customs documentation, carrier liability, and logistics traceability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of IMP/kit shipments between supply chain nodes (manufacturer to depot, depot to site, site to site, site to depot for returns). Captures shipment number, origin, destination, carrier, tracking number, shipment date, expected delivery date, actual delivery date, temperature excursion flag, cold-chain logger data reference, contents (kits/batches), and shipment status. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and logistics provider integrations.'
}

Table supply.shipment_line {
  shipment_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shipment line record within the IMP supply chain. Primary key for the shipment_line data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply depot or distribution center from which this shipment line was dispatched. Used for depot-level inventory depletion tracking and supply chain analytics.']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: shipment_line captures imp_product_code (STRING) and imp_description (STRING) as denormalized attributes that duplicate data from imp_master. Adding imp_master_id as a FK to supply.imp_master normaliz']
  kit_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the randomization kit or IMP package unit included on this shipment line. Enables kit-level traceability from depot to site. TRANSACTION_LINE role: RESOURCE_REFERENCE category.']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Each shipment line ships a specific manufacturing batch/lot. shipment_line.lot_number is a denormalized batch identifier (lot_number and batch_number are synonymous in pharmaceutical supply chain). Ad']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment header record. Establishes the header/detail relationship linking this line to its parent IMP shipment transaction. TRANSACTION_LINE role: HEADER_REFERENCE category.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this IMP shipment line is associated. Enables study-level supply accountability reporting and cross-domain analytics.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial site (destination) for this shipment line. Used for site-level inventory management, accountability reconciliation, and monitoring visit preparation.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Each shipment line carries kits for a specific treatment arm — critical for blinded supply chain management and per-arm accountability reconciliation. treatment_arm_code is a denormalized plain column']
  blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP kit on this shipment line is blinded (True) or unblinded/open-label (False). Supports blinding integrity controls and restricts access to treatment assignment information per ICH E9 and GCP requirements.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued by the manufacturer for the IMP lot/batch on this shipment line. Required for GMP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP on this shipment line requires cold-chain logistics management (True) or can be shipped at ambient temperature (False). Drives carrier selection, packaging requirements, and temperature monitoring obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment line record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) GCP.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs or import/export declaration number associated with this shipment line for cross-border IMP shipments. Required for regulatory compliance with import/export regulations and trade documentation.']
  discrepancy_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for any quantity discrepancy between shipped and received amounts on this line. Captured during site receipt confirmation and used for drug accountability investigation and CAPA tracking.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form of the IMP on this shipment line (e.g., TABLET, CAPSULE, INJECTION, INFUSION, PATCH, LIQUID). Used for cold-chain handling classification and dispensing instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: TABLET|CAPSULE|INJECTION|INFUSION|PATCH|LIQUID|POWDER|GEL|CREAM|SUPPOSITORY — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the IMP lot/batch on this shipment line. Used for expiry management, site dispensing eligibility checks, and regulatory compliance. IMP must not be dispensed to subjects after this date.']
  import_license_number varchar [note: 'Import license or permit number required for cross-border shipment of IMP to the destination country. Applicable for regulated markets including EU, Japan (PMDA), Australia (TGA), and Canada (Health Canada).']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable kit identifier assigned by the RTSM (Randomization and Trial Supply Management) system, such as Medidata Rave RTSM. Used for physical label matching and kit-level accountability reconciliation.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IMP kit on this shipment line indicating its treatment assignment type. Values: ACTIVE (investigational drug), PLACEBO (matching placebo), COMPARATOR (active comparator), RESCUE (rescue medication), OPEN_LABEL (unblinded open-label supply). Critical for blinding integrity and supply accountability.']
  line_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments specific to this shipment line, capturing any special handling instructions, site-specific requirements, or operational remarks not captured in structured fields.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent shipment, used to order and uniquely identify each line within a shipment transaction. TRANSACTION_LINE role: LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of this shipment line, tracking progression from dispatch through site receipt confirmation. TRANSACTION_LINE role: LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT category (status outcome). Values: PENDING (awaiting dispatch), SHIPPED (dispatched from depot), RECEIVED (confirmed at site), PARTIALLY_RECEIVED (partial receipt confirmed), REJECTED (refused or failed QC at receipt), CANCELLED (line voided).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s lot or batch number assigned to the IMP units on this shipment line. Critical for drug accountability, recall traceability, and regulatory compliance per GMP requirements.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the IMP lot/batch on this shipment line was manufactured. Used for shelf-life calculation, stability tracking, and GMP compliance documentation.']
  quantity_discrepancy decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between quantity_shipped and quantity_received for this shipment line. A non-zero value triggers a discrepancy investigation per drug accountability SOPs. Positive value indicates shortage; negative indicates overage.']
  quantity_received decimal(18,4) [note: 'Actual quantity of IMP units confirmed as received at the destination site for this shipment line. May differ from quantity_shipped in cases of partial receipt, damage, or discrepancy. Used for accountability reconciliation.']
  quantity_shipped decimal(18,4) [note: 'Number of units, kits, or packs of IMP dispatched on this shipment line. TRANSACTION_LINE role: LINE_QUANTITY category. Used for inventory depletion, accountability reconciliation, and supply planning.']
  quarantine_status varchar [note: 'Current quarantine status of the IMP on this shipment line. Values: NOT_QUARANTINED (available for dispensing), QUARANTINED (held pending quality review), RELEASED (released from quarantine after investigation), DESTROYED (disposed of due to quality failure or expiry).']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'Randomization number assigned by the RTSM system to the kit on this shipment line, linking the kit to a specific subject randomization slot. Used for treatment assignment traceability and unblinding procedures.']
  receipt_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the receiving site has confirmed receipt of the IMP units on this shipment line. True = receipt confirmed by site personnel; False = receipt not yet confirmed. Supports drug accountability reconciliation.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the receiving site confirmed receipt of the IMP units on this shipment line. Used for cold-chain compliance verification, accountability records, and site inventory updates.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the IMP lot/batch must be retested to confirm continued potency and stability before further use or dispensing. Applicable for certain IMP formulations per ICH stability guidelines.']
  source_system_line_ref varchar [note: 'Original line identifier or reference number from the source operational system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM shipment line ID or SAP S/4HANA delivery item number). Enables traceability back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  storage_condition_code varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the IMP on this shipment line as specified in the IMP dossier. Drives cold-chain logistics requirements and site storage compliance monitoring. Values: AMBIENT, REFRIGERATED (2–8°C), FROZEN (≤-20°C), CONTROLLED_ROOM_TEMP (15–25°C), PROTECT_FROM_LIGHT.']
  strength varchar [note: 'Strength or concentration of the IMP on this shipment line (e.g., \'10 mg\', \'100 mg/mL\'). Captured as a string to accommodate varied unit expressions. Used for dispensing verification and protocol compliance.']
  temperature_excursion_details varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any temperature excursion recorded during transit for this shipment line, including duration, temperature range observed, and initial impact assessment. Populated only when temperature_excursion_flag is True.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion was recorded during transit for the IMP on this shipment line (True = excursion detected). Triggers quality investigation and potential quarantine of affected IMP per GDP and GMP requirements.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity shipped on this line (e.g., KIT, VIAL, TABLET, CAPSULE, SYRINGE, PACK). Aligns with the IMP supply catalog and RTSM configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: KIT|VIAL|TABLET|CAPSULE|SYRINGE|PACK|BOTTLE|AMPOULE|BLISTER|SACHET — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment line record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail for each IMP shipment, capturing individual kit numbers or batch quantities included in a shipment. Each line records the IMP/kit reference, quantity shipped, unit of measure, lot/batch number, expiry date, and line-level receipt confirmation status. Enables granular kit-level traceability within a shipment transaction.'
}

Table supply.dispensing_record {
  dispensing_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each investigational medicinal product (IMP) dispensing event record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Dispensing of IMP must occur under an active CTA. Clinical data managers and supply teams j']
