Adding imp_master_id as a FK to supply.imp_master normalizes the IMP reference, linking each dispensing event to the author']
  kit_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.kit_assignment. Business justification: kit_assignment is the IRT-driven authorization record; dispensing_record is its physical execution. Linking them enables reconciliation between IRT-assigned kits and actually dispensed kits — a standa']
  kit_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the kit definition record that specifies the IMP kit type, dose strength, pack size, and labeling configuration dispensed. Links to supply.kit_definition for full kit specification.']
  kit_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.kit_inventory. Business justification: Each dispensing event dispenses a specific physical kit instance. dispensing_record.kit_number is a denormalized string identifier for the kit; adding kit_inventory_id creates a proper FK to the physi']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Each dispensing event uses IMP from a specific manufacturing batch (dispensing_record.batch_number is denormalized). Adding manufacture_batch_id creates a proper FK to the batch record, enabling full ']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Dispensing errors (wrong dose, wrong kit, missed dose) are a primary source of protocol deviations. protocol_deviation has trial_subject_id but no dispensing_record_id. This link supports deviation ro']
  rand_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_code. Business justification: IRT/RTSM dispensing workflow: each dispensing event is authorized by a specific randomization code. GCP accountability requires tracing every dispensed kit to its rand_code. No existing FK covers this']
  randomization_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.randomization_assignment. Business justification: IMP dispensing must be traceable to the specific randomization assignment to verify correct treatment arm kit was dispensed — a core GCP blinding integrity and RTSM audit requirement. Clinical trial a']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: IMP drug accountability reconciliation and CRA site monitoring require all dispensing records to be directly traceable to the study site. CRAs pull site-level dispensing logs during monitoring visits;']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: GCP requires that IP dispensing is verified against the protocol-defined visit schedule. Linking dispensing_record to study_visit_schedule enables protocol deviation detection (dispensing outside the ']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: GCP compliance and IMP accountability require linking each dispensing event directly to the subject\'s randomization record. Enables per-subject dispensing-vs-randomization reconciliation reports requi']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: CRAs performing source data verification must cross-reference IMP dispensing events against eCRF visit-level data completeness and SDV status. This link enables the SDV workflow and IMP accountability']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit during which the IMP was dispensed. Links dispensing to the visit schedule for protocol compliance tracking.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled trial subject who received the IMP dispensing. Core party reference for subject-level drug accountability and compliance with GCP requirements.']
  accountability_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispensing record has been included in and reconciled as part of the site-level drug accountability reconciliation (True) or is pending reconciliation (False). Required for study close-out and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing batch (lot) number of the IMP dispensed, as printed on the kit label. Required for drug accountability, pharmacovigilance traceability, and regulatory recall management. Aligns with GMP and GCP requirements.']
  cold_chain_maintained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cold chain storage and transport requirements were maintained for this IMP dispensing event (True) or a temperature excursion was recorded (False). Critical for temperature-sensitive biologics and vaccines. Null for non-cold-chain products.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dispensing record was first created in the source system (Medidata Rave RTSM). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements for contemporaneous data capture and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record compliance.']
  dispensed_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the site staff member who performed the IMP dispensing, captured at time of dispensing for GCP audit trail. Stored as a denormalized field to preserve the historical record per ALCOA+ enduring data requirements even if staff records change.']
  dispensed_by_role varchar [note: 'The professional role of the staff member who performed the IMP dispensing (e.g., pharmacist, Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC), Principal Investigator (PI)). Used to verify that dispensing was performed by qualified personnel per GCP and site delegation log requirements.']
  dispensing_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the IMP was dispensed to the subject. Derived from dispensing_timestamp for day-level reporting, protocol visit window compliance checks, and CDISC SDTM EX domain mapping.']
  dispensing_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the IMP dispensing was executed — whether via automated RTSM assignment, manual override (with justification), emergency supply, or backup procedure. Used for quality oversight and GCP compliance monitoring.']
  dispensing_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by site staff at the time of dispensing, capturing any relevant observations, deviations, or special circumstances associated with this IMP dispensing event. Supports GCP audit trail and query resolution.']
  dispensing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this dispensing record. Tracks the workflow state from initial dispensing through return, destruction, or reconciliation for GCP drug accountability compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dispensed|returned|partially_returned|destroyed|lost|reconciled — promote to reference product]']
  dispensing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the IMP was dispensed to the subject at the investigational site. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset per ALCOA+ requirements for contemporaneous data capture.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form of the IMP dispensed (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection). Required for drug accountability records and regulatory submissions. Aligns with IMP labeling and CDISC controlled terminology. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|injection|infusion|patch|oral_solution|powder|suppository — promote to reference product]']
  dose_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The numeric dose amount of IMP dispensed to the subject for this dispensing event, expressed in the unit specified by dose_unit. Core quantitative field for pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis and protocol compliance verification.']
  dose_number varchar [note: 'Sequential dose number for this subject within the study, indicating which administration in the subject\'s treatment sequence this dispensing event represents (e.g., Dose 1, Dose 2). Used for protocol compliance and exposure analysis.']
  dose_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the dose amount dispensed (e.g., mg, mcg, mL, IU). Required for unambiguous dose interpretation in drug accountability, PK analysis, and CDISC SDTM EX domain submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mg|mcg|g|mL|IU|mg/kg|mcg/kg|mg/m2 — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the IMP kit dispensed, as printed on the kit label. Captured at dispensing to verify that no expired product was administered and to support expiry management and drug accountability reconciliation.']
  is_unblinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blinding for this dispensing record has been broken (True) or remains blinded (False). Set to True only upon authorized emergency unblinding or at database lock (DBL). Access to unblinded records is strictly controlled per GCP and study blinding procedures.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'The unique kit number (barcode or label identifier) of the IMP kit dispensed to the subject. Core drug accountability field used to trace individual kit units from depot to subject. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dispensing event was associated with a protocol deviation (True) or was compliant with the protocol (False). Triggers linkage to the compliance.protocol_deviation record for regulatory reporting and CAPA management.']
  quantity_dispensed decimal(18,4) [note: 'The number of units (e.g., tablets, capsules, vials, syringes) of IMP dispensed to the subject in this dispensing event. Used for inventory depletion tracking, drug accountability reconciliation, and return quantity verification.']
  quantity_returned decimal(18,4) [note: 'The number of IMP units returned by the subject at a subsequent visit, as recorded against this dispensing event. Used for drug accountability reconciliation to calculate actual consumption and compliance with protocol dosing instructions.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity dispensed (e.g., tablet, capsule, vial, syringe). Distinguishes the dispensing unit from the dose unit for accurate drug accountability and inventory reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|syringe|patch|sachet|kit — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which this dispensing record was formally reconciled as part of the drug accountability reconciliation process. Populated when accountability_reconciled is True. Used for study close-out documentation and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  return_date date [note: 'The date on which unused IMP units from this dispensing event were returned by the subject to the site. Used for drug accountability reconciliation and calculation of subject compliance with dosing regimen.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the IMP was administered to the subject (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous). Required for CDISC SDTM EX domain submission and pharmacokinetic (PK) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|intradermal|intrathecal — promote to reference product]']
  rtsm_confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation number generated by the Medidata Rave RTSM system at the time of dispensing. Serves as the authoritative external business identifier for this dispensing event, used for drug accountability reconciliation and audit trail verification.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the Medidata Rave RTSM dispensing module that corresponds to this dispensing event. Enables traceability back to the operational system of record for audit, reconciliation, and data lineage purposes per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  subject_compliance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The calculated medication compliance percentage for this dispensing event, derived from quantity dispensed minus quantity returned divided by expected consumption, expressed as a percentage. Critical for per-protocol (PP) population eligibility determination and protocol deviation assessment.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion was recorded for the IMP kit at or prior to this dispensing event (True) or not (False). When True, requires sponsor/QA assessment of IMP usability before dispensing is confirmed. Supports cold-chain accountability.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'The reason for breaking the blind on this dispensing record, if applicable. Populated only when is_unblinded is True. Required for GCP audit trail and regulatory reporting of unblinding events. Null when blinding is intact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dispensing record was last modified in the source system. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail compliance, change tracking, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'The protocol-defined name or label of the study visit at which the IMP was dispensed (e.g., \'Visit 2 - Week 4\', \'Day 1\'). Captured at time of dispensing for protocol compliance verification and CDISC SDTM EX domain mapping.']

  Note: 'Captures each IMP dispensing event at an investigational site, recording the subject identifier, visit reference, kit number dispensed, IMP name, dose, quantity dispensed, dispensing date and time, dispensing staff, and RTSM confirmation number. Serves as the primary drug accountability record at the subject level. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM dispensing module. Critical for GCP compliance and accountability reconciliation.'
}

Table supply.return_record {
  return_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each IMP/kit return record in the supply chain accountability system.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: IMP returns and destruction must be authorized and documented under the CTA. Destruction authorization references the CTA; regulatory inspectors verify that returned IMP was handled in compliance with']
  compliance_protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: IMP returns with accountability discrepancies (quantity mismatch, blinding breach, missing returns) constitute protocol deviations. Linking return_record to the protocol_deviation replaces the denorma']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Outstanding return record discrepancies and unreconciled returns are tracked as pre-DBL checklist items. The reconciliation_status on return records must be resolved before database lock is authorized']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply depot that is the destination for site-to-depot returns or the originating depot for expired stock returns.']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_deviation. Business justification: Return discrepancies (quantity mismatch, blinding breach, damaged IMP) that generate protocol deviations must be traceable to the specific return event for GCP audit and CAPA management. return_record']
  disposition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.disposition. Business justification: Subject disposition events (withdrawal, completion, death) are the primary triggers for IMP return. Linking return_record to disposition enables end-of-study IMP accountability reconciliation and regu']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: IMP return procedures are strictly governed by SOPs (GCP/GMP requirement). Return records must reference the applicable return SOP for regulatory inspection readiness and accountability reconciliation']
  enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.enrollment. Business justification: Drug accountability reconciliation requires returned IMP to be attributed to a specific enrollment episode. When a subject re-screens and re-enrolls, return records must be tied to the correct enrollm']
  kit_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the kit definition record describing the IMP kit type, dosage form, and pack configuration associated with this return.']
  kit_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.kit_inventory. Business justification: Each return event returns a specific physical kit instance. return_record.kit_number is a denormalized string identifier; adding kit_inventory_id creates a proper FK to the physical kit record. kit_nu']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Return records reference a specific manufacturing batch (return_record.batch_number is denormalized). Adding manufacture_batch_id creates a proper FK to the batch record, completing the accountability']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit. Business justification: CRAs verify IP return records during site monitoring visits as a mandatory GCP accountability check. Linking return_record to the monitoring_visit that verified the return enables direct traceability ']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Return records with unexpected conditions (tamper evidence failure, quantity discrepancy, temperature excursion) trigger quality events. return_record links to protocol_deviation but has no quality_ev']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Return shipments (site-to-depot) are tracked in the shipment table. return_record.shipment_tracking_number is a denormalized string; adding shipment_id creates a proper FK to the return shipment recor']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this IMP return is being documented. Links the return to the sponsoring protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site initiating or receiving the return. Used for site-level drug accountability reconciliation.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: IMP return reconciliation requires calculating compliance (quantity_used vs. expected dose per treatment arm) per randomization number. Linking return_record to subject_randomization enables per-subje']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Per-arm return accountability is a GCP requirement — returned kit quantities must reconcile against dispensed quantities per treatment arm. return_record has no treatment_arm FK, preventing per-arm re']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject returning IMP to the site. Nullable for site-to-depot or expired stock returns where no subject is involved.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number of the returned IMP. Required for drug accountability, quality investigations, and regulatory submissions.']
  blinding_intact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blinding of the returned IMP kit was maintained at the time of return (True = blinding preserved; False = unblinding occurred or suspected). Critical for double-blind study integrity.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional observations, notes, or contextual information recorded by site staff, CRA, or depot personnel regarding this return event. Supports ALCOA+ completeness requirements.']
  courier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics or courier company responsible for transporting the returned IMP from site to depot. Required for GDP-compliant cold-chain documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this return record was first created in the data platform. Part of the mandatory ALCOA+ audit trail for electronic records under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  destruction_authorization_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the sponsor or depot authorization to destroy the returned IMP. Links to the destruction certificate in the eTMF (Veeva Vault). Required before any IMP destruction can proceed.']
  destruction_date date [note: 'Date on which the returned IMP was physically destroyed per the authorized destruction method. Completes the drug accountability lifecycle for the returned units.']
  destruction_method varchar [note: 'Method used to destroy the returned IMP, as specified in the protocol or sponsor instructions. Must comply with local environmental and pharmaceutical waste regulations.']
  discrepancy_explanation varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the site or CRA to account for any quantity discrepancy between dispensed and returned IMP. Required for regulatory audit trail when discrepancy_quantity is non-zero.']
  discrepancy_quantity varchar [note: 'Numeric difference between the expected return quantity (dispensed minus used) and the actual quantity returned. A non-zero value triggers a drug accountability discrepancy investigation.']
  dispensing_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the returned IMP (e.g., tablet, vial, syringe). Required for accurate drug accountability reconciliation across different dosage forms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|vial|syringe|patch|sachet|ampoule — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the returned IMP as printed on the label. Used to confirm whether the product was within shelf life at the time of dispensing and return.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'The unique RTSM-assigned kit number or barcode identifier for the specific IMP kit being returned. Enables unit-level drug accountability traceability.']
  quantity_dispensed varchar [note: 'Number of dispensing units originally dispensed to the subject or site for the associated kit. Stored on the return record to enable direct accountability reconciliation without joining to dispensing records.']
  quantity_returned varchar [note: 'Number of dispensing units (tablets, vials, syringes, etc.) returned. Used in drug accountability reconciliation to verify compliance with dispensing records.']
  quantity_used varchar [note: 'Number of dispensing units confirmed as consumed by the subject, calculated or reported at the time of return. Supports compliance and adherence analysis.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the returned IMP or kit was physically received at the destination (site or depot). Used to calculate transit time and confirm chain of custody.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date on which the drug accountability reconciliation for this return was completed and confirmed by the responsible party (CRA or site staff).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the drug accountability reconciliation process for this return, tracking whether dispensed vs. returned quantities have been verified and any discrepancies resolved.']
  return_condition varchar [note: 'Physical condition of the IMP or kit at the time of return, as assessed by the site or depot receiving staff. Critical for determining disposition (destruction vs. re-use) and identifying cold-chain or handling failures.']
  return_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the IMP or kit was physically returned by the subject to the site, or dispatched from the site to the depot. Principal business event date for drug accountability.']
  return_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this return transaction, typically generated by the RTSM (Medidata Rave) or CTMS. Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory audit.']
  return_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative or coded reason for the return, such as study completion, subject withdrawal, protocol deviation, product expiry, or damaged packaging. Supports root cause analysis and supply planning.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the return record, tracking progression from initiation through receipt, reconciliation, and final destruction or disposition.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return event indicating the reason and direction of the return: subject returning unused IMP to site, site returning stock to depot, expired stock return, damaged stock return, or return triggered by a protocol deviation.']
  rtsm_return_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this return transaction as recorded in the RTSM (Medidata Rave). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational RTSM system.']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Courier or logistics provider tracking number for the return shipment from site to depot. Enables cold-chain monitoring and chain-of-custody verification during transit.']
  sponsor_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor has been formally notified of this return event, particularly relevant for returns involving temperature excursions, damaged product, or significant quantity discrepancies.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cold-chain temperature excursion was recorded or reported for the returned IMP during storage or transit (True = excursion occurred). Triggers quality investigation and potential destruction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording the most recent modification to this return record. Required for audit trail integrity and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the CRA or pharmacist completed Source Data Verification (SDV) of the return record against source documents at the site or depot.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) or pharmacist who verified the return quantities and condition during a monitoring visit or depot check. Supports Source Data Verification (SDV).']

  Note: 'Documents IMP/kit returns from subjects to sites and from sites to depots. Captures return type (subject return, site-to-depot, expired stock), kit numbers or quantities returned, return date, condition on return (intact, partially used, damaged), reconciliation status, and destruction authorization reference. Essential for drug accountability reconciliation and regulatory compliance.'
}

  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Regulatory destruction reporting (FDA, EMA) requires per-treatment-arm destruction quantities for final IMP accountability. destruction_record has no treatment_arm FK, preventing per-arm destruction r']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date on which the destruction was formally authorized by the sponsor or designated authority. Must precede the destruction_date to confirm proper authorization workflow.']
  authorization_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned or sponsor-issued authorization number approving the destruction of the IMP/kits. Required for GCP audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness. Serves as the primary business identifier for this destruction event.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number of the IMP being destroyed. Required for pharmaceutical traceability and post-destruction accountability reconciliation.']
  certificate_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the Certificate of Destruction was formally issued by the destruction facility. May differ from the actual destruction date; both are required for complete GCP documentation.']
  certificate_of_destruction_ref varchar [note: 'Document reference number or identifier for the official Certificate of Destruction issued by the destruction facility. This document is a mandatory GCP audit trail artifact and must be filed in the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  cold_chain_maintained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cold-chain temperature requirements were maintained during transport to the destruction facility (True) or a cold-chain excursion occurred (False). Relevant for temperature-sensitive IMP products.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or contextual information related to the destruction event not captured in structured fields. Supports GCP audit trail completeness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this destruction record was first created in the system. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail compliance and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record integrity.']
  destruction_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the IMP/kits were physically destroyed. This is the principal real-world event date for the destruction transaction, distinct from authorization or record creation dates.']
  destruction_method varchar [note: 'The physical or chemical method used to destroy the IMP/kits (e.g., incineration, chemical neutralization). Must comply with applicable environmental and pharmaceutical waste regulations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: incineration|chemical_neutralization|autoclaving|landfill|pharmaceutical_waste_disposal|other — promote to reference product]']
  destruction_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason driving the destruction of the IMP/kits (e.g., expired product, returned unused, rejected due to quality failure). Required for accountability reconciliation and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: expired|returned_unused|rejected_quality|protocol_amendment|study_termination|damaged|other — promote to reference product]']
  destruction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the destruction record, tracking progression from authorization request through completion or cancellation.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any discrepancies identified between the quantity/kits expected for destruction and those actually destroyed. Required when reconciliation_status is \'discrepancy_identified\' or \'discrepancy_resolved\'.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the IMP batch being destroyed. Relevant when destruction_reason is \'expired\'; used to confirm that expired product has been appropriately removed from the supply chain.']
  facility_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the destruction facility is located. Used for jurisdiction-specific regulatory compliance tracking.']
  facility_license_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory license or permit number of the destruction facility, confirming it is authorized to handle and destroy pharmaceutical/IMP waste.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the licensed facility (depot, third-party destruction vendor, or site) where the physical destruction was carried out.']
  imp_name varchar [note: 'Name or description of the Investigational Medicinal Product (IMP) being destroyed. Provides human-readable identification of the drug substance for audit and reporting purposes.']
  is_blinded_destruction boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the destruction was performed under blinded conditions (True) to preserve study integrity, or unblinded (False). Relevant for double-blind studies where kit contents must not be revealed during destruction.']
  kit_count_destroyed varchar [note: 'Number of randomization kits destroyed in this event, as tracked by the RTSM system. Distinct from quantity_destroyed which counts individual IMP units; kit count is the RTSM-level accountability unit.']
  kit_number_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or range-notation list of RTSM kit numbers included in this destruction event (e.g., \'KIT-001,KIT-002\' or \'KIT-001 to KIT-050\'). Provides granular kit-level traceability for GCP audit and regulatory inspection.']
  quantity_destroyed varchar [note: 'Total number of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials, syringes) destroyed in this destruction event. Used for accountability reconciliation against dispensed and returned quantities.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity destroyed (e.g., kit, vial, tablet). Ensures consistent accountability reconciliation across different IMP dosage forms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kit|vial|tablet|capsule|syringe|blister_pack|bottle — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date on which the IMP accountability reconciliation for this destruction event was completed and confirmed. Marks closure of the destruction accountability cycle.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the IMP accountability reconciliation for this destruction event, confirming that quantities destroyed match quantities returned and recorded in the RTSM. Required for GCP accountability and regulatory inspection.']
  regulatory_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority was formally notified of the destruction event. Populated only when regulatory_notification_required is True.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the destruction event requires formal notification to a regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA) per applicable regulations or study protocol requirements.']
  rtsm_destruction_confirmation_code varchar [note: 'Confirmation or transaction identifier generated by the RTSM system (Medidata Rave) when kit destruction is recorded in the randomization and trial supply management system. Enables reconciliation between the destruction record and the RTSM inventory ledger.']
  sponsor_representative varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor or CRO representative who oversaw or confirmed the destruction on behalf of the study sponsor. Relevant for multi-party accountability in outsourced destruction activities.']
  temperature_excursion_noted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion was documented for the IMP prior to or during transport to destruction. Provides additional context for destruction_reason when product integrity was compromised.']
  tmf_filing_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Certificate of Destruction and supporting documentation filing in the Trial Master File (TMF) or electronic TMF (eTMF). Required for inspection readiness per ICH E6(R2) and TMF Reference Model.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this destruction record. Required for ALCOA+ audit trail compliance and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record integrity.']
  weight_destroyed_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight in kilograms of the IMP/pharmaceutical waste destroyed. Required by some environmental and waste management regulations for pharmaceutical destruction reporting.']
  witness_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the qualified witness present during the physical destruction of the IMP/kits. Witness attestation is a GCP requirement for IMP destruction events.']
  witness_signature_date date [note: 'Date on which the witness signed the destruction record or certificate, confirming their presence during the destruction event. Required for ALCOA+ compliance.']
  witness_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or role of the destruction witness (e.g., QA Manager, Pharmacist, CRA). Confirms the witness has appropriate qualifications per GCP requirements.']

  Note: 'Records the destruction of returned, expired, or rejected IMP/kits. Captures destruction authorization number, destruction date, destruction method (incineration, chemical neutralization), facility, quantity and kit numbers destroyed, witness details, and certificate of destruction reference. Required for GCP audit trails and regulatory inspection readiness.'
}

Table supply.temperature_excursion {
  temperature_excursion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each cold-chain temperature excursion event record in the clinical supply domain.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Product quality investigations link temperature excursions to adverse events when degraded IMP may have been dispensed. Pharmacovigilance teams and QA must correlate excursion records with AEs for roo']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.capa. Business justification: Temperature excursions with adverse disposition decisions require a formal CAPA under GMP/GCP. Linking directly to the capa record enables traceability from excursion to corrective action, supports in']
  central_monitoring_alert_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.central_monitoring_alert. Business justification: Centralized RBM systems generate central_monitoring_alerts when temperature excursion patterns breach KRI thresholds across sites. Linking temperature_excursion to the triggering central_monitoring_al']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Temperature excursions affecting IMP integrity may require regulatory notification to the health authority under the CTA. The CTA defines reporting obligations and timelines; excursion records must re']
  compliance_protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Temperature excursions affecting dispensed or stored IMP constitute protocol deviations requiring formal reporting under ICH E6(R2). Linking temperature_excursion to the resulting protocol_deviation r']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.depot. Business justification: Temperature excursions can occur at depot storage facilities, not just during transit or at sites. temperature_excursion already has site_id (cross-domain) and shipment_id/kit_inventory_id (in-domain)']
  deviation_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.deviation_classification. Business justification: Temperature excursions are classified as protocol deviations under GCP. The deviation_classification record defines regulatory reporting requirements, CAPA triggers, and subject safety impact rules th']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Temperature excursion handling and impact assessment must follow an approved deviation management SOP (GCP/GMP requirement). Linking excursion records to the governing SOP provides audit trail evidenc']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Temperature excursion impact assessment requires the IP\'s stability profile and storage specifications from the study protocol. Direct FK to study.investigational_product enables cross-domain excursio']
  kit_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational medicinal product (IMP) kit definition affected by this excursion. Links to kit_definition for storage condition thresholds and product identity.']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: temperature_excursion captures affected_batch_numbers (STRING) as a denormalized multi-value field. Adding manufacture_batch_id as a FK to supply.manufacture_batch normalizes the primary batch referen']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit. Business justification: GCP requires CRAs to review and document temperature excursions during monitoring visits as part of IP storage compliance verification. Linking excursion records to the monitoring visit that identifie']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Temperature excursions are a primary trigger for quality events in clinical supply. temperature_excursion already links to compliance_capa and protocol_deviation but has no quality_event_id. QA teams ']
  randomization_kit_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.kit_assignment. Business justification: When a temperature excursion affects a kit already assigned to a subject via RTSM, the disposition decision (use/quarantine/replace) requires knowing the RTSM assignment status. This FK enables subjec']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply chain shipment record if the excursion occurred during in-transit logistics. Null if the excursion occurred at a static storage location (depot or site).']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study during which this temperature excursion occurred. Links the excursion to the governing protocol and sponsor.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical site or depot where the excursion was detected or occurred. Null if the excursion occurred in-transit between locations.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Temperature excursion reports are essential TMF documents (ICH E6 R2 requirement, DIA TMF Zone 5). temperature_excursion has document_sop_id but no tmf_document_id. Clinical QA and TMF managers expect']
  affected_batch_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IMP manufacturing batch/lot numbers affected by this excursion. Required for traceability to the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) and stability data package submitted to the sponsor and regulatory authorities.']
  affected_kit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of IMP kits exposed to the temperature excursion. Used for impact scoping, supply replacement planning, and regulatory reporting.']
  affected_kit_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IMP kit numbers (as assigned in the RTSM/IRT system, e.g., Medidata Rave) that were exposed to the temperature excursion. Captured as a denormalized string for audit readiness; detailed kit-level records are maintained in the supply kit inventory.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) has been determined to be required as a result of this excursion event. True = CAPA required; False = no CAPA required. Drives workflow integration with MasterControl CAPA tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this temperature excursion record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  data_logger_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the electronic temperature data logger or continuous monitoring device that recorded the excursion. Used for equipment traceability, calibration verification, and audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11 and EU GMP Annex 11.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'The mechanism or system that first detected the temperature excursion: data_logger (electronic temperature logger device), continuous_monitor (real-time monitoring system), manual_check (routine manual temperature check), irt_alert (Interactive Response Technology system alert from Medidata Rave RTSM), courier_report (reported by logistics provider), site_report (reported by site staff).']
  disposition_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor or QA team formally issued the disposition decision for the affected IMP kits. Required for regulatory audit trail and supply chain accountability reconciliation.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'The final disposition decision made by the sponsor or QA team following the impact assessment. Distinct from impact_assessment_outcome in that this represents the formal, authorized decision (which may differ from the initial assessment recommendation). Drives downstream supply replacement and accountability reconciliation actions.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from excursion start to excursion end. Populated once the excursion is closed. Used by QA and the sponsor to assess cumulative thermal exposure against stability data. Derived operationally from start/end timestamps but stored for audit and reporting efficiency.']
  excursion_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) at which the temperature returned within the acceptable range, marking the end of the excursion event. Null if the excursion is still ongoing at time of record creation.']
  excursion_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the circumstances of the temperature excursion, including any contextual information provided by the site, depot, or courier at the time of detection. Supports GCP audit readiness and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  excursion_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable reference number assigned to this excursion event, used in sponsor communications, QA investigations, and regulatory audit trails. Typically generated by the RTSM or CTMS system (e.g., Medidata Rave or Oracle Siebel CTMS).']
  excursion_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone) at which the temperature excursion began — i.e., when the temperature first breached the defined threshold. This is the principal real-world event time for the excursion. Critical for duration calculation and impact assessment.']
  excursion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the excursion event record. Drives QA workflow: open (newly detected), under_investigation (impact assessment in progress), pending_disposition (awaiting sponsor/QA decision), closed (disposition finalized), voided (false alarm or data entry error confirmed).']
  excursion_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the excursion by the nature of the breach: high_temperature (exceeded upper limit), low_temperature (fell below lower limit), freeze_thaw (frozen product that should not be), humidity (relative humidity breach), combined (multiple parameter breach simultaneously).']
  impact_assessment_outcome varchar [note: 'The outcome of the scientific/QA impact assessment determining the usability of the affected IMP: use (product confirmed acceptable for continued use), quarantine (product held pending further evaluation), destroy (product deemed unusable and scheduled for destruction), pending (assessment not yet completed), return_to_sponsor (product to be returned to sponsor for evaluation). Critical for GCP compliance and patient safety.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes where in the cold-chain the excursion occurred: depot (central or regional IMP warehouse), site (investigational site pharmacy or storage), in_transit (during shipment between locations), courier_hub (third-party logistics hub), customs (held at customs/border control).']
  logger_calibration_date date [note: 'The most recent calibration date of the temperature data logger at the time of the excursion. Required to validate the reliability of the recorded temperature data for GCP audit readiness and regulatory submissions.']
  lower_threshold_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The minimum acceptable storage temperature (in degrees Celsius) defined for the affected IMP, as specified in the kit definition or product stability data. Breach of this threshold constitutes a low-temperature excursion.']
  max_temp_recorded_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The highest temperature value (in degrees Celsius) recorded during the excursion event. Used to assess the severity of a high-temperature excursion against product stability specifications.']
  mean_kinetic_temp_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The Mean Kinetic Temperature (MKT) in degrees Celsius calculated for the excursion period. MKT is the single calculated temperature that, if maintained over the storage period, would produce the same thermal stress as the actual temperature fluctuations. Used per USP <1079> and WHO guidelines for stability impact assessment.']
  min_temp_recorded_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The lowest temperature value (in degrees Celsius) recorded during the excursion event. Used to assess the severity of a low-temperature or freeze-thaw excursion against product stability specifications.']
  replacement_kits_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether replacement IMP kits need to be dispatched to the affected site or depot as a result of the disposition decision (e.g., quarantine or destroy outcome). Drives supply replenishment workflow in the RTSM (Medidata Rave).']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the person (e.g., CRC, CRA, site pharmacist, depot manager) who first reported or logged the temperature excursion into the system. Supports ALCOA+ attributability requirements.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the excursion was formally reported or entered into the system. May differ from excursion_start_timestamp if there was a delay in detection or reporting. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneity assessment.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause identified during the CAPA or investigation process: equipment_failure (refrigeration/monitoring device malfunction), process_failure (SOP not followed), human_error (incorrect handling), logistics_failure (carrier/transit failure), environmental (external weather or facility event), unknown (root cause not yet determined). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|process_failure|human_error|logistics_failure|environmental|unknown — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause of the temperature excursion, as documented during the QA investigation. Supports CAPA development and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  rtsm_excursion_reference varchar [note: 'The excursion or alert reference identifier as recorded in the RTSM system (Medidata Rave IRT/RTSM module). Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational source system.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the temperature excursion. Null if sponsor_notification_required is False or notification has not yet been sent. Used to track compliance with contractual and regulatory notification timelines.']
  sponsor_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor must be formally notified of this excursion per the clinical supply agreement or protocol requirements. True = notification required; False = not required (e.g., minor excursion within pre-defined acceptable limits).']
  stability_data_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product stability data (from the Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier or stability package) was formally reviewed as part of the impact assessment for this excursion. True = reviewed; False = not yet reviewed. Required for GCP compliance documentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this temperature excursion record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and incremental data processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  upper_threshold_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'The maximum acceptable storage temperature (in degrees Celsius) defined for the affected IMP, as specified in the kit definition or product stability data. Breach of this threshold constitutes a high-temperature excursion.']

  Note: 'Records cold-chain temperature excursion events detected during IMP storage or transit. Captures excursion start and end timestamps, affected location (depot, site, in-transit), temperature range breached, duration, affected kit/batch numbers, impact assessment outcome (use/quarantine/destroy), and sponsor/QA disposition decision. Critical for cold-chain compliance and GCP audit readiness.'
}

Table supply.accountability_reconciliation {
  accountability_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the drug accountability reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — periodic reconciliation event with a defined lifecycle (open → reconciled / discrepancy-flagged).']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: GCP ICH E6 requires IMP accountability reconciliation at the treatment arm level. Regulators and inspectors review arm-level dispensed, returned, and destroyed quantities. Without a direct arm FK, arm']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation discrepancies discovered during GCP audits are recorded as audit findings. This link enables direct navigation from the reconciliation record to the specific audit fi']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_capa. Business justification: Reconciliation discrepancies exceeding thresholds trigger CAPAs per GCP requirements. accountability_reconciliation already links to audit_finding and inspection_finding but has no direct compliance_c']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation reports are submitted to health authorities as part of CTA compliance obligations in EU member states. Reconciliation records must reference the applicable CTA for re']
  compliance_protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Unresolved IMP accountability discrepancies constitute protocol deviations under ICH E6(R2). Linking accountability_reconciliation to the protocol_deviation record enables regulatory reporting, CSR de']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: GCP requires IMP accountability reconciliation to be complete before database lock. The database_lock_event tracks pre_lock_qc_checklist_complete and sdv_complete. Linking accountability reconciliatio']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_deviation. Business justification: Accountability reconciliation discrepancies (wrong dose, missed return) directly generate subject deviations. Regulatory inspectors and CAPA processes require direct traceability from reconciliation r']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation must be performed per an approved SOP (ICH GCP requirement). Linking reconciliation records to the governing SOP provides audit trail evidence of procedural complianc']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation is performed at epoch boundaries (end of treatment epoch, end of extension, study close). Epoch-level reconciliation is a standard GCP requirement enabling period-spe']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational medicinal product (IMP) being reconciled. A single reconciliation record covers one IMP or comparator product at the site for the period.']
  inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection_finding. Business justification: Regulatory inspectors (FDA, EMA) routinely issue findings against IMP accountability records. Linking accountability_reconciliation to the inspection_finding enables direct traceability from the inspe']
  kit_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.kit_definition. Business justification: Drug accountability reconciliation is performed at the kit type level — reconciling quantities dispensed, returned, and destroyed for a specific kit definition. accountability_reconciliation already h']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: ICH E6 R2 GCP requires the PI to personally sign off on IMP accountability reconciliation. The existing investigator_name is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to principal_investigator enables PI']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation discrepancies are a named quality event type in clinical supply QA. Linking accountability_reconciliation to quality_event supports QA reporting, inspection readiness']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation is performed against the randomization scheme to verify kit usage matches expected allocation ratios. Regulatory inspections (FDA, EMA) require reconciliation reports']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation reports prepared for regulatory submissions and GCP inspections must reference the SAP version that defines analysis populations and reconciliation scope. The sap_ver']
  site_cra_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site_cra_assignment. Business justification: Drug accountability reconciliation is performed by the assigned CRA during monitoring visits. The cra_name column is a denormalized reference to the CRA assignment. This FK normalizes that relationshi']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Accountability reconciliation records are essential site file documents per ICH E6 R2. CRAs verify reconciliation records during site file review. Direct FK enables site file completeness tracking for']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this drug accountability reconciliation is performed. Links the reconciliation to the study protocol, IMP details, and regulatory submission context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which this drug accountability reconciliation is conducted. Each reconciliation is site-specific and covers all IMP activity at that site for the defined period.']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation is performed per subject per visit. Linking accountability_reconciliation to subject_visit_data allows CRAs to confirm that eCRF data completeness and SDV status alig']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: IMP accountability reconciliation is an ICH GCP essential TMF document (Section 8.3). The reconciliation record must link to its filed TMF document to support inspection readiness. inspection_ready_fl']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: GCP requires per-treatment-arm accountability reconciliation — dispensed, returned, and destroyed quantities must balance per arm. accountability_reconciliation has no treatment_arm FK despite being t']
  visit_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.visit_report. Business justification: ICH GCP E6 requires IP accountability reconciliation findings to be documented in monitoring visit reports. Linking accountability_reconciliation directly to visit_report enables regulatory inspectors']
  actual_closing_balance_units varchar [note: 'Physical count of IMP units on hand at the site at the end of the reconciliation period, as verified by the site staff and/or CRA during the monitoring visit. This is the ground-truth inventory count.']
  adjusted_units varchar [note: 'Net quantity adjustment applied to the reconciliation (positive or negative) to account for documented corrections such as data entry errors, kit relabeling, or sponsor-approved adjustments. Zero if no adjustments were made.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or contextual notes entered by the CRA, site staff, or data manager regarding this reconciliation. Supplements the discrepancy_detail field for general narrative.']
  cra_signoff_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA / monitor signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation. Null if cra_signoff_flag is False.']
  cra_signoff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) / monitor has reviewed and signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation record. True = signed; False = pending. Required at every monitoring visit per ICH E6(R2).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this drug accountability reconciliation record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) principles.']
  destroyed_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of IMP units destroyed at the site or returned to depot for destruction during the reconciliation period. Destruction must be documented per sponsor SOP and applicable regulations.']
  discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual discrepancy line items identified during this reconciliation (e.g., missing kit, unaccounted return, dispensing record mismatch). Distinct from discrepancy_units which measures total quantity variance.']
  discrepancy_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative describing the nature, root cause, and resolution status of identified discrepancies. Captured by the CRA or site staff. Required when discrepancy_count > 0. Supports regulatory inspection readiness and CAPA documentation.']
  discrepancy_resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution status of identified discrepancies. \'not_applicable\' = no discrepancy found; \'open\' = discrepancy identified, investigation not started; \'under_investigation\' = root cause analysis in progress; \'resolved\' = discrepancy explained and documented; \'escalated\' = referred to sponsor QA or regulatory authority.']
  discrepancy_units varchar [note: 'Quantity difference between expected_closing_balance_units and actual_closing_balance_units (actual minus expected). A non-zero value triggers discrepancy investigation. Positive = surplus; negative = shortage.']
  dispensed_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of IMP units dispensed to subjects at the site during the reconciliation period. Sourced from dispensing records in Medidata Rave RTSM and eCRF dispensing logs.']
  expected_closing_balance_units varchar [note: 'Calculated expected closing balance: opening_balance_units + received_units - dispensed_units + returned_units - destroyed_units + adjusted_units. Used as the reference value against which the physical count is compared to identify discrepancies.']
  expired_units_count varchar [note: 'Number of IMP units that had expired as of the reconciliation period end date. Expired units must be quarantined and destroyed per sponsor SOP; their presence in usable inventory constitutes a protocol deviation.']
  expiry_date_earliest date [note: 'The earliest expiry date among all IMP units currently on hand at the site as of the reconciliation period end date. Used to flag impending expiry risk and trigger resupply or destruction actions.']
  gcp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation record is fully compliant with GCP §8.3.13 requirements (both investigator and CRA sign-offs obtained, no unresolved discrepancies, all required fields populated). True = compliant; False = non-compliant or pending.']
  inspection_ready_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this reconciliation record and its supporting documentation are complete and ready for regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.). True = inspection-ready; False = documentation gaps exist. Supports eTMF completeness tracking.']
  investigator_signoff_date date [note: 'The date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) or delegated sub-investigator signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation. Null if investigator_signoff_flag is False.']
  investigator_signoff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) or delegated sub-investigator has reviewed and signed off on this drug accountability reconciliation record. True = signed; False = pending. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  next_reconciliation_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled due date for the next drug accountability reconciliation at this site for this IMP. Derived from the monitoring visit schedule or protocol-defined reconciliation frequency. Used for proactive compliance tracking.']
  opening_balance_units varchar [note: 'The quantity of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials, kits) on hand at the site at the start of the reconciliation period. Carried forward from the closing balance of the prior reconciliation period or from the initial receipt if this is the first period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the reconciliation period covered by this record. Defines the closing boundary for all IMP transactions included in this reconciliation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the reconciliation period covered by this record. Together with period_end_date, defines the window of IMP activity (receipts, dispensing, returns, destruction) being reconciled.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation was identified during this reconciliation (e.g., expired IMP dispensed, incorrect kit dispensed, dispensing outside visit window). True = deviation identified; False = no deviation. Triggers deviation reporting workflow.']
  received_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of IMP units received at the site from the depot or sponsor during the reconciliation period. Sourced from shipment receipt records in Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which the drug accountability reconciliation was physically performed (i.e., the date of the physical count and record review). This is the principal business event date for this transaction, distinct from sign-off dates.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this reconciliation record, typically generated by the RTSM or CTMS (e.g., RECON-SITE001-20240315). Used in monitoring reports, TMF filings, and regulatory inspection packages. Maps to the accountability record reference in Oracle Siebel CTMS and Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow status of the reconciliation record. \'open\' = in progress; \'reconciled\' = balances confirmed with no discrepancy; \'discrepancy_flagged\' = unresolved quantity mismatch requiring investigation; \'pending_review\' = awaiting CRA or investigator sign-off; \'closed\' = finalized and archived post-DBL.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reconciliation trigger. \'routine_monitoring\' = performed at a scheduled CRA monitoring visit; \'pre_dbl\' = required prior to Database Lock (DBL); \'site_closeout\' = performed at study close-out; \'interim\' = ad hoc between visits; \'triggered\' = initiated due to a discrepancy or audit finding.']
  returned_units varchar [note: 'Total quantity of IMP units returned from subjects to the site (unused or partially used) during the reconciliation period. Includes returns captured in Medidata Rave RTSM return logs.']
  rtsm_reconciliation_ref varchar [note: 'The reconciliation reference or transaction identifier assigned by the Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) system (Medidata Rave) for this accountability event. Enables traceability back to the source system record.']
  source_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the physical or electronic source document (e.g., drug accountability log, dispensing record binder, eTMF artifact ID in Veeva Vault) that supports this reconciliation record. Required for SDV and regulatory inspection.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any cold-chain temperature excursion was recorded for IMP stored at this site during the reconciliation period. True = at least one excursion event occurred; False = no excursion. Excursions may affect IMP usability and require sponsor assessment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which IMP quantities are counted and reconciled (e.g., kit, tablet, vial). Must be consistent with the dispensing unit defined in the kit definition and RTSM configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kit|tablet|capsule|vial|ampoule|syringe|patch|sachet — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this drug accountability reconciliation record was most recently modified. Required for audit trail completeness under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles. Tracks the last state change in the reconciliation lifecycle.']

  Note: 'Periodic drug accountability reconciliation record at the site level, capturing the reconciliation period, opening balance, receipts, dispensed quantities, returned quantities, destroyed quantities, closing balance, discrepancy count and detail, reconciliation status (open, reconciled, discrepancy-flagged), investigator sign-off, and CRA/monitor sign-off. Required at every monitoring visit and prior to database lock (DBL). Supports GCP §8.3.13 compliance, regulatory inspection readiness, and site close-out.'
}

Table supply.resupply_order {
  resupply_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the resupply order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Resupply orders to EU/EEA sites require confirmation of valid CTA status in the destination member state before order approval and dispatch. Supply operations teams perform this CTA gate check as part']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: In adaptive trial designs, interim analysis results (sample_size_reestimated, revised_sample_size, adaptation_description) directly trigger resupply orders. Clinical supply teams must trace which inte']
  kit_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IMP kit type or kit definition being requested in this resupply order, as defined in the RTSM kit catalogue.']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit. Business justification: CRAs identifying low stock or accountability gaps during a monitoring visit directly trigger resupply orders. Linking resupply_order to the triggering monitoring_visit supports IP accountability audit']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version in effect at the time the resupply order was generated. Ensures supply is aligned with the current approved protocol and dosing schedule.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound shipment record created to fulfill this resupply order. Populated once the order transitions to a dispatched or fulfilled state.']
  source_depot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the depot or distribution center from which the IMP kits will be dispatched to fulfill this resupply order. Supports depot-to-site and depot-to-depot supply chain tracking.']
  stratification_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.stratification_cell. Business justification: RTSM resupply triggers fire at stratification-cell level when cell-specific stock is depleted. Linking resupply_order to stratification_cell enables cell-level stock management and prevents enrollment']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which the resupply order was generated. Links the order to the sponsoring protocol and IMP supply plan.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site or depot that triggered or is the destination of the resupply order. Identifies the requesting or receiving location in the supply chain.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IMP supply plan or forecast that this resupply order is fulfilling. Links operational orders back to strategic supply planning for variance analysis.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study treatment arm for which the IMP kits are being resupplied. Ensures blinded or unblinded supply allocation aligns with randomization scheme.']
  accountability_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP kits in this resupply order have been reconciled in the drug accountability log at the receiving site or depot. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the resupply shipment was confirmed as received at the destination site or depot. Used to calculate fulfillment lead time and identify supply delays.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the resupply order was reviewed and approved by the supply manager or depot coordinator prior to dispatch.']
  blinding_level varchar [note: 'Blinding status of the IMP kits in this resupply order. Determines handling, labelling, and access control requirements during supply chain operations.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the resupply order was cancelled. Populated only when order_status is \'cancelled\'. Required for supply accountability and audit trail.']
  cold_chain_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP kits in this resupply order require cold-chain logistics (temperature-controlled transport and storage). Drives courier selection and packaging requirements.']
  courier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics provider or courier company assigned to transport the resupply shipment. Used for tracking, accountability, and vendor performance monitoring.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this resupply order record was first captured in the system of record. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  expected_fulfillment_date date [note: 'Target date by which the resupply order is expected to be delivered to the requesting site or depot, based on standard lead times and courier schedules.']
  expiry_date_earliest date [note: 'The earliest expiry date among the IMP kits included in this resupply order. Critical for expiry management to ensure kits will remain within shelf life for the expected dispensing window.']
  is_emergency_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this resupply order was processed as an emergency override, bypassing standard approval workflows due to critical patient supply risk. Triggers mandatory post-hoc documentation.']
  min_stock_threshold varchar [note: 'The minimum stock level (in units/kits) at the requesting site or depot that triggered the resupply order. Captured at the time of order creation for audit and analytics.']
  order_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supply manager notes, special handling instructions, or contextual information related to this resupply order. Supports operational communication between depots and sites.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the resupply order was formally placed or triggered, either by the RTSM resupply algorithm or by a supply manager. This is the principal business event timestamp for the order.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the resupply order, typically generated by the RTSM (Medidata Rave) or supply management system. Used in communications with depots, couriers, and sponsors.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the resupply order workflow, from initial trigger through fulfillment or cancellation. Drives operational dashboards and escalation logic.']
  quantity_approved varchar [note: 'Number of IMP kits or units approved for dispatch after supply manager or depot review. May differ from quantity_requested due to stock availability or protocol constraints.']
  quantity_requested varchar [note: 'Number of IMP kits or units requested in this resupply order, as specified at the time of order creation by the RTSM algorithm or supply manager.']
  quantity_shipped varchar [note: 'Actual number of IMP kits or units included in the outbound shipment fulfilling this resupply order. Populated at dispatch and used for accountability reconciliation.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantities specified in this resupply order (e.g., kit, vial, blister pack). Ensures consistent interpretation across depots and sites.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date on which the drug accountability reconciliation was completed for the IMP kits delivered under this resupply order. Populated when accountability_reconciled is True.']
  requesting_location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether the resupply order was initiated by an investigational site, a regional depot, or a central pharmacy. Determines routing and fulfillment logic.']
  rtsm_order_reference varchar [note: 'The native order reference or transaction ID assigned by the RTSM system (Medidata Rave) when the resupply order was generated. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit.']
  rtsm_trigger_rule varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the RTSM resupply algorithm rule that triggered this order (e.g., minimum stock level rule, days-of-supply threshold rule). Null for manually initiated orders.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this resupply order originated (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_order_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or unique identifier of this resupply order in the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM internal order ID). Enables cross-system traceability and reconciliation.']
  stock_at_trigger varchar [note: 'Actual inventory count at the requesting site or depot at the moment the resupply order was triggered. Used to validate trigger logic and support supply accountability reconciliation.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the IMP kits in this resupply order during transit and at the destination. Determines packaging, courier, and handling instructions.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Courier-assigned tracking number for the shipment fulfilling this resupply order. Enables real-time shipment tracking and delivery confirmation.']
  trigger_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the resupply order was automatically triggered by the RTSM resupply algorithm (e.g., minimum stock threshold breach) or initiated manually by a supply manager or other authorized personnel.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this resupply order record. Used for change detection, ETL incremental loads, and audit compliance.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the resupply order indicating the speed of fulfillment required. Emergency orders may trigger expedited cold-chain logistics and override standard lead times.']

  Note: 'Captures resupply order requests triggered by RTSM resupply algorithms or manual supply manager intervention. Records the requesting site or depot, IMP/kit type requested, quantity requested, urgency level, order date, expected fulfillment date, fulfillment status, and linked shipment reference. Enables end-to-end resupply workflow tracking from trigger to delivery.'
}

Table supply.import_permit {
  import_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the import permit record in the clinical supply lakehouse. Primary key for the import_permit data product.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization.
