Business justification: Import permits for IMP in EU/EEA are issued in the context of a CTA. Regulatory and supply teams must link import permits to the authorizing CTA for compliance audits. `cta_reference` is a denormalize']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_country. Business justification: Import permits are country-specific regulatory documents tied to a study\'s country activation. Linking import_permit to study.study_country enables country-level regulatory tracking — health authority']
  country_registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.country_registration. Business justification: Import permits for comparator or approved reference products are issued in the context of a country registration. Country registration governs which products can be imported; regulatory and supply tea']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.depot. Business justification: Import permits are associated with the receiving depot — the depot that will receive the cross-border IMP shipment. The permit authorizes importation to a specific country/facility. Adding depot_id li']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational medicinal product (IMP) covered by this import permit. Identifies the specific drug substance or device being imported.']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: US import permits for IMP reference the IND number as the regulatory authorization basis. Supply and regulatory teams link import permits to the IND for FDA compliance and annual report preparation. `']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Import permits are issued by health authorities against a specific protocol/IND version. Regulatory submissions for import authorization reference the protocol version. Linking import_permit to study.']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Import permits are regulatory documents filed in CTA/IND submission binders. import_permit links to tmf_document but not regulatory_binder. Regulatory affairs teams include import permits in submissio']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical supply shipment that this import permit authorizes. Enables traceability between the permit and the physical IMP shipment.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this import permit is required. Links the permit to the specific trial protocol and IMP.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Import permits are ICH GCP essential TMF documents (Zone 5 — Regulatory). Each import permit record must link to its filed TMF document for country-level regulatory compliance tracking and inspection ']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the legal entity (CRO, sponsor, or local affiliate) that submitted the import permit application to the regulatory authority. May differ from the sponsor when a CRO acts as the applicant.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date on which the import permit application was formally submitted to the issuing regulatory authority. Used to track regulatory lead times and application-to-approval cycle times.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally approved and issued the import permit. Null if the permit has not yet been approved. Used to calculate approval lead time and schedule shipments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the import permit record was first created in the clinical supply management system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs entry or declaration number assigned by the customs authority upon import clearance. Links the import permit to the customs clearance record for audit and accountability purposes.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) registration number for the study. Required on EU import permits to link the IMP importation to the registered clinical trial.']
  export_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country from which the IMP is being exported. Required for bilateral import/export permit matching and customs documentation.']
  hs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'World Customs Organization Harmonized System (HS) tariff classification code for the IMP. Required for customs clearance and import duty determination. Typically 6-10 digits depending on the destination country.']
  imp_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the IMP as stated on the import permit, including drug name, dosage form, strength, and any blinding information. Must match the IMP details on the permit document exactly for customs compliance.']
  import_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the destination country into which the IMP is being imported. Determines applicable regulatory requirements and customs procedures.']
  inn_name varchar [note: 'International Nonproprietary Name (INN) of the active pharmaceutical ingredient as recognized by the WHO. Used in regulatory submissions and customs declarations to identify the drug substance.']
  issuing_authority_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the regulatory authority that issued the permit (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, AUS, CAN, DEU). Identifies the jurisdiction of the issuing body.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the national regulatory authority or government body that issued the import permit (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, Health Canada). Used for regulatory correspondence and compliance reporting.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory or operational notes related to the import permit, such as special conditions imposed by the authority, customs broker instructions, or escalation details.']
  permit_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the scanned or electronic copy of the import permit document stored in the eTMF (Veeva Vault). Enables direct retrieval of the official permit document for customs and audit purposes.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number assigned by the issuing regulatory authority. This is the externally-known identifier used in regulatory correspondence, customs declarations, and shipment documentation.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the import permit. Drives shipment release decisions and regulatory compliance tracking. Draft = in preparation; Submitted = application filed with authority; Approved = permit granted; Rejected = application denied; Expired = validity period elapsed; Revoked = permit withdrawn by authority.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the import permit by scope of authorization. Single_shipment covers one consignment; multi_shipment covers a defined number of shipments; blanket covers all shipments within a validity period; emergency is expedited authorization; re_export covers onward shipment to another country.']
  permitted_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum quantity of IMP authorized for import under this permit. Expressed in the unit defined by permitted_quantity_unit. Enables supply planning and prevents over-shipment against permit limits.']
  permitted_quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the permitted_quantity field (e.g., units, vials, tablets, capsules, kg, g, mg, L, mL). Must align with the IMP dosage form and packaging configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: units|vials|tablets|capsules|kg|g|mg|L|mL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  permitted_shipment_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of individual shipments authorized under this permit. Applicable for multi_shipment permit types. Null for single_shipment or blanket permits where shipment count is not restricted.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally rejected the import permit application. Populated only when permit_status is \'rejected\'. Used to track regulatory response timelines and initiate resubmission.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the regulatory authority for rejecting the import permit application. Populated when permit_status is \'rejected\'. Used to guide resubmission and CAPA activities.']
  renewal_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before permit expiry that the renewal application must be submitted to the issuing authority. Used to trigger automated renewal reminders and prevent supply disruption.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this import permit requires periodic renewal before the study completes. Triggers proactive renewal workflow to prevent supply chain disruption due to permit expiry.']
  requires_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IMP requires cold-chain logistics management during import (refrigerated, frozen, or ultra-frozen transport). Drives logistics planning and customs clearance prioritization.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority revoked a previously approved import permit. Populated only when permit_status is \'revoked\'. Triggers immediate shipment hold and regulatory notification.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the regulatory authority for revoking a previously approved import permit. Populated when permit_status is \'revoked\'. Triggers regulatory notification and supply chain hold.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Required storage condition for the IMP as specified on the import permit and in the IMP dossier. Drives cold-chain logistics requirements during import and customs clearance. Must align with kit_definition storage conditions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the import permit record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  utilized_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of IMP actually imported against this permit to date. Used to track permit utilization, remaining authorized quantity, and accountability reconciliation. Expressed in the same unit as permitted_quantity_unit.']
  utilized_shipment_count varchar [note: 'Number of shipments that have been made against this permit to date. Used to track permit utilization and determine remaining shipment capacity for multi_shipment permits.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the import permit is legally effective and shipments may be made under its authority. Typically coincides with or follows the approval date.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date on which the import permit expires and is no longer valid for authorizing IMP shipments. Critical for preventing shipment delays due to expired permits. Null for open-ended permits.']

  Note: 'Manages country-level import permits and licenses required for cross-border IMP shipments. Captures permit number, issuing authority, country, IMP reference, permitted quantity, permit validity dates, application date, approval date, and permit status. Enables regulatory compliance tracking for international clinical supply logistics and prevents shipment delays due to missing permits.'
}

Table supply.depot_inventory {
  depot_inventory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each depot inventory record. Primary key for the depot_inventory data product in the supply domain Silver layer.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Depot inventory records are scoped to a CTA for the country. Import permit numbers on depot inventory tie back to CTA-authorized shipments; regulatory inspectors verify depot inventory against the CTA']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the depot (central or regional warehouse) where this inventory is physically held. Links to the depot master record.']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the IMP master record identifying the investigational product whose stock is tracked in this inventory line.']
  kit_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the kit definition record specifying the kit type, dosage form, and packaging configuration for this inventory. Supports RTSM-driven kit-level tracking.']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Depot inventory is tracked by batch (depot_inventory.batch_number is denormalized). Each depot inventory record represents stock of a specific manufacturing batch at a depot. Adding manufacture_batch_']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Depot inventory quality issues (storage condition breach, expired stock found in available status, physical count discrepancy) trigger quality events. depot_inventory has no compliance link. This supp']
  receipt_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Depot inventory records are created or updated when a shipment is received at the depot. Adding receipt_shipment_id links the depot inventory record to the inbound shipment that created/updated it. Th']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this IMP inventory is allocated. Enables study-level supply accountability and reconciliation.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Depot inventory is tracked per treatment arm for RTSM-driven resupply and blinded stock management. Per-arm depot stock levels drive resupply order generation and are reported in supply chain dashboar']
  accountability_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the drug accountability reconciliation process for this inventory record. Reconciliation confirms that all dispensed, returned, and destroyed quantities account for the total received, as required by GCP and regulatory authorities.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned batch or lot number for the IMP stock held in this inventory record. Critical for GMP traceability, recall management, and regulatory accountability.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis (CoA) issued by the manufacturer for this batch. The CoA confirms the batch meets specification and is required for QP release and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  destruction_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the destruction certificate issued when qty_destroyed units were disposed of. Required for drug accountability reconciliation and regulatory submission documentation.']
  erp_material_document_number varchar [note: 'Material document number from SAP S/4HANA corresponding to the goods receipt or inventory movement transaction for this batch. Supports financial reconciliation between supply chain and procurement/finance.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date assigned to this IMP batch by the manufacturer. Inventory with expiry_date in the past must be quarantined or destroyed per GMP requirements. Drives automated expiry alerts in RTSM.']
  import_permit_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory import permit or customs clearance number associated with this batch\'s receipt at the depot. Required for cross-border IMP shipments and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  inventory_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable identifier for this depot inventory record as assigned by the RTSM or CTMS system (e.g., INV-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this depot inventory record. Drives supply availability calculations, RTSM allocation logic, and GMP disposition decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Available|Allocated|Quarantined|Expired|Destroyed|Returned|On_Hold — promote to reference product]']
  is_cold_chain boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IMP batch requires continuous cold-chain management throughout storage and distribution. Drives cold-chain logistics planning, temperature monitoring requirements, and qualified carrier selection.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IMP batch is classified as a controlled substance requiring enhanced security, dual-custody handling, and DEA/national narcotics authority reporting for inventory accountability.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the kit contents held in this inventory record. Distinguishes IMP, comparator, placebo, rescue medication, ancillary supplies, and open-label kits for supply planning and accountability.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent physical inventory count was conducted at the depot for this batch/kit type. Supports GMP compliance scheduling and audit readiness.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this depot inventory record, whether from an RTSM transaction, physical count, or manual adjustment. Supports audit trail and data lineage per ALCOA+ principles.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date on which the IMP batch was manufactured. Used alongside expiry date to calculate remaining shelf life and support GMP batch traceability.']
  min_stock_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity of available stock that must be maintained at this depot for this IMP/kit type before a replenishment order is triggered. Supports proactive supply planning and prevents stockouts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for supply chain staff to record observations, discrepancy explanations, QP comments, or other contextual information relevant to this depot inventory record. Supports audit trail documentation.']
  physical_count_discrepancy varchar [note: 'Difference between qty_last_physical_count and the system-recorded qty_on_hand at the time of the physical count. A non-zero value triggers a discrepancy investigation per GMP requirements.']
  qp_release_date date [note: 'Date on which the Qualified Person formally released this batch for clinical use. Null if release is pending or the batch has been rejected.']
  qp_release_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Qualified Person (QP) batch release decision for this inventory. Only QP-released stock may be dispensed to clinical sites per EU GMP Annex 13 requirements.']
  qty_allocated varchar [note: 'Number of kits or units reserved for pending outbound shipments or site replenishment orders that have been confirmed but not yet physically dispatched. Prevents double-allocation in RTSM.']
  qty_available varchar [note: 'Number of kits or units available for new allocation or shipment. Calculated as qty_on_hand minus qty_allocated minus qty_quarantined minus qty_expired. Represents the net usable stock for RTSM dispensing decisions.']
  qty_destroyed varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of kits or units destroyed at this depot for this batch. Supports drug accountability reconciliation and regulatory destruction certificate documentation.']
  qty_expired varchar [note: 'Number of kits or units that have passed their expiry date and are no longer eligible for dispensing. Tracked separately to support destruction reconciliation and regulatory accountability reporting.']
  qty_last_physical_count varchar [note: 'Actual kit or unit count recorded during the most recent physical inventory count at the depot. Used to reconcile system-recorded quantities against physical stock and identify discrepancies.']
  qty_on_hand varchar [note: 'Total number of kits or units of this IMP batch currently physically present at the depot, regardless of allocation status. The gross stock figure before netting allocated, quarantined, or expired quantities.']
  qty_quarantined varchar [note: 'Number of kits or units placed under quarantine status due to quality investigations, temperature excursions, damaged packaging, or pending QP release. Quarantined stock is excluded from available supply.']
  qty_returned varchar [note: 'Number of kits or units returned from clinical sites to this depot. Returned stock is typically quarantined pending QP review before any disposition decision (reuse, destruction, or reconciliation).']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why stock was placed in quarantine status. Required for GMP deviation documentation and QP disposition decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Temperature_Excursion|Damaged_Packaging|QP_Hold|Recall|Stability_Failure|Pending_Release|Investigation — promote to reference product]']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which this batch/kit stock was physically received at the depot. Used for FIFO/FEFO inventory management, shelf-life calculations, and GMP receipt documentation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this depot inventory record was first created in the system, typically upon receipt of the batch at the depot. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.']
  reorder_quantity varchar [note: 'Standard quantity to be ordered when stock falls below the minimum threshold. Used by RTSM and supply planners to generate replenishment requests to the manufacturing site or central depot.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date by which the IMP batch must be retested to confirm continued potency and stability before further use. Applicable for products with retest periods rather than hard expiry dates.']
  rtsm_inventory_code varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned to this inventory record within the RTSM system (Medidata Rave). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational RTSM source system.']
  storage_condition_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature-controlled storage zone within the depot where this inventory is held (e.g., Ambient, Refrigerated 2–8°C, Frozen -20°C, Ultra-Low -80°C). Drives cold-chain logistics and compliance with IMP storage requirements.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion event has been recorded for this inventory batch during storage at the depot. A True value triggers quarantine and QP review before further use.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The dispensing or counting unit used for all quantity fields in this inventory record (e.g., Kit, Vial, Tablet). Ensures consistent quantity interpretation across RTSM, ERP, and accountability reports. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Kit|Vial|Tablet|Capsule|Syringe|Bottle|Blister_Pack — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks IMP and kit stock levels held at each depot, including quantities by batch, kit type, and storage condition zone. Records receipt date, current quantity on-hand, quantity allocated for pending shipments, quantity quarantined, quantity expired, and last physical inventory count. Distinct from site_inventory which tracks site-level stock; depot_inventory covers central and regional warehouse holdings.'
}

Table monitoring.monitoring_plan {
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the monitoring plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: EU/ICH GCP requires monitoring plans to be scoped to the authorized CTA. Regulatory affairs teams verify monitoring plan alignment with CTA conditions and authorized subject counts. A clinical-researc']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Every monitoring plan is governed by a specific monitoring SOP (e.g., on-site monitoring SOP, SDV SOP). GCP requires traceability from the monitoring plan to the controlling SOP. QA auditors and inspe']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: The IND is the governing regulatory application defining safety reporting obligations and protocol requirements that monitoring plans must enforce. CRAs and regulatory affairs teams jointly reference ']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: Monitoring plans specify IP accountability requirements (ip_accountability_required flag exists). Linking to the specific IP enables IP-specific monitoring procedures and accountability checklists — a']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Monitoring plans are authored against a specific protocol version. TMF section 07 and regulatory submissions require traceability from monitoring plan to protocol. CRAs reference protocol version when']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: Monitoring plans define SDV scope, IRT/RTSM verification procedures, and blinding-aware monitoring strategies based on the randomization scheme. A clinical-research expert expects monitoring_plan to r']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Protocol amendments directly trigger monitoring plan revisions (new SDV scope, visit frequency, critical data elements). Linking the monitoring plan to the driving amendment is required for regulatory']
  regulatory_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.strategy. Business justification: The regulatory strategy defines target agencies, submission timelines, and development phase that directly determine the monitoring approach, RBM strategy, applicable regions, and data privacy require']
  reporting_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.reporting_obligation. Business justification: Monitoring plans must embed safety reporting obligation timelines (SAE 15-day, SUSAR 7-day) to define escalation triggers and KRI thresholds. GCP requires monitoring plans to reference applicable repo']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Risk-based monitoring (RBM) plans are formally derived from compliance risk assessments. The risk_assessment output defines monitoring intensity, SDV scope, and visit frequency. A clinical-research do']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_study. Business justification: A monitoring plan governs a specific study. Every CTMS and RBM system requires direct study-to-monitoring-plan traceability for enrollment dashboards, oversight reporting, and ICH E6 R2 risk-based mon']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The monitoring plan is a required TMF essential document (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 4). Linking the plan record to its TMF filing enables TMF completeness tracking and inspection readiness verifica']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the reason for this version amendment, such as protocol changes, regulatory feedback, or lessons learned from interim monitoring data. Required for audit trail and TMF documentation.']
  applicable_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory regions or jurisdictions to which this monitoring plan applies (e.g., USA, EU, JPN, AUS, CAN). Determines which regulatory authority requirements (FDA, EMA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada) govern monitoring activities.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the monitoring plan was formally approved by the authorized signatory (e.g., Sponsor, CRO Quality Assurance). Required for regulatory compliance and TMF filing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual (e.g., Sponsor representative, CRO Quality Assurance lead) who formally approved this version of the monitoring plan. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  capa_resolution_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days within which a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) identified during monitoring must be resolved and closed by the site or sponsor. Defined in the monitoring plan to ensure timely remediation.']
  centralized_monitoring_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether centralized statistical monitoring (e.g., Key Risk Indicator (KRI) dashboards, data trend analysis) is included as a component of the monitoring strategy for this study.']
  closeout_visit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Site Close-Out Visit (COV) is mandated for all sites under this monitoring plan upon study completion or site termination.']
  cra_qualification_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of minimum qualifications, certifications, and experience required for Clinical Research Associates (CRAs) assigned to monitor sites under this plan (e.g., therapeutic area experience, GCP certification, sponsor-specific training).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring plan record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  critical_data_elements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative description of data fields designated as critical per ICH E6(R2), such as primary efficacy endpoints, informed consent dates, eligibility criteria, and safety data. These elements receive mandatory SDV regardless of overall SDV scope.']
  critical_processes varchar [note: 'Description of study processes designated as critical per ICH E6(R2), such as randomization, informed consent, Investigational Product (IP) accountability, and Serious Adverse Event (SAE) reporting. These processes receive heightened monitoring focus.']
  data_privacy_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of applicable data privacy and patient data protection requirements governing monitoring activities under this plan, including GDPR, HIPAA, and local data protection laws relevant to the study regions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the monitoring plan ceases to be operative, typically upon supersession by a new version or study close-out. Nullable for the currently active version.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the monitoring plan becomes operative and binding for study monitoring activities. Aligns with study initiation or amendment approval date.']
  escalation_trigger_criteria varchar [note: 'Defined conditions or thresholds that trigger escalation of monitoring intensity, additional site visits, or Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) initiation. Examples include KRI breaches, repeated protocol deviations, or SAE under-reporting.']
  gcp_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether current Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training certification is a prerequisite for all Clinical Research Associates (CRAs) executing monitoring activities under this plan.']
  high_risk_visit_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Monitoring visit interval in calendar days applicable to sites classified as High risk tier. Typically shorter than the standard interval to ensure heightened oversight.']
  icf_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether review of signed Informed Consent Forms (ICFs) for all enrolled subjects is a mandatory component of each monitoring visit under this plan.']
  interim_visit_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in calendar days between scheduled interim monitoring visits for an average-risk site. Actual frequency may be adjusted based on site risk tier and Key Risk Indicator (KRI) triggers.']
  ip_accountability_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Investigational Product (IP) accountability checks (dispensing, return, destruction reconciliation) are mandated as part of on-site monitoring visits under this plan.']
  kri_tolerance_limits varchar [note: 'Narrative or structured description of the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) thresholds and tolerance limits defined in this monitoring plan. Specifies the upper/lower bounds for metrics such as query rate, protocol deviation rate, and enrollment rate that trigger escalation actions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this monitoring plan record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and version control requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2).']
  low_risk_visit_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Monitoring visit interval in calendar days applicable to sites classified as Low risk tier. Typically longer than the standard interval, reflecting reduced oversight requirements.']
  missing_data_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable percentage of missing or incomplete data fields in the Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) before a site is flagged for corrective action under the centralized monitoring program.']
  monitoring_approach varchar [note: 'Primary monitoring strategy defined for the study. On-Site involves Clinical Research Associate (CRA) visits to investigational sites; Remote involves off-site review of electronic data; Centralized involves statistical and data-driven oversight; Hybrid combines multiple approaches per Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) principles.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the monitoring plan, typically formatted as MP-[StudyCode]-[SequenceNumber]. Used in Trial Master File (TMF) references and regulatory submissions.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the monitoring plan. Draft indicates work-in-progress; Under Review indicates pending approval; Approved is the active operative version; Superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; Retired indicates no longer applicable.']
  protocol_deviation_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable protocol deviation rate (expressed as a percentage of total subject visits or data points) before escalation actions are triggered at a site. Defined per the RBM strategy.']
  query_rate_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable data query rate (expressed as a percentage of total data points entered) before centralized monitoring flags a site for additional review or on-site visit. Supports Electronic Data Capture (EDC) quality oversight.']
  rbm_strategy varchar [note: 'Overarching Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy classification applied to the study. Determines the balance between on-site, remote, and centralized monitoring activities based on study risk profile per ICH E6(R2) and FDA/EMA guidance.']
  remote_monitoring_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether remote (off-site) monitoring activities are permitted under this monitoring plan as a complement or alternative to on-site visits, per the approved RBM strategy.']
  sdv_percentage_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of source data records or data points that must be verified per site visit or monitoring cycle, as defined in the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).']
  sdv_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of Source Data Verification (SDV) to be performed across the study. 100% SDV requires verification of all data points; Risk-Based SDV targets high-risk data fields; Critical Data Only restricts SDV to protocol-defined critical data elements; No SDV applies to centralized monitoring-only studies.']
  site_initiation_visit_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Site Initiation Visit (SIV) is mandated for all sites under this monitoring plan prior to first patient enrollment.']
  site_risk_tier_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used to assign risk tiers (e.g., High, Medium, Low) to investigational sites. Determines how site-level risk scores are calculated to drive adaptive monitoring frequency and intensity.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the monitoring plan (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 2.1). Incremented with each approved amendment to support audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  visit_report_submission_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days after a monitoring visit within which the CRA must submit the completed monitoring visit report to the sponsor or CRO. Typically 5–10 business days per GCP standards.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining the risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy for a clinical study. Specifies monitoring approach (on-site, remote, centralized), SDV scope and percentage thresholds, visit frequency rules, critical data/process definitions per ICH E6(R2), KRI tolerance limits, site risk tiers, and trigger-based escalation criteria. One plan per study with versioned amendments. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}
