  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: GCP requires CRAs to verify blinding integrity during on-site visits. Linking monitoring_visit to blinding_config enables visit reports to document which blinding configuration was in effect and wheth']
  edc_study_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: During monitoring visits, CRAs verify the correct EDC build version is deployed at the site — a GCP requirement. Traceability from visit to active EDC build is essential for audit trails and regulator']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Monitoring visits are scoped to study epochs (treatment vs. follow-up). SDV scope, visit frequency, and protocol deviation assessment differ by epoch. CRAs and CTMS systems schedule visits by epoch to']
  monitoring_cra_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.cra_assignment. Business justification: A monitoring visit is conducted by a CRA who is formally assigned to the site via a cra_assignment record. The monitoring_visit has employee_id → workforce.employee, but lacks a direct FK to the cra_a']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_plan. Business justification: Every monitoring visit is conducted under the authority of a monitoring_plan. The monitoring_plan defines visit types, frequencies, SDV scope, and objectives that govern each monitoring_visit. This is']
  monitoring_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit_schedule. Business justification: A monitoring visit is the execution of a planned monitoring visit schedule entry. The monitoring_visit_schedule defines the planned visit (planned date, visit type, SDV scope, objectives), while monit']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: During monitoring visits, CRAs verify that the active randomization list version at the site matches the approved IRT/RTSM list. Linking monitoring_visit to rand_list enables audit trail documentation']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Regulatory GCP inspections directly review monitoring visit records. Linking monitoring visits to the inspection under which they were reviewed enables inspection readiness packages and supports spons']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: During every monitoring visit, the CRA reviews the investigator site file (ISF). Linking monitoring_visit to site_file enables tracking which site file version was current at visit time — critical for']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) for which this monitoring visit is conducted. Links the visit to the parent study entity.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Monitoring visits must document the protocol amendment in effect at time of visit — required for GCP compliance and FDA/EMA regulatory inspection readiness. Amendment-driven SDV scope and visit object']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site being monitored. Identifies the physical or virtual location where the monitoring visit takes place.']
  action_items_count varchar [note: 'Total number of action items raised for the site as a result of this monitoring visit. Supports action item tracking, escalation management, and site accountability.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the monitoring visit concluded. Used to calculate actual visit duration and compare against expected duration benchmarks.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the monitoring visit commenced. Compared against scheduled_start_date to measure visit adherence and scheduling compliance.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan is required as a result of findings identified during this monitoring visit. Triggers CAPA workflow in MasterControl.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the monitoring visit record was first created in the CTMS. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP data integrity standards.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical findings (e.g., GCP violations, patient safety concerns, data integrity issues) identified during the monitoring visit. Triggers mandatory escalation and CAPA workflows.']
  duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual duration of the monitoring visit expressed in days (may be fractional for partial-day visits). Supports resource planning, CRA productivity metrics, and visit efficiency analysis.']
  ecrf_pages_reviewed varchar [note: 'Total number of Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) pages reviewed during the monitoring visit. Supports workload measurement and data quality tracking.']
  icf_compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Informed Consent Form (ICF) compliance was reviewed and verified for enrolled subjects during this monitoring visit. Covers version currency, signature completeness, and re-consent requirements.']
  ip_accountability_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Investigational Product (IP) accountability (receipt, dispensing, return, destruction records) was verified during this monitoring visit, as required by GCP.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major findings (significant GCP deviations not rising to critical level) identified during the monitoring visit. Supports risk stratification and CAPA prioritization.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor findings (low-risk observations or administrative issues) identified during the monitoring visit. Supports trend analysis and site quality improvement.']
  next_visit_planned_date date [note: 'Tentative planned date for the next monitoring visit to this site, as agreed during this visit or determined by the monitoring plan. Supports CRA scheduling and site management.']
  next_visit_type varchar [note: 'Type of the next monitoring visit recommended or planned for this site as determined during this visit. Supports forward scheduling and monitoring plan management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative notes captured by the CRA during or after the monitoring visit, summarizing key observations, site interactions, and follow-up actions not captured in structured fields.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary objectives and scope of the monitoring visit (e.g., ICF review, SDV of safety data, protocol deviation follow-up, IP accountability). Embedded from visit type classification.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall qualitative outcome assessment of the monitoring visit as determined by the CRA and/or monitoring management. Drives escalation decisions and CAPA initiation.']
  pi_meeting_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal meeting with the Principal Investigator (PI) was conducted during this monitoring visit. Required for certain visit types per GCP and sponsor SOPs.']
  protocol_deviations_identified varchar [note: 'Number of protocol deviations identified during this monitoring visit. Drives deviation management workflows, CAPA initiation, and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  queries_closed varchar [note: 'Number of previously open data queries resolved and closed during this monitoring visit. Supports query resolution tracking and site data quality metrics.']
  queries_opened varchar [note: 'Number of data queries raised against the site during or as a result of this monitoring visit. Tracks data quality issues identified and drives query management workflows.']
  report_due_date date [note: 'The contractually or SOP-defined deadline by which the CRA must submit the monitoring visit report following visit completion. Typically within 10 business days per GCP guidelines.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the monitoring visit report in its authoring and approval workflow. Tracks progression from draft through TMF filing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not-started|draft|submitted|reviewed|approved|filed — promote to reference product]']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the CRA submitted the monitoring visit report. Compared against report_due_date to measure report timeliness compliance.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Categorical risk classification of the investigational site at the time of this monitoring visit, derived from the RBM risk score. Determines monitoring strategy and visit frequency.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score assigned to the site at the time of this monitoring visit, derived from the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) risk assessment framework. Drives monitoring frequency and SDV intensity decisions.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the monitoring visit is scheduled to conclude. Used to calculate expected visit duration and resource allocation.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'The planned calendar date on which the monitoring visit is scheduled to begin, as agreed with the site. Used for CRA travel planning, site notification, and visit scheduling KPIs.']
  sdv_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of subject data points or CRF pages subjected to Source Data Verification (SDV) during this visit. Key metric for Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy compliance and data quality assurance.']
  sdv_subjects_reviewed varchar [note: 'Number of enrolled subjects whose source data was reviewed and verified during this monitoring visit. Supports SDV coverage tracking and RBM metrics.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor has been formally notified of critical or major findings identified during this monitoring visit, as required by GCP and sponsor SOPs.']
  subjects_enrolled_at_visit varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects enrolled at the investigational site at the time of this monitoring visit. Provides enrollment context for SDV coverage calculations and site performance assessment.']
  tmf_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the monitoring visit report and associated documents filed in the Electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) in Veeva Vault. Enables cross-system traceability between CTMS and eTMF.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the monitoring visit record was last modified in the CTMS. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  visit_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the monitoring visit was conducted on-site at the investigational site, remotely (e.g., via EDC/eTMF access), or as a hybrid combination. Supports Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy tracking.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the monitoring visit within the CTMS (e.g., MV-2024-0042). Used for cross-system referencing, TMF filing, and regulatory correspondence.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the monitoring visit within the CTMS workflow. Drives operational dashboards, CRA workload planning, and TMF completeness tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|scheduled|in-progress|completed|cancelled|rescheduled — promote to reference product]']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the monitoring visit by its purpose in the study lifecycle. SIV = Site Initiation Visit; IMV = Interim Monitoring Visit; COV = Close-Out Visit; RMV = Remote Monitoring Visit; FUV = Follow-Up Visit. Drives standard objectives, expected duration, and required TMF documents.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each CRA monitoring visit to an investigational site. Captures visit type (SIV, IMV, COV, RMV, FUV), visit mode (on-site, remote), scheduled/actual dates, assigned CRA, status, and outcome. Visit type classification attributes (standard objectives, expected duration, required TMF documents) are embedded. Primary operational entity in Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

Table monitoring.visit_report {
  visit_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the monitoring visit report record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit. Business justification: A visit_report is the formal document authored by a CRA following a monitoring_visit. Every visit_report belongs to exactly one monitoring_visit. This is a fundamental 1:N parent-child relationship — ']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Visit reports reference the protocol version for GCP compliance documentation and TMF artifact traceability. Regulatory inspections (FDA 21 CFR Part 312, ICH E6 R2) require protocol-version-specific v']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Visit reports frequently trigger quality events when data integrity issues, process failures, or systemic SDV discrepancies are identified. QA teams require the visit report → quality event link to tr']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Visit reports are primary GCP inspection evidence. Inspectors request visit reports by site and date range. Linking visit_report to regulatory_inspection enables direct retrieval of reports within ins']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Visit reports document the state of the site file reviewed during the visit (regulatory_documents_reviewed, icf_review_conducted attributes). A direct FK to site_file provides GCP-required traceabilit']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this monitoring visit report was generated. Links the report to the parent protocol and sponsor.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Visit reports must document which protocol amendment was active at time of visit — critical for regulatory inspections. Amendment-driven reconsent verification and SDV scope changes are tracked per vi']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the monitoring visit was conducted. Enables site-level performance tracking and regulatory oversight.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF when this monitoring visit report is filed as an essential TMF document under Zone 11 of the TMF Reference Model. Enables cross-system traceability between the data lakehouse and the eTMF.']
  action_items_outstanding_count varchar [note: 'Number of action items from previous monitoring visit reports that remain unresolved at the time of this visit. Key indicator of site compliance and follow-up effectiveness.']
  action_items_raised_count varchar [note: 'Total number of action items raised in this monitoring visit report requiring follow-up by the site or sponsor. Drives escalation workflows in the CTMS.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the CAPA record initiated in MasterControl as a result of findings from this monitoring visit. Enables cross-system traceability between the visit report and the CAPA management system.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan is required as a result of findings identified during this monitoring visit. Triggers CAPA workflow initiation in MasterControl.']
  cra_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the CRA\'s electronic signature on the monitoring visit report, confirming authorship and accuracy of the report content. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliant electronic records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this visit report record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) and audit trail requirements.']
  icf_review_conducted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CRA reviewed Informed Consent Form (ICF) processes and documentation for enrolled subjects during this monitoring visit. Critical GCP compliance check.']
  ip_accountability_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CRA reviewed investigational product (IP) accountability records, including dispensing logs, return records, and storage conditions, during this monitoring visit.']
  ip_storage_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether investigational product (IP) storage conditions (temperature, security, labeling) were found to be compliant with protocol and regulatory requirements during this visit.']
  lab_samples_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CRA reviewed laboratory sample collection, processing, storage, and shipment records during this monitoring visit.']
  monitoring_approach varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the monitoring visit was conducted on-site at the investigational site, remotely (e.g., via EDC/eTMF access), or as a hybrid combination. Supports Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy reporting.']
  next_visit_recommended_date date [note: 'CRA\'s recommended date for the next monitoring visit based on site risk assessment, enrollment activity, and outstanding issues identified during this visit. Supports monitoring schedule planning in the CTMS.']
  open_queries_count varchar [note: 'Number of data queries outstanding (open) in the EDC system at the time of the monitoring visit. Reflects data quality status and site responsiveness.']
  protocol_deviation_severity varchar [note: 'Highest severity level of protocol deviations identified during this monitoring visit. Determines escalation requirements and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  protocol_deviations_identified varchar [note: 'Number of protocol deviations identified at the site during this monitoring visit. Drives CAPA initiation and regulatory reporting obligations.']
  queries_raised_count varchar [note: 'Number of new data queries raised by the CRA during this monitoring visit. Supports EDC query management tracking and site data quality metrics.']
  queries_resolved_count varchar [note: 'Number of previously open data queries resolved during or as a result of this monitoring visit. Tracks site responsiveness and data cleaning progress.']
  regulatory_documents_reviewed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CRA reviewed the site\'s regulatory binder (including IRB/IEC approvals, investigator CVs, and delegation logs) during this monitoring visit.']
  report_approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA supervisor or Clinical Trial Manager formally approved the monitoring visit report. Marks the report as finalized for eTMF filing.']
  report_authored_date date [note: 'The date on which the CRA completed authoring the monitoring visit report. Regulatory guidelines require reports to be issued within a defined number of days post-visit; this field enables compliance tracking.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the monitoring visit report within the authoring and approval workflow. Drives eTMF filing readiness and regulatory completeness checks.']
  sdv_coverage_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of eligible data points or subjects for which SDV was completed during this visit. Reflects the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) strategy applied and supports regulatory audit readiness.']
  sdv_discrepancies_found varchar [note: 'Number of discrepancies identified between source documents and electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) data entries during SDV activities. Key quality metric for data integrity assessment.']
  sdv_source_documents_reviewed varchar [note: 'Number of source documents reviewed during Source Data Verification (SDV) activities conducted during this monitoring visit. Supports SDV coverage reporting and risk-based monitoring metrics.']
  site_overall_rating varchar [note: 'CRA\'s overall qualitative rating of the site\'s performance and GCP compliance as assessed during this monitoring visit. Used for risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy adjustments and escalation decisions.']
  subjects_active_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects currently active (on-study) at the site at the time of the monitoring visit. Supports site workload assessment and monitoring frequency planning.']
  subjects_completed_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who have completed the study at the site as of the visit date. Used for site performance reporting and study progress tracking.']
  subjects_discontinued_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who have discontinued from the study at the site as of the visit date. Supports dropout rate analysis and protocol deviation investigation.']
  subjects_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects enrolled at the site as of the visit date. Captured in the visit report to track enrollment progress against site targets.']
  subjects_screened_count varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects screened at the site as of the visit date. Used to calculate screen failure rates and assess site recruitment performance.']
  supervisor_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the CRA supervisor\'s electronic signature on the monitoring visit report, confirming review and approval. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliant electronic records and eTMF filing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this visit report record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The date on which the monitoring visit was conducted at the investigational site or remotely. For multi-day visits, this is the first day of the visit. Principal real-world event date for this transaction.']
  visit_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the monitoring visit concluded. Populated for multi-day visits to capture total visit duration. Used for resource planning and visit effort reporting.']
  visit_report_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable identifier for the monitoring visit report as assigned by the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS) or eTMF. Used for cross-system reference and regulatory traceability (e.g., MVR-2024-0042).']
  visit_summary_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative summary authored by the CRA describing the overall conduct of the monitoring visit, key observations, site status, and general impressions. Core content of the monitoring visit report per GCP requirements.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the monitoring visit that generated this report. Determines the scope of activities performed and regulatory expectations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Site Initiation Visit|Interim Monitoring Visit|Remote Monitoring Visit|Close-Out Visit|For Cause Visit|Co-Monitoring Visit — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Formal monitoring visit report authored by the CRA following each on-site or remote monitoring visit. Captures visit summary narrative, findings, SDV results, protocol deviations identified, outstanding issues, action items raised, and CRA/supervisor sign-off. Stored in Veeva Vault eTMF as an essential TMF document (reference: TMF Reference Model Zone 11).'
}

Table monitoring.sdv_record {
  sdv_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each Source Data Verification (SDV) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Source data verification of AE records is a critical monitoring activity. SDV records for AE fields must reference the specific adverse_event being verified to support data integrity reporting, SDV co']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: SDV scope and strategy differ by arm in multi-arm and dose-escalation trials (e.g., higher SDV % for experimental arm). Arm-level SDV metrics are required for adaptive trial oversight and DSMB safety ']
  bioanalytical_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.bioanalytical_result. Business justification: SDV of bioanalytical/PK results is a distinct regulatory requirement from safety lab SDV. CRAs verify bioanalytical results against source chromatograms and run records. sdv_record has lab_result_id f']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record in MasterControl initiated as a result of a systemic SDV discrepancy pattern at the site. Supports quality management and regulatory compliance.']
  discrepancy_query_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the data query raised in Medidata Rave EDC as a result of the discrepancy identified during SDV. Populated when sdv_outcome is \'discrepancy_found\' and a query has been issued.']
  ecrf_field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: SDV verifies specific eCRF field values against source documents. ICH E6(R2) requires field-level SDV traceability. Linking sdv_record to ecrf_field enables SDV coverage reporting by field, supports a']
  ecrf_form_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific eCRF form (e.g., Medidata Rave form) containing the data point that was verified during SDV.']
  field_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.field_entry. Business justification: SDV is the process of verifying a specific EDC field_entry value against the source document. Linking sdv_record to field_entry enables direct traceability from SDV outcome to the exact data record ve']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: ICH E6 GCP SDV process: CRAs verify lab result values in the eCRF against the lab-reported source document. sdv_record captures field-level verification; linking to the specific lab_result being verif']
  monitoring_action_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an action item or follow-up task raised in Oracle Siebel CTMS as a result of this SDV finding. Supports escalation workflows and site management accountability.']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit. Business justification: An SDV record captures source data verification performed during a specific monitoring visit. While sdv_record already links to visit_report (which links to monitoring_visit), a direct FK to monitorin']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a protocol deviation record if the SDV discrepancy resulted in a protocol deviation being raised. Links SDV findings to the protocol deviation tracking workflow in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: SDV discrepancies with data integrity impact are a primary source of quality events in clinical trials. Linking sdv_record to quality_event enables QA teams to trace data integrity quality events back']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: FDA/EMA inspectors specifically review SDV records to verify data integrity and source document verification coverage. Inspection readiness requires direct traceability from SDV records to the inspect']
  specimen_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.specimen_collection. Business justification: ICH E6 GCP SDV process: CRAs verify specimen collection records (collection time, fasting status, collection method) against eCRF entries. specimen_collection is a primary source document reviewed dur']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this SDV record was generated. Links to the study master entity.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the source data and eCRF data point reside. Supports site-level SDV performance tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: SDV records document verification of data at protocol-defined visits. visit_name is a denormalized reference to study_visit_schedule. Linking enables SDV coverage reporting by visit type and protocol ']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: CRAs performing SDV on randomization-related eCRF fields (randomization number, treatment arm) must cross-reference the subject\'s actual randomization record. GCP/ICH E6 requires SDV traceability to s']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: SDV records are performed at the subject visit level. Linking sdv_record to subject_visit_data enables reconciliation of SDV completion status at the visit level, supports SDV coverage dashboards, and']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled study subject whose eCRF data point is being verified against source documents.']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.version. Business justification: SDV must verify data against the correct document version (e.g., the specific ICF version under which a subject was consented, the protocol version in effect at data collection). GCP requires version-']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: SDV is performed at the procedure/field level. Each SDV record verifies a specific eCRF field mapping to a protocol-defined visit procedure. This enables SDV coverage reporting by procedure type and c']
  visit_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.visit_report. Business justification: SDV records are captured during monitoring visits and their results are summarized in the visit_report. The existing monitoring_visit_report_ref (STRING) is a text reference to the visit report — this']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SDV record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ Contemporaneous principle.']
  discrepancy_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the discrepancy type when sdv_outcome is \'discrepancy_found\'. Supports root cause analysis, CAPA workflows, and site quality trending. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transcription_error|missing_data|date_error|unit_error|coding_error|other — promote to reference product]']
  discrepancy_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the discrepancy identified between the eCRF field value and the source document value. Populated only when sdv_outcome is \'discrepancy_found\'. Supports query generation and protocol deviation assessment.']
  ecrf_page_number varchar [note: 'The page or section reference within the eCRF form where the verified data point is located. Supports precise audit trail navigation and source document traceability in Medidata Rave.']
  is_critical_data_point boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this eCRF field is designated as a critical data point per the study\'s Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) plan or Data Management Plan (DMP). Critical data points are prioritized for SDV under ICH E6(R2) Addendum.']
  is_sdv_waived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SDV was formally waived for this data point per the approved RBM plan or protocol amendment. A waiver must be documented and justified in the monitoring plan. Supports RBM audit trail.']
  not_verifiable_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the data point could not be verified against source documents when sdv_outcome is \'not_verifiable\'. Supports site follow-up actions and regulatory audit readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: source_unavailable|illegible|missing_record|access_denied|other — promote to reference product]']
  rbm_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'The risk score assigned to this data point or subject visit by the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) algorithm at the time SDV was triggered. Supports RBM strategy documentation and audit trail for targeted SDV decisions.']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SDV discrepancy has potential regulatory impact (e.g., affects primary endpoint data, safety data, or regulatory submission data). Triggers escalation to the sponsor and regulatory team.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the discrepancy was formally resolved and the resolution status was updated to \'resolved\' or \'closed\'. Used for monitoring KPI calculations such as query resolution cycle time.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of how the discrepancy was resolved, including any eCRF corrections made, source document clarifications obtained, or justifications provided by the site. Supports audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current resolution status of the discrepancy identified during SDV: open (discrepancy raised, awaiting resolution), resolved (eCRF corrected or explanation accepted), closed (verified resolved and signed off), or cancelled (discrepancy withdrawn). Tracks lifecycle of SDV findings.']
  sdv_coverage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of eligible data points for this subject visit that have been verified as part of this SDV activity. Supports monitoring KPI reporting and RBM coverage tracking at the visit level.']
  sdv_method varchar [note: 'The SDV sampling strategy applied to this data point: 100% SDV (all data points verified), targeted (specific critical fields), risk-based (driven by RBM algorithm), or triggered (initiated by a specific risk signal or query). Supports RBM strategy documentation.']
  sdv_outcome varchar [note: 'The result of the SDV activity for this specific data point: verified (eCRF matches source), discrepancy_found (mismatch identified), not_verifiable (source document unavailable or illegible), or pending (SDV not yet completed). Core status field for ALCOA+ compliance.']
  sdv_performed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the CRA performed the SDV activity for this specific data point. Principal business event timestamp. Supports ALCOA+ Contemporaneous principle and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  sdv_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this SDV record, used in monitoring visit reports, audit trails, and regulatory submissions. Generated by Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  sdv_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SDV was performed on-site at the investigational site, remotely via electronic access to source data, or as part of centralized statistical monitoring. Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy tracking.']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source document used for verification (e.g., medical record, lab report, ECG tracing). Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ Original principle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_record|lab_report|ecg_tracing|imaging_report|pharmacy_record|nursing_notes|other — promote to reference product]']
  source_document_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value as recorded in the original source document (e.g., medical record, lab report, nurse notes) against which the eCRF field value was verified. Supports ALCOA+ Attributable and Original principles.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this SDV record as assigned by the originating system (Oracle Siebel CTMS or Medidata Rave). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail between the Silver Layer and the operational system of record.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The protocol-assigned subject number (e.g., site-subject identifier such as 001-001) used to identify the subject in the context of the study without exposing personal identity. Classified as restricted due to potential re-identification risk under HIPAA and GDPR.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this SDV record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for waiving SDV on this data point, referencing the applicable RBM plan section or risk assessment. Populated only when is_sdv_waived is True.']

  Note: 'Source Data Verification (SDV) record capturing the verification of individual eCRF data points against source documents at the site. Tracks subject visit, eCRF field or data point verified, SDV outcome (verified, discrepancy found, not verifiable), discrepancy description, resolution status, and CRA performing SDV. Supports ALCOA+ compliance and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.'
}

Table monitoring.visit_deviation_finding {
  visit_deviation_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each protocol deviation finding record captured during monitoring or data review activities in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings for late or missing AE/SAE reporting must reference the specific adverse_event. Regulatory submissions (e.g., IND safety reports) require traceability from the deviation fi']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Deviations in dose-escalation or multi-arm studies are arm-specific (e.g., wrong dose level in arm B). Arm-level deviation tracking is required for DSMB safety reviews and interim analysis reports in ']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Repeat finding analysis requires cross-referencing monitoring deviation findings with audit findings. When the same issue appears in both monitoring and audit contexts, QA teams must link them to iden']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated CAPA workflow record in MasterControl when the deviation severity requires formal corrective and preventive action. Null if no CAPA is required.']
  compliance_protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: A visit deviation finding identified during CRA monitoring directly generates or maps to the formal protocol_deviation record in compliance. Regulatory reporting and CSR preparation require this trace']
  deviation_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.deviation_classification. Business justification: Deviation findings must be classified per the standardized deviation_classification taxonomy for SDTM DV domain mapping, regulatory reporting, and CSR inclusion decisions. The finding\'s deviation_cate']
  deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.subject_deviation. Business justification: Monitoring-identified deviation findings must be reconciled with subject-level deviation records for CAPA tracking and regulatory reporting (ICH E6 R2). Linking visit_deviation_finding to subject_devi']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: IE criteria violations are among the most serious protocol deviations tracked in visit_deviation_finding. Linking to eligibility_criterion enables structured CAPA tracking, IRB/IEC reportability deter']
  inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection_finding. Business justification: Regulatory inspections frequently reference prior monitoring deviation findings. Linking visit_deviation_finding to inspection_finding enables inspection readiness teams to demonstrate that monitoring']
  lab_kit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_kit. Business justification: Deviation findings are raised when incorrect or expired lab kits are used for specimen collection. The finding must reference the specific lab_kit record to document the non-compliant kit (lot number,']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Protocol deviation documentation for lab-related violations (e.g., subject continued on study despite lab value meeting stopping criteria, missed mandatory safety lab). Linking to the specific lab_res']
  monitoring_action_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.action_item. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings identified during monitoring visits typically generate action items for site remediation. visit_deviation_finding has capa_required (BOOLEAN) and capa_id but lacks a direct']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_visit. Business justification: A protocol deviation finding is identified during a specific monitoring visit. While visit_deviation_finding already links to visit_report (which links to monitoring_visit), a direct FK to monitoring_']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings must reference the specific protocol version deviated from. Regulatory reporting to IRB/IEC and FDA requires protocol-version-specific deviation records. visit_deviation_fi']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Regulatory inspectors review protocol deviation findings from monitoring visits. Inspection readiness reports require tracing deviation findings to inspection records. visit_finding already has this F']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Deviations are assessed against the amendment in effect at time of occurrence. Regulatory submissions and CAPA tracking require knowing which amendment version was active. Standard GCP documentation p']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the protocol deviation occurred. Enables site-level deviation trending and risk-based monitoring (RBM) analytics.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings involving randomization errors (wrong treatment arm, IRT failure, eligibility violation post-randomization) must be traceable to the specific subject_randomization record. ']
  subject_visit_data_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.subject_visit_data. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings identified during monitoring visits are tied to specific subject visit data records where the deviation occurred (e.g., missing assessments, out-of-window visits). This lin']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings must be documented and filed in the eTMF per ICH GCP. Inspectors require direct traceability from each deviation finding record to its supporting TMF-filed evidence documen']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (patient) affected by this protocol deviation. Nullable when the deviation is site-level or procedural rather than subject-specific.']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: Protocol deviations are frequently procedure-specific (wrong blood draw timing, missed assessment). Linking deviation findings to visit_procedure enables root cause analysis by procedure type and driv']
  visit_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.visit_report. Business justification: Protocol deviation findings identified during a monitoring visit are documented in the visit_report. visit_deviation_finding already links to monitoring_visit, but the direct link to the formal visit_']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) workflow must be initiated for this deviation. Typically True for major deviations and recurring minor deviations. Drives CAPA creation in MasterControl.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the deviation was identified and approved prospectively (before the deviation occurred, as a planned exception) or retrospectively (after the deviation occurred). Prospective deviations may be pre-approved by the sponsor or IRB/IEC; retrospective deviations require post-hoc review.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, clarifications, or contextual notes related to the deviation finding that do not fit into structured fields. Captured by the CRA or data manager in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the deviation finding record was first created in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  data_integrity_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol deviation compromised the integrity or reliability of the clinical data collected for the affected subject or visit. Impacts subject\'s inclusion in the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) or Per Protocol (PP) analysis populations.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the protocol deviation was identified. Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) effectiveness analysis and helps identify whether centralized monitoring or on-site monitoring is more effective at detecting specific deviation types.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the protocol deviation, including what occurred, which protocol section was deviated from, and the specific circumstances. Captured by the Clinical Research Associate (CRA) in Oracle Siebel CTMS during monitoring visit documentation.']
  deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the deviation finding record within the monitoring workflow. Drives CAPA initiation, sponsor notification, and regulatory reporting workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  identification_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the protocol deviation was first identified, either during an on-site monitoring visit, remote monitoring review, or data management query. Used to calculate detection lag and reporting deadline compliance.']
  identified_by varchar [note: 'Name or role identifier of the person who identified the protocol deviation (e.g., CRA name, data manager, site staff). Supports accountability tracking and ALCOA+ attributability requirements.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Narrative assessment of the actual or potential impact of the deviation on subject safety, data integrity, and study validity. Informs the severity classification and determines whether regulatory reporting is required.']
  irb_iec_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the protocol deviation was formally reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). Null if irb_iec_reportable is False or reporting is pending.']
  irb_iec_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) per applicable regulations and the IRB/IEC-approved protocol. Drives automated notification workflows.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the deviation finding record. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) electronic records requirements.']
  occurrence_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the protocol deviation actually occurred at the site. This is the real-world event date, distinct from the identification date. Critical for regulatory reporting timelines and IRB/IEC notification windows.']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'The specific section number or clause of the study protocol that was deviated from (e.g., \'Section 6.3.2 - Visit Window\'). Enables protocol-level deviation frequency analysis and supports regulatory submission documentation.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation represents a recurrence of a previously identified deviation of the same category at this site. Recurring deviations trigger escalated CAPA requirements and may indicate systemic site quality issues.']
  regulatory_authority_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA) as an important protocol deviation (IPD) or substantial protocol amendment trigger. Applies primarily to major deviations affecting subject safety.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the protocol deviation was formally resolved and closed in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Null if the deviation is still open or under review. Used to calculate deviation resolution cycle time KPIs.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the actions taken to resolve the protocol deviation, including immediate corrective actions implemented at the site. Distinct from the formal CAPA plan, this captures the immediate remediation steps.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the identified root cause of the protocol deviation, as determined through investigation by the site or sponsor. Used to drive CAPA development and systemic issue identification across sites.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized categorical classification of the deviation root cause to enable cross-site and cross-study trending. Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) signal detection and CAPA effectiveness analysis.']
  sdtm_dv_category varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM DV domain category (DVCAT) for the deviation, used for regulatory submission dataset classification. Populated during CDISC data transformation in SAS Drug Development for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions.']
  sdtm_dv_term varchar [note: 'The standardized CDISC SDTM DV domain term (DVTERM) describing the deviation, used for regulatory submission datasets. Enables direct mapping to SDTM DV domain for NDA/BLA/MAA submissions processed through SAS Drug Development.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Regulatory severity classification of the protocol deviation. \'major\' deviations may affect subject safety or data integrity and typically require IRB/IEC and sponsor reporting. \'important_protocol_deviation\' (IPD) is the EMA/EU CTR classification for deviations requiring regulatory notification. \'minor\' deviations have no significant impact on subject safety or data integrity.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating operational system (Oracle Siebel CTMS) for this deviation finding. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements between the Silver Layer lakehouse and the system of record.']
  sponsor_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the protocol deviation was formally reported to the study sponsor. Null if sponsor_reportable is False or reporting is pending. Used to track compliance with sponsor notification timelines.']
  sponsor_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be formally reported to the study sponsor per the Clinical Trial Agreement (CTA) and sponsor SOPs. All major deviations and important protocol deviations (IPDs) are typically sponsor-reportable.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The site-assigned subject number (screening or randomization number) of the affected trial participant. This is the pseudonymized identifier used in clinical data systems per GDPR and HIPAA de-identification standards. Distinct from the internal subject_id surrogate key.']
  subject_safety_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol deviation had an actual or potential impact on the safety of the affected subject. A True value typically triggers mandatory IRB/IEC notification and may require sponsor safety review.']

  Note: 'Record of each protocol deviation identified at a site during monitoring or data review. Captures deviation category and severity (major, minor, important), description, root cause, affected subject, dates of occurrence and identification, IRB/IEC and sponsor reporting status, and classification attributes (prospective/retrospective). Linked to CAPA workflow when required. Now includes deviation classification taxonomy as embedded attributes.'
}

  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study subject (patient) associated with this finding, where the finding is subject-specific (e.g., missing ICF, unreported AE for a specific subject). Null for site-level or system-level findings not attributable to a specific subject.']
  validation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.validation_rule. Business justification: Visit findings are often raised because a specific validation rule was violated at the site. Linking finding to the triggering validation rule enables root cause analysis, repeat-finding detection, an']

Linking to visit_procedure enables procedure-level quality metrics and targeted CRA retraining — standard finding ']
  visit_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.visit_report. Business justification: Individual findings recorded by a CRA are documented within a visit_report. visit_finding already has visit_id → monitoring_visit, but the direct link to visit_report is missing. The existing visit_re']
  closed_date date [note: 'The date on which the finding was formally closed in the CTMS following CRA verification of the corrective action. Distinct from resolved_date as closure may require additional sponsor or QA sign-off after resolution.']
  cra_verification_comment varchar [note: 'Narrative comment entered by the CRA when verifying the adequacy of the site\'s corrective action. Documents the CRA\'s assessment of whether the resolution is satisfactory and the finding can be closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this finding record was first created in the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_integrity_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has a direct impact on the integrity of clinical trial data, including eCRF entries, source documents, or audit trails. True findings may affect the reliability of data submitted in regulatory dossiers (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA).']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the site is required to resolve the finding and provide evidence of corrective action. Determined based on finding severity and sponsor/CRO standard operating procedures (SOPs). Critical findings typically require resolution within 24-72 hours.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which this finding was escalated beyond the standard CRA-site resolution process. Null if escalation_level is \'none\'. Used to track escalation timeliness and sponsor oversight effectiveness.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation level of the finding indicating how far up the oversight hierarchy the finding has been escalated. none: no escalation required. cra_manager: escalated to CRA line manager or clinical operations lead. sponsor: escalated to sponsor medical monitor or clinical operations. regulatory_authority: escalated to or reported to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, etc.) or IRB/IEC.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the finding domain as observed by the CRA. Categories align with GCP monitoring focus areas: data_integrity (eCRF/EDC discrepancies), informed_consent (ICF process issues), ip_accountability (investigational product handling), safety_reporting (AE/SAE reporting gaps), regulatory_compliance (IRB/IEC, regulatory submissions), protocol_deviation (departure from approved protocol). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: data_integrity|informed_consent|ip_accountability|safety_reporting|regulatory_compliance|protocol_deviation — promote to reference product]']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the finding as documented by the CRA, including the specific observation, the GCP or protocol requirement that was not met, and the context in which the finding was identified. This text is included verbatim in the monitoring visit report.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible, human-readable unique reference number assigned to this finding within the Clinical Trial Management System (CTMS). Used in monitoring visit reports, sponsor communications, and regulatory correspondence.']
  finding_subcategory varchar [note: 'Granular sub-classification within the finding category providing more specific context (e.g., \'missing source document\', \'late SAE reporting\', \'temperature excursion\', \'unsigned ICF\'). Supports detailed root cause analysis and CAPA trending.']
  gcp_reference varchar [note: 'The specific GCP guideline, regulatory requirement, or protocol section that was not met, as cited by the CRA in the finding (e.g., \'ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9.1\', \'21 CFR 312.62(b)\', \'Protocol Section 8.3\'). Provides the regulatory basis for the finding.']
  identified_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the CRA identified and documented this finding during the monitoring visit or remote review. Represents the principal real-world event date for this observation.']
  monitoring_approach varchar [note: 'The monitoring methodology used when the finding was identified. on_site: CRA physically present at the investigational site. remote: CRA conducting review via electronic access to site data. centralized: statistical or data-driven review performed centrally without site visit. Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy analysis.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Description of the preventive measures implemented or planned to prevent recurrence of this finding. Captured as part of the CAPA workflow and documented in the monitoring visit report follow-up.']
  regulatory_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has potential regulatory impact requiring notification to or action by a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, etc.) or ethics committee (IRB/IEC). True findings may require expedited reporting or inclusion in regulatory submissions.']
  repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified finding at the same site for the same category. Repeat findings are a key risk indicator in risk-based monitoring (RBM) and may trigger escalation or CAPA initiation.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the finding in its resolution lifecycle. open: finding identified, awaiting site response. in_progress: site has acknowledged and is working on resolution. resolved: corrective action completed and verified by CRA. closed: finding formally closed after CRA confirmation. escalated: finding escalated to sponsor or regulatory authority due to non-resolution.']
  resolved_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the finding was resolved and the corrective action was verified as complete by the CRA. Null if the finding has not yet been resolved. Used to calculate resolution cycle time for KPI reporting.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'The identified root cause of the finding as determined through investigation by the site or CRA. Documented as part of the CAPA or corrective action process. Examples: \'inadequate staff training\', \'system configuration error\', \'process gap\', \'workload/resource constraint\'.']
  sdv_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required Source Data Verification (SDV) for this finding has been completed by the CRA. Applicable only when sdv_required is True.']
  sdv_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) is required as part of the corrective action for this finding. True when the finding involves data discrepancies that require verification against original source documents.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity classification of the finding indicating its potential impact on subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance. Critical: immediate risk to subject safety or data integrity requiring urgent action. Major: significant GCP deviation requiring prompt corrective action. Minor: low-risk observation requiring correction. Observation: informational note with no required action.']
  site_response varchar [note: 'Narrative response provided by the site (Principal Investigator or Clinical Research Coordinator) acknowledging the finding and describing the corrective actions taken or planned. Captured in the CTMS and included in the monitoring visit report.']
  site_response_date date [note: 'The date on which the site provided its formal response to the finding. Used to measure site responsiveness and compliance with response timelines defined in the monitoring plan.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The de-identified alphanumeric subject number (e.g., site-subject number format) associated with this finding, as used in the eCRF and monitoring visit report. Retained as a denormalized reference for reporting without requiring a join to the subject table.']
  subject_safety_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has a direct or potential impact on the safety or welfare of study subjects. True findings require immediate escalation per GCP requirements and may trigger safety reporting obligations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this finding record was most recently modified in the CTMS. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Type of monitoring visit during which this finding was identified. site_initiation: pre-enrollment site setup visit. interim_monitoring: routine on-site or remote monitoring visit. close_out: end-of-study visit. remote: centralized or remote monitoring review. for_cause: triggered visit due to identified risk or concern.']

  Note: 'Individual finding or observation recorded by a CRA during a monitoring visit or remote review. Captures finding category (data integrity, consent, IP accountability, safety reporting, regulatory compliance), severity, description, site response, resolution status, and due date. Findings are aggregated into the visit report and may trigger action items or CAPAs.'
}

  action_item_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-visible reference number for the action item as assigned by Oracle Siebel CTMS (e.g., AI-STUDY001-00042). Used in monitoring visit reports, CAPA correspondence, and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether the action item represents a corrective action (addressing an existing finding), preventive action (mitigating future risk), observation (noted but not requiring immediate action), recommendation, or escalation.   closure_evidence_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the evidence provided to confirm resolution of the action item (e.g., updated SOP reference, re-training record, corrected eCRF entry, SDV confirmation). Required for GCP-compliant closure documentation.']
