  data_integrity_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding underlying this action item compromises the integrity of clinical trial data (e.g., eCRF entries, source data, audit trail). When True, may require data query issuance in Medidata Rave EDC and notification to data management.']
  days_overdue varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the action item has been open past its due date (or extended due date if applicable). Populated by Oracle Siebel CTMS automated workflow; zero for on-time or not-yet-due items. Used for site performance KPI reporting and regulatory inspection readiness dashboards.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the responsible party must complete and close the action item. Used by Oracle Siebel CTMS automated escalation workflows to flag overdue items and notify CRA supervisors and sponsor representatives.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the action item was formally escalated. Null if is_escalated is False. Used to measure escalation response time and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why the action item was escalated, including the triggering condition (e.g., overdue beyond threshold, critical safety implication, repeated non-compliance). Required when is_escalated is True.']
  extended_due_date date [note: 'Revised due date granted after a formal extension request and approval. Null if no extension has been granted. Tracks due date changes for audit trail and SLA compliance reporting.']
  gcp_reference varchar [note: 'Specific GCP guideline section, regulatory citation, or SOP reference that the finding violates or that governs the required corrective action (e.g., ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9.0, FDA 21 CFR 312.62). Supports regulatory inspection documentation.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action item has been formally escalated to a higher authority (e.g., sponsor medical monitor, regulatory affairs, DSMB) due to non-resolution, critical priority, or subject safety risk. Drives escalation notification workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this action item record in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  monitoring_action_item_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the action item by the type of finding or compliance gap it addresses. Drives routing logic, escalation thresholds, and regulatory reporting groupings in Oracle Siebel CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protocol_deviation|sdv_finding|regulatory_compliance|safety_reporting|data_quality|consent|supply_chain|training — promote to reference product]']
  monitoring_action_item_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the monitoring finding, protocol deviation, or compliance gap that necessitated this action item. Includes observed facts, regulatory reference, and expected corrective action per GCP documentation standards (ALCOA+).']
  monitoring_action_item_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the action item lifecycle in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Overdue status is set automatically when the due date passes without closure. Closed status requires documented evidence of resolution.']
  monitoring_visit_type varchar [note: 'Type of monitoring visit during which the action item was identified. Denormalized from the monitoring visit record for reporting efficiency. Supports analysis of finding patterns by visit type (e.g., higher deviation rates at initiation visits).']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the action item indicating urgency and potential impact on subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance. Critical items trigger immediate escalation workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  raised_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the action item was formally raised and entered into Oracle Siebel CTMS. Serves as the principal business event date for SLA calculation, aging analysis, and regulatory inspection timelines.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action item represents a recurring finding previously identified at the same site or study. Recurring findings may trigger escalation to a formal CAPA workflow and are tracked for site performance risk scoring.']
  regulatory_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential regulatory impact of the finding underlying this action item. Drives prioritization for regulatory submission readiness, inspection preparation, and escalation to regulatory affairs. Aligned with FDA and EMA inspection classification frameworks.']
  response_date date [note: 'Date on which the site formally submitted their response to the action item. Used to measure site responsiveness KPIs and to determine whether the response was received within the required timeframe.']
  response_text varchar [note: 'Formal written response provided by the site (PI or CRC) acknowledging the action item and describing the planned or completed corrective actions. Captured in Oracle Siebel CTMS as part of the action item resolution workflow.']
  responsible_party_role varchar [note: 'Functional role category of the party accountable for resolving the action item. Distinguishes between site staff (PI, CRC), CRA, sponsor, or functional team (data management, safety). Used for workload distribution and escalation routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: site_staff|cra|sponsor|pi|crc|data_management|safety — promote to reference product]']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating activity or process that generated this action item (e.g., on-site monitoring visit, remote monitoring review, SDV finding, protocol deviation, safety signal, audit). Supports risk-based monitoring (RBM) analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: on_site_visit|remote_monitoring|sdv_review|protocol_deviation|safety_signal|audit|sponsor_review — promote to reference product]']
  subject_safety_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the finding underlying this action item has a direct or potential impact on subject safety. When True, triggers expedited review by the medical monitor and may require safety reporting obligations per ICH E2A.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the action item finding or required corrective action. Used as the primary display label in CTMS dashboards, monitoring visit reports, and escalation notifications.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the CRA or monitor to verify that the corrective action was implemented and the action item can be closed (e.g., SDV, document review, on-site visit, remote review). Supports GCP audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Formal action item raised as a result of a monitoring finding, protocol deviation, or escalation trigger. Tracks action description, responsible party (site staff, CRA, or sponsor), due date, priority level, current status (open, in-progress, overdue, closed), escalation flag, and closure confirmation with evidence. Managed in Oracle Siebel CTMS with automated escalation workflows for overdue items. Primary operational entity for site performance management, regulatory inspection readiness, and GCP compliance tracking.'
}

Table monitoring.risk_indicator {
  risk_indicator_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the centralized monitoring risk indicator (Key Risk Indicator / KRI) record within the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) framework.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record initiated in response to this KRI breach, as tracked in MasterControl. Links the risk indicator to its remediation workflow.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: Risk indicators for primary endpoint data quality (missing assessments, outlier values) must link to the specific study endpoint. RBM frameworks explicitly tie KRIs to endpoints for data quality overs']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: KRIs are epoch-specific (treatment epoch dropout rate vs. follow-up epoch completion rate). Epoch-scoped risk indicators drive visit frequency adjustments in RBM — standard ICH E6 R2 compliant monitor']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Lab-facility-scoped KRIs (turnaround time compliance, result rejection rate, temperature excursion frequency) are core RBM metrics tracked per lab facility. risk_indicator has lab_panel_id but not lab']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) plans define KRIs per lab panel (e.g., safety panel completion rate, critical value rate by panel). Linking risk_indicator to lab_panel enables centralized monitoring dashb']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RBM monitoring plan document that defines this KRI, linking the indicator to its governing plan version and associated thresholds.']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: KRIs defined for randomization compliance (IRT error rate, allocation imbalance, unblinding frequency) are scheme-specific. Linking risk_indicator to rand_scheme enables RBM dashboards to filter and r']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Risk indicators (KRIs) are operationalized from compliance risk assessments — the risk assessment defines which indicators to monitor and their thresholds. Linking risk_indicator to risk_assessment en']
  signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.signal. Business justification: In pharmacovigilance-integrated RBM, a safety signal can define a new KRI or directly map to a risk indicator threshold. Risk indicators tracking AE/SAE rates must reference the originating signal to ']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: KRIs in RBM are defined per statistical endpoint (e.g., missing primary endpoint data rate, primary endpoint query rate). ICH E6(R2) and SAP-aligned monitoring plans require KRIs to be traceable to th']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this Key Risk Indicator (KRI) is defined. Links the KRI to its parent protocol and RBM monitoring plan.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site for which the KRI value is computed. A single KRI definition may produce one computed record per site, enabling site-level risk scoring within the RBM framework.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current risk alert status of the KRI for the site, derived by comparing the computed value against amber and red thresholds. Drives site risk scoring and monitoring intensity decisions in the RBM framework.']
  amber_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold value at which the KRI triggers an amber (warning) alert, indicating elevated risk requiring heightened monitoring attention. Defined in the RBM monitoring plan.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Formal description or formula specifying how the KRI value is computed (e.g., \'Number of screen failures / Number of subjects screened × 100\'). Ensures reproducibility and auditability of centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) outputs.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) must be initiated in response to the KRI breach. Drives workflow integration with MasterControl CAPA tracking.']
  computation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the KRI value was most recently computed for the site. Used to assess data currency and trigger recomputation schedules in the RBM monitoring plan.']
  computation_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the KRI is recomputed for each site (e.g., weekly for enrollment KRIs, monthly for data quality KRIs). Defined in the RBM monitoring plan.']
  computation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the KRI value was computed, including timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  computed_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current computed numeric value of the KRI for the associated site, derived from the source system data as of the last computation run. This is the principal quantitative fact of the risk indicator record.']
  consecutive_breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive computation cycles during which the KRI has remained at or above the amber or red threshold. Used to trigger escalation workflows and CAPA initiation per the RBM monitoring plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this KRI record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which the KRI input data is extracted for computation (e.g., Medidata Rave EDC for data quality KRIs, Oracle Argus Safety for safety KRIs, Oracle Siebel CTMS for enrollment KRIs).']
  denominator_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Raw denominator component of the KRI computation (e.g., total subjects screened, total expected data points, total SAEs reported). Stored alongside the numerator to enable independent verification of the computed value.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this KRI definition became active and applicable to the study and site. Supports versioning of KRI definitions when monitoring plan amendments are issued.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this KRI definition was superseded or retired. Null for currently active KRI definitions. Supports historical analysis and monitoring plan amendment tracking.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current KRI alert status and breach history require formal escalation to the sponsor, Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB), or senior management per the RBM escalation procedure.']
  indicator_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of what the KRI measures, its business rationale, and how it supports risk detection within the RBM framework.']
  indicator_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the KRI as defined in the RBM monitoring plan (e.g., \'Screen Failure Rate\', \'Missing Data Rate\', \'SAE Reporting Timeliness\'). Used in dashboards, monitoring visit reports, and sponsor communications.']
  indicator_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KRI definition within the monitoring plan. Active KRIs are computed and monitored; draft KRIs are under review; retired KRIs are no longer in use.']
  indicator_type varchar [note: 'Quantitative type of the KRI value, indicating whether the computed metric is a rate, absolute count, percentage, ratio, or duration in days. Governs how the value is interpreted against thresholds.']
  is_centralized_monitoring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this KRI is part of the Centralized Statistical Monitoring (CSM) program, as opposed to a site-level operational indicator. Supports differentiation of CSM KRIs from operational monitoring metrics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for CRA or data manager annotations regarding the KRI computation, alert status, or remediation context. Supports monitoring visit report narrative and sponsor communication.']
  numerator_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Raw numerator component of the KRI computation (e.g., number of screen failures, number of missing data points, number of overdue SAE reports). Stored to support audit and recomputation validation.']
  prior_computed_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'KRI computed value from the immediately preceding computation cycle for the same site. Enables trend analysis and detection of rapid deterioration or improvement in site risk profile.']
  red_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric threshold value at which the KRI triggers a red (critical) alert, indicating a significant risk requiring immediate escalation and corrective action per the RBM monitoring plan.']
  reference_period_end date [note: 'End date of the data window used to compute the KRI value. Together with reference_period_start, defines the exact temporal scope of the KRI computation for reproducibility and audit.']
  reference_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the data window used to compute the KRI value (e.g., rolling 30-day window start, or study start date for cumulative KRIs). Defines the temporal scope of the computation.']
  risk_indicator_category varchar [note: 'Business category classifying the domain of risk the KRI monitors. Supports stratified risk reporting across enrollment, data quality, safety, protocol compliance, and Investigational Product (IP) accountability domains.']
  risk_score_contribution decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted contribution of this KRI to the overall site risk score, expressed as a percentage (0–100). Reflects the KRI\'s relative importance in the composite site risk scoring model.']
  sdv_impact varchar [note: 'Defines the impact of this KRI\'s alert status on the Source Data Verification (SDV) strategy for the site. A red alert may trigger targeted or increased SDV; a green status may support reduced SDV per the risk-adaptive monitoring plan.']
  sponsor_defined boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this KRI was defined by the sponsor (True) or by the CRO as part of its standard RBM methodology (False). Supports differentiation of sponsor-mandated versus CRO-standard risk indicators.']
  threshold_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the KRI alert is triggered when the computed value exceeds (above) or falls below (below) the defined amber/red thresholds. Critical for correct alert logic in centralized statistical monitoring (CSM).']
  trend_direction varchar [note: 'Directional trend of the KRI value compared to the prior computation cycle. Supports proactive risk management by identifying sites where risk is worsening before threshold breach.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the KRI value and thresholds are expressed (e.g., \'%\', \'days\', \'count\', \'ratio\'). Ensures consistent interpretation of computed values across sites and studies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this KRI record, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the KRI definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.1), incremented when the calculation method, thresholds, or data source is amended. Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this KRI in the composite site risk scoring model, reflecting its relative importance compared to other KRIs in the monitoring plan. Used in weighted risk score calculations.']

  Note: 'Centralized monitoring risk indicator (also known as Key Risk Indicator or KRI) defined within the RBM monitoring plan. Captures indicator name, category (enrollment, data quality, safety, protocol compliance, IP accountability), calculation method, threshold values (amber/red), data source, and current computed value per site. Supports centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) and site risk scoring in the RBM framework.'
}

Table monitoring.site_risk_assessment {
  site_risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each periodic site risk assessment record within the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) framework.']

  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: In EU/EEA trials, site risk assessments must be contextualized against the CTA authorization status for that member state. Monitoring intensity and visit frequency decisions depend on whether the CTA ']
  dmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: Site risk assessments score data quality metrics (query rate, data entry lag, SDV completion) defined in the DMP. The DMP\'s critical data fields and data review plan are direct inputs to risk scoring ']
  enrollment_performance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.enrollment_performance. Business justification: RBM site risk assessments directly consume enrollment performance metrics (screen failure rate, dropout rate, enrollment rate). A FK from site_risk_assessment to enrollment_performance establishes dat']
  inspection_readiness_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_inspection_readiness. Business justification: Site risk assessments and inspection readiness assessments are co-dependent QA processes — risk tier directly informs inspection readiness status and vice versa. QA teams cross-reference both records ']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site entity being assessed within the RBM framework.']
  lab_accreditation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_accreditation. Business justification: Site risk assessments explicitly evaluate laboratory accreditation status as a risk factor. An expiring or lapsed lab accreditation elevates site risk tier. site_risk_assessment already has lab_facili']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.monitoring_plan. Business justification: A site_risk_assessment is conducted within the framework of a monitoring_plan. The monitoring_plan defines the RBM strategy, KRI tolerance limits, and risk tier methodology that govern how site risk a']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Site risk assessments evaluate compliance against a specific protocol version. Regulatory inspections require protocol-version-specific risk assessment records. site_risk_assessment has no protocol_id']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Post-inspection risk re-assessment is a standard GCP process. Regulatory inspection outcomes directly drive site risk tier changes and monitoring intensity recommendations. Risk management teams updat']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Site-level monitoring risk assessments are informed by or linked to the broader compliance risk_assessment for the study/site. RBM frameworks require that monitoring intensity decisions (risk_tier, re']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Site risk assessments evaluate document completeness as a key risk dimension. Linking site_risk_assessment to site_file allows direct traceability between the risk score and the site file reviewed — e']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) for which this site risk assessment is being conducted.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Risk assessments must be re-triggered after protocol amendments per ICH E6 R2. Linking to the amendment that triggered reassessment is standard RBM documentation — enables audit trail of risk tier cha']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational study site being assessed. Links this risk assessment to the specific site-study combination in the site domain.']
  tmf_completeness_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_completeness. Business justification: RBM risk assessments incorporate TMF completeness as a direct risk factor — missing or overdue TMF documents elevate site risk tier. Risk assessors reference the specific TMF completeness assessment w']
  ae_reporting_timeliness_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Adverse Events (AEs) reported within the protocol-defined and regulatory-required timeframes during the assessment period. A critical patient safety KRI.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk assessment was formally approved by the designated reviewer. Required for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status of this risk assessment. Must be approved by a qualified reviewer before the recommended monitoring intensity adjustments are enacted in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which this periodic site risk assessment was performed. Drives the scheduling of the next assessment cycle.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this risk assessment record as tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Follows the format SRA-{SiteCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}.']
  assessment_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the data observation window used to calculate Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) and site performance metrics for this assessment.']
  assessment_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the data observation window used to calculate Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) and site performance metrics for this assessment.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of this risk assessment record in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Drives approval routing and downstream monitoring intensity decisions.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the risk assessment trigger: initial (first assessment at site activation), periodic (scheduled interval), triggered (unscheduled due to a risk signal or protocol deviation), or close_out (final assessment at study end).']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan is required as a result of this risk assessment. Set to TRUE when critical KRI breaches or major protocol deviations are identified.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site risk assessment record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  critical_kri_breaches_count varchar [note: 'Number of KRI breaches classified as critical severity (i.e., exceeding the upper alert threshold). Critical breaches may trigger immediate unscheduled monitoring visits or escalation.']
  data_entry_lag_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days between the date of clinical observation and the date of entry into the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave) during the assessment period. A key KRI for data quality monitoring.']
  enrollment_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual subject enrollment as a percentage of the planned enrollment target for the assessment period. Significant under-enrollment is a KRI that may indicate site performance issues.']
  kri_breaches_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) that exceeded their defined threshold limits during the assessment period. A key input to the overall risk score calculation.']
  major_protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of major (significant) protocol deviations recorded at the site during the assessment period. Major deviations may affect subject safety or data integrity and are a high-weight KRI.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled due date for the next periodic site risk assessment, calculated based on the approved monitoring visit frequency and risk tier. Drives scheduling in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  outstanding_queries_count varchar [note: 'Number of open, unresolved data queries in the EDC system (Medidata Rave) at the end of the assessment period. Reflects site responsiveness and data management performance.']
  overall_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric risk score for the site calculated from weighted Key Risk Indicator (KRI) values. Higher scores indicate greater risk. Used to determine risk tier classification and adaptive monitoring intensity.']
  previous_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall risk score from the immediately preceding assessment cycle. Enables trend analysis and score delta calculation to detect improving or deteriorating site performance.']
  previous_risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier assigned during the immediately preceding assessment cycle. Used to identify tier escalations or de-escalations and trigger CAPA workflows when a site moves to a higher risk tier.']
  protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of protocol deviations recorded at the site during the assessment period. Includes both major and minor deviations as classified per the study deviation management plan.']
  query_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Electronic Case Report Form (eCRF) fields that generated data queries during the assessment period. High query rates indicate data quality issues and are a primary KRI in the RBM framework.']
  recommended_monitoring_intensity varchar [note: 'Assessor\'s recommendation for adjusting the monitoring visit frequency and SDV scope based on the current risk tier and KRI findings. Drives adaptive monitoring plan updates in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  recommended_sdv_scope_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended percentage of eCRF data fields to be subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) in the next monitoring cycle, adjusted based on the current risk tier.']
  recommended_visit_frequency varchar [note: 'Specific recommended visit frequency for the next monitoring cycle, derived from the risk tier and monitoring intensity recommendation. Used to update the site monitoring schedule in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  risk_assessment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative field capturing the assessor\'s qualitative observations, contextual factors, and rationale for the risk score and recommended monitoring adjustments not captured by structured KRI fields.']
  risk_management_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the study-level Risk Management Plan (RMP) under which this assessment was conducted. Ensures traceability of KRI thresholds and scoring methodology to the applicable plan version.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical risk tier assigned to the site based on the overall risk score thresholds defined in the Risk Management Plan. Directly drives monitoring visit frequency and Source Data Verification (SDV) scope in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  screen_failure_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of screened subjects who failed to meet eligibility criteria and were not enrolled during the assessment period. Elevated screen failure rates are a KRI for patient recruitment quality.']
  sdv_completion_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of Source Data Verification (SDV) tasks completed against the planned SDV scope for the assessment period. Reflects monitoring coverage and data integrity assurance.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from Oracle Siebel CTMS for this risk assessment record. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail back to the operational system of record.']
  subject_dropout_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of enrolled subjects who discontinued participation during the assessment period. High dropout rates are a KRI that may indicate site management or patient engagement issues.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this site risk assessment record. Required for change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Periodic risk assessment of an investigational site within the RBM framework. Captures overall site risk score, individual KRI values and threshold breaches, risk tier classification (low, medium, high), assessment date, assessor, recommended monitoring intensity adjustment, and approval status. Drives adaptive monitoring visit frequency and SDV scope decisions in Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

Table monitoring.monitoring_cra_assignment {
  monitoring_cra_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the CRA assignment record in the monitoring domain. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: MASTER_AGREEMENT — represents a long-running binding assignment relationship between a CRA and a study site.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.activation. Business justification: CRA monitoring assignments are created upon or after site activation; the activation record is the operational trigger. Linking monitoring_cra_assignment to activation allows CTMS to enforce that assi']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active CAPA record in MasterControl associated with this CRA-site assignment. Populated when capa_required is True. Supports quality management traceability.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: CRA assignments must be scoped to countries where a valid CTA exists. Monitoring activities cannot proceed without CTA authorization. Linking CRA assignments to the governing CTA ensures regulatory co']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the risk-based monitoring (RBM) plan governing this assignment. Determines visit frequency, SDV scope, and remote vs. on-site monitoring strategy applicable to this CRA-site pairing.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which the CRA is assigned monitoring responsibilities. Links the assignment to the study protocol and sponsor context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site for which the CRA holds monitoring responsibilities under this assignment. Drives site coverage and visit authorization in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'The actual business date on which the CRA was formally assigned to the site-study combination by the project manager or line manager. May differ from effective_start_date if there is a future-dated activation.']
  assignment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or qualifications relevant to this CRA-site assignment. May include site-specific monitoring considerations, language requirements, or sponsor-specific directives.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the CRA assignment record as generated by Oracle Siebel CTMS (e.g., ASSGN-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference, audit trails, and communication with sponsors and site teams.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CRA assignment. \'pending\' indicates awaiting activation; \'active\' is the operational state; \'on_hold\' reflects temporary suspension; \'transferred\' indicates handover to another CRA; \'completed\' reflects study close-out; \'cancelled\' indicates the assignment was voided.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CRA\'s role within this site-study assignment. \'primary\' indicates the main responsible CRA; \'backup\' covers absence; \'lead\' oversees a cluster of sites; \'co_monitor\' shares responsibilities; \'regional_lead\' manages a geographic territory. Drives visit authorization and workload planning in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) has been triggered for this CRA-site assignment due to identified quality issues, protocol deviations, or KRI breaches. When True, a CAPA record should exist in MasterControl.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the assigned site is located. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, travel planning, and regional reporting.']
  ctms_assignment_ref varchar [note: 'Native assignment record identifier from Oracle Siebel CTMS. Preserved for cross-system traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and the operational source system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the CRA\'s monitoring responsibilities for this site-study combination formally end. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Populated upon transfer, study close-out, or cancellation. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the CRA\'s monitoring responsibilities for this site-study combination formally begin. Used for visit authorization windows and workload planning. Aligns with MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent escalation was formally raised for this CRA-site assignment. Populated when escalation_status transitions to \'escalated\'. Used for SLA tracking and management reporting.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Current escalation state for this CRA-site assignment. \'none\' indicates no escalation; \'flagged\' indicates a potential issue identified; \'escalated\' indicates formal escalation to management or sponsor; \'resolved\' indicates the escalation has been closed.']
  gcp_training_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assigned CRA holds a current, valid Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training certification at the time of assignment. Regulatory requirement per ICH E6(R2) and FDA 21 CFR Part 312. Sourced from MasterControl training records.']
  last_visit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed monitoring visit conducted by this CRA at the assigned site under this study. Used to calculate visit overdue status and schedule the next visit.']
  monitoring_approach varchar [note: 'Primary monitoring methodology authorized for this CRA-site assignment under the risk-based monitoring (RBM) plan. \'on_site\' requires physical presence; \'remote\' uses EDC and electronic review; \'hybrid\' combines both; \'centralized\' is performed by a central team without site visits.']
  next_planned_visit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next monitoring visit by this CRA at the assigned site. Populated by Oracle Siebel CTMS visit scheduling logic based on visit frequency and last visit date. Supports proactive workload planning.']
  open_action_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently open action items assigned to the site or CRA under this assignment in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Supports escalation workflows and site management oversight.']
  open_protocol_deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently open protocol deviations identified by this CRA at the assigned site that have not yet been resolved or closed. Supports escalation decisions and CAPA tracking.']
  open_query_count varchar [note: 'Number of currently open data queries raised by this CRA against the assigned site\'s eCRF data in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Used for site performance monitoring and CRA workload tracking.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CRA assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and ALCOA+ compliance requirements for clinical trial data integrity.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CRA assignment record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and ALCOA+ compliance requirements for clinical trial data integrity.']
  remote_access_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRA has been granted remote access authorization to the site\'s EDC system (Medidata Rave) and source data systems for remote monitoring activities under this assignment.']
  sdv_scope_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of source data fields subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) for this CRA-site assignment as defined in the monitoring plan. Reflects the risk-based SDV strategy; lower values indicate targeted/risk-based SDV rather than 100% verification.']
  sdv_strategy varchar [note: 'Qualitative classification of the SDV strategy applied to this assignment. \'full\' requires 100% SDV; \'targeted\' focuses on specific data fields; \'risk_based\' applies adaptive SDV based on KRI signals; \'critical_data_only\' limits SDV to primary endpoints and safety data.']
  site_count_assigned varchar [note: 'Number of investigational sites assigned to this CRA under the current study at the time of this assignment record. Supports workload benchmarking and capacity management per CRA.']
  site_initiation_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CRA is authorized to conduct the Site Initiation Visit (SIV) for the assigned site under this study. Authorization is contingent on GCP training currency and protocol training completion.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor was formally notified of this CRA assignment or assignment change. Populated when sponsor_notified is True. Supports audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this CRA assignment or any change to the assignment (e.g., transfer, status change). Supports sponsor oversight and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational territory code assigned to the CRA for this study, as defined in Oracle Siebel CTMS territory management. Used for regional workload balancing, travel planning, and site coverage reporting.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the geographic or organizational territory associated with this CRA assignment (e.g., \'Northeast US\', \'Western Europe\'). Supports territory-based reporting and resource planning.']
  total_visits_completed varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of monitoring visits completed by this CRA at the assigned site under this study assignment. Supports workload reporting, performance evaluation, and site coverage tracking.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'Date on which the CRA assignment was formally transferred to another CRA. Populated when assignment_status transitions to \'transferred\'. Used for continuity tracking and audit purposes.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for transferring this CRA assignment to another CRA. Populated when assignment_status transitions to \'transferred\'. Supports audit trail and continuity documentation per GCP requirements.']
  visit_frequency_weeks varchar [note: 'Target interval in weeks between monitoring visits for this CRA-site assignment as specified in the monitoring plan. Used for visit scheduling, overdue visit detection, and compliance reporting.']
  workload_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the CRA\'s total monitoring workload capacity allocated to this specific site-study assignment (0.00–100.00). Used for capacity planning, resource utilization reporting, and ensuring no CRA is over-allocated across concurrent assignments.']

  Note: 'Assignment record linking a CRA to a specific site and study for monitoring responsibilities. Captures assignment dates, type (primary, backup, lead CRA), territory, workload allocation percentage, and status. Drives visit authorization, workload planning, and site coverage in Oracle Siebel CTMS.'
}

Table monitoring.monitoring_visit_schedule {
  monitoring_visit_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the planned monitoring visit schedule record within Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.activation. Business justification: Monitoring visit schedules are generated only after site activation is confirmed. Linking monitoring_visit_schedule to activation enables scheduling systems to validate activation status before genera']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: Monitoring visit schedules are epoch-scoped — more frequent visits during treatment epoch, reduced frequency during follow-up. Epoch-based scheduling drives visit frequency in RBM. monitoring_visit_sc']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: Visit schedules are triggered or adjusted based on interim analysis data cutoff dates — monitoring_visit_schedule.is_rbm_triggered and scheduling_basis directly reference interim analysis events. Doma']
  monitoring_cra_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.cra_assignment. Business justification: A visit_schedule is planned for a specific CRA-site-study assignment. The cra_assignment captures the CRA responsible for monitoring a site, and the visit_schedule defines when that CRA will visit. Li']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the monitoring plan from which this scheduled visit is derived, linking the schedule to its governing risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy.']
  rescheduled_from_monitoring_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the original visit schedule record from which this record was rescheduled. Enables audit trail of rescheduling history.']
  risk_indicator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the risk indicator (KRI) that triggered this visit when scheduling_basis is Risk-Based Trigger. Links the scheduled visit to the specific risk signal that necessitated it.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this monitoring visit is scheduled.']
  study_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_milestone. Business justification: RBM visit scheduling is explicitly triggered by milestone achievement (enrollment milestones drive monitoring frequency changes). milestone_reference is a denormalized plain-text reference to study_mi']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the monitoring visit is planned.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the planned monitoring visit was cancelled. Required when schedule_status is \'Cancelled\'. Supports audit and compliance review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this visit schedule record was first created in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Supports audit trail requirements per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  ctms_schedule_ref varchar [note: 'The native record identifier or reference number for this visit schedule as stored in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the source CTMS.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this version of the visit schedule record is considered active and valid. Used for slowly changing dimension (SCD) tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date until which this version of the visit schedule record is considered active. Null for the current active version. Used for SCD tracking in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  enrollment_trigger_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of subjects enrolled at the site that triggers this visit when scheduling_basis is Enrollment-Based (e.g., visit triggered after every 5 subjects enrolled).']
  frequency_interval_days varchar [note: 'The number of days between consecutive monitoring visits as defined in the monitoring plan. Drives calendar-based scheduling and CRA workload forecasting. Null for non-calendar-based visits.']
  is_overdue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the planned monitoring visit has passed its window_latest_date without being completed or rescheduled. Used for escalation tracking and site risk assessment.']
  is_rbm_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this visit was triggered by the Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM) system (e.g., KRI breach, centralized monitoring signal) rather than by a pre-set calendar schedule.']
  notification_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the site (Principal Investigator and Clinical Research Coordinator) must be notified of the upcoming monitoring visit, per the monitoring plan\'s advance notice requirements.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the visit notification was sent to the investigational site. Used to verify compliance with advance notice requirements in the monitoring plan.']
  planned_duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'The expected duration of the monitoring visit in days as planned in the monitoring schedule. Used for CRA capacity planning and resource allocation.']
  planned_visit_date date [note: 'The target calendar date on which the monitoring visit is planned to occur, representing the principal business event date for scheduling and CRA workload forecasting.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The priority assigned to this planned monitoring visit for CRA workload management and scheduling. Critical visits (e.g., for-cause, safety-triggered) take precedence over routine interim visits.']
  rescheduling_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the planned monitoring visit was rescheduled. Required when schedule_status is \'Rescheduled\'. Supports audit and compliance review.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this visit schedule record as assigned in Oracle Siebel CTMS (e.g., VS-2024-0042). Used for cross-system reference and audit trail.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the planned monitoring visit schedule record within Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  scheduling_basis varchar [note: 'The basis or rationale used to determine when this visit should occur: Calendar-Based (fixed interval), Risk-Based Trigger (driven by KRI/risk indicator breach), Milestone-Based (linked to study milestone such as first patient enrolled), Enrollment-Based (triggered by subject enrollment count), or Regulatory-Required (mandated by regulatory authority).']
  sdv_scope varchar [note: 'The Source Data Verification (SDV) scope category for this planned visit: 100% SDV (all data points), Risk-Based SDV (targeted by risk), Critical Data Only (primary endpoints and safety data), or No SDV (remote/centralized monitoring only).']
  sdv_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned percentage of source data to be verified during this monitoring visit as defined in the monitoring plan. Reflects the risk-based SDV strategy for this visit.']
  sponsor_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor approved this planned monitoring visit schedule. Populated only when sponsor_approved is True.']
  sponsor_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor has reviewed and approved this planned monitoring visit schedule, particularly relevant for risk-based or for-cause visits requiring sponsor authorization.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The username or system account identifier of the user who last modified this visit schedule record in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Required for audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this visit schedule record was most recently modified in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Supports audit trail requirements per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of this visit schedule record, incremented each time the schedule is modified (e.g., rescheduled, scope changed). Supports audit trail and change history per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  visit_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the planned monitoring visit will be conducted on-site at the investigational site, remotely (e.g., via EDC review), or as a hybrid combining both approaches.']
  visit_objectives varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the specific objectives and scope of work planned for this monitoring visit, as defined in the monitoring plan or visit notification (e.g., ICF review, IP accountability, protocol deviation follow-up).']
  visit_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number of this planned visit within the site\'s monitoring schedule for the study (e.g., 1 = first interim visit, 2 = second interim visit). Used for ordering and CRA workload planning.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the planned monitoring visit by its purpose within the study lifecycle: Site Initiation Visit (SIV), Interim Monitoring Visit, Close-Out Visit, Remote Monitoring Visit, For-Cause Visit, or Pre-Study Visit.']
  window_earliest_date date [note: 'The earliest acceptable date on which the monitoring visit may be conducted while still satisfying the monitoring plan window requirements. Used to define the permissible scheduling window.']
  window_latest_date date [note: 'The latest acceptable date on which the monitoring visit may be conducted while still satisfying the monitoring plan window requirements. Visits beyond this date are considered overdue.']
  window_tolerance_days varchar [note: 'The number of days of allowable deviation on either side of the planned visit date that defines the acceptable scheduling window (e.g., ±14 days). Used to compute window_earliest_date and window_latest_date.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or system account identifier of the user who created this visit schedule record in Oracle Siebel CTMS. Required for audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ (Attributable).']

  Note: 'Planned monitoring visit schedule for a site within a study, derived from the monitoring plan. Captures planned visit type, planned visit window (earliest and latest acceptable dates), visit frequency interval, basis for scheduling (risk-based trigger, calendar-based, milestone-based), and schedule status. Used in Oracle Siebel CTMS for visit planning and CRA workload forecasting.'
}

Table monitoring.visit_checklist {
  visit_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for visit_checklist']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record in MasterControl initiated as a result of a critical or major finding on this checklist item. Supports end-to-end quality management traceability.']

Business justification: Visit checklist items are executed per specific SOPs — CRAs must verify SOP compliance during monitoring visits.   monitoring_action_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the action item record created in Oracle Siebel CTMS as a result of a finding on this checklist item. Enables traceability from checklist finding to resolution.']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the monitoring visit during which this checklist was executed. Links the checklist to the parent visit record in the monitoring domain.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a protocol deviation record identified and linked to this checklist item finding. Enables traceability between monitoring checklist findings and formal protocol deviation management.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Visit checklists are protocol-version-specific — checklist items reference protocol sections for GCP compliance verification. Regulatory inspections require traceability from checklist to protocol ver']

Business justification: The visit checklist is the primary tool CRAs use to review the investigator site file during monitoring visits. Linking visit_checklist to site_file enables tracking which site file was reviewed again']

Business justification: Visit checklists include items to verify site staff training compliance against specific SOP training requirements. Linking visit_checklist to sop_training_requirement enables CRAs to verify that chec']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this monitoring checklist was completed. Enables study-level aggregation of checklist compliance.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the monitoring visit and checklist execution occurred.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Visit checklists are structured around protocol-defined visit schedules. CRAs use the visit schedule to verify all required procedures were performed and documented at each visit. This traceability is']

A FK to tmf_document enables direct trac']
