Business justification: Checklist items map to specific visit procedures (verify PK sample collected per procedure, confirm ECG performed). Linking checklists to visit_procedure enables procedure-level compliance tracking an']
  visit_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to monitoring.visit_report. Business justification: Monitoring checklists are completed during a visit and their results feed directly into the visit_report. visit_checklist already links to monitoring_visit, but the direct link to the formal visit_rep']
  action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up action item has been raised for this checklist item (True) due to a finding or non-compliance. Triggers action item creation workflow in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) is required for the finding identified on this checklist item. Critical and major findings typically mandate CAPA initiation per GCP and SOP requirements.']
  checklist_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the checklist record, typically generated by Oracle Siebel CTMS (e.g., CHK-2024-00123). Used for cross-system reference and audit trail.']
  checklist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the overall monitoring checklist record. Tracks progression from initial creation through CRA sign-off. \'void\' indicates the checklist was invalidated (e.g., visit cancelled).']
  checklist_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the checklist template used for this visit instance (e.g., \'v3.1\'). Versioned per SOP updates managed in MasterControl. Ensures the correct template version is applied per effective date.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Per-item completion status recorded by the CRA during the monitoring visit. \'failed\' indicates the item was assessed and found non-compliant, triggering a finding or action item.']
  cra_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment entered by the CRA for this checklist item, capturing observations, context, or follow-up notes that do not constitute a formal finding. Supports inspection readiness and knowledge transfer.']
  cra_signoff_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the CRA electronically signed off on this checklist item or the overall checklist, per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements. Captures the precise moment of attestation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this monitoring checklist record was first created in the source system. Supports audit trail, ALCOA+ compliance, and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the finding or non-compliance identified for this checklist item. Populated by the CRA when completion_status is \'failed\' or finding_severity is not \'none\'. Supports monitoring visit report generation.']
  finding_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of any finding identified during assessment of this checklist item. Aligns with GCP inspection-readiness severity tiers. \'none\' when item is compliant.']
  inspection_ready_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist record has been reviewed and confirmed as inspection-ready (True) for regulatory authority inspections (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Set during quality review cycles.']
  is_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist item is applicable for the specific visit instance (True) or has been marked not applicable (N/A) by the CRA with justification. Supports conditional checklist logic.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist item is mandatory (True) or optional (False) for the given visit type. Mandatory items must be completed for the checklist to be signed off. Drives compliance scoring.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the checklist item being assessed. Groups items for reporting and compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: regulatory_binder|icf_compliance|ip_accountability|ecrf_data_entry|safety_reporting|lab_samples|site_staff|tmf_documents|protocol_adherence|subject_eligibility — promote to reference product]']
  item_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the CRA completed the assessment of this specific checklist item. May differ from visit_date for multi-day visits or remote follow-up assessments. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the CRA must verify or perform for this checklist item, including acceptance criteria and reference to applicable SOP or regulatory requirement.']
  item_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the specific checklist item being assessed (e.g., \'Regulatory Binder Current\', \'ICF Version Verification\', \'IP Accountability Log Review\'). Sourced from the checklist template.']
  item_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this checklist item within its category, defining the display and execution order for the CRA during the monitoring visit. Sourced from the checklist template definition.']
  not_applicable_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text justification provided by the CRA when marking a checklist item as not applicable (is_applicable = False). Required for mandatory items marked N/A to maintain audit trail per ALCOA+ principles.']
  rbm_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this checklist item was triggered or prioritized by the risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategy (True), as opposed to being a standard routine item. Supports RBM effectiveness reporting.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or SOP reference that this checklist item is designed to verify compliance with (e.g., \'ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18\', \'21 CFR 312.62\', \'EU CTR 536/2014 Article 48\'). Supports inspection readiness.']
  sdv_performed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) was performed as part of this checklist item assessment (True). Relevant for eCRF and data entry checklist items under risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategies.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Original record identifier from Oracle Siebel CTMS or MasterControl from which this checklist record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  supervisor_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the CRA supervisor or line manager reviewed and approved the completed checklist. Supports quality oversight and two-level sign-off workflows per GCP requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this monitoring checklist record was last modified in the source system. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the monitoring visit and checklist execution occurred. Principal business event date for this checklist record. Used for compliance reporting and visit frequency analysis.']
  visit_mode varchar [note: 'Modality of the monitoring visit — on-site, remote, or hybrid. Affects which checklist items are applicable and how source data verification (SDV) is conducted under risk-based monitoring (RBM) strategies.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Type of monitoring visit for which this checklist was used. Determines which checklist items are applicable (e.g., site initiation visits include regulatory binder setup items not present in interim visits).']

  Note: 'Monitoring checklist template and completion record used by CRAs during visits. Defines checklist items (regulatory binder, ICF, IP accountability, eCRF, safety reporting), item categories, mandatory/optional flags, and captures per-item completion status and CRA sign-off. Versioned per SOP updates. Supports consistent monitoring execution and inspection readiness.'
}

Table monitoring.central_monitoring_alert {
  central_monitoring_alert_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the central monitoring alert record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete business event (alert generation) with a clear lifecycle from generation through disposition.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record opened in MasterControl as a result of this alert. Null when capa_required is False or CAPA has not yet been created.']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts are triggered by endpoint data anomalies (primary endpoint outliers, missing assessments). Linking alerts to the specific study endpoint enables endpoint-level data quality o']
  inspection_readiness_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.inspection_readiness. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts are triggered by inspection readiness gaps (missing documents, overdue TMF filings). Linking central_monitoring_alert to inspection_readiness enables direct traceability from']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts generated during an interim analysis window require documentation for DSMB reporting and firewall integrity. Alerts must reference the interim_analysis record to confirm unbl']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: RBM central monitoring alerts are directly triggered by anomalous lab results (critical values, out-of-range patterns). The alert must reference the specific lab_result that triggered it for investiga']
  monitoring_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the risk-based monitoring (RBM) plan under which this alert was generated, linking the alert to the governing monitoring strategy and threshold configuration.']
  monitoring_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the monitoring visit (on-site or remote) triggered or linked to this alert as a result of the escalation disposition. Null when no visit has been triggered. Links to the monitoring_visit product.']
  prior_alert_central_monitoring_alert_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the most recent prior central_monitoring_alert record for the same KRI and site, enabling trend analysis and repeat breach tracking. Null when is_repeat_alert is False.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the protocol deviation record raised as a result of this alert when the disposition is PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_RAISED. Null when no protocol deviation is associated.']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts (KRI breaches, data anomalies, statistical outliers) are a primary trigger for quality events in RBM programs. Linking central_monitoring_alert to quality_event enables QA te']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts triggered by randomization imbalances, IRT anomalies, or allocation ratio drift must reference the specific rand_scheme being monitored. This supports KRI computation for ran']
  risk_indicator_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Key Risk Indicator (KRI) or centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) signal that triggered this alert. Null when the alert is manually triggered without a specific KRI linkage.']
  safety_report_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.safety_report_submission. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts on SAE reporting timeliness KRI breaches or aggregate safety signals directly trigger expedited safety report submissions (IND safety reports, DSURs). Linking enables traceab']
  serious_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.serious_adverse_event. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts are triggered by SAE reporting delays, unexpected SAE rates, or documentation gaps. Centralized monitoring of SAE reporting compliance is a named KRI process in RBM; the aler']
  signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.safety_signal. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts triggered by safety signal detection (elevated AE rate for a MedDRA term) must reference the safety_signal driving the alert. Risk-based monitoring (RBM) frameworks explicitl']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: RBM frameworks (TransCelerate, ICH E6(R2)) generate central monitoring alerts specifically for endpoint-critical data quality issues (e.g., missing primary endpoint data rate). Linking alerts to the s']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this central monitoring alert was generated. Links the alert to the study protocol context.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the anomaly or Key Risk Indicator (KRI) threshold breach was detected. Core contextual dimension for risk-based monitoring (RBM) triage.']
  susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.susar. Business justification: SUSAR 7-day and 15-day reporting deadlines are critical KRIs monitored centrally. A central monitoring alert triggered by a SUSAR reporting deadline breach must reference the specific SUSAR record to ']
  tmf_completeness_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_completeness. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts are triggered by TMF completeness KRI breaches (e.g., missing document rate exceeding threshold). The alert must reference the specific completeness assessment that triggered']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to subject.trial_subject. Business justification: In Risk-Based Monitoring (RBM), central monitoring alerts are frequently triggered by subject-level anomalies (outlier lab values, missing data patterns, protocol deviations). Regulatory guidance on c']
  validation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.validation_rule. Business justification: Central monitoring alerts are triggered by systematic validation rule failure patterns across sites — a core RBM process. Traceability from alert back to the specific edit check rule that triggered it']
  alert_category varchar [note: 'Broad business domain category of the alert, used for routing, reporting, and escalation prioritization. DATA_QUALITY: data entry or consistency issues. SAFETY: adverse event or safety reporting concerns. ENROLLMENT: subject recruitment or screen failure anomalies. PROTOCOL_COMPLIANCE: protocol deviation patterns. REGULATORY: regulatory document or submission concerns. OPERATIONAL: site operational performance issues.']
  alert_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alert was generated by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system or manually created by a central monitor. This is the principal real-world business event time. BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.']
  alert_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the alert, formatted as CMA-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used in communications, reports, and audit trails to uniquely reference an alert across systems. Sourced from Oracle Siebel CTMS. BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  alert_severity varchar [note: 'Risk-stratified severity level of the alert, driving escalation urgency and response timelines. CRITICAL: immediate action required, potential patient safety or data integrity risk. HIGH: significant risk requiring prompt review within defined SLA. MEDIUM: moderate risk requiring scheduled review. LOW: informational, monitor for trend.']
  alert_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alert within the review and disposition workflow. OPEN: generated and awaiting assignment. UNDER_REVIEW: assigned reviewer is actively assessing. ESCALATED: elevated to on-site visit or senior oversight. CLOSED: reviewed and dispositioned. CANCELLED: determined to be erroneous or duplicate. LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  alert_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alert by the nature of the triggering condition. KRI_THRESHOLD_BREACH: a Key Risk Indicator exceeded its amber or red threshold. DATA_ANOMALY: a data pattern inconsistency detected by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system. STATISTICAL_OUTLIER: site-level metric is a statistical outlier relative to the study population. MANUAL: alert raised by a central monitor without an automated trigger. ENROLLMENT_SIGNAL: enrollment rate deviation. SAFETY_SIGNAL: potential safety concern flagged centrally. CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the alert was assigned to a reviewer for investigation. Used to measure assignment SLA compliance from alert generation to assignment.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) must be initiated as a result of this alert. Set to True when disposition is CAPA_INITIATED or when a confirmed critical finding requires systematic remediation.']
  consecutive_breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of consecutive computation cycles in which the triggering KRI has breached its threshold at this site prior to and including this alert. A higher count indicates a persistent risk pattern and informs escalation urgency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alert record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Distinct from alert_generated_timestamp which captures the business event time. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Action decision made following review of the alert. ESCALATE_ONSITE_VISIT: alert severity warrants an unscheduled or triggered on-site monitoring visit. REMOTE_FOLLOW_UP: issue to be addressed via remote monitoring or site communication. NO_ACTION_REQUIRED: alert reviewed and no further action needed. CAPA_INITIATED: a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) has been opened. PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_RAISED: a protocol deviation record has been created. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ESCALATE_ONSITE_VISIT|REMOTE_FOLLOW_UP|NO_ACTION_REQUIRED|CAPA_INITIATED|PROTOCOL_DEVIATION_RAISED — promote to reference product]']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which the alert was formally escalated to a higher oversight level. Null when escalation_required is False. Used to track escalation timeliness and compliance with monitoring plan escalation SLAs.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this alert has been escalated to a higher level of oversight (e.g., sponsor, Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB), or senior management). True when disposition is ESCALATE_ONSITE_VISIT or when severity is CRITICAL.']
  is_repeat_alert boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this alert is a recurrence of a previously closed alert for the same KRI and site within the current study. True when a prior alert for the same KRI-site combination was closed and the KRI has breached again, signaling unresolved systemic issues.']
  kri_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific Key Risk Indicator (KRI) that breached its threshold and triggered this alert (e.g., KRI-AE-001 for adverse event reporting timeliness). Denormalized from the risk_indicator product for alert-level reporting without requiring a join. Null for manually triggered alerts.']
  kri_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) that triggered the alert (e.g., \'Adverse Event Reporting Timeliness\', \'Query Resolution Rate\'). Denormalized for alert reporting and dashboard display without requiring a join to the risk_indicator product.']
  observed_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual computed value of the Key Risk Indicator (KRI) or data signal at the time the alert was generated. Compared against threshold_value to confirm the breach. QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category per TRANSACTION_HEADER non-monetary role.']
  reference_period_end date [note: 'End date of the data analysis window used by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system to compute the KRI value that triggered this alert. Together with reference_period_start, defines the exact data window under review.']
  reference_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the data analysis window used by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system to compute the KRI value that triggered this alert. Defines the temporal scope of the anomaly.']
  review_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the reviewer completed the assessment and recorded a disposition for the alert. Used to calculate review cycle time and SLA compliance.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the assigned reviewer must complete the review and disposition of the alert, as defined by the monitoring plan SLA for the given alert severity level.']
  review_narrative varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative entered by the reviewer documenting the findings, rationale for the review outcome, and justification for the chosen disposition. Supports audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness per ALCOA+ principles.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Conclusion reached by the reviewer after investigating the alert. CONFIRMED: the anomaly or KRI breach is genuine and requires action. FALSE_POSITIVE: the alert was triggered in error or the signal is not clinically meaningful. INCONCLUSIVE: insufficient data to determine; requires further monitoring. PENDING: review not yet completed.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Native record identifier from the originating operational system (Oracle Siebel CTMS or CSM engine) for this alert. Enables lineage tracing and reconciliation between the Silver Layer and the system of record.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor was formally notified of this alert. Null when sponsor_notified is False. Used to verify compliance with sponsor notification SLAs defined in the monitoring plan.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this alert, as required for critical or high-severity alerts under the monitoring plan and sponsor oversight obligations per ICH E6(R2).']
  threshold_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the breached threshold was the amber (warning) or red (action-required) threshold level as defined in the monitoring plan. Drives escalation logic and response SLA.']
  threshold_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The amber or red threshold value that was breached at the time the alert was generated. Captured at alert generation time to preserve the threshold configuration in effect, as thresholds may be revised over the study lifecycle.']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'System or process that generated the alert. AUTOMATED_CSM: centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) engine. MANUAL_ENTRY: entered directly by a central monitor. EDC_INTEGRATION: triggered by Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system data feed (e.g., Medidata Rave). CTMS_RULE: rule-based trigger from Oracle Siebel CTMS. SAFETY_SYSTEM: triggered from Oracle Argus Safety integration.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the observed_value and threshold_value are expressed (e.g., \'percent\', \'days\', \'count\', \'ratio\'). Required for correct interpretation of the KRI metric values in alert review and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this alert record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer, capturing status changes, review updates, and disposition entries. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.']

  Note: 'Automated or manually triggered alert generated by the centralized statistical monitoring (CSM) system when a KRI threshold breach or data anomaly is detected at a site. Captures alert type, triggering KRI or data signal, site and study context, alert severity, alert generation date, assigned reviewer, review outcome, and disposition (escalated to on-site visit, remote follow-up, no action required). Supports proactive risk management in the RBM framework.'
}

Table biostatistics.sap {
  sap_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Statistical Analysis Plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: The SAP is developed under a specific regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA). IND annual reports and NDA/BLA submissions require the current SAP to be associated with the application. Regulatory affairs']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: SAP authoring requires direct traceability to the protocol version it operationalizes. Regulatory submissions (FDA/EMA) require the SAP to reference the specific protocol; biostatisticians version the']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: ICH E9 requires the SAP to describe the randomization method. The SAP must reference the exact rand_scheme version it was designed around for regulatory submission alignment. The existing randomizatio']
  regulatory_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.strategy. Business justification: The regulatory strategy defines the submission pathway, primary endpoint, and analysis population that the SAP must align with. Regulatory strategy reviews require confirming SAP alignment with agreed']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) was authored. Links the SAP to its parent study master record.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: CTD Module 5 submission assembly requires knowing which SAP version was included in each NDA/BLA/MAA submission. Regulatory affairs and biostatistics teams must jointly confirm the submitted SAP versi']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF for this SAP version. Enables cross-system traceability between the Databricks Silver Layer and the Veeva Vault document management system.']
  adam_dataset_specifications varchar [note: 'Reference to or summary of the ADaM (Analysis Data Model) dataset specifications linked to this SAP, including key analysis datasets (ADSL, ADAE, ADTTE, ADLB, etc.) and their derivation rules. Sourced from SAS Drug Development and aligned with CDISC ADaM standards.']
  alpha_spending_function varchar [note: 'Statistical alpha spending function used to allocate the overall significance level across planned interim and final analyses (e.g., O\'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets). Applicable only when interim analyses are planned.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential amendment counter for this SAP version. Zero (0) for the original version; incremented by 1 for each formal amendment. Supports amendment history tracking required for regulatory submissions.']
  amendment_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for the SAP amendment, describing the business or scientific reason for changes (e.g., protocol amendment, regulatory feedback, updated endpoint definition). Required for regulatory audit trail per ICH E9.']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the type of statistical analysis governed by this SAP. \'interim\' for pre-specified interim analyses (e.g., DSMB/DMC reviews); \'final\' for the primary confirmatory analysis; \'post_hoc\' for unplanned analyses; \'exploratory\' for hypothesis-generating analyses.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this SAP version received final approval from all required signatories (biostatistics lead, sponsor, and/or medical monitor). Must precede database lock (DBL) for confirmatory trials. Critical regulatory milestone.']
  authoring_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system used to author and manage this SAP document (e.g., SAS Drug Development, Veeva Vault). Supports system-of-record traceability and integration lineage.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the blinding state of the SAP at the time of authoring or approval. \'blinded\' means the SAP was finalized before unblinding; \'unblinded\' means finalized post-unblinding (e.g., for final CSR); \'partially_unblinded\' applies to interim analyses with limited unblinding. Critical for regulatory integrity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SAP record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate).']
  dbl_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinical database was locked (DBL) for the analysis covered by this SAP. The SAP must be approved prior to or on this date for confirmatory analyses. Sourced from Medidata Rave EDC.']
  dsmb_review_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SAP includes or supports interim analyses to be reviewed by a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC). Drives interim analysis planning and blinding procedures.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this SAP version is considered effective and binding for statistical analyses. Typically the approval date. Supports version history and regulatory audit trail.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this SAP version was superseded by a newer version or retired. Null for the currently active version. Supports version history management and regulatory audit trail.']
  estimand_description varchar [note: 'Description of the treatment effect estimand(s) as defined per ICH E9(R1), including the population, variable (endpoint), intercurrent events handling strategy, and summary measure. Mandatory for SAPs submitted to FDA and EMA under ICH E9(R1) framework.']
  interim_analysis_count varchar [note: 'Number of pre-specified interim analyses planned for this study as defined in the SAP. Zero if no interim analyses are planned. Drives alpha spending function selection and DSMB/DMC meeting scheduling.']
  is_current_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the currently active and approved version of the SAP for the study. Only one version per study should have this flag set to True at any given time.']
  itt_population_definition varchar [note: 'Formal definition of the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population as specified in the SAP, including inclusion/exclusion criteria for subjects in this population. The ITT population is the primary analysis population for most confirmatory trials per ICH E9.']
  missing_data_strategy varchar [note: 'Pre-specified strategy for handling missing data in the primary and key secondary analyses (e.g., multiple imputation, LOCF (Last Observation Carried Forward), MMRM, tipping point analysis). Regulatory agencies require explicit pre-specification of missing data approaches.']
  multiplicity_adjustment_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method used to control the family-wise error rate (FWER) or false discovery rate (FDR) when testing multiple hypotheses (e.g., Bonferroni, Holm, Hochberg, Dunnett, hierarchical testing, Benjamini-Hochberg). Required for confirmatory trials with multiple endpoints or doses.']
  power_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Pre-specified statistical power (1 - beta) expressed as a percentage (e.g., 80.00, 90.00) used in the sample size calculation. Represents the probability of detecting a true treatment effect of the assumed magnitude.']
  pp_population_definition varchar [note: 'Formal definition of the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population as specified in the SAP, including criteria for protocol deviations that would exclude subjects. Used as a sensitivity analysis population alongside ITT.']
  primary_endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative definition of the primary efficacy or safety endpoint as specified in the SAP, including the outcome measure, timepoint, and measurement scale. Aligned with the study protocol and regulatory submission.']
  primary_statistical_method varchar [note: 'Description of the primary statistical methodology applied to the primary endpoint analysis (e.g., ANCOVA, mixed-effects model for repeated measures (MMRM), Cox proportional hazards, logistic regression). Sourced from SAS Drug Development SAP authoring.']
  regulatory_impact varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory impact of this SAP version or amendment. \'none\' for administrative changes; \'minor\' for non-material updates; \'major\' for changes affecting primary analysis; \'requires_submission\' for changes requiring regulatory notification or submission.']
  regulatory_submission_alignment varchar [note: 'Identifies the regulatory submission type(s) this SAP is aligned to support (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD). Ensures the SAP meets the statistical requirements of the target regulatory agency. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|CTD|eCTD|none — promote to reference product]']
  safety_population_definition varchar [note: 'Formal definition of the safety analysis population as specified in the SAP, typically all randomized subjects who received at least one dose of study treatment. Used for all safety and adverse event analyses.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects planned for enrollment as specified in the SAP sample size calculation section. Derived from power calculations and protocol-specified assumptions. Used for enrollment tracking and regulatory review.']
  sap_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable identifier for the SAP document, typically assigned by the sponsor or CRO (e.g., SAP-STUDY001-001). Used for regulatory submissions and cross-system referencing in Veeva Vault and SAS Drug Development.']
  sections_modified varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or narrative description of SAP sections that were modified in this amendment (e.g., \'Section 3.1 Primary Endpoint, Section 5.2 Multiplicity Adjustment\'). Supports change impact assessment and regulatory review.']
  sensitivity_analysis_description varchar [note: 'Description of pre-specified sensitivity analyses designed to assess the robustness of primary analysis conclusions to key assumptions (e.g., missing data assumptions, outlier handling, covariate selection). Required per ICH E9(R1).']
  significance_level decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pre-specified two-sided alpha level for hypothesis testing (e.g., 0.0500 for 5%). Defines the threshold for declaring statistical significance. May be adjusted for multiplicity. Stored as a decimal fraction.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the Statistical Analysis Plan document, typically including the study name, indication, and phase (e.g., \'Statistical Analysis Plan for a Phase III Randomized Controlled Trial of Drug X in Indication Y\').']
  tlf_specifications varchar [note: 'Reference to or summary of the Tables, Listings, and Figures (TLF) shells or specifications associated with this SAP. Defines the outputs to be produced by the statistical programming team in SAS Drug Development for the CSR.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'Date on which the study blind was broken for the analysis covered by this SAP version. Null if the SAP was finalized while the study remained blinded. Supports regulatory audit trail for pre-specification of analyses.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this SAP record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the SAP document following semantic versioning convention (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0). Incremented with each amendment. Supports version history tracking and regulatory audit trail.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this SAP version. \'draft\' indicates authoring in progress; \'in_review\' indicates under biostatistics or sponsor review; \'approved\' indicates final sign-off obtained; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'retired\' indicates withdrawn.']

  Note: 'Statistical Analysis Plan master record for a clinical study. Captures SAP version history (including amendments with rationale, sections modified, and regulatory impact), authoring status, approval workflow, blinding status, primary/secondary endpoint definitions, analysis populations (ITT, PP, safety), statistical methods, multiplicity adjustments, sensitivity analysis specifications, and regulatory submission alignment. Sourced from SAS Drug Development and Veeva Vault. SSOT for all statistical planning decisions for a study.'
}

Table biostatistics.sap_amendment {
  sap_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SAP amendment record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the sap_amendment data product.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: sap_amendment already has analysis_population_changed: BOOLEAN flag indicating when an amendment modifies the analysis population definition. When this flag is TRUE, the amendment should reference the']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Audit findings (e.g., analysis method not pre-specified, population definition inconsistency) can mandate SAP amendments. Linking the SAP amendment to the triggering audit finding is required for CAPA']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: SAP amendments with regulatory_notification_required=true must be filed with specific health authorities. Direct FK identifies which authority requires notification, supporting regulatory notification']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: sap_amendment has interim_analysis_changed: BOOLEAN flag. When an SAP amendment modifies the interim analysis plan (e.g., changes stopping rules, alpha spending, or adds/removes an interim look), it s']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: When sap_amendment.randomization_scheme_changed=true, regulators and auditors require direct traceability to the specific rand_scheme version that triggered the SAP amendment. This is a GCP audit trai']
  sample_size_calc_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sample_size_calc. Business justification: sap_amendment has sample_size_changed: BOOLEAN flag. When an SAP amendment changes the sample size, it should reference the specific sample_size_calc record that documents the revised calculation. Thi']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent Statistical Analysis Plan document that this amendment revises. Links the amendment to the base SAP record.']
  signal_evaluation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.signal_evaluation. Business justification: SAP amendments are sometimes triggered by safety signal evaluations requiring new safety endpoints, modified stopping rules, or additional subgroup analyses. Regulatory agencies require traceability b']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this SAP amendment was issued. Enables study-level traceability of all SAP revisions.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: FDA/EMA expect SAP amendments to explicitly reference the protocol amendment that triggered them. sap_amendment.protocol_amendment_ref is a denormalized text reference; replacing it with a proper FK e']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: SAP amendments with regulatory impact must be submitted to health authorities as IND amendments. The sap_amendment has \'regulatory_notification_required\' and \'regulatory_impact_assessment\' fields. Thi']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved SAP amendment document stored in the Veeva Vault eTMF or document management system. Provides direct linkage to the controlled document artifact.']
  adam_dataset_impact varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of ADaM datasets (e.g., ADSL, ADAE, ADTTE, ADLB) whose specifications are affected by this SAP amendment. Drives downstream ADaM dataset re-specification and re-validation.']
  amendment_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative justification for why the SAP was amended. Must document the scientific, operational, or regulatory basis for each change to satisfy GCP audit trail requirements.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SAP amendment document. Tracks progression from authoring through approval and eventual supersession by a later amendment.']
  amendment_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the SAP amendment summarizing the primary change (e.g., \'Amendment 1: Revised Primary Endpoint Analysis Population\'). Used in document headers and regulatory submissions.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment scope: major (changes to primary/key secondary endpoints, analysis populations, or statistical methods), minor (clarifications or secondary analysis changes), or administrative (formatting, typo corrections, contact updates).']
  amendment_version_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable version label for the amended SAP document (e.g., v1.1, v2.0, v3.1). Aligns with document management versioning conventions in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  analysis_population_changed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the definition of any analysis population (e.g., Intent-to-Treat (ITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety population). Population changes affect the integrity of the statistical inference.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the SAP amendment received final approval from the responsible statistician and sponsor. This is the effective date from which the amended analysis plan governs study conduct.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the responsible statistician or biostatistics director who approved this SAP amendment. Captured at time of approval for regulatory audit trail integrity.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the biostatistician or statistical programmer who authored this SAP amendment. Captured at time of authorship for audit trail integrity per ALCOA+ principles.']
  authored_date date [note: 'Date on which the SAP amendment was completed by the author and submitted for review. Establishes the contemporaneous authorship timestamp per ALCOA+ principles.']
  blinding_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this amendment has any impact on study blinding integrity. Amendments that require access to unblinded data must be documented and controlled to prevent bias.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Concise executive summary of all changes introduced by this amendment, suitable for inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) statistical section and regulatory submission cover letter.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this SAP amendment record was first created in the data platform. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  dbl_date date [note: 'Date on which the clinical database was locked at the time this amendment was classified. Used to determine pre- vs. post-DBL status and regulatory impact.']
  dsmb_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the DSMB/DMC must be notified of this SAP amendment, particularly when interim analysis plans or stopping rules are modified.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this SAP amendment becomes operationally effective and supersedes the prior version. May differ from approval date if a transition period is required.']
  interim_analysis_changed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the interim analysis plan, including timing, stopping rules, alpha spending, or DSMB/DMC charter alignment.']
  irb_iec_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SAP amendment requires notification or submission to the IRB/IEC overseeing the study. Relevant when SAP changes affect subject safety or consent.']
  missing_data_method_changed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment revises the strategy for handling missing data (e.g., Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF), multiple imputation, mixed models). Missing data methodology is a key regulatory focus area.']
  pre_dbl_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment was issued before (True) or after (False) the Database Lock (DBL). Pre-DBL amendments are standard; post-DBL amendments require heightened regulatory scrutiny and justification.']
  primary_endpoint_changed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment includes a change to the primary efficacy endpoint definition or analysis. Primary endpoint changes post-DBL are subject to heightened regulatory scrutiny.']
  randomization_scheme_changed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment modifies the randomization scheme or stratification factors used in the statistical analysis. Changes to randomization factors require protocol-level review.']
  regulatory_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessed level of regulatory impact of this SAP amendment on the study\'s regulatory submission package (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). Drives escalation, notification, and documentation requirements.']
  regulatory_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires formal notification to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.) as part of a protocol amendment or IND safety report.']
  review_completed_date date [note: 'Date on which the quality review of the SAP amendment was completed and the document was forwarded for approval.']
  sample_size_changed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment revises the planned sample size or power assumptions. Sample size changes typically require protocol amendment and regulatory notification.']
  sas_program_reference varchar [note: 'Reference path or identifier for the SAS Drug Development program(s) updated to implement this SAP amendment. Enables traceability between the statistical plan and the analysis code.']
  sections_modified varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or narrative description of the SAP section numbers and titles that were changed in this amendment (e.g., \'3.1 Primary Endpoint, 5.2 Analysis Population, 7.4 Missing Data Handling\'). Enables targeted review of changes.']
  sponsor_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor representative who co-signed or endorsed this SAP amendment, where sponsor sign-off is contractually or regulatorily required.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date on which this amendment was superseded by a subsequent amendment. Null if this is the current active version. Enables point-in-time reconstruction of the SAP version history.']
  tlf_impact varchar [note: 'Description or list of Tables, Listings, and Figures (TLFs) in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) that are affected by this SAP amendment. Used to scope re-programming and QC activities.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this SAP amendment record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail completeness.']

  Note: 'Versioned amendment record for a Statistical Analysis Plan. Tracks each SAP revision including amendment number, rationale for change, sections modified, pre- or post-database-lock classification, author, approver, approval date, and regulatory impact assessment. Enables full audit trail of SAP evolution across the study lifecycle.'
}

Table biostatistics.analysis_population {
  analysis_population_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the analysis population record within the biostatistics data product. Primary key for the analysis_population entity.']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Analysis populations in clinical trials are frequently arm-specific (e.g., ITT for active arm vs. placebo). SAP population definitions and regulatory submissions require traceability of each populatio']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) document version that formally defines and governs this analysis population. Ensures traceability from population definition to the authoritative SAP.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this analysis population is defined. Links the population definition to its parent study context.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Analysis populations (ITT, PP, Safety) are explicitly defined and reviewed in regulatory submissions (Module 5 CSR). Health authorities evaluate population definitions during NDA/BLA review. Direct FK']
  acronym varchar [note: 'Standardized abbreviation for the analysis population (e.g., ITT, mITT, PP, SAF, PK, PD). Used in TLF titles, CSR statistical sections, and ADaM dataset variable labels.']
  adam_dataset_name varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM dataset name where the ADSL flag variable for this population resides (typically ADSL). Used to locate the population flag in the ADaM submission package.']
  adsl_flag_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM ADSL dataset flag variable name (e.g., ITTFL, SAFFL, PPROTFL, MITTFL, PKFL) that identifies subjects belonging to this population. Must conform to CDISC ADaM naming convention ending in \'FL\'.']
  analysis_phase varchar [note: 'Designates the analysis phase in which this population definition is applied: interim analysis, final analysis, post-hoc analysis, or exploratory analysis. Governs which TLF shells and CSR sections reference this population.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the analysis population is evaluated under blinded, unblinded, or open-label conditions at the time of analysis. Critical for DSMB/DMC interim reviews and final unblinding records.']
  cdisc_standard_version varchar [note: 'The version of the CDISC ADaM standard applied to the derivation of this analysis population (e.g., ADaM IG v1.3, ADSL v1.1). Required for FDA/EMA electronic submission conformance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this analysis population record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  dbl_date date [note: 'The date on which the study database was locked (DBL) for the analysis associated with this population. Subject counts and population assignments are finalized as of this date.']
  dose_received_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether receipt of at least one dose of investigational product is a mandatory inclusion criterion for this analysis population. Distinguishes ITT (randomized only) from Safety (randomized and dosed) populations.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this version of the analysis population definition became effective, supporting SAP amendment versioning. Enables temporal tracking of population definition changes across SAP versions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this version of the analysis population definition was superseded or retired, supporting SAP amendment versioning. Null if this is the current active version.']
  exclusion_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the criteria that would exclude a subject from this analysis population despite meeting inclusion criteria (e.g., major protocol deviations, missing primary endpoint data). Defined in the SAP.']
  inclusion_criteria_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the criteria a subject must meet to be included in this analysis population, as defined in the SAP. Captures the logical rules (e.g., randomized and received at least one dose of study drug).']
  missing_endpoint_handling varchar [note: 'The pre-specified method for handling missing primary endpoint data for subjects in this population, as defined in the SAP (e.g., LOCF - Last Observation Carried Forward, multiple imputation, observed cases only). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: locf|bocf|wocf|multiple_imputation|observed_cases|none — promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for biostatistician notes, clarifications, or amendments related to this analysis population definition. May capture DSMB/DMC recommendations, protocol amendment impacts, or sponsor queries.']
  planned_sample_size varchar [note: 'The pre-specified planned number of subjects for this analysis population as determined by the sample size calculation in the SAP. Serves as the denominator benchmark for enrollment tracking and power calculations.']
  population_code varchar [note: 'Short, unique alphanumeric code identifying the analysis population within the study (e.g., ITT, PP, SAF, MITT, PK). Used as the CDISC ADaM ADSL flag variable name prefix and in TLF shell headers.']
  population_finalized_date date [note: 'The date on which the analysis population definition was formally finalized and approved in the SAP, prior to unblinding. Supports pre-specification audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  population_logic_summary varchar [note: 'Concise programmatic or pseudo-code summary of the population derivation logic used in SAS Drug Development ADaM programming (e.g., RANDFL=\'Y\' AND DOSEFL=\'Y\'). Bridges the narrative SAP definition and the SAS implementation.']
  population_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the analysis population as defined in the SAP (e.g., Intent-to-Treat Population, Per Protocol Population, Safety Population, Modified Intent-to-Treat Population, Pharmacokinetic Population).']
  population_rationale varchar [note: 'Scientific and regulatory rationale for defining this analysis population, as documented in the SAP. Explains why this population is appropriate for the associated analysis objectives and how it aligns with regulatory guidance.']
  population_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the analysis population definition. Tracks progression from initial SAP drafting through biostatistics review, approval, and potential supersession by an amended SAP version.']
  population_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the analysis population by its primary analytical purpose. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|safety|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic|per_protocol|modified_itt|other — promote to reference product]']
  primary_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this population is designated as the primary analysis population for the study as specified in the SAP. The primary population drives the primary efficacy endpoint analysis and regulatory submission.']
  protocol_deviation_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether subjects with major protocol deviations are excluded from this analysis population. Typically True for Per Protocol (PP) populations and False for ITT populations.']
  randomization_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether randomization is a mandatory inclusion criterion for subjects in this analysis population. True for ITT and most efficacy populations; may be False for screening failure or safety populations.']
  regulatory_primary_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this population is the pre-specified primary population for regulatory submission purposes (NDA/BLA/MAA). Distinct from internal primary flag; reflects the population declared in the IND/protocol.']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'The specific section number or identifier within the SAP document that formally defines this analysis population (e.g., Section 5.2.1). Enables direct traceability from the data product to the governing statistical document.']
  sap_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SAP document in which this analysis population definition was introduced or last amended (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Supports version-controlled traceability for regulatory audit.']
  sas_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SAS Drug Development program used to derive and validate the ADSL flag variable for this analysis population (e.g., adsl.sas). Enables traceability from population definition to the validated SAS code.']
  sensitivity_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this population is designated as a sensitivity analysis population (as opposed to a primary or secondary analysis population). Sensitivity populations test the robustness of primary analysis conclusions.']
  subject_count_at_dbl varchar [note: 'The number of subjects assigned to this analysis population at the time of Database Lock (DBL). This is the final, locked count used in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) and regulatory submission.']
  subject_count_interim varchar [note: 'Number of subjects assigned to this analysis population at the most recent interim analysis cut, if applicable. Used by the DSMB/DMC for interim review and blinded/unblinded reporting.']
  submission_population_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this analysis population is included in the regulatory submission package (NDA/BLA/MAA/eCTD). Populations marked True must have fully validated ADaM datasets and TLF outputs.']
  tlf_shell_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier(s) for the TLF shells that use this analysis population as their denominator or subject set. Links the population definition to the output specifications in the SAP/TLF shell document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this analysis population record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation status of the SAS programming for this analysis population\'s ADSL flag variable in SAS Drug Development. Tracks QC progress from initial programming through double-programming validation.']

  Note: 'Defines the analysis populations used in a study (e.g., Intent-to-Treat, Per Protocol, Safety, Modified ITT, Pharmacokinetic). Captures population name, acronym, inclusion/exclusion logic description, CDISC ADSL flag variable name, population size at DBL, and SAP reference. SSOT for population definitions consumed by ADaM datasets and TLF shells.'
}

Table biostatistics.sample_size_calc {
  sample_size_calc_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each sample size calculation record within the biostatistics domain.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: sample_size_calc has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which population the sample size calculation targets. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referentia']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Sample size calculations are performed per treatment arm (per-arm enrollment targets, allocation ratios). FDA/EMA require per-arm sample size justification in regulatory submissions. sample_size_calc ']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.endpoint. Business justification: Sample size calculations are driven by primary endpoint effect size assumptions. Regulatory submissions require traceability from sample size justification to the specific endpoint definition. primary']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Sample size justifications must satisfy specific health authority statistical requirements (FDA vs EMA power thresholds, non-inferiority margins per agency guidance). Direct FK identifies which author']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Sample size calculations are submitted as part of IND protocol amendments. The IND application governs the statistical protocol under which the calculation was made. Direct FK supports IND amendment h']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific protocol version under which this sample size calculation was conducted, supporting traceability to protocol amendments.']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_scheme. Business justification: Sample size calculations depend directly on the randomization scheme\'s allocation_ratio, block structure, and stratification. Regulatory submissions require sample size justification to reference the ']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) document that governs or references this sample size calculation.']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: Sample size calculations are performed for specific statistical endpoints — the primary endpoint drives the power calculation, and secondary endpoints may have their own calculations. Linking sample_s']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this sample size calculation was performed.']
  accrual_period_months varchar [note: 'The assumed patient accrual (enrollment) period in months, used in time-to-event sample size calculations to determine the number of events required.']
  allocation_ratio varchar [note: 'The randomization allocation ratio between treatment arms (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1\', \'1:1:1\'), specifying the relative number of subjects assigned to each arm.']
  alpha_level decimal(10,6) [note: 'The pre-specified Type I error rate (significance level, alpha) used in the sample size calculation, typically 0.05 for two-sided or 0.025 for one-sided tests.']
  alpha_spending_method varchar [note: 'The alpha-spending function applied to control Type I error across planned interim analyses (e.g., O\'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets). Null or \'none\' if no interim analysis is planned.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for revising a prior version of the sample size calculation, such as updated assumptions from interim data, protocol amendment, or regulatory feedback.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the sample size calculation was formally approved by the responsible biostatistician, marking readiness for inclusion in regulatory submissions.']
  calc_date date [note: 'The date on which this sample size calculation was performed or finalized, serving as the principal business event date for regulatory traceability.']
  calc_identifier varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code or reference number assigned to this sample size calculation, used in regulatory submissions and protocol documentation (e.g., SSC-2024-001).']
  calc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sample size calculation record, from initial draft through regulatory approval to archival.']
  calc_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this sample size calculation (e.g., v1.0, v2.1), enabling tracking of revisions driven by protocol amendments or updated assumptions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sample size calculation record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  dropout_rate decimal(10,6) [note: 'The assumed proportion of enrolled subjects expected to discontinue or be lost to follow-up, used to inflate the calculated sample size to ensure adequate evaluable subjects.']
  effect_size decimal(18,6) [note: 'The clinically meaningful difference or effect size assumed between treatment arms (e.g., mean difference, odds ratio, hazard ratio) used as the basis for the sample size calculation.']
  effect_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the assumed effect size (e.g., mmHg, %, hazard ratio, odds ratio, mean difference), providing dimensional context for regulatory interpretation.']
  event_rate_control decimal(10,6) [note: 'The assumed event rate or response rate in the control arm, applicable for binary or time-to-event endpoints. Null for continuous endpoints.']
  event_rate_treatment decimal(10,6) [note: 'The assumed event rate or response rate in the treatment arm, applicable for binary or time-to-event endpoints. Null for continuous endpoints.']
  follow_up_period_months varchar [note: 'The assumed minimum follow-up period in months after the last patient enrolled, used in time-to-event calculations to ensure sufficient events are observed.']
  hypothesis_type varchar [note: 'The statistical hypothesis framework applied (superiority, non-inferiority, equivalence), which determines the directionality and margin used in the sample size formula.']
  interim_analysis_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more interim analyses are planned for this study, which may require alpha-spending adjustments to the sample size calculation.']
  median_survival_control decimal(18,6) [note: 'The assumed median survival time in the control arm for time-to-event endpoints, used in log-rank or exponential model-based sample size calculations. Null for non-survival endpoints.']
  non_inferiority_margin decimal(18,6) [note: 'The pre-specified non-inferiority or equivalence margin (delta) used in the hypothesis test, applicable when hypothesis_type is non_inferiority or equivalence. Null for superiority designs.']
  number_of_arms varchar [note: 'Total number of treatment arms (including control) in the study design, used to determine the allocation structure and multiplicity adjustments.']
  number_of_events_required varchar [note: 'The minimum number of primary endpoint events (e.g., deaths, progressions) required to achieve the specified power for time-to-event analyses. Null for non-event-driven endpoints.']
  power decimal(10,6) [note: 'The pre-specified statistical power (1 minus Type II error rate, beta) used in the calculation, representing the probability of detecting the assumed effect size. Typically 0.80 or 0.90.']
  primary_endpoint_type varchar [note: 'Statistical nature of the primary efficacy endpoint (e.g., continuous, binary, time-to-event, ordinal, count) that drives the choice of sample size formula.']
  reference_source varchar [note: 'Citation or reference for the data source used to derive the assumed effect size, standard deviation, or event rate (e.g., published literature citation, prior Phase II study identifier, internal pilot data).']
  sample_size_per_arm varchar [note: 'The calculated number of subjects required per treatment arm to achieve the specified power at the given alpha level, accounting for the assumed effect size and standard deviation.']
  software_name varchar [note: 'Name of the validated statistical software used to perform the sample size calculation (e.g., PASS, nQuery Advisor, SAS PROC POWER, R), required for regulatory audit trail.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the statistical software used (e.g., PASS 2023, nQuery 9.1), required for reproducibility and regulatory validation documentation.']
  standard_deviation decimal(18,6) [note: 'The assumed population standard deviation for continuous endpoints, derived from prior studies or literature, used in the sample size formula. Null for non-continuous endpoints.']
  statistical_method varchar [note: 'The specific statistical formula or method used for the calculation (e.g., two-sample t-test, log-rank test, chi-square test, Kaplan-Meier), providing methodological traceability.']
  study_design_type varchar [note: 'The clinical trial design type (e.g., parallel group, crossover, factorial, adaptive) that determines the statistical methodology applied in the sample size calculation.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'Clinical development phase of the study (Phase I through Phase IV) for which the sample size is being calculated, influencing regulatory expectations and statistical rigor.']
  test_sidedness varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the statistical test is one-sided or two-sided, directly affecting the critical value and resulting sample size.']
  total_enrollment_target varchar [note: 'The total number of subjects to be enrolled (randomized) after inflating the total sample size by the assumed dropout rate, representing the actual recruitment target submitted in the protocol.']
  total_sample_size varchar [note: 'The total number of subjects required across all treatment arms before applying the dropout inflation, representing the minimum evaluable sample.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this sample size calculation record was most recently modified, supporting version control and audit trail requirements.']

  Note: 'Records sample size calculations performed during study design and protocol development. Captures study phase, primary endpoint type, assumed effect size, standard deviation, alpha level, power, dropout rate, allocation ratio, calculated sample size per arm, total enrollment target, software used (e.g., PASS, nQuery), and version. Supports regulatory submission and protocol amendments.'
}
