  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each ADaM dataset specification record. Primary key for this entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: adam_dataset_spec has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which analysis population the dataset serves. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integ']
  edc_study_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: ADaM dataset specs are programmed against source data structures defined in a specific EDC build. When the EDC build changes, ADaM specs must be reviewed for impact. Domain experts expect this link fo']
  lab_panel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_panel. Business justification: ADaM ADLB datasets are sourced from specific lab panels. ADaM programmers and validators must trace which lab panel definition (analyte list, SDTM domain, CDISC codes) feeds a given dataset spec. This']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan that governs the derivation rules and analysis objectives for this ADaM dataset. Ensures traceability from dataset design to the approved SAP.']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: ADaM dataset specs are derived from specific SDTM datasets. Biostatisticians and programmers reference the source SDTM dataset record for version, QC status, and data cut date when building ADaM specs']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this ADaM dataset specification is authored. Links the specification to its parent protocol and study context.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: ADaM datasets are submitted to FDA/EMA as CDISC-required components of NDA/BLA/MAA submissions. The submission_flag attribute on adam_dataset_spec confirms this business reality. Direct FK supports su']
  adam_ig_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC ADaM Implementation Guide to which this dataset specification conforms (e.g., \'ADaM IG v1.3\', \'ADSL v1.1\', \'BDS v1.1\'). Required for regulatory submission conformance validation.']
  analysis_variable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variable is a primary analysis variable (AVAL, AVALC, or equivalent) used directly in statistical analyses and TLF production. True = analysis variable; False = supporting/descriptive variable. Guides programmer prioritization.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this ADaM dataset specification was formally approved by the lead biostatistician and/or statistical programming lead. Marks the specification as ready for SAS programming implementation.']
  authored_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the biostatistician who authored this ADaM dataset specification. Establishes accountability and is referenced in the audit trail for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this ADaM dataset contains unblinded treatment assignment data. Blinded = treatment codes masked; Unblinded = actual treatment assignments visible (post-DBL or for open-label studies); Open-Label = study design is inherently unblinded. Governs access control and data release procedures.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for changes introduced in this version of the specification relative to the prior version (e.g., \'SAP amendment v2.0 added new secondary endpoint\', \'Protocol amendment PA-003 added new visit\'). Required for change control documentation.']
  codelist_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific CDISC or sponsor-defined codelist associated with this variable (e.g., \'NY\', \'SEX\', \'RACE\', \'DTYPE\'). Referenced in the define.xml ItemDef and used for PINNACLE 21 conformance validation.']
  controlled_terminology varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled terminology codelist applicable to this variable (e.g., \'CDISC SDTM CT RACE\', \'NCI Thesaurus C66742\', \'MedDRA v26.0 PT\', \'WHO-DD\'). Ensures standardized values for regulatory submission and cross-study comparability.']
  core_designation varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM core designation indicating the requirement level for this variable. Required = must be present in all datasets of this class; Expected = should be present when applicable; Permissible = may be included at sponsor discretion. Drives conformance validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this ADaM dataset specification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and record lineage per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  dataset_class varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM structural class of the dataset. ADSL = Subject-Level Analysis Dataset (one record per subject); BDS = Basic Data Structure (one or more records per subject per analysis parameter per analysis timepoint); OCCDS = Occurrence Data Structure (one or more records per subject per occurrence of an event); OTHER = datasets not conforming to a standard class.']
  dataset_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive label for the dataset (e.g., \'Subject-Level Analysis Dataset\', \'Adverse Events Analysis Dataset\'). Used in SAS PROC CONTENTS output and regulatory submission metadata.']
  dataset_name varchar [note: 'Short, uppercase dataset name conforming to CDISC ADaM naming conventions (e.g., ADSL, ADAE, ADTTE, ADLB, ADCM, ADEX, ADRS). Maximum 8 characters per CDISC standard. This is the externally-known identifier used by SAS programmers and regulatory submissions.']
  dataset_purpose varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the analytical purpose of this dataset, including the primary analysis populations it supports, key endpoints addressed, and its role in the Clinical Study Report (CSR). Authored by the biostatistician.']
  define_xml_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC Define-XML standard used to generate the define.xml metadata file for this dataset (e.g., \'Define-XML v2.0\', \'Define-XML v2.1\'). Required for FDA and EMA electronic submission packages.']
  derivation_algorithm varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative or pseudocode describing the derivation logic for this variable, including source variables, transformation rules, imputation methods (e.g., LOCF), and conditional logic. The primary programming instruction for SAS programmers. Must be unambiguous and traceable to the SAP.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the ADaM dataset specification is effective and authoritative for programming use. For superseded versions, this marks the start of the version\'s validity period.']
  interim_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this dataset specification is intended for use in an interim analysis (True) or the final analysis (False). Interim analysis datasets may have restricted access and require DSMB/DMC review procedures.']
  key_variables varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of variable names that form the unique record key for this dataset (e.g., \'USUBJID\' for ADSL; \'USUBJID, PARAMCD, ADT\' for BDS datasets). Defines the grain of the dataset and is critical for merge/join operations in SAS programming.']
  pinnacle21_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the Pinnacle 21 (formerly OpenCDISC) conformance validation run against this ADaM dataset. Pass = no errors; Pass with Warnings = minor issues noted; Fail = conformance errors requiring remediation; Not Run = validation pending. Required for FDA submission readiness.']
  programming_language varchar [note: 'Statistical programming language used to implement this ADaM dataset specification. SAS is the regulatory standard; R and Python may be used for exploratory or supplemental analyses. Governs QC and validation approach.']
  qc_method varchar [note: 'Quality control methodology applied to validate this ADaM dataset. Double Programming = independent programmer recreates dataset and compares; Peer Review = senior programmer reviews code and output; Independent QC = separate QC team validates; Not Required = exploratory/non-submission dataset.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current QC completion status for this ADaM dataset. Not Started = QC not yet initiated; In Progress = QC underway; QC Complete = dataset passed QC validation; Failed QC = discrepancies found requiring programmer remediation.']
  sas_format varchar [note: 'SAS display format assigned to this variable (e.g., \'DATE9.\', \'DATETIME20.\', \'$CHAR200.\', \'8.2\'). Governs how values are displayed in SAS output and define.xml DisplayFormat attribute. Required for SAS Drug Development programming specifications.']
  sas_informat varchar [note: 'SAS informat used to read raw values into this variable (e.g., \'YYMMDD10.\', \'E8601DT.\', \'$CHAR200.\'). Required for date/datetime variables derived from ISO 8601 SDTM character dates. Specified in SAS Drug Development programming specs.']
  sas_program_reference varchar [note: 'File path or program name of the SAS program in SAS Drug Development that produces this ADaM dataset (e.g., \'/studies/STUDY001/adam/programs/adsl.sas\'). Links the specification to its implementing program for traceability and QC.']
  source_variable varchar [note: 'Name of the source SDTM or predecessor ADaM variable(s) from which this variable is derived (e.g., \'AE.AEDECOD\', \'DM.RFSTDTC\', \'ADSL.TRT01P\'). Supports CDISC traceability requirements and audit trail for regulatory submissions.']
  spec_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ADaM dataset specification. Draft = initial authoring; Under Review = submitted for biostatistics/programming review; Approved = formally signed off for programming use; Superseded = replaced by a newer version; Retired = no longer applicable.']
  spec_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this ADaM dataset specification document following major.minor versioning (e.g., 1.0, 1.1, 2.0). Incremented upon each approved revision to support change control and audit traceability.']
  submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ADaM dataset is included in the regulatory submission package (NDA, BLA, MAA, or IND). True = included in eCTD submission; False = internal analysis only. Governs define.xml inclusion and FDA/EMA conformance validation requirements.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the specification was superseded by a newer version. Null for the current active version. Supports version history and audit trail for regulatory inspection.']
  timing_variable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variable is a CDISC ADaM timing variable (e.g., ADT, ADTM, ADY, AVISIT, AVISITN). True = timing variable subject to ADaM timing variable conventions. Used for conformance checking and define.xml classification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this ADaM dataset specification record. Supports change tracking and audit trail per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  variable_label varchar [note: 'Descriptive SAS variable label (max 256 characters) providing a human-readable description of the variable (e.g., \'Unique Subject Identifier\', \'Parameter Code\', \'Analysis Value\'). Appears in SAS PROC CONTENTS and submission define.xml.']
  variable_length varchar [note: 'SAS variable length in bytes. For character variables, specifies maximum string length (e.g., 200 for USUBJID). For numeric variables, typically 8 (SAS default double precision). Required for SAS dataset creation and define.xml generation.']
  variable_name varchar [note: 'SAS-compliant variable name for this specification record (uppercase, max 8 characters per SAS/CDISC convention, e.g., USUBJID, PARAMCD, AVAL, DTYPE). Each row in this table represents one variable within the dataset specification.']
  variable_type varchar [note: 'SAS data type of the variable. Char = character/string variable; Num = numeric variable. Governs SAS dataset structure and informat/format assignments in programming specifications.']

  Note: 'Specification record for each ADaM (Analysis Data Model) dataset produced for a study, including variable-level detail. Captures dataset name (e.g., ADSL, ADAE, ADTTE, ADLB), dataset class (ADSL, BDS, OCCDS, OTHER), purpose, key variables with derivation algorithms, variable types, lengths, controlled terminology references, CDISC ADaM core designations (Required/Expected/Permissible), source SDTM domains, programming language, SAS program reference, and approval status. SSOT for ADaM dataset and variable design consumed by SAS programmers.'
}

Business justification: adam_variable_spec currently has estimand_reference: STRING — a free-text reference to an estimand. This should be normalized to a proper FK to biostatistics.estimand. ADaM variables that implement es']
  primary_adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent ADaM dataset to which this variable specification belongs (e.g., ADSL, ADAE, ADLB). Links variable-level spec to its dataset container.']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan that governs the derivation rules and analytical intent for this variable specification.']
  stratification_factor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.stratification_factor. Business justification: ADaM ADSL stratification variables (STRAT1, STRAT2) must be derived from the randomization stratification factors. CDISC ADaM programming requires the variable spec to reference the exact stratificati']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this ADaM variable specification is defined. Scopes the variable spec to a specific protocol.']
  adam_structure varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM structural classification of the parent dataset: ADSL (Subject-Level Analysis Dataset), BDS (Basic Data Structure — one record per subject per parameter per analysis timepoint), OCCDS (Occurrence Data Structure — for events like AEs and CMs), or OTHER.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the variable specification was formally approved by the responsible biostatistician or lead programmer. Marks the transition from draft to approved status in the specification lifecycle.']
  baseline_flag varchar [note: 'Name of the associated baseline flag variable (e.g., ABLFL) used to identify the baseline record for this analysis variable. Supports change-from-baseline derivations and is required for BDS datasets.']
  change_from_baseline_var varchar [note: 'Name of the corresponding change-from-baseline variable (CHG) or percent change-from-baseline variable (PCHG) derived from this analysis variable. Establishes the analytical linkage between AVAL and its change metrics.']
  codelist_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific CDISC controlled terminology codelist associated with this variable (e.g., NY, DTYPE, PARAMCD, AVISIT). Used in DEFINE-XML generation and submission package validation.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, clarifications, or reviewer comments regarding the variable specification. May include sponsor-specific guidance, open questions, or cross-references to SAP sections.']
  controlled_terminology_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the applicable CDISC controlled terminology codelist, MedDRA dictionary, WHO-DD, or CTCAE version governing the permissible values for this variable. Example: \'CDISC CT DTYPE 2023-09-29\', \'MedDRA v26.0 PT\', \'CTCAE v5.0\'.']
  core_designation varchar [note: 'CDISC ADaM core classification indicating the mandatory status of the variable: Required (must be present), Expected (should be present unless not applicable), Permissible (optional, may be included), or Conditionally Required (required when specific conditions are met). Drives compliance validation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this variable specification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  define_xml_display_format varchar [note: 'Display format specification for this variable as it will appear in the DEFINE-XML submission metadata file (define.xml). Includes display format string and any associated decode mapping for coded values. Required for FDA/EMA electronic submission packages.']
  derivation_algorithm varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the derivation logic used to compute or assign the variable\'s value. Includes source variable references, transformation rules, imputation methods (e.g., LOCF), and conditional logic. Enables automated SAS program generation and independent QC programmer validation.']
  format_name varchar [note: 'SAS format or informat name applied to this variable for display or input purposes (e.g., DATE9., DATETIME20., $CHAR200.). Used in SAS program generation and dataset creation.']
  imputation_method varchar [note: 'Description of the missing data imputation method applied to this variable as specified in the SAP. Examples: LOCF (Last Observation Carried Forward), BOCF (Baseline Observation Carried Forward), multiple imputation, worst-case, or none. Critical for regulatory submission transparency.']
  is_analysis_variable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variable is designated as a primary or secondary analysis variable (AVAL, AVALC, or equivalent) used directly in statistical analyses as defined in the SAP.']
  is_key_variable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variable is part of the dataset\'s sort key (BY variables) as defined in the ADaM dataset specification. Key variables determine dataset sort order and are required for PROC SORT and merge operations in SAS.']
  is_param_variable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this variable is a PARAM/PARAMCD/PARAMN grouping variable used in BDS (Basic Data Structure) or OCCDS datasets to identify the analysis parameter. Drives parameter-level filtering in TLF production.']
  origin varchar [note: 'CDISC-defined origin classification indicating how the variable value is obtained: Derived (computed from other variables), Assigned (set by sponsor/programmer per protocol), Collected (directly from eCRF/EDC), Protocol (defined in study protocol), or Not Available.']
  population_flag varchar [note: 'Name of the analysis population flag variable this specification defines (e.g., ITTFL for Intent-to-Treat, SAFFL for Safety, PPROTFL for Per Protocol, FASFL for Full Analysis Set). Null if the variable is not a population flag.']
  predecessor_variable varchar [note: 'Name of the predecessor variable from which this variable is directly derived, as required in DEFINE-XML metadata. Establishes the data lineage chain from SDTM collected data through ADaM derived variables for regulatory traceability.']
  qc_date date [note: 'Date on which the independent QC programmer completed verification of this variable\'s derivation. Recorded as part of the audit trail for regulatory submission readiness.']
  qc_programmer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the independent QC programmer responsible for verifying the derivation of this variable through independent programming. Required for dual-programming QC methodology compliance.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current QC lifecycle status of this variable specification. Tracks progression from initial draft through independent QC programmer review to final approval. QC Passed indicates the derivation has been independently verified by a second programmer. Locked indicates the spec is frozen for DBL.']
  sas_program_reference varchar [note: 'Name or path of the SAS program in SAS Drug Development that implements the derivation for this variable. Enables direct linkage between the variable specification and its executable SAS code for QC and audit purposes.']
  significant_digits varchar [note: 'Number of significant digits to retain for numeric variables in analysis outputs and TLF displays. Defined in the SAP and applied during SAS program generation to ensure consistent rounding across tables, listings, and figures.']
  source_dataset varchar [note: 'Primary SDTM or ADaM source dataset from which this variable is derived or mapped (e.g., AE, LB, VS, ADSL). Supports traceability from analysis results back to raw clinical data.']
  source_variables varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of source variable names (from SDTM datasets or other ADaM datasets) used in the derivation of this variable. Supports data lineage tracing and ALCOA+ compliance. Example: \'SDTM.AE.AESTDTC, SDTM.DM.RFSTDTC\'.']
  spec_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this variable specification document. Draft indicates work in progress; In Review indicates under biostatistician or sponsor review; Approved indicates finalized and signed off; Superseded indicates replaced by a newer version; Retired indicates no longer applicable.']
  spec_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this variable specification record, following semantic versioning (e.g., v1.0, v1.1, v2.0). Incremented when derivation algorithm, source variables, or controlled terminology references are updated. Supports audit trail and change management.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area or disease indication for which this variable specification is designed (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Relevant for CDISC Therapeutic Area User Guides (TAUGs) compliance and cross-study reuse.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this variable specification record. Required for change tracking and audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  variable_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable SAS variable label (up to 40 characters) describing the variable\'s business meaning. Used in SAS output headers, TLF footnotes, and reviewer documentation. Example: \'Analysis Value\', \'Parameter Code\'.']
  variable_length varchar [note: 'SAS storage length for the variable. For Character variables, specifies maximum character width (e.g., 200). For Numeric variables, typically 8 (SAS default double-precision float). Used in SAS dataset creation and DEFINE-XML.']
  variable_name varchar [note: 'The SAS-compliant variable name as defined in the ADaM dataset specification. Must conform to CDISC ADaM naming conventions: uppercase, up to 8 characters, starting with a letter. Examples: USUBJID, AVAL, DTYPE, PARAMCD.']
  variable_order varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this variable within the ADaM dataset column order as defined in the specification. Determines the column sequence in the SAS dataset and DEFINE-XML variable listing. Required for consistent dataset structure across programming and QC.']
  variable_role varchar [note: 'CDISC-defined role classification describing the variable\'s functional purpose within the dataset structure. Drives DEFINE-XML metadata generation and reviewer interpretation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Identifier|Topic|Timing|Grouping Qualifier|Result Qualifier|Record Qualifier|Synonym Qualifier|Variable Qualifier|Rule — promote to reference product]']
  variable_type varchar [note: 'SAS data type classification for the variable: Character (text/categorical values) or Numeric (continuous, date, or flag values). Drives SAS program generation and DEFINE-XML metadata.']

  Note: 'Detailed variable-level specification within an ADaM dataset. Captures variable name, label, type (character/numeric), length, derivation algorithm, controlled terminology reference, CDISC ADaM core designation (Required/Expected/Permissible/Conditionally Required), source variable(s), and QC status. Enables automated SAS program generation and reviewer validation.'
}

Table biostatistics.tlf_shell {
  tlf_shell_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each TLF shell specification record in the catalog. Primary key for the tlf_shell data product.']
  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.adam_dataset_spec. Business justification: tlf_shell has adam_dataset_name (STRING) referencing the ADaM dataset that feeds the TLF shell. Normalizing to a FK to adam_dataset_spec.adam_dataset_spec_id creates a proper referential link. adam_da']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: tlf_shell has display_population (STRING) referencing the analysis population displayed in the TLF. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integrity. di']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: TLF shells are authored for specific treatment arms (e.g., \'Treatment A 100mg vs. Placebo\'). tlf_shell.treatment_arm is a denormalized text field. A proper FK enables arm-level TLF completeness checks']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_endpoint. Business justification: TLF shells are authored for specific protocol endpoints (primary, secondary, exploratory). CSR authoring and regulatory submission package assembly require endpoint-to-output traceability. This FK ena']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: TLF shells are scoped to specific study epochs (e.g., treatment-period efficacy tables, follow-up safety listings). SAP TLF specifications define outputs per epoch. Biostatisticians and programmers us']
  estimand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.estimand. Business justification: A TLF shell is designed to display results for a specific estimand per ICH E9(R1) requirements. Each shell specification should be directly linked to the estimand it addresses, enabling regulatory rev']
  randomization_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: TLF shells are produced by treatment arm — the treatment_arm plain text field is a denormalized reference to the treatment_arm entity. SAS programmers need the exact arm_code and arm_name from the ran']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan version that governs this TLF output. Ensures traceability from output to the authorizing SAP document.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this TLF shell is defined. Links the output specification to its parent study context.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: TLF shells for visit-level analyses (lab tables by visit, PK profiles by visit, vital signs by visit) reference specific protocol visit schedule entries. SAP TLF specifications are designed per protoc']
  abbreviations varchar [note: 'List of abbreviations and their definitions used within this TLF output (e.g., \'SD=Standard Deviation; CI=Confidence Interval; n=number of subjects\'). Required for regulatory submission readability per ICH E3.']
  analysis_timepoint varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output is produced for an interim analysis, the final analysis, or both. Governs blinding controls and DSMB/DMC reporting requirements in SAS Drug Development.']
  analysis_visit varchar [note: 'Study visit or visit window covered by this TLF output (e.g., Baseline, Week 12, End of Treatment, Follow-Up). Derived from the ADaM AVISIT variable and SAP visit schedule definition.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the lead statistician or biostatistics manager who formally approved this TLF shell for programming. Required for regulatory audit trail and submission readiness.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output is produced under blinded or unblinded conditions. Unblinded outputs are restricted to authorized personnel (e.g., DSMB/DMC, unblinded statistician) and require access controls per GCP and ICH E9.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for the most recent revision to the TLF shell specification (e.g., SAP amendment, protocol deviation, QC feedback, regulatory agency request). Required for audit trail integrity per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  csr_section_reference varchar [note: 'Section number within the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where this TLF output will be placed (e.g., Section 11.4.1, Appendix 16.1.1). Aligns with ICH E3 CSR structure for regulatory submission.']
  ctcae_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CTCAE grading scale applied in this TLF output for adverse event severity classification (e.g., CTCAE v5.0). Applicable to safety tables and listings presenting AE/SAE data.']
  display_format varchar [note: 'File format in which the TLF output is delivered (e.g., RTF for CSR word processing, PDF for regulatory submission, SAS7BDAT for data review). Drives output generation settings in SAS Drug Development.']
  footnotes varchar [note: 'Footnote text to be displayed beneath the TLF output in the CSR and submission package. Captures abbreviation definitions, statistical notes, data source qualifications, and population clarifications as specified in the SAP.']
  is_primary_endpoint boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output presents results for a primary efficacy or safety endpoint as defined in the study protocol and SAP. Primary endpoint outputs receive highest priority and regulatory scrutiny.']
  is_regulatory_submission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output is included in the regulatory submission package (NDA/BLA/MAA/eCTD). Submission outputs require additional QC validation and compliance with FDA/EMA/PMDA formatting standards.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the TLF shell specification record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation principles.']
  locf_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF) imputation method is applied in this TLF output for handling missing data. Specified in the SAP missing data handling section.']
  meddra_soc varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) associated with this TLF output when the output presents safety or adverse event data. Enables standardized safety reporting alignment with MedDRA coding hierarchy.']
  orientation varchar [note: 'Page orientation for the TLF output display (portrait or landscape). Determined by the number of columns and display width requirements; specified in the TLF shell for SAS programmer implementation.']
  output_category varchar [note: 'Functional grouping of the TLF output within the study (e.g., Demographics, Efficacy, Safety, Pharmacokinetics, Laboratory). Used to organize the TLF catalog and assign programming resources. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: demographics|efficacy|safety|pharmacokinetics|laboratory|vital_signs|exposure|disposition|concomitant_medications|other — promote to reference product]']
  output_file_name varchar [note: 'Expected file name of the produced TLF output artifact (e.g., t_14_1_demog.rtf, f_05_1_km.pdf). Used for output delivery tracking and eCTD submission package assembly.']
  output_finalized_date date [note: 'Date on which the TLF output was finalized and locked for inclusion in the CSR or regulatory submission. Aligns with the Database Lock (DBL) milestone and submission timeline.']
  output_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the TLF output (e.g., T-14.1, L-02.3, F-05.1). Used by SAS programmers and medical writers to reference outputs in the CSR and submission packages.']
  output_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the table, listing, or figure as it will appear in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) and regulatory submission. Authored per ICH E3 display conventions.']
  output_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the output as a Table, Listing, or Figure (TLF). Drives program template selection, display formatting rules, and CSR section placement in SAS Drug Development.']
  page_size varchar [note: 'Paper size specification for the TLF output (A4 for EMA/MHRA/TGA submissions; Letter for FDA submissions). Ensures regulatory submission formatting compliance across jurisdictions.']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Programming priority classification for this TLF output (Tier 1=primary efficacy/safety outputs required for DBL; Tier 2=secondary outputs; Tier 3=supportive/exploratory outputs). Drives resource allocation and delivery scheduling.']
  production_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TLF shell and its associated program in the production workflow. Tracks progression from initial shell authoring through QC review to final approved output for CSR inclusion.']
  program_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date by which the SAS program producing this TLF output is scheduled to be delivered for QC review. Used for project milestone tracking and CSR timeline management.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SAS program file responsible for producing this TLF output (e.g., t_14_1_demog.sas). Used as the SSOT for program-to-output traceability in SAS Drug Development and the TLF catalog.']
  program_path varchar [note: 'Full directory path to the SAS program file within the study programming environment (e.g., /studies/STUDY001/programs/tables/). Enables automated program execution and audit trail in SAS Drug Development.']
  qc_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the independent QC reviewer who validated the TLF shell specification and/or the produced output. Supports double-programming QC traceability per CDISC and GCP standards.']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'Section number or identifier within the SAP that specifies the statistical methodology for this TLF output (e.g., Section 5.3.2). Provides direct traceability from the output shell to the governing statistical specification.']
  shell_approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the TLF shell specification was formally approved for programming. Marks the transition from specification to active development in the TLF production lifecycle.']
  shell_created_date date [note: 'Date on which the TLF shell specification was first created in the system. Establishes the start of the output lifecycle for audit and project tracking purposes.']
  shell_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and instructions from the biostatistician to the SAS programmer regarding special handling, display requirements, or clarifications for this TLF output. Not displayed in the final output.']
  shell_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the TLF shell specification (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Tracks revisions to the shell driven by SAP amendments, protocol changes, or QC feedback. Enables version-controlled traceability.']
  statistical_method varchar [note: 'Description of the primary statistical method applied in this output (e.g., ANCOVA, Kaplan-Meier, descriptive statistics, logistic regression). Referenced from the SAP and used by SAS programmers to implement the correct analysis.']

  Note: 'Table, Listing, and Figure (TLF) shell specification for a study. Captures output number, title, footnotes, display population, statistical method reference, program name, output type (table/listing/figure), SAP section reference, priority tier, and production status. SSOT for TLF catalog consumed by SAS programmers and medical writers during CSR production.'
}

Table biostatistics.tlf_output {
  tlf_output_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each produced TLF output instance. Serves as the primary key for the tlf_output data product within the biostatistics domain.']
  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific ADaM dataset version used as input for generating this TLF output, ensuring full traceability from output back to source analysis data.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: tlf_output has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which population the output covers. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integrity and enables ']
  ctd_dossier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ctd_dossier. Business justification: TLF outputs are packaged into CTD Module 5 of the regulatory dossier. Regulatory publishing teams must track which TLF outputs are included in each dossier version. Direct FK supports dossier complete']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: TLF outputs can be produced specifically for interim analyses (interim_analysis_flag exists on tlf_output). Adding interim_analysis_id FK links the TLF output to the specific interim analysis it was p']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) version that governs the analysis methodology, population definitions, and output specifications for this TLF.']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: A TLF output instance is produced to present results for a specific statistical endpoint. One statistical endpoint can generate multiple TLF outputs across production runs, versions, and interim/final']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this TLF output was produced. Links the output to the governing study protocol and sponsor context.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Finalized TLF outputs (tables, listings, figures) are included in CTD Module 5 of NDA/BLA/MAA submissions. The tlf_output has \'submission_package_ref\' (plain text denormalization). This FK enables sub']
  tlf_shell_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TLF shell specification that defines the structure, layout, and metadata template from which this output was produced.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Final TLF outputs (tables, listings, figures) are filed as TMF documents within the CSR package in the eTMF. This link enables filing status tracking and inspection-readiness verification confirming e']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the analysis type this TLF output supports: primary (confirmatory), secondary, exploratory, interim (pre-specified), or sensitivity analysis. Determines the regulatory weight and reporting requirements for the output.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Final approval lifecycle status of this TLF output. Approved outputs are eligible for inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) and regulatory submissions. Superseded outputs have been replaced by a newer version.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the TLF output received formal approval, captured as an electronic signature event per 21 CFR Part 11 requirements. Null if not yet approved.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output was produced from blinded or unblinded data. Unblinded outputs are restricted to authorized personnel only and are subject to strict access controls to preserve trial integrity prior to database lock.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, production notes, known limitations, or reviewer comments associated with this TLF output instance. Supports communication between programmers, biostatisticians, and medical writers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this TLF output record was first created in the system, capturing the initial registration of the production run. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  csr_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where this TLF output is cited or embedded (e.g., Section 11.4.1, Appendix 16.1.9). Supports CSR authoring traceability per ICH E3 structure.']
  dataset_lock_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output was produced from a pre-database lock (DBL), post-database lock, or interim analysis dataset. Critical for distinguishing final regulatory submission outputs from exploratory or interim analyses.']
  dataset_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the ADaM dataset used as input for this TLF production run (e.g., v1.0, v2.3-interim). Ensures traceability between the output and the exact data snapshot used, critical for database lock (DBL) compliance.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which this TLF output was formally delivered to the sponsor, regulatory team, or CSR authoring team. Used for milestone tracking and contractual deliverable compliance.']
  discrepancy_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrepancies identified between the primary programmer output and the QC programmer output during validation. A count of zero indicates a clean validation pass. Non-zero counts require resolution before final approval.']
  discrepancy_resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how identified discrepancies between primary and QC programmer outputs were investigated and resolved. Required when discrepancy_count is greater than zero to support audit trail and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  is_final_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this TLF output instance is the final approved version intended for inclusion in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) or regulatory submission. Only one version per output_number should have this flag set to True.']
  output_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the TLF output indicating the statistical domain it covers (e.g., demographics, efficacy, safety, pharmacokinetics, laboratory). Used for organizing outputs within the CSR and regulatory submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: demographics|efficacy|safety|pharmacokinetics|laboratory|vital_signs|exposure|disposition|concomitant_medications|other — promote to reference product]']
  output_file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the produced TLF output (e.g., RTF for Word-compatible tables, PDF for final regulatory submissions, SAS7BDAT for dataset outputs). Determines compatibility with CSR authoring tools and eCTD submission requirements.']
  output_file_name varchar [note: 'Name of the output file generated by the SAS program (e.g., t_14_1_1_demog.rtf, l_16_2_3_ae.pdf). Used for file management, eTMF archival, and regulatory submission packaging.']
  output_file_path varchar [note: 'Full repository or file system path to the stored TLF output file in SAS Drug Development or the eTMF system. Enables direct retrieval for CSR authoring, regulatory review, and inspection readiness.']
  output_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this TLF output as defined in the SAP or TLF shell specification (e.g., T-14.1.1, L-16.2.3, F-11.4). Used for cross-referencing in the Clinical Study Report (CSR).']
  output_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the TLF output as it appears in the header of the produced document (e.g., \'Summary of Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — Safety Population\'). Used for CSR cross-referencing and regulatory submission.']
  output_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the statistical output as a Table (T), Listing (L), or Figure (F), corresponding to the three standard output categories in clinical trial reporting per ICH E3 and CDISC conventions.']
  output_version varchar [note: 'Version label of this TLF output instance (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, vFinal). Tracks the production history of the output across multiple runs, enabling identification of the current approved version versus superseded drafts.']
  production_environment varchar [note: 'Environment in which this TLF output was generated: development (exploratory/testing), validation (UAT), or production (regulatory-grade). Only production-environment outputs are eligible for regulatory submission.']
  production_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SAS program was executed to produce this TLF output instance. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the production lifecycle, supporting ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.']
  qc_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the QC validation process for this TLF output was completed and the qc_status was set to a terminal state (passed, failed, or waived). Supports milestone tracking and SLA compliance.']
  qc_status varchar [note: 'Current QC validation status of this TLF output. Indicates whether the output has passed independent validation, is pending review, is under active review, has failed validation, or has been formally waived per documented justification.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Sequential run number for this TLF output, incremented each time the SAS program is executed for the same output_number. Enables tracking of the full production history from first run to final delivery.']
  sas_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SAS program file used to produce this TLF output in SAS Drug Development (e.g., t_14_1_1_demog.sas). Provides full program traceability for audit and replication purposes.']
  sas_program_path varchar [note: 'Full file system or repository path to the SAS program in SAS Drug Development used to produce this TLF output. Supports traceability and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  sas_program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the SAS program used to generate this output, as tracked in SAS Drug Development version control. Enables exact reproduction of results and supports regulatory audit trails.']
  unblinding_authorization_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the formal unblinding authorization (e.g., DSMB/DMC recommendation, sponsor authorization memo) that permitted production of this unblinded TLF output. Required for audit trail when blinding_status is \'unblinded\'.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this TLF output record was last modified, tracking any updates to status, QC results, approval, or metadata. Required for audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  validation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to validate the accuracy of this TLF output. Double programming involves an independent programmer replicating the output from scratch; peer review involves a qualified reviewer checking the program and output without independent replication.']

  Note: 'Produced TLF output instance generated from a TLF shell specification. Captures output file reference, production run date, SAS program name and version, dataset version used, programmer, QC programmer, validation method (double programming, peer review), QC status, discrepancy count, final approval status, and delivery date. Tracks the production lifecycle of each statistical output from first run to final delivery, including full program traceability.'
}

Table biostatistics.interim_analysis {
  interim_analysis_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each interim analysis record within the clinical trial biostatistics domain.']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: interim_analysis has analysis_population (STRING) indicating which population was analyzed at the interim. Normalizing to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id enforces referential integr']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: Interim analyses must operate under a defined blinding configuration governing firewall maintenance, unblinding scope, and independent statistician access. ICH E9(R1) and DSMB charters require that in']
  commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.commitment. Business justification: Post-approval interim analyses are often mandated by regulatory commitments (post-marketing study conditions of approval). Direct FK from interim_analysis to commitment supports commitment fulfillment']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: An interim analysis is performed on data that has been locked via a database lock event. The FK links the interim analysis to the specific DBL event that defined the data cut, enabling audit traceabil']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: Interim analyses are planned at epoch boundaries (end of treatment epoch, end of follow-up). This FK enables DSMB meeting scheduling aligned to epoch completion, alpha-spending function application at']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: Interim analyses are conducted on subjects randomized under a specific rand_list version active at the data cutoff date. DSMB reporting requires the interim analysis to cite the exact randomization li']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Adaptive trial interim analyses (sample size re-estimation, population enrichment) directly trigger protocol amendments submitted to health authorities. The interim_analysis has \'adaptation_descriptio']
  safety_report_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.safety_report_submission. Business justification: Interim analyses generating safety signals trigger expedited IND safety reports (DSURs, SUSARs). The interim_analysis has \'regulatory_submission_required\' flag. This FK links the interim analysis data']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) version that governs the methodology and pre-specified rules for this interim analysis.']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: Each interim analysis uses a specific locked SDTM dataset version at the data cutoff date. Data managers and statisticians must trace which SDTM dataset was used for each interim analysis for audit, r']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: An interim analysis evaluates one or more statistical endpoints defined in the SAP. Linking interim_analysis to statistical_endpoint provides direct traceability of which endpoint was assessed at each']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study for which this interim analysis is conducted.']
  study_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_milestone. Business justification: Interim analyses are tracked as critical-path milestones in CTMS. Linking interim_analysis to study_milestone enables integrated timeline management, contractual milestone reporting to sponsors, and r']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Interim efficacy/safety data from adaptive trials may be submitted to health authorities. The interim_analysis has \'regulatory_submission_required\' flag; linking to submission tracks which submission ']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Interim analysis statistical reports and data packages are essential TMF documents filed per GCP requirements. Linking interim_analysis to tmf_document enables TMF completeness tracking and traceabili']
  triggered_signal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.signal. Business justification: Interim analyses may formally confirm or trigger a safety signal that meets stopping criteria. Role-prefix \'triggered_\' distinguishes this from a general signal reference; DSMB packages and regulatory']
  actual_analysis_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the interim analysis was executed and results were produced. May differ from planned date due to enrollment pace or operational factors.']
  adam_dataset_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the CDISC ADaM datasets used as input to this interim analysis, ensuring traceability between the analysis results and the underlying data snapshot.']
  adaptation_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any pre-specified design adaptation implemented as a result of this interim analysis (e.g., sample size re-estimation, dose selection, population enrichment). Populated only when analysis_outcome is \'adapt\'.']
  alpha_allocated decimal(8,6) [note: 'The portion of the overall one-sided or two-sided alpha (Type I error rate) allocated to this specific interim look by the alpha spending function. Used to derive the efficacy stopping boundary.']
  alpha_spending_function varchar [note: 'The pre-specified alpha spending function used to allocate the overall Type I error rate across interim looks, as defined in the SAP. Common functions include O\'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets O\'Brien-Fleming approximation, Lan-DeMets Pocock approximation, Hwang-Shih-DeCani, or a custom function.']
  alpha_spending_parameter decimal(8,6) [note: 'Numeric parameter (gamma) for the selected alpha spending function, where applicable (e.g., the gamma parameter for Hwang-Shih-DeCani function). Null for functions that do not require a parameter.']
  analysis_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this interim analysis, used in regulatory submissions, DSMB/DMC charters, and TMF documentation (e.g., IA-STUDY001-001).']
  analysis_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal number identifying this interim analysis within the study (e.g., 1 for first interim, 2 for second interim). Used to track the series of planned looks at accumulating data.']
  analysis_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal decision or recommendation resulting from the interim analysis: continue (study proceeds as planned), stop_efficacy (early termination for demonstrated benefit), stop_futility (early termination for lack of benefit), stop_safety (early termination for safety concerns), adapt (pre-specified design adaptation implemented), or pending (DSMB/DMC recommendation not yet issued).']
  analysis_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the interim analysis: planned (scheduled but not yet initiated), in_progress (analysis underway), completed (results finalized), cancelled (analysis removed from plan), or on_hold (temporarily suspended).']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interim analysis by its primary statistical purpose: efficacy (test for early stopping due to benefit), futility (test for early stopping due to lack of benefit), safety (review of accumulating safety data), adaptive (pre-specified adaptation of design parameters), combined_efficacy_futility, or administrative (data review without hypothesis testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|futility|safety|adaptive|combined_efficacy_futility|administrative — promote to reference product]']
  conditional_power decimal(5,4) [note: 'Probability of achieving a statistically significant result at the final analysis, conditional on the data observed at this interim look and assuming the originally hypothesized treatment effect. Used in futility assessments.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the interim analysis results and associated outputs, controlling access and distribution. Interim analysis results are typically restricted to prevent unblinding of the broader study team.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interim analysis record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  data_cutoff_date date [note: 'The date through which subject data was included in the interim analysis dataset. All data collected after this date is excluded from the analysis. Critical for regulatory traceability and blinding integrity.']
  dsmb_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) reviewed the unblinded interim analysis results (True = DSMB/DMC reviewed; False = internal review only).']
  dsmb_meeting_date date [note: 'Date on which the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) convened to review the interim analysis results and issue a recommendation.']
  efficacy_boundary_value decimal(10,6) [note: 'Pre-specified critical value (e.g., z-statistic or p-value threshold) for the efficacy stopping boundary at this interim look. If the observed test statistic exceeds this boundary, early stopping for efficacy is triggered.']
  events_observed varchar [note: 'Number of primary endpoint events (e.g., deaths, disease progression events) observed as of the data cutoff date. Applicable for event-driven trials; used to compute the actual information fraction.']
  events_planned_total varchar [note: 'Total number of primary endpoint events required for the final analysis as specified in the SAP and sample size calculation. Used as the denominator for the information fraction in event-driven trials.']
  firewall_maintained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a blinding firewall was maintained between the unblinded interim analysis team and the blinded study team (True = firewall in place; False = firewall not maintained or not applicable). Critical for regulatory compliance in adaptive trials.']
  futility_boundary_binding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the futility stopping boundary is binding (True = mandatory stop if crossed; False = non-binding/advisory only). Binding futility boundaries affect the overall Type I error calculation.']
  futility_boundary_value decimal(10,6) [note: 'Pre-specified critical value for the futility stopping boundary at this interim look. If the observed test statistic falls below this boundary, early stopping for futility (lack of efficacy) may be triggered. May be binding or non-binding per the SAP.']
  information_fraction_actual decimal(5,4) [note: 'Observed proportion of total planned statistical information actually accumulated at the time of this interim analysis. May differ from planned fraction due to enrollment variability or event rate changes.']
  information_fraction_planned decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pre-specified proportion of the total planned statistical information (e.g., events, subjects) accumulated at the time of this interim analysis, expressed as a decimal between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.5000 = 50% of planned events). Drives alpha spending calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, operational notes, deviations from the pre-specified plan, or DSMB/DMC commentary relevant to this interim analysis that is not captured in structured fields.']
  observed_p_value decimal(10,8) [note: 'The nominal p-value from the primary hypothesis test at this interim analysis. Must be interpreted in the context of the alpha spending boundary, not as a standalone significance threshold.']
  observed_test_statistic decimal(10,6) [note: 'The actual computed test statistic (e.g., z-score, chi-square, log-rank statistic) from the interim analysis dataset. Compared against efficacy and futility boundaries to determine the outcome.']
  planned_analysis_date date [note: 'Pre-specified calendar date on which the interim analysis is planned to be conducted, as documented in the SAP and DSMB/DMC charter.']
  regulatory_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interim analysis results or the resulting design adaptation must be submitted to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.) as part of an IND, NDA, BLA, or MAA amendment (True = submission required; False = not required).']
  results_disclosed_externally boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interim analysis results have been disclosed externally (e.g., to regulators, in a public registry, or in a publication) prior to study completion (True = disclosed; False = not disclosed). Tracks blinding integrity at the study level.']
  revised_sample_size varchar [note: 'Updated total sample size determined through the pre-specified sample size re-estimation procedure at this interim analysis. Null if sample_size_reestimated is False.']
  sample_size_reestimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a pre-specified sample size re-estimation was performed as part of this interim analysis (True = sample size re-estimated; False = no re-estimation performed).']
  sas_program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the SAS Drug Development program or analysis package used to execute this interim analysis, enabling reproducibility and audit traceability.']
  stopping_rule_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific stopping rule(s) triggered at this interim analysis, including the criterion crossed and the observed vs. boundary values. Populated only when stopping_rule_triggered is True.']
  stopping_rule_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any pre-specified stopping rule (efficacy, futility, or safety) was triggered by the results of this interim analysis (True = stopping rule crossed; False = no stopping rule triggered).']
  subjects_enrolled_at_cutoff varchar [note: 'Total number of subjects enrolled in the study as of the data cutoff date for this interim analysis. Provides context for the information fraction and statistical power.']
  unblinding_authorization_date date [note: 'Date on which formal authorization was granted to unblind the interim analysis data, as documented in the unblinding log. Required for audit trail and blinding integrity compliance.']
  unblinding_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the extent of treatment assignment unblinding performed for this interim analysis: full (all treatment assignments revealed to the analysis team), partial (only aggregate or summary-level unblinding), or none (blinded analysis only). Critical for blinding integrity management and regulatory compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this interim analysis record was most recently modified, supporting audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'Records planned and executed interim analyses for a study. Captures interim analysis number, type (efficacy, futility, safety, adaptive), planned information fraction, actual analysis date, DSMB/DMC involvement flag, unblinding scope (full/partial/none), stopping rules triggered, outcome (continue/stop/adapt), alpha spending function parameters, and confidentiality controls. Critical for regulatory compliance and blinding integrity management.'
}

Table biostatistics.database_lock_event {
  database_lock_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for database_lock_event']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: DBL SOPs require documenting which blinding configuration was in effect at database lock — confirming whether lock occurred under blinded or unblinded conditions. Regulatory inspectors verify this for']
  dmp_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.dmp. Business justification: The DMP version in effect at time of lock is a regulatory traceability requirement (ICH E6 R2, 21 CFR Part 11). The DBL event must reference the governing DMP to confirm data collection and review pro']
  edc_study_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.edc_study_build. Business justification: A DBL event locks a specific EDC study build version; this is operationally critical for audit trails and unlock procedures. edc_system_lock_reference is a denormalized string reference to the build, ']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: Pre-lock QC checklists require reconciliation of all randomized subjects in the locked dataset against the active rand_list version. The subjects_locked_count must match the rand_list\'s allocated_slot']
  regulatory_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_milestone. Business justification: Database lock is a critical regulatory milestone tracked in regulatory timelines for NDA/BLA submission planning. Direct FK from database_lock_event to regulatory_milestone supports critical path anal']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: A database lock event is executed against a specific version of the Statistical Analysis Plan. The SAP version in effect at the time of DBL governs which analyses are performed post-lock. Linking data']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this database lock event applies. Links the DBL event to the parent study master record.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Database lock events directly trigger and gate regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA filing, interim analysis submissions). The DBL date is a prerequisite for submission package finalization. Direct FK enab']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The database lock memo/certificate is a required TMF essential document (DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 4). Replacing the denormalized lock_memo_reference string with a proper FK to tmf_document enables']
  actual_lock_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the database was formally locked. This is the principal business event date for the DBL milestone and is recorded in the eTMF, regulatory submissions, and CSR statistical sections.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential amendment number for post-lock data corrections. Zero for the original lock; increments with each formal unlock and re-lock cycle. Tracks the amendment history of the locked database.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Formal description of the reason for unlocking and re-locking the database after the initial lock. Documents the nature of the data correction or update requiring the amendment. Null for the original lock (amendment_number = 0).']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'The type of statistical analysis for which this database lock is being performed. Determines the downstream workflow: interim analyses feed DSMB/DMC reviews; final analyses feed CSR and regulatory submissions (NDA/BLA/MAA).']
  biostatistics_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the Biostatistics representative provided electronic authorization for the database lock. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  coding_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all medical coding assignments (MedDRA for adverse events/medical history, WHO-DD for concomitant medications) have been completed and verified in the EDC system prior to database lock.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this database lock event record was first created in the lakehouse (Silver Layer). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ standards.']
  data_cut_date date [note: 'The clinical data cut-off date defining which subject data and observations are included in this lock. All data with a collection date on or before this date is included in the locked dataset. Critical for interim and partial locks.']
  dbl_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for this database lock event (e.g., DBL-2024-001). Used in regulatory submissions, TMF filings, and cross-system references in Medidata Rave and Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  dm_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the Data Management representative provided electronic authorization for the database lock. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  dsmb_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the DSMB/DMC was formally notified of the database lock and provided access to the locked dataset for their review.']
  dsmb_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) must be notified of this database lock event, typically for interim analysis locks where the DSMB reviews unblinded data.']
  lock_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of data included in this lock event. Full study locks the entire study database; interim locks a defined data cut for interim analysis; partial locks a subset of data (e.g., specific sites or forms); cohort locks a defined patient cohort; site-specific locks data from one or more named sites.']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the database lock event. Tracks progression from planning through execution and any subsequent unlock/re-lock cycles required for data corrections or amendments.']
  lock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the database lock was executed in the EDC system (Medidata Rave). Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail compliance and ALCOA+ data integrity standards.']
  lock_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lock is a hard lock (no further data changes permitted without formal unlock and re-lock procedure) or a soft lock (data is locked for analysis but minor corrections may still be applied under controlled conditions). Hard locks are required prior to final DBL and regulatory submission.']
  medical_monitor_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the Medical Monitor provided electronic authorization for the database lock. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or operational notes related to this database lock event that are not captured in structured fields. Includes deviations from planned timelines or special circumstances.']
  outstanding_query_count varchar [note: 'Number of open data queries (discrepancies) remaining unresolved in the EDC system (Medidata Rave) at the time of database lock. A non-zero count must be formally justified and documented per GCP and ALCOA+ requirements.']
  outstanding_query_justification varchar [note: 'Formal written justification for proceeding with database lock when outstanding queries remain unresolved. Required by GCP and ALCOA+ when outstanding_query_count is greater than zero. Documented in the DMP and lock memo.']
  planned_lock_date date [note: 'The originally scheduled calendar date for the database lock event as defined in the Data Management Plan (DMP) and study timeline. Used for milestone tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS and project planning.']
  pre_lock_qc_checklist_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all items on the pre-database lock Quality Control (QC) checklist have been completed and signed off prior to lock execution. The checklist covers data cleaning, query resolution, coding completion, and SAE reconciliation.']
  pre_lock_qc_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the pre-database lock QC checklist was formally completed and all items signed off. Must precede the actual lock date.']
  sae_reconciliation_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation between the EDC system (Medidata Rave) and the safety database (Oracle Argus Safety) for Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) has been completed and confirmed prior to database lock.']
  sdv_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Source Data Verification (SDV) has been completed to the required percentage or scope across all sites prior to database lock. SDV confirms EDC data matches source documents per GCP requirements.']
  sites_locked_count varchar [note: 'Total number of investigative sites whose data is included in this database lock event. Relevant for partial and site-specific lock scopes.']
  study_part varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific study part, period, or epoch covered by this lock event (e.g., Part A, Part B, Period 1, Run-in, Treatment, Follow-up). Applicable for multi-part or adaptive study designs.']
  subjects_locked_count varchar [note: 'Total number of study subjects whose data is included in this database lock event. Used for completeness verification and comparison against enrollment targets defined in the SAP.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'The date on which treatment assignment unblinding was performed following the database lock. Null if unblinding_required is false or unblinding has not yet occurred.']
  unblinding_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether treatment unblinding is required as part of or immediately following this database lock event. Applicable for blinded studies where the lock precedes the formal unblinding procedure for final or interim analysis.']
  unlock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which the database was formally unlocked to permit post-lock data corrections. Null if the database has not been unlocked. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this database lock event record was last modified in the lakehouse (Silver Layer). Tracks record-level changes for audit trail and data lineage compliance.']

  Note: 'Database Lock (DBL) event record for a study or study part. Captures lock scope (full study, interim, partial), planned and actual lock date, pre-lock QC checklist completion status, outstanding query count at lock, lock authorization approvers (DM, biostatistics, medical monitor), lock type (hard/soft), and post-lock amendment records. SSOT for DBL milestone consumed by biostatistics, data management, and regulatory domains.'
}

Table biostatistics.statistical_endpoint {
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each statistical endpoint record defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Primary key for the statistical_endpoint data product.']
  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.adam_dataset_spec. Business justification: statistical_endpoint has adam_dataset_name (STRING) referencing the ADaM dataset used for the endpoint analysis. Normalizing to a FK to adam_dataset_spec.adam_dataset_spec_id creates a proper referent']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: statistical_endpoint has analysis_population as a STRING field referencing the population for the endpoint analysis. Normalizing this to a FK to analysis_population.analysis_population_id eliminates t']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Regulatory approvals are granted based on specific statistical endpoints (approved_indication references endpoint results). Direct FK from statistical_endpoint to approval supports post-approval endpo']
  estimand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.estimand. Business justification: Per ICH E9(R1), each statistical endpoint is defined in the context of an estimand. statistical_endpoint has estimand_treatment_policy (STRING) which is a partial reference to the estimand. Adding est']
  protocol_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_endpoint. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) and regulatory submissions require explicit traceability from SAP statistical endpoints to protocol-defined endpoints. Statisticians map each protocol endpoint to its statistical operationa']
  randomization_treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Statistical endpoints are defined for specific treatment comparisons referencing the control arm and active arm. The non_inferiority_margin, effect_measure, and hypothesis_type on the endpoint are def']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) document version in which this endpoint is formally defined. Links endpoint traceability from SAP authoring through TLF production.']
  special_designation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.special_designation. Business justification: Surrogate endpoints used for accelerated approval (special_designation.surrogate_endpoint_used) map to specific statistical endpoints. Direct FK supports accelerated approval endpoint validation, FDA/']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this statistical endpoint is defined. Enables endpoint-level traceability to the study protocol.']
  tlf_shell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.tlf_shell. Business justification: statistical_endpoint has tlf_reference (STRING) pointing to the TLF shell that displays the endpoint results. Normalizing this to a FK to tlf_shell.tlf_shell_id creates a proper referential link betwe']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Statistical endpoint specifications are filed as part of the SAP in the eTMF. Linking statistical_endpoint to its governing TMF document enables inspection-readiness checks confirming each endpoint is']
  alpha_spending_function varchar [note: 'Alpha spending function used to allocate Type I error across interim and final analyses for this endpoint when interim_analysis_flag is True: O\'Brien-Fleming, Pocock, Lan-DeMets, Hwang-Shih-DeCani, or none. Defined in the SAP to control overall Type I error.']
  alternative_hypothesis varchar [note: 'Formal statement of the alternative hypothesis (H1) for this endpoint as specified in the SAP (e.g., \'Overall survival is superior in the treatment arm compared to control\'). Defines the direction of the test (superiority, non-inferiority, equivalence).']
  analysis_timepoint varchar [note: 'Scheduled visit or time window at which the endpoint is measured or evaluated (e.g., \'Week 24\', \'End of Treatment\', \'Month 12\'). May represent a single timepoint or a range as defined in the SAP.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this endpoint definition was formally approved in the SAP by the biostatistics lead and sponsor. Must precede database lock (DBL) to satisfy pre-specification requirements for confirmatory endpoints.']
  confidence_interval_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Pre-specified confidence interval level for the treatment effect estimate (e.g., 95.00 for a 95% CI, 97.50 for a one-sided 97.5% CI). Defined in the SAP and reported in TLFs and regulatory submissions.']
  covariates varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of pre-specified covariates and stratification factors included in the statistical model for this endpoint (e.g., \'baseline_value, region, age_group\'). Defined in the SAP per EMA guideline on adjustment for baseline covariates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this statistical endpoint record was first created in the system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  csr_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Clinical Study Report (CSR) section where this endpoint\'s results are reported (e.g., \'Section 11.4.1\'). Supports traceability from SAP endpoint definition to the final regulatory submission document per ICH E3.']
  effect_measure varchar [note: 'The statistical measure used to quantify the treatment effect for this endpoint (e.g., \'Mean Difference\', \'Hazard Ratio\', \'Odds Ratio\', \'Risk Difference\', \'Least Squares Mean Difference\'). Specified in the SAP and reported in TLFs and the CSR.']
  endpoint_classification varchar [note: 'Hierarchical classification of the endpoint within the study\'s inferential framework: primary (confirmatory, controls Type I error), secondary (supportive), exploratory (hypothesis-generating), or safety (adverse event-related). Drives multiplicity adjustment strategy.']
