  endpoint_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the endpoint, used in SAP documents, TLF shells, and regulatory submissions. Follows the pattern EP-{STUDY_CODE}-{SEQ}.']
  endpoint_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the statistical endpoint as stated in the SAP, including the precise definition of the outcome measure, population, and timeframe.']
  endpoint_name varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable name of the statistical endpoint as defined in the SAP (e.g., \'Overall Survival\', \'Change from Baseline in HbA1c at Week 24\'). Used in TLF titles and regulatory submissions.']
  endpoint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the statistical endpoint definition within the SAP authoring and versioning workflow: draft (being authored), under_review (in statistical/medical review), approved (finalized in SAP), locked (post-database lock, immutable), superseded (replaced by a newer version), or retired (no longer active).']
  endpoint_type varchar [note: 'Scientific domain classification of the endpoint: efficacy (clinical benefit), pharmacokinetic (PK - drug exposure), pharmacodynamic (PD - drug effect), biomarker (biological marker), patient_reported_outcome (PRO - patient self-report), or safety. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: efficacy|pharmacokinetic|pharmacodynamic|biomarker|patient_reported_outcome|safety — promote to reference product]']
  hypothesis_type varchar [note: 'Type of statistical hypothesis being tested for this endpoint: superiority (treatment better than control), non-inferiority (treatment not worse by a defined margin), equivalence (treatment within bounds of control), or exploratory (no formal hypothesis). Drives regulatory interpretation of results.']
  interim_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this endpoint is included in one or more pre-specified interim analyses (e.g., for Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) review or adaptive design decision rules). True if the endpoint is evaluated at interim; False if final analysis only.']
  locked_date date [note: 'Date on which this endpoint definition was locked following database lock (DBL), rendering it immutable for regulatory submission purposes. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  measurement_scale varchar [note: 'Statistical measurement scale of the endpoint variable: continuous (numeric), binary (two outcomes), categorical (nominal groups), ordinal (ranked categories), or time_to_event (survival/event-time). Determines the appropriate statistical test method.']
  missing_data_method varchar [note: 'Pre-specified method for handling missing data for this endpoint (e.g., \'Multiple Imputation\', \'Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF)\', \'Baseline Observation Carried Forward (BOCF)\', \'Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM)\', \'Complete Case Analysis\'). Defined in the SAP per ICH E9(R1). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: multiple_imputation|locf|bocf|mmrm|complete_case|worst_case — promote to reference product]']
  multiplicity_adjustment_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method applied to control the family-wise error rate (FWER) or false discovery rate (FDR) when testing multiple endpoints: Bonferroni, Hochberg, Holm, Benjamini-Hochberg, fixed-sequence, gatekeeping, or none. Required for regulatory submissions with multiple primary or secondary endpoints. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bonferroni|hochberg|holm|benjamini_hochberg|fixed_sequence|gatekeeping|none — promote to reference product]']
  multiplicity_testing_family varchar [note: 'Name or label of the multiplicity testing family or hierarchy to which this endpoint belongs (e.g., \'Primary Family\', \'Secondary Family 1\'). Used to group endpoints within a gatekeeping or hierarchical testing procedure defined in the SAP.']
  non_inferiority_margin decimal(18,6) [note: 'Pre-specified non-inferiority margin (delta) for non-inferiority or equivalence endpoints, representing the largest clinically acceptable difference between treatment and control. Required by FDA and EMA for non-inferiority trial designs.']
  null_hypothesis varchar [note: 'Formal statement of the null hypothesis (H0) for this endpoint as specified in the SAP (e.g., \'There is no difference in overall survival between treatment and control arms\'). Required for confirmatory endpoints per ICH E9.']
  regulatory_designation varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority-specific designation of this endpoint indicating its role in submissions to FDA, EMA, or both. Enables endpoint-level traceability for multi-regional regulatory submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fda_primary|ema_primary|fda_secondary|ema_secondary|fda_exploratory|ema_exploratory|global — promote to reference product]']
  sap_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SAP document in which this endpoint was defined or last modified (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\'). Supports version-controlled traceability of endpoint definitions across SAP amendments prior to database lock.']
  sensitivity_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more pre-specified sensitivity analyses are defined for this endpoint in the SAP to assess the robustness of the primary analysis results. True if sensitivity analyses are planned; False otherwise.']
  significance_level decimal(6,4) [note: 'Pre-specified Type I error rate (alpha) for this endpoint, typically 0.05 for primary endpoints or adjusted values for multiplicity-controlled secondary endpoints. Defined in the SAP prior to database lock.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this endpoint definition was sourced or managed (e.g., SAS Drug Development for SAP authoring, Veeva Vault for document management). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  statistical_test_method varchar [note: 'The primary statistical test or model used to analyze this endpoint (e.g., \'ANCOVA\', \'Log-rank test\', \'Cox proportional hazards model\', \'Mixed Model for Repeated Measures (MMRM)\', \'Wilcoxon rank-sum test\'). Specified in the SAP and implemented in SAS Drug Development. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ancova|log_rank|cox_ph|mmrm|wilcoxon|logistic_regression|cmh — promote to reference product]']
  subgroup_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether pre-specified subgroup analyses are defined for this endpoint in the SAP (e.g., by age, sex, region, disease severity). True if subgroup analyses are planned; False otherwise. Relevant for FDA and EMA subgroup reporting requirements.']
  testing_sequence_order varchar [note: 'Numeric position of this endpoint within the pre-specified hierarchical or fixed-sequence testing procedure. Determines the order in which endpoints are tested to control Type I error. Lower values are tested first.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this statistical endpoint record was last modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance for SAP version control.']

  Note: 'Master record for each statistical endpoint defined in the SAP for a study. Captures endpoint name, classification (primary, secondary, exploratory, safety), endpoint type (efficacy, PK, PD, biomarker, PRO), measurement scale, analysis timepoint(s), statistical test method, null hypothesis, multiplicity adjustment method (Bonferroni, Hochberg, Holm), and regulatory designation. Enables endpoint-level traceability from SAP to TLF.'
}

Table biostatistics.estimand {
  estimand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the estimand definition record within the biostatistics data product. Primary key for the estimand entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.adam_dataset_spec. Business justification: estimand has adam_dataset_name (STRING) referencing the ADaM dataset that implements the estimand analysis. Normalizing to a FK to adam_dataset_spec.adam_dataset_spec_id creates a proper referential l']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: An estimand is defined for a specific analysis population (target population is a core component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework). estimand has analysis_population_code (STRING) as a text referen']
  endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_endpoint. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) defines estimands as operationalizations of specific clinical endpoints. Regulatory submissions require explicit estimand-to-endpoint traceability. estimand.endpoint_name is a denormalized ']
  objective_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.objective. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) requires each estimand to directly address a specific study objective. Regulatory submissions and SAPs must demonstrate estimand-to-objective traceability. estimand already links to study.e']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_protocol_amendment. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) requires estimand updates when protocol amendments change intercurrent event strategies or populations. Direct FK from estimand to regulatory_protocol_amendment supports amendment impact as']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) document version in which this estimand is formally specified and aligned. Ensures traceability between the estimand definition and the governing SAP.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this estimand is defined. Links the estimand to its parent protocol and study-level metadata.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) estimands must be formally documented in the SAP filed in the TMF. Linking estimand to its TMF document provides direct regulatory traceability for inspectors verifying that each estimand i']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) requires each estimand to define the treatment comparison explicitly. Biostatisticians and regulators reviewing the estimand framework expect a direct FK to the treatment_arm being compared']
  adam_parameter_code varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM PARAMCD value identifying the specific analysis parameter within the ADaM dataset that corresponds to this estimand\'s endpoint variable. Enables direct linkage to TLF outputs and CDISC submission datasets.']
  alpha_level decimal(6,5) [note: 'The pre-specified significance level (Type I error rate) allocated to this estimand in the confirmatory testing procedure (e.g., 0.05000, 0.02500 for two-sided tests). Null for exploratory estimands.']
  analysis_timepoint varchar [note: 'The primary timepoint or time window at which the endpoint variable is assessed for this estimand (e.g., \'Week 24\', \'End of Treatment\', \'Month 12\', \'Event-driven at 300 events\'). Defines the temporal scope of the estimand.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this estimand definition was formally approved in the SAP authoring workflow. Establishes the regulatory-effective date for the estimand specification.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the estimand analysis is conducted under blinded, unblinded, or open-label conditions. Relevant for interim analyses and DSMB/DMC review packages where unblinding records must be maintained.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this estimand record was first created in the SAS Drug Development / biostatistics data system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  csr_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the section of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where results for this estimand are reported (e.g., \'Section 11.4.1\'). Supports ICH E3 CSR structure compliance and regulatory submission traceability.']
  effect_direction varchar [note: 'The inferential objective of the estimand — whether the analysis is designed to demonstrate superiority, non-inferiority, or equivalence of the treatment condition relative to the comparator.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the estimand definition is operationally effective for analysis execution. Used for version control when estimands are amended mid-study.']
  endpoint_type varchar [note: 'Statistical nature of the endpoint variable. Determines the appropriate population-level summary measure and analysis methodology (e.g., continuous endpoints use mean difference; time-to-event use hazard ratio).']
  estimand_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the estimand, used in SAP documents, regulatory submissions, and cross-functional communications. Follows the pattern EST-{STUDYCODE}{SEQ}.']
  estimand_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the estimand definition record within the SAP authoring and regulatory submission workflow. Drives version control and approval gating in SAS Drug Development.']
  estimand_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the estimand within the study\'s endpoint hierarchy. Determines regulatory weight and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: primary|key_secondary|secondary|exploratory|sensitivity — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  intercurrent_event_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the specific intercurrent event(s) addressed by this estimand (e.g., \'Discontinuation of study treatment due to adverse event\', \'Initiation of rescue medication\'). Provides regulatory-submission-ready documentation.']
  intercurrent_event_strategy varchar [note: 'The primary strategy applied to handle intercurrent events (ICEs) for this estimand, per ICH E9(R1) Section 3.2. Defines how post-ICE data are treated in the analysis (e.g., treatment policy ignores ICE occurrence; hypothetical posits ICE did not occur).']
  interim_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this estimand is evaluated at one or more pre-specified interim analyses (e.g., for DSMB/DMC review, adaptive design decision points, or early stopping rules).']
  is_confirmatory boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this estimand is part of the confirmatory (inferential) testing hierarchy, as opposed to exploratory or descriptive analyses. Confirmatory estimands are subject to multiplicity control procedures defined in the SAP.']
  is_primary_endpoint boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this estimand corresponds to the study\'s primary endpoint. Primary endpoint estimands carry the highest regulatory weight and drive sample size calculations and confirmatory testing procedures.']
  label varchar [note: 'Short human-readable label or title for the estimand (e.g., \'Primary Efficacy Estimand\', \'Key Secondary Estimand 1\'). Used in TLF headers, CSR statistical sections, and regulatory submission narratives.']
  missing_data_strategy varchar [note: 'The statistical strategy applied to handle missing outcome data for this estimand (e.g., Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF), multiple imputation, mixed model for repeated measures). Distinct from the ICE strategy — addresses data gaps rather than post-ICE observations.']
  multiplicity_adjustment_method varchar [note: 'The statistical method used to control Type I error across multiple estimands or endpoints in the confirmatory testing hierarchy (e.g., \'Bonferroni\', \'Hochberg\', \'Hierarchical testing\', \'Gatekeeping procedure\'). Null if estimand is exploratory.']
  non_inferiority_margin decimal(18,6) [note: 'The pre-specified non-inferiority margin (delta) for this estimand, applicable when the effect direction is non-inferiority. Expressed in the units of the summary measure. Null for superiority or equivalence estimands.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional biostatistician commentary, regulatory discussion points, or cross-references not captured in structured fields. Supports regulatory submission narrative preparation and internal review discussions.']
  population_level_summary varchar [note: 'The \'Summary Measure\' component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework — the statistical measure used to summarize the endpoint at the population level (e.g., \'Difference in means\', \'Odds ratio\', \'Hazard ratio\', \'Difference in proportions\').']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory framework or agency guideline under which this estimand is defined and will be submitted. Determines the specific regulatory expectations for estimand documentation in the Clinical Study Report (CSR). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ICH_E9R1|FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sap_alignment_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this estimand definition is currently aligned with the approved SAP version. Misalignment triggers a SAP amendment workflow. Critical for regulatory inspection readiness and DBL (Database Lock) governance.']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'Specific section number or identifier within the SAP document where this estimand is formally defined and described (e.g., \'Section 4.2.1\'). Enables direct cross-referencing between the estimand record and the governing SAP.']
  sensitivity_analysis_description varchar [note: 'Description of the associated sensitivity analyses planned to assess the robustness of the primary estimand result to deviations from assumptions, per ICH E9(R1) Section 3.8. References specific SAP sections where sensitivity analyses are detailed.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'Date on which this estimand version was superseded by a newer version due to a SAP amendment or protocol change. Null for the current active version. Supports historical audit trail.']
  supplementary_analysis_description varchar [note: 'Description of supplementary analyses associated with this estimand that provide additional context or explore alternative assumptions beyond the primary and sensitivity analyses, per ICH E9(R1) Section 3.9.']
  target_population_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the \'Population\' component of the ICH E9(R1) estimand framework — the set of subjects to whom the question of interest applies (e.g., \'All randomized patients with Type 2 Diabetes and baseline HbA1c ≥ 7.5%\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this estimand record. Used for change tracking, SAP version management, and regulatory inspection readiness in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the estimand definition (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented upon each approved amendment to the SAP or protocol that affects the estimand specification.']

  Note: 'Estimand definition record per the ICH E9(R1) framework for a study endpoint. Captures treatment condition, target population, variable (endpoint), intercurrent event handling strategies (treatment policy, hypothetical, composite, while on treatment, principal stratum), population-level summary measure, associated sensitivity analyses, and SAP alignment status. SSOT for estimand framework documentation required by modern FDA/EMA regulatory submissions.'
}

Table randomization.rand_scheme {
  rand_scheme_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the randomization scheme record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: A randomization scheme is designed and activated under a specific CTA. Regulatory agencies authorize the trial — including its randomization methodology — via the CTA. Traceability from rand_scheme to']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Randomization schemes are designed and executed under a governing randomization SOP (e.g., SOP for randomization list generation, IRT management). GCP requires that the applicable SOP version be trace']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Randomization schemes are designed against a specific protocol version per ICH E9(R1) and GCP requirements. When a protocol amendment changes stratification factors or allocation ratios, the scheme mu']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this randomization scheme is defined. Links the scheme to its parent protocol.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: GCP requires that randomization scheme versions be traceable to the protocol amendment that triggered them (e.g., new arm addition, stratification change). Direct FK to study_protocol_amendment on ran']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The randomization scheme/plan is a GCP-required essential document filed in the TMF. Inspection readiness and TMF completeness tracking require direct linkage from the scheme record to its TMF filing.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the randomization scheme was activated in the IRT/RTSM system and made available for subject allocation. Marks transition to \'active\' status.']
  allocation_algorithm varchar [note: 'The statistical randomization algorithm used to assign subjects to treatment arms. \'simple\' = unrestricted random; \'block\' = fixed block sizes; \'stratified_block\' = blocks within strata; \'minimization\' = dynamic balancing; \'adaptive\' = response-adaptive; \'covariate_adaptive\' = covariate-adaptive randomization.']
  allocation_ratio varchar [note: 'Ratio of subjects allocated to each treatment arm (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1:1\', \'1:1:1\'). Defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and protocol. Drives kit assignment quantities in RTSM.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the randomization scheme received formal biostatistics and QA approval, authorizing its use in the study. Marks transition from \'draft\' to \'approved\' status.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the individual (QA or biostatistics lead) who formally approved the randomization scheme. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding level applied to this randomization scheme. Determines who has access to treatment arm assignments and governs unblinding procedures. Critical for blinding integrity management.']
  block_size_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of permissible block sizes used in block or stratified-block randomization (e.g., \'4,6,8\'). Variable block sizes reduce predictability. Null for non-block algorithms.']
  cdisc_sdtm_domain varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code associated with randomization data derived from this scheme (typically \'RAND\' or \'SUPPRAND\'). Supports SDTM submission package preparation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this randomization scheme record was first created in the lakehouse. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  generation_method_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the randomization list generation methodology, including algorithm parameters, SAS/R procedure used, and any special considerations. Included in the Randomization Plan and CSR.']
  generation_software varchar [note: 'Name and version of the software used to generate the randomization list (e.g., \'SAS 9.4\', \'R 4.2.1\', \'Oracle Clinical One 21.3\'). Required for regulatory submission and reproducibility validation.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of 3-letter ISO country codes where this randomization scheme is applicable (e.g., \'USA,GBR,DEU\'). Relevant for multi-regional trials with country-specific regulatory requirements.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a scheme amendment requires re-submission to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC). Relevant when stratification factors or allocation ratios change.']
  irt_scheme_reference varchar [note: 'The scheme identifier or configuration reference as recorded in the IRT/RTSM system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One scheme ID or Medidata Rave RTSM list ID). Enables cross-system traceability.']
  irt_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system used to execute this randomization scheme (e.g., \'Oracle Clinical One\', \'Medidata Rave RTSM\', \'Almac IXRS\'). Identifies the operational system of record for subject allocation.']
  is_centralized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether randomization is performed centrally via IRT/IVRS/RTSM (True) or locally at the site (False). Centralized randomization is the standard for GCP-compliant multi-center trials.']
  is_stratified_by_site boolean [note: 'Indicates whether randomization is stratified by investigative site. Site stratification is common in multi-center trials to ensure balance across sites.']
  is_variable_block boolean [note: 'Indicates whether variable (random) block sizes are used rather than a fixed block size. Variable blocks reduce the ability to predict upcoming assignments and are preferred for double-blind studies.']
  list_generation_date date [note: 'Date on which the randomization list was generated using the specified software and seed. Required for audit trail and regulatory submission documentation.']
  minimization_objective_function varchar [note: 'For minimization or covariate-adaptive algorithms, the mathematical objective function used to minimize imbalance across covariates (e.g., \'range\', \'variance\', \'weighted_range\'). Null for non-minimization schemes.']
  minimization_probability decimal(5,4) [note: 'For minimization algorithms, the probability (0.0–1.0) of assigning a subject to the arm that minimizes imbalance (e.g., 0.8000 = 80% biased coin). Null for non-minimization schemes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, amendment rationale, or special instructions related to this randomization scheme. Not intended for structured data.']
  random_seed varchar [note: 'Numeric seed value used to initialize the pseudo-random number generator for reproducible list generation. Classified confidential to protect blinding integrity; disclosed only at database lock (DBL) or unblinding. Required for regulatory audit and CSR appendix.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this randomization scheme version has been included in a regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, or eCTD package). Triggers additional change-control requirements.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which the randomization scheme was retired or superseded. Null if the scheme is still active. Used for version lifecycle management and TMF archival.']
  sap_version_reference varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that specifies this randomization scheme. Ensures traceability between the scheme implementation and the approved statistical methodology.']
  scheme_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this randomization scheme within the RTSM/IRT system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One or Medidata Rave RTSM). Used as the business key for cross-system reference.']
  scheme_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the randomization scheme (e.g., \'Phase III Stratified Block Randomization v2\'). Used in reports, TMF documentation, and biostatistics deliverables.']
  scheme_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the randomization scheme. \'draft\' = under development; \'approved\' = biostatistics/QA sign-off obtained; \'active\' = in use for subject allocation; \'suspended\' = temporarily halted (e.g., DSMB hold); \'retired\' = superseded or study closed.']
  scheme_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the randomization scheme (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented when the scheme is amended post-approval. Supports audit trail and regulatory submission traceability.']
  strata_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct strata defined by the combination of stratification factors. Used to validate that the randomization list covers all strata and to monitor enrollment balance.']
  stratification_factors varchar [note: 'Semicolon-separated list of stratification factors used to define strata for stratified randomization (e.g., \'site;gender;disease_severity\'). Defined in the protocol and SAP. Null for non-stratified schemes.']
  total_randomization_slots varchar [note: 'Total number of randomization positions (slots) generated in the scheme list. Represents the maximum number of subjects that can be randomized under this scheme version.']
  treatment_arm_count varchar [note: 'Number of treatment arms (including placebo/control) defined in this randomization scheme. Must align with the protocol and allocation ratio.']
  unblinding_procedure varchar [note: 'Defines the authorized unblinding procedure for this scheme. \'emergency_only\' = individual subject emergency unblinding; \'dsmb_only\' = unblinding restricted to DSMB/DMC; \'dbk_full\' = full unblinding at database lock; \'partial_sponsor\' = sponsor statistician partial unblinding for interim analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this randomization scheme record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and version control in the silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master definition of a randomization scheme for a clinical study. Captures the allocation algorithm type (simple, block, stratified, minimization, adaptive), block sizes, allocation ratio, seed values, and scheme version. Owned by the biostatistics/RTSM team and referenced by all randomization activities. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave RTSM module.'
}

Table randomization.rand_list {
  rand_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the randomization list record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: GCP requires randomization list generation and validation to follow a documented SOP. Regulatory inspectors verify SOP compliance for list generation. A clinical-research expert would expect rand_list']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_epoch. Business justification: Randomization lists are epoch-specific per CDISC SDTM TE domain mapping requirements. The epoch in which randomization occurs must be formally linked for SDTM submission datasets and protocol complian']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Randomization lists are generated for a specific protocol version; when a protocol amendment changes allocation, a new list version is required. Regulatory inspection readiness and TMF completeness re']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the randomization scheme (algorithm configuration, block sizes, stratification factors) from which this list was generated.']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Protocol amendments that change randomization trigger generation of a new rand_list version. Linking rand_list to the governing protocol_amendment supports regulatory audit trails and GCP inspection r']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this randomization list was generated. Links the list to its parent protocol.']
  superseded_by_list_rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer randomization list that supersedes this one. Null if this is the current active version. Supports version chain traceability.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Randomization lists are essential TMF documents per DIA TMF Reference Model (Zone 7). TMF completeness assessments and inspection readiness checks require direct traceability from the randomization li']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Randomization list approval follows a formal document workflow (generation → QC review → sponsor approval → activation). GCP requires documented approval chains for randomization lists. The workflow p']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the randomization list was activated in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system and made available for subject assignment.']
  allocated_slots varchar [note: 'Number of randomization slots that have been assigned to subjects at the time of record. Used to monitor enrollment capacity and list utilization.']
  allocation_ratio varchar [note: 'Ratio of subject allocation across treatment arms (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1:1\'). Defined in the protocol and SAP; must match the generated list structure.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the randomization list was formally approved for use in the study by the authorized statistician or delegate. Required for regulatory compliance.']
  blinding_level varchar [note: 'Degree of blinding applied to this randomization list. Determines which roles (subject, investigator, sponsor) are masked to treatment assignment. Critical for blinding integrity management.']
  block_size_sequence varchar [note: 'Encoded representation of the block sizes used in permuted block randomization (e.g., \'2,4,4,2\'). Classified as confidential to protect blinding integrity during the study.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization list record was first captured in the data platform. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  dsmb_access_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) / Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) has been granted access to the unblinded version of this randomization list for interim safety reviews.']
  generated_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the biostatistician or system process that generated the randomization list. Supports ALCOA+ attributability requirement.']
  generation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or statistical method used to generate the randomization list (e.g., permuted block, minimization, stratified). Documented in the SAP and protocol.']
  generation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the randomization list was computationally generated by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for this artifact.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Country or region code(s) for which this randomization list is applicable (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes, comma-separated). Relevant for multi-regional trials with country-specific lists.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether IRB/IEC approval is required before activating this randomization list (e.g., for protocol amendments that change the randomization scheme).']
  irt_list_type varchar [note: 'Scope of the randomization list as configured in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system — whether it applies globally across all sites, to specific sites, strata, or countries.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether treatment assignments in this list are masked (True) or open-label (False). Drives access control and unblinding procedures in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system.']
  is_stratified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this randomization list uses stratified randomization (True) or unstratified simple/block randomization (False).']
  kit_assignment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this randomization list is linked to investigational product kit assignment in the RTSM/supply chain module. When True, kit numbers are assigned at the point of randomization.']
  list_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the randomization list, including any special conditions, amendments, or notes relevant to its use in the study.']
  list_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier or code for this randomization list as referenced in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system and the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP). Used for regulatory traceability.']
  list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the randomization list. Controls whether the list is available for subject assignment in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|superseded|retired — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]']
  list_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the randomization list (e.g., v1.0, v2.0). Incremented when the list is regenerated or amended, supporting audit trail and regulatory traceability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, amendment rationale, or regulatory comments associated with this randomization list.']
  randomization_seed varchar [note: 'The random number generator seed value used to produce this list. Classified as confidential to protect blinding integrity. Required for reproducibility validation and regulatory inspection.']
  sap_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier or section reference in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that specifies the randomization methodology for this list. Ensures traceability to the approved statistical methodology.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this randomization list was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_list_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this randomization list in the originating IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One). Used for cross-system reconciliation.']
  stratification_factors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of stratification factors applied when generating this randomization list (e.g., \'site, disease_severity, age_group\'). Reflects the protocol-defined stratification variables.']
  superseded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization list was superseded by a newer version. Null if the list is still active. Supports version lifecycle audit trail.']
  total_slots varchar [note: 'Total number of randomization positions (slots) in this list, representing the maximum number of subjects that can be assigned using this list.']
  treatment_arm_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct treatment arms (including placebo/control) covered by this randomization list. Validates alignment with the protocol-defined study design.']
  unblinding_procedure varchar [note: 'Defined procedure type for unblinding this randomization list. Governs when and how treatment assignments may be revealed (e.g., emergency unblinding, post-DBL unblinding). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: emergency_unblinding|scheduled_dbl_unblinding|dsmb_unblinding|regulatory_unblinding — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization list record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and quality control validation performed on this randomization list prior to activation. Ensures the list meets protocol specifications before use.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the randomization list completed User Acceptance Testing (UAT) validation. Null if validation has not yet been performed.']

  Note: 'The generated randomization list produced from a randomization scheme. Stores the ordered sequence of treatment assignments, list version, generation timestamp, generation method, blinding level, and approval status. A rand_list is the authoritative artifact used by IRT/IVRS/RTSM to assign subjects to treatment arms. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.'
}

Table randomization.rand_code {
  rand_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each randomization code (slot) record in the Silver layer. Primary key for this data product.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site to which this randomization code is allocated. Site-specific lists restrict code consumption to the assigned site.']
  irt_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier generated by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system at the moment of code assignment. Used for reconciliation between the EDC and RTSM systems.']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent randomization list from which this code slot was generated. A study may have multiple lists (e.g., per stratum or per site).']
  stratification_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.stratification_cell. Business justification: Each rand_code slot belongs to a specific stratification cell that determines its allocation. rand_code currently stores stratification_cell_code (STRING) and stratification_cell_description (STRING) ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) to which this randomization code belongs. Links the code to its parent study context.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: Each rand_code slot is allocated to exactly one treatment arm. rand_code currently stores treatment_arm_code (STRING) and treatment_arm_label (STRING) as denormalized references. Adding treatment_arm_']
  allocation_ratio_denominator varchar [note: 'Denominator of the randomization allocation ratio (e.g., 3 in a 2:1 ratio, representing total parts). Together with the numerator, documents the intended allocation balance.']
  allocation_ratio_numerator varchar [note: 'Numerator of the randomization allocation ratio for the treatment arm assigned to this code (e.g., 2 in a 2:1 ratio). Used to verify list balance and document the randomization scheme.']
  assigned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization code was consumed and assigned to a subject via the IRT/RTSM system. The principal business event timestamp for this entity. Null if not yet assigned.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this randomization code slot: available (not yet consumed), assigned (linked to a subject), cancelled (voided without assignment), replaced (superseded by a new code), reserved (held for a specific site/stratum), expired (past validity window). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: available|assigned|cancelled|replaced|reserved|expired — promote to reference product]']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Blinding scheme applied to this randomization code: double_blind (subject and investigator blinded), single_blind (subject only), open_label (no blinding), triple_blind (including sponsor). Governs access controls on treatment arm data.']
  block_number varchar [note: 'Block identifier within the randomization list to which this code belongs. Used for permuted-block randomization schemes to verify block completeness and balance.']
  block_size varchar [note: 'Number of randomization slots in the block containing this code. For variable block size designs, records the actual block size assigned to this slot.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for cancellation of this randomization code (e.g., subject screen failure, site closure, protocol amendment). Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization code was cancelled or voided in the IRT/RTSM system. Null if the code was not cancelled.']
  code_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric randomization code identifier as generated by the RTSM/IRT system (e.g., \'RC-0042\'). This is the business-facing code printed on the randomization schedule and referenced in regulatory submissions.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the site where this randomization code is allocated. Used for country-level stratification and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this randomization code record was first created in the source IRT/RTSM system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  dispensing_visit_number varchar [note: 'Protocol-defined visit number at which the IMP kit linked to this randomization code is to be dispensed to the subject. Aligns with the study schedule of assessments.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this randomization code is no longer valid for assignment. Codes past expiry are automatically set to \'expired\' status by the IRT/RTSM system.']
  irt_system_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the IRT/RTSM system that generated and managed this randomization code (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Supports multi-system reconciliation and data lineage.']
  is_emergency_unblinding_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this randomization code is eligible for emergency unblinding by site staff via the IRT system (True) or requires sponsor/DSMB authorization (False). Governs site-level unblinding access controls.']
  is_rescue_medication_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment arm assigned to this code permits rescue medication dispensing (True) or not (False). Drives IRT/RTSM dispensing logic for rescue kits.']
  is_unblinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment for this code has been formally unblinded (True) or remains blinded (False). Set to True upon emergency or end-of-study unblinding events.']
  kit_code varchar [note: 'Pre-assigned IMP kit code linked to this randomization slot for dispensing via IRT/RTSM. Connects the randomization event to the supply chain kit that must be dispensed to the subject.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to this randomization code record in the source system. Required for change tracking and ALCOA+ audit trail compliance.']
  list_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the randomization list from which this code was generated (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Tracks list amendments due to protocol changes and supports regulatory audit trail.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes or comments entered by the IRT/RTSM administrator regarding this randomization code (e.g., manual override justification, reconciliation notes).']
  protocol_amendment_number varchar [note: 'Protocol amendment version under which this randomization code was generated or last modified. Tracks list changes driven by protocol amendments for regulatory traceability.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'Statistical algorithm used to generate this randomization code (e.g., permuted_block, minimization, adaptive). Documented for regulatory submission and biostatistical validation.']
  randomization_seed varchar [note: 'Cryptographic seed value used by the randomization algorithm to generate this code list. Classified as confidential to protect blinding integrity. Required for regulatory reproducibility validation.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Sponsor-defined geographic region code (e.g., \'NA\', \'EU\', \'APAC\') associated with the site for this code. Used as a stratification factor in multi-regional trials.']
  replacement_code_number varchar [note: 'Code number of the replacement randomization slot issued when this code was cancelled or replaced. Enables traceability of code replacement chains in the audit trail.']
  sequence_position varchar [note: 'Sequential ordinal position of this code within its randomization list. Determines the order in which codes are consumed during subject enrollment.']
  source_system_code_reference varchar [note: 'Native primary key or unique identifier of this randomization code record in the originating IRT/RTSM source system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One). Enables reconciliation and data lineage tracing back to the system of record.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'Documented reason for unblinding this randomization code, required for regulatory audit trail. Null if the code has not been unblinded.']
  unblinding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the treatment arm assignment was formally unblinded. Null if the code remains blinded. Captured per ALCOA+ principles for regulatory audit trail.']

  Note: 'Individual randomization code (slot) within a randomization list. Each code represents a single subject allocation slot with its sequential position number, pre-assigned treatment arm, stratification cell membership, kit code linkage for IMP dispensing, blinded label visible to site staff, and assignment status (available, assigned, cancelled, replaced). The atomic unit consumed when a subject is randomized via IRT/RTSM — once assigned, the code is permanently linked to that subject. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.'
}

Table randomization.treatment_arm {
  treatment_arm_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for treatment_arm']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_arm. Business justification: randomization.treatment_arm is the operational implementation of study.study_arm (the protocol-defined arm). Linking them is required for SDTM ARM domain mapping, CSR reporting, and protocol complianc']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Treatment arms are explicitly authorized under a CTA; different CTAs in different countries may authorize different arms. Regulatory inspectors verify that only CTA-authorized arms were randomized. Di']
  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'FK to study.investigational_product']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Treatment arms are added, modified, or removed via protocol amendments. Regulatory submissions and CDISC SDTM ARM datasets must reference the amendment that introduced or changed each arm. amendment_v']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study to which this treatment arm belongs. Links the arm definition to its parent study protocol.']
  allocation_ratio_denominator varchar [note: 'Denominator component of the randomization allocation ratio for this arm (e.g., 3 in a 2:1 ratio where total parts = 3). Together with the numerator, defines the proportional allocation of subjects to this arm.']
  allocation_ratio_numerator varchar [note: 'Numerator component of the randomization allocation ratio for this arm (e.g., 2 in a 2:1 ratio). Used with the denominator to compute the target allocation percentage and configure the randomization schedule in IRT/RTSM systems.']
  analysis_population varchar [note: 'Primary analysis population associated with this treatment arm for statistical reporting (e.g., Intent-to-Treat (ITT), Modified ITT (mITT), Per Protocol (PP), Safety). Drives CDISC ADaM ADSL population flags and SAS Drug Development analysis programs.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this treatment arm definition received formal approval (e.g., sponsor sign-off, protocol approval, IRT/RTSM UAT sign-off). Required for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness. Managed via MasterControl or Veeva Vault workflow.']
  arm_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this treatment arm was closed to new randomizations or formally ended per protocol or protocol amendment. Nullable for arms that remain open. Used for enrollment closure tracking and database lock (DBL) preparation.']
  arm_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number defining the display order of this treatment arm in study reports, listings, and tables. Used for consistent ordering in CSR outputs, SDTM datasets, and regulatory submission documents.']
  arm_start_date date [note: 'Date on which this treatment arm became active and eligible for subject randomization, as defined in the protocol or IRT/RTSM configuration. Used for enrollment timeline tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  arm_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the treatment arm. Controls whether subjects can be randomized to this arm, whether supply kits can be assigned, and whether the arm is available for data entry in the EDC. Managed in Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave RTSM.']
  arm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the treatment arm by its role in the study design. Drives regulatory reporting, safety analysis population definitions, and CDISC SDTM ARMCD mapping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: experimental|active_comparator|placebo|open_label_extension|run_in|follow_up — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  blinding_role varchar [note: 'Blinding designation for this treatment arm indicating the level of masking applied. Critical for maintaining blinding integrity in the study. Restricted access to prevent unblinding of sponsor, investigator, or subject. Managed in Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this treatment arm record was first created in the system, conforming to ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate). Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and GCP.']
  crossover_sequence varchar [note: 'For crossover study designs, defines the treatment sequence this arm represents (e.g., \'AB\', \'BA\', \'ABC\'). Used in CDISC SDTM SEARM (Sequence Arm) variable and period-specific analysis in ADaM datasets.']
  dose_level varchar [note: 'Dose level designation for the investigational product in this arm (e.g., \'10mg\', \'50mg\', \'100mg\', \'High Dose\', \'Low Dose\'). Used in dose-escalation studies, PK/PD analyses, and CDISC SDTM EX domain mapping.']
  dosing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency of investigational product administration for subjects in this arm (e.g., \'QD - Once Daily\', \'BID - Twice Daily\', \'QW - Once Weekly\'). Mapped to CDISC Controlled Terminology FREQ variable in the EX domain.']
  edc_arm_code varchar [note: 'Treatment arm code as configured in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system (Medidata Rave EDC or Oracle Clinical One). Used for eCRF field population, data validation rules, and SDTM mapping. Ensures consistency between EDC and IRT arm definitions.']
  irt_arm_code varchar [note: 'Treatment arm code as configured in the Interactive Response Technology (IRT) / Interactive Voice Response System (IVRS) / Randomization and Trial Supply Management (RTSM) system (Medidata Rave RTSM). Used for kit assignment, supply reconciliation, and randomization code generation. May differ from the SDTM arm code.']
  is_blinded_to_investigator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment is masked from the Principal Investigator (PI) and site staff. Critical for double-blind study integrity and GCP compliance documentation.']
  is_blinded_to_subject boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment is masked from the study subject. Supports blinding integrity documentation required by ICH E9 and GCP. Distinct from investigator or sponsor blinding.']
  is_control_arm boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treatment arm serves as the control arm (placebo or active comparator) in the study. Used in statistical analysis to define the reference group for primary and secondary endpoint comparisons.']
  max_subject_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of subjects permitted in this treatment arm. Enforced by IRT/RTSM to prevent over-enrollment. May be set for adaptive designs or safety-driven enrollment caps.']
  min_subject_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of subjects required in this treatment arm to maintain statistical validity and meet protocol-defined endpoints. Used by DSMB/DMC for interim analysis decisions and by biostatistics for power calculations.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this treatment arm record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Updated on every change to the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, clarifications, or sponsor-specific instructions related to this treatment arm definition. Not used for regulatory submissions; intended for internal data management and CTMS operational use.']
  planned_subject_count varchar [note: 'Target number of subjects planned for enrollment and randomization into this treatment arm as defined in the study protocol and sample size calculation. Used for enrollment tracking, site feasibility, and supply planning.']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section(s) of the study protocol document that defines this treatment arm (e.g., \'Section 5.2 - Treatment Arms and Dosing Regimen\'). Supports traceability between the data model and the governing protocol document stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'Statistical randomization method used to allocate subjects to this treatment arm. Drives IRT/RTSM algorithm configuration in Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One. Documented in the SAP and protocol.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this treatment arm definition has been included in a regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, or CTD). Used for submission readiness tracking and eCTD package assembly in Veeva Vault.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Route by which the investigational product is administered to subjects in this arm. Mapped to CDISC Controlled Terminology for the EX domain EXROUTE variable. Drives supply kit configuration and nursing/pharmacy instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) section that defines the analysis methodology for this treatment arm. Ensures traceability between arm definitions and the pre-specified statistical analysis approach used in SAS Drug Development.']
  sdtm_arm_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The exact ARM variable value as it will appear in the CDISC SDTM DM (Demographics) and ARM domains for regulatory submission. Must conform to CDISC Controlled Terminology and be consistent across all SDTM datasets submitted in the eCTD package.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this treatment arm definition was sourced or is mastered (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and Silver layer ETL audit in the Databricks Lakehouse.']
  stratification_factors varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of stratification factors applied during randomization to this arm (e.g., \'region, disease severity, prior treatment\'). Defined in the protocol and SAP. Used to ensure balanced allocation across subgroups and configure IRT/RTSM stratification layers.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for temporary suspension of randomization to this treatment arm (e.g., safety signal, supply shortage, protocol amendment). Documented for regulatory compliance and DSMB/DMC review. Populated only when arm_status is \'suspended\'.']
  target_allocation_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of randomized subjects to be allocated to this treatment arm as specified in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and protocol. Used for enrollment monitoring, supply forecasting, and IRT/RTSM configuration. Example: 33.33 for a 1:1:1 three-arm study.']
  treatment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the investigational treatment, comparator, or placebo administered in this arm, including drug name, formulation, dose, route of administration, and dosing schedule as specified in the protocol.']
  unblinding_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the emergency unblinding procedure applicable to this treatment arm, including authorized personnel, system access method (e.g., IRT/IVRS emergency unblinding), and documentation requirements. Classified confidential to protect blinding integrity.']
  washout_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for washout between treatment periods for this arm in crossover or multi-period study designs. Defined in the protocol based on pharmacokinetic (PK) half-life data. Used for visit scheduling and IRT/RTSM period management.']

  Note: 'Master definition of each treatment arm within a clinical study. Captures arm name, arm code, arm type (experimental, active comparator, placebo, open-label extension), blinding role, target allocation percentage, and arm status. SSOT for treatment arm definitions referenced by randomization codes, subject assignments, and supply domain kit assignments.'
}

Table randomization.stratification_factor {
  stratification_factor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the stratification factor record within the randomization scheme. Primary key for this entity.']
  ecrf_field_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.ecrf_field. Business justification: Stratification factors are collected via specific eCRF fields — the stratification_factor.ecrf_field_reference plain-text column is a denormalized reference to data.ecrf_field. A proper FK enables EDC']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) requires traceability between randomization stratification factors and the eligibility criteria from which they derive (e.g., disease severity, prior therapy). SAP and protocol review depen']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent randomization scheme to which this stratification factor belongs. Each factor is owned by exactly one scheme.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this stratification factor is defined. Enables direct study-level filtering without joining through the scheme.']
  adam_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC ADaM variable name in the ADSL dataset corresponding to this stratification factor (e.g., \'STRAT1\', \'BSEVSEV\'). Used in primary and sensitivity analyses per the Statistical Analysis Plan.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this stratification factor configuration was formally approved by the biostatistics or RTSM team. Required for regulatory audit trail and TMF documentation.']
  covariate_adjustment_method varchar [note: 'Statistical method used to adjust for this stratification factor in the primary analysis: \'ancova\' for Analysis of Covariance, \'cmh\' for Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel test, \'logistic_regression\' for binary endpoints, \'none\' if not used as a covariate in analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this stratification factor record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_source_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the system or method from which the stratification factor value is captured at randomization: \'ecrf\' from the EDC eCRF, \'ivrs\' from Interactive Voice Response System, \'irt\' from IRT/RTSM, \'central_lab\' from laboratory results, \'manual_entry\' for site-entered values.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this stratification factor ceased to be effective for new randomizations, typically due to protocol amendment or study close-out. Null for currently active factors.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this stratification factor became effective for subject randomization, typically aligned with protocol approval or amendment effective date. Subjects randomized on or after this date are stratified using this factor.']
  factor_code varchar [note: 'Short, system-level alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the stratification factor within the scheme (e.g., REGION, DISEASE_STAGE, GENDER). Used as the programmatic key in RTSM/IRT configuration and SDTM mapping.']
  factor_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the stratification factor, including its clinical rationale, how it is assessed, and its role in balancing treatment arms as documented in the Statistical Analysis Plan.']
  factor_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the stratification factor as displayed in the RTSM/IRT interface and statistical analysis plan (e.g., \'Geographic Region\', \'Baseline Disease Severity\', \'Prior Treatment Status\').']
  factor_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the stratification factor within the scheme design workflow: \'draft\' during initial configuration, \'approved\' after biostatistics/RTSM sign-off, \'active\' when live in the IRT system, \'suspended\' during protocol amendment review, \'retired\' when permanently decommissioned.']
  factor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the factor\'s measurement scale: \'categorical\' for nominal groupings (e.g., region), \'continuous_binned\' for numeric ranges converted to categories (e.g., age groups), \'ordinal\' for ranked categories (e.g., disease severity), \'binary\' for two-level factors (e.g., yes/no prior treatment).']
  irt_parameter_name varchar [note: 'The exact parameter name as configured in the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One) for this stratification factor. Used for system integration and UAT validation.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stratification factor is currently active and in use for subject randomization (True) or has been deactivated via protocol amendment (False). Inactive factors are retained for historical traceability.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stratification factor value is masked from unblinded personnel (True) or visible to all study team members (False). Relevant for double-blind studies where certain stratification criteria could reveal treatment assignment.']
  is_primary_factor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this factor is designated as a primary stratification factor (True) versus a secondary or exploratory factor (False) in the Statistical Analysis Plan. Primary factors are used in the primary efficacy analysis.']
  is_used_in_primary_analysis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stratification factor is included as a covariate in the primary statistical analysis model (True) or is used only for randomization balance without inclusion in the primary analysis (False). Relevant for ANCOVA and CMH test specifications in the SAP.']
  level_count varchar [note: 'Total number of enumerated levels (strata) defined for this stratification factor. Drives the dimensionality of the stratification matrix and the number of randomization lists required. For example, a 3-region factor has level_count = 3.']
  max_imbalance_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable imbalance (as a percentage or absolute count, per unit_of_imbalance) between treatment arms within any stratum defined by this factor before a balancing action is triggered in the minimization algorithm.']
  minimization_variant varchar [note: 'Specific variant of the minimization algorithm applied for this factor: \'pocock_simon\' for the Pocock-Simon method, \'weighted_minimization\' for factor-weighted minimization, \'biased_coin\' for biased-coin minimization, \'deterministic\' for deterministic minimization, \'none\' if minimization is not applicable.']
  minimization_weight decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this stratification factor in a minimization or weighted minimization algorithm (e.g., Pocock-Simon). Values typically range from 0.0 to 1.0, with higher weights indicating greater influence on treatment allocation balance. Null if minimization is not used.']
  missing_value_handling varchar [note: 'Defines the RTSM system behavior when the stratification factor value is missing or unknown at the time of randomization: \'block_randomization\' to prevent randomization, \'assign_default_level\' to use a pre-specified default level, \'flag_and_hold\' to alert the site, \'exclude_from_minimization\' to proceed without this factor.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, biostatistician comments, or RTSM configuration remarks related to this stratification factor that do not fit into structured fields. Not used for regulatory submissions.']
  protocol_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section of the study protocol document that defines this stratification factor (e.g., \'Section 8.2.1 Stratification Factors\'). Enables traceability from the data model to the governing protocol.']
  randomization_algorithm varchar [note: 'The randomization algorithm applied in conjunction with this stratification factor: \'permuted_block\' for block randomization within strata, \'minimization\' for adaptive minimization, \'stratified_block\' for stratified permuted block, \'dynamic_allocation\' for covariate-adaptive methods.']
  rtsm_factor_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier assigned to this stratification factor within the source RTSM/IRT system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM internal factor ID or Oracle Clinical One factor GUID). Used for system integration, reconciliation, and audit trail linkage.']
  rtsm_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the RTSM/IRT/IVRS system in which this stratification factor is configured and operationalized for subject randomization and kit assignment.']
  sap_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific section of the Statistical Analysis Plan that describes the use of this stratification factor in primary and sensitivity analyses (e.g., \'SAP Section 5.3 Stratification\'). Supports regulatory audit traceability.']
  sdtm_variable varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM variable name that maps to this stratification factor for regulatory submission datasets (e.g., \'STRAT1\', \'REGION1\'). Used in SDTM TR/SUPPFA domains and ADaM ADSL derivations.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer defining the display and processing sequence of this stratification factor within the randomization scheme. Lower values appear first in RTSM screens, SAP tables, and stratification matrix axes.']
  unit_of_imbalance varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the max_imbalance_threshold is expressed as a \'percentage\' of subjects within the stratum or as an absolute \'subject_count\'. Determines how the RTSM system evaluates balance during randomization.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this stratification factor record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks configuration changes and supports change control audit trail under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  validation_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule or logical expression applied to validate the stratification factor value at the time of randomization (e.g., \'BMIBL >= 18 AND BMIBL < 30\'). Prevents invalid factor assignments and ensures data integrity per ALCOA+ principles.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of this stratification factor definition, incremented with each protocol amendment or RTSM configuration change (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.0\'). Supports change control and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Defines the stratification factors used in a randomization scheme, including factor name, factor code, data source (eCRF field reference), factor type (categorical, continuous-binned), enumerated levels (level code, label, sort order, active status), and optional minimization parameters (weight value, algorithm variant such as Pocock-Simon or weighted minimization). Each factor belongs to one randomization scheme and its levels define the axes of the stratification matrix. Owned by the biostatistics/RTSM team as part of scheme design.'
}

Table randomization.stratification_cell {
  stratification_cell_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a stratification cell record within the randomization scheme. Primary key for this entity.']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent randomization scheme under which this stratification cell is defined. Links the cell to its governing allocation algorithm.']
  stratification_factor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the stratification factor that contributes a level to this cell definition.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this stratification cell is defined. Enables cross-study reporting and enrollment balance monitoring.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the treatment arm associated with this stratification cell. Enables arm-level enrollment balance monitoring within each stratum.']
  actual_enrolled_count varchar [note: 'Current count of subjects who have been randomized and enrolled into this stratification cell. Updated in real time from the IRT/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM / Oracle Clinical One) upon each randomization event.']
  allocation_ratio varchar [note: 'Ratio of subject allocation across treatment arms within this stratification cell (e.g., \'1:1\', \'2:1\', \'1:1:1\'). Defined in the protocol and SAP and configured in the RTSM system.']
  balance_tolerance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage deviation from the target enrollment count before the cell is flagged as imbalanced. Defined in the SAP and used by the RTSM system to trigger balance alerts.']
  block_size varchar [note: 'Size of the permuted block used for randomization within this stratification cell. A value of zero or null indicates variable block size or non-block-based methods. Stored as confidential to protect blinding integrity.']
  cell_balance_status varchar [note: 'Current enrollment balance status of this stratification cell relative to its target. \'balanced\' indicates enrollment is within acceptable tolerance; \'under_enrolled\' or \'over_enrolled\' indicate deviation from target; \'closed\' indicates the cell is no longer accepting subjects; \'suspended\' indicates temporary halt.']
  cell_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this stratification cell within the study randomization scheme. Used in IRT/IVRS/RTSM systems such as Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One for cell-level kit assignment and allocation tracking.']
  cell_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the stratification cell, including the clinical rationale for the factor level combination and any protocol-specific notes.']
  cell_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable composite label describing the full combination of stratification factor levels that define this cell (e.g., \'Age>=65 / Male / ECOG 0-1\'). Used in study reports, CSR tables, and enrollment dashboards.']
  cell_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal sequence number of this cell within the randomization scheme, used for consistent ordering in reports, listings, and IRT/RTSM system configuration.']
  cell_status varchar [note: 'Operational lifecycle status of the stratification cell within the randomization scheme. \'active\' means the cell is open for randomization; \'inactive\' means it has been deactivated; \'closed\' means enrollment is complete; \'suspended\' means temporarily halted; \'draft\' means not yet activated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this stratification cell record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  enrollment_close_date date [note: 'Date on which this stratification cell was closed to further subject randomization, either due to reaching the enrollment cap or protocol-driven closure.']
  enrollment_open_date date [note: 'Date on which this stratification cell was opened for subject randomization and enrollment, as recorded in the RTSM system.']
  factor_level_code varchar [note: 'The system-level alphanumeric code for the specific factor level assigned to this cell (e.g., \'MILD\', \'M\', \'NA\'). Used for IRT/RTSM system configuration and programmatic identification of the level within the randomization engine.']
  factor_level_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The specific level value assigned to this stratification factor within this cell (e.g., \'Mild\', \'Male\', \'North America\'). This is the core data element that defines the cell\'s position on this factor\'s axis and is required for SDTM STRATM variable mapping.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region stratification factor level associated with this cell (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific), when geography is used as a stratification factor. Supports regional enrollment balance monitoring.']
  is_active_for_enrollment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stratification cell is currently open and accepting new subject randomizations. Controlled by the RTSM system and updated when enrollment caps or protocol amendments are applied.']
  is_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the treatment arm assignment within this stratification cell is blinded to investigators and subjects. True for double-blind studies; False for open-label designs.']
  is_primary_defining_factor boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this stratification factor is the primary axis used to define and label this cell within the randomization scheme. Used by biostatisticians to identify the dominant stratification dimension for reporting and SAP documentation purposes.']
  last_randomization_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent subject randomization event recorded within this stratification cell. Used to monitor cell activity and detect stalled enrollment.']
  level_sequence_order varchar [note: 'The ordinal position of this factor\'s contribution within the cell\'s composite label definition. Determines the display order of factor levels in the cell label (e.g., factor 1 = Disease Severity, factor 2 = Gender, factor 3 = Region) and is required for consistent RTSM cell label generation.']
  maximum_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of subjects permitted in this stratification cell per treatment arm. Enrollment is capped at this value to prevent over-representation of a stratum.']
  minimum_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of subjects required in this stratification cell to maintain statistical validity of the study, as specified in the SAP. Triggers an alert if actual enrollment falls below this threshold.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or comments regarding this stratification cell, such as enrollment challenges, protocol deviation impacts, or DSMB/DMC recommendations affecting cell management.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the study protocol under which this stratification cell definition was established or last amended. Ensures traceability of cell definitions to specific protocol versions for regulatory submissions.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'Randomization algorithm applied within this stratification cell (e.g., permuted block, minimization, dynamic allocation). Determines how subjects are allocated to treatment arms within the cell.']
  randomized_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who have received a randomization code assignment within this stratification cell, which may differ from enrolled count if subjects are randomized but not yet dosed.']
