  rtsm_cell_reference varchar [note: 'External cell identifier as configured in the RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One IRT). Used for reconciliation between the data lakehouse and the operational RTSM system.']
  sap_version varchar [note: 'Version of the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) that defines the stratification factors, cell structure, and enrollment targets for this cell. Critical for regulatory audit traceability.']
  screened_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who have been screened and assessed for eligibility within this stratification cell, used to monitor the screening-to-enrollment funnel per stratum.']
  sdtm_stratum_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'CDISC SDTM-compliant stratum value code for this cell, mapped to the STRATCD variable in the RAND domain. Required for regulatory submission datasets and SDTM-to-ADaM derivations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this stratification cell record originated (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Used for data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  target_enrollment_count varchar [note: 'Protocol-specified target number of subjects to be enrolled in this stratification cell per treatment arm, as defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and randomization schedule.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or process that last modified this stratification cell record. Required for electronic records compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ attributability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this stratification cell record. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  withdrawn_count varchar [note: 'Number of subjects who were enrolled in this stratification cell but subsequently withdrew or were discontinued, used to assess cell-level dropout impact on balance.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or system identifier of the user or process that created this stratification cell record. Required for electronic records compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ attributability.']

  Note: 'Represents the unique combination of stratification factor levels that defines a single stratum cell within a randomization scheme. Stores the cell identifier, composite cell label, target subject count per arm within the cell, actual enrolled count, and cell balance status. Used to monitor enrollment balance across strata.'
}

Table randomization.subject_randomization {
  subject_randomization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate primary key for each subject randomization or re-randomization event record in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: A subject randomization event is governed by a specific blinding configuration that determines what information is masked. subject_randomization currently stores blinding_type (STRING) as a denormaliz']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: In EU trials, each subject randomization occurs under a specific CTA for the member state. Regulatory reporting to national competent authorities requires linking subject randomizations to the governi']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: SDTM TE domain mapping and epoch-level enrollment reporting require direct epoch attribution on the randomization event. Subject randomization occurs within a specific epoch (typically treatment); dir']
  ethics_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_approval. Business justification: GCP requires that each subject randomization occurs under a valid, current ethics approval. Linking subject_randomization to ethics_approval enables per-subject enrollment compliance verification, sup']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: US IND annual reports require subject enrollment and randomization counts per IND. subject_randomization already has clinical_trial_authorization_id for EU/global CTAs but lacks ind_application_id for']
  irt_transaction_id varchar [note: 'The unique transaction reference number generated by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One) at the time of the randomization call. Used for audit trail reconciliation between the EDC and RTSM systems.']
  original_randomization_subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'For re-randomization events, the subject_randomization_id of the original randomization record that this event supersedes. Null for initial randomization events. Enables lineage tracing across re-randomization chains.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Each randomization event is performed under a specific protocol version; when a subject is randomized after a protocol amendment, the applicable version must be recorded for GCP compliance, SDTM DM do']
  rand_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_code. Business justification: A subject randomization event consumes exactly one rand_code slot from the randomization list. subject_randomization currently stores randomization_number (STRING) as a denormalized reference to the r']
  rand_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_list. Business justification: A subject randomization event is performed against a specific randomization list version. subject_randomization currently stores randomization_list_version (STRING) as a denormalized reference. Adding']
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.protocol_amendment. Business justification: Re-randomizations and amendment-driven randomization changes require an authorizing protocol amendment. Regulatory audit trails and CDISC SDTM datasets must reference the specific amendment version. a']
  stratification_cell_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.stratification_cell. Business justification: A subject is randomized into a specific stratification cell based on their baseline characteristics. subject_randomization currently stores stratification_cell_code (STRING) as a denormalized referenc']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this randomization event occurred. Links to the study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the subject was randomized.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Randomization occurs at a specific protocol-defined visit (flagged is_randomization_visit in study_visit_schedule). Linking subject_randomization to the scheduled visit enables protocol deviation dete']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: A subject randomization event assigns the subject to exactly one treatment arm. subject_randomization currently stores treatment_arm_code (STRING) and treatment_arm_description (STRING) as denormalize']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical trial subject (participant) who is being randomized.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'The blinding level applied to this randomization event as defined in the study protocol (e.g., double_blind, open_label). Determines access controls for unblinding procedures and kit dispensing instructions.']
  block_number varchar [note: 'The block number within the randomization schedule from which this subject\'s allocation was drawn. Confidential to preserve blinding integrity during the study. Used for post-DBL stratification balance verification.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for cancellation of the randomization event. Null for non-cancelled records. Required for audit trail completeness and IRT reconciliation.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which a randomization event was cancelled or voided in the IRT/RTSM system. Null for active or completed randomizations. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for site staff or sponsor to document any notable circumstances surrounding the randomization event (e.g., IRT system downtime, manual override justification, protocol deviation context). Supports audit trail narrative.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the investigational site where the randomization occurred. Supports geographic stratification analysis and regional regulatory reporting (FDA, EMA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this randomization record was first captured in the Silver layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  eligibility_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all inclusion/exclusion criteria were confirmed as met prior to this randomization event (True = confirmed eligible; False = eligibility not confirmed or waived). Supports protocol deviation detection and ITT/PP population analysis.']
  irt_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system that processed the randomization transaction. Supports multi-system audit trails and source data verification (SDV) activities.']
  is_unblinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blinding for this subject\'s randomization has been broken (True = unblinded; False = still blinded). Critical for blinding integrity monitoring and DSMB/DMC reporting. Triggers downstream safety and regulatory workflows when True.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'The investigational product kit number assigned to the subject at randomization by the RTSM system. Links the randomization event to the supply chain dispensing record. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM kit assignment transaction.']
  randomization_date date [note: 'The calendar date of the randomization event (yyyy-MM-dd). Derived from randomization_timestamp for day-level reporting, enrollment tracking, and CDISC SDTM DM.RANDDT mapping. Stored separately to support date-only queries without timestamp precision.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'The statistical randomization algorithm applied to generate this allocation (e.g., permuted block, minimization, stratified block). Defined in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) and randomization specification document.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The unique randomization number assigned to the subject by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system at the time of randomization. This is the externally-known business identifier for the randomization event and is used across EDC, RTSM, and regulatory submissions. Corresponds to RNDMNO or equivalent field in Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.']
  randomization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this randomization event. \'randomized\' indicates a successful initial allocation; \'re-randomized\' indicates a subsequent allocation event; \'cancelled\' indicates the randomization was voided; \'pending\' indicates the IRT transaction is in progress; \'failed\' indicates an IRT system error prevented completion.']
  randomization_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone offset) at which the subject was randomized by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system. This is the principal business event timestamp and the ALCOA+ contemporaneous record of the allocation event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  randomization_type varchar [note: 'Classifies whether this record represents an initial randomization event, a re-randomization (subject re-entering the study), or a replacement randomization (e.g., IRT error correction). Drives downstream analysis population flags (ITT, PP).']
  randomizing_user_role varchar [note: 'The role of the user or system that performed the randomization (e.g., CRC - Clinical Research Coordinator, PI - Principal Investigator, IRT_system for automated randomizations). Supports delegation log compliance and audit trail.']
  rerandomization_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for re-randomizing the subject. Null for initial randomization events. Required for protocol deviation tracking and regulatory submission narratives.']
  sequence_within_block varchar [note: 'The position of this subject\'s allocation within the randomization block. Confidential to preserve blinding integrity. Used post-DBL for randomization quality review and biostatistical verification.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this randomization event in the originating source system (Medidata Rave RTSM or Oracle Clinical One). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this randomization record was sourced (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer provenance tracking.']
  stratification_factor_1_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the first protocol-defined stratification factor captured at the time of randomization (e.g., disease severity: mild/moderate/severe). Stored individually to support stratification balance analytics and CDISC SDTM SUPPRAND dataset generation.']
  stratification_factor_2_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the second protocol-defined stratification factor captured at the time of randomization (e.g., geographic region: North America/Europe/Asia-Pacific). Supports stratification balance reporting and CDISC SDTM SUPPRAND.']
  stratification_factor_3_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of the third protocol-defined stratification factor captured at the time of randomization (e.g., prior treatment: yes/no). Nullable when fewer than three stratification factors are defined in the protocol.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The site-assigned subject identifier (e.g., site prefix + sequential number) used in the eCRF and EDC system. Distinct from the randomization number; used to cross-reference the subject across Medidata Rave EDC and CTMS.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for breaking the blind for this subject\'s randomization. Null if still blinded. Required for regulatory audit trail and DSMB/DMC reporting.']
  unblinding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which the blinding was broken for this subject\'s randomization. Null if the subject remains blinded. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this randomization record was last modified in the Silver layer lakehouse. Tracks data corrections, status transitions, and unblinding events. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a subject\'s randomization or re-randomization event. Captures the subject identifier, study site, randomization number assigned, treatment arm allocated, stratification cell, randomization timestamp, IRT/IVRS transaction reference, randomizing user (CRC/IRT system), randomization status (randomized, cancelled, re-randomized), and for re-randomization events: original randomization reference, re-randomization reason (screen failure re-entry, protocol deviation, IRT error), and authorizing protocol amendment. This is the SSOT for all subject-to-treatment-arm allocation events including initial and subsequent randomizations. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.'
}

Table randomization.irt_transaction {
  irt_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each IRT/IVRS/RTSM transaction record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product.']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the supply depot or pharmacy from which the investigational product kit was sourced for this transaction. Supports supply chain traceability and depot-level inventory reconciliation.']
  epoch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.epoch. Business justification: IRT/RTSM dispensing transactions must be attributed to study epochs for epoch-level supply forecasting, compliance reporting, and SDTM EX domain generation. Direct epoch FK on the high-frequency irt_t']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this IRT transaction was executed. Links the transaction to the parent protocol and sponsor.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the IRT transaction was initiated. Used for site-level monitoring and SDV reconciliation.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: IRT transactions (randomization, dispensing, return) are triggered at specific protocol visits. Linking to study_visit_schedule enables visit-level IRT transaction auditing, protocol deviation detecti']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: IRT transactions record the treatment arm code assigned or dispensed during the transaction. irt_transaction currently stores treatment_arm_code (STRING) as a denormalized reference. Adding treatment_']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) involved in this IRT transaction, such as the subject being randomized or receiving a kit dispensing.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the blinding state of the transaction at the time of execution. Tracks whether the treatment assignment was revealed (unblinded) as part of an emergency unblinding or DSMB/DMC review.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for cancellation of a randomization, kit assignment, or other IRT transaction. Required for GCP audit trail when transaction_type is cancellation or transaction_status is cancelled.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the investigational site where the IRT transaction was performed. Supports regional regulatory reporting and supply chain logistics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this IRT transaction record was first captured and persisted in the data platform (silver layer). Supports ALCOA+ attributability and audit trail requirements.']
  dispensing_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the investigational product kit was dispensed to the subject. Relevant for kit_assignment transaction types. Used for supply chain reconciliation and compliance with protocol dispensing windows.']
  dose_level varchar [note: 'The dose level assigned or changed during this IRT transaction (e.g., 10mg, 20mg, 40mg). Relevant for dose-change and titration transaction types. Captured as a string to accommodate protocol-defined dose descriptors.']
  electronic_signature_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRT transaction was executed with a valid electronic signature as required by 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11. True if an e-signature was captured; False if not applicable or not captured.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the investigational product kit assigned during this transaction. Critical for supply chain compliance to ensure no expired kits are dispensed to subjects.']
  failure_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason describing why the IRT transaction failed or was cancelled. Populated only when transaction_outcome is failure or transaction_status is cancelled/failed. Supports root cause analysis and CAPA.']
  irt_config_version varchar [note: 'The validated configuration version of the IRT/RTSM system build active at the time of the transaction. Required for regulatory audit trail to confirm the correct randomization schedule and supply rules were applied.']
  irt_system_name varchar [note: 'The name of the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system that generated this transaction (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle IRT, Almac IXRS). Supports multi-system study environments and source system traceability.']
  irt_system_reference_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique transaction reference number assigned by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM transaction ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']
  irt_system_version varchar [note: 'The validated version of the IRT/RTSM system at the time the transaction was executed. Required for regulatory submission traceability and system validation documentation per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the investigational product kit assigned to the subject during this IRT transaction. Links to the supply chain domain for kit tracking, expiry management, and depot reconciliation.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigational product kit assigned (e.g., active, placebo, rescue medication, titration pack). Supports supply chain analytics and blinding verification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or annotations entered by the operator at the time of the IRT transaction. Captures contextual information not covered by structured fields, such as site-specific circumstances.']
  operator_role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the operator who performed the IRT transaction (e.g., Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC), Clinical Research Associate (CRA), Principal Investigator (PI), pharmacist). Supports access control auditing and GCP compliance.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'The version of the study protocol that was in effect at the time this IRT transaction was executed. Ensures traceability of randomization and dispensing decisions to the applicable protocol amendment.']
  query_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an open data discrepancy query exists against this IRT transaction record in the EDC system. Supports data management workflows and database lock readiness assessment.']
  randomization_method varchar [note: 'The algorithm or method used to generate the randomization assignment for this transaction (e.g., stratified block, minimization, dynamic). Captured from the RTSM configuration at the time of the transaction.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The unique randomization code assigned to the subject by the RTSM system at the time of randomization. Determines treatment arm allocation and links to the randomization schedule. Central to blinding integrity.']
  sdv_date date [note: 'The date on which Source Data Verification (SDV) was completed for this IRT transaction record. Populated only when sdv_flag is True.']
  sdv_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IRT transaction record has been subject to Source Data Verification (SDV) by a Clinical Research Associate (CRA). True if SDV has been completed; False if pending.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native primary key or record identifier of this transaction as stored in the originating IRT/RTSM source system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM internal record ID). Enables lineage tracing from the silver layer back to the operational system.']
  stratification_factor_1 varchar [note: 'The value of the first stratification factor used in the randomization algorithm at the time of this transaction (e.g., disease severity, geographic region, age group). Captured from the RTSM at the point of randomization.']
  stratification_factor_2 varchar [note: 'The value of the second stratification factor used in the randomization algorithm at the time of this transaction (e.g., prior therapy, biomarker status). Nullable if the study uses fewer than two stratification factors.']
  stratification_factor_3 varchar [note: 'The value of the third stratification factor used in the randomization algorithm at the time of this transaction. Nullable if the study uses fewer than three stratification factors.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The human-readable subject identifier assigned by the IRT/RTSM system at the time of randomization (e.g., site-subject number such as 001-001). Used for cross-system reconciliation with EDC and eCRF data. Classified as restricted PII as it can identify a trial participant.']
  transaction_outcome varchar [note: 'The technical outcome of the IRT transaction as reported by the RTSM system, indicating whether the transaction was successfully processed or encountered an error condition. Distinct from transaction_status which reflects the business lifecycle state.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the IRT transaction indicating whether it was successfully processed, failed, cancelled, or is pending confirmation in the RTSM system.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the IRT/IVRS/RTSM transaction event occurred in the source system. This is the principal real-world business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps. Conforms to yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IRT transaction indicating the business event performed. Drives downstream processing logic for supply management and randomization audit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: randomization|re_randomization|kit_assignment|dose_change|titration|cancellation|unblinding|screen_failure|withdrawal — promote to reference product]']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for emergency unblinding of a subject\'s treatment assignment (e.g., SAE requiring knowledge of treatment, DSMB review). Populated only when blinding_status is unblinded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this IRT transaction record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks corrections, reversals, or status updates applied after initial capture.']

  Note: 'Detailed log of all IRT/IVRS/RTSM system transactions related to randomization and kit dispensing. Captures transaction type (randomization, re-randomization, cancellation, kit assignment, dose change, titration), transaction timestamp, IRT system reference number, site identifier, subject identifier, operator ID, and transaction outcome. Provides the full audit trail of IRT interactions for GCP compliance and SDV. Sourced from Medidata Rave RTSM.'
}

Table randomization.kit_assignment {
  kit_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for kit_assignment']
  compliance_protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Kit dispensing errors (wrong kit, wrong dose, expired kit) result in protocol deviations. The kit_assignment has a protocol_deviation_flag but no FK to the deviation record. Linking directly enables d']
  irt_transaction_id varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier generated by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM) at the time of kit assignment. Serves as the business key for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.']
  kit_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.kit_inventory. Business justification: kit_assignment (RTSM-driven) must reference the physical kit_inventory record to close the loop between RTSM assignment and physical IMP stock. This FK is essential for IMP accountability — confirming']
  rand_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.rand_code. Business justification: A kit assignment corresponds to a specific randomization code slot. kit_assignment currently stores randomization_number (STRING) as a denormalized reference to the rand_code. Adding rand_code_id FK p']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study under which this kit assignment was made. Links the kit assignment to the parent study protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the kit was dispensed to the subject. Identifies the dispensing site for IMP accountability and supply chain tracking.']
  study_visit_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_visit_schedule. Business justification: Kit dispensing occurs at protocol-defined visits (is_dosing_visit flag in study_visit_schedule). Linking kit_assignment to the scheduled visit enables dispensing compliance reporting, visit-window adh']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject\'s randomization event that triggered this kit assignment. Establishes the link between the randomization domain and the supply/kit dispensing domain.']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduled or unscheduled study visit at which the IMP kit was dispensed to the subject.']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: A kit assignment is made to a subject in a specific treatment arm. kit_assignment currently stores treatment_arm_code (STRING) and treatment_arm_description (STRING) as denormalized references. Adding']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the enrolled study subject who received the IMP kit. Links the kit assignment to the subject randomization record for IMP accountability.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the kit assignment record, tracking the kit from initial assignment through dispensing, return, destruction, or loss. Essential for IMP accountability and regulatory compliance.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the reason for the kit assignment: initial dispensing at randomization, resupply at a subsequent visit, replacement of a damaged/lost kit, dose titration, or rescue medication dispensing.']
  blinding_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the kit assignment is blinded (treatment identity concealed), unblinded (treatment identity revealed, e.g., after emergency unblinding or study completion), or open-label. Critical for maintaining trial integrity and regulatory compliance.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for site staff or data management to record additional notes or observations related to the kit assignment event (e.g., reason for kit replacement, notes on subject compliance, dispensing anomalies).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the subject demonstrated acceptable dosing compliance for this kit assignment based on the quantity dispensed versus quantity returned. True if compliance meets protocol-defined threshold; False if a compliance issue is identified.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this kit assignment record was first created in the data system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) GCP.']
  dispensing_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the kit dispensing has been confirmed and acknowledged in the IRT/RTSM system by the site staff. True when confirmed; False when pending confirmation. Supports site monitoring and data quality checks.']
  dispensing_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the IMP kit was physically dispensed to the subject at the investigational site. Corresponds to the CDISC SDTM EXSTDTC (Exposure Start Date) and is critical for dosing compliance and PK/PD analysis.']
  dispensing_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the IMP kit dispensing transaction was recorded in the IRT/RTSM system. Supports ALCOA+ compliance (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) for audit trail purposes.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form of the IMP contained in the kit (e.g., Tablet, Capsule, Injection). Aligns with CDISC SDTM EX domain EXDOSFRM and supports IMP accountability and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Tablet|Capsule|Injection|Infusion|Patch|Oral Solution|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dose_level varchar [note: 'The protocol-defined dose level associated with the kit (e.g., \'Low Dose\', \'High Dose\', \'10mg\', \'50mg\'). Supports dose-response analysis and PK/PD modeling. May differ from treatment arm in dose-escalation or titration studies.']
  irt_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system that generated and recorded the kit assignment transaction. Important for multi-system studies and audit trail traceability.']
  kit_batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing batch or lot number of the IMP kit. Required for IMP accountability, pharmacovigilance investigations, and regulatory submissions. Enables traceability from subject exposure back to the manufacturing batch.']
  kit_expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the IMP kit as labeled by the manufacturer or packaging facility. Ensures that expired kits are not dispensed to subjects and supports IMP accountability and supply chain management.']
  kit_number varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric identifier assigned to the investigational medicinal product (IMP) kit by the RTSM/IRT system or packaging facility. Used for IMP accountability and reconciliation across the supply chain.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigational medicinal product kit indicating whether it contains active IMP, placebo, comparator drug, rescue medication, or is open-label or blinded. Critical for treatment arm integrity and blinding management.']
  protocol_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a protocol deviation was associated with this kit assignment event (e.g., wrong kit dispensed, kit dispensed outside the protocol visit window, expired kit dispensed). True if a deviation is linked; False otherwise.']
  quantity_dispensed varchar [note: 'The number of IMP units (e.g., tablets, vials, patches) dispensed to the subject as part of this kit assignment. Used for dosing compliance calculations and IMP accountability reconciliation.']
  quantity_returned varchar [note: 'The number of IMP units returned by the subject from this kit assignment. Used to calculate compliance (quantity dispensed minus quantity returned) and for IMP accountability at site level.']
  randomization_number varchar [note: 'The unique randomization number assigned to the subject by the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system at the time of randomization. Serves as the primary cross-reference between the randomization event and the kit dispensing record.']
  return_date date [note: 'The date on which the subject returned unused IMP units from this kit assignment to the investigational site. Null if no return has occurred or is not applicable.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current return status of the IMP kit: whether return is pending, the kit has been returned, the return requirement has been waived, or return is not applicable (e.g., fully consumed). Supports IMP accountability and site monitoring.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the IMP in the kit is administered to the subject (e.g., Oral, Intravenous, Subcutaneous). Aligns with CDISC SDTM EX domain EXROUTE and is required for regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Oral|Intravenous|Subcutaneous|Intramuscular|Topical|Inhaled|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stratification_factor_1 varchar [note: 'The value of the first stratification factor applied at randomization for this subject (e.g., \'Disease Severity: Mild\', \'Region: North America\'). Captured at the time of kit assignment to preserve the randomization context for statistical analysis.']
  stratification_factor_2 varchar [note: 'The value of the second stratification factor applied at randomization for this subject (e.g., \'Prior Treatment: Yes/No\', \'Age Group: <65/≥65\'). Null if fewer than two stratification factors are defined in the protocol.']
  subject_number varchar [note: 'The study-assigned subject identifier (e.g., site number + sequential subject number) used to identify the subject in the EDC and RTSM systems. Classified as restricted PII as it can be used to trace back to an individual patient.']
  temperature_excursion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temperature excursion was reported for this kit prior to or during dispensing. True if a temperature excursion was recorded; False otherwise. Relevant for temperature-sensitive IMPs and IMP accountability.']
  unblinding_date date [note: 'The date on which the kit assignment was unblinded, revealing the treatment arm identity. Populated only when blinding_status transitions to \'Unblinded\'. Null for blinded or open-label assignments.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'The documented reason for emergency or planned unblinding of the kit assignment, if applicable (e.g., \'SAE requiring treatment knowledge\', \'Study completion\', \'DSMB review\'). Null if the kit remains blinded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this kit assignment record was last modified. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records regulations.']

  Note: 'Records the assignment of an investigational medicinal product (IMP) kit to a subject at a specific visit, as triggered by the IRT/RTSM system following randomization or resupply. Captures kit number, kit type, dispensing site, dispensing visit, dispensing date, subject randomization reference, treatment arm, and kit return status. Links the randomization domain to the supply domain for IMP accountability.'
}

Table randomization.blinding_config {
  blinding_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the blinding configuration record within the randomization domain.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Blinding configurations are governed by a blinding maintenance SOP. GCP requires traceability between the blinding plan and the SOP defining how blinding is maintained and broken; this FK supports com']
  previous_version_blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding version of this blinding configuration, enabling version chain traceability for regulatory audit and TMF completeness.']
  rand_scheme_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent randomization scheme to which this blinding configuration belongs, linking blinding rules to the allocation algorithm.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_study. Business justification: Blinding configuration is a study-level regulatory artifact required for TMF section 08 and FDA/EMA submissions. Direct study_id FK on blinding_config enables study-level blinding compliance reporting']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The blinding plan is a GCP-required essential TMF document. TMF completeness assessments and regulatory inspections require direct traceability from the blinding configuration record to its filed TMF ']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time at which this blinding configuration was formally approved, captured as an auditable electronic record per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  blinding_method varchar [note: 'Physical or procedural technique used to maintain blinding integrity: identical_packaging (active and placebo packaged identically), dummy_tablets (inert tablets matching active), double_dummy (two sets of dummy treatments for crossover), over_encapsulation (active drug encapsulated to match placebo), matching_placebo, or other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: identical_packaging|dummy_tablets|double_dummy|over_encapsulation|matching_placebo|other — promote to reference product]']
  blinding_rationale varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for the selected blinding type and method as documented in the study protocol, explaining why the chosen approach is scientifically and ethically appropriate.']
  blinding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blinding design applied to the study: open_label (all parties aware of treatment), single_blind (subject unaware), double_blind (subject and investigator unaware), triple_blind (subject, investigator, and sponsor/CRA unaware). Governs access control rules for treatment assignment data.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this blinding configuration, used in RTSM/IRT system references and regulatory documentation (e.g., BLIND-001, BC-STUDY-A-DBL).']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing this blinding configuration, used in CTMS and study documentation (e.g., \'Double-Blind Double-Dummy Phase III Config\').']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the blinding configuration: draft (under development), active (in use for the study), superseded (replaced by a newer version), retired (no longer applicable).']
  cra_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Clinical Research Associates (CRAs) performing site monitoring are blinded to treatment assignment. True = CRA cannot see treatment arm during Source Data Verification (SDV).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when this blinding configuration record was first created in the data platform, supporting audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.']
  dsmb_unblinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) / Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) has access to unblinded treatment data for interim safety reviews. True = DSMB operates with unblinded data.']
  edc_access_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the EDC system (e.g., Medidata Rave) enforces field-level or form-level access restrictions to prevent blinded users from viewing treatment assignment data in the eCRF.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this blinding configuration becomes binding and operationally enforced for the study, aligned with protocol version effective date.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Date on which this blinding configuration ceases to be binding. Null indicates the configuration remains in effect indefinitely or until superseded.']
  emergency_unblinding_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether emergency unblinding is permitted at the site level for individual subjects in life-threatening situations, as governed by the protocol and IRB/IEC approval.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR data protection requirements apply to the blinding configuration and associated treatment assignment data, relevant for EU-based studies or EU subject data.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HIPAA privacy and security rules apply to the blinding configuration and associated treatment data, relevant for US-based studies involving protected health information (PHI).']
  interim_analysis_unblinding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether treatment assignment data is unblinded for interim analysis purposes (e.g., for DSMB/DMC review or adaptive design decisions). True = unblinded interim analysis is planned.']
  investigator_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Principal Investigator (PI) and site staff are blinded to treatment assignment. True = investigator does not know the subject\'s treatment arm.']
  irb_iec_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) approved this blinding configuration as part of the study protocol review.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to this blinding configuration require prior approval from the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) before implementation.']
  irt_system_enforced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether blinding access controls are enforced programmatically within the IRT/IVRS/RTSM system (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM). True = system prevents unauthorized access to treatment codes.']
  kit_label_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether investigational product kit labels are blinded (i.e., do not reveal treatment assignment). True = kit labels show only kit number and dispensing instructions, not treatment identity.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording the most recent modification to this blinding configuration record, supporting change audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or clarifications regarding this blinding configuration not captured in structured fields (e.g., site-specific blinding exceptions, packaging vendor notes).']
  randomization_code_masked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the randomization code (treatment arm identifier) is masked/encrypted in the RTSM and EDC systems for blinded users. True = treatment codes are not visible to blinded roles.']
  sae_unblinding_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing whether treatment assignment is unblinded when a Serious Adverse Event (SAE) or Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction (SUSAR) is reported: always_unblind, unblind_if_susar (only for SUSARs), maintain_blind (blinding maintained for SAE reporting), case_by_case (medical monitor decision).']
  sponsor_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sponsor personnel are blinded to treatment assignment. True = sponsor staff cannot access unblinded treatment data during the study.']
  statistician_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the biostatistician is blinded to treatment assignment during analysis. True = statistician works with blinded data until Database Lock (DBL) and unblinding per the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  subject_blinded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study subject (patient) is blinded to their treatment assignment. True = subject does not know their treatment arm.']
  unblinding_authorization_roles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of organizational roles authorized to initiate or approve unblinding (e.g., \'Medical Monitor, Pharmacovigilance Lead, DSMB Chair\'). Governs system-enforced access controls in RTSM/IRT.']
  unblinding_documentation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether each unblinding event must be formally documented with reason, date, and authorizing personnel, as required for regulatory audit trail and TMF completeness.']
  unblinding_procedure varchar [note: 'Defined procedure by which treatment assignment may be revealed: emergency_envelope (physical sealed envelope at site), irt_system (automated unblinding via IRT/IVRS/RTSM), sponsor_pharmacovigilance (unblinding for SAE/SUSAR reporting), dsmb_request (unblinding at DSMB request), study_completion (unblinding at end of study/DBL). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: emergency_envelope|irt_system|sponsor_pharmacovigilance|dsmb_request|study_completion|investigator_request — promote to reference product]']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for this blinding configuration record, supporting change control and audit trail requirements (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented upon each approved amendment.']

  Note: 'Defines the blinding configuration for a randomization scheme, including blinding type (open-label, single-blind, double-blind, triple-blind), blinded roles (subject, investigator, sponsor, CRA, statistician), blinding method (identical packaging, dummy tablets, double-dummy), and the system-enforced access controls for unblinded data. Governs who can see treatment assignments and under what conditions.'
}

Table randomization.unblinding_request {
  unblinding_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the unblinding request record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this transactional entity.']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: An unblinding request is made to break the blinding defined in a specific blinding configuration. Adding blinding_config_id FK provides direct traceability to the blinding configuration being overridd']
  causality_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.causality_assessment. Business justification: Unblinding requests are frequently triggered by a specific causality assessment that cannot be completed without knowing the treatment arm. Regulatory GCP requires documenting the clinical justificati']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Unblinding procedures must follow a defined SOP (emergency unblinding SOP). GCP requires that each unblinding request be traceable to the SOP version in effect at the time; this FK supports audit trai']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Emergency unblinding events must be reported to the FDA under IND safety reporting obligations (21 CFR 312.32). Direct link from unblinding_request to ind_application supports the IND safety reporting']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Emergency unblinding events must be documented as protocol deviations per ICH E6(R2) and reported to IRB/IEC. The unblinding_request has a boolean \'protocol_deviation_raised\' flag but no FK to the act']
  serious_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Serious Adverse Event record that triggered an emergency unblinding request. Null when the unblinding reason is not SAE-driven (e.g., planned DBL unblinding or interim analysis).']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) for which the unblinding request was raised. Links to the study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the subject is enrolled and where the unblinding request originated.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the randomization record (allocation event) for the subject whose treatment assignment is being unblinded. Links to the randomization domain master record.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Emergency and non-emergency unblinding requests with their outcomes are GCP-required records that must be filed in the eTMF as essential documents. Regulatory inspectors specifically review unblinding']
  treatment_arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.treatment_arm. Business justification: unblinding_request.treatment_arm_revealed is currently a free-text STRING capturing the treatment arm that was revealed during unblinding. This should be normalized to a proper FK referencing treatmen']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose treatment assignment is being unblinded. Applicable for single-subject emergency unblinding events; may be null for planned study-level unblinding (e.g., DBL or interim analysis).']
  triggering_lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Emergency unblinding requests triggered by critical lab findings (e.g., severe hepatotoxicity, critical hematology values) must document the specific lab result that justified unblinding per ICH E6 GC']
  triggering_susar_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.susar. Business justification: SUSAR identification is a primary regulatory trigger for emergency unblinding. ICH E2A requires that SUSARs may necessitate unblinding to complete expedited reporting. unblinding_request has serious_a']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Unblinding requests follow a formal multi-step approval workflow with defined roles, SLA, and escalation paths. GCP and ICH E6 require documented approval processes for unblinding. The workflow produc']
  approval_level_count varchar [note: 'Total number of approval levels required for this unblinding request based on the study protocol and urgency classification. Drives the multi-step approval workflow routing in the RTSM system.']
  approver_1_decision varchar [note: 'Decision rendered by the first-level approver on the unblinding request. Approved advances the request; rejected terminates it; escalated routes to a higher authority; deferred postpones pending additional information.']
  approver_1_rationale varchar [note: 'Free-text rationale provided by the first-level approver explaining their decision. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory audit trail, particularly for rejections and escalations.']
  approver_1_role varchar [note: 'Clinical or organizational role of the first-level approver at the time of their decision. Captured at decision time for audit trail integrity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_monitor|sponsor_representative|cro_medical_director|irb_chair|dsmb_member|other — promote to reference product]']
  approver_1_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the first-level approver rendered their decision. Used to calculate approval cycle time and assess compliance with urgency SLAs.']
  approver_2_decision varchar [note: 'Decision rendered by the second-level approver on the unblinding request. Follows the same decision taxonomy as level 1. Null when a second approval level is not required.']
  approver_2_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the second-level approver rendered their decision. Null when a second approval level is not required.']
  blinding_broken_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the blinding was actually broken as a result of this request (True) or whether the request was resolved without revealing the treatment assignment (False). Used for protocol deviation tracking and blinding integrity reporting.']
  clinical_justification varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative provided by the requestor describing the clinical rationale for unblinding. For SAE-driven requests, includes the nature of the adverse event, patient condition, and why knowledge of treatment assignment is medically necessary. Required for GCP compliance and audit trail.']
  conditions_of_approval varchar [note: 'Any conditions or restrictions imposed by the approver(s) on the approved unblinding (e.g., \'information to be shared only with treating physician\', \'subject must be discontinued from study\'). Null when no conditions were imposed or when the request was not approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this unblinding request record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  current_approval_level varchar [note: 'The approval step currently pending action within the multi-level approval chain. Increments as each approver completes their review. Used for workflow routing and escalation monitoring.']
  dsmb_dmc_referral_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unblinding request was referred to or reviewed by the DSMB/DMC (True) or not (False). Applicable for interim analysis unblinding or escalated emergency cases requiring independent oversight.']
  final_outcome varchar [note: 'The definitive resolution of the unblinding request after all approval levels have been completed. Approved means the treatment assignment was revealed; rejected means the request was denied; withdrawn means the requestor retracted the request; escalated means the request was referred to a higher authority (e.g., DSMB/DMC).']
  irb_iec_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the IRB/IEC must be notified of this unblinding event per the study protocol or applicable regulations (True) or not (False). Drives regulatory notification workflow.']
  irb_iec_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the IRB/IEC was formally notified of the unblinding event. Null if notification is not required or has not yet been sent. Used for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  protocol_deviation_raised boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an unplanned or unauthorized unblinding event associated with this request resulted in a protocol deviation being raised (True) or not (False). Links to the compliance domain for deviation tracking.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the unblinding request, typically generated by the RTSM/IRT system (e.g., Oracle Clinical One or Medidata Rave). Used in regulatory correspondence, audit trails, and DSMB/DMC communications.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the unblinding request workflow. Tracks progression from initial submission through multi-level approval to final resolution. Drives GCP audit trail and DSMB/DMC oversight reporting.']
  requestor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who submitted the unblinding request, captured at the time of submission for audit trail integrity per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ requirements. Preserved even if the workforce record is later modified.']
  requestor_role varchar [note: 'Clinical role of the individual who submitted the unblinding request at the time of submission. Captured at request time to preserve audit integrity even if the person\'s role changes later. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pi|sub_investigator|crc|sponsor_medical_monitor|cra|other — promote to reference product]']
  required_by_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which the unblinding decision is clinically required, as specified by the requestor. Used to enforce urgency SLAs and escalation triggers. For emergency requests, this may be within hours of submission.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this unblinding request originated. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  source_system_request_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this unblinding request in the originating operational system (Oracle Clinical One or Medidata Rave RTSM). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the lakehouse.']
  sponsor_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the study sponsor was formally notified of the unblinding event. Required for emergency unblinding events per GCP and sponsor agreements. Null if not yet notified.']
  submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the unblinding request was formally submitted by the requestor in the RTSM/IRT system. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction. Critical for measuring response time against urgency SLAs and for regulatory audit trail.']
  unblinding_reason varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the clinical or regulatory justification for the unblinding request. Drives urgency classification and approval routing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sae_medical_emergency|regulatory_requirement|dsmb_interim_analysis|database_lock|planned_unblinding|other — promote to reference product]']
  unblinding_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the unblinding request pertains to a single subject (emergency or medical necessity) or to the entire study (planned events such as Database Lock, interim analysis, or final analysis).']
  unblinding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the treatment assignment was actually revealed to the authorized recipient. Null if the request was rejected or withdrawn. Critical for GCP audit trail and regulatory reporting timelines.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this unblinding request record was most recently modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Tracks the latest state change for audit trail and data quality monitoring.']
  urgency_level varchar [note: 'Clinical urgency classification of the unblinding request. Emergency indicates immediate patient safety risk requiring unblinding within hours; urgent indicates within 24 hours; routine indicates planned/scheduled unblinding with no immediate safety concern.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the requestor when withdrawing an unblinding request before final resolution. Null when the request was not withdrawn. Required for audit trail completeness.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a request to unblind a subject\'s treatment assignment, encompassing the full request-through-resolution lifecycle including multi-level approval steps. Captures requestor identity, requestor role, clinical justification (SAE, medical emergency, regulatory requirement, planned DBL unblinding, interim analysis), urgency level, request timestamp, approval chain (approver identity, role, decision, rationale, conditions, timestamp per approval step), final outcome (approved/rejected/escalated), unblinding timestamp, and the unblinded treatment arm revealed. Covers both emergency single-subject unblinding and planned study-level unblinding events. Critical for GCP compliance, 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail, and DSMB/DMC oversight. Sourced from Oracle Clinical One and Medidata Rave RTSM.'
}

Table randomization.unblinding_approval {
  unblinding_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each unblinding approval record in the randomization domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  electronic_signature_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the 21 CFR Part 11-compliant electronic signature applied by the approver at the time of decision. Links to the electronic signature audit record in the eTMF or EDC system for regulatory submission readiness.']
  serious_adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Serious Adverse Event (SAE) record that triggered the unblinding request, when applicable. Links the approval to the safety event in Oracle Argus Safety for integrated safety reporting and SUSAR assessment.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which the unblinding approval is being recorded. Enables study-level audit trail and regulatory reporting.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the subject is enrolled and where the unblinding event is occurring.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the subject\'s randomization record whose treatment arm assignment is the subject of the unblinding request. Links to the randomization domain for treatment arm and kit assignment context.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Trial Master File (TMF) document record in Veeva Vault eTMF where the signed unblinding approval form is archived. Required for inspection readiness and regulatory submission completeness.']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trial subject (patient) whose treatment assignment blinding is being reviewed for unblinding. Classified as restricted PII as it can be linked to an individual participant.']
  unblinding_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent unblinding request that this approval decision is associated with. Links the approval record to the originating request in the multi-level approval workflow.']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Each unblinding approval decision is a discrete workflow step requiring direct traceability to the governing workflow instance for GCP audit trail. Regulatory inspectors verify that each approver\'s de']
  approval_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions or restrictions attached to a conditional approval decision (e.g., \'Unblinding permitted only to treating physician; sponsor team remains blinded\'). Populated only when approval_status is conditionally_approved.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level within the multi-tier approval hierarchy (e.g., 1 = first-line approver such as PI, 2 = second-line such as Medical Monitor, 3 = final authority such as Sponsor Medical Officer). Supports multi-level approval workflow tracking required by GCP.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for this approval record, used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-system references (e.g., UBA-STUDY01-202401). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the unblinding approval decision. Reflects the outcome of the approver\'s review: approved (unblinding authorized), rejected (unblinding denied), escalated (referred to higher authority), pending (awaiting decision), withdrawn (request retracted), or conditionally_approved (approved with attached conditions). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|rejected|escalated|pending|withdrawn|conditionally_approved — promote to reference product]']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who rendered the approval or rejection decision. Required for GCP audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature attribution. Classified as confidential as it identifies a study personnel member.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the individual who rendered the approval decision. Defines the authority level within the multi-level unblinding approval hierarchy. Valid roles include: medical_monitor, pi (Principal Investigator), sponsor_medical_officer, dsmb_chair (Data Safety Monitoring Board Chair), irb_representative (Institutional Review Board), cro_medical_director. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: medical_monitor|pi|sponsor_medical_officer|dsmb_chair|irb_representative|cro_medical_director — promote to reference product]']
  blinding_maintained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the overall study blinding integrity was maintained following this unblinding event (True = blinding maintained for the broader study population; False = blinding compromised at study level). Critical for assessing impact on study validity.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or observations recorded by the approver beyond the formal decision rationale. May include follow-up actions required, safety observations, or cross-references to related records.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this unblinding approval record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail completeness per ALCOA+ principles.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative explanation provided by the approver justifying the approval, rejection, escalation, or conditional approval decision. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory audit trail to demonstrate that the decision was medically and procedurally justified.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone) at which the approver rendered their decision on the unblinding request. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction, critical for GCP audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  delegation_of_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approver was acting under a formal delegation of authority (True = delegated authority; False = primary role holder). Relevant when a PI delegates unblinding approval authority to a sub-investigator or designee.']
  emergency_unblinding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this unblinding approval was processed under emergency procedures (True = emergency unblinding bypassing standard multi-level approval; False = standard approval workflow). Emergency unblinding events require expedited regulatory notification.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the approval decision was escalated to a higher authority rather than resolved at the current approval level. Populated only when approval_status is \'escalated\'.']
  escalation_target_role varchar [note: 'The role to which the unblinding decision has been escalated when approval_status is \'escalated\'. Identifies the next authority in the approval chain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sponsor_medical_officer|dsmb_chair|irb_chair|regulatory_authority|cro_medical_director — promote to reference product]']
  gcp_compliance_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approver explicitly confirmed that the unblinding procedure was conducted in accordance with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines and the applicable protocol SOP (True = confirmed; False = not confirmed or exception noted).']
  irb_iec_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) must be notified of this unblinding event per protocol or regulatory requirements (True = notification required; False = not required).']
  irb_iec_notified_date date [note: 'The date on which the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) was formally notified of the unblinding event. Populated when irb_iec_notification_required is True and notification has been completed.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required regulatory and sponsor notifications were dispatched following this approval decision (True = notifications sent; False = pending notification). Supports compliance tracking for expedited safety reporting timelines.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which regulatory and sponsor notifications were dispatched following the approval decision. Populated when notification_sent is True. Used to verify compliance with expedited reporting timelines (e.g., 7-day/15-day SAE reporting).']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'The version of the study protocol that was in effect at the time of the unblinding approval decision. Required to confirm that the unblinding procedure followed the correct protocol version\'s unblinding SOP.']
  response_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in hours between the submission of the unblinding request and the rendering of this approval decision. Used for KPI monitoring of emergency unblinding response times and SOP compliance.']
  signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time at which the approver applied their 21 CFR Part 11-compliant electronic signature to the approval decision. May differ from decision_timestamp if signature was applied after the verbal/written decision.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this unblinding approval record originated (e.g., Medidata Rave RTSM, Oracle Clinical One, Veeva Vault, Oracle Argus Safety, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating operational system (e.g., Medidata Rave workflow ID, Oracle Clinical One approval reference). Enables traceability from the Silver layer back to the system of record for audit and reconciliation.']
  treatment_arm_revealed varchar [note: 'The treatment arm or randomization code that was disclosed to the approver upon approval of the unblinding request (e.g., \'Active Drug A 10mg\', \'Placebo\'). Populated only after approval is granted and unblinding is executed. Classified as confidential as it contains blinded study information.']
  unblinding_reason_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the reason driving the unblinding request being reviewed. Supports regulatory reporting and trend analysis of unblinding events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: emergency_medical|sae_reporting|regulatory_requirement|dsmb_review|study_completion|protocol_deviation — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this unblinding approval record was last modified. Supports audit trail integrity and change detection per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Records the approval or rejection decision for an unblinding request. Captures the approver role (medical monitor, PI, sponsor medical officer), decision (approved, rejected, escalated), decision rationale, decision timestamp, and any conditions attached to the approval. Maintains the multi-level approval audit trail required by GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 for emergency unblinding events.'
}

Table regulatory.submission {
  submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each regulatory submission record. Primary key for the submission data product.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: A regulatory submission belongs to a specific regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). The submission table currently stores application_number as a STRING denormalized reference. Adding applicati']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Every regulatory submission is directed to a specific health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.). The submission table currently stores target_agency as a STRING denormalized reference. Adding health_aut']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this regulatory submission is made.']
  agency_acknowledgement_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or acknowledgement identifier issued by the health authority upon receipt of the submission (e.g., FDA STN number, EMA submission number). Used for all subsequent agency correspondence.']
  agency_action_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the health authority issued its regulatory decision or action (approval, complete response letter, refusal to file, approvable letter, etc.). Compared against target_action_date to measure review performance.']
  agency_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the health authority jurisdiction to which this submission is directed (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, CAN, AUS). Supports multi-regional submission tracking.']
  agency_decision varchar [note: 'The formal regulatory decision issued by the health authority in response to this submission (e.g., approved, complete response letter (CRL), refuse to file (RTF), approvable, not approvable, withdrawn). Drives downstream regulatory strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|complete_response|refuse_to_file|approvable|not_approvable|withdrawn|pending — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agency_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the submission. Triggers the start of the agency review clock for PDUFA/regulatory review timelines.']
  cover_letter_title varchar [note: 'The official title or subject line of the cover letter accompanying this submission, as filed with the health authority. Provides a human-readable summary of the submission purpose for regulatory correspondence tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this submission record was first created in the system of record (Veeva Vault). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctd_module_scope varchar [note: 'Identifies which CTD module(s) are included in this submission (e.g., Module 1 - Administrative, Module 2 - Summaries, Module 3 - Quality, Module 4 - Nonclinical, Module 5 - Clinical). Supports dossier completeness tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: module_1|module_2|module_3|module_4|module_5|all_modules — promote to reference product]']
  dossier_format varchar [note: 'Technical format of the regulatory dossier submitted to the health authority: eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document), NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission), VNeeS (Veterinary NeeS), or paper. Determines validation and processing requirements.']
  drug_product_name varchar [note: 'The proprietary or non-proprietary name of the investigational drug, biologic, or device that is the subject of this regulatory submission. Provides direct product context for the submission record.']
  ectd_operation varchar [note: 'The eCTD lifecycle operation applied to documents in this submission sequence: new (first submission of a document), replace (supersedes a prior version), append (adds to an existing document), or delete (removes a document from the dossier). Governs dossier evolution per ICH M8.']
  filing_fee_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the applicable regulatory filing fee (e.g., FDA PDUFA application fee, EMA fee) has been paid for this submission. Non-payment can result in refuse-to-file action.']
  indication varchar [note: 'The specific disease, condition, or patient population for which the investigational product is being developed or for which approval is sought in this submission. Aligns with MedDRA preferred term coding.']
  inn_name varchar [note: 'The WHO-assigned International Nonproprietary Name (INN) for the active substance that is the subject of this submission. Provides standardized global drug identification aligned with WHO-DD.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is subject to an expedited review pathway (e.g., FDA Breakthrough Therapy, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, EMA PRIME scheme). Drives prioritization in regulatory operations workflows.']
  is_resubmission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is a resubmission following a prior agency rejection, complete response letter (CRL), or refuse-to-file (RTF) action. Triggers specific regulatory review timelines under PDUFA.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, internal commentary, or regulatory strategy notes associated with this submission event. Not transmitted to the agency; for internal regulatory operations use only.']
  phase varchar [note: 'The clinical development phase associated with this submission (e.g., Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3, Phase 4, pre-IND, post-approval). Contextualizes the submission within the drug development lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phase_1|phase_1_2|phase_2|phase_2_3|phase_3|phase_4|pre_IND|post_approval — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  prior_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the immediately preceding submission in the dossier lifecycle that this submission amends, replaces, or responds to. Enables reconstruction of the full dossier evolution chain.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Narrative or coded reason for this submission event (e.g., initial IND filing, protocol amendment for safety update, response to FDA information request, annual IND report, post-approval labeling change). Provides business context for the submission trigger.']
  regulatory_procedure_type varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory procedure under which this submission is filed (e.g., standard review, priority review, accelerated approval, breakthrough therapy, orphan drug, centralized procedure, mutual recognition procedure, decentralized procedure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard_review|priority_review|accelerated_approval|breakthrough_therapy|orphan_drug|centralized|mutual_recognition|decentralized|rolling_review — promote to reference product]']
  resubmission_class varchar [note: 'For FDA resubmissions following a complete response letter (CRL), the classification of the resubmission: Class 1 (2-month review goal) or Class 2 (6-month review goal), based on the nature of the response. Null or not_applicable for non-resubmissions.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit zero-padded sequence number identifying the specific eCTD submission sequence within a dossier lifecycle (e.g., 0000 for initial submission, 0001 for first amendment). Conforms to ICH M8 eCTD specification.']
