  submission_category varchar [note: 'High-level regulatory category of the submission indicating the application type: IND (Investigational New Drug), NDA (New Drug Application), BLA (Biologics License Application), MAA (Marketing Authorization Application), CTA (Clinical Trial Authorization), or IND amendment, protocol amendment, safety report, etc. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|CTA|IND_amendment|protocol_amendment|safety_report|annual_report|other — promote to reference product]']
  submission_date date [note: 'The actual calendar date on which the submission was transmitted or delivered to the target health authority. This is the principal real-world event date for regulatory timeline calculations and compliance tracking.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'The technical method by which the submission was delivered to the health authority (e.g., electronic gateway such as FDA ESG or EMA eSubmission Gateway, physical media, agency portal, email, or paper). Relevant for audit trail and compliance with agency technical requirements.']
  submission_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for this submission as assigned by the health authority or internal tracking system (e.g., IND number, NDA number, sequence number within an eCTD dossier). Used for cross-referencing with agency correspondence.']
  submission_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the submission: draft (being prepared), pending (awaiting dispatch), submitted (sent to agency), validated (passed technical validation), accepted (agency acknowledged receipt), rejected (agency refused), or withdrawn (sponsor retracted). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending|submitted|validated|accepted|rejected|withdrawn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone) at which the submission was electronically transmitted to the health authority gateway. Used for exact regulatory deadline compliance and audit trail purposes per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the submission event in the dossier lifecycle: original (first submission), amendment (change to an approved application), supplement (post-approval change), response (reply to agency query or complete response letter), or withdrawal (retraction of a prior submission).']
  target_action_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which the health authority is expected to take action on the submission (e.g., PDUFA goal date for FDA NDA/BLA reviews, EMA opinion deadline). Critical for regulatory milestone tracking and resource planning.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The clinical or disease area that the investigational product targets (e.g., oncology, cardiology, neurology, infectious disease, rare disease). Used for portfolio analytics and regulatory strategy segmentation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this submission record was most recently modified in the system of record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Technical validation outcome of the eCTD dossier package against agency-published validation criteria (e.g., FDA eCTD Validation Criteria, EMA eCTD Specification). Indicates whether the submission passed automated technical checks prior to or upon agency receipt.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault (eTMF/Regulatory Submissions module) to the submission package or primary dossier document. Enables direct traceability to the source system of record.']

  Note: 'Master record for regulatory submissions and eCTD sequences sent to health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada). Each record represents a discrete submission event with sequence number, submission type (original, amendment, supplement, response, withdrawal), eCTD lifecycle operation (new, replace, append, delete), CTD module scope, submission category, regulatory procedure type, submission date, target agency, validation status, dossier format (eCTD/NeeS/paper), and submission reason. Enables granular tracking of dossier evolution over time. SSOT for all regulatory submission events managed via Veeva Vault.'
}

Table regulatory.application {
  application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the regulatory application record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for all downstream joins to submissions, correspondence, approvals, and commitments under this dossier.']
  accelerated_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this application is approved or being reviewed under the FDA Accelerated Approval pathway, based on a surrogate or intermediate clinical endpoint reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit. Requires post-approval confirmatory trials.']
  agency_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the health authority jurisdiction (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, CAN, AUS). Supports multi-regional dossier tracking and jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting.']
  agency_receipt_date date [note: 'Date the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the application. May differ from initial_filing_date if the agency issues a receipt acknowledgment letter after a validation check.']
  annual_report_last_submitted_date date [note: 'Date the most recent IND Annual Report was submitted to FDA. Used to calculate the next due date and to confirm compliance with 21 CFR 312.33 annual reporting obligations.']
  application_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned application number issued by the health authority (e.g., IND 123456, NDA 210001, BLA 761234, EMA/H/C/004321, CTA-2024-001). This is the official dossier identifier used in all agency correspondence and is the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this entity.']
  application_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory application. \'active\' = application is open and under review or in use; \'approved\' = marketing authorization or IND in good standing with approval; \'withdrawn\' = sponsor voluntarily withdrew; \'refused\' = agency issued a refuse-to-file or complete response letter with no resubmission; \'on_clinical_hold\' = FDA-issued clinical hold per 21 CFR 312.42; \'inactive\' = IND placed in inactive status.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory application by submission type. IND = Investigational New Drug; NDA = New Drug Application; BLA = Biologics License Application; MAA = Marketing Authorization Application; CTA = Clinical Trial Authorization; IMPD = Investigational Medicinal Product Dossier.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority granted marketing authorization or IND approval. Null for applications that have not yet been approved. Critical milestone for commercial launch planning and post-approval commitment tracking.']
  breakthrough_therapy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FDA has granted Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) for this application, enabling intensive FDA guidance and rolling review. Granted when preliminary clinical evidence shows substantial improvement over available therapy for serious conditions.']
  clinical_hold_date date [note: 'Date on which the FDA issued the clinical hold order for this application. Null if no clinical hold has been imposed. Used to track hold duration and response timelines.']
  clinical_hold_status varchar [note: 'Current clinical hold status issued by FDA under 21 CFR 312.42. \'none\' = no hold; \'partial\' = hold on specific studies or patient populations; \'full\' = all clinical investigations halted; \'lifted\' = hold previously issued but now removed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory application record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  ctd_module varchar [note: 'Indicates which CTD modules are included in this application. M1=Regional Administrative; M2=Summaries; M3=Quality; M4=Nonclinical; M5=Clinical. \'full\' indicates all modules are present. Supports dossier completeness tracking.']
  dossier_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code of the primary language in which the regulatory dossier is submitted (e.g., ENG for English, JPN for Japanese, DEU for German). Relevant for multi-regional submissions requiring translation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the application became legally effective and binding. For INDs, this is 30 days after FDA receipt unless a clinical hold is issued. For approved applications, this is the date the approval letter takes effect.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the application or authorization expires, if applicable. Relevant for CTAs with fixed authorization periods and some national approvals. Null for open-ended authorizations such as US NDA/BLA approvals.']
  fast_track_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FDA has granted Fast Track Designation for this application, facilitating development and expediting review of drugs to treat serious conditions and fill unmet medical needs. Enables rolling review and more frequent FDA interactions.']
  ind_annual_report_due_date date [note: 'Next due date for the IND Annual Report submission to FDA, calculated as 60 days before the anniversary of the IND effective date. Tracks compliance with mandatory annual reporting obligations.']
  ind_number varchar [note: 'FDA-assigned IND serial number for investigational applications (e.g., IND 123456). Populated only for IND-type applications. Used in all FDA correspondence, annual reports, and safety reporting.']
  ind_type varchar [note: 'Subclassification of the IND by purpose. Commercial IND is sponsor-held for drug development; Research IND is investigator-initiated; Treatment IND allows expanded access; Emergency IND is for single-patient emergency use.']
  initial_filing_date date [note: 'Date the application was first submitted to the health authority. For INDs, this is the date the IND package was received by FDA. For NDAs/BLAs, this is the date of original submission. This is the principal BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this entity.']
  lead_agency varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory health authority to which this application is filed. Determines the regulatory pathway, submission format, and review timelines applicable to this dossier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|Health Canada|TGA|ANVISA|NMPA|Other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lifecycle_phase varchar [note: 'Current development phase associated with this regulatory application. Tracks the application from pre-IND through post-approval lifecycle. Supports portfolio planning and regulatory strategy alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_ind|phase_1|phase_2|phase_3|nda_bla_maa|post_approval|discontinued — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional regulatory context, strategic notes, or agency-specific instructions not captured in structured fields. Used by regulatory affairs teams for internal documentation.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA Office of Orphan Products Development (OOPD) or EMA Committee for Orphan Medicinal Products (COMP) has granted Orphan Drug Designation for this application. Confers 7-year market exclusivity (FDA) or 10-year (EMA) and fee waivers.']
  pdufa_goal_date date [note: 'FDA-assigned PDUFA action date by which the agency commits to completing its review of an NDA or BLA. Applicable only to marketing applications under PDUFA. Drives internal milestone planning and commercial readiness timelines.']
  priority_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has granted Priority Review designation to this NDA or BLA, reducing the standard review clock from 12 months to 6 months. Applicable to drugs offering major advances in treatment or providing treatment where no adequate therapy exists.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the drug, biologic, or device covered by this application. May be the INN (International Nonproprietary Name), proprietary brand name, or code name at the investigational stage.']
  product_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the investigational or approved product by modality. Determines the applicable regulatory pathway, CMC requirements, and review division assignment at the agency.']
  rolling_review boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority has granted rolling review, allowing submission and review of completed sections of the dossier before the full application is submitted. Applicable under FDA Fast Track and EMA PRIME/rolling review procedures.']
  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor\'s designated regulatory contact as listed on the application cover sheet (e.g., FDA Form 1571 for INDs). This individual is the official point of contact for agency communications.']
  submission_format varchar [note: 'Technical format used for dossier submission to the health authority. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the standard for FDA, EMA, PMDA; NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission) is a transitional format; paper submissions are legacy.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification within the application type. Examples for IND: Commercial, Research, Treatment, Emergency; for NDA: 505(b)(1) Full, 505(b)(2), 505(j) ANDA; for BLA: Original, Supplement, Efficacy Supplement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Commercial|Research|Treatment|Emergency|505(b)(1)|505(b)(2)|505(j)|Original|Supplement|Efficacy Supplement — promote to reference product]']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Broad disease or medical specialty area covered by the application (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease, Immunology). Used for portfolio segmentation, resource allocation, and regulatory strategy reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory application record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  veeva_vault_dossier_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier linking this application record to the corresponding eTMF/regulatory dossier in Veeva Vault (the system of record for regulatory submissions and document management). Enables traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the source system.']

  Note: 'Master record for regulatory applications (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA, IMPD) filed with health authorities. Tracks application number, application type and subtype, therapeutic area, indication, sponsor reference, lead agency, application status (active, approved, withdrawn, refused), initial filing date, approval date, lifecycle phase, and type-specific attributes including IND number/type/annual report dates for investigational applications and PDUFA goal date/priority review/breakthrough therapy/orphan drug designations for marketing applications. Parent entity for all submissions, correspondence, approvals, and commitments under a given regulatory dossier. SSOT for the complete regulatory application lifecycle.'
}

Table regulatory.health_authority {
  health_authority_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the health authority record in the regulatory reference master. Primary key for all downstream FK references across submission, application, correspondence, and authorization records.']
  acronym varchar [note: 'Widely recognized abbreviation for the health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, HC). Used in reporting dashboards, regulatory tracking systems, and internal communications.']
  agency_code varchar [note: 'Short, standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, HC, TGA, ANVISA, NMPA). Used as a business key in regulatory workflows, submission routing, and eCTD gateway configuration.']
  agency_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the health authority record. Active indicates the agency is currently accepting submissions; inactive indicates it is no longer operational; merged indicates it has been absorbed into another agency; superseded indicates it has been replaced by a successor body.']
  annual_safety_report_type varchar [note: 'Type of periodic safety report required by this health authority for ongoing clinical trials (e.g., DSUR - Development Safety Update Report per ICH E2F for EMA/MHRA, IND Annual Report per 21 CFR 312.33 for FDA, PBRER for post-marketing). Drives safety reporting schedule configuration.']
  authority_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the health authority by its jurisdictional scope: national (single country, e.g., FDA, PMDA), supranational (multi-country, e.g., EMA), regional (sub-national or regional body), or reference_agency (recognized reference authority used by other agencies for reliance procedures).']
  clinical_trial_registry_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority requires mandatory registration of clinical trials in a public registry (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov for FDA, EU Clinical Trials Register for EMA, JAPIC for PMDA) as a condition of authorization or publication. Drives trial registration workflow in CTMS.']
  contact_division_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary regulatory division or office within the health authority responsible for receiving submissions and correspondence for the sponsor\'s therapeutic area (e.g., FDA CDER Office of New Drugs, EMA Human Medicines Division, PMDA New Drug Division). Used to address regulatory correspondence and meeting requests.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address for regulatory correspondence with the health authority\'s primary contact division. Used for submission cover letter addressing, meeting request submissions, and regulatory query responses.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Official telephone number for the health authority\'s primary regulatory contact division. Used for urgent regulatory communications, inspection notifications, and meeting coordination.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the jurisdiction in which the health authority operates (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, AUS, CAN, BRA, CHN). Drives country-level regulatory strategy and submission planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health authority record was first created in the regulatory reference master. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles for regulatory data.']
  ctd_format_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority mandates submission of dossiers in the ICH Common Technical Document (CTD) format. True for ICH member agencies (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada); may be false for non-ICH jurisdictions with proprietary submission formats.']
  data_privacy_framework varchar [note: 'Primary data privacy legislation or framework applicable to clinical trial data collected under this health authority\'s jurisdiction (e.g., GDPR for EMA/MHRA, HIPAA for FDA, PIPEDA for Health Canada, APPI for PMDA). Governs patient data handling, consent requirements, and cross-border data transfer restrictions.']
  ectd_gateway_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the health authority\'s eCTD submission gateway endpoint (e.g., FDA ESG sender ID, EMA DUNS number for eSubmission). Required for configuring automated eCTD transmission via Veeva Vault and validating submission acknowledgement receipts.']
  ectd_specification_version varchar [note: 'Version of the eCTD technical specification required by this health authority for electronic submissions (e.g., 3.2.2, 4.0). Governs the XML backbone structure, validation criteria, and submission package format for IND, NDA, BLA, and MAA applications.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this health authority record is considered valid and active for use in regulatory submissions and correspondence. Supports temporal validity tracking for agencies that have been established, reorganized, or renamed.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this health authority record is no longer valid (e.g., due to agency merger, dissolution, or supersession). Null indicates the record is currently active with no planned end date. Supports historical regulatory record integrity.']
  electronic_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority mandates electronic submission (eCTD or equivalent) for regulatory applications. True for FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA; may be false for agencies still accepting paper or hybrid submissions. Governs Veeva Vault submission package configuration.']
  fee_schedule_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the current regulatory fee schedule for this health authority is effective. Fee schedules are typically updated annually (e.g., FDA PDUFA fees effective October 1 each fiscal year). Used to ensure correct fee amounts are applied in SAP S/4HANA budget planning.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete official name of the health authority organization (e.g., U.S. Food and Drug Administration, European Medicines Agency, Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency). Used in regulatory correspondence, submission cover letters, and CTD/eCTD dossier headers.']
  gcp_guideline_version varchar [note: 'Version of the GCP guideline mandated or recognized by this health authority (e.g., ICH E6(R2), ICH E6(R3), EU CTR GCP Annex). Governs clinical trial conduct standards, monitoring requirements, and inspection readiness criteria applicable to studies submitted to this agency.']
  ich_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority is a full member or observer of the International Council for Harmonisation of Technical Requirements for Pharmaceuticals for Human Use (ICH). ICH membership determines applicability of harmonized guidelines (CTD, eCTD, GCP, safety reporting) and mutual recognition procedures.']
  ind_application_type varchar [note: 'Local equivalent application type for initiating clinical trials under this health authority\'s jurisdiction (e.g., IND for FDA, CTA for EMA/MHRA/Health Canada, JIND for PMDA, CTN for TGA). Drives submission template selection and regulatory timeline planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|CTA|JIND|CTN|ANVISA_CTA|NMPA_IND — promote to reference product]']
  ind_review_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the health authority has to review and respond to a clinical trial authorization application before the sponsor may proceed (e.g., 30 days for FDA IND, 30 days for EMA CTA under EU CTR). Critical for study start-up timeline planning.']
  ind_submission_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard regulatory fee amount charged by this health authority for an IND or CTA application in the currency specified by submission_fee_currency. May be zero for agencies that do not charge IND/CTA fees (e.g., FDA does not charge IND fees). Used for regulatory budget planning.']
  inspection_authority boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this health authority has the mandate and capability to conduct Good Clinical Practice (GCP) inspections of clinical trial sites, sponsors, and CROs. True for FDA, EMA (via national competent authorities), MHRA, PMDA. Drives inspection readiness program scope.']
  local_language_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority requires submission documents (or portions thereof) to be translated into the local national language in addition to English. True for agencies such as PMDA (Japanese), NMPA (Chinese), ANVISA (Portuguese). Drives translation cost and timeline planning.']
  mailing_address varchar [note: 'Official mailing address of the health authority\'s primary contact division for paper-based regulatory correspondence, original document submissions, and formal notifications where electronic submission is not accepted.']
  mutual_recognition_agreements varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mutual recognition agreement frameworks in which this health authority participates (e.g., FDA-EMA Parallel Scientific Advice, MHRA-EMA MRA, ACSS - Australia-Canada-Singapore-Switzerland). Informs regulatory reliance strategy and submission optimization.']
  nda_application_type varchar [note: 'Local equivalent marketing authorization application type for this health authority (e.g., NDA/BLA for FDA, MAA for EMA, J-NDA for PMDA, ANDS for Health Canada). Used to configure submission workflows, fee schedules, and review clock tracking in Veeva Vault. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NDA|BLA|MAA|J-NDA|ANDS|ARTG — promote to reference product]']
  nda_submission_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard regulatory submission fee amount charged by this health authority for a full marketing authorization application (NDA/BLA/MAA) in the currency specified by submission_fee_currency. Used for regulatory budget planning and SAP S/4HANA accounts payable. Updated annually per agency fee schedules.']
  non_fatal_sadr_reporting_clock_hours varchar [note: 'Mandatory expedited safety reporting timeline in hours for non-fatal, non-life-threatening SUSARs to this health authority (e.g., 15 calendar days / 360 hours for FDA and EMA). Used alongside the fatal SUSAR clock to configure Oracle Argus Safety reporting deadlines.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for regulatory affairs teams to capture additional context, special submission requirements, known agency preferences, or recent regulatory guidance updates specific to this health authority that are not captured in structured fields.']
  paper_submission_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority still accepts paper-based regulatory submissions as an alternative or supplement to electronic submissions. Relevant for agencies in transition to full eCTD mandates or for specific submission types (e.g., original INDs, amendments).']
  priority_review_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days in the expedited or priority review clock for marketing authorization applications under this health authority (e.g., 180 days for FDA priority NDA review, 150 days for EMA accelerated assessment). Supports regulatory strategy planning for breakthrough and priority designations.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Geopolitical or regulatory region associated with the health authority (e.g., North America, European Union, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, Middle East & Africa). Supports regional regulatory strategy grouping and multi-regional submission planning.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework or legislation governing the health authority\'s operations (e.g., 21 CFR Parts 312/314/601 for FDA, EU CTR 536/2014 for EMA, PMDA Act for Japan, Medicines Act for MHRA). Critical for determining applicable submission requirements and compliance obligations.']
  review_procedure_type varchar [note: 'Primary review procedure applicable under this health authority (e.g., Standard Review, Priority Review, Accelerated Assessment, Breakthrough Therapy, PRIME, Conditional Approval, Orphan Drug). Determines review timelines and regulatory strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|priority|accelerated|breakthrough|prime|conditional|orphan — promote to reference product]']
  sadr_reporting_clock_hours varchar [note: 'Mandatory expedited safety reporting timeline in hours for fatal or life-threatening SUSARs to this health authority (e.g., 7 calendar days / 168 hours for FDA, 7 days for EMA). Drives automated deadline calculation in Oracle Argus Safety for SUSAR and ICSR submissions.']
  standard_review_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days in the standard regulatory review clock for marketing authorization applications under this health authority (e.g., 365 days for FDA standard NDA review, 210 days for EMA MAA). Used for regulatory milestone planning and submission timeline modeling.']
  submission_fee_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for regulatory submission fees charged by this health authority (e.g., USD for FDA PDUFA fees, EUR for EMA fees, JPY for PMDA fees). Used in SAP S/4HANA for regulatory fee budget planning and accounts payable processing.']
  submission_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639 language code(s) required for regulatory submissions to this health authority (e.g., en for English, ja for Japanese, zh for Chinese, de/fr/it for Switzerland). Governs translation requirements for CTD modules, labeling, and patient-facing documents.']
  submission_portal_url varchar [note: 'Official URL of the health authority\'s electronic submission portal (e.g., FDA ESG gateway, EMA eSubmission portal, PMDA eCTD gateway). Used by regulatory affairs teams to route eCTD dossier submissions and track submission acknowledgements.']
  trial_registry_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary public clinical trial registry mandated by this health authority for trial registration (e.g., ClinicalTrials.gov for FDA, EU Clinical Trials Register for EMA, JAPIC CTI for PMDA, ANZCTR for TGA). Used to configure trial registration workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this health authority record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, data lineage, and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']

  Note: 'Reference master for global health authority organizations (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada, TGA, ANVISA, NMPA, etc.). Stores agency code, full name, country/region, regulatory framework, submission portal URL, eCTD gateway identifier, contact division, applicable regulatory procedures, and fee schedules. Used as a foreign key lookup across all submission, application, correspondence, and authorization records in the regulatory domain.'
}

Table regulatory.submission_sequence {
  submission_sequence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each eCTD submission sequence record within the regulatory application dossier.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) to which this submission sequence belongs.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Each eCTD submission sequence is sent to a specific health authority. The regulatory_authority_code and submission_country_code STRING fields on submission_sequence are denormalized references to heal']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Each eCTD submission sequence is packaged as a regulatory binder. Regulatory publishing teams build a binder per sequence; linking submission_sequence to regulatory_binder enables sequence-level binde']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this submission sequence, where the sequence relates to a specific study (e.g., a clinical study report submission). Null for application-level sequences not tied to a single study.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Each eCTD submission sequence generates a cover letter and eCTD validation report filed as TMF documents. Linking submission sequences to their TMF documents enables sequence-level document tracking a']
  veeva_vault_submission_id varchar [note: 'External identifier of this submission sequence record within Veeva Vault RIM Submissions module, the system of record for regulatory dossier management. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational eTMF/RIM system.']
  agency_acknowledgement_number varchar [note: 'Official confirmation or acknowledgement number issued by the regulatory authority upon receipt of the submission sequence (e.g., FDA ESG acknowledgement ID, EMA CESP submission ID). Used for tracking and correspondence reference.']
  agency_action_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority issued a formal action or decision on this submission sequence (e.g., approval letter, complete response letter, refuse-to-file letter, Day 120 list of questions). Null if no action has been issued.']
  agency_action_type varchar [note: 'Type of formal regulatory action issued by the agency in response to this submission sequence. Drives downstream regulatory strategy and response planning.']
  agency_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority officially acknowledged receipt of this submission sequence. Triggers the start of the agency review clock (e.g., PDUFA clock for FDA, Day 0 for EMA).']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this submission sequence reflecting the sensitivity of the dossier content. Governs access controls within Veeva Vault and the lakehouse. \'highly_confidential\' applies to trade secret CMC data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this submission sequence record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  ctd_module_scope varchar [note: 'Identifies which CTD module(s) are primarily affected by this submission sequence. Module 1: Administrative/Regional; Module 2: Summaries; Module 3: Quality; Module 4: Nonclinical; Module 5: Clinical. \'multiple\' indicates cross-module content.']
  ctd_section_list varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of specific CTD section identifiers (e.g., \'3.2.P.1, 3.2.P.2, 5.3.5.1\') included or modified in this submission sequence. Enables granular tracking of which dossier sections evolved with each sequence.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in this eCTD submission sequence package. Supports completeness verification and dossier audit.']
  dossier_package_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label assigned to the eCTD submission package for this sequence (e.g., \'NDA 123456 Seq 0012 — CMC Amendment\'). Used for identification in Veeva Vault and internal tracking systems.']
  dossier_package_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total file size in megabytes of the eCTD submission package for this sequence. Used for submission gateway capacity planning and agency file size compliance checks.']
  ectd_operation varchar [note: 'Specifies the eCTD XML backbone lifecycle operation applied to documents in this sequence. \'new\' adds documents for the first time; \'replace\' supersedes a prior document; \'append\' adds to an existing document set; \'delete\' removes a document from the active dossier view.']
  ectd_validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the eCTD technical validation was performed for this submission sequence. Used to confirm validation was completed prior to submission deadline.']
  ectd_validation_error_count varchar [note: 'Total number of technical validation errors identified during eCTD validation of this submission sequence. A value of zero is required for a passing validation status.']
  ectd_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the eCTD technical validation check performed against the agency\'s published validation criteria prior to or upon submission. \'pass\' indicates full conformance; \'warning\' indicates non-critical issues; \'fail\' indicates the sequence was rejected for technical non-conformance.']
  ectd_validation_tool varchar [note: 'Name and version of the eCTD validation software used to validate this submission sequence (e.g., \'Lorenz docuBridge v5.2\', \'Extedo eCTD Manager v8.1\', \'Veeva Vault RIM Submissions v23.1\'). Required for audit trail and reproducibility.']
  ectd_validation_warning_count varchar [note: 'Total number of non-critical technical validation warnings identified during eCTD validation of this submission sequence. Warnings do not block submission but must be reviewed and justified.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission sequence qualifies for or was filed under an expedited regulatory pathway (e.g., FDA Fast Track, Breakthrough Therapy, Priority Review; EMA PRIME). True if expedited designation applies.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission sequence contains or is primarily driven by safety-related content (e.g., IND Safety Report, SUSAR narrative, labeling safety update, DSUR). True if safety content is the primary driver.']
  prior_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit sequence number of the immediately preceding submission sequence that this sequence supersedes or builds upon. Enables reconstruction of the dossier evolution chain and supports lifecycle operation traceability.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the sponsor must submit a response to an agency information request or complete response letter associated with this submission sequence. Null if no response is required.']
  review_clock_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority\'s formal review clock begins for this submission sequence (e.g., PDUFA Day 1 for FDA, Day 0 for EMA). Derived from agency acknowledgement and used for PDUFA/review deadline tracking.']
  review_clock_target_date date [note: 'Target date by which the regulatory authority is expected to complete review of this submission sequence, calculated from the review clock start date per applicable PDUFA or EMA timelines. Critical for regulatory milestone planning.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit zero-padded sequence number uniquely identifying this submission within the regulatory application lifecycle (e.g., 0000 for original, 0001 for first amendment). Assigned per ICH M8 eCTD specification and used by agency systems to order dossier evolution.']
  submission_subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification of the submission type providing regulatory-specific granularity (e.g., Prior Approval Supplement, Changes Being Effected, Complete Response, Major Amendment, Minor Amendment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prior_approval_supplement|changes_being_effected|complete_response|major_amendment|minor_amendment|annual_report — promote to reference product]']
  submission_unit varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory submission unit or grouping within the eCTD backbone that this sequence belongs to (e.g., \'IND Safety Report\', \'NDA Module 5 Clinical Study Report\', \'BLA CMC Supplement\'). Aligns with Veeva Vault RIM submission unit taxonomy.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this submission sequence record, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual eCTD submission sequences within a regulatory application. Each sequence has a sequence number, submission type (original, amendment, supplement, response, withdrawal), sequence date, eCTD lifecycle operation (new, replace, append, delete), CTD module scope, submission reason, and validation status (eCTD technical validation result). Enables granular tracking of dossier evolution over time.'
}

Table regulatory.regulatory_protocol_amendment {
  regulatory_protocol_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the protocol amendment record in the regulatory domain. Primary key for this entity.']
  ethics_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_submission. Business justification: Protocol amendments trigger ethics re-submissions. Linking the regulatory amendment to its ethics submission enables coordinated tracking of parallel regulatory and ethics review timelines, a standard']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Protocol amendments are submitted to specific health authorities and ethics committees. The health_authority STRING field and submission_country_code on protocol_amendment are denormalized references ']
  parent_application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA/CTA) under which this protocol amendment is filed.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific study protocol document that this amendment modifies.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent clinical study to which this protocol amendment belongs.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Protocol amendments are submitted to health authorities as part of regulatory submissions (eCTD sequences). Linking protocol_amendment to the specific submission provides submission-level amendment tr']
  submission_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission_sequence. Business justification: A protocol amendment is submitted as part of a specific eCTD submission sequence. The regulatory_protocol_amendment table currently stores ectd_sequence_number as a STRING denormalized reference. Addi']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Protocol amendment approval letters, ethics committee opinions, and agency responses are TMF essential documents. Linking amendment records to their TMF documents enables inspection readiness verifica']
  affected_sections varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list or narrative description of the specific protocol sections modified by this amendment (e.g., \'Section 4.1 Inclusion Criteria, Section 6.2 Dosing Schedule\'). Supports impact assessment and site notification planning.']
  agency_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the protocol amendment submission. Initiates the regulatory review period clock per applicable regulations.']
  agency_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority granted formal approval or no-objection for the protocol amendment. For non-substantial amendments, this may be the date of implicit approval after the notification period.']
  agency_response_comments varchar [note: 'Summary of conditions, queries, or comments provided by the health authority in their formal response to the amendment submission. Drives follow-up actions and response preparation.']
  agency_response_date date [note: 'Date on which the formal response (approval, rejection, clinical hold, information request) was received from the health authority.']
  agency_response_type varchar [note: 'Type of formal response received from the health authority regarding the amendment submission. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|approved_with_conditions|clinical_hold|information_requested|rejected|no_objection|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol amendment document was finalized and signed off internally, prior to regulatory submission. Represents the official amendment authoring date per ALCOA+ principles.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for the amendment within the study protocol (e.g., AM-001, AM-002). Used to track the version history of protocol changes and referenced in regulatory submissions.']
  amendment_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed scientific, safety, or operational justification for the protocol amendment as documented in the amendment cover letter and regulatory submission package. Required by health authorities to assess the necessity and appropriateness of the change.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the protocol amendment across all regulatory and ethics review processes. Drives workflow routing in Veeva Vault and Oracle Siebel CTMS. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|withdrawn|implemented — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  amendment_title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title summarizing the primary purpose or scope of the protocol amendment (e.g., \'Addition of New Biomarker Assessments\', \'Dose Escalation Cohort Expansion\').']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the amendment per regulatory definitions: substantial (requires prior regulatory/ethics approval), non-substantial (notification only), administrative (clerical corrections), or urgent_safety (immediate safety measure). Determines the regulatory submission pathway and timelines.']
  amendment_version varchar [note: 'Version label of the amended protocol document (e.g., v2.0, v3.1) as tracked in Veeva Vault regulatory binder. Distinct from amendment number — version reflects the resulting protocol document version after the amendment is applied.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Concise narrative summary of all changes introduced by this amendment, including additions, deletions, and modifications to protocol text. Included in the amendment cover letter submitted to health authorities and IRB/IEC.']
  clinical_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority has placed a clinical hold on the study as a result of this amendment submission or related safety concerns. A clinical hold suspends enrollment and/or ongoing study conduct.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this protocol amendment record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  dsmb_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or Data Monitoring Committee (DMC) must be notified of this protocol amendment, typically required for amendments affecting safety monitoring, stopping rules, or interim analysis plans.']
  ethics_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the IRB/IEC granted approval for the protocol amendment. Required before implementation at sites under the jurisdiction of that ethics committee.']
  ethics_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol amendment was submitted to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) for review and approval.']
  global_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment was submitted simultaneously to multiple health authorities as part of a global regulatory strategy, requiring coordinated timelines across FDA, EMA, PMDA, and other agencies.']
  icf_version_impacted varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the Informed Consent Form (ICF) that must be updated as a result of this amendment. Links to the ICF version management workflow in Clinpal and the document management system.']
  implementation_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol amendment was operationally implemented at clinical sites, including updated procedures, training, and revised eCRF forms in Medidata Rave. Must be on or after all required regulatory and ethics approvals.']
  planned_implementation_date date [note: 'Target date for operational implementation of the amendment at clinical sites, used for project planning and site readiness tracking in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  prior_approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment requires prior approval from the health authority before implementation (true for substantial amendments) versus notification-only (false for non-substantial/administrative amendments).']
  reconsent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether enrolled subjects must provide updated informed consent (ICF re-consent) as a result of this amendment. Triggers ICF version update workflow in Clinpal and site notification via Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  review_deadline_date date [note: 'Calculated or mandated deadline by which the health authority must respond to the amendment submission per applicable regulations (e.g., 30-day FDA IND amendment review window, EMA assessment timeline). Used for escalation and follow-up tracking.']
  safety_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the protocol amendment has a direct impact on subject safety, risk-benefit profile, or safety monitoring procedures. Triggers expedited regulatory review and DSMB/DMC notification requirements.']
  site_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which clinical sites were formally notified of the approved protocol amendment and required to begin implementation, including updated training and revised procedures. Tracked in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor or CRO regulatory affairs contact responsible for preparing and submitting this protocol amendment. Used for regulatory correspondence tracking.']
  subject_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amendment affects subjects currently enrolled in the study, requiring re-consent, protocol deviation assessment, or updated procedures for ongoing participants.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the protocol amendment was formally submitted to the primary health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA). Marks the start of the regulatory review clock.']
  submission_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the health authority upon receipt of the amendment submission (e.g., FDA IND amendment serial number, EMA submission tracking number). Used for all subsequent regulatory correspondence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this protocol amendment record, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  urgent_safety_measure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this amendment was implemented as an urgent safety measure prior to regulatory approval, as permitted under GCP and EU CTR for immediate risk mitigation. Requires retrospective notification to health authorities.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF/regulatory binder for the amendment package. Provides direct traceability to the source document management system of record.']

  Note: 'Tracks protocol amendments submitted to health authorities and ethics committees. Captures amendment number, amendment type (substantial/non-substantial), amendment rationale, affected study sections, submission date to each agency, agency acknowledgment date, approval date, implementation date, and impact on ongoing subjects. Links to the parent application and study protocol. Managed via Veeva Vault regulatory binder.'
}

  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection. Business justification: Regulatory correspondence (FDA information requests, inspection-related queries) is frequently triggered by or related to inspections. Linking correspondence to inspection records supports the complet']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Formal regulatory correspondence is exchanged with specific health authorities. The agency_code STRING field on correspondence is a denormalized reference to health_authority.agency_code. Adding this ']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Clinical hold letters, information requests, and agency queries reference specific protocol versions. Regulatory teams managing correspondence responses need direct FK to protocol for response prepara']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study or protocol to which this regulatory correspondence pertains. Supports study-level regulatory intelligence and inspection readiness.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) with which this correspondence is associated. Links correspondence to its parent dossier context.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory correspondence (agency letters, complete response letters, information requests) are TMF Zone 1 essential documents. Inspectors review correspondence files; linking correspondence records t']
  action_required varchar [note: 'Description of the specific action(s) required by the sponsor or CRO in response to this correspondence (e.g., \'Submit updated clinical protocol within 30 days\', \'Provide additional safety data for Module 5\'). Drives task assignment and workflow.']
  agency_division varchar [note: 'The specific division, office, or directorate within the health authority handling this correspondence (e.g., FDA CDER Division of Oncology Products, EMA Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use). Supports targeted regulatory relationship management.']
  agency_reference_number varchar [note: 'The official reference number assigned by the health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada) to this correspondence or the associated submission. Used for tracking agency-side docket and regulatory intelligence.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'The protocol amendment number associated with this correspondence, when the correspondence relates to a protocol amendment submission or agency review thereof. Links correspondence to the specific amendment version.']
  clinical_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this correspondence is associated with an FDA clinical hold order (full or partial) under 21 CFR Part 312.42. When True, triggers immediate escalation and study suspension workflows.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this correspondence record, governing access controls and disclosure restrictions. Restricted items may contain commercially sensitive regulatory strategy or patient safety data.']
  correspondence_date date [note: 'The official date on which the correspondence was issued, sent, or received. This is the principal real-world event date used for regulatory timeline calculations and compliance tracking.']
  correspondence_number varchar [note: 'Internally assigned unique reference number for this correspondence record, used for tracking and cross-referencing within the CTMS and eTMF (e.g., CORR-2024-00123). Serves as the business identifier for audit trails.']
  correspondence_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the correspondence record within the regulatory workflow. Drives action queues, escalation triggers, and inspection readiness dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|sent|received|under_review|response_pending|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  correspondence_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the formal communication type. Drives workflow routing and response obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: information_request|complete_response_letter|refuse_to_file|meeting_minutes|agency_query|label_negotiation|clinical_hold|safety_update_request|protocol_amendment_query|inspection_notice — promote to reference product]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the health authority jurisdiction to which this correspondence pertains (e.g., USA for FDA, DEU/NLD for EMA, JPN for PMDA, GBR for MHRA, CAN for Health Canada). Enables multi-country regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this correspondence record was first created in the system (Veeva Vault / CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctd_section varchar [note: 'The CTD/eCTD dossier section to which this correspondence relates (e.g., Module 2.7.4 Summary of Clinical Safety, Module 5.3.5 Reports of Controlled Clinical Studies). Enables dossier-level regulatory intelligence.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the correspondence was received from the health authority (inbound) or sent to the health authority (outbound) by the sponsor or CRO. Critical for response tracking and regulatory intelligence.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this correspondence has been escalated to senior regulatory leadership or the sponsor due to urgency, clinical hold, refuse-to-file, or overdue response. Drives escalation workflow in CTMS.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this correspondence has been reviewed and confirmed as inspection-ready for regulatory authority audits (FDA, EMA, MHRA). Supports TMF completeness and inspection readiness assessments.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code of the correspondence document (e.g., \'en\' for English, \'ja\' for Japanese, \'de\' for German). Required for multi-regional submissions and translation tracking.']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'For correspondence related to agency meetings, the FDA meeting type classification (Type A, B, C) or equivalent EMA/PMDA meeting category. Applicable when correspondence_type is meeting_minutes or meeting_request. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type_A|Type_B|Type_C|pre_IND|end_of_phase_1|end_of_phase_2|pre_NDA|advisory_committee|none — promote to reference product]']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority level indicating the urgency of the correspondence and required response. Critical items (e.g., clinical holds, refuse-to-file letters) require immediate escalation.']
  received_date date [note: 'The date on which the correspondence was physically or electronically received by the sponsor or CRO (for inbound) or confirmed received by the agency (for outbound). May differ from correspondence_date due to postal or electronic transmission delays.']
  response_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the sponsor or CRO submitted the response to the health authority. Used to calculate response timeliness and compliance with regulatory deadlines.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which a response must be submitted to the health authority. Calculated based on agency-mandated timelines (e.g., 30-day FDA information request response window). Critical for inspection readiness and compliance.']
  response_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal response to the health authority is required for this correspondence. When True, activates response tracking, due date monitoring, and escalation workflows.']
  response_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required response has been submitted to the health authority. Used in conjunction with response_required_flag and response_due_date for compliance monitoring and inspection readiness.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD submission sequence number associated with this correspondence, when the correspondence is linked to a specific eCTD submission sequence. Enables traceability between correspondence and dossier submission history.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this correspondence record was ingested into the lakehouse (e.g., Veeva Vault eTMF, Oracle Siebel CTMS). Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor or CRO regulatory affairs representative responsible for this correspondence. Used for accountability, audit trail, and inspection readiness.']
  subject_matter varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured description of the primary subject or topic of the correspondence (e.g., \'Protocol Amendment Review\', \'Clinical Hold Notification\', \'Safety Data Request\'). Used for regulatory intelligence and search.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Structured executive summary of the correspondence content, key regulatory questions raised, commitments made, or decisions communicated. Used for regulatory intelligence, briefing documents, and inspection preparation.']
  tmf_section varchar [note: 'The TMF reference model section under which this correspondence is filed in the eTMF (e.g., Zone 1.2 Regulatory, Zone 1.2.1 Health Authority Correspondence). Ensures correct eTMF filing and completeness tracking.']
  transmission_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the correspondence was transmitted to or received from the health authority (eCTD electronic submission gateway, email, fax, postal mail, hand delivery). Required for audit trail and ALCOA+ compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this correspondence record was most recently modified in the system. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and data lineage in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF where the correspondence document is stored. Enables direct linkage between the correspondence metadata record and the source document in the eTMF system of record.']

  Note: 'Records all formal communications between the sponsor/CRO and health authorities. Includes correspondence type (information request, complete response letter, refuse-to-file, meeting minutes, agency query, label negotiation), direction (inbound/outbound), agency reference number, correspondence date, response due date, response date, subject matter, and associated submission or application. Supports inspection readiness and regulatory intelligence.'
}

Business justification: Type A agency meetings are often convened specifically to address compliance inspection outcomes (e.g., clinical hold meetings post-inspection). Linking the meeting to the triggering inspection is ess']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Agency meetings are held with specific health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA). The agency_code STRING field on agency_meeting is a denormalized reference to health_authority.agency_code. Adding this FK n']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Type B and Type C agency meetings (End of Phase 2, pre-NDA) are protocol-specific; briefing documents reference the protocol under discussion. Regulatory affairs teams need direct FK from agency_meeti']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study or development program associated with this agency meeting, enabling cross-domain linkage for regulatory strategy planning.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA) under which this agency meeting is requested or conducted.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Agency meeting minutes and briefing documents are TMF Zone 1 essential documents per DIA TMF Reference Model. Linking meeting records to their TMF document enables inspection-ready retrieval of meetin']
  action_items_count varchar [note: 'Total number of action items arising from the agency meeting, as documented in the finalized meeting minutes. Used to track post-meeting follow-up workload and regulatory commitments.']
  agency_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the meeting request. Marks the start of the regulatory response clock for PDUFA-governed timelines.']
  agency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary agency contact or project manager at the health authority assigned to this meeting. Used for correspondence tracking and relationship management.']
  agency_division varchar [note: 'Specific division, office, or therapeutic area unit within the health authority responsible for the meeting (e.g., FDA Office of Oncology Products, EMA Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), PMDA Office of New Drug I).']

  agency_response_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority provided its formal response to the meeting request (granting, denying, or proposing alternatives). Used to measure agency response time against PDUFA commitments.']

  briefing_document_submission_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the meeting briefing document (background package) was submitted to the health authority via eCTD or other regulatory submission channel in Veeva Vault.']
  briefing_document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the meeting briefing document submitted to the health authority, as tracked in Veeva Vault document management. Enables traceability of the exact document version reviewed by the agency.']
  confirmed_meeting_date date [note: 'The date confirmed by the health authority for the meeting to take place. This is the operationally binding date used for scheduling, briefing document preparation, and attendee coordination.']
  confirmed_meeting_time timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone) confirmed by the health authority for the meeting, used for scheduling teleconferences and videoconferences across global time zones.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this agency meeting record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage purposes per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  etmf_section_reference varchar [note: 'The eTMF section or zone reference where the agency meeting documents (request, briefing document, minutes) are filed, per the DIA Reference Model for the TMF. Ensures regulatory inspection readiness and document traceability.']
  follow_up_submission_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the sponsor must submit any follow-up regulatory documents or responses to the health authority as agreed during or after the meeting.']
  follow_up_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agency meeting outcome requires a follow-up regulatory submission (e.g., protocol amendment, IND amendment, additional data package) as a result of agency feedback or commitments made during the meeting.']
  inspection_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required documentation for this agency meeting (request, briefing document, minutes, action items) is complete and filed in the eTMF, making the record inspection-ready for FDA, EMA, or other health authority audits.']
  meeting_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The planned or actual duration of the agency meeting in minutes, as agreed with the health authority. Typically 60–90 minutes for FDA Type B meetings.']
  meeting_format varchar [note: 'The format in which the agency meeting is conducted. Face-to-Face = in-person at agency or sponsor site; Teleconference = audio-only call; Videoconference = video-enabled remote meeting; Written Response Only = agency responds in writing without a live meeting.']
  meeting_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned or internally generated unique reference number for the meeting request, as tracked in Veeva Vault regulatory submissions module and referenced in agency correspondence (e.g., FDA meeting request number).']
  meeting_objectives varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the primary scientific, regulatory, or strategic objectives for the agency meeting, as stated in the meeting request. Captures the key questions and discussion topics the sponsor seeks to resolve.']
  meeting_stage varchar [note: 'The clinical development stage at which the agency meeting is requested, indicating the regulatory milestone context (e.g., Pre-IND, End-of-Phase 2, Pre-NDA/BLA submission). Aligns with FDA Type B meeting sub-categories.']
  meeting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the agency meeting. Planned = internally identified need; Requested = formal meeting request submitted to agency; Granted = agency has agreed to hold the meeting; Scheduled = date and format confirmed; Completed = meeting has occurred; Cancelled = meeting cancelled by either party; Denied = agency declined the meeting request; On Hold = meeting paused pending additional information. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Planned|Requested|Granted|Scheduled|Completed|Cancelled|Denied|On Hold — promote to reference product]']
  meeting_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the meeting per agency-defined categories. FDA meetings are classified as Type A (urgent/critical), Type B (pre-IND, end-of-Phase 2, pre-NDA/BLA), or Type C (other). EMA meetings are classified as Scientific Advice. PMDA meetings as Consultation. Written Response Only indicates no face-to-face or teleconference meeting is held.']
  minutes_draft_date date [note: 'The date on which the draft meeting minutes were prepared by the sponsor following the agency meeting. FDA requires sponsors to submit draft minutes within 30 days of the meeting.']
  minutes_finalized_date date [note: 'The date on which the meeting minutes were finalized and agreed upon by both the sponsor and the health authority. Finalized minutes represent the official record of agency agreements and commitments.']
  minutes_submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor submitted the draft meeting minutes to the health authority for review and comment.']
  nda_bla_number varchar [note: 'The FDA-assigned NDA or BLA application number if the meeting is related to a marketing application. Applicable for pre-NDA/BLA, post-approval, or labeling meetings.']
  open_action_items_count varchar [note: 'Number of action items from the agency meeting that remain open or unresolved. Used for regulatory follow-up tracking and inspection readiness monitoring.']
  outcome_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the overall outcome of the agency meeting with respect to the sponsor\'s objectives. Agreement Reached = agency aligned with sponsor\'s proposed approach; Partial Agreement = some questions resolved; No Agreement = agency did not concur; Pending Agency Response = written response awaited; Information Only = meeting was informational with no formal positions taken.']
  pdufa_response_deadline date [note: 'The regulatory deadline by which the FDA must respond to the meeting request under Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) commitments. Type A: 30 days; Type B: 70 days; Type C: 90 days from receipt of meeting request.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'The name of the investigational medicinal product or biological entity that is the subject of the agency meeting. May be a proprietary name, INN (International Nonproprietary Name), or internal code name. Classified as confidential due to competitive sensitivity.']
  proposed_meeting_date date [note: 'The date proposed by the sponsor in the meeting request for when the meeting should occur. May differ from the agency-confirmed date.']
  questions_submitted_count varchar [note: 'The number of formal questions submitted to the health authority in the meeting request or briefing document. Used to track scope and complexity of the meeting agenda.']
  request_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal meeting request was submitted to the health authority. This is the principal business event timestamp that initiates the regulatory clock for agency response timelines under PDUFA commitments.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease indication relevant to the meeting (e.g., Oncology, Cardiovascular, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for regulatory strategy analytics and agency workload tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this agency meeting record was last modified, supporting audit trail requirements and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF/regulatory submissions module for the meeting request package, briefing document, or meeting minutes. Enables direct traceability to the source document management system.']

  Note: 'Master record for formal meetings with health authorities (FDA Type A/B/C meetings, EMA scientific advice, PMDA consultations). Tracks meeting type, request date, agency-confirmed date, meeting format (face-to-face, teleconference, written response), meeting objectives, briefing document submission date, meeting minutes finalization date, and action items arising. Critical for regulatory strategy planning.'
}

Table regulatory.ctd_dossier {
  ctd_dossier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the CTD dossier record. Primary key for this entity.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) to which this CTD dossier belongs.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: CTD dossier compilation is subject to QA audit before submission to verify completeness and compliance. Linking the dossier to the audit record supports the dossier readiness review process and inspec']
  ethics_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_approval. Business justification: CTD Module 1 includes ethics approval documentation as a mandatory component. Linking the dossier to the ethics approval enables completeness checking and ensures the correct ethics approval version i']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: CTD dossiers are prepared and compiled for specific health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA). The target_agency and target_country_code STRING fields on ctd_dossier are denormalized references to health_au']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: A CTD dossier is physically packaged as a regulatory binder for submission. Publishing teams compile the dossier into a binder; this FK enables dossier-to-binder traceability for eCTD publishing, vali']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory submission sequence this dossier version supports.']
  submission_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission_sequence. Business justification: A CTD dossier is compiled and published for a specific eCTD submission sequence. Linking ctd_dossier to submission_sequence enables tracking which dossier version was included in which sequence, suppo']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: The CTD dossier compilation report and validation report are TMF Zone 1 documents. Linking the dossier record to its TMF document enables inspection-ready retrieval of the dossier package and supports']
  actual_submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the CTD dossier was actually submitted to the regulatory agency. Null until submission occurs.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CTD dossier was formally approved internally for submission. Captures the approval event in the dossier lifecycle per 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature requirements.']
  archival_location varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic archival location of the compiled CTD dossier package (e.g., Veeva Vault path, network share, or physical archive reference). Required for inspection readiness and long-term retention.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for regulatory affairs team notes, submission strategy remarks, or outstanding action items related to the CTD dossier.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CTD dossier record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctd_format varchar [note: 'Submission format of the CTD dossier. eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) is the standard for FDA, EMA, and PMDA. NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission) and paper formats are legacy options.']
  dossier_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the CTD dossier, used in regulatory correspondence and Veeva Vault tracking. Format: CTD-{AppCode}-{Year}-{Seq}.']
  dossier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CTD dossier. Tracks progression from authoring through regulatory submission and archival.']
  dossier_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title of the CTD dossier, typically including the drug name, application type, and submission sequence (e.g., \'DrugX NDA Initial Submission CTD Dossier\').']
  dossier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the dossier submission type. Initial submissions establish the application; amendments and supplements update specific modules; annual reports fulfill periodic reporting obligations.']
  dossier_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the CTD dossier (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented with each major revision or amendment cycle.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'Name of the investigational drug or biologic product that is the subject of the CTD dossier (proprietary name, INN, or code name).']
  dtd_version varchar [note: 'Version of the eCTD Document Type Definition (DTD) or XML schema used for the dossier backbone (e.g., 3.2.2 for FDA, 4.0 for EMA). Determines structural compliance requirements.']
  gap_analysis_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent dossier gap analysis was completed. Used to assess the currency of the gap analysis relative to dossier updates.']
  gap_analysis_status varchar [note: 'Status of the formal gap analysis conducted to identify missing or incomplete content units within the CTD dossier prior to submission.']
  indication varchar [note: 'The therapeutic indication or disease area for which the drug is being developed and for which the CTD dossier is submitted.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CTD dossier has been assessed and confirmed as ready for regulatory agency inspection. True when all required documents are present, validated, and accessible.']
  last_compiled_date date [note: 'Date on which the CTD dossier was most recently compiled and packaged for submission or review. Tracks the currency of the assembled dossier.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CTD dossier record. Required for audit trail and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  module1_status varchar [note: 'Completeness status of CTD Module 1 (Regional Administrative Information), which contains region-specific forms, cover letters, and labeling. Content varies by target agency.']
  module2_status varchar [note: 'Completeness status of CTD Module 2 (CTD Summaries), including the Quality Overall Summary (QOS), Nonclinical Overview, Clinical Overview, Nonclinical Written and Tabulated Summaries, and Clinical Summary.']
  module3_status varchar [note: 'Completeness status of CTD Module 3 (Quality), covering drug substance, drug product, and related quality documentation per ICH M4Q.']
  module4_status varchar [note: 'Completeness status of CTD Module 4 (Nonclinical Study Reports), including pharmacology, pharmacokinetics, and toxicology study reports per ICH M4S.']
  module5_status varchar [note: 'Completeness status of CTD Module 5 (Clinical Study Reports), including all clinical pharmacology, efficacy, and safety study reports per ICH M4E.']
  open_gap_count varchar [note: 'Number of identified content gaps (missing or incomplete documents/sections) that remain unresolved in the current dossier version. Key metric for submission readiness.']
  overall_completeness_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate readiness assessment of the entire CTD dossier across all five modules. Used for dossier gap analysis and submission readiness reviews.']
  planned_submission_date date [note: 'Target date on which the CTD dossier is planned to be submitted to the regulatory agency. Used for milestone tracking and resource planning.']
  prior_submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the prior submission or dossier version that this submission amends or supplements. Enables lineage tracking across submission cycles.']
  publishing_tool varchar [note: 'Name and version of the publishing software used to compile and validate the eCTD dossier (e.g., Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTD Manager, Veeva Vault RIM Publisher). Critical for reproducibility and troubleshooting.']
