  publishing_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the publishing tool used to compile this dossier. Required for audit trail and reproducibility of the eCTD package.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the pharmaceutical or biotech sponsor organization that owns the regulatory application and CTD dossier.']
  total_document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual content units (documents, datasets, study reports) included in this CTD dossier version. Used for completeness tracking and submission package verification.']
  validation_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent eCTD technical validation report was generated for this dossier.']
  validation_report_status varchar [note: 'Status of the eCTD technical validation report generated by the publishing tool. Agencies require a passing validation report before accepting eCTD submissions.']
  veeva_vault_binder_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Veeva Vault submission binder that organizes all CTD documents for this dossier. Enables navigation of the dossier structure within Veeva Vault.']
  veeva_vault_dossier_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault RIM to the CTD dossier record. Used for cross-system traceability between the lakehouse and the Veeva Vault eTMF/regulatory submissions system.']

  Note: 'Master record for the Common Technical Document (CTD) dossier structure and content inventory associated with a regulatory application. Tracks CTD format (eCTD/NeeS/paper), dossier version, completeness status, module-level readiness (Modules 1-5), last compiled date, validation report status, publishing tool used, and archival location in Veeva Vault. Also manages individual content units (documents, datasets, study reports) with CTD section references (e.g., Module 2.7.3, Module 5.3.5.1), document metadata, file format, lifecycle operations, and Veeva Vault document IDs. Serves as the structural backbone for all submission content and enables dossier gap analysis.'
}

Table regulatory.approval {
  approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approval']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Regulatory approvals are granted for specific regulatory applications (NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA). The application_number STRING field on approval is a denormalized reference to application.application_numbe']
  approval_agency_health_authority_id varchar [note: 'FK to regulatory.health_authority']
  approval_health_authority_id varchar [note: 'FK to regulatory.health_authority']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection. Business justification: Pre-approval inspections (PAIs) are a standard FDA/EMA step before granting marketing approval. Linking the approval record to the PAI that supported it is required for the complete regulatory approva']
  primary_approval_health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health authority or regulatory agency (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada, TGA) that issued this approval decision.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Regulatory approvals reference the specific protocol version that was approved. Regulatory binder management and post-approval protocol change tracking require direct FK from approval to protocol. app']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study or program for which this regulatory approval was granted. Enables cross-domain linkage between regulatory decisions and study operations.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory submission (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) that resulted in this approval decision. Links approval back to the originating dossier.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Approval letters and agency decision documents are TMF essential documents (DIA Zone 1). Regulatory teams file approval letters in the TMF; linking approval records to their TMF document enables inspe']
  agency_decision_date date [note: 'The official date on which the health authority issued its approval decision or authorization. This is the principal real-world event date for the approval transaction, as recorded in the agency approval letter.']
  agency_decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Summary of the health authority\'s stated rationale or basis for the approval decision, as documented in the approval letter, assessment report, or public review document. Captures key scientific and regulatory justification.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned unique identifier issued by the health authority for this approval (e.g., NDA 123456, EMA/H/C/004321, IND 12345). This is the official agency-assigned reference number used in all regulatory correspondence.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory approval. Tracks whether the approval is active, conditionally approved (with post-marketing commitments), withdrawn by sponsor, suspended or revoked by the agency, or pending final decision.']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory approval or authorization received. Distinguishes between IND allowance (Investigational New Drug), NDA approval (New Drug Application), BLA approval (Biologics License Application), MAA approval (Marketing Authorization Application), CTA authorization (Clinical Trial Authorization), and label approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND_ALLOWANCE|NDA_APPROVAL|BLA_APPROVAL|MAA_APPROVAL|CTA_AUTHORIZATION|LABEL_APPROVAL|SINDA|IMPD|ORPHAN_DESIGNATION|BREAKTHROUGH_DESIGNATION — promote to reference product]']
  approved_dose varchar [note: 'The approved dose or dose range as specified in the regulatory approval (e.g., \'10 mg once daily\', \'200 mg/m² every 3 weeks\'). Captures the label-approved dosing regimen for the approved indication and population.']
  approved_indication varchar [note: 'The specific disease, condition, or therapeutic indication for which the health authority has granted approval. Reflects the exact label language or CTA scope as approved (e.g., \'Treatment of moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis in adults\').']
  approved_population varchar [note: 'Description of the patient population covered by the approval, including age group, disease severity, prior treatment requirements, or other eligibility criteria as specified in the approved label or CTA (e.g., \'Adults ≥18 years with inadequate response to prior therapy\').']
  conditions_of_approval varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any conditions, restrictions, or requirements attached to the approval by the health authority (e.g., REMS requirements, restricted distribution, mandatory risk minimization measures, specific labeling requirements).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory approval record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form approved by the health authority (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, infusion, patch). Reflects the specific formulation covered by the approval as stated in the approved label.']
  drug_name varchar [note: 'The International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or brand name of the drug, biologic, or investigational medicinal product covered by this approval. Denormalized for regulatory reporting and label management.']
  ectd_sequence_number varchar [note: 'The eCTD sequence number of the submission cycle that received this approval decision. Enables direct linkage to the specific eCTD dossier version in Veeva Vault that was reviewed and approved by the agency.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the regulatory approval becomes legally effective and the sponsor may act upon it (e.g., commence the trial, market the product). May differ from the agency decision date for conditional or phased approvals.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory approval expires or requires renewal (applicable to CTAs, annual IND renewals, and time-limited authorizations). Null for indefinite approvals such as standard NDA/BLA marketing authorizations.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the geographic territory or jurisdictions covered by this approval (e.g., \'United States\', \'European Union - 27 Member States\', \'Japan\'). Supports multi-regional regulatory portfolio management.']
  inn_code varchar [note: 'The WHO-assigned International Nonproprietary Name (INN) code or identifier for the active substance covered by this approval. Supports WHO-DD coding and cross-study drug identification.']
  inspection_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Current inspection readiness status for this approval record, indicating whether all supporting documentation in the eTMF is complete and audit-ready for potential agency inspection. Supports GCP inspection preparedness.']
  irb_iec_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the corresponding IRB (Institutional Review Board) or IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approval associated with this regulatory authorization. Supports ethics and regulatory approval alignment tracking.']
  is_conditional_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approval was granted conditionally (True) or as a full unconditional approval (False). Conditional approvals require the sponsor to fulfill specific post-marketing commitments or confirmatory studies.']
  is_renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approval requires periodic renewal or re-authorization (True) or is granted indefinitely (False). Relevant for CTAs, annual IND reports, and time-limited authorizations in certain jurisdictions.']
  letter_date date [note: 'The date printed on the official agency approval letter or decision notice. May differ from the agency decision date if the letter was issued after the formal decision was recorded.']
  letter_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number or document identifier of the agency approval letter or decision notice (e.g., FDA approval letter reference, EMA decision reference). Used to locate the source document in the eTMF or regulatory binder in Veeva Vault.']
  mah_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) as registered with the health authority. May differ from the sponsor name if the MAH is a subsidiary, licensee, or partner organization. Applicable primarily to NDA/BLA/MAA approvals.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory context, internal commentary, or supplementary information about this approval that does not fit into structured fields. Used by regulatory affairs teams for operational notes.']
  post_marketing_commitments varchar [note: 'Description of post-marketing study commitments, confirmatory trials, or pharmacovigilance obligations required by the health authority as a condition of approval. Includes Phase IV studies, REMS, and risk management plans.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the sponsor must submit a renewal application or annual report to maintain the validity of this approval. Null if renewal is not required. Critical for regulatory compliance tracking.']
  review_pathway varchar [note: 'The regulatory review pathway or designation under which the application was reviewed and approved (e.g., Standard, Priority Review, Accelerated Approval, Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Orphan Drug). Reflects the expedited or standard review classification assigned by the agency.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The approved route of administration for the drug or biologic as specified in the regulatory approval (e.g., Oral, Intravenous, Subcutaneous, Intramuscular, Topical, Inhalation). Reflects the label-approved administration method. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ORAL|INTRAVENOUS|SUBCUTANEOUS|INTRAMUSCULAR|TOPICAL|INHALATION|OTHER — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the pharmaceutical or biotech sponsor organization that holds this regulatory approval. The Marketing Authorization Holder (MAH) or IND holder as recognized by the health authority.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease category associated with the approved indication (e.g., Oncology, Cardiovascular, Immunology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio analytics and regulatory strategy reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory approval record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module) for the approval letter or decision document. Enables direct navigation to the source document in the system of record.']

  Note: 'Records regulatory approvals, authorizations, and decisions received from health authorities. Captures approval type (IND allowance, NDA approval, MAA approval, CTA authorization, label approval), approval date, effective date, approved indication, approved population, conditions of approval, post-marketing commitments, approval letter reference, and agency decision rationale. SSOT for all positive regulatory decisions.'
}

Table regulatory.ind_application {
  ind_application_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the IND application record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: An IND application is filed with a specific health authority (FDA or equivalent). The ind_application table currently stores regulatory_agency as a STRING denormalized reference. Adding health_authori']
  parent_application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application record when this IND is a sub-application or cross-references a master NDA/BLA/MAA application. Enables hierarchical navigation of the regulatory application portfolio.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'The cumulative number of IND amendments (protocol amendments, information amendments, safety reports) submitted to the FDA under this IND. Tracks the volume of regulatory activity and dossier complexity.']
  annual_report_due_date date [note: 'The next due date for the IND Annual Report submission to the FDA. Annual reports are required within 60 days of the anniversary of the IND effective date and must summarize clinical progress, safety data, and manufacturing updates.']
  application_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction in which this application is filed (e.g., USA for FDA, GBR for MHRA, JPN for PMDA). Enables multi-country regulatory portfolio analytics.']
  breakthrough_therapy_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has granted Breakthrough Therapy designation for the investigational product under this IND. Breakthrough Therapy designation provides intensive FDA guidance and rolling review for drugs showing substantial improvement over existing therapies.']
  clinical_hold_date date [note: 'The date on which the FDA issued a clinical hold order on this IND, halting enrollment or dosing of subjects. Nullable for INDs not on clinical hold. Triggers mandatory sponsor response within 30 days.']
  clinical_hold_lifted_date date [note: 'The date on which the FDA lifted the clinical hold and authorized resumption of clinical trials under this IND. Nullable for INDs that have never been on clinical hold or where the hold remains active.']
  clinical_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the FDA\'s stated reason for imposing a clinical hold on this IND. Captures the safety concern, protocol deficiency, or regulatory non-compliance issue cited by the agency. Nullable for INDs not on clinical hold.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this IND application record was first created in the lakehouse data platform. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for audit trail and data lineage purposes. Conforms to 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'The pharmaceutical dosage form of the investigational product (e.g., tablet, capsule, injection, infusion, patch). Captured from the IND CMC section and used for supply chain and regulatory tracking.']
  drug_substance_name varchar [note: 'International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or chemical name of the active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) covered under this IND. Used for regulatory cross-referencing and WHO-DD coding.']
  ectd_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this IND application is submitted in eCTD format via the FDA Electronic Submissions Gateway. True indicates eCTD format; False indicates paper or non-eCTD electronic submission. FDA mandates eCTD for most IND submissions.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which the IND became effective and clinical trials were authorized to commence. Under 21 CFR Part 312.40, an IND becomes effective 30 days after FDA receipt unless FDA places a clinical hold. This is the EFFECTIVE_FROM date for the IND agreement.']
  equivalent_application_number varchar [note: 'The application number assigned by a non-FDA regulatory agency for an equivalent authorization (e.g., EMA CTA number, MHRA CTA reference, PMDA CTN). Used to cross-reference the IND with parallel international submissions.']
  fast_track_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA has granted Fast Track designation for the investigational product under this IND. Fast Track facilitates development and expedites review of drugs treating serious conditions with unmet medical need.']
  fda_application_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the FDA officially acknowledged receipt of the IND application. Starts the 30-day review clock per 21 CFR Part 312.40. May differ from the initial submission date if there were transmission delays.']
  fda_division varchar [note: 'The FDA Center and Division responsible for reviewing this IND application (e.g., CDER/Division of Oncology Products 1, CBER/Division of Vaccines). Determines the regulatory review team and applicable FDA guidance documents.']
  inactivation_date date [note: 'The date on which the IND was placed into inactive status, either by sponsor notification or FDA action. Nullable for active INDs. Represents the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL boundary for the active IND period.']
  ind_number varchar [note: 'Official IND number assigned by the FDA upon receipt of the IND application. This is the externally-known regulatory identifier used in all agency correspondence, safety reports, and annual reports. Format is a numeric string up to 6 digits as assigned by FDA.']
  ind_serial_number_latest varchar [note: 'The most recent serial number assigned to the latest IND submission sequence (original or amendment). IND submissions are numbered sequentially starting from 0000. Used to track the current version of the IND dossier in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  ind_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the IND application by its intended purpose. Commercial INDs are filed by pharmaceutical/biotech sponsors for drug development. Research INDs are filed by investigators for academic studies. Emergency use and treatment INDs cover compassionate use scenarios. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|research|emergency_use|treatment|expanded_access — promote to reference product]']
  indication varchar [note: 'The specific disease, condition, or patient population for which the investigational product is being studied under this IND. Aligns with the MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) for the target indication.']
  initial_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the original IND application was first submitted to the FDA. This is the principal business event date for the IND lifecycle and triggers the 30-day FDA review clock per 21 CFR Part 312.40.']
  investigational_product_code varchar [note: 'Internal sponsor code or development compound number assigned to the investigational product (e.g., ABC-123). Used for cross-referencing across clinical, regulatory, and supply chain systems including RTSM and Oracle Clinical One.']
  investigational_product_name varchar [note: 'The name of the investigational drug, biologic, or compound covered under this IND application. May be a code name, generic name, or proprietary name as designated by the sponsor. Corresponds to the investigational product tracked in Medidata Rave and Oracle Clinical One.']
  last_annual_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent IND Annual Report was submitted to the FDA. Used to track compliance with annual reporting obligations and calculate the next due date.']
  nda_bla_number varchar [note: 'The NDA or BLA application number to which this IND is expected to transition upon successful completion of clinical development. Nullable for early-phase INDs where the downstream marketing application has not yet been filed.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA Office of Orphan Products Development (OOPD) has granted Orphan Drug designation for the investigational product. Orphan designation provides tax credits, market exclusivity, and expedited review for drugs targeting rare diseases affecting fewer than 200,000 US patients.']
  phase_coverage varchar [note: 'The clinical development phase(s) authorized under this IND application. An IND may cover a single phase or span multiple phases. Phase 1 covers first-in-human safety studies; Phase 2 covers efficacy and dose-ranging; Phase 3 covers confirmatory trials; Phase 4 covers post-marketing studies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phase_1|phase_1_2|phase_2|phase_2_3|phase_3|phase_4|all_phases — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  protocol_count varchar [note: 'The total number of clinical study protocols currently authorized and active under this IND application. An IND may cover multiple protocols across different phases and indications.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'The route by which the investigational product is administered to subjects (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical). Sourced from the IND protocol section and CDISC SDTM ROUTE variable.']
  safety_reporting_obligation varchar [note: 'Defines the IND-level safety reporting obligation tier. Standard obligations apply to all INDs. Expedited 7-day reporting applies to fatal or life-threatening unexpected SUSARs. Expedited 15-day reporting applies to other unexpected serious adverse reactions. Drives Oracle Argus Safety workflow configuration.']
  sponsor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the designated sponsor contact person for FDA correspondence related to this IND application. Used for regulatory notifications and agency communication tracking.']
  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the designated sponsor contact person listed on the IND application for FDA correspondence. This individual receives all FDA communications including clinical hold letters and information requests.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the sponsor entity responsible for the IND application as filed with the FDA. The sponsor assumes legal and regulatory accountability for the investigational product and the conduct of the clinical trials under this IND.']
  sponsor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sponsor organization type. Distinguishes between commercial pharmaceutical/biotech companies, academic institutions, government agencies, and Contract Research Organizations (CROs) acting as sponsor.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The disease area or medical specialty that the investigational product targets (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio analytics, regulatory strategy, and resource planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this IND application record was most recently modified in the lakehouse data platform. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail compliance per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF corresponding to the master IND application dossier. Enables direct cross-reference to the regulatory binder and TMF artifact in the document management system.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor formally withdrew the IND application from FDA. Nullable for non-withdrawn INDs. Triggers archival obligations and cessation of all clinical activities under the IND.']

  Note: 'Specialized master record for Investigational New Drug (IND) applications filed with the FDA (or equivalent CTAs with EMA/MHRA). Tracks IND number, IND type (commercial, research, emergency), sponsor name, investigational product name, phase coverage, initial IND submission date, IND status (active, inactive, withdrawn), annual report due dates, safety reporting obligations, and cross-reference to the parent application record.'
}

Table regulatory.regulatory_milestone {
  regulatory_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory milestone record in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA) to which this milestone belongs.']
  ethics_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_approval. Business justification: Ethics approval receipt is a gating regulatory milestone for trial initiation. Linking the milestone record to the ethics approval enables automated milestone completion tracking when ethics approval ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Regulatory milestones are tracked per health authority (FDA PDUFA date, EMA opinion date, PMDA approval date). The agency_code and country_code STRING fields on regulatory_milestone are denormalized r']
  strategy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.strategy. Business justification: Regulatory milestones are planned and tracked as part of a regulatory strategy. The strategy product documents the regulatory strategy plan including planned submission dates and approval targets, whi']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study or protocol for which this regulatory milestone is being tracked.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory submission sequence (e.g., initial filing, amendment, response) associated with this milestone, if applicable.']
  upstream_milestone_regulatory_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier of the predecessor regulatory milestone that must be completed before this milestone can begin, enabling dependency chain tracking and critical path analysis.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency formally accepted the submission for review (e.g., FDA 74-day filing acceptance for NDA/BLA, EMA validation date for MAA). Null if not yet accepted.']
  actual_date date [note: 'The real-world date on which the regulatory milestone was achieved or completed. Null if the milestone has not yet been reached.']
  agency_action_date date [note: 'The date by which the regulatory agency is expected or required to take a formal action (e.g., PDUFA goal date for FDA, Day 120/180 for EMA). Distinct from the planned milestone date as it is set by the agency, not the sponsor.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory agency granted marketing authorization or study approval. Populated only for milestones of type \'approval\'. Null for all other milestone types.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The original approved baseline date for this milestone at the time the regulatory project plan was first formally approved, preserved for variance and trend analysis even after re-baselining.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the milestone status was set to \'completed\', capturing the exact moment of milestone achievement for audit trail and regulatory timeline reporting purposes.']
  completion_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded at the time of milestone completion, capturing any conditions, caveats, agency feedback, or follow-up actions associated with the milestone outcome.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory milestone record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctms_milestone_ref varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier from Oracle Siebel CTMS for the corresponding milestone or project task record, enabling reconciliation between the regulatory milestone tracker and the operational CTMS project plan.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the root cause of a milestone delay when variance_days is positive: agency_query (information request from agency), data_deficiency (incomplete or inadequate data package), resource_constraint (internal capacity issue), protocol_amendment (study design change), safety_hold (clinical hold or safety concern), regulatory_hold (agency-imposed hold), sponsor_decision (business or strategic decision), other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agency_query|data_deficiency|resource_constraint|protocol_amendment|safety_hold|regulatory_hold|sponsor_decision|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delay_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing detailed explanation of the reason for milestone delay or schedule change, supplementing the delay_reason_code with context for regulatory project management and escalation.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Current best-estimate date for milestone completion based on latest scenario planning and risk assessment, used for regulatory timeline management and scenario analysis without formally re-baselining the plan.']
  is_agency_committed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the planned or agency action date for this milestone has been formally committed to or communicated by the regulatory agency (e.g., PDUFA date confirmed, EMA Day 120 clock set). Distinguishes agency-set dates from sponsor-estimated dates.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this milestone lies on the critical path of the regulatory project plan. Critical path milestones directly impact the overall regulatory approval timeline and require heightened monitoring.']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the milestone within the drug development lifecycle: pre_clinical (pre-IND activities), clinical (study execution phases), regulatory (submission and review activities), commercial (post-approval activities).']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the milestone (e.g., RM-IND-FILING, RM-NDA-SUB), used in regulatory project plans, TMF cross-references, and agency correspondence.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory milestone, including scope, deliverables, entry/exit criteria, and any specific regulatory requirements or conditions associated with achieving this milestone.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the regulatory milestone (e.g., \'IND Filing\', \'Phase I Study Start\', \'Phase III Study Start\', \'NDA Submission\', \'PDUFA Date\', \'Approval\', \'Label Finalization\').']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory milestone: planned (not yet started), in_progress (activities underway), completed (milestone achieved), delayed (past planned date without completion), cancelled (milestone removed from plan), on_hold (temporarily suspended pending decision).']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the milestone by its regulatory function: filing (IND/CTA initial filing), study_start (Phase I/II/III initiation), study_completion (DBL or study close-out), submission (NDA/BLA/MAA dossier submission), agency_action (PDUFA date, Day 120/180 response), approval (marketing authorization), label_finalization (prescribing information finalized), other. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: filing|study_start|study_completion|submission|agency_action|approval|label_finalization|amendment|inspection|other — promote to reference product]']
  phase varchar [note: 'Clinical development phase associated with this milestone, enabling phase-level timeline tracking and portfolio analytics across the drug development lifecycle. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_clinical|phase_1|phase_2|phase_3|phase_4|nda_bla|post_approval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled target date for achieving this regulatory milestone, as defined in the regulatory project plan or integrated development timeline.']
  priority_designation varchar [note: 'Special regulatory designation granted by the agency that may affect review timelines and milestone dates (e.g., FDA Priority Review, Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Accelerated Approval, Orphan Drug). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|priority_review|breakthrough|fast_track|accelerated_approval|orphan|prime|conditional_approval|none — promote to reference product]']
  probability_of_success_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0.00–100.00%) that this regulatory milestone will be achieved on or before the planned date, used in risk-adjusted scenario planning and portfolio forecasting.']
  regulatory_strategy_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential free-text field capturing regulatory strategy considerations, agency meeting outcomes, or scenario planning assumptions relevant to this milestone. Classified as confidential due to commercially sensitive strategic content.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Type of agency review cycle applicable to this milestone, which determines the review clock duration: standard (12-month FDA standard review), priority (6-month FDA priority review), rolling (rolling review during development), accelerated (EMA accelerated assessment).']
  revised_planned_date date [note: 'Most recently updated target date for the milestone following a formal re-baseline of the regulatory project plan, reflecting approved schedule changes.']
  scenario_label varchar [note: 'Label identifying the planning scenario under which this milestone date was projected, supporting regulatory timeline scenario planning and what-if analysis: base_case (most likely), optimistic (best case), pessimistic (worst case), risk_adjusted (probability-weighted).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this milestone record was sourced or last updated (e.g., Veeva_Vault for eTMF/regulatory submissions, Siebel_CTMS for project plan milestones, Manual for direct data entry).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory milestone record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  variance_days varchar [note: 'Signed integer representing the difference in calendar days between the actual (or forecast) date and the planned date. Positive values indicate delay; negative values indicate early completion. Enables regulatory timeline KPI reporting.']
  veeva_vault_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier linking this milestone to the corresponding document, dossier, or workflow object in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module), enabling traceability between milestone tracking and the source document management system.']

  Note: 'Tracks key regulatory milestones and planned vs. actual dates for each application and submission. Includes milestone type (IND filing, Phase I start, Phase III start, NDA submission, PDUFA date, approval, label finalization), planned date, actual date, milestone status, variance in days, responsible regulatory lead, and dependency on upstream milestones. Enables regulatory timeline management and scenario planning.'
}

Table regulatory.safety_report_submission {
  safety_report_submission_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each safety report submission record in the regulatory domain. Primary key for this data product.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: IND Annual Reports, DSURs, and expedited safety reports are filed under specific regulatory applications. The ind_number STRING field on safety_report_submission is a denormalized reference to applica']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Safety report submissions (ICSRs, DSURs, PSURs) are audited for completeness and timeliness compliance. Linking safety report submissions to audit records supports pharmacovigilance compliance monitor']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection. Business justification: Safety reporting compliance is a frequent focus of regulatory inspections. Linking safety report submissions to inspection records enables traceability from specific safety reports to inspection findi']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Safety reports are submitted to specific health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA). The agency_code STRING field on safety_report_submission is a denormalized reference to health_authority.agency_code. Addi']
  icsr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.icsr. Business justification: Expedited safety report submissions (ICSRs) are the regulatory filing of ICSR cases. Linking safety_report_submission to the specific ICSR case it reports is fundamental to pharmacovigilance regulator']
  ind_application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.ind_application. Business justification: Safety report submissions (IND Annual Reports, DSURs, expedited safety reports) are filed under a specific IND application. Linking safety_report_submission to ind_application enables tracking all saf']
  parent_submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original or preceding safety report submission record when this record is a follow-up or amendment. Enables lineage tracking across submission versions for the same safety event.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: IND annual reports, expedited safety reports (7-day/15-day), and DSURs reference specific protocol versions. Regulatory safety teams need protocol-level traceability to confirm which protocol version ']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: IND annual reports and expedited safety reports (ICSRs) are organized into regulatory binders per submission package. Linking safety report submissions to their regulatory binder enables submission pa']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: Safety report submissions (DSURs, IND Annual Reports) are generated from a specific SDTM dataset version locked at the data cut date. Regulatory safety teams must document which dataset version underp']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this safety report is submitted. Links to the study master record.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Safety reports (DSURs, IND annual reports, SUSARs) are TMF Zone 1 essential documents. Regulatory inspectors verify safety reporting compliance by reviewing TMF filings; linking safety_report_submissi']
  agency_acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the safety report submission. For FDA IND submissions this is the date of the FDA acknowledgment letter; for EMA SUSAR submissions this is the EudraVigilance gateway acknowledgment date.']
  agency_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference or tracking number assigned by the health authority upon receipt or acknowledgment of the safety report (e.g., FDA case number, EudraVigilance case ID, PMDA receipt number). Used for follow-up correspondence.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the safety report received final internal approval for submission. Used to calculate the internal review cycle time and to verify compliance with expedited reporting timelines.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the qualified person, medical officer, or regulatory lead who provided final approval of the safety report prior to submission. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature and ALCOA+ requirements.']
  argus_safety_case_reference varchar [note: 'Case identifier in Oracle Argus Safety system that is the source of the safety data underlying this submission. Provides traceability from the regulatory submission back to the adverse event case in the pharmacovigilance system of record.']
  causality_assessment varchar [note: 'Sponsor or investigator assessment of the causal relationship between the investigational product and the adverse reaction reported in this submission. Drives the reporting obligation classification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this safety report submission record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  ctis_number varchar [note: 'EU Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS) authorization number under EU CTR 536/2014, replacing EudraCT for trials authorized under the new regulation. Used for SUSAR submissions via the CTIS portal.']
  data_lock_date date [note: 'The date on which the safety database was locked for the purpose of generating this periodic safety report. No new safety data is included after this date. Equivalent to the reporting period end date for periodic reports.']
  dibd date [note: 'Development International Birth Date (DIBD) — the date of the first authorization to conduct a clinical trial with the investigational product in any country. Used as the anchor date for calculating DSUR submission deadlines (due 60 days after DIBD anniversary).']
  due_date date [note: 'Regulatory deadline by which the safety report must be submitted to the health authority. For expedited SUSAR/IND safety reports this is typically 7 or 15 calendar days from initial awareness; for DSURs it is 60 days from the Development International Birth Date (DIBD).']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) number assigned to the clinical trial, used for EMA and EU member state safety report submissions. Format: YYYY-NNNNNN-CC.']
  expedited_report_timeline_days varchar [note: 'Regulatory reporting timeline in calendar days applicable to this expedited submission (typically 7 days for fatal/life-threatening unexpected SARs or 15 days for other unexpected serious adverse reactions). Null for periodic reports.']
  investigational_product_name varchar [note: 'Name of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) or drug substance that is the subject of this safety report. Aligns with the IND/CTA product designation.']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety report is an expedited submission (True) subject to 7-day or 15-day reporting timelines, as opposed to a periodic report. Expedited reports include SUSARs and IND safety reports for unexpected serious adverse reactions.']
  is_follow_up boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this submission is a follow-up (True) to a previously submitted safety report for the same event or reporting period, as opposed to an initial/original submission (False). Follow-up submissions provide updated or additional information.']
  listedness_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the adverse reaction is listed (expected) or unlisted (unexpected) in the current Investigator\'s Brochure (IB) or Reference Safety Information (RSI). Unlisted/unexpected reactions meeting seriousness criteria trigger SUSAR reporting obligations.']
  meddra_pt_code varchar [note: 'Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) code for the primary adverse reaction or event described in this safety report. Standardizes event terminology for regulatory submissions and signal detection.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) System Organ Class (SOC) code associated with the primary adverse reaction in this safety report. Used for aggregate safety analysis and tabulation in periodic reports.']
  regulatory_action_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the regulatory action required or taken as a result of this safety report submission (e.g., IB update, protocol amendment, clinical hold notification, Dear Investigator Letter). Populated when regulatory_action_required is True.']
  regulatory_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this safety report submission has triggered or requires a regulatory action such as a protocol amendment, IB update, informed consent revision, or clinical hold. Supports inspection readiness and CAPA tracking.']
  report_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the safety report being submitted. IND_SAFETY_REPORT = expedited IND safety report (15-day/7-day); DSUR = Development Safety Update Report; PSUR_PBRER = Periodic Safety Update Report / Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report; SUSAR = Suspected Unexpected Serious Adverse Reaction; IND_ANNUAL_REPORT = annual IND progress report; CIOMS = Council for International Organizations of Medical Sciences line listing.']
  report_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the safety report document (e.g., 1.0, 2.0, 1.1 for amendments). Tracks original submissions versus follow-up or amended versions submitted to the same agency.']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the data collection period covered by this safety report, also known as the data lock point (DLP). Defines the cut-off date for safety data included in the report.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the data collection period covered by this safety report (e.g., the beginning of the DSUR or PSUR/PBRER data lock window). Applicable to periodic reports; may be null for expedited reports.']
  seriousness_criteria varchar [note: 'ICH E2A seriousness criterion that triggered the expedited reporting obligation for this submission. Applicable to SUSAR and IND safety reports. FATAL = resulted in death; LIFE_THREATENING = immediately life-threatening; HOSPITALIZATION = required or prolonged inpatient hospitalization; DISABILITY = persistent or significant disability/incapacity; CONGENITAL_ANOMALY = congenital anomaly/birth defect; MEDICALLY_SIGNIFICANT = other medically important condition.']
  submission_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction to which the safety report is submitted (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, CAN, AUS). Supports multi-country submission tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this safety report submission record. Supports audit trail integrity and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ requirements.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module) for the finalized safety report document. Enables direct traceability from this submission record to the source document in the system of record.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory safety report submissions including IND Annual Reports, Development Safety Update Reports (DSURs), Periodic Safety Update Reports (PSURs/PBRERs), and expedited SUSAR/IND safety reports. Captures report type, reporting period, submission due date, actual submission date, agency acknowledgment, report version, and link to the source safety data. Distinct from individual AE/SAE records owned by the safety domain.'
}

Table regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization {
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Clinical Trial Authorization record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Clinical Trial Authorizations (CTAs) are filed with national competent authorities as part of the overall regulatory application process for a development program. Linking CTA to the parent applicatio']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: CTAs are filed with specific national competent authorities (MHRA, BfArM, ANSM). The competent_authority_name and competent_authority_code STRING fields are denormalized references to health_authority']
  parent_cta_clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original or preceding CTA record from which this substantial modification, renewal, or extension derives. Enables reconstruction of the full authorization history chain for a trial in a given member state.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: CTAs are issued for specific protocol versions; health authorities approve the protocol as part of CTA. Regulatory binder management and CTA amendment tracking require direct FK from CTA to protocol. ']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: A Clinical Trial Authorization (CTA) is obtained through a regulatory submission to a national competent authority. Linking clinical_trial_authorization to submission enables tracking which submission']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: CTA approvals (EU/national competent authority decisions) are TMF essential documents per DIA reference model Zone 1. Linking CTA records to their TMF documents enables inspection readiness verificati']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date on which the national competent authority formally granted the Clinical Trial Authorization. This is the principal regulatory event date and the start of the authorized trial period. Corresponds to TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP.']
  authorization_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the Clinical Trial Authorization as determined by the national competent authority. Drives regulatory compliance monitoring, site activation eligibility, and inspection readiness assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|conditionally_authorized|refused|withdrawn|expired|suspended — promote to reference product]']
  authorization_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CTA record by authorization event type: initial authorization, substantial modification (protocol amendment requiring NCA approval), renewal, geographic extension, or withdrawal. Drives workflow and regulatory reporting logic.']
  conditions_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the national competent authority attached specific conditions or obligations to the Clinical Trial Authorization that must be fulfilled for the authorization to remain valid. True if conditions exist; False otherwise.']
  conditions_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of any conditions, restrictions, or obligations attached to the Clinical Trial Authorization by the national competent authority. Populated only when conditions_attached is True.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Clinical Trial Authorization record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and ALCOA+ compliance requirements.']
  cta_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned reference number issued by the national competent authority (NCA) or EU CTIS portal upon authorization of the clinical trial. This is the official business identifier used in all regulatory correspondence and submissions.']
  ctd_section_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the relevant Common Technical Document (CTD) or eCTD dossier section submitted in support of this CTA application (e.g., Module 5 clinical study reports). Supports regulatory dossier traceability.']
  ctis_trial_number varchar [note: 'Unique trial identifier assigned within the EU Clinical Trials Information System (CTIS) under EU CTR 536/2014. Used for multi-country EU submissions and authorization tracking via the centralized CTIS portal.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date on which the national competent authority issued its formal decision (authorization, conditional authorization, refusal, or withdrawal). Used for regulatory timeline KPI reporting and compliance tracking.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Formal outcome of the national competent authority\'s review decision for this CTA application. Distinct from authorization_status in that it captures the point-in-time decision rather than the current lifecycle state.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the Clinical Trial Authorization is legally effective and trial activities may commence in the member state. May differ from authorization_date if conditions or a deferred start apply.']
  ethics_committee_opinion varchar [note: 'Outcome of the ethics committee (IRB/IEC) review associated with this CTA in the member state. Tracks whether a favorable opinion was obtained, which is a prerequisite for trial commencement in most jurisdictions.']
  ethics_opinion_date date [note: 'Date on which the ethics committee (IRB/IEC) issued its opinion for this CTA in the member state. Used to confirm that ethics approval preceded trial commencement and to track parallel review timelines.']
  eudract_number varchar [note: 'European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Clinical Trials (EudraCT) number assigned to the trial, serving as the pan-European unique trial identifier. Used for cross-referencing across EU member states and EU CTIS.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Trial Authorization expires, after which trial activities in the member state are no longer authorized unless a renewal has been granted. Null for open-ended authorizations.']
  gdpr_data_transfer_mechanism varchar [note: 'Legal mechanism under GDPR used to authorize the transfer of personal data of EU trial subjects to non-EU countries in connection with this CTA. Required for EU-based trials involving cross-border data flows.']
  indication_meddra_code varchar [note: 'Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA) Preferred Term (PT) code for the primary disease indication under investigation in this authorized trial. Enables standardized cross-study and cross-authority indication reporting.']
  inspection_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Current inspection readiness status for this CTA record, indicating whether the regulatory documentation and evidence supporting this authorization is ready for NCA or GCP inspection. Supports proactive inspection readiness management.']
  irb_iec_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a separate Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) approval is required in addition to the NCA authorization in the member state. Relevant for countries with parallel ethics and regulatory review tracks.']
  member_state_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code of the EU member state or non-EU country in which this Clinical Trial Authorization was granted by the national competent authority. Enables multi-country authorization tracking.']
  modification_scope varchar [note: 'Brief description of the scope of the substantial modification covered by this CTA record (e.g., protocol amendment, IMP change, new investigational site addition, safety update). Populated for substantial_modification authorization types.']
  national_portal_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier assigned by the national regulatory portal (e.g., MHRA IRAS number, BfArM reference, ANSM reference, Health Canada CTD number) for this CTA. Complements the CTIS trial ID for country-specific tracking.']
  planned_subject_count varchar [note: 'Number of trial subjects planned to be enrolled in the member state under this Clinical Trial Authorization, as declared in the approved protocol. Used for site capacity planning and regulatory reporting.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory framework under which this Clinical Trial Authorization was granted (e.g., EU CTR 536/2014, national regulation, ISO 14155 for medical devices). Determines applicable procedural and reporting requirements.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Trial Authorization application was formally submitted to the national competent authority. Used to calculate regulatory review timelines and track compliance with submission deadlines.']
  substantial_modification_number varchar [note: 'Sequential identifier for substantial modifications to the original CTA, as assigned by the sponsor or NCA. Populated only for authorization_type = substantial_modification. Enables tracking of the amendment history chain.']
  suspension_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Trial Authorization was suspended by the national competent authority or voluntarily by the sponsor. Null if the CTA has never been suspended. Triggers safety and compliance escalation workflows.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for the suspension of the Clinical Trial Authorization, as communicated by the national competent authority or sponsor. Populated only when suspension_date is not null.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Therapeutic area or disease indication covered by the clinical trial authorized under this CTA (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio analytics and regulatory reporting segmentation.']
  trial_phase varchar [note: 'Regulatory phase of the clinical trial covered by this authorization (Phase I through Phase IV), as defined by ICH E8 and applicable NCA guidelines. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, safety reporting thresholds, and monitoring intensity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Phase I|Phase I/II|Phase II|Phase IIa|Phase IIb|Phase III|Phase III/IV|Phase IV — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this Clinical Trial Authorization record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and ALCOA+ compliance requirements.']
  validation_date date [note: 'Date on which the national competent authority confirmed the CTA application was formally valid and complete for review. Marks the start of the regulatory assessment clock under EU CTR 536/2014.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module) for the CTA authorization letter or dossier. Enables direct linkage from this master record to the source regulatory document in the eTMF system of record.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the Clinical Trial Authorization was formally withdrawn, either by the sponsor or by the national competent authority. Null if the CTA has not been withdrawn.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative reason for the withdrawal of the Clinical Trial Authorization. Populated only when withdrawal_date is not null. Required for regulatory reporting and inspection readiness.']

  Note: 'Master record for Clinical Trial Authorizations (CTAs) issued by national competent authorities under EU CTR 536/2014, national regulations, and equivalent non-US frameworks. Tracks CTA reference number, member state, competent authority, trial phase, authorization date, expiry date, conditions attached, substantial modification history, and current authorization status. Complements IND records for non-US regulatory coverage and enables multi-country trial authorization tracking across EU CTIS and national portals.'
}

Table regulatory.strategy {
  strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for strategy']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Regulatory strategies are developed for specific regulatory applications (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA). The ind_number STRING field on strategy is a denormalized reference to application.ind_number. Adding app']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Regulatory strategies target specific lead health authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA). The lead_agency STRING field on strategy is a denormalized reference to health_authority. Adding this FK normalizes the ']
  risk_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.risk_assessment. Business justification: Regulatory strategy development incorporates GCP compliance risk assessments to plan submission timelines and inspection readiness. Strategy teams reference risk tier and residual risk scores when def']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_study. Business justification: Regulatory strategy is developed and maintained per study in CTMS operations, especially for pivotal studies. Regulatory affairs teams need direct study-level strategy traceability for integrated deve']
  adaptive_pathway_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an adaptive licensing or adaptive pathway approach (EMA Adaptive Pathways Pilot, FDA Accelerated Approval) is incorporated into this regulatory strategy, allowing staged approval based on interim data.']
  agency_interaction_plan varchar [note: 'Narrative description of planned agency interactions (e.g., Pre-IND meeting, Type B End-of-Phase 2 meeting, Pre-NDA meeting, Scientific Advice, PRIME eligibility request) including meeting types, timing, and objectives as documented in the strategy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory strategy document was formally approved internally by the Regulatory Affairs leadership and/or sponsor. Marks the transition from draft to approved status in Veeva Vault.']
  breakthrough_therapy_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FDA Breakthrough Therapy Designation (BTD) has been requested or granted for this program, enabling intensive FDA guidance and rolling review benefits.']
  competitive_landscape_notes varchar [note: 'Confidential notes on the competitive regulatory landscape including competitor approval timelines, orphan drug designations, patent cliffs, and market exclusivity considerations that influence the regulatory strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory strategy record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctd_ectd_format_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the submission dossier must be prepared in CTD/eCTD format as required by ICH M4 and the target agencies. True for most major agency submissions (FDA, EMA, PMDA); may be False for certain early-phase or regional submissions.']
  development_phase varchar [note: 'Clinical development phase covered by this regulatory strategy. Determines the regulatory requirements, agency interaction types, and submission milestones applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: phase_1|phase_1_2|phase_2|phase_2_3|phase_3|phase_4|pre_ind|nda_bla_maa — promote to reference product]']
  dossier_format varchar [note: 'Format of the regulatory submission dossier planned under this strategy: eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document), NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission), paper, or regional format for specific agencies.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this version of the regulatory strategy is operationally effective and binding for the program team. Supports version-controlled strategy lifecycle management.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this version of the regulatory strategy is superseded or retired. Null for the currently active version. Enables historical strategy version tracking.']
  fast_track_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FDA Fast Track Designation has been requested or granted, enabling more frequent FDA interactions and eligibility for rolling review of the NDA/BLA.']
  health_authority_scientific_advice_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal scientific advice or protocol assistance from a health authority (e.g., EMA Scientific Advice, FDA Formal Meeting) is planned as part of this regulatory strategy.']
  key_regulatory_risks varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of identified regulatory risks that could impact approval timelines or probability of success (e.g., unresolved safety signals, insufficient efficacy data, CMC concerns, precedent gaps). Supports risk-based regulatory planning.']
  meddra_indication_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) code corresponding to the target indication, enabling standardized cross-study and cross-agency reporting and signal detection alignment.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether orphan drug designation has been sought or granted for this program by FDA (ODD) or EMA (OMP), which confers market exclusivity and fee waivers. True if designation is active or planned.']
  pediatric_investigation_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Pediatric Investigation Plan (EMA PIP) or Pediatric Study Plan (FDA PSP) is required as part of this regulatory strategy, per pediatric regulatory obligations.']
  planned_approval_date date [note: 'Anticipated date of regulatory approval from the lead agency based on standard or expedited review timelines. Used for commercial launch planning and portfolio milestone tracking.']
  planned_ind_submission_date date [note: 'Target date for submission of the IND application to the FDA as planned in this regulatory strategy. Drives pre-IND meeting scheduling and CMC/nonclinical package readiness timelines.']
  planned_nda_bla_maa_submission_date date [note: 'Target date for submission of the marketing authorization application (NDA, BLA, or MAA) as planned in this regulatory strategy. Key milestone for program planning and resource allocation.']
  prime_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether EMA PRIME (PRIority MEdicines) designation has been requested or granted, providing enhanced EMA support for medicines that target unmet medical needs.']
  regulatory_pathway varchar [note: 'Global regulatory pathway designation for this strategy. Captures whether the program pursues standard review, accelerated approval (FDA), Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, PRIME (EMA), Conditional Marketing Authorization, or adaptive pathway. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|accelerated|breakthrough|fast_track|prime|adaptive|conditional — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_precedents varchar [note: 'Summary of relevant regulatory precedents from previously approved products in the same therapeutic class or indication that inform the strategy (e.g., approved comparators, agency guidance documents, advisory committee outcomes).']
  rems_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) is anticipated or required by FDA as a condition of approval, based on the safety profile of the investigational product.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall regulatory risk classification for this strategy based on the risk assessment of approval probability, data package adequacy, and agency precedent. Drives escalation and mitigation planning.']
  rolling_review_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a rolling review submission strategy is planned, allowing completed sections of the dossier to be submitted to the agency before the full application is complete. Applicable for FDA Fast Track and EMA Rolling Review.']
  strategy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique code assigned to this regulatory strategy document within the Veeva Vault regulatory submissions module (e.g., REG-STR-2024-0042). Used for cross-system referencing and TMF indexing.']
  strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory strategy document. Tracks progression from initial drafting through internal review, formal approval, and eventual supersession or retirement when a new version is issued.']
  submission_sequence_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned sequencing approach for multi-regional submissions: simultaneous (all agencies at once), sequential lead-first (lead agency then others), rolling (submit sections as available), or staggered (phased by region). Drives submission timeline planning.']
  submission_type varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory submission type targeted by this strategy (e.g., IND - Investigational New Drug, NDA - New Drug Application, BLA - Biologics License Application, MAA - Marketing Authorization Application, CTA - Clinical Trial Authorization). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|sNDA|sBLA|CTA|amendment — promote to reference product]']
  target_agencies varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all global health authorities targeted for submission under this strategy (e.g., \'FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada\'). Supports multi-regional submission planning and sequencing.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the regulatory strategy document, summarizing the development program, indication, and regulatory pathway scope (e.g., \'Global NDA/MAA Regulatory Strategy for Drug X in Indication Y Phase III\').']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this regulatory strategy record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Required for audit trail and data lineage tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault eTMF/Regulatory Submissions module where the approved regulatory strategy document is stored. Enables direct traceability from this data record to the source document.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the regulatory strategy document following major.minor versioning convention (e.g., 1.0, 2.1). Incremented upon each approved revision to support document lifecycle management in Veeva Vault.']

  Note: 'Documents the regulatory strategy plan for a development program or individual study. Captures target indication, development phase, global regulatory pathway (standard, accelerated, adaptive), key agency interactions planned, submission sequence strategy, risk assessment, regulatory precedents, competitive landscape notes, strategy version, approval date of strategy, and responsible regulatory affairs lead. Supports long-range regulatory planning.'
}

Table regulatory.regulatory_inspection {
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inspection']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.application. Business justification: Pre-approval inspections (PAIs) and other regulatory inspections are directly tied to specific regulatory applications. The application_number STRING field on regulatory_inspection is a denormalized r']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Internal audits are conducted to prepare for regulatory inspections. Linking the regulatory inspection to the internal audit that preceded it supports the inspection readiness workflow and demonstrate']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record created in MasterControl in response to inspection findings. Links inspection events to the quality management system for remediation tracking.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding inspection record in Oracle Siebel CTMS (Clinical Trial Management System). Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CTMS.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Regulatory inspections are conducted by specific health authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA). The inspecting_authority and authority_country_code STRING fields are denormalized references to health_authority.']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) conduct GCP/GLP inspections at central laboratory facilities. The regulatory_inspection table currently links to study_site but not lab_facility. A domain expert expects']
  prior_inspection_regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this inspection to a previous inspection of the same site or sponsor. Populated when is_repeat_inspection is True, enabling inspection history chain analysis.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Regulatory inspections frequently identify or are triggered by protocol deviations. Linking regulatory_inspection to the specific protocol_deviation enables traceability from deviation discovery throu']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: GCP inspections are conducted against specific protocol versions; inspectors verify protocol compliance. Inspection management and CAPA tracking require direct FK from regulatory_inspection to protoco']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Regulatory inspections (GCP, GMP) review the investigator site file. Linking the inspection record to the specific site file enables inspection preparation tracking, completeness gap identification pr']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study that is the subject of this inspection. Links to the study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site being inspected, if the inspection scope is site-level. Null for sponsor-level or system-level inspections.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory inspection reports, CAPA response letters, and agency close-out letters are TMF essential documents. Linking inspection records to their TMF documents enables inspection readiness tracking ']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the inspection concluded, including the closeout meeting. Used to calculate inspection duration and track report receipt timelines.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the inspection commenced on-site or remotely. May differ from scheduled_date due to rescheduling. Used for cycle time and compliance metrics.']
  announcement_date date [note: 'Date on which the inspecting authority formally notified the site, sponsor, or CRO of the upcoming inspection. Used to calculate preparation lead time and assess readiness adequacy.']
  capa_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the sponsor or site must submit a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan in response to the inspection findings. Typically defined by the inspecting authority in the inspection report or cover letter.']
  capa_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the CAPA plan was submitted to the inspecting authority in response to inspection findings. Compared against capa_response_due_date to assess timeliness of regulatory response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection readiness record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical (most severe) findings identified during the inspection. Critical findings indicate practices that may have a direct impact on subject safety or data integrity. Drives CAPA prioritization and regulatory escalation decisions.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the inspection lasted from actual_start_date to actual_end_date. Indicates inspection intensity and is used for resource planning and benchmarking.']
  focus_areas varchar [note: 'Free-text or semi-structured description of the specific areas, processes, or systems targeted by the inspection (e.g., informed consent process, SAE reporting, randomization procedures, data integrity, TMF completeness). Supports targeted readiness preparation.']
  inspection_category varchar [note: 'Categorizes the trigger or purpose of the inspection: Routine (scheduled surveillance), For-Cause (triggered by a specific concern or complaint), Pre-Approval (prior to NDA/BLA/MAA approval), Pre-License (prior to license issuance), or Follow-Up (verifying CAPA closure from a prior inspection).']
  inspection_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection was conducted on-site (inspectors physically present at the facility), remotely (conducted via electronic document review and virtual meetings), or as a hybrid of both modalities. Relevant post-COVID regulatory practice.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the inspection event by the inspecting authority or the sponsor/CRO tracking system (e.g., FDA EIR number, EMA inspection reference). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this transaction.']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the organizational scope of the inspection: Site (investigational site/PI), Sponsor (sponsor organization), CRO (Contract Research Organization), Laboratory (central or local lab), or System (IT/data management system).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection event: Planned (scheduled but not yet announced), Announced (authority has notified the site/sponsor), In-Progress (inspection is actively underway), Completed (inspection activities finished, report pending), Closed (report received and all CAPAs resolved), Cancelled (inspection withdrawn by authority or sponsor).']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection by regulatory discipline. GCP (Good Clinical Practice) covers clinical trial conduct; GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) covers drug manufacturing; GLP (Good Laboratory Practice) covers non-clinical studies; Pharmacovigilance covers safety reporting systems; BIMO (Bioresearch Monitoring) is FDA-specific; Systems covers IT/data integrity audits.']
  inspector_count varchar [note: 'Total number of regulatory inspectors participating in the inspection. Indicates the scale and intensity of the inspection and informs resource planning for the host organization.']
  is_repeat_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection is a follow-up or repeat inspection of the same site or sponsor, typically triggered by unresolved findings from a prior inspection (True = repeat; False = first inspection).']
  is_unannounced boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection was conducted without prior notice to the site or sponsor (True = unannounced/for-cause; False = announced). Unannounced inspections typically indicate heightened regulatory scrutiny.']
