  lead_inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead regulatory inspector assigned by the inspecting authority. Used for correspondence tracking and relationship management with regulatory agencies.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major findings identified during the inspection. Major findings indicate significant non-compliance with GCP or regulatory requirements that could affect subject safety or data reliability but are not immediately critical.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor (other) findings identified during the inspection. Minor findings represent deviations from GCP or regulatory requirements that are unlikely to have a significant impact on subject safety or data integrity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, observations, or internal commentary about the inspection event, readiness activities, or authority interactions not captured in structured fields.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Official outcome classification issued by the inspecting authority upon completion: Satisfactory (no significant findings), VAI (Voluntary Action Indicated — minor deficiencies requiring voluntary corrective action), OAI (Official Action Indicated — significant deficiencies requiring mandatory corrective action), No Action Indicated (FDA-specific: no objectionable conditions), or Pending (outcome not yet issued).']
  readiness_status varchar [note: 'Tracks the internal preparation state for the upcoming inspection: Not Started, In Preparation (readiness activities underway), Ready (all preparation activities completed), Conditionally Ready (ready with known gaps being managed), or Overdue (preparation milestones missed). Distinct from inspection_status which tracks the actual inspection event lifecycle.']
  regulatory_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal regulatory affairs contact or liaison responsible for managing communications with the inspecting authority for this inspection event.']
  report_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the official inspection report (e.g., FDA Establishment Inspection Report, EMA GCP Inspection Report) was received from the inspecting authority. Triggers the CAPA response timeline.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'The planned or announced date on which the inspection is scheduled to begin, as communicated by the inspecting authority or agreed upon internally. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) for planning purposes.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of all inspection findings across all severity levels (critical + major + minor). Provides a single summary metric for inspection severity assessment and benchmarking across studies and sites.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection readiness record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module) for the official inspection report, CAPA correspondence, or inspection readiness package. Enables direct traceability to the source document management system.']

  Note: 'Tracks regulatory inspection readiness activities and inspection events for studies and sites. Captures inspection type (GCP, GMP, GLP, pharmacovigilance), inspecting authority, inspection scope (study, site, sponsor), scheduled date, actual date, inspection outcome (satisfactory, voluntary action indicated, official action indicated), critical/major/minor findings count, CAPA linkage, and inspection report receipt date. Supports proactive inspection preparation.'
}

Table regulatory.commitment {
  commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commitment']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA) under which this commitment was imposed as a condition of approval.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Post-approval commitments frequently originate from compliance inspections (e.g., FDA Form 483 observations). Linking commitment to the originating inspection enables traceability from inspection find']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Post-approval regulatory commitments are imposed by specific health authorities (FDA, EMA). The agency and agency_country_code STRING fields on commitment are denormalized references to health_authori']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Post-approval commitments (e.g., pediatric study requirements, confirmatory trials) reference specific protocol versions. Regulatory affairs teams track which protocol fulfills each commitment for FDA']
  regulatory_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Post-approval regulatory commitments are imposed as conditions of a specific regulatory approval (e.g., post-marketing studies required as conditions of accelerated approval). Linking commitment to th']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the post-marketing clinical study or trial being conducted to fulfill this commitment, where applicable (e.g., a post-marketing required study or confirmatory trial).']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Post-marketing commitment reports and pediatric study plans are TMF documents filed with the agency. Linking commitment records to TMF documents enables inspection-ready retrieval of commitment fulfil']
  accelerated_approval_condition boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment is a confirmatory trial required as a condition of accelerated approval (True) or a standard post-approval commitment (False). Accelerated approval conditions carry heightened regulatory scrutiny.']
  agency_commitment_number varchar [note: 'The official commitment or post-marketing requirement number assigned by the health authority (e.g., FDA PMR/PMC number, EMA obligation number) at the time of approval. Used for regulatory correspondence and agency tracking.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority granted product approval and formally imposed this commitment. Establishes the baseline for commitment timelines.']
  commitment_category varchar [note: 'Regulatory category distinguishing legally binding Post-Marketing Requirements (PMR) mandated by the FDA versus voluntary Post-Marketing Commitments (PMC), or conditions of approval imposed by other agencies such as EMA or PMDA.']
  commitment_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the regulatory commitment as stated in the approval letter or agency decision document. Captures the exact obligation language including scope, objectives, and deliverables required by the health authority.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory commitment. Tracks progression from pending through active execution to fulfillment or overdue state. Deferred indicates agency-approved extension; released indicates the agency has waived the obligation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|fulfilled|overdue|deferred|released|terminated — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory commitment imposed by the health authority. Indicates whether it is a post-marketing study (PMR/PMC), label update, Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS), risk minimization measure, pediatric study, confirmatory trial, or other obligation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: post_marketing_study|label_update|rems|risk_minimization_measure|pediatric_study|confirmatory_trial|other — promote to reference product]']
  completion_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the regulatory commitment was fulfilled and the required deliverable was submitted to or accepted by the health authority. Null if not yet completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory commitment record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record provenance per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctd_module varchar [note: 'The CTD module to which the deliverable for this commitment will be submitted (e.g., Module 5 for clinical study reports, Module 1 for labeling updates). Aligns commitment deliverables with eCTD dossier structure.']
  deferral_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for any agency-approved deferral or extension of the commitment due date. Documents the justification submitted to and accepted by the health authority.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the regulatory commitment must be fulfilled or the required deliverable (e.g., final study report, label update, REMS implementation) must be submitted to the health authority. Drives overdue status calculation.']
  indication varchar [note: 'The approved therapeutic indication or disease area for which the product received approval and to which this commitment is specifically tied.']
  inn_name varchar [note: 'WHO-assigned International Nonproprietary Name (INN) of the active substance subject to this commitment. Provides a globally standardized identifier for the drug substance independent of brand name.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether documentation and evidence for this commitment has been reviewed and confirmed as inspection-ready (True) for potential health authority inspection or audit. Supports regulatory inspection preparedness.']
  is_binding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment is legally binding (True = Post-Marketing Requirement mandated by law, e.g., FDA PMR under FDAAA) or voluntary (False = Post-Marketing Commitment, e.g., FDA PMC). Critical for prioritization and enforcement risk assessment.']
  is_pediatric boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment involves a pediatric study required under the Pediatric Research Equity Act (PREA) or EMA Paediatric Regulation. Enables filtering and reporting on pediatric obligations.']
  last_report_submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent progress report or status update for this commitment was submitted to the health authority. Used to calculate next reporting due date.']
  milestone_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent commitment milestone was achieved or is targeted. Used in conjunction with milestone_description to track incremental progress toward fulfillment.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Description of the current or most recent milestone achieved in fulfilling this commitment (e.g., protocol submitted, enrollment complete, interim analysis submitted, final report submitted). Supports granular progress tracking.']
  next_report_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the next scheduled progress report or status update on this commitment must be submitted to the health authority. Calculated based on reporting frequency and last submission date.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, internal commentary, or operational notes related to this commitment that do not fit into structured fields. Used by regulatory affairs teams for tracking nuances and agency communication history.']
  original_due_date date [note: 'The initial due date as specified in the original approval letter or agency decision, before any agency-approved extensions or deferrals. Preserved for audit trail and compliance tracking.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Internal priority classification assigned to this commitment based on regulatory risk, binding nature, and proximity to due date. Drives resource allocation and escalation decisions within the regulatory affairs function.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Proprietary or brand name of the drug product or biologic to which this regulatory commitment applies, as registered with the health authority.']
  release_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for why the health authority released or waived this commitment (e.g., scientific evidence no longer supports the requirement, alternative data satisfied the obligation). Populated only when commitment_status is released.']
  rems_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the FDA-required Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program associated with this commitment, if the commitment type is REMS. Null for non-REMS commitments.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which progress updates or status reports on this commitment must be submitted to the health authority (e.g., annual, semi-annual, quarterly, one-time final report). Drives scheduling of regulatory submissions.']
  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor\'s designated regulatory affairs contact responsible for managing and tracking this commitment. Business-confidential operational data.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title of the regulatory commitment as referenced in the approval letter or agency correspondence. Used for display and reporting purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory commitment record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module) for the approval letter, agency decision document, or commitment tracking record associated with this commitment. Enables direct traceability to the source regulatory document.']

  Note: 'Tracks post-approval regulatory commitments and post-marketing requirements imposed by health authorities as conditions of approval. Captures commitment type (post-marketing study, label update, REMS, risk minimization measure), commitment description, due date, completion date, status (pending, in progress, fulfilled, overdue), agency-assigned commitment number, and reporting frequency. Ensures compliance with approval conditions.'
}

Table regulatory.special_designation {
  special_designation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a special regulatory designation record in the Clinical Trials lakehouse silver layer.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, or CTA) under which this special designation was requested or granted.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the granting health authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada) that issued or manages this special designation.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary clinical study that provided the evidence base for the designation request (e.g., the pivotal Phase 2 study supporting a Breakthrough Therapy designation request).']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory submission record through which the designation request was filed or through which the designation was referenced in a marketing application (e.g., the NDA/BLA submission citing the Breakthrough Therapy designation).']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Special designation letters (orphan drug, breakthrough therapy, fast track) are TMF essential documents. Regulatory teams file agency designation letters in the TMF; this FK supports TMF completeness ']
  agency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the health authority reviewer or project manager assigned to this designation at the agency (e.g., FDA CDER Rare Diseases Program contact, EMA PRIME rapporteur). Used for regulatory relationship management.']
  agency_letter_date date [note: 'Date of the official agency letter communicating the designation decision (grant, denial, or revocation). Used for regulatory correspondence tracking and TMF filing in Veeva Vault.']
  agency_receipt_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the designation request. Triggers the formal review clock for the agency\'s response timeline.']
  agency_reference_number varchar [note: 'Internal reference or tracking number assigned by the health authority to the designation request or correspondence (e.g., FDA CDER tracking number, EMA EMEA procedure number). Distinct from the designation number issued upon grant.']
  annual_report_due_date date [note: 'Due date for the next mandatory annual progress report to the health authority as required under the designation conditions (e.g., FDA Orphan Drug annual report per 21 CFR Part 316.27). Null if no annual reporting obligation applies.']
  confirmatory_trial_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-approval confirmatory clinical trial is required as a condition of the designation or approval pathway (e.g., mandatory for FDA Accelerated Approval under 21 CFR Part 314 Subpart H). Drives post-marketing commitment tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this special designation record was first created in the Clinical Trials lakehouse. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  denial_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the health authority formally denied the designation request. Null if the designation was not denied. Used to track agency response timelines and inform resubmission strategy.']
  denial_reason varchar [note: 'Summary of the health authority\'s stated rationale for denying the designation request. Captured from the agency\'s denial letter to inform resubmission strategy and regulatory intelligence. Null if not denied.']
  designation_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any conditions, post-designation obligations, or commitments attached to the granted designation by the health authority (e.g., annual progress reports, specific clinical milestones, post-marketing studies for Accelerated Approval). Critical for compliance tracking.']
  designation_number varchar [note: 'Externally assigned unique reference number issued by the health authority for this special designation (e.g., FDA Orphan Drug designation number OD-XXXXXX, Breakthrough Therapy designation reference). Serves as the official business identifier for tracking and correspondence.']
  designation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the special designation. Tracks the progression from initial request through agency review to grant, denial, withdrawal, or revocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Requested|Granted|Denied|Withdrawn|Expired|Revoked|Under Review — promote to reference product]']
  designation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the special regulatory designation granted by the health authority. Determines the expedited development pathway and associated regulatory obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Breakthrough Therapy|Fast Track|Accelerated Approval|Priority Review|Orphan Drug|PRIME|Adaptive Pathways|SAKIGAKE — promote to reference product]']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the special designation expires or lapses, if applicable. Relevant for time-limited designations such as PRIME (EMA) or SAKIGAKE (PMDA) where the designation has a defined validity period. Null for perpetual designations such as FDA Orphan Drug.']
  fee_waiver_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the special designation confers eligibility for a regulatory fee waiver or reduction (e.g., FDA Orphan Drug designation entitles sponsors to a waiver of the NDA/BLA application user fee under PDUFA). True if fee waiver applies.']
  grant_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the health authority officially granted the special designation. Null if the designation has not yet been granted. This is the principal business event date for the designation lifecycle.']
  indication varchar [note: 'Specific disease, condition, or patient population for which the special designation is requested or granted. Aligns with the MedDRA Preferred Term (PT) or Lowest Level Term (LLT) used in the regulatory submission.']
  inn_name varchar [note: 'WHO-assigned International Nonproprietary Name (INN) for the active substance of the investigational product. Used for standardized identification across regulatory submissions and health authority correspondence.']
  market_exclusivity_years varchar [note: 'Number of years of market exclusivity conferred by the designation upon product approval (e.g., 7 years for FDA Orphan Drug, 10 years for EU Orphan Medicinal Product). Null for designations that do not confer market exclusivity (e.g., Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory context, internal commentary, or strategic notes related to this special designation (e.g., agency meeting outcomes, key discussion points, competitive intelligence). Not for official regulatory correspondence.']
  orphan_disease_prevalence varchar [note: 'Stated prevalence or incidence of the rare disease or condition as submitted to the health authority in support of an Orphan Drug Designation request (e.g., \'fewer than 200,000 patients in the US\' per 21 CFR Part 316, or \'≤5 in 10,000 in the EU\' per EC Regulation 141/2000). Applicable only to Orphan Drug designation type.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Proprietary or working name of the investigational medicinal product (IMP) for which the special designation is sought or granted. May differ from the INN (International Nonproprietary Name) at early development stages.']
  request_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the sponsor formally submitted the designation request to the health authority. Marks the start of the agency review clock for expedited designation programs.']
  resubmission_planned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor plans to resubmit the designation request following a denial or withdrawal. Drives regulatory planning and resource allocation.']
  resubmission_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for resubmitting the designation request to the health authority after a denial or withdrawal. Null if resubmission is not planned or not yet scheduled.']
  rolling_review_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designation enables rolling review of the marketing application (submission of completed CTD modules before the full dossier is ready). Applicable to FDA Fast Track and EMA PRIME designations.']
  surrogate_endpoint_used boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designation was granted or the approval pathway relies on a surrogate or intermediate clinical endpoint rather than a traditional clinical outcome endpoint. Relevant for Accelerated Approval and Breakthrough Therapy designations.']
  tax_credit_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the designation qualifies the sponsor for the Orphan Drug Tax Credit (50% of qualified clinical testing expenses in the US under IRC Section 45C). Applicable only to FDA Orphan Drug designations.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Broad medical or scientific domain in which the investigational product is being developed (e.g., Oncology, Rare Disease, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio analytics and regulatory strategy alignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this special designation record. Supports change tracking, audit trail compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/Regulatory module) for the designation request package, agency grant letter, or related regulatory correspondence. Enables direct traceability to the source document in the electronic Trial Master File.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor voluntarily withdrew the designation request or the health authority revoked the granted designation. Null if not withdrawn or revoked.']

  Note: 'Tracks special regulatory designations granted to investigational products by health authorities. Includes designation type (Breakthrough Therapy, Fast Track, Accelerated Approval, Priority Review, Orphan Drug, PRIME, Adaptive Pathways, SAKIGAKE), granting agency, request date, grant date, expiry date, designation conditions, and associated application. Enables tracking of expedited development pathways and associated obligations.'
}

Table regulatory.regulatory_action_item {
  regulatory_action_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory action item record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  agency_meeting_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.agency_meeting. Business justification: Action items frequently arise from agency meetings (FDA Type A/B/C meetings, EMA scientific advice). The source_event_type field on regulatory_action_item can be \'agency_meeting\', and action_items_cou']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA) under which this action item was generated. Provides dossier-level context for the task.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Regulatory action items are frequently generated from audit findings with regulatory implications. Linking action items to audit findings supports the audit-to-regulatory-action traceability chain req']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated CAPA record in MasterControl when this action item is linked to a quality event, inspection finding, or deviation. Nullable when no formal CAPA is required.']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada, etc.) whose interaction generated this action item. Drives jurisdiction-specific reporting and escalation rules.']
  monitoring_action_item_id varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for this action item in Oracle Siebel CTMS, enabling bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CTMS system of record.']
  prior_action_item_regulatory_action_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the previous action item from which this one was derived (e.g., a re-opened or recurring item). Enables lineage tracking across action item generations. Nullable for original items.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Protocol deviations with regulatory reporting requirements generate regulatory action items. Linking action items to protocol deviations supports the deviation-to-regulatory-response workflow required']
  regulatory_correspondence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.correspondence. Business justification: Action items can arise from regulatory correspondence (Complete Response Letters, Information Requests, deficiency letters). The source_event_type field on regulatory_action_item can be \'correspondenc']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Action items frequently arise from regulatory inspection findings (critical, major, and minor findings). Linking regulatory_action_item to regulatory_inspection enables tracking all action items gener']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) to which this regulatory action item belongs. Links the action item to the relevant trial context for reporting and filtering.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory submission (e.g., a response sequence, amendment, or safety report) that triggered or is associated with this action item. Nullable when the action item originates from a meeting or inspection rather than a submission.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory action item responses (agency information requests, inspection findings responses) are filed as TMF documents. Linking action items to their response TMF documents enables inspection-ready ']
  unblinding_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.unblinding_request. Business justification: An unblinding event (especially SAE-triggered) generates regulatory action items: agency notification, protocol deviation reporting, or IND safety report submission. regulatory_action_item already tra']
  action_item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific regulatory task, finding, or commitment that must be addressed. Captures the full context of what is required, including any agency-specified language from meeting minutes or inspection reports.']
  action_item_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-known business identifier for the action item (e.g., RAI-2024-00123). Used in regulatory correspondence, meeting minutes, and inspection response packages to uniquely reference the task.']
  action_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the regulatory action item. Drives operational dashboards, escalation logic, and compliance reporting. \'overdue\' is system-set when due_date has passed without closure.']
  action_item_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the nature of the action item. Drives workflow templates and regulatory reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commitment|information_request|capa|protocol_amendment|label_update|response_to_agency|internal_task|other — promote to reference product]']
  agency_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority formally acknowledged receipt of the response or deliverable. Used to confirm the regulatory clock was stopped and to document agency receipt for the eTMF.']
  agency_mandated_due_date date [note: 'The specific response deadline formally communicated by the health authority (e.g., FDA 30-day information request clock, EMA Day 120 response). Distinct from the internal due_date which may be set earlier to allow review time. Nullable when no formal agency deadline exists.']
  agency_submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the completed response or deliverable was formally submitted to the health authority. Distinct from completion_date (internal closure) — captures the actual agency-facing delivery date for regulatory clock tracking.']
  assigned_owner_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the individual responsible for completing this action item. Used for automated notifications, escalation alerts, and audit trail. Classified as confidential PII per GDPR and HIPAA.']
  assigned_owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual (typically a Regulatory Affairs professional or CRA) responsible for completing this action item. Captured as a denormalized field for operational task management and audit trail purposes.']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory affairs team or functional group responsible for this action item (e.g., \'Global Regulatory Strategy\', \'Submissions Team\', \'Safety Reporting\'). Supports team-level workload reporting and capacity planning.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the action item was fully completed and closed. Nullable until the item reaches \'closed\' status. Used to calculate cycle time and measure regulatory responsiveness KPIs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this regulatory action item record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the action item must be completed. May be agency-mandated (e.g., 30-day response clock from FDA information request) or internally set. Drives overdue status calculation and escalation triggers.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which this action item was escalated. Nullable when escalation_flag is False. Used to track escalation response time and management intervention patterns.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action item has been escalated to senior regulatory leadership or executive management due to risk, overdue status, or strategic importance. True = escalated; False = not escalated.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of why this action item was escalated (e.g., \'Approaching agency deadline with incomplete data package\', \'Potential clinical hold risk identified\'). Provides context for management review.']
  finding_classification varchar [note: 'GCP/GMP inspection finding classification when the action item originates from an inspection finding. Aligns with FDA and EMA inspection classification standards. Nullable for non-inspection-sourced action items.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, interim progress updates, or commentary on the action item that does not fit structured fields. Supports operational communication within the regulatory affairs team.']
  open_date date [note: 'The date on which this action item was formally opened and entered into the regulatory task register. Represents the business event timestamp for the creation of the obligation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the action item, reflecting urgency and regulatory risk. \'critical\' typically indicates a potential clinical hold, safety signal, or imminent agency deadline. Used for resource allocation and escalation decisions.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action item represents a recurrence of a previously identified issue or finding. True = recurrence of a prior finding; False = new issue. Used in quality trend analysis and CAPA effectiveness assessments.']
  regulatory_area varchar [note: 'Functional regulatory domain to which this action item belongs (e.g., clinical, safety, CMC). Enables domain-specific filtering and assignment routing within the regulatory affairs team. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical|safety|cmc|regulatory_affairs|data_management|biostatistics|quality|other — promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory procedure, guideline, or regulation that this action item must satisfy (e.g., \'21 CFR 312.32\', \'EMA/CHMP/ICH/295148/2004\', \'ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18\'). Provides the regulatory basis for the required action.']
  response_document_title varchar [note: 'Title of the formal response document prepared to address this action item (e.g., \'Response to FDA Information Request Letter dated 2024-03-15\'). Supports eTMF filing and regulatory correspondence tracking.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Indicates the review cycle stage of this action item within an ongoing agency interaction (e.g., initial response, follow-up clarification, final closure). Supports multi-round agency dialogue tracking.']
  source_event_reference varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured reference identifier for the originating event (e.g., meeting number, inspection report number, correspondence reference number, or internal review ID). Provides traceability back to the source document or event record.']
  source_event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the business event that originated this action item. Drives workflow routing and reporting dashboards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: agency_meeting|inspection_finding|regulatory_correspondence|internal_review|dsmb_recommendation|other — promote to reference product if additional values are needed]']
  sponsor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the sponsor representative responsible for oversight of this action item. Relevant when the CRO is acting on behalf of a pharmaceutical or biotech sponsor and the sponsor must approve the response.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarizing the regulatory action item (e.g., \'Respond to FDA Information Request on CMC Data\'). Used in dashboards, email notifications, and task lists.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when this regulatory action item record was last modified. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated document or record in Veeva Vault (eTMF or regulatory submissions module), such as the meeting minutes, inspection report, or correspondence document that originated this action item. Enables direct navigation to the source document.']

  Note: 'Tracks action items arising from agency meetings, correspondence, inspection findings, and internal regulatory reviews. Captures action item description, source event (meeting, inspection, correspondence), assigned owner, due date, completion date, priority level, status (open, in progress, closed, overdue), and escalation flag. Provides an operational task register for the regulatory affairs team.'
}

Table regulatory.country_registration {
  country_registration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each country-level product registration record in the regulatory lakehouse silver layer.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA) under which this country registration was granted.']
  ethics_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_approval. Business justification: Country registrations require local ethics approval in many jurisdictions as part of the authorization package. Linking country registration to ethics approval supports the complete country authorizat']
  ethics_submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ethics_submission. Business justification: Country registrations require ethics submissions as part of national authorization. country_registration already has ethics_approval_id but not ethics_submission_id; tracking the submission (not just ']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada, TGA, etc.) that issued or oversees this country registration.']
  procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.procedure. Business justification: A country registration follows a specific regulatory procedure (centralized, decentralized, mutual recognition, national). The country_registration table currently stores regulatory_procedure_number a']
  regulatory_approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: A country registration is the direct outcome of a regulatory approval in a specific country/jurisdiction. Linking country_registration to the approval that granted it provides the approval-to-registra']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Country registrations are initiated through regulatory submissions (initial marketing authorization applications, renewal submissions). Linking country_registration to the initiating submission provid']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Country registration certificates and approval letters are TMF Zone 1 essential documents. Regulatory teams must retrieve registration documentation during inspections and for renewal submissions; thi']
  annual_report_due_date date [note: 'Next due date for the periodic safety update report (PSUR), development safety update report (DSUR), or annual product report required by the health authority in this country to maintain the registration.']
  conditional_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this registration was granted under conditional or accelerated approval, requiring post-marketing confirmatory studies or commitments. True if conditional approval conditions are still outstanding.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code identifying the jurisdiction in which the product is registered (e.g., USA, GBR, JPN, CAN, AUS, DEU).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this country registration record was first created in the regulatory data product. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  ctd_module_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of the Common Technical Document (CTD) dossier submitted for this registration: full (complete Modules 1-5), abridged (generic/hybrid referencing originator data), hybrid (mixed), or bibliographic (literature-based). Determines the regulatory review pathway.']
  dosage_form varchar [note: 'Pharmaceutical dosage form as registered in this country (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution for injection, lyophilized powder). Must match the approved product dossier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|solution_for_injection|lyophilized_powder|oral_solution|patch|inhaler|cream|suppository|suspension — promote to reference product]']
  dossier_format varchar [note: 'Format of the regulatory dossier submitted for this registration: eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document), NeeS (Non-eCTD Electronic Submission), paper, or VNeeS (Veterinary NeeS). Determines submission and archival requirements.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current registration period expires and renewal is required to maintain marketing authorization. Null for registrations that do not expire (e.g., some FDA approvals).']
  indication varchar [note: 'Approved therapeutic indication(s) for which the product is registered in this country, as stated in the approved product labeling or Summary of Product Characteristics (SmPC). May be narrower than the global indication.']
  inn_name varchar [note: 'WHO-assigned International Nonproprietary Name (INN) for the active substance, as recognized in the country registration. Provides a globally consistent scientific identifier independent of brand name.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory file and documentation for this country registration are currently in a state of inspection readiness as assessed by the regulatory affairs team. True if ready for a health authority inspection.']
  last_annual_report_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent annual product report, PSUR, or equivalent periodic safety report was submitted to the health authority for this country registration.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent registration renewal was granted by the health authority. Establishes the start of the current registration period for expiry and renewal due date calculations.']
  last_variation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently approved post-approval variation or amendment to this country registration. Used to track the currency of the approved dossier and labeling.']
  local_agent_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact details (phone, email, or address) for the local regulatory agent or marketing authorization holder in the registered country. Used for regulatory correspondence and inspection coordination.']
  local_agent_name varchar [note: 'Name of the local regulatory agent, marketing authorization holder (MAH), or in-country representative responsible for maintaining the registration and liaising with the national health authority. Required in many jurisdictions for foreign sponsors.']
  market_exclusivity_expiry date [note: 'Date on which the data exclusivity or market exclusivity period expires in this country, after which generic or biosimilar applications may reference the originator dossier. Critical for lifecycle and competitive intelligence planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional regulatory context, authority-specific conditions, post-approval commitments, or operational notes relevant to this country registration that are not captured in structured fields.']
  orphan_drug_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product holds orphan drug designation in this country, conferring market exclusivity and regulatory incentives for rare disease treatments. True if orphan designation is active.']
  patent_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the primary product patent expires in this country. Used in conjunction with market exclusivity for lifecycle management and generic entry forecasting.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Brand or trade name of the medicinal product as approved and registered in this specific country. May differ from the global brand name due to local naming requirements.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date on which the health authority formally granted or issued the product registration or marketing authorization. Represents the effective start of the registration.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or marketing authorization number assigned by the national health authority upon approval (e.g., NDA 123456, EU/1/20/1234, PMDA approval number). This is the externally-known business identifier for the registration.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the country registration: active (valid and in force), expired (past expiry date without renewal), suspended (temporarily halted by authority), withdrawn (voluntarily or involuntarily removed), pending (application submitted, awaiting decision), cancelled (application withdrawn before approval).']
  registration_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory procedure used to obtain this registration: national (single-country), mutual recognition (MRP), decentralized (DCP), centralized (EMA), or bilateral agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: national|mutual_recognition|decentralized|centralized|bilateral|abridged|hybrid — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  reimbursement_status varchar [note: 'Current reimbursement or health technology assessment (HTA) status of the registered product in this country. Indicates whether the product is covered by national health insurance or payer systems, supporting market access planning.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the renewal application must be submitted to the health authority to avoid lapse of the registration. May precede the expiry date by a regulatory-mandated lead time.']
  renewal_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the renewal application was submitted to the health authority. Used to confirm timely submission relative to the renewal due date.']
  route_of_administration varchar [note: 'Approved route of administration as registered in this country (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, intramuscular, topical, inhalation). Must align with the approved product dossier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|intrathecal|transdermal — promote to reference product]']
  strength varchar [note: 'Approved strength or concentration of the active substance as registered in this country (e.g., 10 mg, 100 mg/mL, 500 mg/5 mL). Expressed as a free-text string to accommodate varied unit formats across jurisdictions.']
  submission_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code of the primary language in which the registration dossier was submitted to the health authority (e.g., en, fr, de, ja). Relevant for translation management and local labeling compliance.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'Broad therapeutic area classification for the registered product (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio reporting and market access analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this country registration record. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/Regulatory module) for the approved registration certificate, marketing authorization letter, or associated dossier. Enables direct traceability to the source regulatory document.']

  Note: 'Tracks product registration status and requirements across individual countries and jurisdictions. Captures country, registration number, registration type (national, mutual recognition, decentralized, centralized), registration date, expiry date, renewal due date, local agent name, registered indication, registered dosage form, and current registration status. Supports global regulatory intelligence and market access planning.'
}

Table regulatory.procedure {
  procedure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for procedure']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: Regulatory submission procedures are governed by controlled SOPs in the document management system. Linking procedure records to their governing document SOP enables SOP version control tracking, trai']
  health_authority_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.health_authority. Business justification: Regulatory procedures (centralized, decentralized, national) are defined and governed by specific health authorities. The applicable_agency_code and agency_country_code STRING fields are denormalized ']
  applicable_product_types varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of product types for which this procedure is applicable (e.g., \'small_molecule|biologic|vaccine|gene_therapy|medical_device|combination_product\'). Guides procedure selection during submission planning based on the investigational product category.']
  application_type varchar [note: 'Type of regulatory application to which this procedure applies (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, ANDA, sNDA, sBLA, CTA). Links the procedure to the correct application lifecycle and dossier structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|ANDA|sNDA|sBLA|CTA|IND_amendment|NDA_505b2|PMA|510k — promote to reference product]']
  assessment_report_type varchar [note: 'Type of public assessment report published by the health authority upon completion of review under this procedure (e.g., EPAR for EMA Centralized Procedure, PAR for MHRA, Assessment Report for PMDA, Review Memo for FDA). Used for regulatory intelligence and post-approval transparency tracking.']
  clock_stop_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority may issue a clock stop (List of Outstanding Issues / Major Objection) that pauses the review clock under this procedure. True for EMA centralized procedure (Day 120/180 clock stops); False for FDA standard review. Critical for realistic timeline modeling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory procedure reference record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and ALCOA+ compliance requirements for regulated clinical research data.']
  ctd_modules_required varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of CTD modules required for a complete submission under this procedure (e.g., \'module1|module2|module3|module4|module5\' for a full NDA/MAA; \'module1|module2|module3\' for a CMC-only variation). Guides dossier completeness assessment and gap analysis.']
  data_exclusivity_period_years decimal(4,1) [note: 'Number of years of data exclusivity granted upon approval under this procedure (e.g., 5 years for FDA NCE exclusivity, 8 years data exclusivity + 2 years market exclusivity for EMA). Used in lifecycle management and competitive intelligence planning.']
  dossier_format varchar [note: 'Required dossier submission format for this procedure (e.g., eCTD v3.2.2, eCTD v4.0, NeeS, paper). Drives publishing tool configuration in Veeva Vault and determines the technical submission package structure.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this regulatory procedure definition became or becomes effective and applicable for new submissions. Used to determine which procedure version applies to a given submission based on its filing date.']
  electronic_submission_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether electronic submission (e.g., via FDA ESG, EMA CESP, or equivalent gateway) is mandatory for this procedure. Drives submission method configuration and Veeva Vault publishing workflow.']
  expedited_pathway_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an expedited or accelerated review pathway is available under this procedure (e.g., FDA Priority Review, Breakthrough Therapy, Accelerated Approval; EMA Accelerated Assessment, PRIME scheme). Drives regulatory strategy and timeline planning.']
  expedited_review_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days for expedited or priority review under this procedure (e.g., 180 days for FDA priority review NDA, 150 days for EMA accelerated assessment). Null if no expedited pathway exists for this procedure. Used for PDUFA goal date and accelerated timeline planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this regulatory procedure definition expires or is superseded. Null for currently active procedures with no defined end date. Used to enforce version-controlled procedure selection in submission planning.']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the submission fee associated with this procedure (e.g., USD for FDA, EUR for EMA, GBP for MHRA, JPY for PMDA). Null if no fee applies. Used in financial planning and budget forecasting for regulatory submissions.']
  guidance_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary agency guidance document or procedural guidance that describes this procedure in detail (e.g., FDA Guidance for Industry: Submitting an NDA, EMA Procedural Guidance for MAA, ICH M4 CTD Guidance). Used for regulatory operations training and compliance.']
  local_agent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a local regulatory agent or representative is required in the target jurisdiction to submit under this procedure (e.g., EU Qualified Person for Pharmacovigilance, Japan local agent for PMDA submissions). Impacts resourcing and regulatory operations planning.']
  market_exclusivity_period_years decimal(4,1) [note: 'Number of years of market exclusivity (protection from generic/biosimilar competition) granted upon approval under this procedure (e.g., 12 years for FDA biologic exclusivity, 10 years for EMA). Distinct from data exclusivity. Used in lifecycle and portfolio strategy planning.']
  mutual_recognition_agreement_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Mutual Recognition Agreement (MRA) between jurisdictions is applicable to this procedure, potentially enabling reliance on another agency\'s assessment to streamline review. Relevant for FDA-EMA, FDA-Health Canada, and similar bilateral agreements.']
  orphan_designation_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether orphan drug designation (FDA) or orphan medicinal product designation (EMA) can be applied in conjunction with this procedure, potentially conferring fee waivers, market exclusivity, and protocol assistance.']
  pediatric_plan_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Pediatric Investigation Plan (EMA PIP) or Pediatric Study Plan (FDA PSP) must be submitted or agreed upon as part of this procedure. Drives pediatric regulatory commitment tracking.']
  post_approval_variation_procedure varchar [note: 'Classification of the post-approval variation procedure applicable after initial approval under this procedure (e.g., EMA Type IA/IB/II Variation, FDA Prior Approval Supplement, Changes Being Effected CBE-30, CBE-0, Annual Report). Used to plan lifecycle management submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Type_IA|Type_IB|Type_II|Prior_Approval_Supplement|CBE30|CBE0|Annual_Report|None — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  pre_submission_meeting_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority offers a formal pre-submission meeting (e.g., FDA Type B Pre-NDA meeting, EMA Scientific Advice) for this procedure type. Used in regulatory strategy planning to schedule agency interactions before dossier filing.']
  procedural_requirements varchar [note: 'Free-text description of key procedural requirements, mandatory steps, and conditions that must be met to initiate and complete a submission under this procedure (e.g., pre-submission validation, mandatory scientific advice, specific data package requirements, language requirements). Supports regulatory operations planning.']
  procedure_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the regulatory procedure or pathway (e.g., EU-CENTRAL, FDA-NDA-STANDARD, FDA-NDA-PRIORITY, EMA-DCP, EMA-MRP, PMDA-NATIONAL). Used as the business key for cross-system reference and submission classification.']
  procedure_name varchar [note: 'Full human-readable name of the regulatory procedure or pathway (e.g., \'Centralized Procedure\', \'Decentralized Procedure\', \'Mutual Recognition Procedure\', \'National Procedure\', \'Abbreviated New Drug Application Procedure\'). Used in reports, dossier planning, and regulatory correspondence.']
  procedure_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this regulatory procedure definition. Active: currently applicable and in use for submission planning. Inactive: no longer accepted by the agency. Deprecated: superseded by a newer procedure. Under_revision: procedure definition is being updated.']
  procedure_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the regulatory procedure pathway. Centralized: single EMA review for all EU member states. Decentralized: simultaneous national applications. Mutual Recognition: extension of existing national approval. National: single country review. Abbreviated: generic/biosimilar pathway. Hybrid: combination of full and abbreviated data.']
  rapporteur_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Rapporteur and Co-Rapporteur must be appointed by the CHMP/CVMP for this procedure (applicable to EMA Centralized Procedure). The Rapporteur leads the scientific assessment and prepares the Assessment Report.']
  reference_member_state_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Reference Member State (RMS) must be designated for this procedure (applicable to EMA Decentralized Procedure and Mutual Recognition Procedure). The RMS leads the scientific assessment on behalf of all Concerned Member States.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Geographic regulatory region associated with this procedure (e.g., Europe, North America, Asia Pacific). Used for regional submission strategy planning, portfolio analytics, and regulatory intelligence reporting.']
  regulatory_framework_reference varchar [note: 'Primary legal or regulatory framework citation governing this procedure (e.g., \'21 CFR Part 314\' for FDA NDA, \'Regulation (EC) No 726/2004\' for EMA Centralized Procedure, \'EU CTR 536/2014\' for clinical trial authorization). Provides traceability to the authoritative regulatory source.']
  rems_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) or Risk Management Plan (RMP) may be required as a condition of approval under this procedure. Relevant for FDA REMS and EMA RMP requirements.']
  rolling_review_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the health authority permits rolling review (submission of completed CTD modules before the full dossier is ready) under this procedure. Applicable to FDA Rolling Review and EMA Rolling Review for medicines of major public health interest.']
  standard_fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard regulatory submission fee amount for this procedure in the currency specified by fee_currency_code (e.g., FDA NDA standard application fee per current PDUFA year). Used for regulatory budget planning and financial forecasting. Classified confidential as fee schedules may be commercially sensitive.']
  standard_review_clock_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days defined by the health authority for standard (non-expedited) review of a submission under this procedure (e.g., 365 days for FDA standard NDA review, 210 days for EMA centralized procedure). Used for target action date calculation and timeline planning.']
  submission_fee_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory submission fee is required for this procedure (e.g., FDA PDUFA user fee, EMA fee). Drives financial planning and fee waiver eligibility assessment for orphan or small business designations.']
  subtype varchar [note: 'Further classification within the procedure type (e.g., \'Type II Variation\', \'Type IB Variation\', \'Type IA Variation\', \'Line Extension\', \'New Active Substance\', \'Well-Established Use\', \'Fixed Combination\'). Enables granular submission planning and timeline estimation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: type_ia|type_ib|type_ii|line_extension|new_active_substance|well_established_use|fixed_combination|generic|biosimilar|hybrid — promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this regulatory procedure reference record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and ALCOA+ compliance for regulated clinical research data.']
  validation_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of the formal validation or completeness check criteria applied by the health authority upon receipt of a submission under this procedure (e.g., FDA 60-day filing review criteria, EMA Day 0 validation checklist). Used to prepare complete dossiers and avoid refuse-to-file actions.']
  veeva_vault_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for this regulatory procedure record in Veeva Vault (eTMF/regulatory submissions module). Enables traceability between the silver layer data product and the source system of record for regulatory document management and submission tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference data defining regulatory procedures and pathways available across health authorities. Captures procedure type (centralized, decentralized, mutual recognition, national, abbreviated, hybrid), applicable agency, procedure code, standard review timeline (days), expedited review timeline, applicable product types, and procedural requirements. Used to classify submissions and plan timelines.'
}

Table regulatory.submission_content_unit {
  submission_content_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual content unit (document, dataset, or study report) included within a CTD/eCTD submission sequence. Primary key for this entity.']
  application_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory application (IND/NDA/BLA/MAA) under which this content unit is submitted, supporting application-level dossier completeness tracking.']
  ctd_dossier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CTD/eCTD dossier binder that contains this content unit, enabling dossier-level aggregation and gap analysis.']
  primary_submission_veeva_vault_version_id varchar [note: 'Specific version identifier of the document in Veeva Vault at the time it was included in this submission sequence. Ensures the exact approved version is captured for audit and regulatory traceability.']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: eCTD submission content units (Module 5 clinical study reports, Module 2 summaries) reference specific protocol versions. Publishing and regulatory operations teams need direct FK for eCTD content tra']
  sdtm_dataset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to data.sdtm_dataset. Business justification: Regulatory publishers building CTD Module 5 map each submission content unit (e.g., dataset listing, define.xml) to a specific SDTM dataset. This traceability is required for submission QC and reviewe']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this content unit, particularly relevant for Module 5 clinical study reports, datasets, and study-level documents. Enables study-level dossier completeness tracking.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory submission sequence to which this content unit belongs. Links the content unit to its containing submission record.']
  submission_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission_sequence. Business justification: A submission content unit (document, dataset, study report) is included within a specific eCTD submission sequence. The submission_content_unit table currently stores sequence_number as a STRING denor']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Each submission content unit (eCTD leaf) corresponds to a TMF document. Regulatory publishing teams must trace each submitted document to its TMF record for inspection readiness and post-submission do']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this content unit received final regulatory document approval in Veeva Vault prior to inclusion in the submission. Supports audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  cdisc_standard varchar [note: 'CDISC data standard applicable to this content unit when the document type is a dataset or define file (e.g., SDTM for tabulation datasets, ADaM for analysis datasets, SEND for nonclinical datasets, define-xml for data definitions). Blank for non-dataset documents.']
  cdisc_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CDISC standard applied to this dataset content unit (e.g., SDTM 3.3, ADaM 1.3). Required for FDA and EMA dataset submissions to ensure conformance with the declared standard version.']
  checksum_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm used to generate the checksum value (e.g., MD5, SHA-256). Required for integrity verification during agency validation and archival processes.']
  checksum_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cryptographic hash value (MD5 or SHA-256) of the content unit file, as declared in the eCTD XML backbone. Used to verify file integrity during agency validation, transmission, and archival. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements.']
  content_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this content unit, such as rationale for document inclusion, known limitations, pending updates, or agency-specific instructions. Used by regulatory affairs teams for dossier management.']
  content_unit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this content unit within the submission sequence, typically assigned by the publishing tool (e.g., Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTD Manager) or Veeva Vault. Used for cross-referencing in cover letters and agency correspondence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this submission content unit record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  ctd_module varchar [note: 'Top-level CTD module number (1 through 5) to which this content unit belongs. Module 1 = Administrative/Regional; Module 2 = Summaries; Module 3 = Quality; Module 4 = Nonclinical; Module 5 = Clinical.']
  ctd_section_code varchar [note: 'Standardized CTD section reference code indicating the module and subsection where this content unit is placed (e.g., 2.7.3, 5.3.5.1, 3.2.P.1). Drives dossier structure validation and gap analysis against the ICH M4 table of contents.']
  document_date date [note: 'Official date of the content unit document as stated on the document itself (e.g., report date, approval date of the document). Distinct from submission date; used for document currency assessment and regulatory review.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this content unit within the regulatory dossier workflow. Tracks progression from authoring through agency submission and potential supersession. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|submitted|superseded|deleted|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the content unit as it appears in the eCTD leaf title element and the CTD table of contents. Used for dossier navigation and agency review.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the content unit by document category (e.g., Clinical Study Report, Investigator Brochure, Protocol, Statistical Analysis Plan, Dataset, Labeling, Module 2 Summary). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_study_report|investigator_brochure|protocol|statistical_analysis_plan|dataset|labeling|module_2_summary|nonclinical_study_report|quality_document|correspondence — promote to reference product]']
  dtd_version varchar [note: 'Version of the eCTD Document Type Definition (DTD) or XML schema used for the backbone of the submission sequence containing this content unit (e.g., 3.2.2, 4.0). Determines structural validation rules applied by agency gateway.']
  ectd_operation varchar [note: 'eCTD leaf-level lifecycle operation applied to this content unit in the current submission sequence. \'new\' = first submission of this document; \'replace\' = supersedes a prior version; \'append\' = adds to existing content; \'delete\' = removes a previously submitted document.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Electronic file format of the content unit (e.g., PDF for narrative documents, XPT for CDISC SDTM/ADaM datasets, XML for eCTD backbone). Must comply with agency-specific technical specifications for electronic submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pdf|xml|sas7bdat|xpt|docx|xlsx|txt|html — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_name varchar [note: 'Actual file name of the content unit as stored in the eCTD submission package (e.g., csr-study-001-v1.pdf). Must conform to eCTD file naming conventions (lowercase, no spaces, hyphen-delimited).']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Relative file path of the content unit within the eCTD submission folder structure (e.g., m5/53-clin-stud-rep/535-rep-effic-safety-stud/5351-stud-rep-uncontrol/). Used for dossier navigation and publishing validation.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the content unit file in bytes. Used for submission package size validation, agency gateway transmission compliance, and storage management. Agencies impose maximum file size limits for electronic submissions.']
  gap_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content unit has been identified as a gap (missing or incomplete) during dossier gap analysis. When True, the CTD section requires a new or updated document before submission can proceed.']
  included_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this content unit was formally included in the submission sequence package, as recorded by the publishing tool or Veeva Vault. Represents the principal business event time for this transaction line.']
  is_bookmarked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PDF content unit contains required PDF bookmarks as mandated by agency technical specifications. Bookmark compliance is validated during eCTD technical review.']
  is_hyperlinked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this content unit contains internal eCTD hyperlinks to other documents within the dossier. Hyperlink integrity is a key validation criterion for FDA and EMA submissions.']
  is_searchable_pdf boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this PDF content unit is text-searchable (OCR-processed or natively digital), as required by FDA and EMA for narrative documents submitted in PDF format.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the content unit (e.g., en, ja, de, fr). Required for multi-regional submissions where local language documents are mandated by agencies such as PMDA (Japanese) or Health Canada (French/English).']
  leaf_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique XML leaf element identifier assigned to this content unit within the eCTD backbone XML file. Used for cross-referencing between eCTD sequences and for lifecycle operations (replace/delete) that reference prior leaf IDs.']
  prior_leaf_identifier varchar [note: 'Leaf ID of the previously submitted content unit that this document replaces, appends, or deletes. Required for eCTD lifecycle operations to maintain document lineage across submission sequences.']
  publishing_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the eCTD publishing software used to compile and validate this content unit within the submission package (e.g., Lorenz docuBridge, Extedo eCTD Manager, Certus). Required for technical conformance documentation.']
  publishing_tool_version varchar [note: 'Version of the eCTD publishing software used to compile this content unit. Tracked for technical conformance and reproducibility of the submission package.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the regulatory affairs reviewer who performed quality check on this content unit prior to submission. Supports QC accountability and audit trail requirements.']
  target_agency_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the health authority for which this content unit is specifically prepared or applicable (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA). Some documents are agency-specific (e.g., Module 1 administrative documents differ by region). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|HC|TGA|ANVISA|NMPA — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this submission content unit record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data integrity auditing.']
  validation_error_message varchar [note: 'Descriptive error or warning message returned by the eCTD validation tool or agency gateway when validation_status is \'failed\' or \'warning\'. Used for remediation and resubmission planning.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the eCTD technical validation check performed on this content unit prior to or upon agency receipt. Indicates whether the file passed agency gateway validation criteria (e.g., FDA ECTD Validation Criteria, EMA validation rules).']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier in Veeva Vault RIM (Regulatory Information Management) system, which serves as the system of record for regulatory document management. Enables direct traceability from the dossier content unit back to the source document in Veeva Vault.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual content units (documents, datasets, study reports) included within a CTD/eCTD submission sequence. Captures CTD section reference (e.g., Module 2.7.3, Module 5.3.5.1), document title, document type, document version, file format, file size, checksum, lifecycle operation (new/replace/append/delete), and Veeva Vault document ID. Enables granular dossier content management and gap analysis.'
}

Table document.tmf_document {
  tmf_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each logical TMF document record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. This is the primary key for the tmf_document entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) to which this TMF document belongs. A TMF is organized per study per ICH GCP requirements.']
  tmf_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_artifact. Business justification: Every TMF document is classified under a specific DIA TMF Reference Model artifact. The tmf_artifact STRING column in tmf_document is a denormalized reference to the tmf_artifact taxonomy table. Repla']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was formally approved by the authorized approver(s) within the document control workflow in Veeva Vault. Distinct from the effective date and the version date.']
  archive_date date [note: 'The date on which this document was formally transferred to the long-term archive. Null if the document has not yet been archived.']
  archive_location varchar [note: 'The physical or electronic location identifier where the archived document is stored (e.g., archive facility name, vault path, or storage reference code). Required for document retrieval during regulatory inspections.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, filing instructions, quality review findings, or other contextual remarks associated with this document record. Not part of the document content itself.']
  confidentiality_classification varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this document per the organization\'s information security policy. Restricted = highest sensitivity (e.g., patient data, proprietary formulations); Confidential = sponsor/CRO sensitive; Internal = internal use only; Public = publicly disclosable.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the country for which this document is applicable (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'JPN\'). Relevant for country-specific regulatory submissions, IRB/IEC approvals, and site-level documents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this TMF document record was first created in the source system (Veeva Vault). Represents the audit trail creation event per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ requirements.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known, human-readable unique document identifier assigned within the eTMF or document control system (e.g., DOC-2024-00123). Used for cross-referencing in regulatory submissions and inspections.']
  document_origin varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating party or organization type that produced this document (e.g., Sponsor, CRO, Site, Regulatory Authority, Laboratory, Vendor). Used for document provenance tracking and TMF completeness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sponsor|cro|site|regulatory_authority|laboratory|vendor|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  document_type varchar [note: 'The functional category of the document as defined by the DIA TMF Reference Model artifact taxonomy (e.g., Protocol, Informed Consent Form, Investigator Brochure, Site Initiation Visit Report, SAE Report). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Protocol|Informed Consent Form|Investigator Brochure|Monitoring Visit Report|SAE Report|Regulatory Approval|Laboratory Certificate|Financial Disclosure|Training Record|Other — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this document version is considered operative and binding for the study. For regulatory documents, this is the date the document enters force (e.g., protocol amendment effective date, IB effective date).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this document expires or is no longer valid (e.g., IRB/IEC approval expiry, laboratory accreditation certificate expiry, financial disclosure expiry). Null for documents with no defined expiry.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'The electronic file format of the document (e.g., PDF, DOCX, XLSX, XML). PDF is the standard for finalized TMF documents per eCTD and regulatory submission requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|XML|CSV|TXT|JPG|PNG|other — promote to reference product]']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current filing completeness status of this document against its expected TMF artifact placeholder. Expected = document is anticipated but not yet received; Filed = document has been received and filed; Overdue = document is past its expected filing date; Not Applicable = artifact does not apply to this study/site; Placeholder = temporary placeholder filed.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document has been formally transferred to long-term archival storage per the study close-out and archival process. Archived documents are read-only and subject to retention period controls.']
  is_essential_document boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is classified as an essential document per ICH E6(R2) GCP Section 8 (Trial Master File). Essential documents are those that individually and collectively permit evaluation of the conduct of a trial and the quality of the data produced.']
