  is_gcp_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document is mandated by ICH GCP guidelines as a required TMF document. Used for TMF completeness assessment and inspection readiness reporting.']
  is_inspection_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this document has been reviewed and confirmed as inspection-ready (complete, legible, attributable, and filed correctly per ALCOA+ principles). Used for regulatory inspection readiness dashboards.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) indicating the language in which the document is written (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'de\', \'ja\', \'zh-CN\'). Required for multi-country studies with translated documents.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the document within its controlled lifecycle as managed in Veeva Vault. Drives document control workflows: draft → in_review → approved → effective → superseded/archived. Superseded indicates a newer version exists; archived indicates end-of-life retention. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|effective|superseded|archived|obsolete — promote to reference product]']
  page_count varchar [note: 'The total number of pages in the document. Used for document completeness verification, quality control checks, and archival metadata.']
  quality_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent quality control review of this document was completed. Used for tracking QC cycle times and scheduling periodic re-reviews.']
  quality_review_status varchar [note: 'The outcome of the quality control review performed on this document to verify completeness, accuracy, and compliance with ALCOA+ principles and GCP requirements. Drives inspection readiness and CAPA workflows.']
  quality_reviewer varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the individual who performed the most recent quality control review of this document. Supports audit trail requirements under GCP and 21 CFR Part 11.']
  retention_end_date date [note: 'The calculated date on which the mandatory retention period for this document expires, after which the document may be eligible for destruction per the approved destruction authorization process.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'The mandatory retention period in years for this document as required by applicable regulations and sponsor policy (e.g., 15 years post-study completion per ICH E6(R2), or as specified by the sponsor). Drives archival scheduling and destruction authorization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this document record originates (e.g., \'Veeva Vault\', \'Medidata Rave\', \'MasterControl\'). Supports data lineage and multi-system TMF integration scenarios.']
  sponsor_document_reference varchar [note: 'The sponsor\'s own document reference number or identifier for this document, as assigned by the pharmaceutical or biotech sponsor. Used for cross-referencing between CRO eTMF and sponsor document management systems.']
  title varchar [note: 'The full descriptive title of the TMF document as it appears in the eTMF system (e.g., \'Protocol Amendment 2 - Study XYZ-001\'). Primary human-readable identifier for the document.']
  tmf_document_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the document\'s content, purpose, or scope. Provides additional context beyond the document title for search, classification, and review purposes.']
  tmf_section varchar [note: 'The DIA TMF Reference Model Section code within the assigned Zone (e.g., \'01.01\', \'03.02\'). Provides the second level of the TMF hierarchical classification for document filing and retrieval.']
  tmf_zone varchar [note: 'The DIA TMF Reference Model Zone classification for this document. Zone 01 = Trial Management; Zone 02 = Central Trial Documents; Zone 03 = Regulatory; Zone 04 = IRB/IEC and Other Approvals; Zone 05 = Site Management; Zone 06 = IP and Trial Supplies.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this TMF document record was most recently modified in the source system. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail completeness.']
  vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The native document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault (eTMF system of record). Used to trace the Silver Layer record back to its source system object for reconciliation and lineage purposes.']
  vault_library varchar [note: 'The Veeva Vault library or vault name where this document is stored (e.g., \'eTMF Vault\', \'RIM Vault\', \'QMS Vault\'). Relevant for organizations using multiple Vault instances across eTMF, regulatory, and quality domains.']
  version_date date [note: 'The date on which the current version of the document was authored or formally dated, as stated on the document itself. Distinct from the approval date or effective date.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The current approved version label of this logical document (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\', \'3.0\'). Represents the version of the document as a business entity; full version history is tracked in the document_version product.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing an essential document within the Trial Master File (TMF/eTMF) as defined by the DIA TMF Reference Model. Captures document identity, TMF zone/section/artifact classification, title, version, lifecycle status (draft, approved, effective, superseded, archived), document type, language, originating system (Veeva Vault), confidentiality classification, retention period, and GCP compliance flags. SSOT for all TMF essential documents. Each record represents one logical document (not a version — see document_version for version history).'
}

Table document.tmf_artifact {
  tmf_artifact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each TMF artifact taxonomy record in the DIA TMF Reference Model. This is the primary key for the tmf_artifact reference entity. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a reference/taxonomy table defining the DIA TMF Reference Model artifact classification hierarchy; per-role minimums for MASTER_RESOURCE apply as the closest applicable role given its identity, classification, and lifecycle attributes.]']
  applicability_phase varchar [note: 'Clinical trial phase(s) for which this artifact is applicable per the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., \'Phase I\', \'Phase II\', \'Phase III\', \'Phase IV\', \'All Phases\', \'Phase I/II\'). Enables phase-specific TMF completeness assessments and filing guidance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Phase I|Phase II|Phase III|Phase IV|Phase I/II|Phase II/III|All Phases — promote to reference product]']
  archival_trigger varchar [note: 'The regulatory or business event that triggers the start of the retention period countdown for this artifact type (e.g., \'Database Lock\', \'Marketing Authorization Approval\', \'Study Termination\', \'Last Patient Last Visit\'). Used in archival scheduling and document lifecycle management within the eTMF system.']
  artifact_abbreviation varchar [note: 'Short-form abbreviation or acronym for the artifact type as used in the DIA TMF Reference Model and industry practice (e.g., \'IB\' for Investigator Brochure, \'ICF\' for Informed Consent Form, \'SAP\' for Statistical Analysis Plan). Used in compact reporting views and eTMF system labels.']
  artifact_code varchar [note: 'Standardized DIA TMF Reference Model artifact code in Zone.Section.Artifact format (e.g., 01.01.01 for Zone 1, Section 1, Artifact 1 — Protocol). Serves as the externally-recognized business identifier for the artifact type across sponsors, CROs, and regulatory agencies.']
  artifact_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the artifact type, its purpose within the TMF, and the business context in which it is generated or collected. Provides filing guidance to Clinical Research Associates (CRAs), Clinical Research Coordinators (CRCs), and TMF managers on what constitutes an acceptable document for this artifact slot.']
  artifact_name varchar [note: 'Official DIA TMF Reference Model name for the artifact type (e.g., \'Protocol\', \'Investigator Brochure\', \'Informed Consent Form\'). This is the human-readable identity label used in TMF completeness reports, inspection readiness dashboards, and eTMF system configuration.']
  artifact_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric identifier of the artifact within its section per the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., Artifact 01 within Section 1.1). Provides the third and most granular level of the TMF hierarchy. Used in combination with zone_number and section_number to uniquely locate a document type in the TMF structure.']
  artifact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this artifact taxonomy record within the DIA TMF Reference Model (Active = currently in use, Deprecated = removed from the model, Superseded = replaced by a newer artifact definition, Draft = under review for inclusion). Ensures that TMF completeness assessments use only current, valid artifact definitions.']
  cdisc_controlled_term varchar [note: 'The corresponding CDISC controlled terminology code or term for this artifact type where applicable (e.g., CDISC TMF controlled terminology). Supports CDISC-compliant data submissions and cross-system interoperability between the eTMF and EDC/SDTM data packages submitted to regulatory agencies.']
  completeness_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this artifact type for TMF completeness index calculation (expressed as a percentage or score contribution). Required artifacts typically carry higher weights than Recommended or Optional. Used in the TMF completeness KPI dashboard to produce a weighted completeness score for inspection readiness reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this artifact taxonomy record was first created in the data platform. Provides the audit trail creation timestamp per ALCOA+ principles and supports data lineage tracking for the TMF reference taxonomy.']
  dia_reference_model_version varchar [note: 'Version of the DIA TMF Reference Model from which this artifact definition is sourced (e.g., \'v3.2\', \'v3.1\'). Enables version-controlled TMF taxonomy management and supports migration assessments when the DIA releases updated Reference Model versions. Critical for audit trail and regulatory inspection documentation.']
  document_owner_role varchar [note: 'Standard organizational role responsible for generating, maintaining, and filing documents of this artifact type (e.g., \'Sponsor\', \'Principal Investigator\', \'CRA\', \'CRC\', \'Biostatistician\', \'Regulatory Affairs\'). Used to assign TMF filing responsibilities and drive workflow routing in the eTMF system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this artifact definition became effective and applicable for use in TMF filing and completeness assessments. Aligns with the release date of the DIA TMF Reference Model version referenced by dia_reference_model_version. Supports temporal validity management of the artifact taxonomy.']
  expected_timing varchar [note: 'Describes the expected timing for filing this artifact relative to trial milestones (e.g., \'Before First Patient First Visit\', \'Within 30 days of site initiation\', \'At study close-out\', \'Ongoing throughout trial\'). Used to assess timeliness of TMF filing and flag overdue documents in inspection readiness dashboards.']
  filing_guidance varchar [note: 'Operational instructions for TMF staff on how, when, and where to file documents of this artifact type within the eTMF system (Veeva Vault). Includes guidance on acceptable document formats, naming conventions, version control expectations, and timing relative to trial milestones. Supports ALCOA+ compliance and inspection readiness.']
  ich_gcp_reference varchar [note: 'Specific ICH E6(R2) GCP guideline section(s) that mandate or reference this artifact type (e.g., \'ICH E6(R2) Section 8.2.1\', \'ICH E6(R2) Section 8.3.6\'). Provides the regulatory basis for the artifact\'s required status and supports inspection readiness documentation and regulatory query responses.']
  is_country_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this artifact type requires a separate instance per country in which the trial is conducted (True) or is filed at the global trial level (False). Drives country-level TMF completeness calculations and supports multi-jurisdictional regulatory inspection readiness.']
  is_essential_document boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this artifact type is classified as an Essential Document per ICH E6(R2) Section 8 (True). Essential documents are those that individually and collectively permit evaluation of the conduct of a trial and the quality of the data produced. This flag drives the core TMF completeness index calculation.']
  is_inspection_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this artifact type is considered critical during regulatory inspections by FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, or other health authorities (True). Inspection-critical artifacts are prioritized in pre-inspection TMF readiness reviews and gap remediation activities. Absence of inspection-critical artifacts typically results in a major GCP finding.']
  is_investigator_held boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this artifact type is held in the Investigator Site File (ISF) at the investigational site (True). Supports site file completeness assessments during monitoring visits (SDV/SIV/COV) and regulatory inspections. CRAs use this flag to verify site file completeness against the DIA TMF Reference Model.']
  is_site_specific boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this artifact type requires a separate instance per investigational site (True) or is filed once at the trial or country level (False). Drives the expected document count calculation in TMF completeness metrics — site-specific artifacts multiply by the number of active sites.']
  is_sponsor_held boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this artifact type is held in the Sponsor\'s TMF (True) as opposed to the Investigator Site File (ISF) or a shared location. Supports TMF structure design, responsibility allocation between sponsor and investigator, and completeness tracking across the distributed TMF.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional clarifications, implementation guidance, or sponsor-specific customizations applied to this artifact definition. May include notes on jurisdictional variations, therapeutic area-specific applicability, or deviations from the standard DIA TMF Reference Model definition as agreed with the sponsor.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Additional regulatory citations beyond ICH GCP that reference or mandate this artifact type, including FDA 21 CFR Part 312, EU CTR 536/2014, EMA GCP Guideline, MHRA GCP Guidance, or PMDA requirements. Supports multi-jurisdictional TMF compliance and inspection readiness for global trials.']
  requirement_classification varchar [note: 'DIA TMF Reference Model classification indicating whether this artifact is Required (mandatory per ICH GCP), Recommended (best practice), or Optional (situational) for a compliant TMF. Drives TMF completeness scoring, gap analysis, and inspection readiness assessments. Required artifacts missing from the TMF constitute a GCP finding.']
  requires_electronic_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether documents of this artifact type require a compliant electronic signature per 21 CFR Part 11 or EU eIDAS standards (True). Drives e-signature workflow configuration in Veeva Vault eTMF and ensures compliance with electronic records and signatures regulations.']
  requires_wet_signature boolean [note: 'Indicates whether documents of this artifact type require a handwritten (wet) signature as opposed to an electronic signature (True). Relevant for jurisdictions or document types where electronic signatures are not accepted. Drives document collection workflows and compliance checks in the eTMF system.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Minimum regulatory retention period in years for documents of this artifact type after trial completion or product approval/discontinuation. Drives archival scheduling and destruction authorization workflows. Retention requirements vary by jurisdiction (e.g., FDA requires 2 years post-NDA approval; ICH GCP requires 15 years for certain documents).']
  retirement_date date [note: 'The date on which this artifact definition was retired, deprecated, or superseded and is no longer applicable for new TMF filings. Null for currently active artifact definitions. Used to enforce temporal validity in TMF completeness calculations and prevent filing against obsolete artifact types.']
  section_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the TMF section within its zone per the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., \'Trial Oversight\', \'Central Monitoring\', \'Protocol and Amendments\'). Used in TMF index navigation, filing guidance, and inspection readiness dashboards.']
  section_number varchar [note: 'Numeric section identifier within the TMF zone per the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., \'1.1\' for Trial Management — Trial Oversight). Provides the second level of the three-tier TMF hierarchy (Zone > Section > Artifact) used for document filing and completeness assessment.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'The artifact_code of the newer artifact definition that supersedes this record when artifact_status is \'Superseded\'. Enables forward navigation in the artifact taxonomy version history and supports TMF system migration mapping when DIA Reference Model versions are updated.']
  tmf_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the organizational level at which this artifact is filed within the TMF structure: Trial-level (applies to the entire study), Country-level (applies to a specific country), or Site-level (applies to an individual investigational site). Critical for multi-site, multi-country trial TMF organization and completeness tracking per DIA TMF Reference Model.']
  trial_phase_end varchar [note: 'The trial lifecycle phase by which this artifact should be finalized and filed (Pre-Trial, Start-Up, Conduct, Close-Out, Post-Trial). Defines the closing boundary of the filing window for this artifact type and supports overdue document identification in TMF completeness tracking.']
  trial_phase_start varchar [note: 'The trial lifecycle phase during which this artifact is first expected to be filed (Pre-Trial, Start-Up, Conduct, Close-Out, Post-Trial). Used in conjunction with trial_phase_end to define the filing window for this artifact type and to drive milestone-based TMF completeness assessments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this artifact taxonomy record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail requirements, and incremental data pipeline processing for the TMF reference taxonomy. Required for ALCOA+ compliance.']
  veeva_vault_document_subtype varchar [note: 'The corresponding document subtype classification as configured in Veeva Vault eTMF. Provides the second level of the Veeva Vault document type hierarchy, enabling precise mapping from DIA TMF Reference Model artifacts to the eTMF system\'s document classification taxonomy for automated filing and completeness calculations.']
  veeva_vault_document_type varchar [note: 'The corresponding document type classification as configured in Veeva Vault eTMF, the operational system of record for TMF management. Enables direct mapping between the DIA TMF Reference Model taxonomy and the Veeva Vault document type hierarchy for automated TMF completeness tracking and filing workflow routing.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the TMF zone as defined in the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., \'Trial Management\', \'Central Trial Documents\', \'Regulatory\', \'IRB/IEC and Other Approvals\', \'Site Management\', \'Investigational Product\', \'Safety Reporting\'). Used in TMF index views and inspection readiness reports.']
  zone_number varchar [note: 'Numeric identifier of the TMF zone to which this artifact belongs per the DIA TMF Reference Model (e.g., Zone 1 = Trial Management, Zone 2 = Central Trial Documents, Zone 3 = Regulatory, Zone 4 = IRB/IEC and Other Approvals, Zone 5 = Site Management, Zone 6 = Investigational Product, Zone 7 = Safety Reporting). Drives TMF structural organization and completeness tracking.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining the DIA TMF Reference Model artifact taxonomy — the standardized classification of document types by Zone, Section, and Artifact number (e.g., Zone 1 Section 1.1 Artifact 01 - Protocol). Stores artifact code, artifact name, zone number, zone name, section number, section name, required/recommended/optional classification, applicable trial phase, and ICH GCP reference. Drives TMF completeness metrics and inspection readiness assessments.'
}

Table document.version {
  version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each discrete version record of a controlled document in the Trial Master File (TMF) or Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) library. Primary key of this table.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: The version table tracks version history for ALL controlled documents in the TMF and SOP library, yet currently only has tmf_document_id. SOPs (document_sop) are controlled documents that undergo vers']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent document record for which this version was created. All versions of the same document share this identifier, enabling full version lineage reconstruction.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this document version received final approval and was authorized for use. Null if the version has not yet been approved. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 and EU CTR audit trail requirements.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who granted final approval for this document version. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail and GCP inspection readiness.']
  archival_status varchar [note: 'The archival lifecycle state of this document version. \'active\' = in current use; \'archived\' = moved to long-term archival storage per ICH GCP retention requirements; \'pending_archival\' = scheduled for archival; \'destroyed\' = permanently deleted per retention schedule.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who authored this document version. Captured as part of the ALCOA+ attributable audit trail. Typically a CRA, CRC, Regulatory Affairs specialist, or Biostatistician depending on document type.']
  authored_date date [note: 'The date on which authorship of this document version was completed and the document was submitted for review. Part of the ALCOA+ audit trail for controlled document lifecycle.']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'The CAPA identifier from MasterControl that triggered or is associated with this document version update. Null if the version was not CAPA-driven. Enables traceability between quality events and document changes.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'The documented business or regulatory reason that triggered the creation of this new version (e.g., protocol amendment, regulatory agency feedback, CAPA-driven update, periodic review). Supports change control traceability.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'A concise narrative description of the changes made in this version relative to the immediately preceding version. Required for controlled document change control and regulatory inspection readiness. For initial versions, this field captures the document\'s original purpose.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the nature of changes in this version. \'major\' = substantive content changes requiring re-training; \'minor\' = non-substantive content updates; \'administrative\' = formatting, typo, or metadata corrections only; \'initial\' = first version of the document.']
  checksum_hash varchar [note: 'The SHA-256 cryptographic hash of the document file content at the time of this version\'s creation. Used to verify document integrity and detect unauthorized alterations, supporting 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and ALCOA+ integrity requirements.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'The data classification level assigned to this document version, governing access controls and distribution restrictions within Veeva Vault. Aligns with the organization\'s data classification policy and GDPR/HIPAA requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the regulatory jurisdiction for which this document version was prepared or filed (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, JPN). Null for globally applicable documents.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this document version record was first created in the source system (Veeva Vault). Part of the ALCOA+ contemporaneous audit trail. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known controlled document number or identifier (e.g., SOP-CLN-0042, TMF-PROT-001). Serves as the business-facing identifier used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-references within the Trial Master File.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'The full official title of the document as it appears on the controlled document itself. Used for display, search, and regulatory binder indexing within the eTMF.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document by its functional type within the TMF or SOP library (e.g., Protocol, Informed Consent Form, Investigator Brochure, SOP, Regulatory Submission, Site Agreement, Safety Report). Aligns with DIA TMF Reference Model artifact taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this document version becomes officially effective and binding for use. For SOPs, this is the date from which staff must comply with the procedure. For regulatory documents, this is the date of regulatory approval or acknowledgement.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this document version expires and must be reviewed, renewed, or superseded. Null indicates no scheduled expiry. Critical for SOP periodic review cycles and regulatory document validity tracking.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'The electronic file format of the document version as stored in Veeva Vault (e.g., PDF, DOCX, XLSX, XML). PDF is the standard for approved controlled documents per eCTD and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PDF|DOCX|XLSX|XML|SAS|RTF|HTML — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_size_kb varchar [note: 'The size of the document version file in kilobytes as stored in Veeva Vault. Used for storage management, eTMF completeness validation, and detecting potentially corrupt or empty document uploads.']
  inspection_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this document version is ready for regulatory inspection. \'ready\' = document is complete, approved, and filed correctly; \'gap_identified\' = a filing or completeness gap has been identified; \'remediation_in_progress\' = gap is being addressed; \'not_applicable\' = document not subject to inspection.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 639-1 language code of the document version (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'de\', \'ja\'). Critical for multi-country trials where documents must be translated and filed in local languages per regulatory requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp of the most recent modification to this document version record in the source system. Supports audit trail completeness and change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority to which this document version is relevant or was submitted (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, TGA, Health Canada). Supports regulatory submission tracking and jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada|WHO|Other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which this document version may be destroyed per the applicable regulatory retention schedule. Calculated based on study completion date plus the required retention period (e.g., 15 years post-study for ICH GCP, 2 years post-NDA approval for FDA).']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality reviewer or subject matter expert who reviewed this document version prior to approval. Supports the QA review step in the controlled document workflow.']
  study_protocol_number varchar [note: 'The sponsor-assigned protocol number of the clinical study to which this document version is associated, if applicable. Null for enterprise-level SOPs not tied to a specific study. Enables study-level TMF completeness reporting.']
  superseded_date date [note: 'The date on which this document version was superseded by a newer approved version. Null if the version is still current or was never approved. Used to determine the active version at any point in time for historical audit queries.']
  tmf_section varchar [note: 'The specific section or artifact number within the DIA TMF Reference Model zone (e.g., 01.01, 02.03, 05.07). Used for precise eTMF filing, completeness tracking, and inspection readiness reporting.']
  tmf_zone varchar [note: 'The DIA TMF Reference Model zone to which this document belongs. Zone 1 = Trial Management; Zone 2 = Central Trial Documents; Zone 3 = Regulatory; Zone 4 = Investigational Product; Zone 5 = Site Management. Drives eTMF filing structure and inspection readiness classification.']
  training_completion_deadline date [note: 'The date by which all required staff must complete training on this document version. Null if training is not required. Used to monitor training compliance and flag overdue training in MasterControl.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff training is required upon approval of this document version. True for major version changes to SOPs and GCP-critical documents. Drives training assignment workflows in MasterControl.']
  vault_document_version_reference varchar [note: 'The native document version identifier assigned by Veeva Vault (eTMF/QMS module). Used to trace this Silver Layer record back to its source system record for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  vault_lifecycle_state varchar [note: 'The native lifecycle state label as recorded in Veeva Vault for this document version (e.g., \'Draft\', \'In Review\', \'Approved\', \'Superseded\'). Preserved from the source system to enable reconciliation between the Silver Layer and Veeva Vault.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The human-readable version label for this document version (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0). Follows the organization\'s controlled document versioning convention. Major increments typically indicate substantive content changes; minor increments indicate administrative or editorial changes.']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of this document version within the controlled document workflow. \'draft\' = under authorship; \'in_review\' = circulated for review/approval; \'approved\' = effective and in use; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer approved version; \'obsolete\' = retired from use; \'withdrawn\' = removed prior to approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|superseded|obsolete|withdrawn — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks the full version history of every controlled document in the TMF and SOP library. Each record represents a discrete version of a document, capturing version number, effective date, expiry date, version status (draft, in-review, approved, superseded, obsolete), change summary, author, approver, and the Veeva Vault document version ID. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 and EU CTR audit trail requirements for controlled document versioning.'
}

Table document.document_sop {
  document_sop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SOP record in the controlled document library. Primary key for the document_sop entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  tmf_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_artifact. Business justification: SOPs in the controlled document library are classified under the DIA TMF Reference Model artifact taxonomy. document_sop has a tmf_reference_model_section string attribute that stores a free-text refe']
  applicable_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) or jurisdiction(s) for which this SOP is applicable (e.g., \'USA\', \'EU\', \'GLOBAL\', \'JPN\'). Supports multi-regional regulatory compliance and site-level applicability determination.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the SOP version received final authorized approval from the designated approver(s) in the document management workflow. Distinct from effective date; approval may precede effectivity.']
  approver varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the authorized individual who provided final approval for the current SOP version. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  author varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the individual who drafted or last revised the SOP content. Supports ALCOA+ attributability requirements for controlled documents.']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the CAPA that triggered the creation or revision of this SOP, if applicable. Links document changes to quality events and supports CAPA effectiveness verification.']
  change_summary varchar [note: 'Narrative summary of the key changes made in this version compared to the previous version. Supports change control documentation and enables reviewers to quickly identify what was modified.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the SOP document indicating its sensitivity and access control requirements (e.g., internal, confidential). Governs who may access, distribute, or reference the document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SOP record was first created in the controlled document management system (MasterControl). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements for contemporaneous documentation.']
  deviation_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP version was created or revised as a direct result of a protocol deviation or quality event. When True, the capa_reference field should be populated. Supports quality trend analysis.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the controlled SOP document as stored in MasterControl (e.g., pdf, word). PDF is the standard format for approved/effective SOPs to prevent unauthorized modification per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  document_location_url varchar [note: 'URL or system path to the controlled SOP document in MasterControl or the designated document management repository. Enables direct navigation to the source document for review and compliance verification.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the current approved version of the SOP becomes mandatory for use across the organization. Staff must comply with this version from this date forward. Aligns with EFFECTIVE_FROM category.']
  electronic_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SOP approval and acknowledgment workflow requires electronic signatures compliant with 21 CFR Part 11. When True, all approvals and training acknowledgments must be captured as validated e-signatures.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the SOP is scheduled to expire and must be reviewed, revised, or retired. Typically set at a fixed review cycle (e.g., 2–3 years from effective date). Aligns with EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category.']
  gcp_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP is directly applicable to GCP-regulated clinical trial activities. When True, the SOP is subject to GCP inspection and must comply with ICH E6(R2) requirements.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP governs activities subject to GDPR requirements for patient data privacy and protection. Relevant for SOPs covering data collection, consent, data transfer, and subject data management.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP governs activities subject to HIPAA requirements for protected health information (PHI) in U.S.-based clinical trial operations.']
  inspection_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP has been verified as inspection-ready, meaning it is current, approved, accessible, and all required training records are complete. Supports regulatory inspection preparedness tracking.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1/639-2 language code of the SOP document (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'de\', \'ja\'). Supports multi-language SOP libraries for global clinical trial operations across multiple regulatory jurisdictions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the SOP record in the document management system. Supports audit trail integrity and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ requirements.']
  mastercontrol_doc_reference varchar [note: 'Native document identifier assigned by MasterControl (the system of record for controlled documents). Used for cross-system traceability and integration with MasterControl training, CAPA, and audit modules.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'Specific business process area governed by this SOP within its category (e.g., \'Informed Consent Management\', \'SAE Reporting\', \'Database Lock\', \'Site Initiation Visit\'). Enables granular process-level filtering and gap analysis.']
  purpose varchar [note: 'Brief narrative describing the business purpose and objective of the SOP — why it exists and what regulatory or operational requirement it fulfills. Sourced from the SOP document\'s purpose/objective section.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of regulatory frameworks and standards to which this SOP applies (e.g., \'FDA 21 CFR Part 312, ICH E6(R2), EU CTR 536/2014, GCP\'). Supports regulatory compliance mapping and inspection readiness.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Defined periodic review interval in months for this SOP (e.g., 24 or 36 months). Determines the frequency at which the SOP must be reviewed for continued accuracy and regulatory alignment. Drives review_due_date calculation.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the SOP must undergo its next periodic review to confirm continued applicability and accuracy. Drives inspection readiness and document control compliance.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope of the SOP, defining which activities, processes, personnel, systems, or organizational units are covered. Typically drawn directly from the SOP document\'s scope section.']
  sop_category varchar [note: 'Functional category classifying the SOP by the business domain it governs (e.g., clinical_operations, data_management, safety). Enables domain-based filtering and reporting. Aligns with CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_operations|data_management|safety|regulatory|quality|laboratory|finance|supply_chain|it — promote to reference product]']
  sop_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, organization-assigned unique document number for the SOP (e.g., QA-SOP-00123). Serves as the business identifier used in regulatory submissions, audit trails, and cross-references. Aligns with BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category for MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  sop_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SOP within the controlled document management workflow. Governs whether the SOP is actionable for clinical trial operations. Aligns with LIFECYCLE_STATUS category for MASTER_RESOURCE role. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|effective|superseded|retired|obsolete — promote to reference product]']
  sop_type varchar [note: 'Type of controlled document within the quality management system hierarchy. Distinguishes SOPs from work instructions, policies, guidance documents, templates, and forms. Supports document hierarchy management.']
  superseded_sop_number varchar [note: 'SOP number of the prior version or predecessor document that this SOP replaces. Enables document lineage tracing and ensures retired versions are properly cross-referenced. Null if this is the inaugural version.']
  superseded_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the specific SOP version that this current version supersedes. Complements superseded_sop_number to fully identify the predecessor document version.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the SOP as it appears on the controlled document (e.g., \'Adverse Event Reporting and Expedited Notification Procedure\'). Serves as the human-readable IDENTITY_LABEL for the MASTER_RESOURCE role.']
  training_completion_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from the SOP effective date within which all applicable personnel must complete the required training. Drives training assignment deadlines in MasterControl.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff must complete mandatory training on this SOP before performing the governed activities. When True, a training record must be created in MasterControl for all applicable personnel. Supports GCP training compliance.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Current approved version of the SOP in major.minor format (e.g., \'3.0\', \'2.1\'). Incremented upon each approved revision cycle. Critical for ALCOA+ compliance and audit trail integrity.']

  Note: 'Master entity for Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) in the controlled document library (MasterControl). Each record represents one SOP with: SOP number, title, current version, effective/expiry dates, category (clinical operations, data management, safety, regulatory, quality), owning department, applicable regulatory framework (FDA, EMA, ICH GCP), training requirement flag, and superseded SOP cross-reference. SSOT for the SOP library — distinct from TMF essential documents which are study-specific. SOPs are organization-level controlled documents that govern how clinical trial activities are performed.'
}

Table document.workflow {
  workflow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each document workflow instance in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the document_workflow data product.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: SOPs undergo formal review, approval, and distribution workflows governed by the document_workflow entity. Adding document_sop_id FK to document_workflow enables SOP lifecycle management within the sa']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Regulatory binders undergo review and approval workflows before submission to health authorities. workflow currently links to tmf_document, document_sop, and version but lacks a direct link to regulat']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this document workflow. Enables study-level aggregation of document control activities for inspection readiness and TMF completeness reporting.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Document approval workflows are triggered by regulatory submissions (e.g., pre-submission document review workflows, label approval workflows). workflow.regulatory_submission_flag indicates this relat']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled document record that this workflow instance governs. Links the workflow to the specific Trial Master File (TMF) or SOP document undergoing review, approval, or distribution.']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_version. Business justification: Document workflows govern a specific version of a document through its review and approval lifecycle. The document_version STRING column in document_workflow is a denormalized reference to the control']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the entire document workflow reached a terminal state (approved, rejected, or cancelled). Used to calculate total workflow cycle time and measure against target_completion_date for SLA performance reporting.']
  archival_date date [note: 'Date on which the document was formally archived in the eTMF system following workflow completion. Null until archival is executed. Used to confirm compliance with ICH GCP and regulatory retention period requirements.']
  archival_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document must be archived upon workflow completion per ICH GCP retention requirements. Drives post-approval archival workflow initiation in Veeva Vault for long-term TMF retention compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this document_workflow record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the system audit creation timestamp for data lineage and ETL traceability.']
  current_step_name varchar [note: 'Name of the active step within the workflow at the time of record capture (e.g., \'Quality Review\', \'Medical Review\', \'Regulatory Approval\', \'Final Sign-Off\'). Identifies where in the process the document currently resides.']
  current_step_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the current workflow step within the overall workflow template sequence. Enables progress tracking and percentage-complete calculations across multi-step document approval processes.']
  distribution_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved document was distributed to all designated recipients. Populated upon completion of the distribution step. Used to confirm timely dissemination per GCP requirements.']
  distribution_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved document must be distributed to designated recipients (sites, investigators, sponsors) upon workflow completion. Triggers the distribution workflow step in Veeva Vault upon approval.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document being processed (e.g., \'Protocol\', \'Informed Consent Form (ICF)\', \'Investigator Brochure\', \'SOP\', \'Regulatory Submission\', \'Site File\'). Aligns with DIA TMF Reference Model artifact taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: protocol|icf|investigator-brochure|sop|regulatory-submission|site-file|csr|safety-report|amendment|other — promote to reference product]']
  electronic_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more steps in this workflow require a 21 CFR Part 11 compliant electronic signature. Drives system enforcement of e-signature capture and audit trail requirements in Veeva Vault.']
  electronic_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the final binding electronic signature was applied to the document within this workflow. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance. Null if electronic_signature_required is False or signature has not yet been applied.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow instance has been formally escalated due to non-response, SLA breach, or rejection requiring management intervention. True triggers escalation notifications to supervisory roles per the workflow escalation policy.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for workflow escalation. Supports root cause analysis of document control bottlenecks and informs process improvement initiatives for GCP-compliant document management.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the workflow was formally escalated. Populated only when escalation_flag is True. Used to measure escalation response time and track resolution of escalated document control issues.']
  gcp_critical_document_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document is classified as a GCP essential document per ICH E6(R2) Section 8. Critical documents require mandatory workflow completion before study activities can proceed and are prioritized in inspection readiness reviews.']
  initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the document workflow instance was first created and launched in Veeva Vault. Serves as the principal business event timestamp for the workflow lifecycle and the start point for overall cycle time measurement.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this workflow and its associated document have been reviewed and confirmed as inspection-ready per GCP and regulatory authority standards. Supports pre-inspection TMF completeness assessments by FDA, EMA, MHRA, or other regulatory bodies.']
  instance_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier assigned by Veeva Vault to this specific workflow instance. Used for cross-system traceability and audit trail correlation with the source eTMF system.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this workflow instance, reflecting urgency of document review and approval. Critical priority is assigned to GCP essential documents, regulatory submissions with imminent deadlines, and safety-related documents.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document being processed through this workflow is intended for regulatory submission (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD). True triggers additional compliance checks and approval requirements per FDA/EMA submission standards.']
  rejection_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the document has been rejected and returned for revision within this workflow instance. High rejection counts indicate document quality issues and are tracked for quality metrics and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) analysis.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or categorized reason provided when a workflow step is rejected. Documents the specific deficiency identified by the reviewer, supporting document quality improvement and GCP audit trail requirements.']
  sla_breach_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the workflow or any of its steps has exceeded the defined Service Level Agreement (SLA) timeframe. True when step_completion_date or actual_completion_date exceeds the corresponding due date. Drives escalation and KPI reporting.']
  step_action_taken varchar [note: 'The specific action performed by the assignee at the current or last completed workflow step. Captures the decision outcome (approved, rejected) or process action (delegated, escalated) for audit trail completeness.']
  step_assignee_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual assigned to the current workflow step. Captured at time of assignment for audit trail integrity per ALCOA+ (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) requirements.']
  step_assignee_role varchar [note: 'Business role responsible for completing the current workflow step (e.g., \'Principal Investigator (PI)\', \'Clinical Research Associate (CRA)\', \'Regulatory Affairs Manager\', \'Quality Assurance (QA) Reviewer\'). Role-based assignment supports GCP delegation of authority.']
  step_comment varchar [note: 'Free-text comment or rationale provided by the step assignee when completing, rejecting, or escalating the workflow step. Supports GCP documentation requirements and provides context for rejection decisions.']
  step_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the current or most recently completed workflow step was actioned and closed. Compared against step_due_date to determine SLA compliance and cycle time analytics.']
  step_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the workflow step action was recorded in Veeva Vault. Provides sub-day granularity required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic audit trail and time-stamped electronic signature records.']
  step_due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the current workflow step as defined by the workflow SLA configuration. Used to calculate SLA breach status and trigger escalation notifications for overdue document reviews.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Overall target date by which the entire document workflow (all steps) must be completed. Derived from workflow SLA rules and used for inspection readiness tracking and TMF completeness KPI reporting.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Veeva Vault workflow template used to instantiate this workflow (e.g., \'SOP Review and Approval\', \'Regulatory Document Approval\', \'ICF Review Workflow\'). Identifies the standard process blueprint applied.']
  template_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the workflow template at the time this workflow instance was initiated. Ensures reproducibility and audit traceability when templates are updated over time.']
  tmf_zone varchar [note: 'DIA TMF Reference Model zone classification for the document being processed. Categorizes the document into the appropriate TMF structural zone to support inspection readiness and TMF completeness assessments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: trial-management|site-management|ip-management|subject-information|safety-reporting|central-laboratory|statistics|central-monitoring — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_steps varchar [note: 'Total number of steps defined in the workflow template for this workflow instance. Used in conjunction with current_step_sequence to calculate workflow completion percentage for inspection readiness dashboards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this document_workflow record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Tracks the most recent state change for incremental processing and audit trail completeness.']
  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document workflow instance within Veeva Vault. Tracks progression from initiation through review, approval decision, and terminal states. Supports GCP-compliant document control audit trails.']
  workflow_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the workflow by its primary business purpose within the document lifecycle. Distinguishes review-only workflows from approval workflows, distribution workflows, archival workflows, and amendment workflows.']

  Note: 'Tracks the lifecycle workflow state of a document through its review, approval, and distribution process within Veeva Vault. Captures workflow instance ID, workflow template name, current workflow step, step assignee role, step due date, step completion date, workflow status (initiated, in-review, pending-approval, approved, rejected, cancelled), escalation flag, and SLA breach indicator. Supports GCP-compliant document control workflows.'
}

Table document.signature {
  signature_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each electronic signature record applied to a controlled document. Primary key for the document_signature data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: The signature table records electronic signatures per 21 CFR Part 11 on controlled documents. Currently it links to tmf_document and workflow, but SOPs (document_sop) also require electronic signature']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) with which the signed document is associated. Enables study-level signature completeness reporting and TMF inspection readiness tracking.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site associated with the signed document, where applicable (e.g., site-level ICF, site initiation documents). Supports site-level TMF completeness and inspection readiness metrics.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled document record in the document master that this signature was applied to. Links the signature to its parent TMF/eTMF document.']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific version of the document at the time the signature was applied. Ensures the signature is bound to an immutable document version, satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 integrity requirements.']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_workflow. Business justification: Electronic signatures are applied within the context of a document workflow step (review, approval, distribution). document_signature has workflow_step_name and workflow_step_sequence as STRING/INT fi']
  audit_hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the signed document content and signature metadata at the time of signing. Provides tamper-evidence and integrity verification for the electronic signature record, satisfying 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10(a) data integrity requirements.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes entered by the signer at the time of signing. May capture the signer\'s attestation statement, conditional approval notes, or reviewer observations. Stored as part of the immutable signature audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp when the signature request record was first created in the source system (Veeva Vault). Supports audit trail completeness and ALCOA+ compliance.']
  device_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the device (workstation, tablet, or mobile device) used to apply the electronic signature. Supports forensic audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 system access controls validation.']
  digital_certificate_serial varchar [note: 'Serial number of the digital certificate used when the signature method is digital_certificate or smart_card. Enables certificate chain validation and revocation checking for PKI-based electronic signatures.']
  due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the signer must apply their electronic signature, as defined in the document workflow configuration. Used for SLA tracking, escalation triggering, and inspection readiness reporting.']
  hash_algorithm varchar [note: 'Cryptographic algorithm used to generate the audit hash for this signature record (e.g., SHA-256, SHA-512). Required to enable future hash verification and to document the cryptographic standard applied at the time of signing.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this signature record has been reviewed and confirmed as complete and compliant for regulatory inspection purposes. Used in TMF inspection readiness dashboards and GCP audit preparation.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device from which the electronic signature was applied. Captured as part of the 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail to support identity verification and forensic investigation. May be considered PII under GDPR.']
  is_regulatory_submission_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this signature is part of a document package intended for regulatory submission (e.g., IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, eCTD). When True, the signature record is subject to heightened integrity and retention requirements.']
  last_reminder_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent automated reminder notification sent to the signer. Supports escalation management and overdue signature tracking.']
  locale varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 locale code representing the language and regional settings active at the time of signing (e.g., en-US, de-DE, ja-JP). Relevant for multi-national trials to confirm the signer viewed the document in their preferred language.']
  meaning varchar [note: 'The legally defined meaning associated with the electronic signature, as required by 21 CFR Part 11. Specifies the intent of the signer (e.g., reviewed = content reviewed for accuracy; approved = authorized for release; acknowledged = receipt confirmed).']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the electronic signature event, as assigned by Veeva Vault or the controlling eTMF system. Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory inspection referencing.']
  reminder_sent_count varchar [note: 'Number of automated reminder notifications sent to the signer for a pending signature request. Used to track escalation activity and measure signature workflow efficiency in TMF completeness dashboards.']
  requested_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the signature request was initiated and sent to the signer. Used to calculate signature turnaround time and to track overdue signature tasks in inspection readiness dashboards.']
  signature_method varchar [note: 'Authentication method used to apply the electronic signature. Determines the technical compliance pathway under 21 CFR Part 11 (biometric vs. non-biometric). Required for regulatory inspection to demonstrate identity verification rigor.']
  signature_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the electronic signature record. Pending = awaiting signer action; Applied = successfully executed; Voided = invalidated due to document revision or administrative action; Superseded = replaced by a newer signature; Rejected = signer declined to sign.']
  signature_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the signature type per applicable regulatory framework. Electronic = standard 21 CFR Part 11 e-signature; Wet Ink Scanned = paper signature digitized; Advanced Electronic = eIDAS AES; Qualified Electronic = eIDAS QES with qualified certificate.']
  signed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the electronic signature was applied by the signer. This is the principal real-world event timestamp for the signature transaction. Must be system-generated and tamper-evident per 21 CFR Part 11 §11.10(e).']
  signer_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual who applied the electronic signature, captured at the time of signing. Stored as a point-in-time snapshot to preserve the audit trail even if the user record changes.']
  signer_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or company the signer represents at the time of signing (e.g., sponsor company name, CRO name, investigative site institution). Supports multi-party trial agreements and sponsor delegation tracking.']
  signer_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the signer within the document workflow at the time of signing. Determines the legal weight and workflow position of the signature. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: author|reviewer|approver|sponsor_representative|quality_assurance|investigator|regulatory_affairs|data_manager — promote to reference product if additional roles are required]']
  signer_title varchar [note: 'Professional title or job designation of the signer at the time of signing (e.g., Principal Investigator, Medical Monitor, Sponsor Representative, Biostatistician). Captured as a point-in-time snapshot for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this signature record was sourced (primarily Veeva Vault eTMF module). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and multi-system reconciliation.']
  source_system_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Native identifier of the signature record in the originating source system (e.g., Veeva Vault internal signature object ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the Silver Layer and the operational system for reconciliation and audit.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier representing the local timezone of the signer at the time of signing (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Required to accurately interpret the signed_timestamp in multi-national trial contexts and for regulatory inspection.']
  tmf_artifact_number varchar [note: 'DIA TMF Reference Model artifact number identifying the document type being signed (e.g., 01.01.01 for Protocol, 06.02.01 for Informed Consent Form). Enables artifact-level signature completeness tracking.']
  tmf_zone varchar [note: 'DIA TMF Reference Model Zone classification of the document to which this signature belongs (e.g., Zone 01 - Trial Management, Zone 02 - Central Trial Documents, Zone 03 - Regulatory). Supports TMF completeness metrics and inspection readiness reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp of the most recent modification to the signature record (e.g., status change, void action). Required for audit trail completeness and change detection in the Silver Layer ETL pipeline.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the signature was voided or invalidated. Required when signature_status = \'voided\' to maintain a complete and auditable record of signature lifecycle events per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the signature was voided or invalidated. Supports the complete lifecycle audit trail for the signature record and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  workflow_step_name varchar [note: 'Name of the document workflow step within which this signature was collected (e.g., \'Author Review\', \'QA Approval\', \'Sponsor Sign-Off\'). Maps to the Veeva Vault workflow configuration and supports workflow analytics and bottleneck identification.']
  workflow_step_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence position of this signature step within the overall document approval workflow. Enables ordered reconstruction of the full signature chain for regulatory inspection and audit trail review.']

  Note: 'Records electronic signatures applied to controlled documents per 21 CFR Part 11 requirements. Captures signer identity, signer role (author, reviewer, approver, sponsor representative), signature date and timestamp, signature meaning (reviewed, approved, acknowledged), signature method (username/password, biometric), IP address, and audit hash. Provides the legally binding e-signature audit trail for regulatory submissions and inspections.'
}

Table document.site_file {
  site_file_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Investigator Site File (ISF) or Regulatory Binder record in the lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  clinical_trial_authorization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.clinical_trial_authorization. Business justification: Site files must contain the CTA approval for the country where the site operates — a TMF essential document requirement. Linking site files to their governing CTA enables verification that the correct']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study with which this site file is associated. Links the ISF to its parent protocol.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which this site file is maintained. Ties the ISF to a specific site entity.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the site file was formally activated and made available for document filing, coinciding with or following site initiation. Marks the start of the file\'s active lifecycle.']
  applicable_gcp_guideline varchar [note: 'The specific GCP guideline version or regulation that governs the conduct of this site file. Determines essential document requirements, retention obligations, and inspection readiness criteria.']
  archival_date date [note: 'Date on which the site file was transferred to long-term archival storage per ICH GCP and sponsor retention requirements. Typically 15 years post-study completion for IND studies.']
  archival_location varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic location where the site file has been archived post-study closure (e.g., sponsor archive facility name, Veeva Vault archive vault path, or third-party archive vendor name).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the site file was formally closed following site close-out activities. After this date, no new documents should be filed except for archival or regulatory response purposes.']
  completeness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric percentage (0.00–100.00) representing the proportion of expected essential documents that are present and current in the site file. Calculated at the time of the last completeness review. Used for KPI reporting and inspection readiness dashboards.']
  completeness_status varchar [note: 'Overall completeness assessment of the site file relative to the expected essential documents per ICH GCP Section 8 and the DIA TMF Reference Model. Drives inspection readiness scoring and CRA monitoring activities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the site file record was first created in the system of record (Veeva Vault or CTMS). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  custodian_role varchar [note: 'Role of the designated custodian responsible for the site file. PI (Principal Investigator) is the accountable party per GCP; CRC (Clinical Research Coordinator) typically manages day-to-day file maintenance.']
  delegation_log_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current, signed Delegation of Authority (DoA) log is present in the site file, documenting all site staff authorized to perform study-related tasks and their qualifications.']
  electronic_vault_path varchar [note: 'Folder path or document library location within Veeva Vault eTMF where the electronic site file is stored. Used for direct navigation and audit trail purposes.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the site file is maintained electronically (eTMF via Veeva Vault), in paper form at the site, or as a hybrid of both. Drives inspection readiness procedures and retrieval workflows.']
  file_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to this site file, used in regulatory correspondence, inspection readiness tracking, and eTMF cross-referencing. Typically generated by the CTMS or Veeva Vault.']
  file_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the site file. Active indicates the file is in use during the study; Under Review indicates a quality or completeness review is in progress; Archived indicates post-study archival; Closed indicates the site has been closed out; Pending Activation indicates the file is being set up prior to site initiation.']
  file_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the site file by its regulatory and operational purpose. ISF (Investigator Site File) is the primary site-level essential document collection; Regulatory Binder holds IRB/IEC submissions and approvals; Pharmacy Binder tracks investigational product accountability; Laboratory Binder holds lab certifications and normal ranges; Investigator Binder holds PI-specific documents.']
  financial_disclosure_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether signed financial disclosure forms for the investigator and sub-investigators are present in the site file, as required by FDA regulations to identify potential conflicts of interest.']
  icf_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current, IRB/IEC-approved Informed Consent Form (ICF) is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.2.']
  inspection_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Assessment of the site file\'s readiness for regulatory inspection by authorities such as FDA, EMA, or MHRA. Ready indicates the file meets all GCP and regulatory requirements; Needs Remediation indicates minor gaps requiring correction; Critical Gaps indicates significant deficiencies that must be resolved before inspection.']
  investigator_cv_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current, signed curriculum vitae (CV) for the Principal Investigator and all sub-investigators is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.2.']
  irb_iec_approval_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the current, valid IRB (Institutional Review Board) or IEC (Independent Ethics Committee) approval letter is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.2.']
  lab_certification_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether current laboratory certifications (e.g., CLIA, CAP) and normal reference ranges for the site\'s clinical laboratory are present in the site file, as required by ICH GCP Section 8.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, or other authority) at which this site file was reviewed. Used for inspection history tracking and readiness planning.']
  last_inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Outcome classification of the most recent regulatory inspection of the site file. NAI (No Action Indicated), VAI (Voluntary Action Indicated), and OAI (Official Action Indicated) are standard FDA classification categories. Drives CAPA requirements and sponsor oversight actions.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent completeness review or quality check of the site file was performed, typically by a CRA (Clinical Research Associate) during a monitoring visit or central review.']
  missing_document_count varchar [note: 'Number of essential documents identified as missing or outstanding from the site file at the time of the last review. Drives follow-up actions and inspection readiness remediation.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next completeness review or quality check of the site file. Used for scheduling CRA monitoring visits and proactive inspection readiness management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or observations related to the site file that are not captured in structured fields. Examples include inspection preparation notes, custodian handover comments, or archival instructions.']
  open_action_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of outstanding action items or findings related to the site file that have not yet been resolved. Derived from monitoring visit findings and quality reviews. Used for inspection readiness and site risk assessment.']
  physical_location varchar [note: 'Description of the physical storage location of the site file at the investigational site (e.g., room number, cabinet, shelf). Applicable for paper or hybrid files. Required for inspection readiness and retrieval.']
  protocol_on_file boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the signed and dated study protocol (and all applicable amendments) is present in the site file. A mandatory essential document per ICH GCP Section 8.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory authority with jurisdiction over the investigational site and its site file requirements. Determines applicable GCP guidelines, retention periods, and inspection standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health Canada|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the mandatory regulatory retention period for the site file expires, after which the file may be destroyed per applicable regulations. Calculated based on study completion date and jurisdiction-specific retention requirements.']
  sponsor_review_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor or CRO has completed a formal review of the site file for completeness and GCP compliance. Required as part of sponsor oversight obligations under ICH GCP Section 5.']
  sponsor_review_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor or CRO completed the most recent formal review of the site file. Supports audit trail and oversight documentation requirements.']
  tmf_section varchar [note: 'DIA TMF Reference Model section code within the assigned zone (e.g., 06.01 for Site Initiation). Provides granular classification for document filing and completeness tracking.']
  tmf_zone varchar [note: 'DIA TMF Reference Model zone classification under which this site file\'s documents are organized. Zones provide a standardized taxonomy for essential document categorization across the TMF.']
  total_expected_documents varchar [note: 'Total count of essential documents expected to be present in the site file based on the study protocol, applicable regulations, and DIA TMF Reference Model requirements for this site and study phase.']
  total_filed_documents varchar [note: 'Actual count of documents currently filed and verified within the site file. Used in conjunction with total_expected_documents to derive completeness metrics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the site file record. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles for data integrity.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF to the site file container or root document object. Enables direct cross-system traceability between the lakehouse record and the source eTMF system.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing the Investigator Site File (ISF) or Regulatory Binder maintained at each investigational site. Captures site file ID, associated study, associated site, file type (ISF, regulatory binder, pharmacy binder), physical or electronic location, custodian (PI or CRC), completeness status, last review date, and inspection readiness status. Tracks the site-level essential document collection required by ICH GCP Section 8.'
}

Table document.tmf_completeness {
  tmf_completeness_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each point-in-time TMF completeness assessment record. Primary key of this product.']
  prior_assessment_tmf_completeness_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding TMF completeness assessment record for this study, enabling trend analysis and completeness trajectory tracking over time.']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: TMF completeness assessments are conducted at the site level and evaluate the completeness of the Investigator Site File (ISF). tmf_completeness already has study_site_id but lacks a direct reference ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this TMF completeness assessment was conducted. Links to the study master record.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: TMF completeness assessments are conducted at the site level (per DIA TMF Reference Model). Linking tmf_completeness to study_site enables site-level TMF gap reporting, per-site inspection readiness s']
  assessed_by_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the individual who performed the TMF completeness assessment, such as TMF Manager, QA Specialist, Clinical Research Associate (CRA), Study Manager, or Regulatory Affairs.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the TMF completeness assessment was performed. Represents the point-in-time snapshot date for this record.']
  assessment_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the period covered by this TMF completeness assessment. For periodic assessments, this defines the end of the reporting window.']
  assessment_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the period covered by this TMF completeness assessment. For periodic assessments, this defines the beginning of the reporting window.']
  assessment_reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code assigned to this completeness assessment, used for tracking and cross-referencing in Veeva Vault eTMF and inspection-readiness reports.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the completeness assessment record within Veeva Vault: draft (initiated but not yet submitted), in_progress (actively being assessed), completed (assessment data entry finished), reviewed (QA review completed), approved (formally approved by TMF manager or QA lead).']
