  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assessment trigger: scheduled (routine periodic), ad_hoc (unplanned), pre_inspection (triggered by regulatory inspection notice), milestone (triggered by a study milestone such as first patient in or database lock), or close_out (triggered by study close-out activities).']
  capa_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the CAPA record initiated in MasterControl as a result of critical TMF completeness gaps identified in this assessment. Null if no CAPA was required.']
  completeness_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum completeness percentage threshold required for this study at this point in time, as defined in the TMF plan or sponsor SOP. Used to determine whether the study is inspection-ready.']
  completeness_trend varchar [note: 'Directional trend of TMF completeness compared to the prior assessment: improving (percentage increased), stable (no significant change), declining (percentage decreased), or not_applicable (first assessment for this study).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this TMF completeness assessment record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field per ALCOA+ and 21 CFR Part 11 requirements.']
  critical_document_gap_count varchar [note: 'Number of missing or overdue artifacts classified as \'critical\' per the DIA TMF Reference Model or sponsor TMF plan (e.g., protocol, ethics approval, signed ICF). Critical gaps pose the highest inspection risk.']
  etmf_system_snapshot_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the eTMF system snapshot or report generated in Veeva Vault at the time of this assessment. Enables traceability back to the source system record for audit purposes.']
  expected_artifact_count varchar [note: 'Total number of TMF artifacts (documents) expected to be filed for this study at the time of assessment, based on the applicable DIA TMF Reference Model zones and study-specific TMF plan.']
  filed_artifact_count varchar [note: 'Total number of TMF artifacts that have been filed (uploaded and indexed) in the eTMF system (Veeva Vault) at the time of assessment.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative summarizing the key findings, gaps, and observations identified during this TMF completeness assessment. Supports inspection-readiness reporting and CAPA initiation.']
  inspection_readiness_risk varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating for inspection readiness based on completeness gaps, overdue artifacts, and zone-level deficiencies. Supports risk-based TMF monitoring and escalation decisions.']
  is_inspection_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the TMF is deemed inspection-ready based on the overall completeness percentage meeting or exceeding the completeness target. True = inspection-ready; False = not inspection-ready.']
  missing_artifact_count varchar [note: 'Number of expected TMF artifacts that have not yet been filed at the time of assessment. Calculated as expected_artifact_count minus filed_artifact_count, excluding overdue artifacts.']
  next_assessment_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled due date for the next TMF completeness assessment for this study, based on the TMF plan frequency (e.g., monthly, quarterly). Supports proactive inspection readiness monitoring.']
  overall_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The overall TMF completeness percentage calculated as (number of filed artifacts / number of expected artifacts) × 100. Principal quantitative KPI for inspection readiness monitoring. Expressed as a percentage with two decimal places.']
  overdue_artifact_count varchar [note: 'Number of TMF artifacts that are past their required filing deadline (per the TMF plan or DIA TMF Reference Model timelines) and have not yet been filed. A key inspection-readiness risk indicator.']
  pending_review_artifact_count varchar [note: 'Number of TMF artifacts that have been filed but are awaiting QC review or approval in Veeva Vault. These are filed but not yet finalized, and may affect the effective completeness rate.']
  rejected_artifact_count varchar [note: 'Number of TMF artifacts that were filed but subsequently rejected during QC review (e.g., wrong version, illegible, incorrect metadata). Rejected artifacts are not counted as filed for completeness purposes.']
  remediation_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this assessment identified gaps requiring formal remediation actions (e.g., CAPA initiation, escalation to study team). True = remediation required; False = no remediation required.']
  review_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the QA reviewer completed their review of this completeness assessment.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Full name of the QA reviewer or TMF lead who reviewed and approved the completeness assessment. Required for inspection-readiness documentation.']
  study_phase varchar [note: 'The clinical development phase of the study at the time of assessment (Phase 1, 2, 3, 4, or combined phases). Contextualizes completeness expectations against phase-specific TMF requirements.']
  study_status_at_assessment varchar [note: 'The operational status of the clinical study at the time this TMF completeness assessment was conducted. Used to contextualize completeness expectations — e.g., a study in close-out has higher completeness requirements than one in start-up.']
  tmf_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of the TMF assessed: full_study (entire sponsor TMF), sponsor_tmf (sponsor-held documents only), site_tmf (site-held documents only), or investigator_site_file (ISF at a specific site). Determines which DIA TMF Reference Model zones and artifacts are in scope.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this TMF completeness assessment record was last modified in the data platform. Supports audit trail and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  zone_1_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 1 (Trial Management), covering documents related to trial management, oversight, and administration.']
  zone_2_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 2 (Central Trial Documents), covering the protocol, amendments, investigator brochure, and other central study documents.']
  zone_3_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 3 (Regulatory), covering regulatory submissions, approvals, and correspondence with health authorities such as FDA, EMA, and PMDA.']
  zone_4_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 4 (IRB/IEC and Other Approvals), covering ethics committee approvals, informed consent forms (ICF), and other site-level approvals.']
  zone_5_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 5 (Site Management), covering site selection, initiation, monitoring, and close-out documents.']
  zone_6_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 6 (Investigational Product), covering IP manufacturing, labelling, shipping, accountability, and destruction records.']
  zone_7_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 7 (Safety Reporting), covering SAE reports, SUSARs, DSURs, and safety communications filed in the TMF.']
  zone_8_completeness_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Completeness percentage for DIA TMF Reference Model Zone 8 (Statistics and Data Management), covering the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP), Data Management Plan (DMP), randomization documentation, and database lock records.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a point-in-time TMF completeness assessment for a study, capturing the assessment date, assessed by (QA or TMF manager), overall completeness percentage, number of expected artifacts, number of filed artifacts, number of missing artifacts, number of overdue artifacts, and completeness by TMF zone. Supports ongoing inspection readiness monitoring and DIA TMF Reference Model compliance reporting.'
}

Table document.inspection_readiness {
  inspection_readiness_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the document inspection readiness record. Primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal CAPA record created in MasterControl to address inspection findings. Links this inspection readiness record to the quality management CAPA workflow.']
  primary_inspection_etmf_vault_study_id varchar [note: 'The Veeva Vault eTMF system identifier for the study\'s TMF vault, enabling direct cross-reference to the source system of record for document completeness metrics and readiness scores.']
  regulatory_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_inspection. Business justification: Document inspection readiness assessments are conducted in preparation for specific regulatory inspections. Linking the document readiness record to the regulatory inspection event enables cross-domai']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Inspection readiness assessments are conducted at the site file level — the ISF/regulatory binder maintained at each investigator site is the primary artifact reviewed during GCP inspections. Linking ']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site when the TMF inspection readiness record is scoped to a specific site file (tmf_scope = \'site-specific\'). Null for full-study or sponsor-section scoped records.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Inspection readiness assessments are conducted pre-submission (NDA/BLA/MAA) to ensure TMF completeness. Linking inspection readiness records to the target submission enables submission-gated readiness']
  tmf_completeness_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_completeness. Business justification: Inspection readiness assessments are directly informed by TMF completeness assessments. The tmf_completeness_percentage DECIMAL in document_inspection_readiness is a denormalized value from tmf_comple']
  actual_inspection_end_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the regulatory authority or sponsor inspection concluded. Used to calculate inspection duration and trigger post-inspection response workflows.']
  actual_inspection_start_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the regulatory authority or sponsor inspection commenced. Populated once the inspection begins; used to measure planning accuracy against target inspection date.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan is required as a result of inspection findings. True when critical or major findings necessitate a structured CAPA response per GCP quality management requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inspection readiness record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles.']
  critical_gaps_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical-severity document gaps or deficiencies that could result in a regulatory finding or inspection observation if not remediated prior to inspection. Subset of open_findings_count.']
  dia_tmf_zone_coverage varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of DIA TMF Reference Model zones (e.g., Zone 1-Trial Management, Zone 2-Central Trial Documents, Zone 3-Regulatory, Zone 4-IRB/IEC, Zone 5-Site Management, Zone 6-IP, Zone 7-Safety Reporting, Zone 8-Statistics) included in the scope of this readiness assessment.']
  inspecting_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority or organization conducting or expected to conduct the inspection (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Drives jurisdiction-specific readiness requirements and document standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health Canada|ANVISA|CFDA|sponsor|internal — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority formally notified the sponsor or CRO of the upcoming inspection. Triggers the formal inspection preparation timeline and escalation procedures.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal outcome classification issued by the regulatory authority following inspection closure. FDA classifications: No Action Indicated (NAI), Voluntary Action Indicated (VAI), Official Action Indicated (OAI). Populated post-inspection; null or \'pending\' during active inspection.']
  inspection_readiness_tier varchar [note: 'Red-Amber-Green (RAG) traffic-light classification of overall inspection readiness. \'green\' indicates ready for inspection; \'amber\' indicates preparation in progress with manageable gaps; \'red\' indicates significant deficiencies requiring urgent remediation before inspection.']
  inspection_report_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority issued the formal inspection report or Form FDA 483 (for FDA inspections). Triggers the response timeline for addressing inspection observations.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory or sponsor inspection for which readiness is being tracked. GCP inspections are conducted by regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA); TMF inspections focus on document completeness; sponsor inspections are internal quality audits; for-cause inspections are triggered by specific concerns. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gcp|tmf|sponsor|regulatory-authority|for-cause|routine — promote to reference product]']
  last_readiness_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent internal TMF readiness review was conducted. Used to assess currency of the readiness assessment and trigger periodic re-reviews per SOP.']
  major_gaps_count varchar [note: 'Number of major-severity document gaps identified during TMF readiness review. Major gaps are significant deficiencies that require remediation but are less severe than critical gaps.']
  minor_gaps_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor-severity document gaps or observations identified during TMF readiness review. Minor gaps are low-risk deficiencies that should be addressed but do not pose immediate inspection risk.']
  missing_documents_count varchar [note: 'Number of expected TMF artifacts per the DIA TMF Reference Model that are entirely absent from the eTMF system. Distinct from overdue documents which are expected but not yet filed.']
  next_readiness_review_date date [note: 'The date on which the next scheduled internal TMF readiness review is planned. Supports proactive inspection readiness management and escalation when overdue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for the TMF Manager to document key observations, risk factors, remediation priorities, or contextual information relevant to the inspection readiness status of this study\'s TMF.']
  open_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unresolved inspection readiness findings (gaps, deficiencies, or observations) identified during internal TMF reviews. A key KPI for inspection readiness dashboards.']
  overdue_documents_count varchar [note: 'Number of TMF documents that are past their expected filing deadline per the TMF filing timeline or DIA TMF Reference Model expectations. Indicates timeliness risk for inspection readiness.']
  previous_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent prior regulatory inspection for this study, if applicable. Used to assess inspection history, recurrence risk, and whether prior findings have been adequately remediated.']
  previous_inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'The formal outcome of the most recent prior regulatory inspection for this study. Provides historical context for current readiness planning and risk assessment.']
  readiness_reference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this inspection readiness record, used in communications with sponsors, regulatory authorities, and internal tracking systems (e.g., Veeva Vault eTMF). Format: IR-{StudyCode}-{Sequence}.']
  readiness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0.00–100.00) representing the overall inspection readiness of the study\'s TMF, calculated based on document completeness, quality metrics, and open findings. Sourced from Veeva Vault eTMF completeness metrics or internal scoring methodology.']
  readiness_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the TMF inspection readiness process for this study. \'not-started\' indicates no preparation has begun; \'in-preparation\' indicates active remediation; \'ready\' indicates the TMF has passed internal review; \'under-inspection\' indicates the regulatory authority is actively inspecting; \'closed\' indicates the inspection cycle is complete.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the sponsor or CRO must submit a formal written response to the regulatory authority\'s inspection observations or findings. Typically 15 business days for FDA Form 483 responses.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the upcoming regulatory inspection. Populated when sponsor_notified is True.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of the upcoming regulatory inspection. Required for sponsor oversight and co-inspection preparation per GCP obligations.']
  study_protocol_number varchar [note: 'The sponsor-assigned protocol number for the clinical study associated with this TMF inspection readiness record. Enables cross-referencing with regulatory submissions and sponsor systems.']
  target_inspection_date date [note: 'The anticipated or scheduled date on which the regulatory authority or sponsor inspection is expected to commence. Used for readiness planning and milestone tracking.']
  tmf_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope of the TMF covered by this inspection readiness record. \'full-study\' covers the entire TMF; \'site-specific\' covers a single investigator site file; \'sponsor-section\' covers sponsor-held documents only; \'investigator-section\' covers investigator-held documents only.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this inspection readiness record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) ALCOA+ principles.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this inspection readiness record, incremented each time a formal readiness review is conducted and the record is updated. Supports audit trail and version history tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking the inspection readiness status of a study\'s TMF for regulatory authority inspections (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Captures study reference, inspection type (GCP, TMF, sponsor), target inspection date, readiness status (not-started, in-preparation, ready, under-inspection, closed), readiness score, number of open findings, number of critical gaps, responsible TMF manager, and last readiness review date. Distinct from compliance domain inspection records — this entity is document-centric.'
}

Table document.distribution {
  distribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each document distribution record in the controlled distribution log.']
  document_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_sop. Business justification: SOPs are formally distributed to staff as controlled documents requiring acknowledgement and training completion. document_distribution already handles TMF document distribution; adding document_sop_i']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Regulatory submission binders are distributed to health authorities as part of the submission process. distribution tracks controlled distribution of approved documents including regulatory submission']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Controlled documents (protocols, amendments, ICFs) are distributed to investigator sites and filed in the site\'s Investigator Site File (ISF). distribution already has study_site_id but lacks a direct']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this document distribution was performed. Enables study-level distribution tracking and inspection readiness.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site that received the document, if the distribution is site-specific. Null for sponsor-level or CRO-level distributions.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Regulatory submissions trigger mandatory document distributions (updated protocols, ICFs, investigator brochures to sites). Linking distribution records to the submission that required them enables su']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled document (SOP, protocol, amendment, ICF, etc.) that was distributed. Links to the document master record in the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific approved version of the document that was distributed. Ensures traceability to the exact version received by each recipient.']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Distribution of controlled documents is typically triggered by a completed approval workflow. Linking distribution.workflow_id → workflow.workflow_id allows traceability from the approval workflow to ']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'The date on which the recipient formally acknowledged receipt of the distributed document. Null if acknowledgement has not yet been received or is not required.']
  acknowledgement_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the recipient must acknowledge receipt of the document, as defined by the distribution plan or SOP. Used to identify overdue acknowledgements and trigger escalation workflows.']
  acknowledgement_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which the recipient acknowledged receipt of the document (e.g., electronic signature in Veeva Vault, email reply, physical signature on distribution log). Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  acknowledgement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient is required to formally acknowledge receipt of the document. Mandatory for GCP-critical documents such as protocols, amendments, and Informed Consent Forms (ICFs). Drives overdue acknowledgement tracking.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the recipient\'s acknowledgement was recorded in the system. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature and audit trail requirements.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the recipient\'s location. Used for country-level distribution compliance tracking and regulatory reporting to authorities such as FDA, EMA, and PMDA.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this distribution record was first created in the system. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements.']
  days_to_acknowledge varchar [note: 'The number of calendar days allowed from distribution date for the recipient to acknowledge receipt, as specified in the distribution plan or governing SOP. Drives due date calculation and KPI reporting.']
  distribution_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the document was formally distributed to the recipient. This is the principal business event date for the distribution record, used for GCP compliance timelines and inspection readiness.']
  distribution_method varchar [note: 'The channel or mechanism used to distribute the document to the recipient (e.g., Veeva Vault system notification, email, physical courier, CTMS portal). Relevant for GCP-compliant controlled distribution audit trails.']
  distribution_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this distribution event, typically generated by the document management system (e.g., Veeva Vault). Used for audit trail references and regulatory inspection queries.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'The organizational scope of this distribution event, indicating whether the document was distributed globally, by country, to a specific site, department, or individual recipient.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document distribution record. Tracks progression from initiation through delivery and acknowledgement. Used for inspection readiness dashboards and overdue acknowledgement tracking.']
  distribution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the document distribution was initiated or completed in the system (e.g., Veeva Vault notification sent). Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic audit trail requirements.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Controlled document number (e.g., SOP-0042, PROT-2024-001) as assigned by the document control system. Denormalized for audit trail completeness.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Title of the document as it appeared at the time of distribution. Captured as a denormalized field to support audit trail completeness per ALCOA+ principles without requiring a join.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document type distributed. Drives distribution requirements, acknowledgement obligations, and regulatory filing rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Protocol|Amendment|ICF|SOP|Investigator Brochure|Regulatory Submission|Safety Letter|Lab Manual|Other — promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the distributed document version becomes effective and must be implemented by the recipient. Distinct from distribution date; critical for protocol amendments and SOP updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context about the distribution event, such as special instructions, delivery exceptions, or reasons for delayed distribution.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the document recipient at the time of distribution. Used for electronic distribution audit trail and follow-up communications. Classified as confidential PII.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who received the document. Captured as a denormalized field to preserve the audit trail at the time of distribution, as required by ALCOA+ principles.']
  recipient_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization to which the recipient belongs (e.g., investigational site name, sponsor company, CRO (Contract Research Organization) name, IRB/IEC name). Supports organization-level distribution tracking.']
  recipient_organization_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the recipient\'s organization type. Enables segmentation of distribution records by stakeholder category for compliance reporting and inspection readiness.']
  recipient_role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the document recipient within the clinical trial (e.g., Principal Investigator (PI), Clinical Research Coordinator (CRC), Clinical Research Associate (CRA), Sponsor representative). Determines distribution obligations and acknowledgement requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PI|CRC|CRA|Sponsor|IRB/IEC|Regulatory Authority|CRO Staff|Biostatistician|Data Manager|Other — promote to reference product]']
  reminder_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent acknowledgement reminder was sent to the recipient. Null if no reminder has been sent.']
  reminder_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an acknowledgement reminder notification has been sent to the recipient for this distribution. Used to track follow-up actions for overdue acknowledgements.']
  superseded_version_number varchar [note: 'The version number of the previously distributed document that this distribution supersedes. Enables tracking of version transitions and ensures recipients are aware of obsolete versions.']
  tmf_artifact_number varchar [note: 'The DIA TMF Reference Model artifact number associated with the distributed document (e.g., 01.01.01 for Study Plan). Enables structured TMF completeness tracking and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  tmf_zone varchar [note: 'The TMF zone classification under the DIA TMF Reference Model where this distribution record is filed (e.g., Zone 01 - Trial Management, Zone 04 - Investigational Sites). Supports eTMF completeness and inspection readiness.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the recipient completed the required training associated with the distributed document. Null if training is not required or not yet completed. Supports GCP training compliance tracking.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient is required to complete associated training upon receipt of this document (e.g., SOP training, protocol training). Links to training acknowledgement tracking in MasterControl.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this distribution record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements.']
  veeva_vault_distribution_reference varchar [note: 'The native distribution event identifier assigned by Veeva Vault for this distribution record. Supports reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the Veeva Vault source system.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The native document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF for the distributed document. Enables direct traceability back to the source system record for audit and inspection purposes.']

  Note: 'Tracks the controlled distribution of approved documents (SOPs, protocols, amendments, ICFs) to recipients. Captures distribution date, document version distributed, recipient name, recipient role, recipient organization (site, sponsor, CRO), distribution method (Veeva Vault notification, email, physical), acknowledgement required flag, acknowledgement date, and distribution status. Supports GCP-compliant controlled distribution and training acknowledgement tracking.'
}

Table document.regulatory_binder {
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the regulatory binder record in the lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: A regulatory binder is assembled to package a specific submission (IND, NDA, annual report). Publishing teams build binders per submission; linking binder to submission enables submission tracking, eC']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the TMF document that is filed within the regulatory binder.']
  agency_action_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority issued its formal regulatory action (approval, complete response letter, refusal to file, withdrawal, etc.) on this submission. Marks the end of the primary review cycle.']
  agency_action_type varchar [note: 'The type of regulatory action taken by the health authority on this submission (e.g., approval, complete response letter (CRL), refuse to file (RTF), withdrawal). Captures the outcome of the regulatory review cycle.']
  agency_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority officially acknowledged receipt of the submission. Marks the start of the regulatory review clock (e.g., PDUFA clock for FDA, Day 0 for EMA). Distinct from submission_date which is the date filed by the sponsor.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the health authority granted marketing authorization, IND clearance, or equivalent regulatory approval for this submission. Null if not yet approved or if submission was withdrawn. Critical for commercial launch planning and regulatory milestone reporting.']
  archival_date date [note: 'The date on which this regulatory binder was transferred to long-term archival storage per ICH GCP retention requirements. Null if not yet archived. ICH E6(R2) requires retention for at least 2 years after marketing authorization or study discontinuation.']
  binder_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier assigned to the regulatory binder or eCTD sequence package (e.g., IND-2024-001, NDA-125678-SEQ003). Used for cross-system reference and health authority correspondence.']
  binder_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory binder. Tracks progression from document assembly through health authority review to final disposition. Values: in_preparation (being assembled), submitted (filed with agency), under_review (agency review in progress), approved (marketing authorization or IND clearance granted), withdrawn (submission retracted).']
  binder_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the regulatory binder or submission package (e.g., \'Initial IND Application for Compound XYZ\', \'NDA Module 5 Clinical Study Reports\'). Used for identification and document management.']
  binder_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory binder by submission type. Determines the applicable regulatory framework and required document set. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: IND|NDA|BLA|MAA|CTA|annual_report|IND_amendment|NDA_supplement|BLA_supplement|CTA_amendment — promote to reference product]']
  completeness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of required documents that have been collected and filed in this regulatory binder relative to the expected document set per DIA TMF Reference Model. Used for inspection readiness dashboards and regulatory milestone tracking. Range: 0.00 to 100.00.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this regulatory binder per the organization\'s information security policy. Regulatory submissions typically contain commercially sensitive and proprietary data. Values: restricted (highest sensitivity), confidential (commercially sensitive), internal (internal use only), public (publicly disclosed).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory binder record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate).']
  ctd_module varchar [note: 'Identifies which CTD module(s) are included in this binder (M1=Administrative/Regional, M2=Summaries, M3=Quality, M4=Nonclinical, M5=Clinical, full=complete dossier). Aligns with ICH M4 CTD structure for eCTD submissions.']
  ctd_section varchar [note: 'The CTD module and section under which this document is filed within the regulatory binder (e.g., M2.7.2, M5.3.5.1). This placement is specific to the binder-document combination — the same document may be filed under different CTD sections in different submissions.']
  document_count varchar [note: 'Total number of documents included in this regulatory binder at the time of submission. Used for completeness verification, inspection readiness, and DIA TMF Reference Model compliance tracking.']
  document_role_in_submission varchar [note: 'The functional role this document plays within this specific regulatory submission binder (e.g., primary_evidence, supporting_reference, administrative). The same document may serve different roles in different binders.']
  ectd_version varchar [note: 'The eCTD specification version used to structure and validate this submission (e.g., 3.2.2 for legacy eCTD, 4.0 for next-generation eCTD). Determines the XML backbone schema and validation rules applied during submission compilation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authorization or IND/CTA clearance expires and requires renewal. Applicable for conditional approvals, time-limited authorizations, and annual IND renewals. Null for permanent approvals.']
  inclusion_date date [note: 'The date on which this TMF document was formally added to the regulatory binder. Belongs to the relationship, not to either entity — the same document may have been added to different binders on different dates.']
  is_ectd_format boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory binder is assembled and submitted in eCTD (Electronic Common Technical Document) format as required by ICH M8. True = eCTD format; False = non-eCTD (e.g., paper or NeeS format for legacy submissions).']
  is_inspection_ready boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this regulatory binder has been reviewed and certified as inspection-ready per GCP and DIA TMF Reference Model standards. True = inspection-ready; False = not yet certified. Critical for regulatory inspection preparedness programs.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, regulatory strategy notes, agency feedback summaries, or operational comments related to this regulatory binder. Not intended for structured data; used for qualitative annotations by regulatory affairs staff.']
  pdufa_target_date date [note: 'The FDA PDUFA goal date by which the agency commits to completing its review and taking action on the submission. Applicable to NDA/BLA submissions to FDA. Used for regulatory timeline management and commercial launch planning.']
  planned_submission_date date [note: 'The internally planned or target date for submitting the regulatory binder to the health authority. Used for project planning, resource allocation, and regulatory milestone tracking. Compared against actual submission_date to measure submission timeliness.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the mandatory regulatory retention period for this binder expires, after which the documents may be destroyed per applicable regulations. Calculated based on archival_date plus jurisdiction-specific retention requirements (e.g., 15 years post-approval for EU, 2 years post-marketing authorization for ICH).']
  review_pathway varchar [note: 'The regulatory review designation or pathway applicable to this submission (e.g., standard, priority review, accelerated approval, breakthrough therapy, orphan drug, conditional marketing authorization). Determines review timelines and agency commitments.']
  sequence_number_in_binder varchar [note: 'The ordering or sequence position of this document within its CTD section in the binder. Used to control document ordering during eCTD compilation and health authority review. Specific to this binder-document filing.']
  submission_category varchar [note: 'Categorizes the submission as an original filing, amendment, supplement, annual report, or response to agency queries. Determines the review pathway and applicable regulatory timelines.']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory binder was formally submitted to the target health authority. This is the principal business event date for the submission lifecycle and is used for regulatory timeline tracking and PDUFA/review clock calculations.']
  submission_medium varchar [note: 'The physical or electronic medium used to deliver the submission to the health authority (electronic = eCTD gateway/portal, paper = physical binders, hybrid = combination). FDA mandates electronic submission for most NDA/BLA/IND filings.']
  submission_portal varchar [note: 'The electronic gateway or portal used to transmit the submission to the health authority (e.g., FDA ESG - Electronic Submissions Gateway, EMA eSubmission Gateway, PMDA eCTD Gateway). Used for submission tracking and technical troubleshooting.']
  submission_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit eCTD sequence number assigned to this submission package (e.g., \'0000\' for initial submission, \'0001\' for first amendment). Conforms to ICH M8 eCTD specification and is used by health authorities to track submission history.']
  target_agency varchar [note: 'The regulatory health authority to which this binder is submitted (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA). Determines applicable regulatory requirements, submission format, and review timelines. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada|ANVISA|NMPA|COFEPRIS — promote to reference product]']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The clinical therapeutic area or disease indication covered by this regulatory submission (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology, Infectious Disease). Used for portfolio management, regulatory strategy, and agency routing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory binder record was last modified. Required for audit trail compliance under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles. Used to detect stale records and track binder lifecycle changes.']
  veeva_vault_binder_reference varchar [note: 'The unique document or binder object identifier in Veeva Vault (eTMF/Regulatory module) that corresponds to this regulatory binder record. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  withdrawal_date date [note: 'The date on which the sponsor formally withdrew the regulatory submission from health authority review. Null if not withdrawn. Used for regulatory portfolio tracking and lessons-learned analysis.']
  withdrawal_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for withdrawing the regulatory submission (e.g., safety concerns, commercial decision, insufficient efficacy data). Populated only when withdrawal_date is set. Required for regulatory portfolio governance.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a regulatory submission binder or eCTD sequence package assembled for health authority submissions (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA). Captures binder ID, binder type (IND, NDA, BLA, MAA, CTA, annual report), submission sequence number, target agency (FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA), submission date, eCTD format flag, binder status (in-preparation, submitted, under-review, approved, withdrawn), and Veeva Vault regulatory binder reference. Distinct from the regulatory domain submission record — this entity owns the document collection.'
}

Table document.archival_record {
  archival_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each archival record entry tracking the disposition of a TMF document or study file at study close-out.']
  approval_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.approval. Business justification: Regulatory approvals determine document retention requirements and trigger archival obligations. Linking archival records to the approval that mandated retention enables retention period calculation, ']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Archival of study documents is triggered by the database lock event. Linking archival_record to database_lock_event replaces the denormalized database_lock_date with a proper FK, enabling retention pe']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Regulatory submission binders (eCTD sequences, regulatory binders) must be archived at study close-out per retention requirements. archival_record tracks archival disposition but currently has no link']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: At study close-out, Investigator Site Files (ISFs) are archived. archival_record tracks the archival disposition of TMF documents and study files, and already has study_site_id, but lacks a direct ref']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this archival record applies. Enables study-level archival compliance reporting.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site whose documents are being archived, if applicable. Null for sponsor-level TMF documents.']
  tmf_artifact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_artifact. Business justification: Archival records reference the DIA TMF Reference Model artifact classification for retention period determination and regulatory compliance. tmf_reference_model_artifact (STRING) is a denormalized ref']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the TMF document or study file record being archived. Links to the source document entity in the document management domain.']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.document_version. Business justification: Archival records capture the disposition of a specific document version at study close-out. The document_version STRING column is a denormalized reference. Adding document_version_id FK links the arch']
  workflow_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.workflow. Business justification: Archival of TMF documents is a workflow-driven process — documents go through an archival workflow before being formally archived. workflow has archival_date and archival_required fields indicating it']
  archival_date date [note: 'The date on which the document or study file was formally transferred to the archive. This is the principal business event date for the archival transaction, distinct from the record creation timestamp.']
  archival_location varchar [note: 'Physical or electronic location where the archived document is stored. For physical archives, this includes the facility name, building, room, shelf, and box reference. For electronic archives, this includes the system name and repository path (e.g., Veeva Vault long-term archive vault name).']
  archival_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording additional context, exceptions, or special instructions related to the archival record (e.g., partial document archival, known deviations from standard archival procedures, special handling instructions).']
  archival_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the archival record. \'pending\' = awaiting archival; \'archived\' = successfully archived; \'retrieved\' = temporarily retrieved from archive; \'destroyed\' = permanently destroyed per retention schedule; \'on_hold\' = destruction or retrieval suspended (e.g., litigation hold).']
  archival_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the document is archived electronically (eTMF/long-term electronic archive), physically (paper-based archive facility), or as a hybrid (both electronic and physical copies retained).']
  archive_facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical or electronic archive facility or vendor responsible for storing the archived documents (e.g., Iron Mountain, sponsor internal archive, Veeva Vault long-term archive). Supports vendor management and retrieval logistics.']
  archive_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number assigned to the archived document or file by the archive facility or eTMF system (e.g., Veeva Vault document ID or physical archive box/folder reference). Used for retrieval and audit trail purposes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction whose regulatory requirements govern the retention and archival of this document (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, JPN). Determines applicable retention regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this archival record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate).']
  custodian_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or individual currently responsible for the custody and safekeeping of the archived documents (e.g., sponsor name, CRO name, third-party archive vendor). Supports accountability and retrieval logistics.']
  custody_transfer_date date [note: 'Date on which formal custody of the archived documents was transferred to the sponsor or designated archive custodian. Null if transfer has not occurred. Supports chain of custody documentation.']
  destruction_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number of the formal destruction authorization document or approval record that sanctioned the permanent destruction of the archived file. Provides the audit trail required by GCP and regulatory agencies.']
  destruction_authorized_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the authorized individual (e.g., sponsor representative, QA director) who formally approved the destruction of the archived document. Required for GCP compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  destruction_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the archived document was permanently destroyed following expiry of the retention period and completion of the destruction authorization process. Null if document has not been destroyed.']
  destruction_method varchar [note: 'The method used to permanently destroy the archived document upon expiry of the retention period (e.g., shredding for physical documents, secure digital deletion or degaussing for electronic records). Required for GCP compliance audit trail.']
  document_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the archived document per ICH GCP essential document categories (e.g., essential document, non-essential, regulatory submission document, site file, sponsor file). Determines applicable retention requirements.']
  document_title varchar [note: 'Human-readable title or name of the document or study file being archived, as recorded in the eTMF (Veeva Vault) or physical archive index.']
  etmf_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'The unique document identifier assigned by Veeva Vault eTMF to the archived document. Enables direct traceability between the archival record in the lakehouse and the source document in the operational eTMF system.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this archival record and its associated document have been verified as inspection-ready (True) or require remediation before a regulatory inspection (False). Supports FDA, EMA, MHRA, and PMDA inspection preparation.']
  litigation_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a litigation hold (legal hold) has been placed on this archived document, preventing destruction regardless of retention expiry. True = document is under legal hold; False = no hold in effect.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory authority whose requirements govern the archival and retention of this document (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Drives jurisdiction-specific retention period and destruction rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  retention_basis varchar [note: 'The regulatory or contractual basis that determines the retention period for this document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ICH_GCP_15yr|FDA_21CFR_312.62|EMA_EU_CTR|sponsor_requirement|PMDA_requirement|MHRA_requirement|other — promote to reference product]']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The calculated date on which the mandatory retention period expires and the document becomes eligible for destruction, based on the archival date plus the retention period. Drives destruction scheduling workflows.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'The required retention period in years for the archived document, as determined by the applicable regulatory requirement or sponsor policy (e.g., 15 years per ICH GCP, 2 years post-NDA approval per 21 CFR 312.62, or longer per sponsor requirement).']
  retrieval_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the archived document was physically or electronically retrieved from the archive. Null if not yet retrieved. Distinct from retrieval_request_date to track turnaround time.']
  retrieval_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for retrieving the archived document from the archive (e.g., regulatory inspection, audit, litigation, study amendment, sponsor request). Supports access control and audit trail requirements.']
  retrieval_request_date date [note: 'Date on which a formal request was made to retrieve the archived document from the archive facility. Null if no retrieval has been requested. Supports archive access tracking and SLA monitoring.']
  return_to_archive_date date [note: 'Date on which a previously retrieved document was returned to the archive. Null if the document has not been retrieved or has not yet been returned. Ensures chain of custody integrity.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the sponsor was formally notified of the archival or destruction of the document, as required by ICH GCP and applicable regulations. Null if notification is not required or not yet sent.']
  study_closeout_date date [note: 'The date on which the clinical study was formally closed out, triggering the archival obligations for TMF documents. Used to calculate retention period start dates and compliance deadlines.']
  tmf_reference_model_zone varchar [note: 'The DIA TMF Reference Model zone classification of the archived document (e.g., Zone 01 - Trial Management, Zone 02 - Central Trial Documents, Zone 03 - Regulatory, Zone 04 - IRB/IEC, Zone 05 - Site Management). Supports inspection readiness and completeness assessment.']
  transfer_of_custody_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether custody of the archived documents has been formally transferred to the sponsor or a designated third-party archive (True) or remains with the CRO (False). Relevant for CRO-managed studies at close-out.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this archival record was last modified. Supports audit trail requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles, enabling detection of unauthorized or unexpected record changes.']

  Note: 'Master entity tracking the archival disposition of TMF documents and study files at study close-out per ICH GCP requirements. Captures archival date, archival location (physical archive, long-term electronic archive), archive reference number, retention period (years), retention basis (ICH GCP 15 years, FDA 21 CFR 312.62, sponsor requirement), archival status (pending, archived, retrieved, destroyed), destruction date, and destruction authorization. Ensures compliance with post-study document retention obligations.'
}

Table document.site_filing {
  site_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the site_filing association']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the investigational site file (ISF or Regulatory Binder) in which this document is filed.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the TMF essential document that has been filed into the site file.']
  document_position_in_file varchar [note: 'The tab, section, or folder position of this document within the physical or electronic site binder (e.g., \'Section 3, Tab 2\'). This positional reference belongs to the filing relationship, not to the document or the binder independently.']
  filing_date date [note: 'The date on which this TMF document was physically or electronically filed into the site file. Belongs to the filing event, not to the document or the binder independently.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this document\'s filing at this specific site. A document may be FILED at one site and MISSING at another simultaneously — this status belongs to the site-document combination, not to either entity alone.']
  is_original_or_copy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site holds the original document or a certified/uncertified copy. This is a GCP-critical distinction that belongs to the filing relationship — the same document may be the original at the sponsor TMF and a certified copy at the site.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which a Clinical Research Associate (CRA) last verified this specific document at this specific site during a monitoring visit. Belongs to the site-document filing record, not to the document globally or the site file as a whole.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Site Filing event between site_file and tmf_document. It captures the act of filing a specific essential document into a specific investigational site\'s ISF or Regulatory Binder, as required by ICH GCP Section 8. Each record links one site_file to one tmf_document and carries filing-specific attributes — filing date, filing status, original-vs-copy designation, binder position, and CRA review date — that exist only in the context of this site-document filing relationship. This is the operational record used by CRAs during monitoring visits and by QA teams during inspection readiness assessments.. Existence Justification: In clinical trial operations, the same essential documents (protocol, Investigator\'s Brochure, ICF, IRB/IEC approval) must be physically filed at every participating investigational site\'s ISF/Regulatory Binder. A single TMF document (e.g., the approved protocol v3.0) is filed across dozens of sites simultaneously, and each site file contains hundreds of distinct TMF documents. The business actively manages this filing relationship — tracking whether each document has been filed at each site, whether it is an original or certified copy, its position in the binder, and its filing date — which is a core GCP compliance activity audited during regulatory inspections.'
}

Table compliance.audit {

  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.country. Business justification: Audits are scoped to specific countries for GCP compliance. Linking to the study country record enables country-level audit frequency reporting, risk-based monitoring plans, and sponsor oversight of c']

  investigational_product_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.investigational_product. Business justification: GCP requires IMP-specific audits (manufacturing, labeling, accountability). Audit scope must be traceable to the specific investigational product for regulatory inspection readiness, TMF completeness,']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.investigational_site. Business justification: GCP site audits are conducted at specific investigational sites. Linking audit to investigational_site enables site audit history reporting, inspection readiness dashboards, and audit frequency tracki']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: GCP/GLP lab facility audits are a named compliance process. Sponsors and CROs conduct periodic audits of central labs to verify accreditation, data integrity, and GCP compliance. Auditors need to refe']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: Regulatory agencies (FDA, EMA) conduct PI-directed audits; CROs audit PI qualifications and conduct. Linking audit to principal_investigator enables PI audit history tracking, debarment risk assessmen']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: GCP audits are scoped to a specific protocol version. Direct FK to protocol enables audit-readiness reports, TMF completeness checks, and regulatory inspection preparation tied to the exact protocol v']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: Statistical process audits (biostatistics audits, programming audits) are scoped to a specific SAP. Auditors review SAP compliance, TLF production against SAP specifications, and analysis population d']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Site audits are conducted against the investigator site file — the site file is the primary artifact reviewed during a GCP site audit. A QA expert expects this link to scope the audit to the correct s']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this audit. Nullable for cross-study or enterprise-level audits (e.g., system or vendor audits not tied to a single protocol).']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.study_site. Business justification: Audits are scoped to a specific study-site relationship (a site participating in a particular trial). Linking audit to study_site enables per-study audit tracking, protocol-specific audit findings agg']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Audits generate TMF-filed documents (audit plans, reports, sponsor notifications). GCP requires audit documentation in the TMF. A QA expert expects this link for TMF completeness tracking and inspecti']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which audit fieldwork actually concluded. Used to compute actual audit duration and assess deviation from planned schedule.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which audit fieldwork actually commenced. Compared against planned_start_date to measure schedule adherence and audit program performance.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the audit by initiating party and trigger: internal (self-initiated QA), external (sponsor or third-party), regulatory_inspection (FDA/EMA/MHRA inspector-led), for_cause (triggered by specific concern or signal), or routine (scheduled per audit plan).']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique audit reference number assigned by MasterControl at audit creation (e.g., AUD-2024-00123). Used in regulatory correspondence, CAPA linkage, and TMF filing.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope, defining the boundaries, processes, systems, or study activities subject to evaluation. Documented in the audit plan and referenced throughout the audit lifecycle.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit record within MasterControl: planned (scheduled, not yet started), in_progress (fieldwork underway), completed (report issued), cancelled (audit withdrawn), or on_hold (paused pending resolution).']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit by the subject being evaluated: system (EDC, CTMS, safety systems), process (SOPs, workflows), vendor (CRO, lab, IRT), site (investigational site GCP), or TMF (Trial Master File completeness). Drives audit planning templates and checklist selection in MasterControl.']
  capa_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the auditee must submit a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan in response to audit findings. Calculated from report_issue_date per sponsor or regulatory requirements.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal CAPA plan is required as a result of this audit. Set to True when critical or major findings are identified. Triggers CAPA workflow initiation in MasterControl.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in MasterControl. Supports ALCOA+ data integrity requirements (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) and audit trail compliance.']
  critical_finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical GCP findings identified during the audit. Critical findings indicate conditions that may directly impact subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance and require immediate CAPA. Sourced from MasterControl finding records.']
  follow_up_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up audit has been determined necessary to verify CAPA effectiveness or re-assess compliance after an unsatisfactory or needs_improvement outcome.']
  gcp_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version of the ICH GCP guideline applied during this audit (e.g., ICH E6(R2), ICH E6(R3)). Ensures traceability of the regulatory standard in force at the time of audit execution.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of regulatory inspection by trigger: routine (scheduled surveillance), for_cause (triggered by safety signal or complaint), pre_approval (NDA/BLA/MAA submission review), or post_approval (post-marketing commitment). Populated only for regulatory_inspection category audits.']
  location varchar [note: 'Modality of audit execution: on_site (auditor physically present at auditee location), remote (conducted via video conference and electronic document review), or hybrid (combination of both). Relevant for resource planning and post-COVID audit program management.']
  major_finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of major GCP findings identified during the audit. Major findings represent significant non-compliance that could affect subject rights, safety, or data reliability but are not immediately life-threatening.']
  minor_finding_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor GCP findings identified during the audit. Minor findings represent isolated deviations that do not significantly impact subject safety or data integrity but require corrective action.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit observations (non-findings) documented during the audit. Observations are areas for improvement that do not constitute formal GCP non-compliance but are noted for quality enhancement.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Overall quality determination issued at audit close: satisfactory (no significant findings), needs_improvement (minor findings requiring corrective action), or unsatisfactory (critical/major findings indicating systemic GCP non-compliance). Drives CAPA initiation and escalation decisions.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the audit plan document used to conduct this audit (e.g., v1.0, v2.1). Supports document control traceability and links to the approved audit plan in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date on which audit fieldwork is planned to conclude. Used to calculate planned audit duration and monitor schedule adherence.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled calendar date on which audit fieldwork is planned to commence, as documented in the annual audit plan or risk-based audit schedule. Used for resource planning and audit readiness tracking.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority conducting or overseeing the inspection (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Populated for regulatory_inspection category audits; nullable for internal audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health Canada|ANVISA|COFEPRIS — promote to reference product]']
  report_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the final audit report was formally issued to the auditee and sponsor. Triggers the CAPA response timeline and is a key metric for audit cycle time KPIs.']
  risk_priority_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk prioritization score (typically 1–100) calculated from risk factors such as subject safety criticality, protocol complexity, site performance history, and time since last audit. Drives risk-based audit scheduling per ICH E6(R2) R2 addendum requirements.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical risk classification derived from risk_priority_score: critical, high, medium, or low. Used for audit frequency determination, resource allocation, and escalation thresholds in the quality management system.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sponsor has been formally notified of the audit outcome and findings. Required for sponsor oversight obligations under ICH E6(R2) and FDA guidance on oversight of clinical investigations.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary narrative of the audit outcome, key findings, and overall GCP compliance assessment as documented in the final audit report. Supports audit readiness reviews and regulatory inspection preparation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title of the audit as recorded in MasterControl (e.g., \'GCP Site Audit – Site 1042 – Protocol XYZ-001\'). Used in audit registers, dashboards, and TMF filing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit record in MasterControl. Required for audit trail integrity under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data governance standards.']

  Note: 'Master record for all internal and external GCP audits conducted across studies, sites, systems, and vendors. Captures audit type (system, process, vendor, site, TMF), scope, lead auditor, planned and actual dates, audit status, sponsoring organization, and overall audit outcome (satisfactory, needs improvement, unsatisfactory). Includes risk-based audit prioritization score. Sourced from MasterControl audit management module. SSOT for audit identity and lifecycle within the compliance domain.'
}

Table compliance.audit_finding {
  audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each audit finding record in the compliance data product. Primary key for the audit_finding entity.']
  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.adam_dataset_spec. Business justification: Data management and biostatistics audits frequently cite ADaM dataset specification deficiencies — non-CDISC-compliant derivations, missing controlled terminology, incorrect variable definitions. Link']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent audit record from which this finding was generated. Links the finding to its originating GCP audit event.']
  bioanalytical_assay_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.bioanalytical_assay. Business justification: GLP/GCP audits of bioanalytical laboratories specifically review assay validation and method performance. audit_finding has implicated_system as a text field. Linking to the specific bioanalytical_ass']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record triggered by this finding, if applicable. A finding may initiate a formal CAPA lifecycle in MasterControl.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: audit_finding.sop_reference is a free-text string referencing the SOP implicated in the finding. Replacing it with a structured FK to sop.sop_id normalizes the relationship, enables JOIN-based SOP imp']
  deviation_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.deviation_classification. Business justification: audit_finding.protocol_deviation_classification is a free-text string that should reference the standardized deviation_classification taxonomy. Adding a FK to deviation_classification.compliance_devia']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Audit findings at investigator sites directly reference the site file being audited (missing documents, filing gaps). A QA expert expects this link to drive site file remediation actions and track whi']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study associated with this audit finding. Enables study-level quality metrics and regulatory traceability.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site implicated in this finding, if the finding is site-specific. Null for system-level or sponsor-level findings.']
  tlf_output_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.tlf_output. Business justification: Statistical audits cite specific TLF outputs as deficient — incorrect statistical methods applied, wrong population used, programming errors. Linking audit findings to the specific TLF output cited su']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Audit findings cite specific TMF documents as evidence of non-compliance. GCP audit reports require traceable references to the exact TMF document reviewed. Replaces denormalized plain-text tmf_docume']
  audit_report_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier or version reference of the formal audit report in which this finding was published. Links the finding to the official audit report stored in Veeva Vault eTMF.']
  auditee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual, department, or organizational unit that is the subject of the audit finding. Business-confidential reference for accountability tracking.']
  auditee_role varchar [note: 'Professional role or function of the auditee (e.g., Principal Investigator, Clinical Research Coordinator, Data Manager, Sponsor Representative). Supports workforce quality analytics.']
  auditor_organization varchar [note: 'Name of the organization to which the auditor belongs (e.g., internal QA department, sponsor QA team, third-party audit firm). Distinguishes internal from external audit sources.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding mandates the initiation of a formal CAPA process. Critical and major findings typically require CAPA; minor findings and observations may not.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date on which the QA team formally verified and closed the finding after confirming that the corrective action was implemented and effective. Null if the finding remains open.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this audit finding record was first created in MasterControl and ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail and ALCOA+ attributability requirements.']
  data_integrity_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the finding has a confirmed, potential, or no impact on clinical trial data integrity. Relevant to ALCOA+ principles and regulatory submission reliability.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'The date on which the finding was formally escalated. Null if escalation_flag is False. Used to measure escalation timeliness and response.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has been escalated to senior management, the sponsor, or a regulatory authority due to severity, recurrence, or patient safety risk.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the finding per GCP audit standards. Critical: systemic failure with patient safety or data integrity risk. Major: significant GCP deviation. Minor: low-risk procedural gap. Observation: noted for improvement without formal deviation.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the finding as documented by the auditor. Describes the observed deficiency, evidence reviewed, and the specific GCP or regulatory requirement that was not met.']
  finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the finding at the time of audit. Used in audit reports, management responses, and CAPA tracking. Format: AF-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the finding within the quality management workflow. Tracks progression from identification through resolution and closure verification.']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarizing the nature of the finding. Used in audit reports, dashboards, and management communications.']
  ich_gcp_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Specific clause or section of the ICH E6(R2) GCP guideline that was violated or is relevant to this finding (e.g., \'5.18.4\', \'4.9.0\'). Enables regulatory traceability and trend analysis by GCP requirement.']
  identified_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the finding was formally identified and documented during the audit. Represents the principal real-world event date for this transaction record.']
  implicated_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system or application implicated in the finding (e.g., Medidata Rave, Oracle Argus Safety, Veeva Vault, MasterControl). Relevant for system-level findings distinct from site-level findings.']
  management_response varchar [note: 'Full text of the auditee\'s formal management response to the finding, including root cause analysis, proposed corrective actions, and preventive measures. Captured verbatim from MasterControl.']
  patient_safety_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the finding has a confirmed, potential, or no impact on patient safety. Critical for risk-based quality management and escalation decisions.']
  prior_finding_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the previously identified finding that this record repeats, if repeat_finding_flag is True. Enables lineage tracking of recurring quality issues.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The clinical trial business process area implicated by the finding (e.g., Informed Consent Management, Adverse Event Reporting, Source Data Verification, Randomization, Drug Accountability). Enables process-level quality trending. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: informed_consent|adverse_event_reporting|source_data_verification|randomization|drug_accountability|protocol_deviation|data_management|site_monitoring|regulatory_submission|training_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  qa_reviewer_comment varchar [note: 'QA reviewer\'s assessment of the adequacy of the management response and corrective action. Documents acceptance, rejection, or request for additional information during the closure review cycle.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory authority whose requirements are cited in this finding. Enables jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting and inspection readiness tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|OHRP|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory citation from applicable authority (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 312.62, EMA Directive 2001/20/EC, EU CTR 536/2014 Article 58) that underpins the finding. Supports regulatory inspection readiness.']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified and closed finding at the same site, system, or process area. True signals potential systemic quality failure and escalation risk.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'The contractual or procedurally mandated deadline by which the auditee must submit a formal management response to the finding. Typically 14–30 days post-audit report issuance per SOP.']
  response_received_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the auditee\'s formal management response was received by the QA team. Used to calculate response timeliness and SLA compliance.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorized root cause of the finding as determined during root cause analysis (e.g., training gap, process failure, system error, resource constraint, communication breakdown). Enables systemic quality trend analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_gap|process_failure|system_error|resource_constraint|communication_breakdown|documentation_failure|oversight_gap — promote to reference product]']
  sop_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SOP in effect at the time the finding was identified. Critical for determining whether the deviation was against a current or superseded procedure.']
  subject_count_affected varchar [note: 'Number of study subjects potentially affected by the finding. Used to assess the scope and patient safety impact of the deficiency. Zero for system or process findings with no direct subject impact.']
  target_closure_date date [note: 'The planned date by which the finding is expected to be fully resolved and closed, as agreed between the QA team and the auditee. Distinct from response_due_date which covers only the management response.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this audit finding record. Tracks the last modification for change management, audit trail, and data lineage purposes.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the QA team to verify that the corrective action for this finding was effectively implemented prior to closure (e.g., document review, on-site visit, remote review, interview, system audit).']
