  Note: 'Transactional record of individual findings identified during an internal or sponsor-initiated GCP audit. Captures finding reference number, finding category (critical, major, minor, observation), finding description, ICH GCP clause reference, regulatory citation, affected process area, site or system implicated, management response, response due date, and finding status. Each finding is linked to a parent audit record and may trigger a CAPA. Distinct from inspection_finding: audit findings are internally generated, managed through internal quality processes, and do not carry formal regulatory response obligations. Sourced from MasterControl.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_inspection {
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the regulatory authority inspection record. Primary key for the inspection data product in the compliance domain.']
  country_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.country. Business justification: Regulatory inspections are country-specific events. Linking to the study country record enables country-level inspection history reporting, inspection readiness dashboards, and regulatory authority tr']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.investigational_site. Business justification: Regulatory inspections (FDA, EMA) target specific investigational sites. Linking compliance_inspection to investigational_site enables site inspection history tracking, repeat inspection flagging, and']
  principal_investigator_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.principal_investigator. Business justification: FDA and EMA routinely inspect principal investigators directly (Form FDA 482 directed at PI). Linking compliance_inspection to principal_investigator enables PI inspection history reporting, disqualif']
  prior_inspection_compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous inspection record that this inspection follows up on. Populated only when repeat_inspection is True, enabling inspection history chain analysis.']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Regulatory inspections are scoped to specific regulatory submissions/binders (e.g., NDA, IND inspection). Linking inspection to regulatory binder enables inspection scope definition and post-inspectio']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study record when the inspection is study-specific in scope. Null for systems-level inspections not tied to a single protocol.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory inspections reference TMF documents (inspection notifications, agency correspondence, responses). ICH GCP E6 mandates these as essential documents. A regulatory affairs expert expects this ']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the regulatory inspection concluded. Used to calculate actual inspection duration and compare against scheduled window.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the regulatory inspection commenced. May differ from the scheduled start date due to rescheduling or logistical changes.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether one or more Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plans are required as a result of inspection findings. When True, CAPA records must be created and tracked in MasterControl.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the inspected facility is located. Supports geographic reporting and logistics planning for inspection hosting.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Final regulatory classification assigned by the inspecting agency upon completion of the inspection. NAI (No Action Indicated) means no significant deviations found; VAI (Voluntary Action Indicated) means minor deviations requiring voluntary corrective action; OAI (Official Action Indicated) means significant deviations requiring mandatory regulatory action. Pending until the agency issues the final classification letter.']
  classification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority issued the final inspection classification (NAI/VAI/OAI) or equivalent outcome letter. Marks the start of any mandatory CAPA response timelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as critical severity by the inspecting agency. Critical findings indicate conditions that may directly impact subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance and typically require immediate corrective action.']
  form_483_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the FDA issued a Form 483 (Inspectional Observations) at the close of the inspection. Applicable only to FDA inspections. True if a Form 483 was issued; False otherwise.']
  inspected_entity_type varchar [note: 'Category of the organization or entity being inspected. Distinguishes between sponsor-level, Contract Research Organization (CRO)-level, investigational site-level, central laboratory, or Institutional Review Board (IRB)/Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) inspections.']
  inspecting_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory health authority conducting the inspection (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, Health Canada, TGA). Determines applicable regulatory framework and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|Health Canada|TGA|ANVISA|COFEPRIS|Other — promote to reference product]']
  inspection_mode varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection was conducted physically on-site at the inspected facility, remotely via electronic document review and virtual meetings, or as a hybrid combination of both modalities.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned or internally-generated unique alphanumeric reference number for the inspection, used for cross-referencing with regulatory agency correspondence and the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  inspection_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the breadth of the inspection — whether it is limited to a specific clinical study, covers broader quality systems and processes, or focuses on a specific compliance area such as data integrity or safety reporting.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record, from initial notification through final classification and closure. Drives audit readiness workflows and CAPA tracking in MasterControl.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection based on the regulatory agency\'s rationale for initiating it. Routine inspections are scheduled as part of standard oversight; For-Cause inspections are triggered by specific concerns or signals; Pre-Approval (PAI) inspections occur prior to NDA/BLA/MAA approval decisions.']
  inspector_count varchar [note: 'Total number of regulatory inspectors participating in the inspection team from the health authority. Informs resource planning for inspection hosting and document preparation.']
  lead_inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary regulatory inspector assigned by the health authority to lead the inspection. Used for correspondence tracking and relationship management with the inspecting agency.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as major severity by the inspecting agency. Major findings represent significant deviations from GCP or regulatory requirements that could potentially affect subject safety or data reliability.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of findings classified as minor or other severity by the inspecting agency. Minor findings are deviations that are unlikely to have a significant impact on subject safety or data integrity but require corrective action.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, internal observations, or qualitative commentary about the inspection that is not captured in structured fields. Supports audit readiness documentation.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally notified the sponsor, CRO, or site of the upcoming inspection. Critical for calculating preparation lead time and triggering audit readiness activities.']
  preliminary_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of preliminary observations or findings communicated by the inspectors during or immediately after the inspection (e.g., via a Form FDA 483 or equivalent). Provides an early indicator of inspection severity before final classification.']
  quality_system_area varchar [note: 'Specific quality system area or functional domain covered by the inspection scope (e.g., Clinical Operations, Data Management, Pharmacovigilance, Regulatory Affairs, Laboratory Operations). Enables trend analysis of inspection focus areas across the organization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Clinical Operations|Data Management|Pharmacovigilance|Regulatory Affairs|Laboratory|Supply Chain|Quality Assurance|Biostatistics — promote to reference product]']
  repeat_inspection boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection is a follow-up or repeat inspection of the same entity by the same regulatory authority, typically triggered by unresolved findings from a prior inspection. True if this is a repeat inspection.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority issued the formal inspection report (e.g., Establishment Inspection Report for FDA, or GCP Inspection Report for EMA) to the inspected organization.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which the inspected organization must submit a formal written response to the regulatory authority addressing all inspection findings and outlining planned or completed corrective actions.']
  response_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the formal written response to the inspection findings was submitted to the regulatory authority. Compared against response_due_date to assess timeliness of regulatory response.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the inspection window as communicated by the regulatory authority. Together with scheduled_start_date defines the expected inspection duration.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the on-site or remote inspection as communicated by the regulatory authority in the inspection notification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection record. Supports change tracking and audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  warning_letter_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the regulatory authority issued a formal Warning Letter or equivalent enforcement communication following the inspection. A Warning Letter signals serious GCP non-compliance and triggers mandatory regulatory response.']

  Note: 'Master record for regulatory authority inspections conducted by agencies such as FDA, EMA, PMDA, MHRA, or Health Canada at sponsor, CRO, or investigational site level. Captures inspection type (routine, for-cause, pre-approval), inspecting agency, inspection scope (study-specific or systems), lead inspector name, notification date, inspection window, and final classification (no action indicated, voluntary action indicated, official action indicated). Distinct from internal audits — inspections are externally initiated by health authorities.'
}

Table compliance.inspection_finding {
  inspection_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual regulatory inspection finding record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete regulatory compliance event with its own lifecycle, formal response obligations, and escalation pathway.']
  adam_dataset_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.adam_dataset_spec. Business justification: FDA/EMA inspections routinely cite ADaM dataset specification deficiencies (Pinnacle21 validation failures, non-standard derivation algorithms, define.xml inconsistencies). Linking inspection findings']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record in MasterControl that was initiated to address this inspection finding. A single CAPA may address multiple findings; this field links the finding to its primary CAPA for lifecycle tracking.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent regulatory inspection record under which this finding was issued. Links the finding to the overarching inspection event, authority, and scope.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: inspection_finding.sop_reference is a free-text string identifying the SOP implicated in the regulatory finding. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id normalizes the relationship, enabling structured a']
  deviation_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.deviation_classification. Business justification: Inspection findings issued by regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA) are classified using the same standardized deviation classification taxonomy used for protocol deviations. inspection_finding.find']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Regulatory inspection findings (FDA Form 483, EMA findings) cite specific lab results as evidence of GCP violations. inspection_finding has data_integrity_impact. Linking to the specific lab_result is']
  prior_finding_inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the earlier inspection finding that this finding repeats or is related to. Populated only when repeat_finding is True. Enables lineage tracking of recurring deficiencies across inspection cycles.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: inspection_finding has a boolean flag protocol_deviation_related (BOOLEAN) indicating that the finding is related to a protocol deviation. This boolean should be backed by a proper FK to the specific ']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: FDA/EMA inspection findings frequently cite SAP deficiencies — inadequate multiplicity adjustment, missing sensitivity analyses, non-ICH E9(R1) estimand framework compliance. Linking inspection findin']
  site_file_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.site_file. Business justification: Regulatory inspection findings at investigator sites reference the site file under inspection. A regulatory affairs expert expects this link to track which site file deficiencies generated inspection ']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) affected by this inspection finding. Enables cross-domain linkage between compliance findings and study-level data.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site at which the inspection finding was identified. Null if the finding applies at the sponsor or CRO level rather than a specific site.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory inspection findings (FDA Form 483, EMA findings) reference specific TMF documents as evidence. Inspectors cite exact documents; this FK replaces the plain-text tmf_document_reference, enabl']
  affected_process_area varchar [note: 'The specific clinical trial process or functional area implicated by the finding (e.g., \'Informed Consent Management\', \'SAE Reporting\', \'Investigational Product Accountability\', \'Source Data Verification\', \'Protocol Deviation Management\'). Used for root cause analysis and SOP compliance tracking.']
  affected_subject_count varchar [note: 'Number of study subjects (patients) directly impacted by the deficiency identified in this inspection finding. Used to assess the scope of subject safety risk and to prioritize remediation efforts.']
  audit_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether all required documentation, CAPA evidence, and response materials for this finding have been compiled and are ready for re-inspection or follow-up audit review. Supports inspection readiness programs and executive reporting.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal CAPA is required to address this inspection finding. Critical findings and major findings typically mandate a CAPA; \'other\' findings may be addressed through a written commitment without a full CAPA.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally accepted the response and closed the inspection finding. Null if the finding remains open or under review. Used for regulatory commitment tracking and audit readiness metrics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection finding record was first created in the system (MasterControl or Databricks Silver Layer). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  data_integrity_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the finding compromises the integrity of clinical trial data submitted or to be submitted to regulatory authorities. \'Confirmed\' data integrity findings may invalidate study data and affect regulatory submissions. Aligns with ALCOA+ principles.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection finding has escalated to a formal regulatory enforcement action. \'Warning_letter\' indicates FDA has issued a Warning Letter. \'Consent_decree\' indicates a legal agreement with FDA. \'Import_alert\' indicates an FDA import restriction. \'Clinical_hold\' indicates FDA has placed a clinical hold on the study. Null or \'none\' if no escalation has occurred.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'Thematic or process category to which the finding belongs, used for trend analysis and CAPA prioritization (e.g., Informed Consent, Adverse Event Reporting, Protocol Compliance, Data Integrity, Investigational Product Management, Monitoring, Regulatory Submissions). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: informed_consent|adverse_event_reporting|protocol_compliance|data_integrity|investigational_product|monitoring|regulatory_submissions|subject_safety|record_keeping|staff_training — promote to reference product]']
  finding_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory severity classification of the inspection finding as assigned by the inspecting authority. \'Critical\' indicates a condition that poses significant risk to subject safety or data integrity and may result in a Warning Letter or consent decree. \'Major\' indicates a significant deficiency requiring corrective action. \'Other\' (also termed \'minor\' or \'observation\' by some authorities) indicates a lesser deficiency. Aligns with EMA GCP inspection classification and FDA enforcement terminology.']
  finding_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory authority formally issued or documented this finding during the inspection. This is the principal real-world event date (TRANSACTION_HEADER: BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP at date granularity), distinct from the inspection start date and the response deadline.']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the inspection finding as documented by the regulatory authority, including the specific deficiency observed, the regulatory standard violated, and any factual basis cited (e.g., verbatim text from FDA Form 483 observation or EMA GCP inspection report).']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Externally-assigned sequential identifier for this finding within the inspection, as issued by the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA Form 483 observation number, EMA GCP inspection finding reference). Serves as the business-facing identifier for regulatory correspondence and response tracking.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection finding from issuance through resolution. \'Open\' indicates the finding has been issued and awaits a formal response. \'Response_submitted\' indicates the sponsor/CRO has submitted a written response. \'Under_review\' indicates the authority is evaluating the response. \'Closed\' indicates the authority has accepted the response and closed the finding. \'Escalated\' indicates the finding has progressed to a Warning Letter, consent decree, or equivalent enforcement action.']
  finding_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title or headline summarizing the nature of the inspection finding, as recorded in MasterControl. Used for executive dashboards and regulatory commitment registers.']
  gcp_principle_violated varchar [note: 'The specific ICH GCP principle or section that the finding determines was violated (e.g., \'ICH E6(R2) 4.8 - Informed Consent\', \'ICH E6(R2) 5.18 - Monitoring\', \'ICH E6(R2) 6.4 - Protocol Amendments\'). Supports regulatory submission quality and inspection readiness.']
  inspection_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority that issued the inspection finding (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Determines the applicable response format, deadline, and escalation pathway. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|ANVISA|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_finding_source varchar [note: 'The type of regulatory document in which this finding was formally issued. \'Form_483\' refers to FDA Inspectional Observations. \'Warning_letter\' refers to an FDA Warning Letter. \'EIR\' refers to an FDA Establishment Inspection Report. \'GCP_inspection_report\' refers to an EMA or MHRA GCP inspection report.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory inspector or lead inspector who identified and documented this finding. Recorded for correspondence and response addressing purposes. Classified as confidential as it identifies a government official in a regulatory context.']
  protocol_deviation_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection finding is directly related to a protocol deviation or series of protocol deviations. Enables cross-domain linkage between compliance findings and protocol deviation management.']
  record_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual (typically a QA manager or Regulatory Affairs lead) responsible for managing the response and resolution of this inspection finding within the sponsor or CRO organization. Supports accountability tracking in MasterControl.']
  regulatory_citation varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory reference cited by the inspecting authority as the basis for the finding (e.g., \'21 CFR 312.62(b)\', \'ICH E6(R2) Section 4.9.0\', \'EU CTR 536/2014 Article 47\'). Critical for formal response drafting and legal review.']
  repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a recurrence of a previously identified deficiency at the same site or sponsor level. Repeat findings signal systemic quality failures and may trigger escalated regulatory enforcement action.']
  response_adequacy_assessment varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority\'s assessment of whether the sponsor\'s formal response to the finding is adequate. \'Adequate\' results in finding closure. \'Inadequate\' or \'Partially_adequate\' requires a supplemental response. \'Pending_review\' indicates the authority has not yet issued its assessment.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Formal deadline by which the sponsor or CRO must submit a written response to the regulatory authority. For FDA Form 483 observations, this is typically 15 business days from the last day of inspection. For EMA GCP findings, deadlines vary by classification. Drives executive-level regulatory commitment management.']
  response_submitted_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the formal written response to the inspection finding was submitted to the regulatory authority. Used to calculate response timeliness and compliance with regulatory deadlines.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the root cause to enable trend analysis across findings. \'Process\' indicates a procedural gap; \'Training\' indicates inadequate staff training; \'System\' indicates an EDC, CTMS, or IT system failure; \'Communication\' indicates a breakdown in information flow; \'Resource\' indicates insufficient staffing or budget; \'Oversight\' indicates inadequate monitoring or QA oversight.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Documented root cause of the deficiency identified in the inspection finding, as determined through the CAPA investigation process. Supports systemic quality improvement and recurrence prevention.']
  sponsor_response_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the sponsor\'s or CRO\'s formal written response to the inspection finding, including the root cause identified, immediate corrective actions taken, and preventive measures committed. Stored in MasterControl and linked to the eTMF in Veeva Vault.']
  subject_safety_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the finding has a direct or potential impact on subject safety. \'None\' indicates no subject safety risk. \'Potential\' indicates a theoretical risk that has not materialized. \'Confirmed\' indicates documented harm or risk to enrolled subjects.']
  supplemental_response_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for submission of a supplemental or follow-up response when the initial response was assessed as inadequate or partially adequate by the regulatory authority. Null if no supplemental response is required.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this inspection finding record. Supports audit trail requirements and change tracking under 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+ principles.']
  warning_letter_reference varchar [note: 'Official reference number of the FDA Warning Letter or equivalent regulatory enforcement communication issued in connection with this finding. Populated only when escalation_status is \'warning_letter\'. Used for regulatory commitment tracking and legal response management.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual findings issued by a regulatory authority during a formal inspection. Captures finding number, regulatory citation (e.g., FDA Form 483 observation, EMA GCP non-compliance), finding classification (critical, major, other), detailed description, affected study or site, formal response deadline, and resolution status. Linked to the parent inspection record. Distinct from audit_finding: inspection findings are issued by external health authorities, carry formal legal response obligations (e.g., FDA 483 response within 15 business days), and may escalate to warning letters or consent decrees. Regulatory inspection findings require executive-level response and are tracked separately for regulatory commitment management.'
}

Table compliance.capa {
  capa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the CAPA record. Primary key for the CAPA lifecycle master record sourced from MasterControl CAPA module.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal or external audit that generated the finding triggering this CAPA. Null if the CAPA was not initiated from an audit. Enables audit-to-CAPA traceability.']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: A CAPA can be initiated directly from a regulatory inspection (source_type=\'inspection\'). While inspection_finding->compliance_capa links findings to CAPAs, a CAPA may also reference the inspection it']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: capa.sop_reference is a free-text string identifying the SOP associated with the CAPA. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id enables structured linkage between CAPAs and the SOPs they address or update']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: CAPAs arising from statistical audit or inspection findings require SAP amendments as corrective actions. Linking the CAPA to the SAP it corrects enables tracking of authority-mandated or QA-driven st']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical study associated with this CAPA. Links the CAPA to the relevant study protocol for study-level quality tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the investigational site associated with this CAPA, if site-specific. Null for sponsor-level or cross-site CAPAs. Supports site-level quality performance analytics.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: CAPAs produce corrective action documents (SOP revisions, training records, investigation reports) that must be filed in the TMF per GCP. A QA expert expects this link to verify CAPA closure documenta']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual calendar date on which all corrective and preventive actions were completed and verified. Null until CAPA is closed. Used to calculate cycle time and on-time closure rate KPIs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the CAPA plan was formally approved by the authorized QA approver in MasterControl. Marks the transition from initiation to active execution phase.']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the action is corrective (addressing an existing non-conformance), preventive (addressing a potential non-conformance), or both. Drives workflow routing in MasterControl.']
  closure_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative justification provided by the responsible owner and QA approver at the time of CAPA closure, summarizing evidence that all actions were completed and the root cause was resolved. Required for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  compliance_capa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the CAPA record. Drives workflow routing, escalation triggers, and regulatory reporting. \'Overdue\' is system-set when target completion date is passed without closure.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions planned or implemented to address the identified root cause and prevent recurrence of the quality issue. Core CAPA deliverable per GCP requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the CAPA record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  data_integrity_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality issue underlying this CAPA had or could have had an impact on clinical data integrity. Triggers data review, potential database query, and regulatory notification workflows.']
  effectiveness_check_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date on which the effectiveness of the implemented corrective and preventive actions will be or was verified. Required by GCP to confirm the CAPA resolved the root cause.']
  effectiveness_check_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the methodology and findings from the effectiveness check, including evidence reviewed and conclusions drawn. Supports regulatory inspection readiness.']
  effectiveness_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the effectiveness verification assessment confirming whether the implemented CAPA actions successfully resolved the root cause and prevented recurrence. Drives CAPA closure or re-opening decisions.']
  gcp_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the impact of the underlying quality issue on GCP compliance and data integrity. Drives CAPA priority, escalation, and regulatory reporting decisions. Aligned with FDA and EMA GCP inspection classification standards.']
  immediate_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any immediate containment or interim actions taken at the time of CAPA initiation to mitigate the impact of the quality issue before the full corrective action is implemented.']
  initiation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the CAPA was formally initiated and entered into MasterControl. Serves as the start of the CAPA lifecycle clock for overdue tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  mastercontrol_record_number varchar [note: 'Native record identifier assigned by the MasterControl CAPA module. Used for source system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  preventive_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the preventive actions planned to eliminate the potential for recurrence of similar quality issues across the organization or study portfolio.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Risk-based priority level assigned to the CAPA, reflecting the severity and urgency of the underlying quality issue. Drives escalation timelines and resource allocation.']
  problem_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the quality issue, non-conformance, or finding that triggered the CAPA. Captures the \'what happened\' narrative for audit trail and regulatory inspection purposes.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAPA addresses a recurring quality issue that has been the subject of a prior CAPA. True signals a systemic quality problem requiring escalated management attention.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable CAPA reference number assigned by MasterControl upon initiation (e.g., CAPA-2024-00123). Used in audit trails, regulatory submissions, and sponsor communications.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAPA is associated with a finding that requires regulatory reporting to FDA, EMA, MHRA, or other health authority. Drives regulatory submission workflow in Veeva Vault.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional area accountable for CAPA execution (e.g., Clinical Operations, Data Management, Regulatory Affairs, Quality Assurance). Used for departmental quality KPI reporting.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized category classifying the root cause of the quality issue (e.g., training deficiency, process gap, system failure, communication breakdown, resource constraint). Used for trend analysis and systemic quality improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_deficiency|process_gap|system_failure|communication_breakdown|resource_constraint|documentation_error|oversight_failure — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the identified root cause of the quality issue, as determined through root cause analysis (RCA). Supports regulatory inspection readiness and CAPA justification.']
  source_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating record that triggered this CAPA (e.g., audit finding ID, inspection finding ID, protocol deviation ID, or QC failure ID). Enables traceability back to the root triggering event.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Category of the triggering event that initiated the CAPA. Classifies whether the CAPA originated from an internal audit, regulatory inspection, protocol deviation, QC failure, self-identified quality issue, complaint, or safety signal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: audit_finding|inspection_finding|protocol_deviation|qc_failure|self_identified|complaint|safety_signal — promote to reference product]']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the study sponsor was formally notified of this CAPA. Null if sponsor_notified_flag is False. Supports sponsor oversight audit trail.']
  sponsor_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this CAPA. Required for CRO-managed studies where sponsor oversight obligations apply per ICH E6(R2) GCP.']
  subject_safety_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality issue underlying this CAPA had or could have had a direct impact on subject safety. Triggers expedited review and potential safety reporting obligations.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which all corrective and preventive actions must be completed and the CAPA closed. Used for overdue status calculation, escalation triggers, and sponsor/regulatory reporting.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the quality issue or finding that triggered the CAPA. Used for identification in dashboards, reports, and audit-readiness reviews.']
  training_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective or preventive action includes a mandatory training component. When True, triggers training assignment workflow in MasterControl training management module.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp recording when the CAPA record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports change tracking, audit trail integrity, and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA) initiated in response to audit findings, inspection findings, protocol deviations, QC failures, or self-identified quality issues. Captures CAPA reference number, root cause category, root cause description, corrective action plan, preventive action plan, responsible owner, target completion date, actual completion date, effectiveness check date, and CAPA status (open, in-progress, closed, overdue). Sourced from MasterControl CAPA module. SSOT for CAPA lifecycle.'
}

Table compliance.capa_action_item {
  capa_action_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual action item within a CAPA plan. Primary key for this transactional line record. Entity role: TRANSACTION_LINE — each action item is a discrete line within a parent CAPA plan header, with its own owner, due date, and completion lifecycle.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent CAPA plan to which this action item belongs. A single CAPA plan may contain multiple discrete action items tracked independently. Serves as the HEADER_REFERENCE for this TRANSACTION_LINE entity.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: capa_action_item.related_sop_number is a free-text string referencing the SOP relevant to the action item. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id normalizes the relationship, enabling structured trackin']
  action_item_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this action item within MasterControl (e.g., CAPA-AI-2024-0042). Used in audit trails, regulatory inspection responses, and cross-system references. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this line record.']
  action_item_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the action item within the CAPA execution lifecycle. Drives CAPA closure eligibility — all action items must reach \'completed\' or \'cancelled\' before the parent CAPA can be closed. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|completed|overdue|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  action_item_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the action item by its nature within the CAPA framework: corrective (addresses the identified nonconformance), preventive (prevents recurrence), effectiveness check (verifies the CAPA resolved the issue), root cause analysis, training, process change, or documentation update. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corrective|preventive|effectiveness_check|root_cause_analysis|training|process_change|documentation_update — promote to reference product]']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the action item was actually completed and evidence was submitted. Compared against planned_completion_date to measure CAPA execution timeliness. Null until the action item reaches \'completed\' status.']
  assigned_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional unit of the assigned owner (e.g., Quality Assurance, Data Management, Clinical Operations). Used for workload analysis, departmental CAPA metrics, and escalation routing.']
  capa_action_item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the specific action to be taken, including scope, methodology, and expected outcome. Provides the full context required for the assigned owner to execute the action and for auditors to assess adequacy of the response.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this action item was formally closed in MasterControl following successful completion and verification. Null until the item reaches a terminal state (completed or cancelled). Used to calculate cycle time metrics.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, progress updates, or contextual information entered by the action item owner or QA reviewer during the execution lifecycle. Supports ongoing communication and audit trail within MasterControl.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this action item record was first created in MasterControl. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this entity. Supports ALCOA+ auditability and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic record requirements.']
  days_open varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days this action item has been open, calculated from opened_timestamp to either actual_completion_date (if closed) or the current date (if still open). Used as a KPI for CAPA execution timeliness and audit readiness dashboards. Note: this is a business-captured operational metric from MasterControl, not a derived aggregate.']
  due_date_extension_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the planned completion date has been extended for this action item. A high extension count is a quality risk indicator and may trigger escalation or QA review. Tracked per MasterControl audit trail requirements.']
  effectiveness_check_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the effectiveness check for this action item must be completed. Null if effectiveness_check_required is False. Used to schedule and track post-implementation reviews.']
  effectiveness_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-completion effectiveness check is required for this action item to confirm the corrective or preventive action achieved its intended outcome. Typically mandated for critical or repeat findings per GCP quality standards.']
  effectiveness_check_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the post-completion effectiveness check for this action item. \'Effective\' confirms the action resolved the root cause; \'ineffective\' triggers re-opening of the CAPA or creation of a new CAPA.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date on which this action item was formally escalated. Null if escalation_flag is False. Used to measure escalation response time and management accountability.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action item has been escalated to senior management or QA leadership due to overdue status, repeated extensions, or high risk. Triggers escalation notifications and management review workflows in MasterControl.']
  evidence_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the evidence provided to demonstrate completion of the action item (e.g., \'Updated SOP-QA-012 v3.0 attached; training records for 12 staff members uploaded\'). Supports QA verification and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  evidence_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or URL to the supporting evidence document stored in Veeva Vault or MasterControl (e.g., document ID, eTMF reference, SOP version number). Links the action item completion to the formal document management system.']
  extension_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation provided by the action item owner or QA approver when the planned completion date is extended. Required for audit trail completeness and regulatory inspection readiness. Null if no extension has been granted.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this action item record in MasterControl. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for this entity. Required for change tracking and audit trail completeness per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  mastercontrol_record_number varchar [note: 'Native record identifier assigned by MasterControl to this action item in the source system. Used for cross-system traceability, data lineage, and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver layer and the MasterControl operational system of record.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this action item was formally opened and assigned to an owner. Serves as the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this TRANSACTION_LINE entity. Distinct from created_timestamp, which reflects system record creation.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date by which the action item is expected to be completed, as agreed upon during CAPA planning. Used to calculate overdue status, track CAPA execution timeliness, and report on compliance with committed timelines to sponsors and regulatory bodies.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to this action item, reflecting urgency and risk impact. Critical and high-priority items are subject to expedited review and escalation procedures. Drives resource allocation and monitoring frequency.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority to which this action item represents a formal commitment, if regulatory_commitment is True (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA). Null if this is an internal CAPA action item without a regulatory commitment dimension. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_commitment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this action item represents a formal commitment made to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) during an inspection or in a regulatory submission response. Regulatory commitment items are subject to heightened monitoring and escalation.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this action item within the parent CAPA plan (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Enables ordered display and reporting of action items within a CAPA. Serves as the LINE_SEQUENCE for this TRANSACTION_LINE entity.']
  source_finding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the specific audit finding, inspection observation, protocol deviation, or nonconformance that this action item directly addresses (e.g., audit finding number, deviation ID). Provides traceability from action item back to the originating quality event.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title summarizing the action item (e.g., \'Revise SOP-QA-012 to include double-check step\'). Used in dashboards, reports, and inspection-ready summaries within MasterControl.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which all required training associated with this action item was completed and documented in MasterControl. Null if training_required is False or training has not yet been completed. Required as part of completion evidence.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this action item requires associated staff training (e.g., training on a revised SOP). When True, training completion records in MasterControl must be linked as evidence before the action item can be verified.']
  verification_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments entered by the QA verifier during the evidence review, including rationale for rejection or conditions for acceptance. Provides the audit trail narrative required for regulatory inspection readiness.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the QA verifier confirmed the completion evidence was adequate and the action item was satisfactorily executed. Null until verification is completed. Required for regulatory inspection audit trail.']
  verification_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the QA verification review of the action item completion evidence. \'Accepted\' closes the verification loop; \'rejected\' returns the item to the owner for rework; \'pending_revision\' indicates partial acceptance with required amendments.']
  verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether independent QA verification of completion evidence is required before this action item can be marked as completed. True for high-risk or critical action items per the CAPA procedure. Drives the QA review workflow in MasterControl.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of individual action items within a CAPA plan. Each CAPA may have multiple discrete action items assigned to different owners with independent due dates and completion statuses. Captures action item description, assigned owner, planned completion date, actual completion date, evidence of completion, and action item status. Enables granular tracking of CAPA execution progress within MasterControl.'
}

Table compliance.protocol_deviation {
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for protocol_deviation']
  activation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.activation. Business justification: Protocol deviations occur within a specific site activation period. Linking protocol_deviation to activation enables deviation trending by activation cohort, root-cause analysis tied to activation qua']
  adverse_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.adverse_event. Business justification: Protocol deviations are frequently caused by or documented in response to adverse events (e.g., dose interruption, visit window violation due to AE). Linking the causative AE to the deviation is requi']
  analysis_population_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.analysis_population. Business justification: Protocol deviations directly determine per-protocol population exclusions (subject_excluded_from_pp_flag exists on protocol_deviation). Linking to the specific analysis population is fundamental for s']
  arm_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.arm. Business justification: Per-arm deviation analysis is required for unblinding risk assessment and per-protocol population analysis. DSMB reports and regulatory submissions stratify deviations by treatment arm. This link enab']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated CAPA record in MasterControl, if a corrective and preventive action has been initiated in response to this deviation.']
  deviation_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.deviation_classification. Business justification: protocol_deviation has deviation_category and deviation_type as free-text strings. The deviation_classification table is the reference master defining the standardized classification taxonomy. Adding ']
  dosing_regimen_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.dosing_regimen. Business justification: Dosing deviations (wrong dose level, frequency, route) are a critical deviation category requiring linkage to the specific regimen. PK/PD analysis and per-protocol population exclusion decisions depen']
  eligibility_criterion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.eligibility_criterion. Business justification: Eligibility criterion violations are the most common protocol deviation type. Regulatory submissions require per-criterion deviation counts for inclusion/exclusion analysis. Linking deviation to the s']
  estimand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.estimand. Business justification: ICH E9(R1) requires deviations to be assessed against estimand strategies (intercurrent events, missing data). Linking protocol deviations to the affected estimand is a regulatory requirement for subm']
  lab_result_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_result. Business justification: Lab results that are missing, out-of-range, or collected outside protocol windows trigger protocol deviations. Linking the specific lab_result that caused the deviation is required for deviation root ']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: Protocol deviations are violations of a specific protocol version. Direct FK to protocol enables deviation analysis by protocol version, supports CSR deviation summaries, and is required for regulator']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: Protocol deviations must be assessed against the governing SAP to determine PP/ITT population exclusions and statistical impact. The SAP version in effect at deviation occurrence drives whether subjec']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.shipment. Business justification: Shipment failures (delayed delivery causing missed dose window, wrong kits shipped) are a documented category of protocol deviations. Linking protocol_deviation to the causative shipment enables root ']
  specimen_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.specimen_collection. Business justification: Specimen collection errors (wrong tube, wrong time, insufficient volume) are among the most frequent protocol deviation types. specimen_collection has has_deviation and deviation_description flags. Li']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: Protocol deviations directly impact specific statistical endpoints — a missed primary endpoint assessment is a critical deviation affecting the primary analysis. Linking deviations to the affected sta']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study (protocol) under which this deviation occurred. Links to the study master record.']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: ICH E6 R2 requires deviations to reference the exact protocol amendment version in effect at occurrence. Regulatory submissions and CSR deviation listings must cite the amendment version. protocol_ver']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the protocol deviation was identified or occurred.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Randomization errors (wrong stratification, eligibility violation, re-randomization) are a primary category of protocol deviations. Regulatory submissions and CSR deviation listings require direct lin']
  subject_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the monitoring visit during which this deviation was identified, if applicable. Links to the monitoring visit record for traceability.']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Protocol deviations must be documented in the TMF per ICH E6(R2). The deviation record references the specific TMF document (e.g., deviation report, PI notification letter). Replaces plain-text tmf_do']
  trial_subject_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the study subject (patient) associated with this deviation, if applicable. Nullable for site-level or system-level deviations not tied to a specific subject.']
  visit_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.visit_procedure. Business justification: Deviations are frequently tied to a specific scheduled procedure (missed PK sample, wrong assessment performed). Procedure-level deviation tracking supports SDV, monitoring reports, and SDTM IE domain']
  audit_readiness_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the current audit readiness state of this deviation record, confirming that all required documentation, reporting, and CAPA activities are complete and the record is inspection-ready.']
  corrective_action_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of the immediate corrective actions taken to address the deviation and prevent recurrence, prior to or independent of a formal CAPA. Captures site-level remediation steps.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the protocol deviation record was first created in the source system (CTMS or EDC). Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements.']
  data_integrity_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the deviation compromised the integrity, reliability, or interpretability of study data. Informs decisions on data exclusion, sensitivity analyses, or database lock.']
  detected_by varchar [note: 'The method or source by which the deviation was detected. Indicates whether it was found during CRA monitoring visit, site self-identification, sponsor audit, regulatory inspection, central data review, or subject report. Used for monitoring effectiveness metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cra_monitoring|site_self_identified|sponsor_audit|regulatory_inspection|data_review|subject_report|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  deviation_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the deviation by severity and regulatory impact. \'Major\' deviations directly affect subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance. \'Minor\' deviations have minimal impact. \'Important\' is used per ICH E3/EMA guidance for deviations affecting subject rights, safety, or data reliability.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the protocol deviation, including what protocol requirement was not met, the circumstances under which it occurred, and any immediate actions taken. Sourced from the eCRF or CTMS deviation entry.']
  deviation_ref_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the protocol deviation record, typically generated by the CTMS (Oracle Siebel CTMS) or EDC (Medidata Rave). Used for cross-system traceability and regulatory correspondence.']
  deviation_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow lifecycle state of the deviation record. \'Open\' indicates newly identified; \'under_review\' indicates active investigation; \'resolved\' indicates corrective action completed; \'closed\' indicates fully adjudicated and archived; \'escalated\' indicates elevated to sponsor or regulatory authority; \'voided\' indicates entered in error.']
  deviation_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the deviation describing which protocol requirement was violated. Common types include: eligibility, dosing, visit_window, prohibited_medication, consent, randomization, assessments, sample_collection, concomitant_medication, data_collection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eligibility|dosing|visit_window|prohibited_medication|consent|randomization|assessments|sample_collection|concomitant_medication|data_collection — promote to reference product]']
  discovery_date date [note: 'The date on which the protocol deviation was first identified or discovered, which may differ from the occurrence date. Used to calculate detection lag and assess monitoring effectiveness.']
  ecrf_form_ref varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific eCRF form or data point in Medidata Rave EDC that is associated with this deviation, enabling direct traceability to the source data record.']
  include_in_csr_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be listed or summarized in the Clinical Study Report (CSR) per ICH E3 requirements. Major and important deviations are typically included.']
  irb_iec_reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was formally submitted to the IRB/IEC. Used to verify compliance with reporting timelines and to support audit readiness.']
  irb_iec_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) per applicable regulations and the IRB/IEC-approved protocol.']
  occurrence_date date [note: 'The date on which the protocol deviation actually occurred (i.e., the date the protocol requirement was not met). This is the principal real-world event date per CDISC SDTM DVSTDTC.']
  pi_notified_date date [note: 'The date on which the Principal Investigator (PI) was formally notified of the deviation. Required for GCP compliance and site management accountability.']
  protocol_requirement_ref varchar [note: 'Specific section, clause, or criterion number within the study protocol that was deviated from (e.g., \'Section 5.3 Inclusion Criterion 2\'). Enables precise traceability to the protocol document.']
  regulatory_authority_reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was formally submitted to the applicable regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, PMDA, etc.), where regulatory reporting was required.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA) as part of IND safety reporting or clinical trial notification obligations.']
  repeat_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation is a recurrence of a previously identified deviation of the same type at the same site or by the same subject. Used for trend analysis and escalation decisions.']
  reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was formally reported to the sponsor, IRB/IEC, or regulatory authority as required. Used to assess compliance with reporting timelines.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was resolved and corrective actions were confirmed as complete. Marks the end of the active deviation lifecycle.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Categorized root cause of the deviation as determined during investigation. Common categories include: training_gap, communication_failure, process_gap, system_error, oversight, workload, protocol_complexity, subject_non_compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_gap|communication_failure|process_gap|system_error|oversight|workload|protocol_complexity|subject_non_compliance — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the identified root cause of the protocol deviation, as documented during the investigation process. Supports CAPA development and trend analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this deviation record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver layer lakehouse.']
  source_system_record_number varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Medidata Rave deviation ID or Oracle Siebel CTMS deviation record ID). Enables upstream reconciliation and audit trail.']
  sponsor_reported_date date [note: 'The date on which the deviation was formally communicated to the sponsor. Used to verify compliance with sponsor notification timelines per the Clinical Trial Agreement.']
  sponsor_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation must be formally reported to the study sponsor per the Clinical Trial Agreement and sponsor SOPs.']
  subject_excluded_from_pp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation resulted in the exclusion of the subject from the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population, as determined by the biostatistics team per the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP).']
  subject_impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the deviation had an impact on subject safety, rights, or well-being. Used to determine reporting obligations and CAPA urgency.']
  tmf_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the deviation documentation filed in the electronic Trial Master File (eTMF) in Veeva Vault. Enables cross-system document traceability for inspection readiness.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the protocol deviation record. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail and change tracking requirements.']
  visit_name varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the study visit (e.g., \'Week 4 Visit\', \'Screening\') during which the deviation occurred, as defined in the protocol schedule of assessments.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of protocol deviations identified during study conduct. Captures deviation reference number, study and site identifiers, subject identifier (if applicable), deviation category (major, minor, important), deviation type (eligibility, dosing, visit window, prohibited medication, consent), description of the deviation, date of occurrence, date of discovery, reporting obligation (IRB/IEC, sponsor, regulatory), and deviation status. Distinct from safety events — deviations are GCP compliance events. Sourced from CTMS and EDC systems.'
}

Table compliance.deviation_classification {
  deviation_classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for deviation_classification']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: deviation_classification.sop_reference_code (STRING) is a free-text reference to the SOP governing this deviation classification type. This is a classic normalization opportunity: replacing the string']
  applicable_phase varchar [note: 'Clinical trial phase(s) to which this deviation classification applies (e.g., \'Phase I\', \'Phase II\', \'Phase III\', \'Phase IV\', \'All\'). Some deviation types are phase-specific (e.g., first-in-human dose escalation deviations). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Phase I|Phase II|Phase III|Phase IV|Phase I/II|Phase II/III|All — promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this deviation classification record was formally approved by the QA authority in MasterControl. Part of the 21 CFR Part 11 compliant audit trail for the quality management system.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) plan must be initiated for deviations of this type. Drives CAPA workflow initiation in MasterControl quality management system.']
  classification_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this deviation classification record: \'draft\' (under review), \'active\' (approved and in use), \'retired\' (no longer applicable to new studies), or \'superseded\' (replaced by a newer version). Controls which classifications are available for selection in EDC and CTMS.']
  classification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this deviation classification record (e.g., \'1.0\', \'2.1\'). Supports version control of the taxonomy in MasterControl, ensuring studies reference the correct classification version at the time of protocol approval.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or clarifications regarding the application of this deviation classification, including edge cases, sponsor-specific interpretations, or historical context. Maintained by the QA team in MasterControl.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this deviation classification record was first created in MasterControl. Supports ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']
  csr_reporting_section varchar [note: 'Section of the Clinical Study Report (CSR) where deviations of this type must be summarized per ICH E3 guidelines (e.g., \'Section 10.2 — Protocol Deviations\', \'Section 12 — Safety\'). Guides medical writers and biostatisticians in SAS Drug Development.']
  data_integrity_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential impact on study data integrity for deviations of this type: \'none\', \'potential\', or \'confirmed\'. Drives data review and query generation priorities in Medidata Rave EDC and informs database lock (DBL) decisions.']
  deviation_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the deviation severity: \'major\' (significant impact on subject safety or data integrity), \'minor\' (minimal impact), or \'important_protocol_deviation\' (IPD — deviations that may significantly affect subject safety, rights, or data reliability per ICH E6(R2) and FDA guidance). Drives regulatory reporting obligations and CAPA requirements.']
  deviation_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification grouping deviation types into functional areas (e.g., \'Eligibility\', \'Dosing\', \'Visit Schedule\', \'Informed Consent\', \'Laboratory\', \'Randomization\', \'Blinding\'). Enables granular analytics and trend reporting across studies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eligibility|dosing|visit_schedule|informed_consent|laboratory|randomization|blinding|data_collection|safety_reporting|concomitant_medication — promote to reference product]']
  deviation_type_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the specific deviation type within the classification taxonomy (e.g., PD-001, PD-002). Used as the externally-known business identifier for cross-study referencing and regulatory submissions. Managed in MasterControl.']
  deviation_type_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the deviation type (e.g., \'Missed Study Visit\', \'Incorrect Dose Administration\', \'Eligibility Criteria Violation\'). Serves as the primary identity label for display in CTMS, EDC, and regulatory reports.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this deviation classification version becomes effective and applicable to new and ongoing studies. Studies initiated on or after this date must use this classification version.']
  ema_guidance_reference varchar [note: 'Specific EMA guideline or regulation section applicable to this deviation type (e.g., \'EMA GCP Guideline CPMP/ICH/135/95\', \'EU CTR 536/2014 Article 54\'). Supports compliance for EU-based clinical trials.']
  fda_guidance_reference varchar [note: 'Specific FDA guidance document and section applicable to this deviation type (e.g., \'FDA Guidance for Industry: Oversight of Clinical Investigations, Section IV\'). Supports regulatory inspection readiness and audit trail documentation.']
  frequency_threshold_count varchar [note: 'Maximum acceptable number of occurrences of this deviation type at a single site before a systematic issue is declared and escalated per the risk-based monitoring plan. Null if no frequency threshold is defined for this type.']
  gcp_principle_violated varchar [note: 'Specific ICH GCP principle(s) violated by deviations of this type (e.g., \'Subject Rights and Safety\', \'Data Integrity\', \'Investigator Responsibilities\'). Used in QA reporting and regulatory inspection responses to demonstrate GCP compliance awareness.']
  ich_guidance_reference varchar [note: 'Specific ICH guideline section(s) applicable to this deviation type (e.g., \'ICH E6(R2) Section 4.5\', \'ICH E8(R1)\'). Provides the authoritative regulatory basis for the classification and reporting requirements.']
  inspection_readiness_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing guidance on how to present and defend deviations of this type during FDA, EMA, or MHRA regulatory inspections. Maintained by the QA team to support audit readiness activities.']
  irb_iec_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations of this type require prompt notification to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) per applicable regulations and sponsor SOPs. Drives site-level compliance workflows in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  is_blinding_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations of this type involve or risk unblinding of treatment assignment in blinded studies. When True, special handling procedures apply to protect study integrity and prevent unintended unblinding during deviation review.']
  itt_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations of this type may trigger exclusion of the affected subject from the Intent-to-Treat (ITT) analysis population. Typically False for most deviation types; True only for critical eligibility violations. Reviewed by biostatistics per the SAP.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this deviation classification record was most recently modified in MasterControl. Part of the 21 CFR Part 11 compliant audit trail. Used to detect taxonomy changes that may affect ongoing studies.']
  mastercontrol_record_number varchar [note: 'Source system record identifier from MasterControl document control system for this deviation classification entry. Enables traceability back to the authoritative quality management system record for audit purposes.']
  meddra_soc_code varchar [note: 'MedDRA System Organ Class (SOC) code associated with this deviation type when the deviation relates to a clinical or safety event. Enables cross-referencing with adverse event coding in Oracle Argus Safety for integrated safety analysis.']
  monitoring_escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether identification of this deviation type at a site requires escalation of monitoring intensity (e.g., triggered on-site visit, increased SDV rate) per the risk-based monitoring plan. Drives monitoring plan adjustments in Oracle Siebel CTMS.']
  per_protocol_exclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations of this type automatically trigger exclusion of the affected subject from the Per Protocol (PP) analysis population. Used by biostatisticians in SAS Drug Development when applying the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) population definitions.']
  regulatory_reporting_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this deviation type meets the threshold requiring regulatory reporting to the FDA, EMA, or other applicable health authority. When True, deviations of this type must be included in IND annual reports, clinical study reports (CSR), or expedited submissions per applicable regulations.']
  reporting_timeframe_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days within which a deviation of this type must be reported to the applicable regulatory authority or sponsor after identification. Null if no mandatory reporting timeframe applies. Used to trigger automated deadline alerts in CTMS.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date on which this deviation classification version was retired or superseded. Null if the classification is currently active. Studies ongoing at retirement may continue using the prior version per protocol freeze rules.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk score (typically 1–10) assigned to this deviation type based on its potential impact on subject safety, data integrity, and regulatory compliance. Used in risk-based monitoring (RBM) algorithms in Oracle Siebel CTMS to prioritize site monitoring activities.']
  sdtm_domain_code varchar [note: 'CDISC SDTM domain code associated with this deviation type (typically \'DV\' for Protocol Deviations). Used to map deviation records to the correct SDTM submission dataset during regulatory data package preparation in SAS Drug Development.']
  sponsor_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether deviations of this type require immediate or expedited notification to the study sponsor per ICH GCP and the clinical trial agreement. When True, the CRA or site must notify the sponsor within the defined timeframe.']
  standard_description_template varchar [note: 'Pre-approved narrative template text for describing deviations of this type in eCRFs, deviation logs, and regulatory submissions. Ensures consistent, ALCOA+-compliant documentation language across studies and sites. Managed in MasterControl.']
  subject_safety_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential impact on subject safety for deviations of this type: \'none\' (no safety implications), \'potential\' (possible safety concern requiring evaluation), or \'confirmed\' (known safety impact). Informs risk-based monitoring prioritization and DSMB review.']
  superseded_by_code varchar [note: 'Deviation type code of the classification record that replaces this one when status is \'superseded\'. Enables forward navigation of the classification version history for regulatory audit trail purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the individual who created this deviation classification record in MasterControl. Supports ALCOA+ attributability requirements and 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the standardized classification taxonomy for protocol deviations used across all studies. Captures deviation category (major, minor, important protocol deviation), deviation type code, regulatory reporting threshold, standard description template, applicable ICH or FDA guidance reference, and whether the deviation type requires IRB/IEC notification. Enables consistent deviation categorization across studies and sponsors. Managed in MasterControl.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_sop {
  compliance_sop_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Standard Operating Procedure record in the quality management system. Primary key for the sop data product.']
  superseded_sop_compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sop record that this version supersedes. Supports version lineage navigation and ensures the document control chain of custody is maintained for regulatory inspection.']
  applicable_roles varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of job roles or role groups required to be trained on and comply with this SOP (e.g., CRA, CRC, PI, QA Manager, Data Manager). Drives training assignment generation in MasterControl.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the SOP version received final authorized approval in MasterControl. Distinct from the effective date; the SOP may be approved before it becomes operationally binding to allow training completion.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the authorized signatory who approved the current version of the SOP. Typically a senior QA or functional head. Required for regulatory inspection readiness and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  audit_finding_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier of the internal or external audit finding that prompted this SOP revision or creation. Supports traceability from audit observations to corrective document updates.']
  capa_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of any CAPA record in MasterControl that triggered the creation or revision of this SOP version. Links SOP updates to root cause corrective actions identified through audits, inspections, or deviation investigations.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the change control record in MasterControl that authorized the creation or revision of this SOP version. Provides traceability from the SOP to the formal change management process.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to the SOP document content, governing who may access and distribute it. Aligns with the organization\'s information security policy and GDPR/HIPAA data handling requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SOP record was first created in the quality management system. Supports ALCOA+ audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  deviation_classification_relevance varchar [note: 'Indicates the severity classification of protocol deviations that non-compliance with this SOP would typically generate (critical, major, minor). Used by QA to pre-classify deviation risk and prioritize SOP compliance monitoring.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'Functional category grouping for the SOP within the document management taxonomy (e.g., Quality Management, Clinical Operations, Data Management, Regulatory Affairs, Safety, Laboratory, Finance). Supports document library navigation and compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality_management|clinical_operations|data_management|regulatory_affairs|safety|laboratory|finance|human_resources|supply_chain|information_technology — promote to reference product]']
