  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SOP document within the quality management system workflow. Controls whether the SOP is actionable for training and compliance purposes. \'approved\' is the only status permitting operational use.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document within the quality management system hierarchy. SOPs define mandatory procedures; work instructions provide step-by-step task guidance; policies set organizational rules; guidance documents are advisory.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the approved SOP version becomes operationally binding for all applicable staff and sites. Training must be completed by this date. Aligns with the ALCOA+ principle of contemporaneous documentation.']
  electronic_signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP requires electronic signatures for approval in compliance with FDA 21 CFR Part 11 and EU Annex 11 electronic records requirements. When True, wet signatures are not acceptable as the sole approval mechanism.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the SOP version is scheduled to expire and must be reviewed, revised, or retired. Typically set to a fixed interval (e.g., 2–3 years) from the effective date per the organization\'s document control SOP.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP governs processes that involve personal data subject to GDPR requirements (e.g., patient data handling, consent management, data subject rights). Drives GDPR compliance review during SOP periodic review.']
  geographic_applicability varchar [note: 'Countries or regions where this SOP is applicable, expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region labels (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, GLOBAL). Relevant for multi-regional trials where regulatory requirements differ by jurisdiction.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP governs processes subject to HIPAA Privacy and Security Rules for U.S.-based studies involving protected health information (PHI). Relevant for studies conducted at U.S. sites.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP has been reviewed and confirmed as current and compliant in preparation for a regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA). Set to True when QA has verified the SOP is up-to-date and all training is complete.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code of the SOP document (e.g., en, fr, de, ja). Supports multi-language document management for global trials and ensures site staff receive SOPs in the appropriate language.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SOP record was most recently updated in the quality management system or the lakehouse Silver Layer. Supports change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail completeness.']
  mastercontrol_doc_number varchar [note: 'Native document identifier assigned by the MasterControl quality management system. Used for direct cross-reference and deep-linking back to the source system record for audit trail and change control purposes.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Name of the organizational department accountable for authoring, maintaining, and periodically reviewing this SOP (e.g., Quality Assurance, Data Management, Regulatory Affairs). Determines the primary escalation path for SOP-related queries.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Job role or title of the individual responsible for the SOP within the owning department (e.g., QA Manager, Director of Data Management). Used for accountability tracking and escalation in audit findings.']
  parent_sop_number varchar [note: 'SOP number of the higher-level or governing SOP under which this SOP sits in the document hierarchy (e.g., a site-specific SOP may reference a global SOP as its parent). Supports hierarchical document structure navigation.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'Primary clinical trial business process area governed by this SOP (e.g., Study Protocol Design, Patient Recruitment, Adverse Event Reporting, Site Monitoring, Data Management, Regulatory Submission). Used for process-level compliance gap analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: study_protocol_design|site_selection|patient_recruitment|informed_consent|data_collection|safety_reporting|regulatory_submission|site_monitoring|biostatistics|study_closeout|laboratory|supply_chain|finance|document_management — promote to reference product]']
  purpose_description varchar [note: 'Brief statement of the business purpose and objective of the SOP, summarizing what process it governs and why it exists. Sourced from the SOP document\'s purpose/objective section.']
  qms_workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the SOP within the MasterControl quality management system workflow. More granular than document_status; tracks the specific workflow step the document is in during its lifecycle (e.g., authoring, peer review, QA review, approval routing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: authoring|peer_review|qa_review|approval|effective|periodic_review|retirement — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Primary regulatory practice standard(s) that this SOP is designed to satisfy: Good Clinical Practice (GCP), Good Laboratory Practice (GLP), Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP), or applicable combinations. Drives which regulatory inspections this SOP is relevant to.']
  regulatory_references varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured list of specific regulatory citations, guidelines, and standards that this SOP implements or references (e.g., ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18, FDA 21 CFR Part 312, EU CTR 536/2014 Article 47). Supports regulatory inspection readiness and gap analysis.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the SOP owner must initiate a periodic review to confirm continued applicability and accuracy. May precede the expiry date to allow time for revision and approval before expiry.']
  revision_summary varchar [note: 'Brief narrative describing the nature and rationale of changes made in this version relative to the superseded version (e.g., \'Updated to reflect ICH E6(R3) requirements; added remote monitoring guidance\'). Required for change control documentation.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the scope and applicability of the SOP, including which studies, sites, roles, or geographies it applies to. Sourced from the SOP document\'s scope section. Critical for determining applicability during inspections.']
  sop_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable document control number assigned to the SOP within MasterControl (e.g., QA-GCP-001). Serves as the business identifier used in audit trails, regulatory submissions, and training records. Unique per SOP title across all versions.']
  sox_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP governs financial reporting or internal control processes subject to SOX compliance requirements (e.g., finance, procurement, project accounting SOPs). Relevant for publicly-listed sponsors and CROs.']
  superseded_version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the immediately preceding SOP version that this current version replaces. Enables version lineage tracing and ensures retired versions are correctly linked in the document control audit trail.']
  title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the SOP as it appears on the document cover page and in the quality management system. Used for search, display, and regulatory filing purposes.']
  training_completion_deadline_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from the SOP effective date within which all applicable staff must complete the required training. Used by MasterControl to auto-calculate individual training due dates and trigger overdue notifications.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of a formal training event is mandatory for all applicable staff before they may operate under this SOP. When True, a training assignment must be created in MasterControl for each in-scope role upon SOP approval.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Current version identifier of the SOP document (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0). Incremented upon each approved revision cycle. Used to ensure staff are trained on and referencing the correct version.']

  Note: 'Master record for Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) managed within the quality management system. Captures SOP number, title, version, effective date, expiry date, SOP owner (department/role), applicable process area, regulatory basis (GCP, GLP, GMP), training requirement flag, document status (draft, under review, approved, retired), and superseded version reference. Sourced from MasterControl document control module. SSOT for SOP identity — distinct from the document domain which manages TMF and regulatory binders.'
}

Table compliance.sop_training_record {
  sop_training_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each SOP training record in the MasterControl training management module. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — captures a discrete training completion event with its own lifecycle.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CAPA record that triggered this retraining event, if applicable. Links training records to quality events such as protocol deviations, audit findings, or inspection observations that required corrective retraining. Null for routine training.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SOP document that this training record is associated with. References the controlled SOP document in MasterControl document control module.']
  electronic_signature_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the 21 CFR Part 11-compliant electronic signature applied by the staff member to confirm training completion. Mandatory for GCP-regulated training records. Links to the electronic signature audit trail in MasterControl.']
  investigational_site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the investigational site associated with this training record, if the training is site-specific. Null for sponsor/CRO staff training not tied to a specific site. Enables site-level training compliance tracking for site activation and monitoring.']
  site_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to site.site_contact. Business justification: SOP training records track GCP and protocol training completion for site staff. Linking sop_training_record to site_contact enables per-staff-member training compliance reporting, delegation log valid']
  sop_training_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop_training_requirement. Business justification: A training record documents the completion of a training requirement. Linking sop_training_record.sop_training_requirement_id → sop_training_requirement.sop_training_requirement_id creates the critica']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the clinical study for which this training is required, if the SOP training is study-specific or protocol-specific. Null for organization-wide SOPs applicable to all studies. Enables study-level training compliance reporting.']
  assessment_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the staff member achieved a passing score on the training assessment. True if assessment_score >= passing_score_threshold; False if below threshold; Null if no assessment was required for this training method.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved by the staff member on the post-training assessment or quiz, expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Applicable only for training methods that include a formal assessment (e-learning, classroom). Null for read-and-understand method. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential attempt number for this training record, indicating how many times the staff member has attempted the training or assessment for this SOP version. First attempt = 1. Supports quality metrics and identification of staff requiring additional support.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or observations related to this training record, such as trainer observations, special circumstances, or quality notes. Supports audit trail completeness and GCP inspection documentation.']
  completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the staff member completed the SOP training. For read-and-understand, this is the date the staff member signed off. For e-learning, this is the date the module was completed. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (date precision appropriate for training compliance).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time at which the training was electronically recorded as completed in MasterControl. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records requirements and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation standards. Distinct from completion_date which captures the business event date.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this training record was first created in the system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional unit of the staff member at the time of training (e.g., Clinical Operations, Data Management, Biostatistics, Quality Assurance). Used for departmental training compliance reporting and gap analysis.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the staff member is required to complete the SOP training. Calculated based on SOP effective date plus the organization\'s training grace period policy. Used to determine overdue status and drive compliance escalations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this training record expires and retraining or refresher training is required. Applicable for SOPs with periodic recertification requirements (e.g., annual GCP training). Null for SOPs with no expiry requirement.']
  is_current_version_training boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training record corresponds to the currently effective version of the SOP. True if the sop_version matches the current approved version; False if the staff member was trained on a superseded version. Critical for GCP inspection readiness checks.']
  passing_score_threshold decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum assessment score percentage required to achieve a passing result for this SOP training (e.g., 80.00%). Enables determination of pass/fail outcome and supports quality metrics on training effectiveness.']
  signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time at which the staff member applied their electronic signature to confirm training completion. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance and ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation. Distinct from completion_timestamp which records system processing time.']
  sop_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the SOP being trained on (e.g., Clinical Operations, Data Management, Safety Reporting, Regulatory Affairs, Quality Assurance, Laboratory). Enables category-level compliance reporting and training gap analysis by functional area. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clinical_operations|data_management|safety_reporting|regulatory_affairs|quality_assurance|laboratory|finance|it_systems|human_resources — promote to reference product]']
  sop_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the SOP document on which the staff member was trained (e.g., \'2.0\', \'3.1\'). Critical for GCP compliance verification — training must be on the current approved version. Enables identification of staff trained on superseded versions.']
  source_record_number varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the source operational system (e.g., MasterControl internal training record ID). Enables traceability back to the originating system for audit and reconciliation purposes per ALCOA+ principles.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this training record was sourced (e.g., MasterControl for system-generated records, Workday for HR-driven training, manual for paper-based records migrated to the lakehouse). Supports data lineage and audit trail.']
  staff_role varchar [note: 'The job role or function of the staff member at the time of training (e.g., Clinical Research Associate, Principal Investigator, Data Manager, Biostatistician). Captured at training time for historical accuracy. Used to verify role-appropriate SOP training coverage during GCP inspections.']
  trainer_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the qualified trainer who delivered the training. Denormalized for audit trail completeness and GCP inspection readiness. Supports historical record integrity even if trainer record is modified.']
  training_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual time in minutes spent by the staff member completing the training. Captured automatically by e-learning systems or recorded manually for classroom and OJT sessions. Used for training effort reporting and curriculum planning.']
  training_method varchar [note: 'The instructional delivery method used for this SOP training event. Read-and-understand (R&U) is the most common GCP method; classroom and e-learning are used for complex SOPs; on-the-job training (OJT) is used for procedural skills. Drives assessment requirements and documentation standards.']
  training_record_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for this training record as assigned by MasterControl (e.g., TR-2024-000123). Used for audit trail references, GCP inspection documentation, and regulatory correspondence. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this training record indicating whether the staff member has completed, is overdue, has been granted a waiver, is in progress, or has not yet started the required SOP training. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the training event indicating whether it is initial training (first time on this SOP), retraining (following a failed assessment or deviation), refresher (periodic recertification), or change-control training (triggered by a new SOP version release).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time at which this training record was last modified. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail integrity and change tracking.']
  waiver_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the Quality Assurance (QA) manager or authorized approver who granted the training waiver. Required for audit trail and GCP inspection documentation when training_status is \'waived\'.']
  waiver_date date [note: 'The date on which the training waiver was formally approved and recorded. Applicable only when training_status is \'waived\'. Supports audit trail completeness per ALCOA+ principles.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Documented justification for granting a training waiver when training_status is \'waived\'. Examples include equivalent prior training, role change making SOP non-applicable, or temporary exemption with documented rationale. Required for audit trail completeness.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing individual staff training completions against required SOPs. Captures staff member identifier, SOP version trained on, training method (read-and-understand, classroom, e-learning, OJT), training completion date, assessment score (if applicable), trainer identifier, and training status (completed, overdue, waived). Enables SOP compliance tracking and training currency verification for GCP inspections. Sourced from MasterControl training management module.'
}

Table compliance.sop_training_requirement {
  sop_training_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SOP training requirement record. Primary key for this entity.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SOP document that this training requirement is based upon. Links to the controlled SOP document record in the document management system.']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study for which this training requirement applies. Null if the requirement is global (not study-specific). Enables study-specific training obligation tracking.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the organizational scope at which this training requirement applies. \'global\' = all staff; \'study_specific\' = only staff assigned to a particular study; \'department_specific\' = only a department; \'role_specific\' = only a job role; \'site_specific\' = only staff at a particular investigational site.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this training requirement was formally approved by the QA or training manager. Part of the electronic approval audit trail required under 21 CFR Part 11.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the QA or training manager who approved this training requirement definition in MasterControl. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  auto_assign_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether MasterControl should automatically assign this training requirement to qualifying personnel when they are assigned to the applicable role, department, or study. When False, manual assignment by a training coordinator is required.']
  capa_required_on_overdue boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal CAPA must be initiated when this training requirement becomes overdue beyond the grace period. Distinct from escalation_required — CAPA is a formal quality system record with root cause analysis obligations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SOP training requirement record was first created in the system. Part of the ALCOA+ audit trail required for GCP and 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  curriculum_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the training curriculum or learning path in MasterControl to which this SOP training requirement belongs. Enables grouping of related SOP requirements into structured training programs (e.g., GCP Onboarding Curriculum, Annual Refresher Curriculum).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this training requirement becomes enforceable. Personnel assigned to the applicable role, department, or study on or after this date are subject to this requirement.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance escalation notification must be triggered if training is not completed within the grace period. When True, MasterControl generates a CAPA or non-conformance record and notifies the QA manager.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which this training requirement is no longer enforceable, typically when the SOP is retired or superseded. Null for open-ended requirements. Used to automatically deactivate requirements in MasterControl.']
  gcp_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training requirement is classified as a GCP training obligation under ICH E6(R2). GCP-applicable requirements are subject to heightened audit scrutiny and must be documented in the Trial Master File (TMF).']
  geographic_applicability varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code(s) or region identifier indicating the geographic jurisdiction(s) where this training requirement applies. Supports multi-regional regulatory compliance (e.g., FDA requirements for USA, EMA requirements for EU member states).']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days after the training due date during which the training may still be completed without triggering a compliance violation or non-conformance. Typically 0–30 days depending on SOP criticality and sponsor requirements.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SOP training is mandatory (True) or optional/recommended (False) for the assigned role, department, or study. Mandatory training must be completed before study participation or role activation per GCP requirements.']
  is_study_participation_prerequisite boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of this SOP training is a prerequisite for a staff member to be activated on a clinical study. When True, the system blocks study assignment until training is recorded as complete.']
  job_function varchar [note: 'The specific job function or functional area for which this training requirement applies (e.g., Clinical Research Associate, Data Manager, Biostatistician, Principal Investigator). Complements role_id with a human-readable functional label for reporting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this SOP training requirement record was most recently updated. Part of the ALCOA+ audit trail. Any modification must be accompanied by a reason for change per 21 CFR Part 11.']
  max_attempts_allowed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a trainee may attempt the training assessment before escalation or remediation is required. Null if unlimited attempts are permitted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or clarifications related to this SOP training requirement. May include sponsor-specific instructions, exceptions, or implementation guidance for training coordinators.']
  passing_score_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage score required to pass the training assessment associated with this SOP requirement. Applicable when training_delivery_method includes an assessment component. Null if no scored assessment is required.']
  phase_applicability varchar [note: 'Specifies the clinical trial phase(s) for which this SOP training requirement is applicable. Enables phase-specific training assignment for staff transitioning between study phases.']
  reason_for_change varchar [note: 'Free-text justification for the most recent modification to this training requirement record. Mandatory for any update per 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements and ALCOA+ data integrity principles.']
  recurrence_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required retraining cycles for recurring training types (e.g., 365 for annual). Null for one-time training types such as \'initial\'. Used by MasterControl to auto-generate the next training due date.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory citation, guideline, or standard that mandates or supports this training requirement (e.g., \'ICH E6(R2) Section 2.8\', \'21 CFR Part 312.53\', \'EU CTR 536/2014 Article 49\'). Used for regulatory inspection readiness and audit documentation.']
  reminder_days_before_due varchar [note: 'Number of days before the training due date that an automated reminder notification should be sent to the trainee and their manager. Configurable per SOP criticality to drive proactive compliance.']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the SOP training requirement record, used for cross-system referencing and audit trail. Follows the format TRQ-[alphanumeric]. Serves as the externally-known identifier in MasterControl.']
  requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SOP training requirement record. \'active\' = currently enforced; \'inactive\' = no longer enforced; \'superseded\' = replaced by a newer version requirement; \'draft\' = under review; \'pending_approval\' = awaiting QA sign-off.']
  sop_version varchar [note: 'Version of the SOP document for which this training requirement was defined. Critical for tracking whether personnel have been trained on the current version versus a superseded version.']
  sponsor_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this training requirement was mandated by the study sponsor as a condition of the clinical trial agreement. Sponsor-required training may have additional documentation and audit trail obligations.']
  therapeutic_area varchar [note: 'The therapeutic area or disease indication for which this training requirement is relevant (e.g., Oncology, Cardiology, Neurology). Used to filter and assign study-specific SOP training requirements to staff working in a given therapeutic area.']
  tmf_documentation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether evidence of training completion for this requirement must be filed in the Trial Master File (TMF) or electronic TMF (eTMF) in Veeva Vault. Required for GCP-essential training per ICH E6(R2) Section 8.']
  training_category varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the SOP training requirement. Used for compliance reporting, training curriculum grouping, and regulatory inspection readiness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: gcp|regulatory|safety|data_management|clinical_operations|quality|sponsor_specific — promote to reference product]']
  training_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Specifies the required method by which the SOP training must be completed. \'read_and_understand\' = staff reads and signs off; \'instructor_led\' = classroom or virtual session; \'elearning\' = online module; \'on_the_job\' = supervised practical; \'assessment_only\' = pass a quiz without formal training.']
  training_material_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier or URL reference to the training material, curriculum, or assessment associated with this SOP requirement in MasterControl or the eTMF. Enables direct navigation to training content from compliance dashboards.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the trigger or frequency basis for this training requirement. \'initial\' = first-time training upon hire or role assignment; \'annual\' = recurring yearly retraining; \'on_change\' = triggered by SOP version update; \'role_assignment\' = triggered by new role; \'study_assignment\' = triggered by assignment to a specific study.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this training requirement record in MasterControl. Part of the ALCOA+ audit trail (Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate) required for GCP compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record defining which SOPs are required training for which roles, departments, or study assignments. Captures role or job function, SOP identifier, training frequency (initial, annual, on-change), grace period days, and whether training is mandatory for study participation. Drives automated training assignment and compliance gap identification in MasterControl. Distinct from the training record (which captures completion) — this defines the obligation.'
}

Table compliance.quality_event {
  quality_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the quality event record within the MasterControl quality management module. Serves as the primary key for all downstream references to this quality event.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal CAPA record initiated in MasterControl as a result of this quality event. Populated only when capa_required is True and the CAPA has been formally opened. Null until CAPA is initiated.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: quality_event.affected_sop_number is a free-text string identifying the SOP implicated in the quality event. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id normalizes the relationship, enabling structured analy']
  database_lock_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.database_lock_event. Business justification: Quality events arising from database lock failures (outstanding queries, failed QC checks, unauthorized lock) must reference the specific DBL event. Required for CAPA management and regulatory inspect']
  kit_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.kit_assignment. Business justification: Kit dispensing errors (wrong kit, temperature excursion, expired kit dispensed) are quality events requiring QMS tracking. The kit_assignment has temperature_excursion_flag and protocol_deviation_flag']
  manufacture_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.manufacture_batch. Business justification: Manufacturing batch failures, OOS results, and QP release holds are quality events under GMP. Linking quality_event to the affected manufacture_batch enables batch recall traceability, GMP deviation m']
  parent_event_quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a prior quality event record that this event is a recurrence of, or a related parent event in a cluster of related quality issues. Populated when recurrence_flag is True or when events are grouped for systemic analysis. Null for first-occurrence events.']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: quality_event.deviation_reference_number is a free-text string referencing a related protocol deviation. Replacing it with a FK to protocol_deviation.compliance_protocol_deviation_id creates a structu']
  specimen_collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.specimen_collection. Business justification: Specimen collection quality failures (hemolysis, lipemia, inadequate volume) are formal quality events requiring CAPA. specimen_collection has hemolysis_grade, lipemia_grade, sample_adequacy. Linking ']
  specimen_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.specimen_shipment. Business justification: Temperature excursions during specimen shipment are a named quality event type in clinical trials. specimen_shipment has temperature_excursion_flag. Linking the shipment to the formal quality event re']
  study_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the clinical study affected by this quality event. Links the event to the specific protocol under which the quality issue was identified.']
  study_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the investigational site where the quality event was identified or originated. Null if the event is study-wide or sponsor/CRO-level rather than site-specific.']
  subject_randomization_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.subject_randomization. Business justification: Randomization system errors (double randomization, IRT failure during randomization call, allocation concealment breach) are quality events in the QMS. QA investigators need direct navigation from the']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Quality events (data integrity issues, process failures) require TMF-filed investigation and resolution documents per GCP. A QA expert expects this link to confirm quality event documentation is filed']
  affected_data_domain varchar [note: 'The CDISC SDTM or clinical data domain affected by the quality event (e.g., AE, CM, LB, VS, DM, DS, EX). Used for data management impact assessment, targeted re-monitoring, and SDTM submission quality review. Null if the event is not data-domain-specific.']
  affected_process varchar [note: 'The specific clinical trial business process or functional area impacted by the quality event (e.g., informed consent management, adverse event reporting, randomization, data entry, site monitoring, regulatory submission). Used for process-level quality trending and SOP compliance analytics.']
  audit_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quality event record has been reviewed and confirmed as complete and audit-ready for regulatory inspection or sponsor audit purposes. True: record is audit-ready. False: record requires additional documentation or review before inspection. Supports TMF/eTMF completeness and inspection readiness programs.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) has been determined to be required for this quality event. True: CAPA must be initiated in MasterControl. False: event can be resolved without formal CAPA. This is the primary escalation decision field in the quality event triage workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the quality event record was first created in MasterControl. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail requirements and ALCOA+ attributability. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  data_integrity_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality event has compromised or potentially compromised the integrity of clinical trial data in the EDC or source documents. True: data integrity affected. False: no data integrity impact. Drives data review, query management, and potential database lock delay decisions.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors, discrepancies, or non-conformances identified within the QC sample reviewed. Used in conjunction with sample_size to compute error rate for quality trending and threshold monitoring.']
  error_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of errors identified relative to the total sample size (error_count / sample_size). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.50%). Used for quality threshold monitoring, KPI reporting, and CAPA escalation triggers. Null if sample_size is zero or not applicable.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the quality event, including what was observed, where it occurred, and the potential impact on subject safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance. Captured at time of identification per ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation standards.']
  event_reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally-visible reference number assigned to the quality event upon creation in MasterControl. Used in audit trails, regulatory correspondence, and CAPA linkage. Format: QE-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality event within the MasterControl workflow. Open: newly identified, awaiting triage. Under Review: QA/QC team actively investigating. Pending CAPA: CAPA determination made, awaiting CAPA initiation. Closed: event resolved and verified. Cancelled: event determined to be invalid or duplicate.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality event by its nature. Drives routing, escalation rules, and CAPA determination logic. Values: data_quality_issue (EDC/eCRF data errors), process_non_compliance (GCP/SOP deviation), system_failure (EDC, CTMS, or IRT system outage/error), vendor_quality_issue (CRO, lab, or third-party vendor failure), qc_review_finding (finding from a formal QC review activity).']
  identification_source varchar [note: 'The activity or mechanism through which the quality event was detected. Drives root cause analysis and process improvement analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qc_review|self_inspection|routine_monitoring|sdv|audit|regulatory_inspection|proactive_surveillance|system_alert — promote to reference product]']
  identified_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the quality event was first identified or detected. This is the principal real-world event date per ALCOA+ contemporaneous documentation requirements. Distinct from the record creation timestamp.']
  immediate_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any immediate containment or corrective actions taken at the time of quality event identification, prior to formal CAPA initiation. Examples: data query raised in EDC, site notified, process halted. Supports ALCOA+ and GCP audit trail requirements.']
  irb_iec_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quality event must be reported to the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) overseeing the study. True: IRB/IEC notification required. False: not required. Driven by protocol deviation severity and subject safety impact.']
  protocol_deviation_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality event as a protocol deviation per ICH E6(R2) and sponsor requirements. Major: deviation that may affect subject safety or data integrity. Minor: deviation with minimal impact. Not Applicable: event is not a protocol deviation (e.g., system failure, vendor issue). Required for regulatory submission deviation summaries.']
  qc_outcome varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the QC review activity that generated this quality event. Pass: quality threshold met, no significant issues. Fail: quality threshold exceeded, formal action required. Conditional Pass: threshold borderline, enhanced monitoring required. Applicable when identification_source is qc_review.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quality event represents a recurrence of a previously identified and closed quality event of the same type at the same site or study. True: recurrence identified. False: first occurrence. Recurrences trigger mandatory CAPA escalation and may indicate CAPA ineffectiveness.']
  regulatory_authority_notified varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada) notified of this quality event, if applicable. Null if regulatory_reportability is not_reportable. Supports regulatory submission tracking and inspection readiness.']
  regulatory_reportability varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether this quality event requires reporting to a regulatory authority (FDA, EMA, MHRA, etc.) or to the sponsor. Reportable: must be submitted to regulatory body. Not Reportable: no regulatory notification required. Under Assessment: reportability determination pending QA review.']
  resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the quality event was formally resolved and closed in MasterControl. Populated when event_status transitions to closed. Used to calculate time-to-resolution KPIs and assess compliance with quality SLA targets.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of how the quality event was resolved, including actions taken, verification of effectiveness, and confirmation that the issue has been addressed. Required for event closure in MasterControl and supports regulatory inspection readiness.']
  review_date date [note: 'Date on which the QA reviewer formally assessed and triaged the quality event in MasterControl. Used to measure triage cycle time and compliance with internal quality SLA targets for event review.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the Quality Assurance (QA) professional who reviewed and assessed the quality event in MasterControl. Supports 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature audit trail and GCP oversight accountability.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause identified during quality event investigation. Used for systemic quality trending, SOP improvement prioritization, and CAPA effectiveness analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_gap|process_gap|system_error|communication_failure|resource_constraint|vendor_failure|protocol_complexity|unknown — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the root cause identified through investigation of the quality event. Captured by the QA investigator following root cause analysis. Supports CAPA development and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Total number of records, data points, or items reviewed during the QC activity that identified this quality event. Used to calculate error rate and assess the statistical significance of the finding. Applicable when the event originates from a QC review.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Risk-based severity classification of the quality event per GCP quality standards. Critical: may impact subject safety or data integrity requiring immediate action. Major: significant GCP deviation with potential regulatory impact. Minor: low-risk deviation with limited impact. Observation: informational finding with no immediate compliance risk.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this quality event was originated or imported into the data lakehouse. Supports data lineage tracking and cross-system quality event reconciliation. Primary source is MasterControl quality management module.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the study sponsor was formally notified of this quality event. Populated when sponsor_notified is True. Used to verify compliance with contractual and regulatory notification timelines.']
  sponsor_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this quality event. True: sponsor notification sent. False: sponsor not yet notified. Required for CRO-sponsor quality agreement compliance and sponsor oversight obligations under ICH E6(R2).']
  subject_safety_impact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quality event has a direct or potential impact on the safety of enrolled study subjects. True: subject safety may be or has been affected. False: no subject safety impact identified. Critical field for regulatory reportability determination and DSMB/DMC notification decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the quality event record in MasterControl. Required for audit trail completeness under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2). Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing quality events identified through QC reviews, self-inspections, routine quality monitoring, or proactive quality surveillance activities. Captures event reference number, event type (data quality issue, process non-compliance, system failure, vendor quality issue, QC review finding), severity classification, affected study or site, description, date identified, source of identification, error metrics (sample size, error count, error rate where applicable), QC outcome, escalation decision (CAPA required yes/no), and event status. Quality events are the primary intake mechanism for all quality signals before formal CAPA determination — they serve as the triage point between routine quality monitoring and formal corrective action. Sourced from MasterControl quality management module.'
}

Table compliance.risk_assessment {
  risk_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each quality risk assessment record within the compliance domain. Serves as the primary key for the risk_assessment data product.']
  blinding_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to randomization.blinding_config. Business justification: ICH E6(R2) risk-based monitoring requires formal risk assessment of blinding integrity (unblinding risk, allocation concealment failure). The risk_assessment has FKs to supply, lab, and safety domains']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Risk assessments can be conducted at inspection-level per the product description. When a risk assessment is scoped to a specific regulatory inspection (e.g., pre-inspection readiness assessment), it ']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_sop. Business justification: Risk assessments are conducted per SOPs and can assess SOP compliance or be governed by a specific SOP. Many other compliance products (audit_finding, inspection_finding, capa_action_item, obligation)']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.depot. Business justification: Depot qualification risk assessments evaluate cold chain capability, geographic coverage, and GMP compliance. Linking risk_assessment to the assessed depot enables risk-based depot selection, qualific']
  imp_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.imp_master. Business justification: IMP-specific risk assessments cover stability risks, cold chain requirements, controlled substance handling, and biological product safety. Linking risk_assessment to imp_master enables product-level ']
  lab_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to laboratory.lab_facility. Business justification: Risk-based monitoring requires formal risk assessments of laboratory facilities as part of vendor qualification and ongoing oversight. Linking risk_assessment to the specific lab_facility enables faci']
  protocol_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.protocol. Business justification: ICH E6 R2 mandates protocol-level risk assessments before study initiation. Risk assessments must be traceable to the protocol they cover for regulatory inspection, sponsor oversight reports, and TMF ']
  study_protocol_amendment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to study.study_protocol_amendment. Business justification: Protocol amendments trigger mandatory risk re-assessments under ICH E6 R2 risk-based monitoring. QA teams must link each risk assessment to the triggering amendment version for audit trail and regulat']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Risk assessments (risk-based monitoring plans, quality risk assessments) are TMF-filed documents per ICH E6(R2). A QA expert expects this link to confirm risk documentation is filed in the TMF and ava']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the risk assessment was formally approved by the authorized QA signatory. Required for regulatory audit trail and 21 CFR Part 11 electronic records compliance.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'The date on which the risk assessment was formally conducted and documented. Represents the principal business event date for this record. Used for trend analysis and review frequency tracking.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the risk assessment record in MasterControl: draft (being authored), in_review (under QA/sponsor review), approved (formally accepted), active (monitoring in progress), closed (risk resolved or accepted), cancelled (assessment voided).']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record must be initiated as a result of this risk assessment. Typically set to True when residual risk rating is critical or high, or when identified gaps meet CAPA threshold criteria.']
  control_measures_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the existing or planned quality control and risk mitigation measures applied to reduce the identified risk (e.g., enhanced SDV, targeted monitoring, additional training, SOP updates, EDC edit checks). Sourced from MasterControl.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this risk assessment record was first created in the source system (MasterControl). Supports audit trail requirements under 21 CFR Part 11 and ICH E6(R2) for electronic records.']
  data_integrity_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the identified risk has a potential or actual impact on clinical data integrity (ALCOA+ principles: Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate). Triggers enhanced data review and potential database lock delay.']
  gcp_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the identified risk has a direct impact on Good Clinical Practice (GCP) compliance. Drives escalation to QA leadership and potential regulatory notification obligations.']
  identified_gaps varchar [note: 'Narrative description of quality gaps, process deficiencies, or compliance vulnerabilities identified during the risk assessment (e.g., missing SDV documentation, incomplete ICF versions, delayed SAE reporting). Drives CAPA initiation and remediation planning.']
  inherent_detectability_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the ability to detect the risk before it causes harm, prior to control measures. Used in FMEA-style risk scoring (Risk Priority Number = Likelihood × Impact × Detectability).']
  inherent_impact_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the severity of impact on patient safety, data integrity, or regulatory compliance if the risk materializes before controls are applied. Component of the inherent risk score calculation.']
  inherent_likelihood_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the probability of the risk occurring before any control measures are applied. Component of the inherent risk score calculation per ICH Q9 risk estimation methodology.']
  inherent_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating derived from the inherent risk score before controls: critical, high, medium, or low. Used for risk prioritization, escalation thresholds, and sponsor/QA reporting dashboards.']
  inherent_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric risk score calculated before control measures are applied, typically derived from likelihood, impact, and detectability scores (e.g., product or weighted sum). Establishes the baseline risk level for prioritization.']
  inspection_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this risk assessment was conducted specifically in preparation for a regulatory inspection (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, or sponsor audit). Enables filtering of inspection-readiness assessments for pre-inspection reporting.']
  mastercontrol_record_number varchar [note: 'The native record identifier assigned by MasterControl (the system of record for this data product) to the risk assessment document. Enables direct traceability back to the source system for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this risk assessment, based on the defined review frequency. Supports proactive risk monitoring and inspection readiness. Calculated from assessment_date and review_frequency_days.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the risk assessment record in MasterControl (e.g., RA-2024-00123). Used for cross-system traceability, audit trails, and regulatory inspection readiness.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory authority whose standards and guidelines govern this risk assessment (e.g., FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada). Determines applicable GCP framework and reporting obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|MHRA|PMDA|TGA|Health_Canada|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  remediation_plan varchar [note: 'Documented plan for addressing identified gaps and reducing residual risk, including specific actions, responsible parties, and target timelines. Distinct from CAPA (which is a formal corrective/preventive action record); this is the risk-level remediation narrative.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation activities for identified gaps: not_started, in_progress, completed, or overdue. Supports inspection readiness dashboards and QA oversight reporting.']
  remediation_target_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the remediation actions identified in the risk assessment. Used for tracking remediation progress and escalation when overdue.']
  residual_detectability_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the ability to detect the risk after control measures are in place. Component of the residual risk score calculation.']
  residual_impact_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the severity of impact after control measures have been applied. Used to calculate the residual risk score.']
  residual_likelihood_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (typically 1–5) representing the probability of the risk occurring after control measures have been applied. Used to calculate the residual risk score.']
  residual_risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating after control measures are applied: critical, high, medium, or low. Drives risk acceptance decisions, escalation to DSMB/DMC, and CAPA initiation. A residual rating of critical or high typically triggers mandatory CAPA.']
  residual_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric risk score calculated after control measures are applied. Represents the remaining risk exposure. Compared against risk acceptance thresholds to determine if further mitigation is required.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Defined interval in calendar days between periodic re-assessments of this risk (e.g., 30, 60, 90, 180 days). Drives scheduling of next_review_date and ensures ongoing risk monitoring per ICH Q9 principles.']
  risk_acceptance_decision varchar [note: 'Formal decision on whether the residual risk level is acceptable: accepted (risk within tolerance), not_accepted (requires further mitigation or CAPA), or conditionally_accepted (accepted with defined monitoring conditions). Documented per ICH Q9 risk acceptance principles.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the risk domain being assessed: data_integrity (ALCOA+ violations, EDC issues), patient_safety (adverse event under-reporting, protocol deviations), regulatory_compliance (GCP, ICH, FDA/EMA non-compliance), or operational (site performance, resource, supply chain). Drives escalation and reporting pathways.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the identified risk, including the potential failure mode, root cause hypothesis, and potential impact on study quality, patient safety, or regulatory compliance. Sourced from MasterControl risk assessment form.']
  risk_owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department of the risk owner (e.g., Quality Assurance, Data Management, Clinical Operations, Regulatory Affairs). Used for departmental risk reporting and workload analysis.']
  risk_subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the risk category (e.g., \'Source Data Verification\', \'Informed Consent Process\', \'SAE Reporting Timeliness\', \'Protocol Deviation Management\'). Enables detailed risk trending and root cause analysis.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defines the level at which the risk assessment was conducted: study-level (entire protocol), site-level (specific investigational site), process-level (e.g., data management, consent), system-level (e.g., EDC, CTMS), or inspection-readiness (pre-inspection GCP audit preparation).']
  sponsor_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this risk assessment and its findings, as required by the Clinical Trial Agreement and GCP obligations. Relevant for high/critical risk ratings.']
  subject_safety_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the identified risk has a potential or actual impact on the safety of study subjects/patients. Triggers mandatory escalation to the Principal Investigator (PI), DSMB/DMC, and sponsor safety team.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title summarizing the subject and scope of the risk assessment (e.g., \'Site-Level GCP Compliance Risk Assessment – Site 1042 Q3 2024\'). Used for identification and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this risk assessment record in the source system. Required for change tracking, audit trail compliance, and Silver layer incremental load processing.']

  Note: 'Master record for quality risk assessments conducted at study, site, process, system, or inspection-readiness level to support risk-based quality management (RBQM). Captures risk assessment reference, scope (study-level, site-level, process-level, inspection-readiness), risk category (data integrity, patient safety, regulatory compliance, operational), risk description, inherent risk score, control measures, residual risk score, risk owner, assessment date, review frequency, identified gaps, and remediation tracking. Supports ICH Q9 risk-based approaches, FDA RBQM guidance, and proactive inspection preparation. Sourced from MasterControl.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_commitment {
  regulatory_commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each regulatory commitment record in the system. Primary key for the regulatory_commitment data product.']
  audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit_finding. Business justification: Regulatory commitments can arise directly from internal or sponsor-initiated audit findings — not only from inspection findings (which are already linked). The existing regulatory_commitment.inspectio']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_capa. Business justification: Regulatory commitments made to authorities (FDA, EMA) often require formal CAPA implementation. The commitment tracks the promise; the CAPA tracks the corrective/preventive action plan. This is not bi']
  compliance_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory inspection record that generated the finding underlying this commitment. Enables full traceability from inspection event through finding to commitment and closure.']
  compliance_sop_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.sop. Business justification: regulatory_commitment.sop_reference is a free-text string identifying the SOP associated with the commitment. Replacing it with a FK to sop.sop_id normalizes the relationship, enabling structured trac']
  dsmb_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to safety.dsmb_review. Business justification: DSMB reviews with stopping rule triggers or safety concerns requiring regulatory notification generate regulatory commitments. dsmb_review.regulatory_notification_required flag indicates this workflow']
  inspection_finding_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.inspection_finding. Business justification: Regulatory commitments frequently arise from specific inspection findings issued by regulatory authorities. regulatory_commitment already has inspection_id→inspection for the overall inspection, but l']
  interim_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.interim_analysis. Business justification: Interim analysis results — especially stopping rule triggers or safety signals — generate regulatory commitments to health authorities. Linking the commitment to the specific interim analysis provides']
  protocol_deviation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.protocol_deviation. Business justification: Regulatory commitments can be made to authorities specifically in response to protocol deviations — particularly major or critical deviations that require formal sponsor commitments to regulators (e.g']
  quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.quality_event. Business justification: Quality events with regulatory reportability (regulatory_reportability field on quality_event) can directly generate regulatory commitments. The current model links regulatory_commitment to compliance']
  regulatory_binder_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.regulatory_binder. Business justification: Regulatory commitments are made in response to specific regulatory submissions/binders (e.g., FDA Complete Response Letter commitments tied to an NDA binder). A regulatory affairs expert expects this ']
  sap_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.sap. Business justification: Regulatory commitments arising from authority inspections frequently mandate SAP amendments (e.g., adding sensitivity analyses, revising multiplicity adjustments). Linking the commitment to the SAP it']
  statistical_endpoint_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to biostatistics.statistical_endpoint. Business justification: Regulatory authorities issue commitments requiring changes to specific statistical endpoints — adding sensitivity analyses, revising non-inferiority margins, or changing the primary endpoint definitio']
  tmf_document_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to document.tmf_document. Business justification: Regulatory commitments made to health authorities must be documented and filed in the TMF (commitment letters, evidence of completion). A regulatory affairs expert expects this link for commitment clo']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the committed action was actually completed and verified. Compared against committed_completion_date to determine on-time performance and overdue status.']
  authority_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory authority formally acknowledged or accepted the commitment response or closure evidence. Marks the external closure of the commitment from the agency perspective.']
  authority_closure_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory authority\'s review of the commitment response. Indicates whether the agency has accepted the response, rejected it, requested additional information, or has it pending review.']
  closure_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative justification documenting the basis for closing this regulatory commitment, including evidence of completion and rationale for determining the committed action has been fully satisfied.']
  commitment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the regulatory commitment as formally communicated to the regulatory authority. Captures the full scope of what was committed, including context from the originating finding or query.']
  commitment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number assigned to the regulatory commitment (e.g., RC-2024-00123). Used in correspondence with regulatory authorities and in audit trails.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory commitment. Tracks progression from open through in-progress, completed, overdue, cancelled, or pending verification by the regulatory authority.']
  commitment_title varchar [note: 'Short, human-readable title summarizing the regulatory commitment. Used in dashboards, reports, and regulatory correspondence for quick identification.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the nature of the commitment (e.g., procedural change, training, system enhancement, documentation update, process improvement, labeling revision). Supports portfolio-level analysis of commitment categories. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: procedural|training|system|documentation|process_improvement|labeling|other — promote to reference product]']
  committed_action varchar [note: 'Specific action(s) formally committed to the regulatory authority in response to the originating finding or query. Describes what the organization has pledged to do, distinct from the broader commitment description.']
  committed_completion_date date [note: 'The date formally committed to the regulatory authority by which the action will be completed. This is the externally-binding deadline communicated in the regulatory response letter or inspection response.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording when this regulatory commitment record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements per 21 CFR Part 11 and ALCOA+.']
  data_integrity_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the originating finding involves a data integrity concern (e.g., ALCOA+ violations, EDC audit trail issues, source data discrepancies). Triggers additional data review and regulatory notification obligations.']
  effectiveness_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the effectiveness check was or is scheduled to be performed to verify that the committed action has resolved the underlying issue.']
  effectiveness_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a post-completion effectiveness check is required to verify that the committed action has achieved its intended outcome and prevented recurrence.']
  effectiveness_check_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the effectiveness check performed after completion of the committed action. Determines whether the commitment has been fully resolved or requires further action.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity of the originating inspection finding that triggered this commitment, aligned with GCP inspection grading standards (Critical, Major, Minor, Observation). Informs risk prioritization.']
  gcp_requirement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific GCP guideline, regulation, or standard that was cited in the originating regulatory finding (e.g., ICH E6(R2) Section 5.18, 21 CFR 312.62). Enables compliance gap analysis.']
  initiation_date date [note: 'The date on which this regulatory commitment record was formally initiated in the system, corresponding to the date the organization acknowledged the regulatory obligation.']
  mastercontrol_record_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corresponding record in MasterControl quality management system, where the commitment may be tracked as part of the document control, CAPA, or audit management workflow.']
  originating_action_reference varchar [note: 'The official reference number or identifier of the regulatory action that generated this commitment (e.g., FDA Warning Letter reference number, inspection report number, agency query tracking number). Enables direct traceability to the source regulatory document.']
  originating_action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory action that gave rise to this commitment. Distinguishes between inspection findings, FDA Warning Letters, Complete Response Letters (CRL), agency queries, Untitled Letters, and consent decrees. Drives workflow and escalation rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inspection_finding|warning_letter|complete_response_letter|agency_query|untitled_letter|consent_decree — promote to reference product]']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the regulatory commitment based on patient safety impact, regulatory risk, and agency expectations. Drives escalation and resource allocation decisions.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this commitment relates to a recurring finding that has been previously identified and committed upon. Recurrent findings carry heightened regulatory risk and may trigger escalated agency scrutiny.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'The regulatory authority to which this commitment was made (e.g., FDA, EMA, PMDA). Determines applicable regulatory framework, reporting timelines, and submission requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDA|EMA|PMDA|MHRA|TGA|Health_Canada|ANVISA|Other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  regulatory_response_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which the organization must submit a formal written response to the regulatory authority regarding this commitment. Distinct from the committed_completion_date, which is the action delivery date.']
  regulatory_response_submitted_date date [note: 'The date on which the formal written response was submitted to the regulatory authority. Used to confirm timely submission and calculate response latency.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional unit accountable for fulfilling the regulatory commitment (e.g., Quality Assurance, Clinical Operations, Regulatory Affairs). Supports departmental performance reporting.']
  sponsor_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the study sponsor was formally notified of this regulatory commitment. Supports sponsor oversight tracking and contractual compliance.']
  sponsor_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the study sponsor has been formally notified of this regulatory commitment. Required for sponsor oversight obligations under GCP and sponsor-CRO agreements.']
  subject_safety_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the originating finding or the commitment has a direct or potential impact on subject safety. Triggers expedited handling and escalation to the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) or sponsor safety team.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System-generated timestamp recording the most recent modification to this regulatory commitment record. Required for audit trail integrity and change tracking per 21 CFR Part 11.']

  Note: 'Master record tracking formal commitments made to regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA, PMDA) in response to inspection findings, warning letters, or agency queries. Captures commitment reference, originating regulatory action (inspection finding, warning letter, complete response letter), commitment description, committed action, responsible owner, committed completion date, actual completion date, and commitment status. Enables tracking of post-inspection regulatory obligations distinct from internal CAPA actions.'
}

Table compliance.ethics_committee {
  ethics_committee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the Institutional Review Board (IRB) or Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) record in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  parent_ethics_committee_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ethics_committee (parent_ethics_committee_id)']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that granted accreditation to this ethics committee (e.g., \'AAHRPP\', \'UKAS\', \'DAkkS\', \'NATA\'). Used to validate the committee\'s standing for regulatory submissions and sponsor qualification checks.']
  accreditation_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current accreditation certificate expires. Triggers renewal workflows and alerts to prevent use of an expired committee for new study submissions. Null if accreditation is perpetual or not applicable.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Official accreditation certificate number issued by the accrediting body. Referenced in regulatory submissions, TMF documentation, and sponsor qualification audits to verify committee standing.']
  accreditation_status varchar [note: 'Current accreditation status of the ethics committee with the relevant accrediting body (e.g., AAHRPP for U.S. IRBs, national competent authority for EU IECs). Determines eligibility to provide ethical oversight for sponsored clinical trials.']
  annual_renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether ongoing studies require annual continuing review and renewal of ethics approval from this committee. True = annual renewal mandatory; False = approval is indefinite until amendment or closure. Drives renewal reminder workflows in CTMS.']
  chair_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the current chairperson of the ethics committee. Required for regulatory correspondence, submission cover letters, and TMF documentation. Classified as confidential as it identifies a named individual in a professional capacity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the ethics committee office is physically located. Used for geographic reporting, site feasibility assessments, and regulatory jurisdiction mapping.']
  committee_category varchar [note: 'Operational category of the ethics committee: \'institutional\' (affiliated with a hospital or university), \'independent\' (standalone commercial IRB/IEC), \'central\' (single IRB/IEC covering multiple sites), \'national\' (government-mandated national body), \'regional\' (covering a geographic region). Drives site assignment and submission routing logic.']
  committee_code varchar [note: 'Unique business-facing alphanumeric code assigned to the IRB/IEC, used as the externally-known identifier in CTMS, eTMF, and regulatory submissions (e.g., \'IRB-MAYO-001\'). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this master-party entity.']
  committee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the oversight body: IRB (Institutional Review Board, U.S.-centric), IEC (Independent Ethics Committee, international), DSMB (Data Safety Monitoring Board), DMC (Data Monitoring Committee), or Ethics_Board (generic national/regional body). Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this master-party entity.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Official email address for submitting applications, amendments, and correspondence to the ethics committee. Serves as the PRIMARY_CONTACT for this master-party entity. Classified as confidential PII as it is an organizational contact email.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Official telephone number for the ethics committee office, used for urgent safety communications, protocol deviation notifications, and SAE reporting. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code representing the primary jurisdiction in which the ethics committee is registered and operates (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Drives regulatory framework applicability (FDA, EMA, MHRA, PMDA, TGA, Health Canada).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ethics committee master record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this master-party entity.']
  ctms_record_number varchar [note: 'Unique record identifier for this ethics committee in Oracle Siebel CTMS (Clinical Trial Management System), enabling cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.']
  engagement_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ethics committee\'s oversight of the clinical program was formally concluded or terminated. Null if the engagement is still active.']
  engagement_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this ethics committee was formally engaged to provide oversight for the clinical program. Marks the beginning of the regulatory relationship for this program-committee pairing.']
  engagement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this program-committee engagement. Tracks whether the committee is actively reviewing the program, suspended, or the engagement has been formally closed.']
  ethics_committee_name varchar [note: 'Official registered name of the IRB or IEC body as it appears on accreditation certificates and regulatory submissions (e.g., \'Mayo Clinic Institutional Review Board\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this master-party entity.']
  ethics_committee_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the ethics committee. \'active\' indicates the committee is fully operational and accepting submissions; \'suspended\' indicates temporary halt of operations; \'dissolved\' indicates permanent closure. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this master-party entity.']
  expedited_review_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ethics committee offers an expedited review pathway for minimal-risk studies or protocol amendments, allowing approval without convening a full committee meeting. True = expedited review available; False = full committee review required for all submissions.']
  fwa_number varchar [note: 'OHRP-issued Federalwide Assurance number for U.S. institutions, confirming commitment to protect human research subjects (format: FWA followed by 8 digits). Required for federally funded research and referenced in IND/NDA submissions to FDA.']
  gdpr_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) applies to submissions and data processed under this ethics committee\'s jurisdiction. True = GDPR requirements apply (EU/EEA-based committee); False = non-EU jurisdiction. Drives data privacy compliance workflows.']
  hipaa_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) privacy and security rules apply to research conducted under this ethics committee\'s oversight. True = HIPAA applies (U.S.-based committee); False = non-U.S. jurisdiction.']
  icf_template_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ethics committee mandates use of its own Informed Consent Form (ICF) template rather than the sponsor\'s template. True = committee-specific ICF template required; False = sponsor template acceptable with committee approval.']
